title: Alarmed Goose Breast (Alarmovaná prsa husí)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose breasts from two or three geese
- salt
- cloves
- peppercorns
- new spice
- vinegar
- bacon
- potatoes
- sweet or sour cream

## Directions

1. From two or three geese, remove the breasts, pull off the skin, salt them and place in a pot with several cloves, peppercorns, new spice and pour over with vinegar. After the breasts have been marinating for several days, clean them, lard them with bacon, place on a baking sheet with the larded side up and roast until red. Slice the breasts diagonally with a sharp knife into strips, reform them in their original shape, add fried potatoes. Pour sweet or sour cream into the sauce and serve separately in a dish.

title: Aleš (Shad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shad
- Butter
- Parsley root, a piece
- Parsley, a bunch of green
- Onion, sliced
- Allspice, a whole piece
- Salt
- Pepper
- Red wine

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the shad
2. Place a piece of butter, a piece of parsley root, a bunch of green parsley, and sliced onion on a pot
3. Place the fish on top
4. Salt and pepper it
5. Pour red wine over it so it is covered
6. Cover and let simmer slowly for three-quarters of an hour
7. Remove the fish to a dish
8. Surround it with sliced onion and keep in a warm place
9. Strain the sauce
10. Make a brown butter and flour roux and fry it
11. Let it simmer briefly
12. Strain again
13. Mix in a piece of butter, juice from one lemon and a pinch of paprika
14. Pour over the fish and serve to the table

title: Amoletky lehké (Light Omelets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons crushed sugar with vanilla or grated lemon peel
- flour (same amount as yolks)
- butter
- preserved jam
- cinnamon
- sugar
- cheese curds

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks with 6 tablespoons of sugar crushed with vanilla or grated lemon peel until foamy.
2. Add flour in amounts equal to the number of yolks, stirring for a quarter hour.
3. Grease an omelet pan with butter and pour a thin batter to the thickness of two fingers, baking over a gentle fire.
4. Turn out onto a plate and spread with preserved jam, roll up, and sprinkle with sugar and vanilla.
5. Alternatively, smaller omelets can be made and instead of jam, filled with sugar and cinnamon or cheese curds with raisins.

title: Amoletky obyčejné (Common Omelets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) milk
- piece of sugar
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- piece of chopped lemon peel
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix milk, sugar, butter, whole eggs, egg yolks, salt, lemon peel and enough flour to make a thick batter.
2. Grease an omelet pan with butter, pour in the batter, quickly fry on both sides and place on a plate.
3. When all omelets are ready, spread with plum jam or sprinkle with chopped cooked meat, fried roll, raisins, almonds, sliced apples or cheese curds.
4. Place on a sheet pan, pour with milk and beaten eggs, and dry in the oven.

title: Amoletky z vajec (Egg Omelet)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- salt
- 15 grams fresh butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs and pour into a pan
2. Add 2 tablespoons flour and mix
3. Salt, add 15 grams fresh butter and pepper, mix well
4. Grease the omelet pan and pour the mixture into it
5. When golden on one side, turn to the other and let cook until golden
6. Fold and serve with meat filling if desired

title: Amoletky zemákové (Potato Omelets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter kilogram (half pound) cooked potatoes
- same amount white flour
- several eggs
- egg white
- salt
- butter
- crackling

## Directions

1. Mix cold grated cooked potatoes with equal amount of white flour, several eggs, egg white, salt and butter, working well.
2. Cut off pieces of dough, roll out like noodles, sprinkle with cracklings, roll up, place side by side on a pan, brush with lard and bake.

title: Amolety kynuté (Fermented Omelets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram flour
- sugar
- 30 grams (2 lots) yeast
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) butter
- salt
- warm milk

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, yeast, butter, salt and as much warm milk as needed for a thick batter.
2. Allow to ferment briefly.
3. Pour onto a greased omelet pan and fry on both sides.
4. They may be rolled up and baked in the oven, or left unrolled, spread with fat and stacked on a plate.

title: Ananasová zmrzlina (Pineapple Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- vyzrálý ananas
- citrónová šťáva
- voda
- cukr

## Directions

1. Vyzrálý ananas rozkrájí se na kousky
2. Rozstrouhá se na struhadle
3. Přimačká se šťávy citrónové
4. Vodou se rozředí
5. Rozvářeným cukrem se osladí
6. Upraví se jako ostatní zmrzliny

title: Ananasový creme (Pineapple Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 deciliters cream
- six egg yolks
- six deka sugar
- a little pineapple juice

## Directions

1. Take 3 deciliters of cream, six egg yolks, six deka sugar and a little pineapple juice and make a very tasty cream

title: Ananasový Paj (Pineapple Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- butter
- egg yolks
- sweet cream
- pineapple
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cream a coffee cup of sugar with half a coffee cup of butter until fluffy
2. Add five beaten egg yolks
3. Add a coffee cup of sweet cream and one grated pineapple
4. Mix well
5. Gently fold in stiffly beaten egg whites from five egg whites
6. Fill two pie pans lined with dough with this mixture and bake
7. This pie is baked without a top crust, so before filling it you must make a thicker rim either from a strip of dough or by folding the edge of the dough

title: Ananasový Punč (Pineapple Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ripe pineapple
- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 1 bottle Burgundy wine
- 1 bottle champagne wine

## Directions

1. Cut a ripe pineapple into small slices
2. Place with 0.5 kilogram of sugar on a plate
3. Pour 1 bottle of Burgundy wine over it
4. Cover with cloth and let sit peacefully for at least an hour
5. Add 1 bottle of champagne wine
6. Stir constantly
7. Pour into long, narrow glasses

title: Ananasový Punč - Another Method (Pineapple Punch - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-2 ripe pineapples
- Sugar
- 1 kilogram sugar
- Wine (white)
- Champagne wine

## Directions

1. Cut 1-2 completely ripe pineapples into slices
2. Heavily sugar them and place on a plate
3. Let them rest peacefully for 24 hours
4. Dissolve 1 kilogram of sugar in wine and pour over
5. Add several bottles of white wine
6. When ready to serve, add 1 bottle of champagne wine
7. Mix everything and serve on the table

title: Ananasový Punč - Another Method III (Pineapple Punch - Another Method III)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kilogram sugar
- 3 liters boiling water
- 1 bottle good Rhine wine
- 1 bottle good rum
- 2 bottles champagne wine
- Juice from pressed pineapple

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1.5 kilograms of sugar in 3 liters of boiling water
2. Add 1 bottle of good Rhine wine and 1 bottle of good rum
3. Heat for an hour on the stove
4. Finally add juice from pressed pineapple
5. The container must be completely covered to prevent the aroma from escaping

title: Andělský dort (Angel Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of flour
- one teaspoon of cream of tartar
- one coffee cup of fine granulated sugar
- eleven egg whites, as fresh and cold as possible
- half teaspoon of vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Mix the cream of tartar with the flour and sift five times
2. sift the sugar five times
3. place the egg whites in a deep bowl and beat with a wire whisk until they stop flowing and stand up when the whisk is lifted, but do not overbeat
4. gradually sprinkle the sifted sugar into the whites while mixing loosely
5. similarly fold in the flour
6. add the vanilla extract, mix, and pour into a clean, completely dry ungreased pan
7. bake in a moderately warm oven for three-quarters of an hour, but do not open the oven during the first quarter hour as the cake will easily collapse
8. invert the pan with the cake onto a plate and let it cool completely in the pan
9. when cooled, remove from the pan without damage
10. coat with sugar icing made by mixing one spoonful of milk with powdered sugar in a cup until it is a thick paste, rubbing briefly, then adding a few drops of vanilla extract and adding more sugar if needed

title: Anglická kaše (English Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams butter
- 4 to 5 tablespoons flour
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- vanilla
- salt
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Melt 250 grams of butter and mix with 4 to 5 tablespoons of flour, brown it, add milk and stir until a thick porridge forms
2. Sprinkle the porridge with sugar, cinnamon and vanilla, salt it and cook until completely thickened
3. Add 6 egg yolks to it, mix everything and cook again for about a quarter hour
4. Remove from heat and place on a plate

title: Anglická slanina s vejci (English Bacon with Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into fine pieces, place on a baking sheet on moderately heated stove and stir constantly.
2. Fried eggs - freshly prepared - are served on thus rendered bacon.

title: Anglické koláčky k čaji (English Tea Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams grated butter
- one kilogram fine flour
- salt
- two beaten eggs
- lukewarm milk
- 36 grams dissolved yeast

## Directions

1. Take 200 grams of grated butter
2. Mix with one kilogram of fine flour
3. Season with salt
4. Beat in two beaten eggs, lukewarm milk, and 36 grams of dissolved yeast
5. Work the dough thoroughly
6. Let rise
7. Roll out
8. Cut small cakes with an inverted glass from the dough
9. Bake until reddish
10. Brush with butter

title: Anglický Punč (English Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon
- 1 kilogram crushed sugar
- juice of 3 lemons
- 1.5 liters boiling water
- rum
- good spirits

## Directions

1. Peel a lemon finely halfway
2. Chop the peel and place it on a baking sheet with 1 kilogram of crushed sugar
3. Extract the juice from 3 lemons and pour it with 1.5 liters of boiling water over the sugar and peel
4. Add rum and good spirits to everything
5. Boil everything together

title: Anglický sýr (English Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- freshly milked milk
- rennet
- salt

## Directions

1. Freshly milked milk maintained at +18 to +20° R. temperature is curdled.
2. The cheese is broken, the whey is poured off; it is heated and poured over the cheese.
3. The cheese is removed, the whey poured off, placed in cloth, pressed. Once pressed, the cheese is left to cool to +12° R., then it is salted, pressed again, and flipped several times.
4. Cheese placed on shelves is turned and flipped daily; in six weeks it develops a firm rind.

title: Another Way to Braise Duck (Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- water
- beef broth
- white wine
- bay leaves
- cloves
- salt
- parsley
- butter
- onion
- flour
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Braise the prepared duck with water and broth, a glass of white wine, 2 bay leaves, cloves, salt and parsley. To the sauce add onion fried in butter and dusted with flour, and lemon juice, then pour over the duck.

title: Anýzové preclíky (Anise Cracknels)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- two small cups flour
- two whole eggs
- half small cup powdered sugar
- two teaspoons ground anise

## Directions

1. Crumble two tablespoons butter with two small cups flour
2. Beat in two whole eggs
3. Add half small cup powdered sugar and two teaspoons ground anise
4. Mix everything well and work into smooth dough
5. Form into small pretzel shapes
6. Place on waxed sheet, brush with beaten egg and bake until golden

title: Apple Mousse with Potato Starch (Jablkové kyselo s moukou zemákovou)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several apples
- 1 1/2 liters water
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- 1/2 liter potato starch flour mixed in cold water
- sugar
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cut several apples into pieces and remove the seeds
2. Cook in 1 1/2 liters of water with cinnamon and lemon zest until completely soft
3. Strain
4. Cook 1 liter of the liquid with 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of sugar
5. Mix in 1/2 liter of potato starch flour that has been mixed in cold water
6. When everything has simmered, turn out onto a plate
7. Let cool in the cellar
8. Serve at table with sugar and sweet cream

title: Apricot Ice Cream (Meruňková zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripest apricots
- Wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Press the ripest apricots through a sieve
2. Cover with wine
3. Sweeten the juice generously

title: Asparagus in Vinegar (Špargel v octě)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized asparagus
- salt
- good vinegar (not too sharp)

## Directions

1. Clean medium-sized asparagus
2. Cut into two-centimeter long pieces
3. Wash
4. Cook in salted water
5. Lay on a sieve to drain
6. Place into jars or stone vessels
7. Pour with good vinegar that is not too sharp
8. After eight days, strain off the vinegar
9. Boil it again
10. Strain it, and when cooled, pour it back over the asparagus
11. Seal the vessel completely and place in a cool location

title: Bachor (Fat Intestine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Omental meat
- Rendered lard
- Raw pork blood
- Pepper
- Ground ginger
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Onion
- Pork lard
- Pork stomach

## Directions

1. Cut omental meat into cubes
2. Add rendered lard
3. Pour in raw pork blood
4. Season with pepper, ground ginger, allspice, cloves, and whole onion fried in pork lard
5. Mix everything together in a bowl
6. Stuff into pork stomach

title: Bachor (Fatty Intestine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- rendered lard
- raw pork blood
- pepper
- ground ginger
- new spice
- flower
- onion
- pork stomach
- veal broth

## Directions

1. Cut veal meat into cubes
2. Add rendered lard and raw pork blood to the meat
3. Season with pepper, ground ginger, new spice, and flower
4. Add whole onion browned in pork lard
5. Mix everything together in a bowl
6. Clean the pork stomach well
7. Stuff the stomach with the mixture
8. Cook in veal broth for half an hour
9. Pierce with a thin stick - if no blood runs out, it is done
10. While still warm, put it in a press and it will compress more as it cools
11. Serve plain or with oil and vinegar

title: Bachor (Pressed Blood Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork spleen and ears, cut into cubes
- rendered lard with cracklings
- raw pork blood
- pepper
- ginger
- clove
- allspice
- onion, fried in lard
- pork stomach

## Directions

1. Cut pork spleen and ears into cubes
2. Add rendered lard with cracklings
3. Pour in raw pork blood
4. Season with pepper, ginger, clove, and allspice
5. Add onion fried in lard
6. Mix everything well
7. Fill the cleaned pork stomach with this mixture
8. Cook it in spleen soup for half an hour
9. It is done when pierced with a thin stick and no blood runs out
10. While hot, place it in a press and compress it further as it cools
11. For an especially good version, add cooked beef or pork tongue cut into larger pieces
12. Cut it cold into slices
13. Arrange on a plate and serve
14. You may also sprinkle it with chopped onion and pour vinegar over it, or serve only with vinegar and pepper

title: Baked Mashed Potatoes (Bramborová kaše pečená)
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed potatoes
- butter
- grated roll

## Directions

1. Prepare the mash according to the previous method
2. Butter a mold and place the mash in it
3. Place pieces of fresh butter on top
4. Sprinkle with grated roll and bake in the oven until golden

title: Barevná poleva (Colored Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- white frosting base
- saffron or cochineal or spinach

## Directions

1. To make yellow frosting, prepare it as white frosting and add several drops of saffron dissolved in a little water
2. For pink frosting, use purified cochineal
3. For green frosting, color with juice from blanched and pounded spinach

title: Barevný rosol (Colored Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- aspic
- spices
- roasted meat
- liver
- burned sugar
- beet juice

## Directions

1. For light aspic, do not add spices or sugar
2. For dark aspic, cook spices, roasted meat and liver with more or less burned sugar into the liquid
3. For red aspic, add beet juice to pale aspic or dark aspic as needed for color

title: Barley Soup
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram beef
- vegetables
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook 1 kilogram of beef with all vegetables and salt

title: Bavlněná polévka (Velvet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- one cup of cream
- six teaspoons of fine flour
- two quarts of boiling beef broth

## Directions

1. Beat six egg yolks in a cup with one cup of cream and six teaspoons of fine flour
2. Pour this with constant stirring into two quarts of boiling beef broth
3. You can season it as you like
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Bavorské hůlky (Bavarian Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- three small cups flour
- two and a half teaspoons baking powder
- one and a half teaspoons cinnamon
- one and a half teaspoons cloves
- one and a half teaspoons nutmeg
- one small cup white sugar
- one small cup yellow sugar (or two small cups white if yellow unavailable)
- two tablespoons syrup
- two tablespoons brandy
- three pieces chocolate from half pound
- four eggs
- two more small cups flour for kneading
- quarter pound unpeeled whole almonds

## Directions

1. Put three small cups flour in a bowl
2. Add two and a half teaspoons baking powder, one and a half teaspoons cinnamon, one and a half teaspoons cloves, one and a half teaspoons nutmeg, one small cup white sugar, and one small cup yellow sugar (or two small cups white if yellow is unavailable)
3. Mix everything well with the flour
4. Add two tablespoons syrup, two tablespoons brandy, three pieces of chocolate, and four eggs
5. Mix well with the flour
6. Pour dough onto a work surface dusted with two more small cups flour and work thoroughly
7. Take a piece, form into a ball and bake in the oven to test: if it rises upward, it is good
8. If it spreads out, add more flour and roll again
9. Work dough on the work surface, add quarter pound unpeeled whole almonds, and mix again
10. Form dough into long rolls the length of the baking sheet and as thick as an egg, or slightly thinner
11. Place them beside each other, half inch apart
12. Brush with egg white and bake in a not-too-hot oven
13. Must bake for over half an hour, then a piece can be tested
14. If inside it is like coarse grain, it must bake longer with the oven open so it becomes like rye bread when cut
15. Turn out and let cool five minutes, then separate one from another and cut into finger-thick pieces

title: Bavorské zelí (Bavarian Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- vypraté zelí (blanched cabbage)
- sádlo nebo tučná šunka a uzenina (lard or fatty ham and sausage)
- sůl (salt)
- voda (water)
- cibule usmaženou (fried onion)

## Directions

1. Dá se vrstva vypraného zelí na pekáček, přidá se dosti sádla neb tučné šunky a uzeniny, vše se osolí
2. Vodou zaleje, s cibulí usmaženou smíchá a do měkká svaří

title: Bači Kobližky (Crayfish Fritters)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of crayfish butter
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) of crayfish tails
- 6 eggs
- 2 soaked rolls
- a little flour
- salt
- butter dough

## Directions

1. Rub together a piece of crayfish butter with 12 decagrams (8 lots) of chopped and beaten crayfish tails.
2. Mix well with 6 eggs.
3. Add 2 soaked rolls, a little flour and salt.
4. Grease the butter dough with egg, place spoonfuls of the mixture on the dough and wrap each one individually.
5. Fry and sprinkle with sugar or with fried green parsley.
6. Typically served at larger lunches after beef.

title: Bači Nádivka (Crayfish Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca bread crumbs
- 2 egg yolks
- crayfish butter
- salt
- parsley
- crayfish meat
- egg white
- asparagus
- beans

## Directions

1. Mix 8 deca bread crumbs with 2 egg yolks
2. Add crayfish butter and season with salt
3. Add chopped parsley and finely chopped crayfish meat
4. Fold in beaten egg white
5. Add cooked asparagus cut into tiny pieces and beans
6. Use for stuffing chickens and similar roasts

title: Bažant (Pheasant)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- salt
- lard for larding
- pepper
- bay leaf
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the pheasant, wash thoroughly, and salt.
2. Lard with bacon, especially on the breast from both sides.
3. Truss like ordinary poultry and place on a baking dish.
4. Put several peppercorns, a bay leaf, a piece of lemon peel, and a piece of chopped bacon underneath.
5. Roast until done, cut into pieces, and arrange neatly on a platter.

title: Beans in Vinegar (Boby (fazole) v octě)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young green bean pods
- potash (a knife tip per liter of water)
- onion
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- vinegar (boiled, diluted with water, cooled)
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut off young green bean pods
2. Wash and cook in strong salted water to which potash is added (a knife tip per liter) so the pods retain their greenness
3. Allow the cooked pods to cool
4. Drain the vinegar from them
5. On the second day add onion, bay leaf, and peppercorns among the beans
6. Layer the beans with these additions into jars
7. Pour over with cooled vinegar prepared as described above and boiled
8. Cover the top with fat
9. Tie up so air cannot enter inside

title: Beef Broth (Hovězí Polévka)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh, not overly fatty beef meat (1/2 kilogram per liter)
- About half the amount in veal meat
- Several chopped veal and beef bones
- Salt
- Vegetable roots

## Directions

1. Do not wash the meat, only wipe it clean with a cloth
2. Place the meat and bones in cold water
3. Bring to a gentle boil over mild heat
4. Remove the foam, as the broth will not be clear otherwise
5. Once the foam has been removed, salt the broth
6. Add vegetable roots
7. Simmer gently for several hours until the broth is strong
8. Skim off the fat
9. Strain the broth through a sieve

title: Beef Soup with Liver Dumplings
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram calf's liver
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 roll, grated
- 3 cloves garlic, peeled and salted
- marjoram
- dill
- pepper

## Directions

1. Grate the calf's liver and remove the sinews
2. Mix with eggs, egg yolks, grated roll, salted garlic cloves, marjoram, dill, and pepper, and blend everything together
3. Drop the dumplings into boiling beef soup using a coffee spoon
4. Cook until done
5. Add a piece of chives
6. Serve at table

title: Beefsteak (Beefsteak)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from sirloin roast
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Remove all membranes and sinews from meat
2. Cut into pieces about three fingers thick and flatten with a mallet
3. Salt and pepper the meat
4. Chop a little onion and fry until golden
5. Place beefsteak in it; if English style, leave for 5 minutes, if well-done, for a quarter hour

title: Beefsteak Dušený (Stewed Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds tender meat, cut into small pieces
- 1 or 2 onions, sliced
- Salt and pepper
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small pieces and place in a pan.
2. Season with pepper and salt.
3. Slice the onions and add to the meat.
4. Cover with water, cover with a lid, and let simmer slowly until the meat is tender.
5. Mix 2 tablespoons butter with 2 tablespoons flour and add to the meat.
6. Let boil for a moment until the gravy is smooth.

title: Beefsteak na Kyselo (Steak with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beefsteak from the round
- 1 tablespoon lard
- 1 large onion, halved
- Salt
- Boiling water
- 1 cup sour cream
- Flour
- 1 tablespoon ketchup
- Vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove all skin and fat from the steak and pound it well.
2. Heat a tablespoon of lard in a pan and brown the steak on both sides on a good high heat.
3. Pour boiling water over it, enough to cover the meat, season with salt, add the halved onion, cover, and let simmer gently.
4. If from young meat, it will be tender in an hour.
5. Remove the meat to a dish and strain the liquid.
6. If too much liquid has boiled away, add boiling water to the desired amount of gravy; for 1½ pounds of meat, aim for about one cup of liquid.
7. Add one cup of good sour cream to the gravy.
8. Thicken slightly with flour, stirring well to avoid lumps.
9. Add one tablespoon of ketchup, then taste and adjust for salt or sourness, adding vinegar if needed.
10. Cut the meat into small pieces and return to the gravy to simmer briefly.

title: Beefsteak na Rošti (Broiled Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beefsteak slices, about an inch thick
- Salt and pepper
- Fresh butter
- Fat or lard for greasing (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the steak slices slightly thicker than usual, almost an inch thick, and pound as for frying.
2. Place in wide, flat wire tongs (broiler) without any other preparation and hold one side over hot coals.
3. When browned on one side, turn the tongs over and cook the other side.
4. When sufficiently cooked, remove to a heated dish, salt and pepper it, and place pieces of fresh butter on top, which will melt on its own.
5. Serve hot.
6. If tongs are not available, you may broil on a griddle, though the flavor will not be quite as good as broiling over coals.
7. Heat the griddle dry over high heat until smoking, then rub with a piece of fat so the steak does not stick.
8. Place the steak slice on it; when cooked on one side, turn it over.
9. When cooked on both sides, salt, pepper, butter it with fresh butter, and serve hot.

title: Beefsteak Pečený (Baked Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 steak slice, pounded or chopped well
- Butter (if the meat is not sufficiently fat or marbled)
- Salt and pepper
- Grated white bread
- A little water

## Directions

1. Place the well-pounded or chopped steak slice on a griddle.
2. If not fatty enough or well marbled, place a piece of butter on it.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Sprinkle with grated white bread.
5. Add a little water so it does not stick to the griddle.
6. Bake about twenty minutes, or longer if you prefer it well done.

title: Beefsteak Smažený (Fried Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beef steak
- 2 tablespoons butter or rendered fat from the meat
- Salt and pepper
- ½ onion, finely chopped
- A few tablespoons of boiling water

## Directions

1. Pound the meat well. If it is not a porterhouse cut, pound it thoroughly to tenderize it.
2. Do not wash the meat; if desired, wipe it with a clean dry cloth.
3. Heat the butter or fat in a pan until very hot.
4. Place the steak in the hot fat, season with salt and pepper on top, and fry on high heat without covering for about two minutes until it browns.
5. The fat must be hot so the meat does not release its juices and becomes tender.
6. Turn the steak and add the finely chopped onion, season the other side with salt and pepper.
7. When the second side is browned, remove the steak.
8. Pour a few tablespoons of boiling water into the pan to make a gravy, then pour this over the steak or serve it separately in a dish.

title: Beet Ice Cream (Revízová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Very ripe beets
- Finely crushed sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Push very ripe beets through a fine sieve
2. Mix with finely crushed sugar
3. Dilute slightly with water
4. Proceed as with simple ice cream

title: Besedové Mandle (Reseda Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- blanched almonds
- sugar
- reseda juice

## Directions

1. In a container on hot stove, coat blanched almonds with boiled sugar to which reseda juice has been added, as described for other almond preparations

title: Bezinkový rosol (Elderberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Elderberries
- Water
- Apples
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Take elderberries, remove stems, wash them and put them to cook with a little water.
2. At the same time, put apples to cook.
3. When the elderberries burst and the water is darkly colored, strain the water from both the elderberries and apples.
4. To three parts apple juice add one part elderberry water.
5. Take one part (or slightly more) sugar and two parts of this mixture and cook in a double boiler.
6. When it thickens with constant stirring, store in a cool place.

title: Biskety Sodové (Soda Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 tablespoon lard
- salt to taste
- 1 1/2 small cups sour milk or buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl, add lard and salt and mix well
2. Moisten 1 teaspoon baking soda with vinegar, add to the sour milk or buttermilk and mix with the prepared flour
3. Dust the rolling pin with flour, turn the dough onto it and roll smooth, but not too stiff
4. Grease a baking sheet, cut rounds with a cutter, place on the sheet and bake in a hot oven

title: Biskety Sodové Jiným Způsobem (Soda Biscuits, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups sifted flour
- 1 tablespoon hard lard
- a little salt
- 2 cups sour milk
- 1 tablespoon baking soda
- 1 tablespoon hot water

## Directions

1. Put 4 cups of sifted flour into a bowl, add 1 tablespoon of hard lard and a little salt and mix everything well with your hands with the flour.
2. Take 2 cups of sour milk; in a small cup dissolve 1 tablespoon of baking soda in 1 tablespoon of hot water, pour it into the sour milk and mix well.
3. Pour the milk into the flour while constantly stirring and work the dough well.
4. If necessary, add flour so that the dough is nicely firm.
5. Roll out the dough, cut round biscuits with a cutter, place them on a baking sheet greased with lard and put into a hot oven.
6. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes and serve hot.

title: Biskety Sodové, Alternative Method (Soda Biscuits - Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 small cups sifted flour
- 1 tablespoon hard lard
- salt to taste
- 2 small cups sour milk
- 1 tablespoon baking soda
- 1 tablespoon hot water

## Directions

1. Put the sifted flour in a bowl, add hard lard and salt and mix thoroughly with hands
2. Take 2 small cups sour milk; dissolve 1 tablespoon baking soda in 1 tablespoon hot water, pour it into the sour milk and mix with the prepared flour

title: Biskupský chlebíček (Bishops Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 cup whole unpeeled almonds
- 1 cup sultana raisins
- butter
- flour for dusting the pan

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs with 1 cup sugar using a wire whisk for half an hour until very thick
2. Mix in 1 cup flour into which you have mixed 1 teaspoon baking powder
3. Add 1 cup whole unpeeled almonds
4. Finally add 1 cup sultana raisins coated in flour
5. Mix everything well
6. Butter an oblong mold and dust with flour
7. Pour batter into the mold and bake slowly for at least half an hour
8. Let cool completely
9. When completely cold, slice into pieces

title: Bisque (Crayfish Soup à la Française)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 40-50 small crayfish
- salted water
- crayfish intestines
- 180 grams fresh butter
- 2 hard rolls
- 2 hard sardines
- 3-4 liters excellent meat soup
- 6 finely chopped mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- chopped onion
- 2 cloves
- crayfish tails
- fried roll

## Directions

1. Remove intestines from 40-50 small crayfish, cook in salted water, remove meat from tails
2. Crush bodies and shells with 180 grams of fresh butter, 2 hard rolls and 2 hard sardines
3. Place on a baking sheet, pour with 3-4 liters of excellent meat soup to which 6 finely chopped mushrooms have been added
4. Salt and pepper, add chopped onion and 2 cloves, cook the soup for 2 hours
5. Strain, add crayfish tails to it and pour over fried roll

title: Bisque de Grenouilles (Bisque of Frogs)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty well-cleaned frog legs, chopped fine
- one small onion, finely chopped
- a piece of green parsley
- a little crushed thyme
- a piece of butter
- salt
- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- two quarts lean broth or parsley water
- a little pale butter roux
- crushed thyme
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Chop twenty well-cleaned frog legs finely, place them in a pan, add a small finely chopped onion, a piece of green parsley, a little crushed thyme and a piece of butter, salt it and let it stew for about a quarter hour
2. Pound it then in a mortar with four hard-boiled egg yolks, put it in a pot, add two quarts of lean broth or parsley water and let it boil for a quarter hour
3. Strain it, brown it with a little pale butter roux, let it boil a little longer, add a little more crushed thyme and pour into a dish over fried bread. You can also add fish dumplings to it
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Bisque z Koroptví (Bisque of Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- two roasted partridges
- two dozen large chestnuts
- one quart beef broth for chestnuts
- one quart beef broth for bread
- one pound bread
- two glasses port wine
- two ounces butter

## Directions

1. Roast two partridges and remove all meat from them
2. Roast and peel two dozen large chestnuts and let them cook in one quart of beef broth
3. In another quart of broth, let one pound of bread cook
4. Finely chop the partridge meat and pound it in a mortar, add the chestnuts and pound again
5. Finally add the bread and pound for several more minutes, press through a fine sieve
6. Pour in the broth and place in a pot with two glasses of port wine
7. Mix it well over the fire, but it must not boil anymore
8. Season to taste, add two ounces of butter, mix well and pour into a serving dish with fried bread cubes
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Bisque z ústřic (Bisque of Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts oysters
- white pepper
- nutmeg
- 2 pieces whole flowers
- bay leaf
- paprika
- 2 ounces butter
- 1 quart beef broth
- 3 pints cooked milk
- 1 pint raw cream
- 1/4 pound butter in small pieces
- fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Put 2 quarts of oysters into a pot with a pinch of white pepper, a pinch of nutmeg, 2 pieces of whole flowers, a bay leaf, a pinch of paprika, 2 ounces butter, and 1 quart beef broth
2. Let boil for 10 minutes, then strain through a sieve
3. Make a light butter roux and strain the broth into it
4. Add 3 pints of cooked milk
5. Chop the oysters finely and add to the soup
6. While stirring constantly, let boil for another 10 minutes
7. Push through a fine sieve
8. Finally stir in 1 pint of raw cream and 1/4 pound butter in small pieces
9. Pour into a tureen and serve with fried bread cubes on a separate plate
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Bitter Orange Ice Cream (Zmrzlina z hořkých pomerančů)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Six bitter oranges
- Sugar
- Juice of several lemons
- Water

## Directions

1. Rub six bitter oranges by their skin against sugar
2. Grate the skin
3. Press juice from the oranges and several lemons
4. Strain the juice
5. Dilute slightly with water
6. Mix and sweeten heavily
7. Proceed as with simple ice cream

title: Bochánky (Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart buttermilk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon baking soda dissolved in 1 tablespoon hot water
- salt
- flour
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Add egg yolks to buttermilk
2. Dissolve baking soda in hot water and add
3. Salt the mixture
4. Add flour until dough is thicker than for pancakes
5. Fold in beaten egg white
6. Pour batter into greased molds or rings on a baking pan
7. Bake in a hot oven for 20 minutes

title: Bochánky Smetanové (Cream Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sweet sour cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon butter
- salt
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Place sour cream in a bowl
2. Mix in egg yolks
3. Add butter and salt
4. Mix baking powder into flour
5. Add flour to make dough thicker than for pancakes
6. Bake according to the method for regular muffins

title: Bohemian Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- dough prepared by method previously mentioned
- filling of choice, prepared
- butter for greasing
- egg, beaten
- sliced almonds or butter mixed with sugar and flour

## Directions

1. Prepare dough by the method previously mentioned, work it until it no longer sticks to the spoon, let it rise, then roll it out as thin as possible on a flour-dusted surface
2. Cut small square pieces from the dough
3. Place a piece of prepared filling in the center of each piece
4. Fold opposite corners of the dough together on both sides
5. Place the tarts on a buttered baking sheet
6. Brush them with beaten egg
7. Sprinkle either with chopped almonds or with butter mixed with sugar and flour
8. Let them rise a little more
9. Bake in a well-heated oven and sprinkle with sugar after baking

title: Borůvková omáčka (Bilberry Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bilberries
- 90 grams (6 lots) sugar
- a little cinnamon
- lemon zest
- potato flour mixed in wine

## Directions

1. Sort through the bilberries, wash them, and mash them in a glazed baking dish with a mashing tool
2. Add sugar, a little cinnamon and lemon zest
3. Strain everything, add potato flour mixed in wine
4. Boil once more

title: Borůvková omáčka (Blueberry Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter blueberries
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) sugar
- a little cinnamon
- lemon peel
- potato flour
- wine

## Directions

1. Sift and wash the blueberries
2. Mash them in an enameled dish with a pestle
3. Add sugar, a little cinnamon, and lemon peel
4. Strain everything
5. Add potato flour mixed in wine
6. Boil once more

title: Borůvková omáčka ze sušených borůvek (Blueberry Sauce from Dried Blueberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter dried blueberries
- 1/4 liter water
- 195 grams sugar (13 lots)
- 3/4 liter red wine
- lemon peel
- potato flour

## Directions

1. Boil the dried blueberries in water
2. Add sugar
3. Pour in red wine
4. Mix in lemon peel
5. Strain the sauce
6. Mix with a small teaspoon of potato flour

title: Borůvkové lívance (Bilberry Fried Pancakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter milk
- 6 eggs
- salt
- flour
- half liter bilberries (blueberries)
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix 6 eggs into half a liter of milk with a little salt and enough flour to make a thick batter.
2. Pour half the batter onto a greased pan, and when it begins to firm, sprinkle half a liter of cleaned bilberries, which should be sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon and covered with the remaining batter.
3. Fry on both sides and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar.

title: Borůvkové lívance (Bilberry Fried Pancakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter milk
- 6 eggs
- salt
- flour
- half liter bilberries
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 6 eggs into half a liter of milk with a little salt and enough flour to form a thick batter.
2. Pour half the batter onto a greased pan, and when it begins to set, sprinkle with half a liter of cleaned bilberries dusted with sugar and cinnamon, cover with remaining batter.
3. Fry on both sides and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar.

title: Bostonský Hnědý Chléb (Boston Brown Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups cornmeal
- one cup rye flour
- one half cup syrup
- one heaping teaspoon baking soda dissolved in hot water
- three cups sour milk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put two cups of cornmeal and one cup of rye flour in a bowl
2. Add one half cup of syrup
3. Dissolve one heaping teaspoon of baking soda in hot water and mix it into three cups of sour milk
4. Add this mixture to the flour and season with salt, mixing well
5. Pour into a greased deep mold, place in a steamer and steam for 4 hours
6. Then place in an oven for 20 minutes to dry it slightly
7. Remove and turn out onto a plate

title: Bouillabaisse
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- eel
- perch
- live sea crayfish
- butter
- onion
- garlic
- bay leaf
- oysters
- salt
- pepper
- lemon
- tomatoes
- saffron
- thyme
- white wine
- water
- parsley
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Take one pike, one eel, two perch and one live sea crayfish
2. Clean the fish well, split them, remove the bones and cut the meat into pieces about half an inch thick
3. Remove the meat from the tail, body and claws of the crayfish and also cut into slices
4. Put two ounces of butter, finely sliced onion, two cloves of grated garlic and a bay leaf into a large pan and let it fry briefly
5. Add the cut fish, crayfish and a dozen large raw oysters, salt, pepper, add lemon cut into slices, three peeled and sliced tomatoes, half a teaspoon of ground saffron, a little thyme, a pint of white wine and enough water to cover the fish
6. Let it cook for about half an hour, then add a tablespoon of chopped fresh parsley and let it cook another minute
7. Remove the fish, crayfish and oysters and put them into a soup bowl
8. Strain the broth through a very fine sieve, let it come to a boil once more, pour it over the fish and serve
9. On a separate plate serve fried bread roll cut into cubes (Serves 8-10 people)
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Bouillon (Strong Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds beef
- one pound veal
- half old hen or old pigeon
- one celery root
- two parsley roots
- one carrot
- one leek
- one onion
- slice of garlic
- half cabbage
- piece of ginger
- one bay leaf
- four quarts cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean everything well.
2. Put beef and veal in a pot with four quarts cold water, let simmer slowly and skim the foam.
3. When the soup is skimmed of foam, salt it, add vegetables and spices and let cook for two hours.
4. Then remove the veal, add the hen or pigeon, and let cook for another three hours.
5. Carefully skim the fat and finally strain the soup through a damp cloth or fine sieve.
6. Serves 12 persons.
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Boží Milosti (Celestine Crusts)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- three teaspoons sugar
- one tablespoon butter
- one quarter coffeepot sweet milk
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat together the eggs, sugar, butter, and sweet milk, seasoning with salt
2. Add flour to make a dough not as stiff as noodle dough
3. Knead well, roll out thin
4. Cut into four-sided pieces and prick with a fork
5. Fry in lard until pink-golden in color

title: Boží Milosti na Jiný Způsob (Celestine Crusts Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffeepot flour
- one coffeepot sweet cream
- two egg yolks
- one whole egg
- salt
- one teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. On a board, combine flour, sweet cream, egg yolks, whole egg, salt, and sugar
2. Mix together and knead the dough like noodle dough
3. Roll out and cut into pieces, then prick with a fork
4. Fry on hot lard and sprinkle with sugar

title: Braid from Potato Flour (Pletenice z mouky zemákové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 milliliters milk
- 180 grams (12 lots) butter
- 250 milliliters potato flour
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Cook butter in milk
2. Add potato flour while stirring constantly over heat
3. Remove from heat and stir until completely cool
4. Mix in egg yolks, salt, and sugar
5. Roll dough into thin strips
6. Cross them over each other
7. Press in the middle
8. Brush with egg white
9. Bake on a baking sheet
10. Sprinkle with sugar

title: Brain Soufflé (Nákyp z Mozku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- four brains
- 8 egg yolks
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- flower water
- salt
- onion
- 2 grated rolls
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Remove membranes and blood from four brains
2. Beat together with the brains: 8 egg yolks, 9 dekagrams butter, a little flower water, salt, chopped onion, and 2 grated rolls
3. Make stiff snow from 8 egg whites and fold in
4. Cook either just in steam or bake in the oven

title: Braised Beef (Dušené hovězí maso)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef meat
- beef fat
- onion
- assorted chopped vegetables
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- beef soup

## Directions

1. Trim all fat from the meat before cooking it for soup
2. Cook the meat
3. Cut the fat into cubes
4. Add onion and all chopped vegetables
5. Salt and add three grains of pepper
6. Braise until brown, being careful not to scorch it by regularly basting with beef soup
7. Mix in 3 spoonfuls of flour
8. Pour more soup over it and simmer

title: Braised Duck (Dušená kachna)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- butter
- beef broth
- wine
- onion
- bay leaf
- celery
- cloves
- pepper
- sardines

## Directions

1. Braise the duck in butter on both sides with beef broth, a glass of wine, onion, bay leaf, cut onion, celery, several cloves, pepper and two sardines. Baste frequently. Remove fat from the sauce, strain and add brown roux.

title: Braised Goose Liver (Dušená játra husí)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- milk
- salt
- butter
- truffle sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the liver in milk, cut it into pieces, salt and place in butter on a baking sheet. Roast on both sides over heat and serve with truffle sauce.

title: Bramborová buchta (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- eight yolks
- grated lemon zest
- salt
- 1 small cup sugar
- three grated cooked potatoes of medium size
- stiff snow from eight whites

## Directions

1. Beat 4 tablespoons butter until frothed
2. Add to it eight yolks, grated lemon zest, a little salt, 1 small cup sugar, three grated cooked potatoes of medium size and stiff snow from eight whites
3. Bake it at moderate heat for about an hour

title: Bramborová omáčka (Potato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter or lard
- 1/2 onion
- 3 large boiled potatoes
- beef broth
- wine vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Stew half a chopped onion in a piece of butter or lard.
2. Add three large boiled and grated potatoes.
3. Add a little beef broth and a little wine vinegar, salt.
4. Let it simmer for half an hour, then strain the sauce.

title: Bramborová šifonáda (Potato Chiffonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cooled potatoes
- Red beets
- Celery, or various salads (chicken, lobster, etc.)
- Thick mayonnaise
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cut cooked cooled potatoes into thin slices
2. Cut rounds from these slices with a mold about one and a half inches in diameter
3. Cut rounds of the same size from red beets
4. Arrange potatoes and beets alternately around the edge of a flat dish so individual rounds slightly overlap
5. Fill the middle of the dish with either diced celery, celery stalks, or various salads
6. Cover the middle with thick mayonnaise
7. Garnish with chopped parsley
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Bramborové knedlíky na jiný způsob (Potato Dumplings - Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- asi šest brambor prostřední velikosti
- dvě vejce
- sůl
- koflík mouky
- rozpálené máslo
- osmaženou cibulkou

## Directions

1. Dej vařit asi šest brambor prostřední velikosti.
2. Když jsou vařené, oloupej je a za horka na vale válečkem rozválej.
3. Shrň je na hromádku, přidej k nim dvě vejce, trochu soli a koflík mouky.
4. Dobře brambory s moukou smíchej a vyválej, přidej ještě trochu mouky, aby to bylo tužší a nerozvařilo se.
5. Rozkrájej těsto na 6 dílů, rukama zakulač a dej vařit do vařící slané vody s dobrým přikrytím.
6. Když se chvíli vaří, jimi zamíchej, aby se od dna vyzvedly.
7. Když jsou vařené, rozkroj na půle, polej rozpáleným máslem a osmaženom cibulkou.

title: Bramborové Nudle (Potato Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- flour
- eggs
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook as many potatoes as will fit on a plate, peel them and while hot mash them on a board with a rolling pin
2. Salt them, add two large spoonfuls of flour and three whole eggs, knead into a dough, then roll out
3. Cut from this noodles about a finger's thickness
4. Grease a shallow pan or baking dish with clarified butter, arrange the noodles in it and bake on both sides until golden
5. These noodles can also be cooked in salted water, sprinkled with grated cheese, and greased with hot lard or butter

title: Bramborové Placky (Potato Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- eggs
- flour
- salt
- lard
- fruit preserves

## Directions

1. Cook well-washed potatoes as many as will fit on a plate, peel them and let them cool completely
2. Grate them on a grater, add two whole eggs, two cupfuls of white flour, salt and knead well
3. Make pancakes about as thick as a straw, bake them on a pan greased with lard on both sides until golden
4. Brush them with hot lard, spread with fruit preserves and serve

title: Bramborové Škubánky (Potato Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled potatoes
- salt
- flour
- butter
- poppy seeds

## Directions

1. Boil about a quart of peeled potatoes, adding about half a spoonful of salt
2. When they are almost done, sprinkle an incomplete cupful of white flour on them and let them boil for about five minutes more
3. The potatoes and flour will finish cooking at the same time
4. Drain the water, salt the cooked potatoes to taste, add a piece of butter and mash everything well with a stick so that no piece of potato or lump of flour remains
5. Cut the mush with a spoon into pieces, place on a plate and sprinkle with poppy seeds and hot melted butter

title: Bramborové Škubánky na Jiný Způsob (Potato Mush, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- flour
- butter or lard
- salt
- poppy seeds
- sugar
- cheese
- milk

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape nice potatoes, cut them into larger pieces, place in cold water, wash them well and cook
2. When they are soft, drain half the water, using an inverted masher poke dense holes from top to bottom
3. Pour two cupfuls of white flour into these holes; poke a bit more with the masher and let it cook, but be careful it does not burn
4. After cooking for about a quarter hour, salt it and mix well with the masher so there are no lumps and add a spoonful of butter
5. Dip a spoon in hot melted butter or lard, take the mush piece by piece and place it on a plate
6. Sprinkle with poppy seeds crushed with sugar or grated cheese and grease with hot melted butter or lard
7. If you want the mush finer, drain all the water as soon as the potatoes are soft, replace it with milk, but be careful it does not burn

title: Bramborový Nákyp (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six medium potatoes
- one cup milk
- one tablespoon butter
- two egg yolks
- two egg whites
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel five or six medium potatoes and boil them.
2. When cooked, drain the water and push the potatoes through a sieve into a bowl.
3. Meanwhile, heat one cup of milk on the stove and add one tablespoon butter to it.
4. To the mashed potatoes add two egg yolks, salt and pepper, the hot milk with butter, and stiff snow beaten from two egg whites, and mix everything together.
5. Grease a mold with butter, pour the mixture into it, and bake in a hot oven.
6. Bake the pudding for about twenty minutes and serve immediately to the table.
7. If the pudding stands after baking, it will fall.

title: Bramborový salát (Potato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- rendered bacon
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Peel boiled potatoes and let them cool
2. Slice them into slices, add finely chopped onion, salt and pepper, mix well
3. Pour rendered bacon over them and then vinegar mixed with half water into which you have mixed a little sugar to give the salad a better taste
4. Mix with a knife and arrange hard-boiled eggs cut into slices on top

title: Bramborový salát na jiný způsob (Potato Salad Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled peeled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- onion, finely chopped
- parsley, chopped
- oil or rendered butter
- vinegar
- sour apples, peeled

## Directions

1. Let boiled peeled potatoes cool, slice them thinly, put on a dish, salt and pepper
2. Sprinkle with finely chopped onion and a bit of chopped parsley if desired
3. Pour oil or rendered butter and vinegar over them and mix gently so the potatoes do not break
4. You may also add peeled sour apples cut into slices

title: Bramborový salát se slatiečkem (Potato Salad with Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- two milk herrings
- salt
- boiled potatoes
- sour apples
- onion
- pepper
- herring roe

## Directions

1. Rinse two milk herrings well, skin them, halve them, remove the bones and cut the herrings into pieces
2. Mix potatoes sliced into rounds with sour apples and onion cut into cubes on a dish, salt and pepper
3. Arrange herring pieces on the surface
4. Mix the herring roe with vinegar and pour this over the salad

title: Bramborový salát se smetanou (Potato Salad with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized boiled potatoes
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- sour cream
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel medium-sized boiled warm potatoes and slice them into not too thin slices
2. Place on a dish and mix in finely chopped onion, salt and pepper then pour the following sauce over: Boil 3 spoonfuls of vinegar, a spoonful of water and a spoonful of sugar
3. Take 2 cups of good thick sour cream, beat three egg yolks into it and pour into the boiling vinegar
4. When it begins to rise, remove from heat and when cooled, pour this over the salad

title: Brambory na loupačku (Boiled Potatoes in Skins)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes clean, place them in a pot, pour cold water over them, salt them slightly and cook until soft
2. Pour off all the water, cover the pot firmly with a clean cloth and let them stand in steam for a while
3. Place them unpeeled on the table and serve with fresh butter and salt on the side

title: Brambory s Marjánkovou Omáčkou (Potatoes with Marjoram Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter or lard
- flour
- garlic cloves
- butter
- marjoram leaves
- beef or mutton broth
- cooked potatoes, sliced
- salt
- white grated ginger
- spices

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of fresh butter or lard in a pot, add two spoonfuls of flour
2. Add a clove of garlic with a piece of butter mashed together and make a brown roux
3. Add a little picked marjoram leaves
4. Pour in beef or mutton broth (the latter gives this gravy a special flavor)
5. Mix well so that a thickened gravy forms
6. Add cooked sliced potatoes to it, salt it, season with a little white grated ginger and spice
7. Let it simmer a while longer

title: Brambory s Mlékem (Potatoes with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- lard
- fresh milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice raw potatoes into the thinnest slices possible
2. Put them in a pot and add about a spoonful of lard, stirring all the while
3. Then add about a cupful of fresh milk, and eat it hot
4. This is a tasty side dish

title: Brambory vařené (Boiled Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and soak them in cold water for an hour
2. Then boil them in boiling water and cook slowly until soft, as tested with a fork
3. Drain the water carefully
4. Remove the lid from the pot, salt the potatoes and shake them so that cold air envelops them from all sides
5. Cover the pot with a lid and let it stand in a cooler place on the stove for about 5 minutes and they are ready to serve

title: Brnkev (Kohlrabi)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi leaves
- salt
- kohlrabi bulbs
- butter or beef fat
- sugar
- flower
- beef broth
- caramelized sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the finest, middle leaves in salted water.
2. Peel the kohlrabi bulbs, slice into discs, place on a baking dish with fresh butter or beef fat, a piece of sugar, flower and salt, and braise.
3. When browned, add beef broth and braise until completely soft.
4. Add pale roux with a piece of caramelized sugar, mix in the chopped cooked green leaves, and usually serve around beef.
5. If to be a standalone dish, serve with fried brain, fried calves' feet, cutlets, or any fried foods.

title: Broskve (Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard but ripe peaches
- 4 pounds of sugar for 8 pounds of fruit
- 2 ounces of whole cinnamon
- 1 quart of vinegar
- cloves

## Directions

1. For this purpose firm, although ripe peaches that adhere to the pit are best suited.
2. Wipe them well with cloth until clean.
3. For 8 pounds of fruit take 4 pounds of sugar, 2 ounces of whole cinnamon and 1 quart of vinegar and let boil for five minutes.
4. Insert two cloves into each peach and boil them in this liquid and when they are soft enough to be easily pierced with a fork, remove them, place into jars, boil the liquid down to half and pour over them, then boil further according to the general rules of preserving.

title: Broskvová povidla (Peach Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sugar, one and a half small cups per half gallon of peaches
- clove
- lemon zest
- nutmeg flower

## Directions

1. Pick peaches, peel the skin finely, and remove the pits, then place in a glazed pot that can be filled with them.
2. Cover so steam cannot escape and place in a hot oven, letting them stew until soft.
3. Mash well with a wooden spoon.
4. For every half gallon of peaches, add one and a half small cups of sugar.
5. Mix well, cover with a lid, and return to the oven, stewing and stirring occasionally, keeping them there until they turn a nice brown color.
6. Place in preserving bottles or stone jars, cover well, and set in a cool place.
7. Peaches stewed this way do not scorch easily and have a pleasant taste.
8. When you want to use them, add a little clove, lemon zest, and nutmeg flower and mix well.

title: Broskvová svařenina (Peach Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches, pitted
- sugar (half cup per cup of peaches)

## Directions

1. Press well-ripened, pitted peaches through a sieve.
2. Add half cup of sugar per cup, let it cook until thickened, stirring constantly and skimming off foam.
3. Remove from heat, let cool halfway, pour into jars, and seal well only after completely cooled.

title: Broskvová svařenina na jiný způsob (Peach Jam, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches
- white sugar (equal amount to the peaches by volume)

## Directions

1. Take nice ripe peaches, wash them well and rub them so that all the fuzz comes off the skin.
2. Slice them into quarters or smaller pieces.
3. Take an equal amount of fine white sugar as peaches and cook both together for 5 or 6 hours with constant stirring.
4. Do not allow it to burn under any circumstances.
5. When it becomes syrupy, pour into jars.

title: Broskvový kompot (Peach Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- developed but not fully ripe peaches
- water
- sugar
- apple puree

## Directions

1. Boil developed but not fully ripe peaches until soft in water
2. Remove and cool in cold water
3. Place on sieves to dry
4. Peel and place in boiled sugar and preserve together with it
5. Boil a little juice with apple puree
6. Arrange in a puree and pour over the preserved peaches

title: Broskvový kompot (Peach Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar (one cup)
- water (five tablespoons)
- ripe peaches

## Directions

1. Put one cup of sugar with five tablespoons of water on a saucepan and let cook about 10 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, peel nice ripe peaches, halve them, and remove the pits.
3. Put them in the boiling syrup and let cook another 10 minutes.

title: Broskvový Paj (Peach Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled peaches
- sugar
- rum (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and pit from peaches
2. Cut into quarters or smaller pieces
3. Sprinkle with sugar
4. Sprinkle with rum if desired
5. Make a pie in the same manner as apple pie
6. Plum pie and apricot pie are prepared the same way, except that peeled plums and apricots are only halved and pitted

title: Broskvový Paj na Jiný Způsob (Peach Pie, Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- pie crust dough
- peaches
- flour
- sugar
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Line a pan with dough
2. Peel the peaches and halve them
3. Remove the pits and arrange the peaches in the pan to fill it
4. Dust with two tablespoons of flour
5. Sprinkle with a coffee cup of sugar, or more if the peaches are not sweet
6. Pour a coffee cup of thick sweet cream over and bake

title: Brukve s vepřovým masem (Kohlrabi with Pork)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- kohlrabi leaves
- salt
- pork meat
- vegetables
- pepper
- pale roux

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi and keep the fine middle leaves, cook in salted water.
2. Slice the kohlrabi into cubes or sticks, place on a baking dish, salt and pepper.
3. Take a nice piece of pork meat, salt it, add to it all the vegetables and cook.
4. When the broth is sufficiently strong, pour it over the kohlrabi, which likewise cooks.
5. When soft, add the chopped green leaves, a little pale roux, and cook everything; finally add the cooked pork meat.

title: Bruselský dort (Brussels Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine cake dough
- layer of almonds
- various optional preserves
- glaze

## Directions

1. Put fine dough into a cake mold
2. Sprinkle a layer of almonds on top
3. Bake slowly in the oven until it turns red
4. Turn out onto a sieve and let cool
5. Coat with various optional preserves
6. Cover the whole cake with nice glaze
7. Decorate tastefully and let dry slowly by the warm stove

title: Brusinky (Cranberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries (large and small)
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select the best and largest cranberries and place in a pot, cover with a lid
2. Place sorted smaller cranberries in another container
3. Bake the cranberries but they must not be too soft - both large and small must be equally soft, then press out the juice
4. For the juice, add half sugar, boil everything together until thick, constantly removing the foam as it rises
5. Pour the boiled juice over the berries, mix, and when everything has cooled, seal in the containers into which it was poured

title: Brzlík telecí (Fried Calf Thymus)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- calf thymus
- salt
- orange
- egg
- grated bread roll
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the thymus and remove all skin, cut into pieces like breaded cutlets
2. Dry, salt, sprinkle with orange zest, dip in beaten egg and coat with grated bread roll
3. Fry in butter until golden

title: Brzlíky s Hnědou Omáčkou (Sweetbreads with Brown Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 4 veal sweetbreads
- fresh parsley, chopped
- 6 shallots
- several mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- brown gravy or beef broth
- hot water
- lemon juice
- 1 onion
- 1 carrot
- parsley root
- 3 tablespoons butter
- ½ wine glass vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch approximately four veal sweetbreads and boil in salted water
2. Drain and cool in cold water
3. Cut into thin slices
4. Chop finely fresh parsley, six shallots, and several mushrooms
5. Stew this mixture in a pot with butter for a quarter hour
6. Heat a piece of butter on the stove and arrange the cut sweetbreads beside each other
7. Season with salt and fry slightly on both sides
8. Add the chopped mixture
9. Pour approximately 2 small cups of brown gravy, beef broth, or hot water over it
10. Let simmer for a quarter hour
11. Remove all fat from the gravy, squeeze lemon juice into it
12. Season with a little more salt if needed and serve hot
13. For the brown gravy: Cut one onion, one carrot, and parsley root into pieces; heat three tablespoons of butter in a pan over high heat until brown; add the vegetables; add half a wine glass of vinegar and a little crushed pepper; season with salt and let cook; strain the gravy through a sieve

title: Brzlíky s houbami (Sweetbreads with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- water
- salt
- bacon
- butter
- white flour
- beef or chicken broth
- wine
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Place sweetbreads in lukewarm salted water and let soak for one hour
2. Put them in water on the fire and let boil for about two minutes
3. Transfer to cold water and let cool
4. Carefully peel them and thread through with bacon
5. Make a thin roux on the stove from good butter and white flour
6. Mix it with strong beef or chicken broth and cook to a smooth sauce
7. Add a glass of wine
8. Place the sweetbreads in this and braise them
9. Add sliced mushrooms that have been braised in wine
10. Let it reduce slightly
11. Place sweetbreads on a dish, pour sauce over them and surround with mushrooms

title: Brzlíky s Houbami (Sweetbreads with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- salt
- warm salted water
- good butter
- white flour
- strong beef or chicken broth

## Directions

1. Place sweetbreads in lukewarm salted water and let soak for an hour
2. Put them on the fire with the water and boil for about two minutes
3. Transfer them to cold water and let cool
4. Carefully peel them and thread them with salt pork
5. Make a thin sauce on the stove from good butter and white flour
6. Dissolve it with strong beef or chicken broth

title: Bublanina (Cherry Cake) - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) butter
- 24 dekagrams ground almonds
- 8 whole eggs
- 7 egg yolks
- snow from 7 egg whites
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- clove
- grated roll
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix butter, ground almonds, whole eggs, egg yolks, snow from egg whites, cinnamon, lemon zest, clove, grated roll and sugar together to form a batter
2. Fill a mold alternately with the mixture and raw cherries
3. Bake the bublanina and sprinkle with sugar

title: Bublanina (Cherry Cake) - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram (1 pound) hard homemade bread
- 1 kilogram (2 pounds) cherries
- 8 egg yolks
- 75 grams (5 lots) sugar
- lemon zest
- spices
- flower
- clove
- 3 and 1/2 deciliters (1 tablespoon) wine
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate the hard bread
2. Put cherries in cold water
3. Beat 8 egg yolks with 75 grams sugar
4. Mix the yolk mixture in a bowl with bread, lemon zest, spices, flower, clove, wine, and snow from 8 egg whites
5. Grease a mold and sprinkle with grated bread
6. Pour in the mixture with cherries and bake

title: Bublanina (Cherry Cake) - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram (2 pounds) cherries
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) butter
- lemon zest
- 4 tablespoons ground sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 5 and 1/4 deciliters (1 and 1/2 tablespoons) sweet sour cream
- flour
- 6 egg whites
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put cherries in cold water
2. Beat or melt 6 dekagrams butter, salt it, add chopped lemon zest, 4 tablespoons ground sugar and beat together in a bowl
3. Add 6 egg yolks and 5 and 1/4 deciliters sweet sour cream
4. Add enough flour to make a thick batter and stiff snow from 6 egg whites
5. Grease a mold with butter and sprinkle with grated roll
6. Lay cherries in the mold, pour in the batter and bake
7. Turn out and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar

title: Bublanina (Fruit Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams flour
- 12 decagrams butter
- 700 ml good milk
- 4 whole eggs
- 8 egg yolks
- snow from 8 egg whites
- salt
- sugar
- lemon zest
- stewed cherries

## Directions

1. Let 24 decagrams of flour and 12 decagrams of butter foam on a baking sheet
2. Pour in 700 ml of good milk and stir until a thick paste forms
3. Let it cool
4. Rub it together with 4 whole eggs, 8 egg yolks, snow from 8 egg whites, salt, sugar, and lemon zest
5. Place half the paste in a mold
6. Place stewed cherries in the middle
7. Finally add the remaining paste
8. When baked, sprinkle with sugar

title: Bublanina s Višněmi (Cake with Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons fresh butter
- 4 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs
- 4 tablespoons sugar with a piece of crushed vanilla
- 2 cups white flour
- 1 cup good cream
- salt
- large cherries

## Directions

1. Rub 2 tablespoons fresh butter until it foams
2. Add to it 4 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs, 4 tablespoons sugar with a piece of crushed vanilla and mix everything well
3. Then mix in 2 cups white flour, 1 cup good cream, salt slightly, and beat well
4. Grease a baking dish with butter, put half the batter into it and on it a layer of beautiful large cherries
5. Sprinkle them with sugar and on top add again the second half of the batter
6. Put it now into a hot oven and let it bake slowly for about an hour

title: Bublinky (Puffs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- butter and sugar in equal parts
- grated lemon zest
- ground cinnamon
- flour, as needed
- 6 eggs
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put a small amount of water, equal parts butter and sugar, grated lemon zest, and ground cinnamon on a hot baking pan
2. Gradually add flour until stiff dough forms
3. Remove the pan and allow dough to cool
4. When cooled, add 6 eggs and 6 egg yolks
5. Heat butter until very hot
6. Dip a spoon in the hot butter, scoop dough the size of a walnut, drop into butter
7. Fry quickly with constant stirring
8. When cooked, remove and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Buchta (Raised Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of butter
- five egg yolks
- six tablespoons of sugar
- lemon zest
- a tiny pinch of pounded flower
- a little salt
- four cupfuls of flour
- a piece of pressed yeast
- four egg whites
- raw cream

## Directions

1. Beat four tablespoons of butter until foamy, add to it five egg yolks, one by one, six tablespoons of sugar, lemon zest, a tiny pinch of pounded flower, a little salt and mix well.
2. Then add four cupfuls of flour, a piece of pressed yeast, the snow from four egg whites and as much raw cream as needed to make a nice stiff dough, beat for half an hour.
3. Grease a large mold with butter, sprinkle with grated bread, put the dough into it, spread butter over the top and let it bake in the oven for three quarters of an hour slowly until golden.
4. Turn out the cake and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Buchta ještě na jiný způsob (Raised Cake - Yet Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight tablespoons of fresh butter
- six tablespoons of fine sugar
- lemon zest
- twelve egg yolks
- twelve tablespoons of white flour
- a little salt
- a piece of pressed yeast
- three egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat eight tablespoons of fresh butter for half an hour, add to it six tablespoons of fine sugar, grated lemon zest, twelve egg yolks and twelve tablespoons of white flour, namely add always one egg yolk and one tablespoon of flour, beat well each time and continue until you use everything up.
2. Then add a little salt and a piece of pressed yeast, work the dough well, then gradually add the stiff snow from three egg whites.
3. Grease a mold with butter, sprinkle with grated bread, put the prepared dough into it, let it rise and then bake in the oven for an hour slowly.

title: Buchta ke kávě (Coffee Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- one cupful of grated butter
- eight tablespoons of fine sugar
- four egg yolks
- a little grated lemon zest
- a little pounded flower
- a little salt
- a piece of pressed yeast
- lukewarm milk or cream
- sugar mixed with cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Put into two pounds of flour one cupful of grated butter, eight tablespoons of fine sugar, four egg yolks, a little grated lemon zest, a little pounded flower, a little salt and mix well.
2. Then add a piece of pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk or cream and the snow from four egg whites, make a medium stiff dough, beat for half an hour and then let it rise.
3. Grease a pan with butter, sprinkle with grated bread, put into it the dough rolled out to the thickness of a finger, spread butter over the surface, let it rise and then bake slowly in the oven.
4. Sprinkle then with sugar mixed with cinnamon.

title: Buchta moravská (Moravian Buchta)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 egg whites
- 500 grams beaten sugar
- 24 egg yolks
- lemon zest from one lemon
- 250 grams grated almonds
- 500 grams sifted flour

## Directions

1. Make stiff snow from 15 egg whites
2. Mix in 500 grams of beaten sugar
3. Add 24 egg yolks
4. Add chopped zest from one lemon
5. Beat everything for half an hour
6. Mix in 250 grams of grated almonds
7. Mix everything and slowly add 500 grams of sifted flour
8. Bake

title: Buchta na jiný způsob (Raised Cake - Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight tablespoons of butter
- twelve whole eggs
- three cupfuls of sifted flour
- four tablespoons of sugar
- half a teaspoon of vanilla extract
- a tiny pinch of salt
- a piece of pressed or dry dissolved yeast
- five egg whites

## Directions

1. Put eight tablespoons of butter on a board, let it warm and beat it for half an hour, then add to it twelve whole eggs and three cupfuls of sifted flour, namely always one egg and one tablespoon of flour, until you use everything up.
2. Then add four tablespoons of sugar, half a teaspoon of vanilla extract, a tiny pinch of salt, a piece of pressed or dry dissolved yeast, beat for a quarter of an hour, then add the stiff snow from five egg whites.
3. Grease a mold with butter, fill it halfway with the prepared dough, set it to warmth and let it rise; when the mold is almost full, put it in the oven and let it bake slowly until golden, which may take about forty minutes, then turn it out carefully and sprinkle generously with sugar.

title: Buchta s mandlemi (Raised Cake with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight tablespoons of butter
- eight eggs
- three tablespoons of fine sugar
- a small cupful of raw cream
- a little pounded flower
- a little salt
- four small cupfuls of dry sifted flour
- one and a half tablespoons of peeled finely cut almonds
- one and a half ounces of citron also finely cut
- a piece of pressed yeast
- five egg whites
- pounded sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat eight tablespoons of butter until foamy, add to it eight eggs, one by one, three tablespoons of fine sugar, a small cupful of raw cream, a little pounded flower, a little salt; mix well.
2. Then add to it four small cupfuls of dry sifted flour, one and a half tablespoons of peeled finely cut almonds, one and a half ounces of citron also finely cut, one piece of pressed yeast, beat for half an hour, then add to it the stiff snow from five egg whites.
3. Grease a mold with butter, fill it halfway with dough and let it rise in a warm place; when the mold is almost full, put it in the oven and let the cake bake until golden, then turn it out carefully and sprinkle with pounded sugar.

title: Buchta s višněmi (Raised Cake with Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of butter
- seven egg yolks
- two tablespoons of sugar
- a little pounded flower
- a little salt
- two cupfuls of good lukewarm raw cream
- white flour
- a piece of pressed yeast
- almonds
- sour cherries preserved in sugar

## Directions

1. Beat four tablespoons of butter until foamy, add seven egg yolks one by one, two tablespoons of sugar, a little pounded flower and salt, two cupfuls of good lukewarm raw cream, mix well.
2. Then add as much fine white flour as needed to make a nice stiff dough, add a piece of pressed yeast and beat so long until the dough no longer sticks to the bowl or the beater.
3. Grease a mold with butter, line it with almonds cut into thin strips, sprinkle with flour and put into it the larger half of the dough, then put a layer of sour cherries preserved in sugar, place the second half of the dough on top and let it rise in a lukewarm place.

title: Buchty (Buchta Buns)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- one-eighth kilogram butter
- 6 decagrams sugar
- lemon zest
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- one kilogram flour
- 3 decagrams diluted yeast
- filling: preserves, curd, or poppy seed

## Directions

1. Rub one-eighth kilogram of butter on a baking sheet until foamy
2. Season with salt
3. Add 6 decagrams sugar, lemon zest, 4 egg yolks, and 2 whole eggs, and rub together
4. Add one kilogram of flour and mix with 3 decagrams of diluted yeast
5. Beat the dough until it no longer sticks to hands
6. Let it rise
7. Roll out and flatten pieces
8. Fill with preserves, curd, or poppy seed
9. Place on greased sheet
10. Oil them
11. Let rise for a quarter hour
12. Bake in oven
13. When baked, sprinkle with sugar and vanilla

title: Butter Churn Acceleration in Winter (Winter Butter Churning)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 liters (12 mázy) of cream
- 15 grams (1 lot) of finely beaten ice
- 3 liters (somewhat less than two mázy) of sour milk

## Directions

1. Add finely beaten ice to 17 liters of cream
2. Heat 3 liters of sour milk in the oven
3. When the sour milk is lukewarm, let it cool
4. In a short time it will churn

title: Butter Preservation with Alder Leaves (Preserving Cottage Cheese from Worms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- curd (tvaroh)
- alder leaves

## Directions

1. Gather alder leaves
2. Place alder leaves on the bottom of a container
3. Add a layer of curd on top
4. Continue alternating layers of alder leaves and curd until the container is full
5. Cover with alder leaves at the top

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart dry flour
- three-quarters pound fresh butter
- six egg yolks
- half a cup cold water
- one tablespoon fine sugar
- salt
- fine filling
- fine paper or parchment
- optional: apples, peaches, plums, strawberries, cherries or other suitable fruit
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Sift one quart of dry flour onto a work surface
2. Crumble three-quarters pound of fresh butter into it finely
3. Mix it, gather into a mound, make a well in the center
4. Put six egg yolks, half a cup of cold water, one tablespoon of fine sugar, and a pinch of salt into the well
5. Mix with a knife, then knead with hands
6. Roll out the dough and fold it, roll it out again and fold again
7. Dust a clean cloth with flour, wrap the dough in it, and let it rest for half an hour in a cold place or on ice
8. Roll out the dough again and cut into square or round pieces
9. Fill with fine filling and form tarts according to the previous recipe
10. Place on a baking sheet lined with buttered paper
11. Alternatively, line a baking dish with dough rolled to about half a finger thickness and place quartered apples, halved fresh peaches, plums, strawberries, cherries or other suitable fruit on top
12. Sprinkle with sugar to taste and bake until golden

title: Bába (Babka)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen hard rolls
- two handfuls almonds
- raisins
- six dekagrams crushed sugar
- cinnamon
- six larger apples or pears
- butter
- three and three-quarters deciliters good sweet sour cream
- three eggs
- six dekagrams butter

## Directions

1. Cut fourteen hard rolls in half, then into small flat cakes.
2. Scald two handfuls almonds, peel, and chop coarsely; wash raisins in cold water, let dry, and pick over.
3. Mix six dekagrams crushed sugar with cinnamon; peel and slice six larger apples or pears and mix everything together.
4. Grease a mold with butter and sprinkle with grated bread.
