3. Add to the liquid 7 pounds of white sugar and 5 pounds of yellow sugar, the finely chopped meat from half a dozen oranges and half a dozen lemons, from which you have removed all the peel, both outer and white.
4. Mix everything well and when the sugar has dissolved, pour into jugs to ferment.
5. When fermented, stopper and store.

title: Diskety (Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 2 full tablespoons baking powder
- 1 tablespoon lard or butter
- salt to taste
- 1 small cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl, add baking powder, lard or butter and salt and mix well
2. Add sweet milk, 1 tablespoon at a time and mix well
3. Dust the rolling pin with flour, turn the dough onto it and add just enough flour so you can roll it to about one inch thickness
4. Cut rounds with a cutter, place on a baking sheet and bake immediately in a hot oven for 20 minutes

title: Divocí holubi pečení (Roasted Wild Pigeons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young wild pigeons
- bacon
- grape leaves

## Directions

1. Young wild pigeons (note: old pigeons have tough meat and must be marinated for several days beforehand) should be wrapped in bacon and grape leaves.
2. Roast slowly until brown on a baking sheet.
3. Baste frequently during roasting and pour the baked sauce over them.

title: Divoký holub (Wild Pigeon)
body:
## Ingredients

- young wild pigeon
- salt
- wine
- vinegar
- onion, sliced
- bay leaves
- piece of lemon peel
- lard, sliced into strips
- butter
- sour cream
- marinade
- pale butter roux
- sour marinade

## Directions

1. Clean young wild pigeons, pluck them, salt them, place in a clay vessel
2. Pour a little wine and vinegar over them, add sliced onion, several bay leaves, a piece of lemon peel and let them lie for at least two days
3. Remove them, dry them, lard them on the breast with lard cut into strips, place on a baking pan, put pieces of butter on them and roast them with frequent basting with sour cream and marinade
4. Make a pale butter roux, mix it with a little of the sour marinade, add the baked broth from the pigeons, a little sour cream and let it cook
5. Place the pigeons on a platter and pour the sauce over them
6. Otherwise wild pigeons and squabs can be prepared just like domestic ones

title: Dobrý a Laciný Pudding (Good and Inexpensive Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup syrup
- half cup hot water
- one teaspoon baking soda
- half cup large pitted and finely chopped raisins
- one beaten egg
- one and a half cups flour
- one cup sugar
- half cup water
- half cup butter
- one large tablespoon rum essence

## Directions

1. Mix half cup syrup, half cup hot water with one teaspoon baking soda added, half cup large pitted and finely chopped raisins, one beaten egg, and one and a half cups flour.
2. Dip a small pudding bag in hot water and sprinkle it with flour.
3. Pour the mixture into it and tie it loosely so that the mixture has enough room to rise when cooking.
4. Boil the bag in boiling water for one hour.
5. For the sauce, cook one cup sugar, half cup water, half cup butter, and one large tablespoon rum essence together for five minutes.

title: Dobrý pivní ocet (Good Beer Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beer dregs
- clean oak shavings

## Directions

1. Place beer dregs into a barrel or other vessels in which clean oak shavings are found
2. Cover the vessel at the top with a lid having many holes (perforated)
3. In a short time the beer will become sour
4. The vinegar is very palatable and healthy; pour it into bottles and it will keep for a long time

title: Domácí jednoduchý dort (Simple Home Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram finest flour
- sufficient raw egg yolks
- abundant finely crushed and sifted sugar
- beaten egg white snow
- finely crushed cinnamon
- vanilla
- cloves
- finely chopped lemon and orange zest

## Directions

1. Vigorously mix and rub together the flour, egg yolks, and sifted sugar
2. Mix in the beaten egg white snow
3. Mix in the finely crushed cinnamon, vanilla, cloves, and finely chopped lemon and orange zest
4. Bake the dough slowly and carefully in buttered molds

title: Domácí kvasnice (Home Made Yeast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kvarty horké vody
- 6 velkých brambor
- lžice cukru
- pint starých kvasnic nebo 2 koláčky suchých

## Directions

1. Přistavte do hrnce 3 kvarty horké vody
2. Oloupejte a rozstrouhejte 6 velkých brambor
3. Když se voda vaří, vlejte je do ní a nechte varem přejít
4. Odstavte, přidejte lžíci cukru
5. Když jsou vlažné, přilejtě pint starých kvasnic, a nemáte-li tyto, tedy dva koláčky suchých
6. Hodně rozmíchejte
7. Když stojí den ve vlažné kuchyni, jsou hotovy pro udělání kvásku na noc, aneb i když ho ráno uděláte, je brzo skynutý

title: Dort cukrový (Sugar Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 grams sugar
- 150 grams finest flour
- finely crushed vanilla
- 5 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 fresh raw egg yolks
- salt

## Directions

1. Take 150 grams sugar, 150 grams finest flour, finely crushed vanilla, five hard-boiled egg yolks sieved, four fresh raw egg yolks, and salt
2. Mix everything into dough
3. Work the dough well
4. Spread into small baking forms
5. Bake until golden yellow
6. If desired as a filled cake, cut the baked cake, fill the inside with juice or fine preserves, and coat the whole cake with lemon glaze

title: Dort liberkový (Pound Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of eggs (8 or 9)
- one pound of sifted sugar
- one teaspoon of almond extract
- two spoonfuls of rum or cognac
- one pound of fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until it becomes foamy
2. add the eggs and beat
3. add the sifted sugar
4. add the almond extract and rum or cognac
5. stir for half an hour
6. finally fold in the fine flour
7. grease the pan with butter and fill it three-quarters full with batter
8. let the cake bake slowly and when cooled, coat with white or red icing, or sprinkle with sugar

title: Dort Oříškový (Nut Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- nut kernels
- eggs
- sugar
- almonds

## Directions

1. Scald and peel nut kernels
2. Beat finely with eggs
3. Proceed as for almond cake

title: Dort se smetanovou pénou (Cream Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- shortcrust pastry
- beaten egg
- butter
- strawberries or other fruit
- sugar
- cream foam
- cream (alternative filling)

## Directions

1. Make shortcrust pastry as in the previous recipe
2. Roll it out to the thickness of a finger
3. Place it on clean paper
4. Cut a round cake using an inverted plate
5. Brush the edges with beaten egg
6. Roll out the remaining dough to two fingers wide and make a border around the round pastry
7. Press the lower edges together
8. Pierce the pastry several times with a small knife to prevent it from puffing up
9. Place on a pan with the paper and bake until golden
10. Let cool completely in a cool place
11. Shortly before serving, fill the center with strawberries or other fruit
12. Sprinkle with sugar and cover with cream foam
13. Alternatively, fill with cream alone and place briefly in the oven to set the cream

title: Dried Green Grapes (Sušené zelené hrozny)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Green grapes
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Preserve and prepare the grapes in the same way as described in the previous section
2. On the second day, pour off the sugar and cook to the sixth degree
3. Add the grape berries, cook everything together, skim the foam
4. Remove the grapes to dry, then sprinkle with finely ground sugar, coat thoroughly, dry again and turn frequently
5. Once completely dried, store between fine paper in boxes

title: Dried Green Mirabelles (Zelené mirabelky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Developed, unripe mirabelles with soft pits
- Soft water
- Alum (small amount)
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pierce the mirabelles several times
2. Place them in soft water containing a little alum
3. Set the container on warm heat
4. Remove from heat as soon as they begin to boil, keeping covered
5. Transfer to cold water
6. Proceed as instructed for plums

title: Dried Pitted Plums (Zadělané švestky sušené)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the pits at the stem
2. Cover the plums with dissolved sugar
3. On the second day, cook together after the drained sugar has been cooked thicker
4. Pour off the sugar again and cook it, add the plums and cook together with the sugar
5. Place on a sieve to dry thoroughly, then dry slowly and completely

title: Drobanec krupični (Semolina Crumble)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram cooked peeled potatoes
- fine semolina
- salt
- goose or pork lard

## Directions

1. Grate cooked potatoes
2. Salt
3. Mix with fine semolina to make thick lumps
4. Spread on baking sheet greased with lard
5. Sprinkle with same lard on top
6. Mix and bake

title: Drobanec mouční (Flour Crumble)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters milk or cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- piece of sugar
- flour
- lemon zest
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix milk or cream, egg yolks, whole eggs, sugar, and lemon zest
2. Add salt
3. Add enough flour to make thick batter
4. Butter baking sheet generously and spread batter not too thick
5. Bake
6. Break up with two forks
7. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon or sugar with vanilla
8. Usually served with preserves

title: Drštky na kyselo (Tripe with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of cooked tripe
- Two cups of water
- Half cup of vinegar
- One onion
- A few grains of new spice
- Four cloves
- A clove of garlic
- Salt
- Two tablespoons of butter
- One tablespoon of sugar
- One heaping tablespoon of flour
- Pepper
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked tripe into pieces and place in a pot
2. Pour over it two cups of water and half cup of vinegar
3. Add one onion finely chopped, a few grains of new spice, four cloves, and a clove of garlic rubbed with salt
4. Let simmer
5. Make a roux from two tablespoons of butter; when it melts, add one tablespoon of sugar and one heaping tablespoon of flour and let it brown well
6. Pour the roux into the tripe, add a pinch of pepper and a pinch of marjoram
7. Let it simmer together and pour onto a platter with the gravy

title: Drštky na kyselo (Tripe with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds cooked tripe
- 2 cups water
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 1 onion
- few grains of allspice
- 4 cloves
- garlic
- salt
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Cut the 2 pounds of cooked tender tripe, place in a pot and pour 2 cups of water and 1/2 cup vinegar over it. Add one finely chopped onion, a few grains of allspice, 4 cloves and a clove of garlic crushed with salt. Let simmer.
2. Make a roux from 2 tablespoons of butter; when melted, add 1 tablespoon of flour.

title: Drůbež v Omáčce (Poultry in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Poultry leftovers
- Onion
- Bay leaves
- Pepper
- Allspice
- Salt
- Egg yolk
- Burnt sugar
- White wine
- Roasted or fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook together with onion, several bay leaves, a little pepper, allspice and salt.
2. When cooked, mix in a pale egg yolk, a piece of caramelized sugar and a little white wine.
3. Let it simmer further and strain over neatly cut poultry leftovers arranged on a baking pan.
4. Let the leftovers heat through in the sauce, arrange them on a platter, pour the sauce over them and garnish with roasted or fried potatoes.

title: Duck Giblets (Drůbky kachní)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- duck necks, heads, feet, gizzards, hearts and wings
- chopped pork meat
- breadcrumb
- pepper
- salt
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Clean the necks, heads, feet, gizzards, hearts and wings. Cook with dumplings made from chopped pork meat and grated breadcrumb, season with pepper and salt, add crushed marjoram, cook and pour over the giblets.

title: Duck in Red Wine (Kachna v červeném víně)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted duck
- hot water
- onion
- lemon zest
- spice
- salt
- brown roux
- red wine
- broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over the roasted duck, add onion, lemon zest, spice and salt, braise briefly. To the sauce add brown roux, a quarter liter of red wine, several tablespoons of broth and juice from half a lemon. When the duck is tender, pour the sauce over it.

title: Duté koláče (Hollow Tarts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 ml water
- 70 grams butter
- 70 grams fine flour
- six eggs
- salt
- grated lemon zest
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a quarter liter of water with 70 grams of butter
2. Add 70 grams of fine flour
3. Make a paste from everything on moderately warm stove
4. Let it cool
5. Beat six eggs into it
6. Season with salt
7. Add grated lemon zest and crushed sugar
8. When everything is well worked, remove the dough by spoon into heaps
9. Roll the tarts from these heaps
10. Place on a greased baking sheet
11. Bake until golden

title: Dušenina z mušlí (Clam Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked bacon or ham, half pound, cut into small cubes
- potatoes, three, cut into small cubes
- onion, finely chopped
- dill
- marjoram
- green parsley
- salt
- pepper
- veal, one pound, cut into half-inch cubes
- tomatoes, stewed or pickled, two cups
- clams, 50, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Place the cubed bacon or ham on the bottom of a deep pot
2. Add the cubed potatoes
3. Sprinkle with finely chopped onion
4. Season with dill, marjoram, green parsley, pepper and salt
5. Add the veal cubes
6. Add two cups of stewed or pickled tomatoes
7. Finally add a layer of finely chopped clams
8. Pour boiling water over all, just barely covering
9. Cover well and let simmer slowly for half an hour without stirring
10. Add two cups of milk and six crushed crackers
11. Mix well, boil for 10 minutes more and serve hot
12. The tomatoes may be omitted if desired

title: Dušená divoká kachna (Stewed Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild duck, plucked and cleaned
- cold water
- lard
- onion, sliced
- carrot
- parsley root
- celery
- bay leaf
- piece of ginger
- grains of pepper
- juniper berries
- cloves
- raw ham, thinly sliced
- salt
- water
- fresh mushrooms
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Place plucked and cleaned wild duck in cold water for half an hour, then lard it
2. Put on the bottom of a pot sliced onion, carrot, parsley root, celery, bay leaf, piece of ginger, several grains of pepper, juniper berries and cloves, and a piece of thinly sliced raw ham
3. Place the duck in this, salt it lightly, add some water and let it braise gently with frequent basting until tender; if it browns too much, cover it with paper
4. Make a sauce from fresh mushrooms, pour into it the strained broth from the duck
5. Pour the sauce over the duck and let it stand for another quarter hour in a warm place
6. The broth from the duck, browned with a roux, mixed with beef broth and cooked to a smooth sauce, is also good

title: Dušená holoubata s hnědou omáčkou (Stewed Squabs with Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter or bacon sliced into thin pieces
- thyme
- parsley
- whole onion
- peppercorns
- ginger
- cloves
- salt
- wine vinegar
- fresh butter
- flour
- sugar
- beef broth
- lemon

## Directions

1. Put a piece of fresh butter or thinly sliced bacon on a roasting pan with a little thyme and parsley, a whole onion, a few peppercorns, a piece of ginger and four cloves
2. Place the squabs on it, salt them and pour two spoonfuls of wine vinegar over them
3. Cover the pan well and let the squabs braise slowly until tender
4. Remove them to another pan, cut them in half and put them on a warm place
5. Meanwhile, put a piece of fresh butter on a small pan to heat
6. Add two scant spoonfuls of flour and a spoonful of sugar and make a brown roux
7. Mix it with the sauce from the squabs
8. Add beef broth and let it boil
9. Strain it through a sieve over the cut squabs
10. Let them heat for about a minute longer on the fire but it must not boil anymore
11. Bring it then immediately to the table
12. You can also add lemon cut into rounds to the sauce

title: Dušená holoubata se šparglem (Stewed Squabs with Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice squabs
- salted water
- butter
- onion
- carrot
- parsley
- salt
- flour
- good beef broth
- egg yolks
- sugar
- lemon juice
- green asparagus heads

## Directions

1. Gut and rinse the squabs well and let them boil about three minutes in salted water
2. Let them cool and dry well
3. Put a piece of butter on a roasting pan with one onion, carrot, and a piece of parsley
4. Place the squabs in it, salt it and let it braise for several minutes
5. Dust a little flour on it, mix it with good beef broth and let it boil for half an hour, skimming the fat from it
6. When the squabs are tender, remove them to another roasting pan and put them on a warm place so they do not cool
7. Let the sauce reduce well now, thicken it with four yolks
8. Add a spoonful of sugar, a little lemon juice, salt it properly and strain it through a sieve over two small bowls of green asparagus heads which you have previously cooked in salted water
9. Place the squabs on a dish and pour the prepared sauce over them

title: Dušená holoubátka (Stewed Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- butter
- onion, sliced into rounds
- bay leaves
- thyme
- juniper berries
- peppercorns
- allspice berries
- ginger
- wine vinegar
- flour
- browned sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the squabs and cut them into quarters
2. Put a piece of butter in a pot and layer the sliced onion
3. Add bay leaves, thyme, several juniper berries, six peppercorns, six allspice berries and a piece of ginger
4. Place the cut squabs on top
5. Pour a little wine vinegar over it
6. Cover with a lid and let it braise
7. Then remove the squabs to another pot and strain the sauce over them
8. Mix about three spoonfuls of flour with a piece of butter by hand and form into several small balls which you place here and there in the sauce
9. Let it simmer slowly with frequent stirring until thick
10. Add a piece of browned sugar to the sauce

title: Dušená Husa (Stewed Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- One older goose
- Sliced bacon
- Veal meat, sliced
- Carrot, sliced into rounds
- Parsley
- Celery
- Salt
- Bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Beef broth
- White wine (optional)

## Directions

1. If you have an older goose that is not suitable for roasting, you can stew it as follows:
2. Put sliced bacon and sliced veal meat on the bottom of a pot.
3. Add carrot sliced into rounds, parsley, and celery.
4. Place the gutted and salted goose on top.
5. Add several bay leaves and several peppercorns.
6. Cover with beef broth and add a little white wine if desired.
7. Cover with a lid and let the goose stew until tender.
8. Remove the goose to a serving dish and arrange celery salad around it.

title: Dušená kuřátka s kyselou omáčkou (Stewed Chicken with Sour Cream Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- butter
- onion
- clove
- carrot
- beef broth
- salt
- flour
- parsley
- egg yolks
- sour cream
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken well and place it in cold water.
2. Place a piece of butter, an onion stuck with a clove, and a piece of carrot in a pot, pour beef broth over it, and salt it.
3. Remove the chicken from cold water and arrange it nicely in the pot, or cut it into pieces and arrange it in the pot, and let it stew, but not until very tender, turning it frequently so it remains nicely white.
4. Place a piece of butter in a pot, a piece of sliced onion, a little chopped fresh parsley, add two tablespoons of flour and make a pale roux, pour a little beef broth over it and mix well.
5. Strain the broth in which you stewed the chicken, skim the fat from it, and pour it into the diluted roux.
6. Place the stewed chicken in this sauce and let it cook a bit.
7. Before serving, beat two egg yolks with a little sour cream, mix it into the sauce, place the chicken on a platter, strain the sauce over it, and serve.
8. If desired, you can squeeze lemon juice into it.

title: Dušená lupiče (Stewed Bass)
body:
## Ingredients

- bass
- salt
- butter
- onion
- bay leaves
- rosemary
- cloves
- celery flower
- peppercorns
- pea water
- wine
- vinegar
- bread crumbs
- flour
- lemon juice
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Quarter the bass, gut and salt it, place butter, onion, bay leaves, a piece of rosemary, three cloves, a piece of celery flower and several peppercorns on a pan, place the bass on it, add a little pea water, a little wine and vinegar and let it stew well covered.
2. Meanwhile place a piece of butter on a small pan, a tablespoon of grated bread, a little flour, let it foam and pour into the sauce in which the bass was stewing, and if it were too little sour, season it with a little lemon juice.
3. Then place the fish on a dish, pour the sauce under it, arrange with lemon slices and bring to table.

title: Dušená Rajská Jablíčka (Stewed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Nice tomatoes
- Fresh butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sugar
- 1 cup sour cream or milk
- 1 spoonful flour

## Directions

1. Dip nice tomatoes into boiling water for two minutes, peel off the skins, cut them in half, place on a pan with a piece of fresh butter, salt, pepper, sprinkle with sugar and let stew for about a quarter hour.
2. Take one cup of sour cream or milk if sour cream is not available, mix in one spoonful of flour, pour into the tomatoes and when it begins to boil, remove from heat and serve with beefsteak or roast.

title: Dušená srnčí játra (Stewed Liver of Venison)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison liver
- butter
- onion
- pepper
- flour
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash venison liver well, remove the skin and cut into thin slices
2. Place a piece of butter on a pan, add finely chopped onion and a little pepper, and arrange the cut liver slices on top
3. Let them brown, then sprinkle with a little flour, add some vinegar, salt and let cook a little longer

title: Dušená telecí játra (Braised Veal Liver)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Bacon
- White pepper
- Onion
- Beef broth or water
- Cream, three and a half deciliters
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the liver, remove all membranes, pierce with bacon, season with pepper, chop onion, add a piece of chopped bacon, add beef broth or water and braise until softened.
2. Strain the sauce, add three and a half deciliters of cream, place the liver in it and simmer a while.

title: Dušená telecí játra (Stewed Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh calf's liver
- butter
- onion
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean fresh calf's liver and remove all skin, then slice it into thin slices
2. Melt a piece of butter on the stove
3. Add a spoonful of finely chopped onion and braise for about a minute
4. Then place the sliced liver in it, add a little ground pepper and braise the liver, constantly turning it with a spatula, until it is no longer bloody but also not too dry
5. Before serving, salt it
6. You can also sprinkle it with a little finely chopped lemon zest

title: Dušená Telecí Kýta (Stewed Leg of Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- bacon, cut into thin strips
- spices
- onion, finely chopped
- green parsley
- salt
- onion, sliced into rounds
- carrot
- lemon
- orange peel, chopped
- white wine, 1 bottle
- brandy, a little
- beef broth, a few tablespoons
- sugar, 1 teaspoon
- spices

## Directions

1. Lard the leg with thin strips of bacon that have been coated in spices, finely chopped onion and green parsley
2. Salt the leg and place in an appropriate pot
3. Add sliced onion, carrot, lemon, chopped orange peel
4. Pour in white wine and a little brandy
5. Add a few tablespoons of beef broth and 1 teaspoon of sugar
6. Season with various spices
7. Cover the pot tightly with a lid and stew over moderate heat until tender
8. Remove the leg to a platter, strain the sauce over it and serve

title: Dušená Šunka (Braised Ham)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned small ham
- Red wine
- Onion
- Bay leaf
- Pepper
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Remove bones from the cleaned small ham
2. Cook in water for about two hours
3. Remove to a baking pan
4. Pour red wine over the ham
5. Add sliced onion, bay leaf, pepper, and cloves underneath
6. When ready to serve, trim the fatty layer from the ham
7. Strain the sauce and pour over the ham on a serving dish

title: Dušené cibule (Stewed Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- small white new onions
- cold water

## Directions

1. Peel small white new onions and let them soak in cold water for half an hour
2. Remove them, place in cold water to cook and when they have cooked for about three minutes, strain the water and pour fresh new water

title: Dušené divoké vepřové (Braised Wild Boar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar meat
- salt
- bacon
- ham slices
- onion
- aromatic vegetables
- bay leaf
- water
- wine
- vinegar
- caper or cucumber sauce

## Directions

1. Salt any piece of wild boar meat.
2. Place on a baking pan and wrap with bacon and ham slices.
3. Lay down with onion and other aromatic vegetables, and bay leaf.
4. Cover with bacon, add water, wine and vinegar.
5. Braise slowly and serve with caper or cucumber sauce.

title: Dušené houby (Stewed Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- fresh butter
- juice of one lemon
- small onion, finely chopped
- salt
- beef broth
- white wine (optional)
- egg yolk and cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel fresh mushrooms, trim the stems a little at the bottom, wash them in fresh water, slice them into strips, put them on a shallow pan on a piece of fresh butter, add lemon juice, finely chopped small onion, a little salt and several tablespoons of good beef broth and let them stew for a quarter hour.
2. Then pour them onto a platter and serve immediately.
3. You can also add a little white wine and mix an egg yolk blended with cream.

title: Dušené houby na jiný způsob (Stewed Mushrooms Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-cleaned mushrooms
- butter
- fresh parsley, chopped
- caraway seeds
- salt
- white wine (optional)
- butter roux
- vinegar (optional)
- sour cream (optional)
- breadcrumbs
- cheese

## Directions

1. Slice well-cleaned mushrooms, wash them in fresh water, put them on a shallow pan on a piece of butter, add a little chopped fresh parsley, a little caraway seeds and salt and let them stew for about three-quarters of an hour with frequent stirring.
2. You can also add a little white wine and then thicken with a thin pale butter roux.
3. Or add a little wine vinegar to the stewed mushrooms, add sour cream and let it cook a little longer.
4. Stewed mushrooms thickened with cream can also be put on a platter in which you want to serve it, sprinkle them with grated breadcrumbs and cheese, top with small pieces of butter and let them brown in the oven.

title: Dušené Hovězí (Stewed Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of finely chopped bacon
- two onions, sliced into rounds
- beef chuck, cleaned and rinsed
- one cup hot water
- salt
- pepper
- one garlic clove
- one tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Put a quarter pound of finely chopped bacon and two onions sliced into rounds in a pot.
2. When it begins to turn yellow, add a piece of cleaned and rinsed beef chuck and let it brown on both sides on high heat.
3. Add one cup of hot water, salt and pepper it, mash one garlic clove with salt and add to the pot.
4. Cover well and let simmer on moderate heat.
5. Watch that it does not burn and occasionally baste with hot water as needed, but not much at once, always about two spoonfuls.
6. Meat from a young animal or aged meat will be stewed in two hours.
7. When it is tender, remove the meat and to the remaining sauce mix in one spoon of flour dissolved in a little water, let it boil and pour over the meat.

title: Dušené hovězí maso (Braised Beef)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- beef fat
- onion
- vegetables
- salt
- pepper (3 grains)
- beef broth
- 3 ladles flour
- eggs
- cabbage
- kohlrabi
- pickled cucumbers
- other vegetables as desired

## Directions

1. Trim all fat from the meat and cut into pieces
2. Cook the meat and cut the fat into cubes
3. Add onion and all chopped vegetables, salt with 3 grains of pepper and braise until brown
4. To prevent burning, baste with beef broth
5. Mix in 3 ladles of flour, pour in more broth and simmer
6. Cut the cooked beef into portions and place on a baking sheet
7. Strain the sauce through a sieve, braise for a quarter hour
8. Place on a platter, pour sauce over it
9. Create a wreath around the meat made from eggs, vegetable marrow, cabbage, kohlrabi, pickled cucumbers, water vegetables or any other vegetables as desired

title: Dušené Hovězí Maso Ještě na Jiný Způsob (Stewed Beef Yet Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of beef from the hindquarters
- wine vinegar
- bacon, cut into thick strips
- salt
- beef broth
- one onion, sliced
- a piece of carrot
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- new spice
- clove
- one heaping spoon of butter
- one spoon flour
- one spoon sugar
- two cups of beef broth or hot water
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pound about three pounds of beef from the hindquarters well with a wooden mallet, put it in a pot, pour a little wine vinegar over it and let it marinate thus for about twenty-four hours.
2. When you then want to prepare the meat, lard it with bacon cut into thick strips, put it in a pot, salt it, add a little vinegar, a little meat beef broth, one sliced onion, a piece of carrot, bay leaf, several peppercorns, new spice, clove, cover it and let it stew slowly until tender.
3. Meanwhile melt one heaping spoon of butter in another pan, put one spoon of flour and one spoon of sugar in it and brown it to make a roux.
4. To this gradually pour in about two cups of beef broth, from which you have skimmed off all fat, or hot water if you do not have broth, and the sauce from the stewed meat, from which you have also skimmed off all fat.
5. Let it boil about a quarter hour until you have from it a thick, brown, pleasantly sour sauce.
6. When you have skimmed off any additional fat, pour the sauce over the stewed meat and let it boil a little more.
7. Then place the meat on a plate, pour part of the sauce over it, bring to the table and serve the rest of the sauce in a separate bowl.
8. You can also add fried potatoes or dumplings to this.

title: Dušené Hovězí Maso na Jiný Způsob (Stewed Beef Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of lard
- one onion, sliced
- bay leaf
- a piece of lemon peel
- clove
- two pounds of beef
- flour
- beef broth
- wine

## Directions

1. Put a piece of lard, one sliced onion, bay leaf, a piece of lemon peel and clove on a pot.
2. Salt and clean two pounds of beef, put it in the pot and let it stew.
3. Sprinkle it then with a little flour and let it stew some more.
4. Then add a little beef broth and a little wine and let it braise until the meat shrinks, then put it on a plate and sprinkle with lemon peel.

title: Dušené hovězí s bramborami (Irish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds meat from the rump, cut into cubes
- approximately six potatoes, peeled and cut into thick slices
- two onions, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- small tablespoons of butter
- water
- half a small cup of water

## Directions

1. Take three pounds of meat from the rump and cut it into not-too-large cubes.
2. Peel about six potatoes and cut them into thick slices, and chop two onions finely.
3. In a deep pot or casserole, place a thin layer of meat and a layer of potatoes, sprinkle with onion, salt and pepper, and add about one small tablespoon of butter.
4. Repeat until all ingredients are used.
5. The last layer on top must be potatoes.
6. Cover with butter, pour about half a small cup of water over it, cover well, and let it braise in the oven for two hours.

title: Dušené hovězí s chlebem (Beef Stew with Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of nice meat from the rump
- bacon
- onion, chopped and fried in butter
- several cloves
- rye bread, cut into cubes
- broth
- white wine (optional)
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a piece of nice meat from the rump, singe it, salt it, and pound it.
2. It is good to thread it with bacon and place it in a pot on chopped onion fried in butter.
3. Add several cloves and let it braise.
4. Cut rye bread into cubes and add an appropriate amount to the meat.
5. Let it braise covered, frequently baste it with broth, and according to preference you can also add a small glass of white wine.
6. Before finishing, remove the meat to a platter, cut it into slices, and pour the strained sauce over it.

title: Dušené Hovězí Závitky (Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of nice beef from the hindquarters
- salt
- all-spice
- flour
- bacon, thinly sliced
- fresh butter
- hot water

## Directions

1. Pound four pounds of nice beef from the hindquarters with a wooden mallet and slice it into cutlets, about a finger thick, pound again.
2. Sprinkle them then with salt, a little all-spice and a little flour, lay bacon thinly sliced on the cutlets, roll up each cutlet, fasten with a skewer and coat in flour.
3. Melt a piece of fresh butter in a pot, dip the rolled cutlets in it, lay them next to each other in the pot, cover and let the cutlets fry about ten minutes on moderate heat, during which time you must turn them once.
4. Then add to the cutlets from the side as much boiling water until they are submerged halfway, but watch that they do not get wet on the surface.
5. Then cover the pot so that the lid fits completely and let the beef rolls simmer on moderate heat for an hour.
6. Then put the rolls on a plate, remove the skewers from them, arrange fried potatoes around them and add the sauce.

title: Dušené Kedlubny čili Brukve (Stewed Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- butter
- sugar
- broth
- salt
- brown roux
- ground ginger
- kohlrabi greens

## Directions

1. Peel several kohlrabi and cut into thin rounds
2. Place butter and sugar on a pan, add cut kohlrabi, add a little broth, salt, and braise until soft
3. Brown with a light brown roux and add ground ginger
4. Select younger leaves from the kohlrabi greens, rinse in clean water and cook until tender, then drain
5. Chop the greens finely, place on pan with butter, add broth and brown with the same roux
6. Arrange on a serving dish in the center of the kohlrabi and border with the chopped greens

title: Dušené Koroptve s Celerem (Braised Partridge with Celery)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- lard for larding
- thick bacon slices
- butter
- small celeries
- broth

## Directions

1. Lard the partridges with lard and brown on both sides in butter with slices of thick bacon.
2. Clean the small celeries, cut into 4-6 pieces, and add to the partridges.
3. Pour with broth and braise everything together until tender.

title: Dušené Koroptve se Zelím (Braised Partridge with Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- older partridges
- lard
- bacon
- onion
- salt
- vegetable roots
- 2 heads cabbage
- smoked sausages

## Directions

1. Clean the older partridges; lard the breasts and thighs with lard and ham.
2. Braise with lard, onion, salt, and vegetable roots.
3. Cut two heads of cabbage into quarters, wash, cool in water, and wring out.
4. Braise the cabbage with smoked meat and lard in broth.
5. When the partridges are half-braised, add them to the cabbage and pour with the juices.
6. When done, arrange the partridges surrounded by cabbage, pour with the juices.
7. Fried sausages may be placed on top of the cabbage.

title: Dušené kuře (Stewed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and gutted chicken
- salt
- butter
- clove
- onion
- parsley
- carrot
- peppercorns
- bay leaf
- bacon
- beef broth or water

## Directions

1. Wash the cleaned and gutted chicken, salt it, and lard it.
2. Place a piece of butter on the breast and put it in a pot just large enough to fit it.
3. Add to it sliced onion, parsley, and carrot, several peppercorns, a bay leaf, and a piece of bacon.
4. Pour beef broth or water over it so that the chicken is covered.
5. Cover first with clean white paper and then with a well-fitting lid.
6. Let the chicken stew until tender, turning it once.
7. Remove the chicken, cut it into pieces, quickly boil the liquid and reduce it on high heat until thick.
8. Skim the fat from it and pour it over the chicken.
9. Young chicken will stew in one and a half hours, older chicken in two hours.

title: Dušené Kuře (Stewed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and drawn chicken
- salt
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- carrot
- pepper
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Clean and draw the chicken well, wash, salt, and truss it
2. Place a piece of butter on the breast
3. Place it in a pan just large enough to hold it
4. Add onion cut into slices, parsley, carrot, several peppercorns, and a bay leaf

title: Dušené kuře s hnědou omáčkou (Stewed Chicken with Brown Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and gutted chicken
- fresh butter
- lemon juice
- onions
- carrot
- leek
- parsnip
- beef broth
- sugar
- white flour
- ginger

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the chicken, wash it, salt it inside, and place a piece of fresh butter in it.
2. Lightly rub the breast with lemon juice and butter, then place it in a pot and cover well.
3. In another pot, arrange thinly sliced onions, two small carrots, some leek and parsnip all cut into slices.
4. Add a piece of butter and let it stew for a while, then pour in a quart of good beef broth and let it cook for half an hour.
5. Half an hour before serving the chicken, strain this sauce over it and let it stew in a covered pot, but make sure the chicken remains completely white and the meat stays juicy.
6. Remove the chicken from the pot and let it drain.
7. Arrange it on a platter and pour good brown gravy over it, prepared as follows: Place a piece of fresh butter in a pot, add a small piece of sugar, and let it heat, then add a little white flour and make a brown thin roux, thin it with good beef broth to make a thickened sauce, add a little ground ginger, and mix well.

title: Dušené kuřátka s vinnou omáčkou (Stewed Chicken with Wine Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- ham
- fresh butter
- flour
- onion
- clove
- lemon
- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- beef broth
- good wine

## Directions

1. Gut and clean the chicken, then cut it into neat pieces and place in cold water for a while.
2. Place a piece of sliced ham and a piece of fresh butter in a pot, dust it with flour, add an onion stuck with a clove, lemon cut into slices, a piece of parsley, carrot, and celery; tie this vegetables together with string.
3. Place the chicken in this and let it stew, then pour in good beef broth and let it cook over moderate heat.
4. When ready to serve, pour in a glass of good wine, squeeze in lemon juice, let it cook a bit more, remove the chicken to a platter, strain the sauce over it through a sieve, and serve.

title: Dušené Lanýže (Braised Truffles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles, washed and peeled
- wine

## Directions

1. Place washed and peeled truffles in a Papillon pot with wine
2. Cook and braise together
3. Remove cooked truffles and place in buttered paper
4. When completely softened, remove the paper
5. Arrange attractively on a plate or dish for serving

title: Dušené Ledvinky Skopové (Braised Mutton Kidneys)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton kidneys
- water
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- glass of wine

## Directions

1. Soak kidneys in water and remove fat
2. Lard them, season with pepper and salt
3. Braise in butter
4. Cook sauce with lemon zest, lemon juice, and a glass of wine
5. Add kidneys to the sauce

title: Dušené raky (Stewed Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- crab meat from tails and large claws
- bread, grated, about a quarter of the meat's weight
- parsley, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- crab shells
- butter, in small pieces
- lemon juice, one
- bread crumbs, layer
- butter, additional pieces

## Directions

1. Carefully remove the meat from the tails and large claws of the crabs
2. Chop finely and mix with about a quarter of grated bread and some finely chopped parsley
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Fill the crab shells with this mixture
5. Arrange on a baking pan
6. Dot with small pieces of butter
7. Squeeze the juice of one lemon over all
8. Sprinkle thickly with bread crumbs
9. Top with additional pieces of butter
10. Cover and let braise in the oven for about twenty minutes

title: Dušené skopové jazyky (Stewed Mutton Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton tongues
- lukewarm water
- butter
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Let the mutton tongues lie in lukewarm water for an hour, then cook them for a quarter hour, and let them cool in cold water.
2. With a sharp knife, remove the skin, cut each tongue in half lengthwise, and season with salt.
3. Put a piece of butter in a pan and line the bottom with about an inch of thinly sliced onion, arrange the halved tongues on it, sprinkle them with ground pepper, place pieces of butter on top, cover the pan well, and let the tongues stew slowly until tender.
4. You must ensure that the onion does not burn; the onion must be as golden and completely tender as the tongues.
5. When serving, arrange the tongues on a dish with the onion in an attractive pyramid and squeeze lemon juice over them.

title: Dušené skopové ledviny (Stewed Mutton Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton kidneys
- salted water
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion
- hot water
- gravy from roasted meat
- red wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Select nice mutton kidneys and cook them for an hour in salted water, then remove the skin and cut them in half lengthwise, then into thin strips.
2. Mix several tablespoons of flour with a little salt and ground pepper and coat the cooked cut kidneys in it.
3. Melt a piece of fresh butter in a pan, add a little finely chopped onion, place the kidneys coated in flour into it, and let them stew for ten minutes, stirring several times.
4. Squeeze the juice of one lemon into it, add hot water or, if available, a little gravy from roasted meat and one bowl of red wine, and let it simmer a few more minutes until thickened, but it must not boil.

title: Dušené skopové ledviny na jiný způsob (Stewed Mutton Kidneys, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton kidneys
- cold water
- butter
- shallots
- mushrooms
- green parsley
- hot water
- brown broth
- flour
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Rinse the kidneys clean, let them drain, cut them in half lengthwise, and slice into thin strips.
2. Melt a piece of fresh butter in a pan, add four finely chopped shallots, several mushrooms, and green parsley, all finely chopped, then the sliced kidneys and let them stew until tender.
3. Remove the kidneys from the pan, add several tablespoons of hot water or, if available, brown broth and a little flour to the juices in the pan, and mix well.
4. Return the kidneys to the pan, season with salt, squeeze lemon juice into it, and let the kidneys stew a little longer, then serve them hot on a dish.

title: Dušené skopové plece (Stewed Shoulder of Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice mutton shoulder
- salt
- string for tying
- cup of fat broth
- onion
- carrot
- bay leaf
- small piece of garlic
- peppercorns
- two cloves
- piece of parsley
- beef broth
- Liebig's meat extract

## Directions

1. Clean a nice mutton shoulder well and wash it clean, remove bones, salt the inside, then roll it and tie it with string.
2. Place the roll into a deep pan, pour a cup of fat broth over it, add onion, carrot, bay leaf, a small piece of garlic, several peppercorns, two cloves, a piece of parsley and let it braise slowly to a golden color.
3. Then pour another cup of beef broth and let it braise until tender.
4. Then remove the roll, remove the string, add a spoon of Liebig's meat extract to the gravy, let it simmer, then skim the fat and strain the gravy through a sieve onto the roll; serve the remaining gravy in a gravy boat.

title: Dušené srnčí plece (Stewed Shoulder of Venison)
body:
## Ingredients

- two venison shoulders
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- lard
- carrot
- cloves
- bay leaf
- vinegar
- meat broth
- additional meat broth for sauce

## Directions

1. Wash two venison shoulders well and remove the bone from the joint
2. Sprinkle them inside with finely chopped onion, parsley, pepper and salt
3. Roll each one up so the filling goes inside, tie well with string, and lard the outer part with bacon, then salt
4. Place carrot and onion sliced into rounds on a pan with several peppercorns, two cloves, a bay leaf and good lard
5. Place the prepared shoulders on this, pour a little vinegar and meat broth over them
6. Let them braise slowly until browned
7. When the venison is tender enough, remove it and place on a platter
8. Remove the string, then add several more spoons of meat broth to the sauce
9. Let it boil a little longer, then strain it through a sieve, skim off the fat and pour it over the braised shoulders
10. You can also serve pepper sauce on the side as for venison saddle

title: Dušené telecí ledvinky (Stewed Veal Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- butter
- onion
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same way as stewed calf's liver (recipe 213)

title: Dušené telecí řízky (Stewed Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg cutlets
- salt
- bacon
- fresh butter
- carrot
- onion
- beef broth
- beef broth
- small onions
- water
- fresh butter
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut neat pieces from the veal leg
2. Salt them and lard with bacon
3. Place a piece of fresh butter on a pan, a piece of carrot and one onion both cut into slices
4. Pour beef broth over this
5. Add the prepared veal cutlets and let them braise on both sides on high heat until browned
6. Frequently baste the cutlets with the sauce they are braising in; however, if the sauce dries too much, you must add beef broth
7. On another pan, place a piece of fresh butter and about three handfuls of small onions
8. Pour a few spoonfuls of water over them and let them braise until tender
9. On another pan, heat a spoonful of butter, add a spoonful of flour, mix it with a small cup of sour cream and let it simmer
10. Pour this sauce onto a plate
11. Arrange the braised cutlets in it and surround with the braised onions

title: Dušené zelí z hlávky (Stewed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- zelí - rozkrájené
- voda - vařící
- máslo nebo sádlo - čerstvé
- cibulka - drobně rozkrájená
- sůl
- mouka - lžíce
- ocet
- hovězí polévka
- cukr - kousek
- pepř - tlučený
- pejoř - tlučený
- kmín

## Directions

1. Rozkrájené zelí polej vařící vodou
2. Sceď, vymačkej, dej na kastrol na kus čerstvého másla nebo sádla
3. Přidej drobně rozkrájenou cibulku a osol
4. Nech to dusit, během dušení tím často zamíchej, aby se to nepřipálilo
5. Popraš to lžicí mouky a do hnědá udus
6. Na konec přilej trochu octa, smíšeného dle chuti s hovězí polévkou a kouskem cukru
7. Nech ještě chvilku povařit
8. Můžeš také přidati trochu tlučeného pejoře a trochu kmínu

title: Dušený hovězí jazyk (Braised Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- One whole beef tongue
- Sliced bacon
- A few pieces of veal
- Two carrots
- Four onions
- Thyme
- Clove
- Beef broth
- Juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and boil the whole beef tongue for about half an hour, or a whole hour if it is large
2. Let it cool, peel off the skin, and trim it neatly
3. Line the bottom of a pot with sliced bacon and a few pieces of veal
4. Add two carrots and four onions, both sliced into rounds, a little thyme and clove
5. Place the prepared tongue on top
6. Pour over enough beef broth until it is completely covered
7. Braise it until the tongue is completely tender, which takes three or four hours
8. From time to time add a little beef broth and squeeze in the juice of one lemon
9. When the tongue is properly tender, remove it from the pot and arrange on a platter
10. Slice it lengthwise from the back end to the front, or to the tip, but so that it remains slightly connected at the front end, and when laid out on the platter, it resembles a heart
11. Set it in a warm place
12. Strain the gravy through a sieve, pour a little over the tongue and serve the rest in a small bowl

title: Dušený zelený hrách (Braised Green Peas)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- butter
- parsley, tied together
- flour
- broth
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Braise green peas in butter with tied parsley
2. Sprinkle with flour and shake often
3. Pour in broth, add a little sugar and salt
4. Cook for a while

title: Dýně (Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- dýně
- sůl
- pepř
- máslo
- cukr
- smetana
- strouhaná žemle
- vejce
- houska

## Directions

1. Oloupej dýni, vyndej jádra, rozkrájej ji na kousky
2. Nech ji vařiti ve slané vodě
3. Vodu sceď, dýni dej na rendlík na kus másla
4. Osol, opepři a nech chvíli dusit
5. Předlož na topinkách nebo také můžeš uvařenou dýni protlačiti sýtkem
6. Přidati cukru, másla a smetany, dobře rozmíchej
7. Dej na pekáč, posyp strouhanou žemí, pomásti a nech v troubě zčervenati
8. Z této husté kaše můžeš také utvořiti malé šišky, které omoči ve vejci
9. Obal v housce a na másle neb sádle do zlatova usmaž

title: Dřišťalková Paštička (Barberry Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- barberry juice
- water

## Directions

1. Cook plenty of sugar with barberry juice, to which a little water has been added, until everything is completely thick
2. Proceed as with the previous pastes

title: Edamský sýr (Edam Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- uncollected, freshly milked milk
- rennet
- salt

## Directions

1. Uncollected, freshly milked milk is strained into a vessel, kept warm, and curdled with the help of rennet.
2. The cheese is broken several times in succession, then the vessel is covered and allowed to stand quietly for about a quarter hour, then the cheese is removed and the whey is poured off.
3. The cheese is allowed to settle, is mixed, and is pressed into curds; when it hardens, it is removed and crumbled again.
4. Cheese molds are soaked in whey, cheese is firmly pressed into them to expel the whey.
5. The cheese removed from the molds is crumbled and crumbled again and placed into molds again, which is repeated several times until the cheese hardens completely.
6. In the molds the cheese is often flipped, pressed, then placed into smaller molds, salted, flipped, and turned.
7. In a few weeks the cheese matures and is very tasty.

title: English Suchar (English Rusk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 milliliters warm milk
- 70 grams butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon good yeast
- 500 grams fine sifted flour
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Add 70 grams butter to 250 milliliters warm milk and stir until dissolved
2. Add two beaten eggs and 1 tablespoon good yeast
3. Mix everything, then add 500 grams fine sifted flour and sugar to taste
4. Mix everything again and let it rise for at least one hour
5. Thoroughly knead everything, shape into small loaves, place on a greased baking sheet and bake until red
6. Once cooled, cut the suchar into thin slices, which are then baked again on both sides on a greased baking sheet

title: Fazole (Green Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bean kernels
- Fresh butter
- Sugar
- Flour
- Sweet sour cream
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. While the bean kernels are so soft that they can be mashed between the fingers, shell them from the pods.
2. On the stove melt a piece of fresh butter with a little sugar, add the beans and let them stew covered.
3. After about twenty minutes, make a thin roux with flour, pour in sweet sour cream (hot rather than cold) and beef broth and let them stew a little more until the liquid is absorbed.
4. The beans should not be watery.
5. For two cups of beans, three spoonfuls of sour cream and one spoonful of broth are sufficient.

title: Fazolová polévka (Bean Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- small white pearl beans
- light butter roux
- beef broth or water with vegetables
- onion
- carrot
- parsley
- parsnip
- garlic
- marjoram
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook small white pearl beans until soft
2. Press them through a sieve
3. Make a light butter roux and pour beef broth or water in which you cooked vegetables (onion, carrot, parsley and parsnip) over it
4. Strain the vegetables and use only the water to thin the bean paste according to preference
5. Salt to taste and season with spices—a bit of garlic mixed with salt and a bit of marjoram

title: Fazolová Polévka (Bean Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small white pearl beans
- Beef soup broth or water in which vegetables were cooked
- Onion
- Carrot
- Parsley
- Parsnip
- Butter
- Salt
- Garlic
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Cook small white pearl beans until soft
2. Press the cooked beans through a sieve
3. Make a light butter roux
4. Pour beef soup broth or water in which vegetables were cooked into the roux
5. Cook onion, carrot, parsley and parsnip in the broth, then strain and use only the liquid
6. Thin the bean paste with the vegetable water according to taste
7. Season with salt
8. Add a small piece of garlic crushed with salt and a little marjoram

title: Fazolové Lusky (String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young string beans
- Salt water
- Butter
- White breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Shell young pods, cut off the tips, pull off the strings, cut each pod in half twice and cook them in salted water.
2. Then drain them, turn them out onto a dish, coat with butter and sprinkle with fried grated white bread.

title: Fazolové Lusky na Kyselo (String Beans with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- String beans
- Salt
- Baking soda
- 1 spoonful butter
- 1 small teaspoon sugar
- 1 large spoonful flour
- 1 spoonful vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the string beans for cooking according to the previous method, salt and cook them for an hour in salted water to which you have added a piece of baking soda the size of a pea.
2. When cooked, make a roux from one spoonful of butter, a small teaspoon of sugar and a large spoonful of flour and when it browns, add it to the beans, pour in one spoonful of vinegar and serve.

title: Fazolové Lusky se Smetanovou Omáčkou (String Beans with Cream Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- String beans
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 spoonful flour
- 2 spoonfuls fresh dill

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in the previous manner and when cooked, add to them one cup of sour cream into which you have mixed one spoonful of flour, add about two spoonfuls of finely chopped dill, let it simmer and serve.

title: Fazolový Salát (Dried Bean Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups dried beans
- cold water
- boiling water
- baking soda (one quarter teaspoon)
- salt
- one tablespoon butter
- one onion, finely chopped
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- pepper

## Directions

1. In the evening, soak two cups of beans in cold water
2. In the morning, drain the water and pour fresh boiling water over them
3. Add one quarter teaspoon of baking soda, salt, and let them cook until soft
4. During cooking, add boiling water as needed to keep the beans always covered with water
5. During cooking, stir in one tablespoon of butter
6. When soft, place on a strainer and let drain
7. Put the beans into a salad bowl
8. Sprinkle with finely chopped onion
9. Mix vinegar with water to taste and add a little sugar
10. While still warm, pour this mixture over the beans
11. Season with pepper and when cooled, serve on the table
12. This goes well with every roast

title: Faširováni holubů (Stuffed Pigeons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned pigeons
- pigeon livers
- smoked bacon
- egg yolk
- rusk
- bacon

## Directions

1. From the cleaned pigeons, cut out the breasts and thighs
2. Scrape the remaining meat from the bones with the livers and a piece of smoked bacon, chop finely
3. Rub in a mortar with a raw egg yolk and rusk, strain through a sieve
4. Spread on the thighs, lard the breasts with bacon, split them, remove the bones, cut off the wing ends

title: Faširování Holubů (Stuffed Pigeons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons, cleaned
- pigeon breasts and thighs
- remaining pigeon meat with bones
- pigeon livers
- piece of smoked bacon
- egg yolk
- bread
- butter
- spice for game sauce

## Directions

1. From cleaned pigeons, cut out the breasts and thighs; scrape the remaining meat from the bones
2. Finely chop the remaining meat with bones, livers, and a piece of smoked bacon
3. Crush this mixture in a mortar with a raw egg yolk and bread, then press through a sieve
4. Spread the mixture on the thighs; lard the breasts with bacon, halve them, remove the bones, and cut off the wing tips
5. Place everything in butter on a baking pan, cover with buttered paper and a lid, braise it
6. Finally place into a sauce prepared as for game

title: Fašírovaná kapusta (Hashed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- young leaves from ordinary or curly cabbage
- salt water
- fresh butter
- onion, finely chopped
- flour
- beef broth or roasting liquid
- ground sugar
- pepper
- ginger, grated

## Directions

1. Pluck young leaves from ordinary or curly cabbage, cook them in salted water until soft enough to mash with fingers
2. Soak in cold water, press out, and chop very finely
3. Place a piece of fresh butter on a pan, add finely chopped onion and a spoonful of flour, let it braise until brown
4. Then add a little beef broth or roasting liquid, add a little ground sugar, pepper and grated ginger, salt and let it cook a bit longer

title: Fašírovaná kapusta (Hashed Colewort)
body:
## Ingredients

- mladé lístky kapusty - obyčejné nebo kadeřavé
- voda - slaná
- voda - studená
- máslo - čerstvé
- cibule - drobně rozkrájená
- mouka - lžíce
- hovězí polévka nebo šťáva z pečeně
- cukr - tlučený
- pepř - tlučený
- zázvor - ustrouhaný

## Directions

1. Ober mladé lístky s obyčejné nebo kadeřavé kapusty
2. Nech je v slané vodě tak dlouho vařit, až je můžeš prsty rozmáčknouti
3. Omoč je ve studené vodě a vytlač
4. Na drobounko je usekej
5. Dej na kuthan kus čerstvého másla a drobně rozkrájenou cibuli
6. Přidej lžíci mouky a nech to do hnědá dusit
7. Potom přilej trochu hovězí polévky nebo šťávy z pečeně
8. Přidej trochu tlučeného cukru, pepře a ustrouhaného zázvoru
9. Osol a nech to ještě chvíli povařit

title: Fašírovaný salát (Stuffed Lettuce Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce leaves
- veal
- ham
- pork
- butter
- bacon
- onion

## Directions

1. Clean the lettuce leaves, blanch them, and place in cold water, then arrange on a platter
2. Finely chop veal and spread on the lettuce leaves
3. Sprinkle with chopped ham
4. Fry with bacon and onion in butter
5. Before serving, slice into rounds

title: Fialková Šťáva (Violet Juice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- violet petals
- boiling water
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck the colored petals of violets carefully so that not even a small green part is mixed in, clean them and place in a stone pot.
2. Pour with boiling water (use a proportionate amount: if many petals, more water is needed, but neither too much nor too little).
3. Cover the container completely and place on warm stove for at least one day, then press out in fine cloth.
4. Mix the strained juice with sugar, add lemon juice and heat everything together slowly over a gentle fire, but it must not boil.
5. As soon as a white skin appears on top, remove the container from the fire, collect the skin and foam and pour the juice into heated bottles, and only when everything has cooled, wrap with bladders and store in dry, cool places.
6. This juice must not come into any metal containers as it would completely lose its delicate blue color.

title: Fialkové Paštičky (Violet Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- violet petals
- lemon juice
- apple pulp
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine, clean, colored violet petals
2. Moisten with a few drops of lemon juice
3. Press the juice through a fine sieve
4. Add fine, sweetened apple pulp
5. Add powdered sugar and mix thoroughly
6. Cook once strongly with constant stirring

title: Fialkové pokroutky (Violet Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several handfuls of violet petals
- boiling water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take several handfuls of violet petals and clean them
2. Place them in a porcelain or stone pot
3. Pour boiling water over them, seal the pot tightly and let it sit on warm stove overnight
4. In the morning strain and press out the liquid
5. Boil the juice with sugar and prepare everything in the already known manner

title: Fine Potato Cakes (Jemné zemákové koláčky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 240 grams (16 lots) butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 195 grams (13 lots) sugar
- lemon peel, grated
- 15 grams (1 lot) bitter almonds
- 240 grams wheat flour
- 275 grams (18 lots) potato flour
- 120 grams (8 lots) raisins
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat butter
2. Gradually add egg yolks
3. Add sugar, grated lemon peel, pounded bitter almonds, wheat flour, potato flour, and raisins
4. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Oil small forms
6. Fill halfway
7. Bake slowly for half an hour

title: Fish Broth (Rybí Polévka)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Several small fish
- Butter
- Spices
- Vegetables
- Egg yolks
- Fried bread croutons
- Cooked, roasted or stewed fish

## Directions

1. If using fish for the table, place in cold water to make better broth (though the fish will taste less good)
2. Better to use several smaller fish so as not to waste a large fish
3. Diligently skim off the foam
4. After the fish has simmered for a quarter hour, strain the broth
5. Boil briefly with sardine butter, spices, and vegetables
6. Add several egg yolks and fried bread croutons
7. For richer broth, press cooked, roasted or stewed fish through a sieve into the broth

title: Flíčky s uzeniuou (Noodles with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked pork meat, well-marbled
- Dough for noodles (flour, eggs, salt)
- Broth from cooking the meat
- Lard or butter for greasing
- Milk or sour cream, 2 koflíky
- Egg yolks, 4
- Butter, new, in pieces
- Breadcrumb crust

## Directions

1. Cook well-marbled smoked pork meat until tender, then chop finely
2. Make dough as for noodles and cut into flíčky (thin noodles)
3. Cook the flíčky in the strained broth in which the meat was cooked
4. Drain them well
5. Grease a mold or shallow pan with lard or butter
6. Layer alternately flíčky and chopped smoked meat, ending with flíčky on top
7. Beat together 4 egg yolks with 2 koflíky milk or sour cream
8. Pour this mixture over the flíčky
9. Top with pieces of new butter
10. Bake slowly in the oven until golden
11. Sprinkle the breadcrumb crust on top and serve at the table

title: Flíčky s uzeným masem (Pancakes with Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 whole eggs
- 1.5 deciliters (half a tablespoon) of sour cream
- Salt
- Chives
- Smoked meat, chopped
- 6 egg yolks
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Mix well 16 whole eggs, 1.5 deciliters of sour cream, a little salt and chives.
2. Bake omelets from this mixture and cut them into strips.
3. Mix chopped smoked meat into them, add 6 egg yolks, mix everything well, pour sour cream over and bake.

title: Francouzký sýr (French Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- warm freshly milked milk (+25-27° R.)
- rennet
- salt

## Directions

1. From warmly milked milk (+25-27° R., if it does not have such temperature it must be heated) cheese is made with the help of rennet.
2. After two hours the cheese is removed and immediately placed into molds. Care is taken so that the whey can drain comfortably.
3. The cheese remains in the molds for a whole day, is flipped, salted on top, which is repeated for several days, and when the cheese is completely cold, it is removed from the molds and placed on straw mats on shelves.
4. The cheese is flipped daily. Depending on whether only warm or also collected milk is given, the cheese becomes either fattier (richer) or more crumbly (drier).

title: Francouzské cukrovinky (French Confections)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams finely pressed and sifted sugar
- 15 egg yolks
- water from orange blossoms
- 50 grams finely chopped candied orange peel
- 230 grams fine flour
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 375 grams of finely pressed and sifted sugar
2. Add 15 egg yolks and stir for at least an hour, always in one direction
3. Add water from orange blossoms
4. Add 50 grams of finely chopped candied orange peel and 230 grams of fine flour
5. Add stiff snow from egg whites
6. Mix and work thoroughly
7. Place in small molds or on a buttered baking sheet
8. Bake slowly at moderate temperature until golden

title: Francouzské piškoty (French Lady Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bowl fine granulated sugar
- six egg yolks
- water
- five egg whites
- two bowls sifted flour

## Directions

1. Beat one bowl of fine granulated sugar with six egg yolks and a little water over heat until hot and thickened
2. Remove from heat and beat until completely cooled
3. Beat five egg whites into stiff meringue
4. Slowly fold in the meringue and on the end fold in two bowls of sifted flour
5. Put into molds or make on paper into finger-length pieces
6. Sprinkle with fine sugar and bake in a hot oven until done

title: Francouzské piškoty (French Sponge Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour (equal weight to 9 eggs)
- sugar (equal weight to 9 eggs)
- 5 egg whites
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Weigh flour and sugar equal to the weight of 9 eggs
2. Beat snow from 5 egg whites
3. Mix in egg yolks, sugar, and finally flour
4. Mix briefly
5. Press through a pastry tube onto a paper-covered sheet
6. Sprinkle with sugar and bake

title: Francouzský dort (French Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound flour
- half pound fresh butter
- one-quarter pound sifted sugar
- one egg
- pinch of salt
- half small cup cold water
- preserved fruit
- prepared batter (sponge cake, almond, or chocolate)

## Directions

1. Put one pound flour on a board
2. Crumble half pound fresh butter into it
3. Add one-quarter pound sifted sugar, one egg, a pinch of salt, and half small cup cold water
4. Mix well and knead thoroughly
5. Place the dough under two plates and let it rest in a cool place for at least one hour
6. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a finger
7. Line a buttered form with it
8. Bake until half done
9. Arrange some preserved fruit around the edges
10. Fill the form to two fingers from the top with prepared batter (sponge cake, almond, or chocolate)
11. Bake slowly for one hour
12. Turn out the cake carefully
13. Sprinkle with sugar

title: French Fried Potatoes (Brambory smažené)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized raw peeled potatoes
- salt water
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut medium-sized peeled raw potatoes lengthwise into eight pieces
2. Let them lie in salt water for one hour
3. Peel the potatoes as thinly as possible; the potato under the skin is the best part
4. Heat plenty of lard in a pot as for frying doughnuts
5. Remove the potatoes from the salt water and dry them well with a cloth
6. Place them in the hot lard so they lie side by side
7. When they begin to turn red, turn them over
8. When you determine with a fork that they are soft inside, remove them with a skimmer
9. Let them drain for a minute over the pot and place on absorbent paper or a sieve to drain further
10. Place them in a heated dish and bring to the table

title: French Mustard (Hořčice po francouzsku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- brown mustard (fifteen decagrams)
- white mustard (fifteen decagrams)
- sugar (thirty decagrams, cooked brown)
- sardines (fifteen decagrams, boned)
- onion (two, finely chopped)
- good wine vinegar (about three deciliters)
- boiled seasoned must

## Directions

1. Thoroughly crush together fifteen decagrams of brown mustard and fifteen decagrams of white mustard
2. Add about thirty decagrams of sugar cooked brown
3. Add fifteen decagrams of boned sardines
4. Add two finely chopped onions
5. Mix everything with about three deciliters of good wine vinegar and boiled seasoned must
6. Strain everything into glass vessels

title: Fried Goose Liver (Pečená husí játra)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large goose liver
- flour
- butter
- lemon juice
- sauce
- breadcrumb

## Directions

1. Cut large goose liver into pieces, roll in flour and butter, fry until golden, sprinkle with lemon juice and serve with sauce. Alternatively, after cutting, salt and pepper the liver, roll in grated breadcrumb and fry in butter. Boil the butter with the juices and serve with the liver on a plate.

title: Fried Potatoes Another Way (Smažené brambory na jiný způsob)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and washed potatoes
- butter
- lard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut peeled and washed potatoes into slices
2. Let them lie in cold water for half an hour, then dry them well with a cloth
3. Place half butter and half lard in a skillet
4. Salt and pepper the potatoes and place them in the skillet and cover tightly
5. Occasionally toss them with a knife and shake the pan until all the slices are nicely browned

title: Fried Rabbit (Smažený Králík)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned rabbit
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- eggs
- grated bread roll
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Divide the well-cleaned rabbit into equal pieces, wash them and dry them well with a cloth.
2. Salt and pepper them, dust with flour, dip in beaten eggs, coat in grated bread roll and fry them in hot butter or lard until golden.
3. They go very well as a side dish with vegetables or potato mash.

title: Fruit Cakes (Ovocné koláče)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough prepared by the previous method
- apples, peaches, plums, strawberries, cherries or other fruit
- sugar

## Directions

1. From dough prepared by the previous method, you can also bake large fruit cakes either in baking dishes or on a baking sheet
2. Roll out the risen dough to about half a finger's thickness
3. Line a baking pan or baking dish with it and cover with quartered apples, halved peaches or plums, strawberries, cherries or other fruit
4. Sprinkle with sugar as needed, and if you use apples, mix the sugar with cinnamon
5. Let rise and bake

title: Fruit Pastes (Ovocné paštiky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- sugar (at least one and a half parts per one part of fruit pulp)

## Directions

1. Cut the fruit into pieces
2. Cook until completely soft
3. Place in fresh water
4. When cooled, strain so water drains better
5. Grate and press through a fine sieve
6. For one part of fruit pulp obtained this way, add at least one and a half parts of crushed sugar
7. Mix everything and cook together
8. When all watery substances have boiled off, remove
9. Place on slate tablets
10. Shape into long, round cakes
11. Allow to dry
12. Place between paper in clean vessels

title: German Cakes (Německé koláče)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as for Czech tarts
- filling of choice
- egg, beaten
- butter
- optional: currants for cheese filling, almonds for jam and poppy seed filling, cherries or sour cherries

## Directions

1. Make dough as for Czech tarts
2. Form into a full sheet of tarts one finger thick with a raised edge
3. Prick with a fork so bubbles do not form
4. Brush edges with beaten egg
5. Brush the middle with butter and fill with filling
6. Curd cheese is usually decorated with currants, jam and poppy seed with chopped almonds; the cake can also be filled in stripes or in a circle with several different fillings of cherries or sour cherries
7. Bake in a well-heated oven and slide from the pan onto paper when done

title: German Fried Potatoes (Brambory osmažené)
body:
## Ingredients

- small potatoes
- lard
- salt
- pepper
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Cook smaller potatoes until soft and let them cool, or use cold leftover potatoes
2. Peel and cut them into slices
3. Place a spoon of good lard in a skillet and let it heat
4. When quite hot, add the potatoes
5. Salt and pepper them and toss them with a knife until they turn reddish
6. Place on a plate and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley

title: Giblet Soup
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose giblets (excluding liver)
- water
- vegetables of all kinds
- salt
- dill
- butter (if goose was lean)
- rice or thin noodles

## Directions

1. Clean goose giblets except liver
2. Chop into small pieces
3. Rinse and place in water (for giblets from one goose use 1.5 liters)
4. Add vegetables of all kinds, salt, and dill
5. If the goose was lean, add a piece of butter and simmer briefly
6. Meanwhile, braise rice in butter and pour the giblet soup over it, or make thin noodles
7. In the latter case, strain the soup and add thickening
8. If there are mushrooms or cauliflower, add them with parsley

title: Glasura dortů (Cake Glaze)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- finely beaten sugar
- egg whites
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Sift finely beaten sugar through a sieve
2. Place egg whites (more or less to taste) on a deep plate
3. Mix with sugar until it turns into a paste
4. Add a little lemon juice
5. Stir and beat continuously; the longer this is done, the better the glaze
6. Spread the prepared glaze quickly onto the cake
7. Place the cake in a warm oven
8. When the glaze is completely dry, then decorate

title: Goose Blood (Husí krev)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- goose fat or butter
- goose blood
- caraway
- salt
- breadcrumb
- milk

## Directions

1. Finely chop half an onion, add goose fat or butter and simmer. To the goose blood add caraway, salt, grated breadcrumb and a little milk. Mix well, pour onto the onion and fry for ten minutes with continuous stirring. Place on a plate, surround with sliced breadcrumb rolls.

title: Goose Breast with Stuffing (Husí prsa naplněná)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose breast with bone
- breadcrumb
- goose fat
- onion
- clove
- new spice
- pepper
- marjoram
- garlic
- eggs
- beef broth
- cabbage

## Directions

1. Carefully separate the breast from the bone and remove the skin. Finely chop the meat and add 1½ grated breadcrumbs, onion fried in goose fat, 4 tablespoons goose fat, a little clove, new spice, pepper, crushed marjoram and crushed garlic. Mix everything well on a board and add two eggs. Place the mixture on the breast bone, put on a baking sheet, pour beef broth over it and roast the breasts. Either serve them whole or cut into pieces. Cabbage is usually added as a side.

title: Goose Liver (Husí játra)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- onion
- goose fat or butter
- sauce

## Directions

1. Carefully separate the goose liver from the bile, salt and pepper it (those who wish may insert a clove into each half for flavor). Finely chop half an onion and simmer it in goose fat or butter. Then add the liver, cover and turn occasionally so it cooks evenly. When tender, pour strained sauce over it. If the livers are large, they can be cut into pieces and surrounded with potatoes.

title: Goose Liver in Aspic (Husí játra v aspíku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful goose liver
- aspic
- lemon slices
- truffles
- smoked tongue

## Directions

1. Braise the beautiful goose liver in a covered baking sheet. When cooled, cut into finger-thickness slices. Grease a mold, place on ice or in cold water, pour a little aspic. When set, line the bottom with lemon slices, truffles or smoked tongue, sprinkle with aspic until everything sets. Place the cut goose liver on top, fill the mold with aspic and after setting, turn out onto a plate and decorate.

title: Goose Liver with Truffles (Husí játra s lanýži)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large goose liver
- truffles
- salt
- bacon
- beef broth
- wine
- butter
- butter paste

## Directions

1. Split large goose liver in half, wash and dry it. Pierce with truffles, salt, wrap in bacon, pour broth and a glass of wine over it and braise for an hour in butter. Meanwhile braise the truffles in broth, wine and butter, surround with liver and cover with butter paste.

title: Goose Sausage (Jaternice z husy)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose meat
- goose fat
- bread soaked in milk
- lemon zest
- onion
- salt
- garlic
- clove
- pepper
- new spice
- ginger
- marjoram
- beef broth
- pork intestines

## Directions

1. Cut meat from the goose bones, remove the skin and chop it. If too fatty, reduce the fat to half a kilogram. Add 3 pieces of bread soaked in milk and squeezed, lemon zest, onion fried in goose fat, salt, crushed garlic, a little clove, pepper, new spice, ginger and crushed marjoram. Thin with beef broth, stuff into pork intestines and either boil then fry in goose fat or smoke them.

title: Grahamův Chléb (Graham Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups sweet milk
- two-thirds cup black syrup (molasses)
- salt to taste
- one teaspoon baking soda dissolved in water
- one quart graham flour

## Directions

1. Put two cups of sweet milk in a bowl and add two-thirds cup of black syrup and a little salt
2. Add one teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in water and mix well
3. Add one quart of flour, beat well with a spoon or beater
4. Pour into a greased baking pan and bake at a moderate temperature for approximately three-quarters of an hour

title: Grape Ice Cream (Zmrzlina z vinných hroznů)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully developed but not completely ripe grapes
- Water
- One to two spoonfuls of dissolved and pressed gum tragacanth
- Sugar
- Elder flowers

## Directions

1. Pick berries from fully developed but not completely ripe grapes
2. Press them
3. Dilute slightly with water
4. Add one to two spoonfuls of dissolved and pressed gum tragacanth
5. Sweeten everything
6. Scald elder flowers and add the obtained juice to the ice cream, giving it a special nutmeg flavor
7. Proceed as with other ice creams

title: Green Beans (Zelené boby)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- green young beans
- alum (a small amount added to soft water)
- sugar (cooked)
- water

## Directions

1. Take green young beans
2. Clean and cut into halves (if thick) or into pieces
3. Wash in soft water with a small amount of alum added
4. Crush with fingers until completely softened
5. Remove and place in fresh water
6. Place in a dish
7. Pour with diluted cooked sugar
8. For three days, always strain off the sugar, boil it, and pour it back over the beans when cooled
9. On the fourth day, boil the sugar to the second stage and cook the beans in it
10. On the fifth day, strain the sugar again and boil it to the fourth stage, add the beans to it and boil together
11. Skim the foam
12. When the preserve has cooled, place in containers

title: Green Currants with Sugar (Zelený ryvíz cukrovanělý)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large, selected, not completely ripe redcurrants
- sugar, dissolved and later boiled to varying thicknesses

## Directions

1. Take a large, selected, not completely ripe bunch of redcurrants with several small bunches tied together.
2. Place on a dish and pour boiling water over them, covering with a lid; repeat this several times until they soften completely.
3. Remove them and place on a sieve to dry.
4. Place on a dish and pour melted sugar over them.
5. Cover with paper and let sit undisturbed.
