6. On the second day, drain the sugar, boil it thicker, add the redcurrants, and heat until it begins to boil.
7. Repeat the same process on the third day.
8. Adding the redcurrants to the sugar must be done very carefully so the berries do not bruise.
9. On the fourth day, drain the sugar again, boil it thick, add the redcurrants, and let it come to a gentle boil.
10. Skim off the foam and place the preserve in containers; when cooled, seal with a bladder.

title: Green Figs (Zelené fíky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, large, green, not fully ripe figs
- pure soft water
- salt
- spinach
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, large, green, not fully ripe figs
2. Pierce several times with a fine knife
3. Place in clean soft water and cook slowly
4. Remove and place again in fresh water
5. On the second day, place again in fresh water on warm stove, salt them, add a little spinach and cook slowly until the figs are completely soft
6. Remove and place in fresh water and when completely cooled, place on sieves to dry quickly
7. Dried figs place on a dish, pour over with thinly dissolved sugar
8. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, skim foam, add the figs and cook with the sugar several times
9. Pour again onto a dish, cover with paper and let sit quietly
10. For two more days, always cook the drained sugar thicker and thicker and always add the figs to cook together with it several times until finally the sugar is cooked in pearls, add the figs, cook several times again, skim foam and place cooled in jars

title: Green Glaze (Zelené polévání)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- egg white
- sugar
- spinach juice

## Directions

1. Mix egg white, sifted sugar, and pressed spinach juice
2. Thicken until the consistency of sour cream
3. Spread on pastry and dry

title: Green Gooseberries (Zelené srstky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, large, not completely ripe gooseberries
- sugar, freshly cooked and later boiled

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, large, but not completely ripe gooseberries.
2. Clean them and carefully remove the seeds with a stick.
3. Place in fresh water and press well until they soften.
4. Heat the water with the gooseberries, but do not boil; when the gooseberries rise to the top, remove from heat and cover the container so they soften away from the stove.
5. When cooled, place in cold water and then into cleaned boiled sugar.
6. Cover again and let sit quietly.
7. On the second day, drain the sugar, cook it separately, and when well reduced, add the gooseberries and continue cooking.

title: Green Preserved Plums (Zelené zavařené švestky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, large green plums with soft pits
- Soft water
- Alum (small amount)
- Fresh water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, large green plums where the pit has not yet hardened
2. Pierce several times with a narrow knife
3. Cook in soft water to which a little alum has been added, until soft
4. Transfer to fresh water and let sit for two days
5. Pour off the water every six hours and replace with fresh water to remove all sourness from the plums
6. Place in a container and cover with strained sugar
7. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it again, skim the foam, and when cooled, pour over the preserves; repeat this for two days
8. On the fourth day, add more sugar and cook to the second degree, add the plums and preserve with it
9. On the fifth day, add even more sugar, cook to the sixth degree, cook the plums with it, skim the foam, and place the preserves in containers

title: Grog
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- sugar
- good rum or arack
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. To a quart of boiling water, dissolve three-quarters of a pound of sugar
2. Add a pint of good rum or arack
3. Put on fire and when hot enough, pour into glasses
4. You can add a little lemon juice if desired
5. If the grog is too strong, you can add a little hot water

title: Guláš (Goulash - Alternate Recipe)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or pork (front or back)
- 30 grams butter or rendered beef fat (2 lots)

## Directions

1. Cut meat into pieces
2. Add 30 grams butter or rendered beef fat

title: Guláš (Goulash)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fatty meat from ribs
- butter
- chopped onion
- caraway seed
- flour
- fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into cubes, salt it, and braise with chopped onion in butter
2. Add caraway seed tied in a piece of cloth
3. After half an hour, dust with flour, stir the sauce, and let it simmer briefly
4. Remove the caraway seed before serving
5. Arrange fried potatoes around the meat on a plate

title: Guláš (Goulash)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- front or back beef or pork meat
- 30 grams butter or rendered beef fat
- 1 large onion or half of a second small one
- pepper
- flour
- water or beef broth
- fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut meat into pieces
2. Heat butter or fat with chopped onion until softened
3. Add chopped meat, season with pepper, and braise covered until the onion turns red and meat softens
4. If meat becomes dry, add water or beef broth
5. Transfer meat carefully to a separate plate
6. Add a tablespoon of flour to the sauce, stir, braise briefly on the stove, and strain over the meat
7. Usually fried potatoes are added

title: Guláš (Goulash)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram beef from ribs
- onion (chopped)
- butter
- caraway seed
- flour
- potatoes (fried)

## Directions

1. Cut meat into cubes, salt it
2. Braise with chopped onion on butter
3. Add caraway seed tied in a piece of cloth
4. After half an hour, dust with flour and mix sauce
5. Simmer briefly
6. Before serving, remove caraway and border with fried potatoes

title: Guláš (Gulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds meat from the shoulder, cut into small cubes
- two tablespoons butter
- one large onion, finely chopped
- 4 cloves
- 8 grains of allspice
- a slice of lemon
- bay leaf
- two tablespoons vinegar
- one tablespoon sugar
- salt
- one tablespoon flour
- water
- 2-3 tablespoons good tomato ketchup
- a pinch of paprika
- fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of meat from the shoulder into small cubes.
2. Put two tablespoons of butter in a pot and one large finely chopped onion; when it begins to turn yellow, add the meat, salt it.
3. Add 4 cloves, 8 grains of allspice, a slice of lemon, a bay leaf, two tablespoons of vinegar, and one tablespoon of sugar.
4. Cover and let it braise.
5. When the meat is tender, dust it with one tablespoon of flour; if it has dried out too much, add water, 2-3 tablespoons of good tomato ketchup, and a pinch of paprika.
6. Mix, let it simmer, and then place on a platter.
7. Serve with fried potatoes.

title: Halušky (povézky) (Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- hard rolls
- milk
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- grated roll
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut hard rolls into 4 or 5 pieces, place one next to the other and pour milk over them
2. When the milk is absorbed, take the rolls before they become soaked, salt on both sides, coat in flour, dip in egg and roll in grated roll
3. Bake on butter until golden
4. Serves as a fast food dish with carrots, lentils, etc.

title: Hamburský Steak (Hamburg Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef from the hindquarters
- one beef kidney fat
- one onion, grated
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- half lard
- half butter

## Directions

1. Chop two pounds of beef from the hindquarters finely, preferably with a meat grinder. In cities you can get it chopped from the butcher, but usually those are various scraps, whereas when you chop it yourself, you make ground meat from whichever part you wish.
2. You can add a piece of beef kidney fat and one grated onion.
3. Salt and pepper it and knead well with your hands, then make small patties about the size of a finger and coat in flour.
4. Put half lard and half butter in a pan and when it begins to brown, place the patties in the fat and let them fry nicely on both sides until golden brown.

title: Hanover Cake (Hanoverský koláč)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup butter
- three egg yolks
- one tablespoon ground sugar with vanilla
- one cup cream or sweet milk
- salt
- two cups flour
- a piece of pressed yeast or dry yeast soaked and drained
- curd cheese or poppy seed filling
- egg yolk for brushing
- sugar mixed with vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream half a cup of butter with three egg yolks
2. Add one tablespoon ground sugar mixed with vanilla, one cup cream or sweet milk, and salt
3. Mix in two cups flour and a piece of pressed yeast or dry yeast that has been soaked and drained, mixing well
4. Butter a baking sheet and spread half the dough on it
5. Brush with curd cheese or poppy seed filling
6. Cover with the remaining half of the dough and press the edges together with your fingers
7. Let rise for a longer time
8. Brush the cake with egg yolk and bake in the oven until golden
9. When cool, cut into squares and sprinkle generously with sugar mixed with vanilla

title: Head Cabbage (Hlávkové zelí)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage of any color
- Wine vinegar
- Pepper
- Bacon
- White sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage, remove the cores and finely chop the leaves
2. Place on a baking sheet with wine vinegar, pepper, bacon and a piece of white sugar
3. Cook everything together
4. Add the cabbage, mix, press down on a plate and pepper the surface to taste

title: Head Cabbage (Zelí hlávkové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh cabbage
- Caraway
- Salt
- Vinegar mixed with water
- Onion
- Bacon
- Pork or goose fat
- Flour
- Beef broth or water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Chop fresh cabbage very finely, place in a pot and add caraway, salt and vinegar mixed with water
2. When softened, set aside
3. Chop onion finely, add chopped bacon and pork or goose fat, and braise so it does not brown
4. Add flour according to the amount of cabbage, fry until it turns red, then add the cabbage
5. Pour in beef broth or water and simmer briefly
6. If darker cabbage is desired, burn a piece of sugar

title: Head Cabbage Another Way (Zelí hlávkové - jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage head
- Butter
- Sugar
- Beef broth
- Caraway
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop or shred the cabbage head and place on a baking sheet
2. Mix with butter, sugar, beef broth, caraway, pepper and vinegar, stirring so that everything becomes equally soft

title: Hlávka na kyselo (Sour Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- hlávka - pěkná tvrdá
- voda - vařící
- cibule - dvě velké
- kmín - přebraný
- sádlo - asi dvě lžíce
- voda - trochu
- brambor - jeden syrový prostřední
- ocet

## Directions

1. Vezmi pěknou tvrdou hlávku, očisti ji a rozkrájej na čtvrtky
2. Všechny košťály náležitě vykrájej
3. Hlávku na drobné nudličky zkrouži
4. Dej zelí do vařící vody, aby ho několikrát var přešel
5. Pečlivě oceď, nebo voda z něho je hořká
6. Usmaž dvě velké cibule, ale jen do žlutá
7. Dej zelí do polévaného hrnku a osol
8. Přidej špetku přebraného kmínu, usmaženou cibulku a asi dvě lžíce pěkného sádla
9. Dej ho zvolna dusit, je-li to třeba, přilej trochu vody
10. Když se zelí půl hodiny dobře dusilo, vezmi jeden syrový brambor střední velikosti
11. Oškrab ho, sestrouhej a rozmíchej asi v koflíku vody
12. Vlej to do zelí a dobře zamíchej
13. Udělej ze lžíce sádla bledou jíšku, kterou také tam zamícháš
14. Dle libosti přidej octa

title: Hlávkový salát (Lettuce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce heads
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- good table oil

## Directions

1. Remove the outer, unsightly leaves from lettuce heads, clean them well, rinse, cut into quarters and salt
2. Arrange on a dish with the hearts in the middle
3. Pour over with vinegar mixed with water and a little good table oil if desired

title: Hlávkový salát s vejci (Lettuce with Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice white or red-speckled lettuce heads
- eggs
- good fresh butter
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. From nice white or red-speckled lettuce heads, remove the outer leaves, cut the head into quarters
2. Rinse several times in clean water and let drain
3. Rub two hard-boiled egg yolks with three spoonfuls of good fresh heated and carefully strained butter, salt it and add a little ground pepper and several spoonfuls of wine vinegar, mix well
4. Pour this onto the prepared lettuce, mix it well, arrange on a salad dish and top with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters

title: Hlávkový salát se smetanou (Lettuce Salad with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce heads
- thick sour cream
- wine vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- chives, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Rinse and cut the lettuce and put it in a dish
2. To a pint of thick sour cream add three spoonfuls of wine vinegar, a little salt, a pinch of pepper and a little finely chopped chives, mix well
3. Pour this over the lettuce, mix well, arrange it and pour remaining cream over top

title: Hlávkový salát se špekem (Lettuce with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- sugar
- bacon

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the lettuce, squeeze it out, arrange on a dish and salt it
2. Mix water with vinegar according to taste and add a little sugar, pour this over the lettuce and mix
3. Meanwhile, render bacon cut into small cubes, when rendered pour it over the lettuce and serve immediately at table

title: Hnědá omáčka cibulová (Brown Onion Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onions, sprinkled with flour
- broth
- vinegar
- sugar
- white wine

## Directions

1. In butter, partially braise the onions sprinkled with flour
2. Add broth, vinegar, sugar and a little white wine
3. Mix everything carefully and simmer
4. When fully cooked, strain through a sieve

title: Hnědá rybí polévka s knedlíčky (Brown Fish Soup with Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- carrot
- onion
- parsley
- several pieces of fish
- allspice
- ginger
- dill
- clove
- lean broth or parsley water
- salt
- sugar
- fish fillet
- milk
- bread roll
- egg yolks
- majoram
- garlic

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a pan and add carrot, onion, parsley, several pieces of fish, allspice, ginger, dill, and clove, all crushed together, and let it braise until brown, being careful not to scorch it
2. Pour lean broth or parsley water over it, salt it and let it simmer for about half an hour
3. Strain the broth, roast a piece of sugar until brown, add it to the broth, and if it is bitter, add a piece of white sugar, some crushed dill and pour it into a bowl over the quenelles
4. For the quenelles: chop fish fillet finely, soak a bread roll in milk and let it cook with a piece of butter on coals into a paste
5. When cooled, add four egg yolks, the chopped fish and a little salt, and if desired, a piece of garlic and some majoram
6. Mix well or pound in a mortar and form small quenelles with two spoons
7. Grease a bowl with butter, place the quenelles on it, cover them with paper and let them cook in steam over boiling water
8. Remove them and pour the broth over them
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Hnědá rybí polévka tlučená (Bisque of Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- clarified butter
- fish
- bread roll
- egg yolks
- white flour
- lean broth or parsley water
- salt
- dill

## Directions

1. Fry three or four pieces of fish in clarified butter with half a bread roll sliced into strips
2. Cut up the fish, remove the bones and pound them together with the fried bread roll and three hard-boiled egg yolks in a mortar
3. Put this into a pot, make a light roux from butter and white flour, dissolve it in lean broth or parsley water, pour it into the pot with the pounded fish
4. Pour in enough broth, salt it, season with dill, let it simmer for a quarter hour and strain through a fine sieve over the fried sliced bread roll
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Hnědý Chléb (Brown Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart cornmeal
- two cups sifted rye flour
- one cup black syrup (New Orleans molasses)
- one small teaspoon baking soda dissolved in hot water
- sour milk as needed
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put one quart of cornmeal and two cups of sifted rye flour in a bowl and add one cup of black syrup
2. Add one small teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in hot water and enough sour milk so that the dough is somewhat thicker than for pancakes
3. Season with salt and mix everything well
4. Pour it into a baking pan and bake at a moderate temperature for four hours

title: Hnědý dort (Brown Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams butter
- at least 6 beaten egg yolks
- 70 grams peeled ground almonds
- 250 grams sugar
- thin lemon zest finely cut
- a little ground cinnamon
- stiff egg white snow
- 200 grams fine flour
- butter for greasing
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 125 grams butter
2. Add at least 6 beaten egg yolks
3. Add 70 grams peeled ground almonds, 250 grams sugar, finely cut thin lemon zest, a little ground cinnamon, stiff egg white snow, and 200 grams fine flour
4. Beat and work everything together thoroughly
5. Pour into baking forms greased with butter and dusted with fine breadcrumbs
6. Bake slowly
7. Sprinkle with sugar and finely cut almonds

title: Hoblovačky (Shaved Wafers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams sugar
- vanilla
- lemon peel, grated from 1/2 lemon
- 18 dekagrams flour
- fennel
- eggs

## Directions

1. Beat 9 dekagrams sugar with vanilla
2. Add the grated peel from 1/2 lemon on a grater
3. Add 18 dekagrams flour and fennel
4. Add enough eggs to form a batter-like dough
5. Grease a baking sheet and pour oblong strips onto it
6. While hot, wrap around a roller and dry in the oven until crisp

title: Holandská omáčka (Hollandaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of flour
- a little water
- six egg yolks or three whole eggs
- one quarter pound of fresh butter
- several grains of pepper
- sufficient amount of pure fish broth or parsley water
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Mix in a cup one spoon of flour with a little water
2. Beat into it six egg yolks or three whole eggs
3. Add one quarter pound of fresh butter, several grains of pepper and sufficient amount of pure fish broth or parsley water
4. Stir constantly over the fire until it begins to rise
5. Add juice of half a lemon and strain the sauce
6. This sauce is served with fish and must be prepared just before use; if it must stand longer, it is placed in a pot in hot water

title: Holandský sýr (Dutch Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- warm freshly milked milk
- rennet
- warm water
- salt

## Directions

1. Warm, freshly milked milk is strained, rennet is added to it, it is curdled, and the cheese is then broken and cut abundantly, but always allowed to settle again, the whey is poured off.
2. When all the whey is poured off, warm water is poured under the cheese; the more water is added, the more crumbly and drier the cheese becomes, which dries quickly and can be kept longer.
3. Cheese that was little watered is more delicious but does not last as long. The water is poured off in about ten minutes, the cheese is removed, broken, worked through, and allowed to settle, then placed into molds, salted, flipped, and placed to ripen.

title: Holoubata nadívaná (Stuffed Squabs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- squab
- 15 gr. butter
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- clove flower
- salt
- rusk
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the squab, draw it carefully so it does not tear, cut off the head and legs, place in cold water
2. For the stuffing: mix 15 gr. butter, one whole egg, one egg yolk, clove flower and salt
3. Grate rusk and add as much as needed
4. Fill the crop with stuffing and tie with white thread
5. Place on a baking dish, add water, roast first on the back then on the breast

title: Holoubata se zeleným hráškem (Squabs with Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-cleaned squabs
- butter
- salt
- flour
- beef broth
- young green peas
- sugar
- clove

## Directions

1. Cut the well-cleaned squabs into quarters
2. Place them in a pot on a piece of butter, salt them and let them braise for half an hour
3. Dust them with two spoonfuls of flour and mix with them
4. After a while, pour two bowls of beef broth and make a smooth sauce
5. Then remove the squabs and strain the sauce
6. Add a quart of young green peas to it, a piece of sugar and a little clove
7. Let it braise for a while, then put the squabs back in it and let everything braise until tender
8. Arrange the squabs in the middle of a dish and attractively surround them with peas

title: Holub divoký (Wild Pigeon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild pigeon
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon peel
- pepper
- allspice berries
- vinegar
- wine
- water
- bacon strips

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the pigeon.
2. Prepare a marinade with chopped onion, thyme, bay leaf, lemon peel, pepper, and allspice berries.
3. Mix equal parts vinegar, wine, and water for the marinade.
4. Marinate the pigeon until the next day.
5. Wrap the pigeon in bacon strips.
6. Roast until it turns red.
7. Either strain the sauce and serve the chopped pigeon on a plate with sauce poured over it, with pastries or lemon cakes around it, or prepare the sauce as described in the following recipe.

title: Holub divoký - jiný způsob (Wild Pigeon - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- marinated wild pigeon
- red roux
- marinade from the pigeon
- burnt sugar
- caramelized potato

## Directions

1. Remove the marinated pigeon from its marinade.
2. Make a red roux and add it to the marinade sauce.
3. Bring to a boil.
4. Add a piece of burnt sugar.
5. Strain everything.
6. Cut the pigeon into quarters and place in the sauce.
7. Let simmer on the coals briefly.
8. Serve surrounded by caramelized potatoes.

title: Holubi (Stuffed Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- young pigeons
- gizzards and livers
- fresh parsley
- bread roll soaked in milk
- fresh butter
- ground clove
- pepper
- eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean young pigeons well and cut off the heads
2. Gut them and lift the skin on the breasts as far as you can
3. Chop the gizzards and livers with a little fresh parsley and bread roll soaked in milk
4. Put this in a bowl and mix it with a piece of fresh butter
5. Season with ground clove and pepper
6. Add two eggs, salt it and mix well
7. Fill the birds with this stuffing
8. Tie the neck with thread so the stuffing does not run out
9. Salt the pigeons and place them on a roasting pan
10. Put a piece of butter under them and a little broth or water
11. Let them roast, basting them frequently with butter
12. When they are tender, sprinkle them with grated bread roll crust and pour the roasted juices over them

title: Holubi na divoko (Wild-Style Pigeons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young pigeons, cleaned
- bacon
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Wrap cleaned young pigeons in bacon.
2. Place on a baking sheet and roast until brown.
3. Remove and take off the bacon.
4. Pour sweet cream over the roasted pigeons.
5. Roast everything again.

title: Holubi s chřestem a podpouštkou (Pigeons with Asparagus and Sub-Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- butter
- raw ham
- onion
- asparagus
- good broth or cooking water
- salt
- asparagus tips
- roux
- nutmeg
- egg yolks
- bread
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Quarter the pigeons and fry in butter
2. In a small baking dish place raw ham cut into cubes, onion and thick hard ends of asparagus, braise everything for a few minutes in butter
3. Pour good broth or cooking water over, salt and simmer covered slowly for an hour with the pigeons
4. Remove the pigeons, keep warm over hot water
5. Cook the asparagus cut into pieces in the broth
6. Thicken the sauce with roux, season with nutmeg, beat in two egg yolks and pour over the pigeons, add cooked asparagus, bread cut into cubes and fried
7. In addition to asparagus, cauliflower may also be used

title: Holubi s máslovou omáčkou (Pigeons with Butter Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- vegetables as for chicken
- butter
- dried mushrooms or rusk or lemon juice
- small dumplings, nuky, or cauliflower

## Directions

1. Cut the pigeon into quarters
2. Add the same vegetables as for chicken and butter, braise until tender
3. Allow to cool, strain the pale roux through a sieve into the broth
4. Chop some dried mushrooms or add rusk or lemon juice, or make small dumplings, nuky, or add cauliflower
5. Simmer all together with the pigeon

title: Holubi s rýží (Pigeons with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- 3 dekagrams butter
- clove flower
- parsley root
- piece of celery
- piece of cabbage
- rice
- smoked meat

## Directions

1. Pluck the squabs, singe them, draw them, place on a baking dish with 3 dekagrams butter, clove flower, half parsley root, piece of celery and piece of cabbage
2. Braise on butter until the squabs are tender, being careful not to damage them
3. Remove the squabs from the broth
4. Pour this broth into rinsed rice, braise it
5. Place the pigeons in it for a moment, or place each separately
6. In that case, the rice may be sprinkled with finely chopped smoked meat

title: Holubí s podpouštkou (Pigeons with Sub-Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- butter
- flour
- strong bouillon
- onion
- thyme
- parsley
- lemon rind
- varied spices
- egg yolks
- white wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Kill the squabs one to two days before preparation, pluck, clean, wash and quarter them
2. With the hearts, gizzards and livers, braise slowly in boiling butter for about ten minutes
3. Salt the pigeons, dust with flour and pour strong bouillon (clear good broth) over them, to which onion, thyme, parsley, lemon rind and varied spices are added
4. Simmer and braise everything slowly
5. Remove the pigeons, strain the sauce, beat in several egg yolks, a little white wine, simmer all, add lemon juice

title: Houbová polévka (Mushroom Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms (hříbky)
- A piece of butter
- Pepper
- Fresh parsley
- Water
- All kinds of vegetables
- Roux (bledá jíška)
- Salt
- 2 egg yolks
- Fried bread

## Directions

1. Clean and slice the mushrooms and sauté them on a baking sheet with nothing else for a while
2. Add a piece of butter and pepper
3. Braise with chopped parsley
4. Cook all vegetables in water, add roux and salt
5. Add the mushrooms with 2 egg yolks
6. Pour over fried bread

title: Houby s vejci (Mushrooms with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and sliced mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- caraway seeds
- pepper
- fresh parsley, chopped
- eggs

## Directions

1. Let cleaned and sliced mushrooms stew in butter with salt and caraway seeds; when the water has evaporated from them, add butter, a pinch of pepper and a little chopped fresh parsley and finally mix it with several eggs.
2. You can also put scrambled eggs in the middle of a platter and surround them with stewed mushrooms.
3. You can also put the eggs in a shallow pan at the same time with the mushrooms and seasonings; this is a fresher method and many prefer it better.

title: Housková kaše pro děti (Bread Porridge for Children)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- hard white rolls
- boiling water
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Finely grate hard white rolls
2. Mix with a little boiling water
3. Add a small amount of butter and a pinch of sugar
4. With constant stirring and gentle heating, make a soft porridge

title: Housková Nádivka (Bread Roll Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- parsley or garlic
- bread rolls
- milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat butter with two eggs
2. Mix with finely chopped parsley or garlic
3. Grate two bread rolls or soak them in milk
4. Season with salt
5. Beat finely
6. Use for stuffing veal breasts or poultry

title: Housky (Twists)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough for rolls
- 1 egg
- poppy seeds, caraway seeds and salt

## Directions

1. You can make twists from the same dough as rolls.
2. Divide the risen dough into small pieces, roll them into longer pieces and always braid 3 pieces together into one twist.
3. Let them rise, then brush them with beaten egg, sprinkle thickly with unground poppy seeds, and let them bake.
4. You can also sprinkle them with salt and caraway seeds or poppy seeds mixed with salt.

title: Housky (Twists)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough (same as for rohlíky)
- egg
- poppy seeds
- salt
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Divide leavened dough into small pieces
2. Roll them into longer pieces
3. Braid three pieces together to form one houska
4. Let them rise
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Sprinkle thickly with unbeaten poppy seeds
7. Bake
8. Alternatively, sprinkle with salt and caraway, or poppy seeds mixed with salt

title: Hovězí drštky (Beef Tripe)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Mixed offal pieces
- Salt
- Brown roux
- Pepper
- Marjoram
- Garlic
- Green parsley
- Water

## Directions

1. Mix together pieces of various offal, clean them well
2. Cook in salted water for three-quarters of an hour
3. Drain the water, add fresh water, salt and cook until tender
4. Drain the broth and add plenty of brown roux, pepper, marjoram and garlic
5. Cook everything together and strain
6. Place the tripe in cold water to cool, then slice into strips
7. Sprinkle with chopped green parsley
8. Place in the prepared sauce and cook for another quarter hour

title: Hovězí dušené s kořením (Beef à la Mode)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon cut into thin strips
- ground clove
- pepper
- salt
- beef meat, trimmed
- lard
- parsley
- onion
- carrot
- several bay leaves
- wine
- braised carrot or cabbage
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into thin strips and roll them in ground clove, pepper, and salt.
2. Thread them properly through trimmed meat.
3. Place it in a pot with heated lard, parsley, onion, and carrot.
4. Add several bay leaves and place it on the fire.
5. When the meat begins to brown, turn it, add a little ground pepper, clove, and allspice.
6. Pour in a little wine, cover it, and let it braise on low heat for about three hours.
7. Then remove the meat to a platter, pour its own juice over it, and serve garnished with braised carrot or cabbage, or decorated with lemon slices.

title: Hovězí jazyk s křenem (Beef Tongue with Horseradish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- salt water
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Boil tongue in salt water
2. While still warm, peel it
3. Cut into slices and serve hot with horseradish
4. Smoked tongue is prepared the same way

title: Hovězí jazyk s polskou omáčkou (Beef Tongue with Polish Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter or fresh lard
- One tablespoon of refined sugar
- One small glass of wine
- A little vinegar
- Meat broth
- Twelve peppercorns
- New spice
- Clove
- Small bay leaf
- A piece of lemon peel
- Salt
- A handful of large picked raisins
- Two ounces of peeled almonds

## Directions

1. Make a roux from butter or fresh lard, add one tablespoon of refined sugar until it browns well
2. Add to this one small glass of wine, a little vinegar and as much meat broth as needed to make a thick gravy
3. Add twelve peppercorns, new spice, clove, a small bay leaf, and a piece of chopped lemon peel
4. Salt and let simmer for a quarter hour, then strain
5. Add a handful of large picked raisins and two ounces of peeled almonds sliced into strips
6. If the gravy is too thick, add a little meat broth, salt to taste and let simmer a little longer
7. Peel the cooked beef tongue, slice it into thick rounds, place in a deep platter and pour the prepared gravy over it

title: Hovězí kotletky (Beef Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from ribs
- 375 grams beef kidney fat (to 1.5 kilograms meat)
- 2 soaked rolls
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- rib bone
- butter
- grated breadcrumbs
- onion
- beef broth
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut meat from ribs and mince
2. Mix with kidney fat, 2 soaked rolls, 2 eggs, salt and pepper
3. Form cutlets from the mixture with a piece of rib bone inserted
4. Dip in butter and roll in grated breadcrumbs
5. Brown chopped onion on butter
6. Add broth and place cutlets in it, cover well and braise
7. When done, simmer remaining juices with lemon and add sauce to cutlets

title: Hovězí ledvinky (Beef Kidney)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef kidney
- cold water
- butter
- cumin
- pepper
- one onion, finely chopped
- garlic clove with butter, ground together
- flour
- several tablespoons good beef broth or calf roast gravy
- salt

## Directions

1. Place beef kidney in cold water and leave it for about an hour to soak thoroughly.
2. Cut it into thin slices.
3. Put a piece of butter, a little cumin and pepper on a board, with one finely chopped onion, or a clove of garlic with butter ground together.
4. Now add the sliced kidney and let it braise.
5. Then salt it, dust with a tablespoon of flour, mix it in, add several tablespoons of good beef broth or calf roast gravy, and let it braise until the kidneys are sufficiently tender.

title: Hovězí ledvinky se smetanovou omáčkou (Beef Kidney with Cream Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef kidney
- cold water
- 2-3 tablespoons vinegar
- a piece of lemon peel
- bay leaf
- 6 grains of allspice
- 4 cloves
- onion, sliced
- one tablespoon butter
- salt
- one cup good, thick sour cream
- one tablespoon flour
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Let the kidney soak for an hour, then cut it into slices.
2. Place in a pot and pour cold water over it and bring to a boil.
3. Skim off the abundant foam that rises; then salt it.
4. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vinegar, a piece of lemon peel, a bay leaf, 6 grains of allspice, 4 cloves, sliced onion, and one tablespoon of butter.
5. Cover and let it cook.
6. When the kidneys are tender, take a bowl of good, thick sour cream, add one tablespoon of flour, mix it well, pour it into the kidneys, and let it simmer a little longer.
7. When removing from heat, add one more egg yolk and mix well.

title: Hovězí maso na kyselo (Beef with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds beef meat
- water
- two tablespoons vinegar
- a piece of lemon peel
- half an onion, finely chopped
- 5-6 grains of allspice
- 4-5 cloves
- bay leaf
- salt
- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon sugar
- one heaping tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Cook about two pounds of beef meat with all the vegetables, or use leftover meat.
2. Cut it into neat pieces and pour water over it until covered.
3. Add two tablespoons of vinegar, a piece of lemon peel, half a finely chopped onion, 5-6 grains of allspice, 4-5 cloves, a bay leaf, and salt.
4. Cover and let it simmer for a while.
5. Prepare a roux from two tablespoons of butter, add one tablespoon of sugar and when the sugar browns, add one heaping tablespoon of flour and let it roast until brown.
6. Mix the roux into the meat and serve.
7. Whoever wants it more sour can add more vinegar to taste, and whoever wants it sweeter can add sugar.

title: Hovězí maso na kyselo (Beef with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beef
- vegetables
- water
- vinegar
- lemon zest
- onion
- allspice berries
- cloves
- bay leaf
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook about 2 pounds of beef with all the vegetables, or use leftover meat
2. Slice it into neat pieces and pour water over them to cover
3. Add 2 spoonfuls of vinegar, a piece of lemon zest, half a finely chopped onion, 5-6 allspice berries, 4-5 cloves, a bay leaf, and salt
4. Cover and let simmer for a while

title: Hovězí maso na kyselo (Sour Beef)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram beef brisket
- vegetables as for beef broth
- pepper
- vinegar
- piece of burnt sugar
- roux
- lemon juice
- thyme
- bay leaf
- bezinky or black gingerbread

## Directions

1. Boil beef with all vegetables used for beef broth, pepper, and vinegar
2. Cut cooked meat into neat pieces and strain the broth
3. Add burnt sugar, roux as much as needed, lemon juice, thyme, and bay leaf to the broth
4. If darker color is needed, add bezinky or black gingerbread
5. Strain over meat laid on a platter

title: Hovězí maso s paprikou (Beef with Paprika)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram beef
- 1/4 kilogram pork
- onion
- paprika to taste
- flour
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Cut meat into pieces
2. Chop and soften the onion
3. Add meat, season with paprika to the tip of a knife and flour as much as fits on a measuring spoon
4. Braise until meat softens
5. If there is little sauce, add beef broth as needed

title: Hovězí na způsob svíčkové (Beef Tenderloin Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds beef without bone
- 1 pound bacon
- onion
- allspice berries
- ginger
- pepper
- bay leaves
- flour
- water
- lemon juice
- lemon zest
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean 4 pounds of beef thoroughly, trimming fat and connective tissue
2. Cut 1 pound of bacon into noodle-like strips and pierce the meat with them
3. Place one larger onion, a few allspice berries, a piece of ginger, pepper, and two bay leaves in a baking dish, then place the meat in it
4. Salt the meat, dust with flour, and pour over with two spoonfuls of water
5. Place in the oven, basting it frequently
6. When the meat is roasted, place on a serving dish and slice into cutlets
7. Add a little lemon juice and finely chopped lemon zest to the gravy, and sweeten it slightly to make it somewhat sweet
8. Pour this over the cutlets or serve the gravy separately

title: Hovězí pečené po americku (Roast Beef, American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from the loin
- salt
- pepper
- fresh butter
- hot water
- onion

## Directions

1. Roasted beef is best when it can be roasted in a larger piece, 6 pounds or more; for a small family, 4 pounds (from two ribs) is sufficient
2. Have the butcher prepare a piece from the loin, remove the ribs, wrap the meat into a cylindrical shape, skewer, and tie it
3. Salt and pepper the meat, place in a deep baking dish, spread about one spoonful of fresh butter on it, pour in a small cup of hot water, and place in a hot oven to roast
4. At first it requires maintaining a good fire so the meat stays juicy; later reduce the heat
5. When it has been roasting for half an hour, sprinkle chopped onion on it and turn it
6. Calculate that the meat should be in the oven for a quarter hour per pound of weight, so a four-pound piece about one hour, larger pieces longer
7. When the meat is roasted, prepare a gravy from the rendered fat
8. If the meat was nicely fatty and much fat rendered, pour off part of it and add a small cup of boiling water; if not necessary, do not dilute the fat
9. Mix one spoonful of flour in a little water and mix into the fat, let it simmer, and serve in a separate dish as gravy for the meat and potatoes

title: Hovězí pečeně myslivecká (Hunter's Style Beef Roast)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- aged beef from ribs
- eggs (mixed)
- choice cured meats (sliced)
- chives
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- beef broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove bones and skin from aged rib meat and pound
2. Mix together eggs, sliced choice cured meats, chives, pepper and salt
3. Spread mixture on the meat and roll it up
4. Tie with string
5. Roast on butter in the oven
6. Simmer in beef broth and lemon juice until cooked

title: Hovězí pečeně na způsob zvěřiny (Beef Roast in the Manner of Game)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- weak vinegar
- bacon
- juniper
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Pound the meat
2. Marinate in weak vinegar
3. Lard with bacon
4. Roast with juniper on butter
5. Brush with sour cream and baste with sour cream

title: Hovězí Polévka Hnědá (Brown Beef Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter kilo of beef or veal liver
- 70 grams (4 lots) of finely chopped beef fat
- quarter of a celery root, split
- 1 whole onion, chopped
- leek
- quarter of a carrot
- a piece of green parsley
- a little white flour
- beef broth
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop the liver; add the chopped beef fat to it, quarter of a split celery root, whole onion chopped, leek, quarter of a carrot, a piece of green parsley and a little white flour
2. After stewing for about 5 minutes, strain into beef broth
3. Slice bread thinly, fry it in butter, place it in a soup bowl, and pour the soup over it

title: Hovězí polévka nudlová (Beef Noodle Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo beef
- salt
- piece of liver
- bones
- water (2.5 liters)
- parsley
- onion
- celery
- noodles

## Directions

1. Rinse the beef thoroughly, season with salt, add a piece of liver and some bones, cook in water
2. When it begins to foam, skim the foam
3. After skimming, add parsley, onion, celery to make the soup slightly sweet (a whole potato can be added if desired)
4. When the water has cooked down by half, strain it and add noodles

title: Hovězí Polévka s Brambory (Soup with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 medium potatoes, peeled and halved or quartered
- salted water
- beef broth, cooked and properly seasoned

## Directions

1. Boil four peeled and halved or quartered potatoes of medium size in salted water
2. When cooked, drain and put in a bowl
3. Pour cooked and properly seasoned beef broth over them

title: Hovězí polévka s krupiěným svítkem (Beef Soup with Groated Souffle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- fresh butter
- milk
- herbs
- flour grits
- salt
- 4 egg whites
- roll crumbs

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks, butter, milk, herbs, flour grits and salt
2. Make stiff snow from egg whites and mix in
3. Spread the mixture on a greased and bread-crumbed baking sheet, bake until golden
4. Cut into cubes and add to beef soup before serving

title: Hovězí Polévka s Mozečkem (Beef Soup with Calf Brains)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- calf brains
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- marjoram
- flour
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- brown, black or white beef broth

## Directions

1. Remove all membranes from the calf brains; beat 3 egg yolks with the brains, season with salt and add marjoram
2. Roll small pieces of brains in flour, then in egg and grated bread crumbs
3. Fry to a golden color, arrange in a soup bowl and pour with brown, black or white beef broth

title: Hovězí polévka s nokami (Beef Soup with Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 35 grams (2 lots) butter
- herbs
- salt
- flour
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks, butter, herbs and salt, add flour and mix
2. Make a snow from the egg whites and slowly add it
3. When the soup is boiling, dip a spoon in it, use the spoon to gather dumplings and drop them into the soup

title: Hovězí Polévka se Stříkankou (Beef Soup with Squirted Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo of meat
- vegetables
- 3 egg yolks
- pieces of butter
- 3 tablespoons of flour
- paper piping cone
- butter for frying
- chives
- good beef broth

## Directions

1. Cook the meat with vegetables; from 3 egg yolks, pieces of butter and 3 tablespoons of flour, make thin dough
2. Using a paper piping cone, squeeze small pieces like peas into hot butter and fry to golden color
3. Place in a soup bowl, sprinkle with chives and pour good beef broth over them

title: Hovězí Polévka z Bramborů (Potato Purée)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium potatoes
- 1 slice white bread
- 3 eggs
- beef broth, 1 quart

## Directions

1. Boil three potatoes of medium size, peel, and let cool
2. Grate the potatoes
3. Cut a slice of white bread and fry until golden
4. Boil three eggs hard, peel, and remove yolks
5. Add the yolks to the fried bread and mash everything together finely
6. Put into a pot, pour 1 quart of beef broth over it and let simmer half an hour
7. Thin with beef broth, strain, and pour into a soup bowl

title: Hovězí Polévka Černá s Chlebem (Black Beef Soup with Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo of meat without fat
- vegetables
- beef fat scraps
- a piece of beef heart
- various vegetables
- sugar
- goose fat
- bread
- 3 whole eggs or 4 hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the meat with vegetables; stew the beef fat scraps, a piece of beef heart and various vegetables, moisten with soup
2. When stewed, brown a piece of sugar and strain everything into the soup
3. Fry bread cut into cubes in goose fat, beat 3 whole eggs or cut 4 hard-boiled eggs into pieces and add to the soup bowl

title: Hovězí sekanina (Beef Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds ground beef
- 1/4 pound finely chopped lard
- 3 heaping tablespoons grated breadcrumbs
- 3 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- sage

## Directions

1. Take 3 pounds of ground beef, 1/4 pound finely chopped lard, three heaping tablespoons of grated breadcrumbs and three beaten eggs. Mix everything together well, salt, season with pepper and sage.
2. Place on a buttered baking sheet, sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs or bread, let bake for an hour and a half, basting frequently with melted butter. Can be served hot or cold.

title: Hovězí v rozsolu (Jellied Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef meat
- salt
- pepper
- allspice flowers
- allspice

## Directions

1. Boil beef for 5-6 hours.
2. Remove bones, fat from the meat and sinews.
3. Tear the meat into strands and cut the sinews.
4. When the water in which it was boiled has cooled, skim off the fat and add the same amount of it to the meat so that it becomes like a paste.
5. Salt it, pepper it, add allspice flowers and allspice, and boil for 15 minutes with constant stirring.
6. Pour into a container and let it cool; when it becomes aspic-like, cut it into slices.

title: Hovězí Závitky z Vařeného Masa (Boiled Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds of boiled beef
- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- three egg yolks
- parsley leaves, cleaned and finely chopped
- ground pepper
- salt
- stale bread, finely grated or bread crumbs or crackers
- three egg whites
- one teaspoon of warmed butter
- one teaspoon water
- lard
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove all glands and fat from one and a half pounds of boiled beef and chop the meat finely.
2. Make a pale roux from butter and flour, then pour enough beef broth into it to make a thick sauce, then let it, stirring constantly, boil for a quarter hour.
3. Then mix in a cup a piece of butter with three egg yolks, add this mixture to the sauce, which you then stir well.
4. To this add the mentioned chopped meat, one spoon of cleaned and finely chopped parsley leaves, a little ground pepper, salt it and mix well with a spoon.
5. Then let it cool completely, sprinkle generously with grated stale bread, bread crumbs or crackers; then divide the prepared meat into 16 portions, and place each portion on a layer of this grated bread spread on a board, then again sprinkle generously with grated bread crumbs, from this make equally large and oblong rolls.
6. Then beat 3 egg whites, add to them one teaspoon of warmed butter, one teaspoon of water and mix well.
7. Dip each roll in this and coat it in grated bread crumbs; then about 20 minutes before you want to put the rolls on the table, melt lard in a pot, place the rolls on a wire basket (frying basket) and hang this in the pot in the heated lard and let them fry.
8. Then turn them with a spoon and let them fry nicely to golden brown, then remove, sprinkle with salt, arrange on a plate, surround with fried parsley and bring them to the table.

title: Hovězí žebra (Ribs of Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or more beef ribs
- lard
- salt
- pepper
- fresh parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Take two or more beef ribs, cut off the backbone, separate the individual ribs and cut them into pieces about six fingers long, being careful not to let the meat slip off. Lard the meat on the ribs slightly, salt, pepper, and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
2. Heat a piece of butter in a skillet, place the prepared ribs in it and let them brown slightly on both sides. Cover the skillet, place in the oven and let the ribs finish cooking. When done, remove to a platter, pour over the strained gravy from which all fat has been completely removed, and arrange fried potatoes around it.

title: Hořké Makronky (Bitter Almond Macaroons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams sweet almonds
- 250 grams bitter almonds
- egg whites from 12 eggs

## Directions

1. Scald 500 grams sweet almonds and 250 grams bitter almonds, peel them, dry on a sieve, grind with egg whites from 12 eggs
2. If the dough is too stiff and thick, add a little more egg whites until a liquid dough forms
3. Spread the dough thinly on a wooden macaroon plate, spread carefully with a knife below, place on a baking sheet and bake slowly

title: Hořčice k masu (French Mustard)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- vinegar
- ground pepper
- salt
- sugar
- mustard powder

## Directions

1. Slice onion into a large cup, pour vinegar over it to cover and let stand for two or three days.
2. Pour the vinegar into a bowl and add a teaspoon of ground pepper, a teaspoon of salt, a tablespoon of yellow sugar and mustard powder as needed to make a thick paste.
3. First mix the mustard powder with a little of the vinegar to avoid lumps.
4. Place on the stove and stir constantly, and when it begins to boil, remove from heat.
5. Serve cold.

title: Hořčičné máslo (Mustard Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound fresh butter
- Three hard-boiled egg yolks
- Three tablespoons ground mustard
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Beat half pound of fresh butter with three hard-boiled egg yolks and three tablespoons of ground mustard, salt it, add a little pepper and place in a cool place.
2. Instead of mustard, two tablespoons of paprika and juice from one lemon may be added.

title: Hrachová kaše (Purée of Dried Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peas
- onion, sliced and browned to golden
- beef broth
- garlic, minced
- smoked meat, roasted beef, fried brain, fried tongue, or sausages

## Directions

1. To the cooked peas add a little diced onion browned to golden, mix it well and press it through a sieve into a pot.
2. Add a little beef broth, add minced garlic to it and let it cook a little longer.
3. Pour it onto a platter, coat with fat and cover with smoked meat, roasted beef fried in butter, fried brain, fried tongue, or sausages.

title: Hrachová Omáčka (Pea Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- broth
- butter
- salt
- onion
- thyme

## Directions

1. Cook the peas and drain the water
2. Add broth
3. Add butter, salt, onion, and thyme
4. Simmer the peas slowly for half an hour
5. Strain the sauce and serve with smoked meat

title: Hrachová polévka (Pea Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- green or dried peas
- soft water
- vegetable roots, sliced
- thyme
- 45 grams (3 lots) butter
- browned meat broth
- 1 tablespoon spinach
- salt
- fried roll

## Directions

1. Soak green or dried peas, boil them the next day in soft water
2. Add sliced vegetable roots, thyme, 45 grams (3 lots) of butter
3. Press through a sieve, add soup, browned meat broth, 1 tablespoon of spinach
4. Salt and pour over fried roll

title: Hrachová polévka (Pea Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- 3 cloves garlic
- Pepper
- Marjoram
- Roux (bledá jíška)
- Fried bread, bread, barley groats, or rice

## Directions

1. Cook the peas until soft
2. Add 3 cloves of garlic, pepper, marjoram, and roux
3. Strain the soup over fried bread, bread, barley groats, or rice

title: Hrachová Polévka (Pea Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- soft water
- seasoning
- vegetables
- salt
- broth
- fried bread roll

## Directions

1. Pick over the peas, wash them, soak overnight and the next day place in cold soft water, add seasoning, chopped vegetables and salt, later add as much broth as needed
2. When the peas are soft, strain the soup and pour over fried bread roll

title: Hrachová polévka (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pint of peas
- good beef broth
- a little chopped marjoram
- a little green parsley
- two grated raw potatoes
- pale roux

## Directions

1. Soak half a pint of peas overnight in cold water and the next day cook until soft
2. Mash the peas and strain into a pot, pour good beef broth over it, add a little chopped marjoram, a little green parsley, two grated raw potatoes and let cook
3. Meanwhile make a pale roux, thicken the broth with it and pour into a bowl over fried and sliced bread rolls
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Hrachová polévka (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pot of peas (for six persons)
- cold water
- beef broth
- a bit of chopped marjoram
- a bit of fresh parsley
- two raw potatoes, grated
- light roux
- fried white bread, cut into pieces

## Directions

1. Take one pot of peas for six persons
2. Let it soak overnight in cold water
3. The next day cook it until soft
4. Mash it and strain it into a pot
5. Pour good beef broth over it
6. Add a bit of chopped marjoram, a bit of green parsley, two grated raw potatoes
7. Let it cook
8. Meanwhile make a light roux, darken the broth with it and pour it into a bowl over fried white bread cut into pieces

title: Hrachová polévka postní (Puree of Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- parsley water
- butter
- onion
- garlic
- parsnip flower
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Cook peas until soft
2. Mix the peas
3. Pour parsley water on them and let simmer a bit more
4. Strain the soup
5. Fry it with a light browned roux, in which you can let a finely chopped onion become pale
6. You can also add about two cloves of well ground garlic, as well as a little parsnip flower and if you wish, also a little marjoram, which however can be omitted
7. Pour the soup over fried bread cubes
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Hrachová polévka postní (Purée of Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- parsley water
- butter
- egg yolk
- small onion
- 2 garlic cloves
- flowers
- marjoram
- bread

## Directions

1. Cook peas until soft and mix them
2. Pour parsley water on them and let cook a little longer
3. Strain the soup and make a roux with butter to which you have added a finely chopped small onion
4. You may also add about 2 garlic cloves well rubbed, as well as a little flowers and, if desired, a little marjoram, which however may be omitted
5. Pour the soup over fried bread
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Hrachová polévka se svítecky (Puree of Peas with Colomhines)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- celery
- salt
- water
- peas
- butter
- egg yolks
- eggs
- marjoram
- milk
- bread
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of parsley and one celery with greens into pieces and let cook in salted water
2. Mix the soft cooked peas, strain the salted water on it, mix it and let simmer a bit more
3. Strain the soup, add a little parsnip flower and fry it a bit with a light browned roux
4. For the colomhines: Rub half an ounce plus a half of fresh butter, add 4 spoonfuls of mixed and sieved peas to it, salt it, press two egg yolks and two whole eggs into it, mix it well
5. Add a little marjoram and half a bread soaked in good milk and then squeezed out, two or three spoonfuls of sweet cream
6. Mix everything well and bake small colomhines from it on a dimpled baking sheet
7. Then cut each into four pieces, place them in a soup bowl
8. When they cool, pour boiling soup on them and serve at table
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Hrachový nákyp (Pea Pudding)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter peas
- butter
- 10 egg yolks
- lemon zest
- sugar
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook 1 liter of peas, pour off the water, press through a sieve and let the porridge cool
2. Meanwhile, rub butter and mix it with 10 egg yolks, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of sugar, salt and nutmeg, then put it with the pea porridge into a greased form and bake for an hour at moderate heat

title: Hrozinková nádivka (Raisin Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raisins
- sultanas
- citron peel
- sugar
- rum

## Directions

1. Rinse the raisins and sultanas
2. Chop into small pieces
3. Add finely chopped citron peel and sugar
4. Moisten with rum
5. Heat on a warm griddle

title: Hrozinková Nádivka (Raisin Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 deca butter
- 4 egg yolks
- bread roll
- sour cream
- raisins
- sliced almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 3 to 4 deca of butter with 4 egg yolks
2. Add grated bread roll moistened and squeezed out in sour cream
3. Mix with raisins, sliced almonds, and sugar
4. Use for stuffing turkeys or bake separately and serve with the roast

title: Hrozinková nádivka - Second Method (Raisin Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 deciliters wine
- 10 dek sugar
- approximately 14 dek finely chopped almonds
- raisins
- sultanas
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Boil the wine with sugar
2. Add finely chopped almonds, raisins, and sultanas in the quantities stated
3. Add finely chopped lemon zest
4. When cooled, use for filling pastries

title: Hrozinkový Paj (Raisin Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee cup of large raisins
- water
- vinegar
- sugar
- butter
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take a coffee cup of large raisins from which you have removed the seeds
2. Place in a glazed pot
3. Add a coffee cup of water, 4 tablespoons of vinegar, half a coffee cup of sugar and not quite a tablespoon of butter
4. Lemon peel is also very good
5. Let all this simmer for about 15 minutes
6. Then add a little more...

title: Hrozinkový Paj (Raisin Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of large raisins
- one coffee cup of water
- four tablespoons of vinegar
- half a coffee cup of sugar
- less than one tablespoon of butter
- lemon zest
- flour
- one egg white
- sugar for sweetening

## Directions

1. Remove seeds from raisins and place in a glazed pot
2. Add one coffee cup of water, four tablespoons of vinegar, half a coffee cup of sugar, and less than one tablespoon of butter
3. Lemon zest is also very good
4. Let this simmer for about 15 minutes
5. Add a little flour so everything is about as thick as a preparation for lemon pie
6. Prepare dough on a baking sheet and bake it halfway first
7. Spread the raisin mixture on the prepared pie crust and bake in the oven
8. When the crust is golden, cover it with beaten and properly sweetened egg white and place in the oven to dry

title: Hrozinkový Pudding (Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup finely chopped suet from kidneys
- one cup sugar
- one cup raisins without seeds
- one cup washed dried currants
- one cup candied citron
- one cup flour
- half pound grated white bread
- four eggs
- four tablespoons brandy
- half teaspoon allspice
- half teaspoon cloves
- one cup milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix one cup finely chopped suet from kidneys, one cup sugar, one cup raisins without seeds, one cup washed dried currants, one cup candied citron, one cup flour, and half pound grated white bread together.
2. Add four eggs, four tablespoons brandy, half teaspoon allspice, and half teaspoon cloves.
3. Add one cup milk with a little salt mixed in.
4. Place everything in a cloth sprinkled with flour and tie it loosely.
5. Boil it; it will rise during cooking.
6. Let it cook for one and a half to two hours.

title: Hrozinky (Raisins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound large raisins
- white wine
- lemon zest
- sugar
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Wash 1/4 pound of fine large raisins in cold water
2. Dry them well
3. Remove all the seeds from them
4. Chop them finely
5. Put them in a small pot with several teaspoons of white wine
6. Add 1/2 finely chopped lemon zest, a little pounded sugar, cloves and cinnamon
7. Let them cook a little and then cool
8. You can use this filling instead of preserved currants, in cakes or tarts

title: Hroznovská svařenina (Grape Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- grape berries
- sugar (half cup per cup of cooked grape pulp)

## Directions

1. Remove grape berries from the stems, wash and squeeze the insides of the berries from their skins into a bowl, putting the skins in another bowl.
2. Cook the grape insides with pits as they are, without adding water, since they yield enough juice.
3. Cook about half an hour until you see the pits have separated from the fruit, then strain through a sieve or squeeze in thin cloth to dryness and put it back to cook with the skins.
4. Add half cup of sugar per cup of cooked grapes, or more according to taste, cook one and a half hours and pour into jars.

title: Hroznová svařenina (Grape Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- Grape berries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the grape berries and press them, squeeze out the insides of the berries from the skins into a bowl and put the skins in another bowl.
2. Put the insides of the berries with the seeds to cook just as they are, without adding water, as they have enough juice.
3. Cook about half an hour, until you see that the seeds have separated from the berry, then strain through a sieve, or squeeze in a thin cloth until dry and put it back to cook along with those skins.
4. Add to each cup of cooked grapes half a cup of sugar, or more to taste, cook one and one-half hours and then put into jars.

title: Hroznový Paj (Grape Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- pie crust dough
- egg
- sugar
- grape seeds

## Directions

1. Line a pan with dough
2. Beat one egg
3. Add half a coffee cup of sugar to it
4. Add a large coffee cup of grape seeds
5. Spread this mixture over the pan
6. Dust with a tablespoon of flour
7. Dot with butter and add a top crust

title: Hroznový Paj (Grape Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough
- one egg
- half a coffee cup of sugar
- one large coffee cup of grape seeds
- flour
- butter scraps

## Directions

1. Line a form with dough
2. Beat one egg
3. Add half a coffee cup of sugar and one large coffee cup of grape seeds to it
4. Spread this mixture in the form
5. Dust with a spoon of flour
6. Place butter scraps and add top crust

title: Hroznový rosol (Grape Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- unripened grape berries
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the grains from unripened grapes and put in a pot or kettle
2. For every 8 pounds of grapes (a small basket of grapes holds 8 pounds, a large one 20 pounds) add 1 small cup of water and let it cook about 1/2 hour until the grains are completely cooked, stirring occasionally so the skins do not stick to the bottom
3. When cooked, set aside and let cool
4. Then strain through thin cloth (cheese cloth)
5. If you want clear jelly, let it only drip and do not squeeze much
6. Measure the juice and pour it back into the pot and for however many small cups of juice, prepare that many small cups of fine sugar
7. Put the sugar in the oven to warm, but do not let it burn and when the juice has cooked 1/2 hour, add the sugar to it, let it cook another 10 minutes, during which collect the foam, then set aside and when it cools, put in jars
8. Jelly from wild grape is also very delicious

title: Hrudí divokého vepře (Roasted Wild Boar Breast)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar breast
- lard
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Lard the wild boar breast as you would beef breast.
2. Pound it like a beefsteak, salt and pepper it.
3. Quickly roast in butter.

title: Hrudí divokého vepře - Jiný způsob (Wild Boar Breast - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar breast
- vinegar
- butter
- sauce from the marinade
- meat broth
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Marinate the breast in vinegar for one to two days.
2. Roast in butter.
3. During roasting, baste with the marinade and good meat broth.
4. The breast can also be prepared with sour cream like hare.

title: Hrušková povidla (Pear Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears, peeled and quartered
- sugar, two pounds for every ten pounds of pears

## Directions

1. In the evening, peel pears and cut into quarters, sprinkle with sugar—two pounds of sugar for every ten pounds of pears.
2. Let sit overnight.
3. The next day, cook for three hours in the juice released by the pears.
4. When cooled, place in stone jars, cover well with strong paper or clean cloth, and store in a cool place.

title: Hruškový kompot (Pear Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- Water
- Sugar
- A piece of whole cinnamon
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cut pears in half, peel them, remove the seeds
2. Cook them like apples in water with sugar, a piece of whole cinnamon and lemon peel
3. Arrange them on a dish and pour thickly boiled juice over them
4. Alternatively, you can use equal parts water and red wine
5. You can also cook the pears whole if you wish

title: Hruškový kompot (Pear Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- water
- sugar
- whole cinnamon stick
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cut pears in half, peel, remove the core, and cook them like apples in water with sugar, a piece of whole cinnamon and lemon zest.
2. Arrange them on a dish and pour the thickly cooked syrup over them.
3. You can also use equal parts water and red wine, and if desired, you can cook the pears whole.

title: Hrách (Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- soft cold water
- salt
- soda (if hard water is used)
- goose or pork lard, or crackling
- boiled smoked meat
- pickled cucumbers or raw cabbage

## Directions

1. Sort the peas the evening before, wash them thoroughly in cold water and let them soak in it overnight.
2. For cooking, take an elongated (tall) but not large pot.
3. Peas nearly double in volume when cooked, so if you want to cook about two small cups of peas, take a quart pot.
4. Drain the water in which the peas soaked and put the peas in the pot and add soft cold water until the pot is full.
5. If you do not have soft water, add a pinch of soda to it.
6. Cover and as the water boils away, replenish with hot water and when it has boiled away, salt it.
7. Then drain the water, pour the peas onto a platter, coat with goose or pork lard or crackling, garnish with boiled smoked meat and serve with pickled cucumbers or raw cabbage.

title: Hrách s kroupami čili svarba (Peas with Barley)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- barley
- salt
- caraway seeds
- lard or melted butter
- smoked meat or fried blood sausage

## Directions

1. Cook the peas according to the previous method and meanwhile also cook the barley with a little salt and roasted caraway seeds until soft and thick.
2. Then drain the peas and alternately layer them with the barley in another pot, let it cook a little longer, shake it in the pot, pour onto a platter and coat with hot lard or melted butter.
3. Sprinkle with crackling if desired and garnish with either smoked meat or fried blood sausage.
4. Alternatively, put the cooked peas alternately with the barley into the platter in which you want to serve it, put peas on top and coat and garnish as described above.

title: Hrách se Smetanou (Peas with Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Butter
- Sugar
- Pale roux
- Sweet cream

## Directions

1. Stew the peas in butter with sugar
2. Add pale roux so it becomes quite thick
3. Pour in one and a quarter decilitres of sweet cream and simmer briefly

title: Hrášek z mouky (Pea Flour)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- flour
- flower
- salt
- butter
- strained broth

## Directions

1. Make dough from 4 eggs, flour, flower and salt (flour enough to make a thin batter)
2. Heat butter in a pan and strain thin dough through a strainer into it
3. Carefully remove individual drops from the butter, place in soup bowl with chopped gills and livers
4. Pour broth over them

title: Hubičky s Pěnou Smetanovou (Kisses with Whipped Cream Foam)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds good sweet cream
- 25 decagrams sugar
- vanilla
- three tablespoons flour
- six raw egg yolks
- sugar candies or biscuits

## Directions

1. Place two pounds of good sweet cream on the stove
2. Add 25 decagrams of sugar and a piece of vanilla
3. Stir until it thickens
4. Add three tablespoons of flour and six raw egg yolks
5. Mix again and strain into a mold where the foam will remain
6. Place on ice
7. When the foam hardens, add sugar kisses or insert biscuits on the sides

title: Humra (Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Tie the lobster's claws
2. Plunge into boiling salted water
3. If about a pound, boil for about a quarter hour; if larger, boil a bit longer
4. Do not overcook or the meat will toughen
5. After cooking, split lengthwise
6. Serve either warm or cold with mayonnaise or with vinegar and spices

title: Hungarian Suchar (Hungarian Rusk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams flour
- 32 grams dissolved yeast
- 125 grams butter
- 125 grams sugar
- 4 eggs
- salt
- warm milk

## Directions

1. For 500 grams flour, take 32 grams dissolved yeast, 125 grams butter, 125 grams sugar, 4 eggs, salt, and mix with warm milk into dough
2. Let the dough rise, then roll it out into thin narrow slices
3. Let the slices rise again on a baking sheet, then bake slowly
4. On the second day, cut them and brush with egg, then sprinkle with sugar and chopped almonds

title: Husa divoká dušená (Braised Wild Goose)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned wild goose
- bacon
- ham
- 125 grams butter
- salt
- spices
- vegetables (vegetable pieces)
- 6-8 juniper berries
- 1/4 liter water
- 1/4 liter vinegar

## Directions

1. Wrap the cleaned goose in bacon.
2. Place on a baking sheet with thin-cut ham and bacon.
3. Add butter, salt, spices, vegetable pieces, and juniper berries.
4. Add 1/4 liter water and 1/4 liter vinegar.
5. Cover the baking sheet tightly and place on hot coals.
6. Baste the goose frequently with boiling water during braising.
7. The goose will become tender in 2-3.5 hours.
8. Remove the bacon and cut the goose into pieces.
9. Strain the sauce, thicken with flour, and simmer.
10. Serve with soft-cooked rice on the side.

title: Husa divoká pečená (Roasted Wild Goose)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild goose
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. A killed wild goose should not hang for long if it is to become a tasty dish.
2. Only young geese can be roasted; older geese can only be marinated or braised.
3. Remove the head, neck, wings, and legs from the plucked and cleaned wild goose.
4. Prepare it like a domestic goose on a baking sheet.
5. Blanch it in boiling water, dry it inside and out, season with salt and pepper.
6. Roast thoroughly, basting frequently or coating with butter.

title: Husa Pečená (Roasted Goose)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt
- caraway seeds
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the goose, remove the innards, wash it thoroughly, remove all membranes, salt it, season with caraway
2. Truss it, place on a baking pan, add water, and roast slowly in the oven
3. If it is fat, prick it while basting under the wings and on the back so all the fat renders out
4. Then carve: wings, neck, breasts and middle section into 4 pieces each, the rump (called the bishop) remains whole
5. Divide the legs into 2 pieces each and arrange everything on a platter
6. Cabbage is usually served with goose

title: Husa s Jablkovou Nádivkou (Goose with Apple Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose, prepared for roasting
- Two cups of stewed, softened and mixed apples
- One cup of grated bread
- A little sage leaves, roasted and sifted
- One onion, finely chopped
- A little red pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the goose for roasting as previously described.
2. Mix together the stewed apples, grated bread, sage leaves, finely chopped onion, and red pepper.
3. Stuff the goose with this mixture.
4. Sew it closed and roast by the previous method.

title: Huspenina citrónová (Lemon Preserve)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- lemon zest
- isinglass
- water

## Directions

1. Rub lemon zest onto sugar
2. Scrape this sugar from the other and press the juice from it
3. Cook finely torn isinglass with water until at least half has boiled away
4. Pour everything into the lemon juice, sweeten again, cook and strain into glass or earthenware containers
5. Place on ice to cool
6. Various aromatic substances (vanilla, etc.) may be added to give it a special flavor

title: Huspenina ryvízová (Currant Preserve)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the currants and crush them
2. Pour a little water over them and cook slowly, stirring constantly
3. Strain through a sieve to draw off the juice better
4. Pour cooked sugar into the juice and cook both until well thickened
5. Remove from heat, let the liquid cool, and if any scum forms on top, remove it slowly
6. Pour into containers when cooled, seal with paper and isinglass, and store in a cold, dry place

title: Huspenina višňová (Cherry Preserve)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- bitter almonds
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the cherries
2. Crush the cherries with several bitter almonds in a mortar
3. Let the mixture rest for four to five hours in a stone pot
4. Press through a clean cloth to extract the juice
5. Sweeten the juice well and place on ice to cool
6. Clove or other flavorings may be added to this preserve if desired

title: Huspenina z vajec do polévky (Egg Jelly for Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several egg yolks
- cold milk or soup
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat several egg yolks in cold milk or soup, salt it, add nutmeg
2. Place in a covered pot in hot water, then set the pot with water aside for three-quarters of an hour so the water maintains a slow boil until the jelly forms
3. Cut the jelly with a spoon and add to the soup

title: Husí Drobečky s Karfiolem (Goose Giblets with Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose giblets
- Cauliflower
- Beef broth
- Fresh butter
- Flour for roux
- Nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the goose giblets by the previous method.
2. Clean a nice cauliflower and cook it either in beef broth or in the broth from the giblets until tender, being careful not to let it fall apart.
3. Remove it and place it in cold water so it remains nicely white.
4. Make a light roux from fresh butter, strain the giblet broth and thicken it with the roux, season lightly with ground nutmeg, add the cooked giblets and cauliflower and let simmer a little longer.
5. Arrange the cauliflower neatly in the center of a serving dish, surround it with the cooked giblets, pour sauce over and bring to the table.

title: Husí Drobečky s Nudlemi (Goose Giblets with Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose giblets
- Eggs
- Flour for noodles

## Directions

1. Cook the goose giblets as previously described.
2. Make fine noodles using two eggs.
3. When the giblets are sufficiently tender, strain the broth and let it settle, then pour it into a pot and let it come to a boil.
4. When it is boiling nicely, cook the noodles in it.
5. Have the neatly cut giblets prepared in a warm place.
6. Place them on a serving dish and arrange the cooked noodles on top.
7. Cooked giblets can also be surrounded with macaroni cooked in salted water, dressed with fat and sprinkled with cheese.

title: Husí Drobečky s Rýží (Goose Giblets with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose giblets
- Celery, small piece
- Parsley root
- Salt
- Three tablespoons fresh butter
- One cup sifted and rinsed rice
- Beef broth
- Flour for roux
- Nutmeg
- Smoked meat, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Boil the goose giblets.
2. When they float, add a piece of celery and parsley root, salt them, and cook until tender.
3. On a stove put three tablespoons of fresh butter and one cup of sifted rice that has been rinsed several times in hot water.
4. Pour a few tablespoons of beef broth over it, salt lightly, and let the rice stew slowly on low heat until tender.
5. Watch carefully so it does not stick to the bottom and add more beef broth as needed.
6. Make a light roux and thin it with a little beef broth, season with nutmeg, salt, and let it cook into a smooth sauce.
7. Finally arrange the cooked goose giblets on a serving dish, pour the sauce over them, make a wreath of rice around them, and place diagonal strips of finely chopped smoked meat on top.
8. Instead of roux, you can also use drippings from the roast or fry a little caraway seed in butter or goose fat, to which add a little chopped fresh parsley.
9. Do not forget to add a little nutmeg and a small amount of saffron to the rice.
10. The giblets include wings, legs, neck, liver, and stomach. Well-cleaned intestines, when turned inside out, can also be used and serve as a good delicacy.

title: Husí Drobečky Zadělávané s Nudlemi (Goose Giblets with Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose giblets, especially the stomach
- carrots
- celery
- parsley
- cabbage
- cauliflower
- salt
- one or two eggs
- white roux
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the giblets, especially the stomach, cut them up, and place in water
2. Add carrots, celery, parsley, cabbage, cauliflower, and salt; cook until completely soft
3. Allow to cool
4. Make thin noodles from one or two eggs
5. Strain the broth in which the giblets were cooked onto a separate baking pan
6. Add a piece of white roux, strain it, and cook the noodles in the boiling broth
7. For a sour taste, add lemon juice
8. Peel the stomach and cut into small pieces, then add with the remaining giblets to the sauce

title: Husí Drůbky s Knedlíčky (Goose Giblets with Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose giblets
- carrots
- celery
- parsley
- cabbage
- cauliflower
- salt
- pale butter roux
- goose livers
- butter
- flour
- garlic
- marjoram
- egg
- bread

## Directions

1. Prepare the giblets as when preparing them with noodles
2. Press pale butter roux through a sieve into the broth in which the giblets were cooked and bring to a boil
3. Meanwhile, make dumplings either from goose livers (cleaned and chopped, removing membranes, with butter, flour, garlic, marjoram, and egg added, all mixed and formed into small dumplings) or from bread (mixing butter, flour, and egg, adding milk if needed and mixing with grated bread)
4. Cook the dumplings in the broth, then add the giblets
5. Instead of noodles, rice, or dumplings, cauliflower or asparagus (previously cooked in salted water) may be used
6. Duck giblets may be prepared the same way as goose giblets

title: Husí Drůbky s Rejží (Goose Giblets with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose giblets
- carrots
- celery
- parsley
- cabbage
- cauliflower
- salt
- rice
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Prepare the giblets as when preparing them with noodles
2. When they have cooked for about half an hour, rinse the rice and place on a separate baking pan
3. Pour about two spoonfuls of broth from the giblets gradually until the rice is completely soft
4. When both are soft, add a little cauliflower and mix the giblets with the rice
5. The only difference is that no roux is added in this case

title: Husí Prsa (Goose Breasts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose breasts with breastbone
- bread, grated
- onion
- goose fat
- flour
- spice
- pepper
- marjoram
- garlic
- eggs
- beef broth
- cabbage

## Directions
