2. Throw all kinds of skins, scraps, and even rotten fruit into the barrel.
3. Place a perforated lid on top so that air has access to the interior.
4. In a short time excellent vinegar develops, which is then strained and poured into containers.

title: Jiný způsob (Another Method - Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of ripe raspberries
- one liter of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Crush half a liter of ripe raspberries and pour one liter of wine vinegar over them, seal the bottle and let it stand in the sun for 14 days, strain, and add to fish, salads, or use it to prepare lemonade.

title: Jiný Způsob (Another Method - Tea Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deka tea
- 1 liter water
- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 4-5 bitter oranges
- 0.5 bottle arak
- 1 bottle good rum

## Directions

1. Brew 4 deka of tea in 1 liter of water
2. Strain onto 0.5 kilogram of sugar that has been grated with 4-5 bitter oranges
3. Pour 0.5 bottle of arak and 1 bottle of good rum over the mixture

title: Jiný způsob (Another Method for Cranberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- juice from small cranberries
- sugar
- large raw cranberries

## Directions

1. Boil juice from small cranberries with sugar and pour hot over large raw cranberries
2. Mix everything and boil again

title: Jiný způsob (Another Method for Preserved Cranberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberry berries
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Sometimes the cranberry berries are simply selected, rinsed, baked and placed in a cool room, and only when needed are they sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon

title: Jiný způsob (Another Method for Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram of fine dried strawberries
- 375 grams of sugar
- water

## Directions

1. For 1/2 kilogram of dried strawberries, take 375 grams of sugar, boil it with water, place the strawberries in it, let them come to a boil once, remove them, boil the sugar until it pulls, place the strawberries back in it for 5 minutes, then carefully place in jars
2. Boil the sugar until completely thick and pour over the strawberries
3. When cooled, seal and store in a cool place
4. Alternatively, for 1/2 kilogram of strawberries, boil 375 grams of sugar until it pulls
5. When the sugar begins to turn red, shake the container, add the strawberries and let stand until the sugar begins to boil, shake the container again
6. Pour the liquid onto a plate, cover with a sieve and let cool
7. The next day, pour into a jar, seal, wrap in hay and place in water where it boils slowly for three-quarters of an hour
8. Then let cool and store the next day

title: Jiný způsob (Another Way - Orange Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram flour
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) ground sifted sugar
- Zest from 1 lemon
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- 15 decagrams (10 lots) butter
- Eggs (as much as needed to make dough like for sweet rolls)
- Jam or preserves for coating
- Sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Put flour on a baking sheet and stir it until it turns red, then let it cool
2. When cooled, place on a work surface and mix with 12 decagrams ground sifted sugar, zest from 1 lemon and lemon juice
3. Add a little salt, 15 decagrams butter and as many eggs as needed to make a dough like for sweet rolls
4. Make the cake either round or spread across the entire baking sheet, make an edge from dough
5. When baked, brush with jam, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon and cut into pieces

title: Jiný způsob (Another Way for Pockets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dough for noodles
- prune butter
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- egg white
- crumb or cheese filling

## Directions

1. Make dough like for noodles and roll out well without letting it dry
2. Cut square pieces with a cutter
3. Mix prune butter with water, sugar, and cinnamon
4. Place on dough squares
5. Cover with another square
6. Press edges with cutter brushed with egg white
7. Boil in salted water
8. Drain
9. Butter and sprinkle with crumb or cheese

title: Jiný Způsob (Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee beans
- chicory (7-8 grams chicory per 150 grams coffee)
- water (three liters)
- milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Before cooking, grind the coffee and add crushed chicory.
2. Heat everything on the stove with constant stirring.
3. Slowly strain (filter) the boiling water through a coffee machine.
4. Let it stand on warm stove for at least ten minutes, then serve with milk and sugar.

title: Jiný způsob (Apple Compote, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled apples
- orange peel
- sugar
- water
- raisins
- figs
- other additions

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cut them into quarters
2. Cut out the seeds
3. Braise on a baking sheet
4. Add finely chopped orange peel, sugar, water, raisins and figs or other additions as desired
5. Boil everything together until the apples are completely soft
6. Arrange nicely on a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Jiný způsob (Bread Cake - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted bread
- 0.5 kilogram finely crushed sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- about 0.5 kilogram almonds
- several eggs
- cinnamon
- vanilla
- cloves
- finely chopped lemon and orange zest
- 16 egg whites
- butter for greasing molds
- finely grated hardtack for dusting molds

## Directions

1. Finely crush the roasted bread, set aside one-eighth of a kilogram separately
2. Mix 0.5 kilogram finely crushed sugar with 16 egg yolks
3. Set the egg whites aside separately
4. Peel about 0.5 kilogram almonds, dry them, finely crush in a mortar and mix with the bread and sugar described above
5. Add several eggs as needed, a little cinnamon, vanilla, cloves, finely chopped lemon and orange zest, etc.
6. Prepare snow from 16 egg whites and mix with the remaining dough
7. Spread into molds buttered and lightly dusted with finely grated hardtack and bake carefully and slowly

title: Jiný způsob (Bread Cake - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams almonds with skins, crushed
- 24 dekagrams sugar
- 2 grated chocolate tablets
- 6 dekagrams bread dried dark and crushed to flour-like consistency
- lemon zest
- grated nutmeg
- 7.5 grams cloves (0.5 lot)
- 7.5 grams cinnamon
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 egg
- snow from 12 egg whites
- ice for decoration

## Directions

1. Mix 24 dekagrams crushed almonds with skins with 24 dekagrams sugar, 2 grated chocolate tablets, 6 dekagrams bread dried dark and crushed to flour-like consistency, lemon zest, grated nutmeg, 7.5 grams cloves, 7.5 grams cinnamon, 12 egg yolks, 1 egg, and snow from 12 egg whites
2. Beat for an hour
3. Bake the cake in a buttered, flour-dusted mold and decorate with ice

title: Jiný Způsob (Cabbage Salad - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage, finely chopped
- finely chopped onion
- bacon or salt pork, fried
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop cabbage finely as mentioned in the previous recipe
2. Mix with finely chopped onion and fried bacon or salt pork while still hot
3. Drizzle with vinegar and mix well
4. Let steam for at least a quarter hour
5. Season with salt and pepper

title: Jiný způsob (Chocolate Cake - Alternative Method 1)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams butter
- 200 grams sugar
- 14 egg yolks
- 200 grams unpeeled almonds
- 200 grams grated chocolate
- 90 grams fine flour
- stiff egg white snow from 14 whites

## Directions

1. Beat 375 grams butter until it foams
2. Slowly add 200 grams sugar
3. Add 14 egg yolks, 200 grams unpeeled almonds, 200 grams grated chocolate, 90 grams fine flour, and stiff egg white snow from 14 whites
4. Mix everything together perfectly
5. Pour into baking forms rubbed with butter
6. Bake at moderate heat in one hour
7. While still warm, coat with glaze
8. Dry again in a heated oven

title: Jiný způsob (Chocolate Cake - Alternative Method 2)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 eggs
- half kilogram sugar
- grated chocolate
- finest flour

## Directions

1. Prepare dough from 20 eggs and half kilogram sugar, beaten well together
2. Add grated chocolate and a little finest flour
3. It is possible to prepare the chocolate cake in the same way as the almond cake, and to add almonds to it (but less than in an almond cake)

title: Jiný způsob (Chocolate Cake - Alternative Method 3)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram sugar
- 14 egg yolks
- 375 grams ground almonds
- 200 grams grated chocolate
- egg white snow from 11 whites

## Directions

1. Take half kilogram sugar and beat with 14 egg yolks
2. Add 375 grams ground almonds and 200 grams grated chocolate
3. Add egg white snow from 11 whites
4. Mix everything together
5. Pour into a baking form greased with butter
6. Bake

title: Jiný způsob (Cinnamon Tubes - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams fine sifted flour
- 125 grams finely ground sugar
- 2 to 3 eggs
- salt
- 8 grams finely ground cinnamon
- 50 grams melted butter
- 1/2 liter lukewarm cream

## Directions

1. Mix 250 grams of fine sifted flour with 125 grams of finely ground sugar
2. Beat in 2 to 3 eggs, season with salt
3. Add 8 grams of finely ground cinnamon and 50 grams of melted butter
4. Knead with 1/2 liter of lukewarm cream into a firm dough
5. Butter the metal molds (tube pans), fill with dough
6. Bake slowly on both sides until golden
7. Remove quickly and wind onto round wooden forms and let cool

title: Jiný způsob (Cod - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod, well-soaked
- salt
- water
- onion
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the well-soaked cod and place in boiling salted water
2. When completely soft, pour over browned onion
3. Serve with buttered potatoes with onion

title: Jiný způsob (Cod - Third Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod, well-soaked
- butter
- water
- parsley, chopped
- pepper
- nutmeg
- flour for thickening
- lemon

## Directions

1. Place well-soaked cod on a baking sheet with butter, water, chopped parsley, pepper, and nutmeg
2. Braise until the cod is tender
3. Transfer to a platter
4. Thicken the remaining cooking liquid with pale roux, strain over the cod
5. Add a piece of butter and simmer briefly
6. Serve in sauce, typically with lemon cut into quarters

title: Jiný způsob (Cream Sauce - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams sugar
- 1/2 liter good sweet cream
- two spoonfuls flour
- vanilla
- lemon peel
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook 60 grams of sugar, 1/2 liter of good sweet cream, two spoonfuls of flour, a piece of vanilla and a piece of uncut lemon peel until thickened
2. Mix in three egg yolks
3. Can be served cold or warm
4. Usually served with buns

title: Jiný způsob (Egg Cake - Alternative Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- about half kilogram butter
- 14 egg yolks
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat about half kilogram butter until it foams
2. Slowly beat 14 egg yolks and 6 eggs into it

title: Jiný způsob (English Cheese, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- morning warm milk
- cream
- rennet
- salt
- beer, vinegar, and saffron (optional for color)

## Directions

1. Take morning warm milk, add cream to it, heat it, and curdle with rennet.
2. The cheese is broken, placed in clean cloth into a mold.
3. Cheeses in cloth are placed in layers into the mold, covered, frequently turned, salted; after four days they are turned out, wrapped in clean linen cloth, and placed in a cellar to ripen.
4. As needed the cheese is flipped, salted, and if it is to have a nice color, it is brushed with beer, vinegar, and dissolved saffron.

title: Jiný způsob (Fish Sauce - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- several anchovies, cleaned and chopped

## Directions

1. Braise several cleaned and chopped anchovies in butter until they soften
2. This sauce is suitable for almost all fish preparations

title: Jiný způsob (Fish Sauce - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sixty dekagrams (four lots) of butter
- flour
- fish broth
- several tablespoons vinegar
- herbs
- salt
- two or three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix sixty dekagrams of butter with flour
2. Add a little fish broth and several tablespoons of vinegar
3. Add herbs and salt
4. Boil with two or three egg yolks

title: Jiný Způsob (Head Lettuce Salad, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- head lettuce
- salt
- wine vinegar
- sour cream
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Remove outer leaves from lettuce and cut
2. Salt and pour boiling water over it
3. Mix one part vinegar with one part good sour cream
4. Pour this mixture over the lettuce
5. Top with hard-boiled eggs, cut in quarters or halves

title: Jiný Způsob (Head Lettuce Salad, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- head lettuce
- wine vinegar
- salt
- bacon
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Remove outer leaves from head
2. Cut lettuce into quarters and salt
3. Pour wine vinegar over it
4. Cut and melt bacon
5. Pour bacon over the lettuce
6. Top with sliced hard-boiled eggs

title: Jiný Způsob (Head Lettuce Salad, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- head lettuce
- wine vinegar
- Provençal oil
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Break off the outer leaves of the head if young
2. Divide into quarters and rinse in several waters
3. Arrange on a plate
4. Season with salt
5. Pour wine vinegar and Provençal oil over it
6. Divide hard-boiled eggs in halves or quarters and arrange around the edge of the plate

title: Jiný způsob (Horseradish Broth - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- three spoonfuls flour
- half liter beef broth
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Heat butter
2. Mix three spoonfuls of flour in it
3. Pour over half liter beef broth
4. Add grated horseradish

title: Jiný Způsob (Lemon Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 195 grams (13 lots) sugar
- zest from 2 lemons
- 195 grams butter
- 12 egg yolks
- juice from 2 lemons
- 150 grams (10 lots) potato flour
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 2 lemons with 195 grams (13 lots) sugar; crush the sugar
2. Beat 195 grams butter until it becomes fluffy
3. Add 12 egg yolks and juice from 2 lemons
4. Add 150 grams (10 lots) potato flour
5. Put the mixture in a buttered mold
6. Serve with wine sauce

title: Jiný Způsob (Lemon Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- fine crushed sugar
- grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Whip 4 egg whites perfectly
2. Mix in sufficient fine crushed sugar and grated lemon zest
3. Work everything well, put in a mold, cover with paper, and bake slowly

title: Jiný způsob (Morel Sauce - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of morels
- parsley
- pale roux
- beef broth
- six raw egg yolks

## Directions

1. Wash a handful of morels and cut them
2. Place on a pan and braise with chopped parsley
3. Mix with pale roux and press through a sieve into beef broth
4. Cook for a short time
5. Mix in six raw egg yolks and stir the sauce until it thickens
6. Let it cool

title: Jiný způsob (Mushroom Sauce - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth
- raw ham
- spice
- wine
- butter
- lemon juice
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cook broth from beef, raw ham and spice
2. Mix with a glass of wine
3. Add butter and lemon juice
4. Add mushrooms cooked until tender
5. Cook everything together
6. Strain
7. Add three to four egg yolks

title: Jiný způsob (Mushroom Sauce - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth
- raw ham
- spices
- wine
- butter
- lemon juice
- mushrooms
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Simmer broth made from beef, raw ham and spices
2. Mix with a glass of wine
3. Add butter and lemon juice
4. Add mushrooms cooked until soft
5. Simmer all together
6. Strain
7. Add three to four egg yolks

title: Jiný způsob (Mushroom Sauce - Third Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams butter
- mushrooms
- lemon juice
- flour
- bouillon
- pepper
- spices

## Directions

1. Melt 125 grams of butter until brown
2. Braise several mushrooms in it
3. Remove them and sprinkle with lemon juice
4. Make a roux from butter and flour
5. Pour in bouillon
6. Season with pepper and spices
7. Add mushrooms
8. Simmer all together

title: Jiný způsob (Oyster Stuffing - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- poultry livers
- calf livers
- fatback
- anchovies
- parsley
- carrot
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop and mix together poultry livers, calf livers, fatback, anchovies, parsley and carrot
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Use for stuffing whole oysters

title: Jiný způsob (Potato Sauce - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- brown roux
- salt
- pepper
- flower
- chopped parsley
- chopped chives
- half liter cream
- cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. Add salt, pepper, flower, chopped parsley and chopped chives to brown roux
2. Mix with half liter cream
3. Cook the sauce
4. Add a plate of sliced cooked potatoes
5. Cook and serve

title: Jiný způsob (Potato Sauce - Fourth Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- butter
- flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Slice onion and brown in butter with a spoonful of flour
2. Pour in 1/2 liter of broth
3. Add salt, pepper and two to three spoonfuls of vinegar
4. Strain over sliced boiled potatoes

title: Jiný způsob (Potato Sauce - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- beef broth
- marjoram
- flowers
- garlic
- fresh mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cook several scraped potatoes in beef broth
2. Strain the broth
3. Press potatoes through a sieve into it
4. Add marjoram
5. Add a little flowers and rubbed garlic strands
6. If using fresh mushrooms add a boletus for flavor

title: Jiný způsob (Potato Sauce - Third Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- small onion
- flour
- butter
- beef broth
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Slice boiled potatoes
2. To fried onion on butter with flour pour in beef broth
3. Add pepper, salt and chopped parsley
4. When sauce is cooked add potatoes and simmer for a while

title: Jiný způsob (Roasted Woodcocks - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Some do not cook the woodcocks for roasting, others do cook them, leaving only the heart and liver inside the woodcocks.
2. Wrap the salted woodcocks in lard.
3. Roast them in abundant butter to which crushed juniper berries have been added.

title: Jiný způsob (Roasted Woodcocks - Third Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- woodcocks
- salt
- juniper berries
- hot water

## Directions

1. Melt butter browned on a baking pan.
2. Place cleaned uncut woodcocks tightly next to each other.
3. Salt them, roast first on the breasts, then on the back slowly.
4. Finally pour two to three tablespoons of hot water over them.
5. Sprinkle with crushed juniper berries and braise slowly again.
6. Woodcocks are roasted in moderate heat for at most half an hour.
7. Serve the woodcocks with strained sauce.

title: Jiný způsob (Rose Hip Sauce - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- pepper
- allspice
- three cloves
- wine vinegar
- roux with butter
- white wine
- burnt sugar
- rose hip preserves

## Directions

1. Slice onion
2. Add pepper, allspice and three cloves
3. Add wine vinegar
4. Simmer all
5. Mix in roux made with butter
6. Strain
7. Add four spoonfuls of wine
8. Add a piece of burnt sugar and two spoonfuls of rose hip preserves

title: Jiný způsob (Sponge Cake - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg whites
- egg yolks
- 36 dekagrams (24 lots) sugar
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) flour

## Directions

1. Make snow from 12 egg whites
2. Add the egg yolks and 36 dekagrams sugar
3. Beat for half an hour
4. Mix with 18 dekagrams flour
5. Bake in a buttered mold and decorate as desired

title: Jiný způsob (Strawberry Compote, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh strawberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean fresh strawberries and mix with sugar
2. After several hours pour off the juice and boil it
3. Shake the strawberries in it several times, then let the compote cool

title: Jiný způsob (Truffle Sauce - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- strong broth or wine

## Directions

1. Clean and peel truffles
2. Slice into thin slices
3. Braise in strong broth or wine until soft
4. Meanwhile prepare a tasty wine sauce
5. Pour the wine sauce over the truffles

title: Jiný způsob (Truffle Sauce - Third Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two to three truffles
- strong meat broth
- red wine

## Directions

1. Peel two to three truffles
2. Cut into small cubes
3. Cook in strong meat broth to which good red wine has been added
4. Strain after cooking
5. Simmer again

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát II (Potato Salad with Egg Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peeled potatoes
- salt
- three egg yolks
- Provençal oil
- chives
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the cooked peeled potatoes and salt them
2. Rub three hard egg yolks with Provençal oil
3. Add finely chopped chives and wine vinegar to the egg yolk mixture
4. Add potatoes to the sauce
5. Mix everything well
6. Arrange on a platter and garnish with fish

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát III (Potato Salad with Anchovies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- salt
- onion
- bacon
- good oil
- wine vinegar
- canned sardines

## Directions

1. Peel, slice, and salt the cooked potatoes
2. Finely chop half an onion
3. Remove all bones from bacon and chop finely
4. Add good oil and wine vinegar to the mixture
5. Mix everything well
6. Arrange on a platter
7. Decorate with sardines arranged in a cross pattern

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát IV (Potato and Apple Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peeled potatoes
- salt
- Meissen apples
- red wine vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel, slice, and lightly salt the cooked potatoes
2. Peel and slice several Meissen apples into thin slices
3. Add the apple slices to the potatoes
4. Pour red wine vinegar mixed with crushed sugar over everything
5. Mix well and arrange on a platter

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát V (Potato Salad with Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peeled potatoes
- vinegar
- salt
- onion
- apples
- pepper
- sugar
- oil

## Directions

1. Slice the cooked peeled potatoes directly into vinegar
2. Add salt, chopped onion, diced apples, pepper, sugar, and oil
3. Mix everything well
4. Can also be served warm by slicing potatoes into hot vinegar and mixing with the other ingredients

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát VI (Warm Potato Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram potatoes
- hot beef broth
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- onion

## Directions

1. Boil, peel, and slice one kilogram of potatoes
2. Place the potatoes on a platter and pour hot beef broth over them
3. Drain the broth
4. Add oil, vinegar, salt, pepper, a little sugar, and finely chopped onion
5. Mix everything well and serve

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát VII (Potato Salad with Parsley and Sardines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- oil
- vinegar
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- sardines
- olives

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the cooked potatoes
2. Mix with oil, vinegar, chopped parsley, salt, and pepper
3. Slice the sardines and add them along with olives that were pickled in vinegar
4. Alternatively, mix potatoes with parsley, chopped onion, sliced hard-boiled eggs, and sardines

title: Jiný způsob - Zemákový salát VIII (Potato Salad with Pickles and Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- sour cucumber
- bacon

## Directions

1. Slice the cooked potatoes and mix with oil, salt, pepper, chopped onion, finely sliced sour cucumber, and chopped bacon
2. Alternatively, first pour beef broth over the potatoes, then add the oil and other ingredients

title: Jiný způsob fazolí (Beans Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- soft water
- bacon
- sour cucumbers

## Directions

1. Pick through beans, wash and cook half-done in soft water
2. Dice bacon and add to beans
3. When soft, drain the water
4. Turn out onto a plate and surround with sour cucumbers

title: Jiný způsob kaše (Porridge Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several spoonfuls flour
- water
- 1 liter water
- 75 grams (5 lots) butter
- salt
- butter for frying
- fried onion

## Directions

1. Mix several spoonfuls of flour well in water
2. Boil 1 liter water with 75 grams butter and salt
3. Add the mixed flour and stir until a thick porridge forms
4. Before serving, pour brown fried butter and fried onion over it

title: Jiný způsob pečené krupice (Baked Semolina Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina
- milk
- butter
- egg yolks
- sugar
- lemon zest
- almonds
- salt
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook semolina in milk and let cool
2. To beaten butter add egg yolks, sugar, lemon zest, chopped almonds, several spoonfuls of salt, semolina and snow from 8 egg whites
3. Put everything in a greased mold and bake for a quarter hour

title: Jiný způsob přípravy Purée ze slepice (Alternative Method for Purée of Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- old hen
- beef broth
- parsley
- celery
- carrot

## Directions

1. Cook an old hen in beef broth until soft
2. Cut out the breasts and chop them finely
3. Pound the rest of the hen in a mortar
4. Put it in a pot and add chopped parsley, celery, and carrot
5. Pour in beef broth and let it cook for one hour
6. Pour it onto roll or pastry and the chopped breasts
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Jiný Způsob Ředkev (Red Radish Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- small red radish
- butter
- flour
- sugar
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Clean small red radish, remove green leaves leaving only the hearts
2. Melt fresh butter and place the radish coated in flour into it
3. After 5 minutes remove the radish onto a plate
4. Make a pale roux with flour and a piece of sugar caramelized to pink, strain this roux into beef broth, add the radish to it, cook and then place around beef roast

title: Jugged Rabbit (Předek Zajíce na Červeno)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare front quarter
- butter or lard
- thyme
- onion
- lemon zest
- bay leaf
- water
- vinegar
- brown flour
- not quite a full small cup of brown grated gingerbread
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the hare front quarter into pieces and place on a clay pot.
2. Add under it a piece of butter or lard, a little thyme, one onion cut into slices, a piece of lemon zest and a bay leaf.
3. Pour over enough water to come about half a finger's height above the hare.
4. Salt it, cover the pot and let it cook.
5. When the hare is almost cooked through, remove it to a plate.
6. Strain the sauce and add a little vinegar to it so the sauce is slightly sour.
7. Dust it lightly with brown flour and add to it not quite a full small cup of brown grated gingerbread.
8. Put a piece of sugar in a small pot, place on high heat and when it browns, watching carefully so it does not burn, dilute it with a little broth.
9. Add this now to the sauce, place the cooked hare in it, let it simmer a little in the sauce, and serve.

title: Jáhly (Millet)
body:
## Ingredients

- millet
- water
- salt
- butter
- onion
- milk
- grated gingerbread
- egg
- milk
- white flour

## Directions

1. Sort the millet and rinse it three times in boiling water
2. For two small cups of millet, use 1.5 quarts of water
3. Salt and let it cook with frequent stirring for 1.5 to 2 hours
4. Turn out onto a dish and dress with butter in which sliced onion has been fried
5. Alternatively, the sorted and rinsed millet can be cooked in milk, salted, sprinkled with grated gingerbread and dressed with melted butter
6. For a more delicate version, beat one egg in a small cup with a little milk and a tablespoon of white flour, pour this over the millet, stir well and let it cook for about another minute

title: Játra s citrónovou omáčkou (Liver with Lemon Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Liver
- Salt
- Butter
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the liver and slice it into thin pieces
2. Salt it, place on a baking sheet with butter, season with pepper and braise
3. When tender, remove and strain the sauce
4. Add the juice of one lemon to the sauce, return the liver to it and cook briefly

title: Játra se smetanou (Liver with Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Liver
- Salt
- Lard
- Pepper
- Flour
- Butter
- 3.5 deciliters sweet cream

## Directions

1. Remove all skin from the liver and salt it
2. Pierce with lard and season with pepper
3. Coat in flour and brown on both sides
4. Place in sweet cream and cook for a quarter hour so the sauce takes on flavor from the liver

title: Játra smažená (Fried Liver)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Salt
- Flour
- Eggs
- Bread roll
- Butter

## Directions

1. Remove all skin from veal liver and slice into thin pieces
2. Salt them, coat in flour, then in egg and grated bread roll
3. Fry in butter
4. Serve with vegetables or potato mash

title: Játrový svítek (Liver Roll)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Liver, half kilogram
- Three eggs
- Two garlic cloves
- Marjoram
- Salt
- Flour
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. Grate half kilogram of liver, add 3 eggs, 2 garlic cloves, a little grated marjoram, salt, flour and bread crumbs until a batter like pancake batter forms.
2. Grease a mold with butter, sprinkle with bread crumbs, place the mixture in it and bake; cut into cubes, place in a soup bowl and pour beef broth over it.

title: Játrový svítek (Liver Roulade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram liver
- 3 eggs
- 2 cloves garlic
- a little grated marjoram
- salt
- nutmeg
- grated bread roll
- butter
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Grate the liver and add 3 eggs, 2 cloves garlic, a little grated marjoram, salt, nutmeg and grated bread roll until a batter forms
2. Grease a mold with butter, sprinkle with grated bread roll, place the mixture in it and bake
3. Cut into cubes, place in a soup bowl and pour over with beef broth

title: Jíškový koláč (Roux Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter kilogram butter
- half kilogram (one pound) flour

## Directions

1. Place butter on a baking pan with half kilogram of flour, make a roux (jíška) and allow to cool.

title: Jíškoý koláč (Orange Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram butter
- 1/2 kilogram (1 pound) flour
- 1/4 kilogram sugar
- Juice from 1/2 orange
- 3 decagrams (2 lots) chopped candied orange peel
- 4 grated hard egg yolks
- Raw eggs (as much as needed to make dough like for sweet rolls)
- Jam or preserves for coating
- Chopped almonds, specially roasted, for topping

## Directions

1. Place butter on a baking sheet with flour, make a pink roux and let it cool
2. On a work surface, add to it 1/4 kilogram sugar, juice from 1/2 orange, 3 decagrams chopped candied orange peel, 4 grated hard egg yolks and as many raw eggs as needed to make a dough like for sweet rolls
3. Roll out the dough on a baking sheet, make an edge from dough and bake
4. Brush with jam and sprinkle with chopped, specially roasted almonds

title: Kachna divoká (Wild Duck)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild duck
- vinegar
- water
- pepper
- new spice
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon peel
- salt
- 2 onions
- roux
- burnt sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck the wild duck except for the head, remove the innards, and lard with bacon if desired.
2. Place on a baking sheet and baste with vinegar mixed with water.
3. Add pepper, new spice, thyme, bay leaf, lemon peel, and salt.
4. Add 2 sliced onions and let it rest for 24 hours.
5. Place everything in the oven and roast.
6. Once roasted, add roux and a piece of burnt sugar, then strain.
7. Chop the duck and serve with the sauce.
8. Alternatively, add grated bread to the sauce instead of roux, strain the sauce around the chopped duck, and garnish with lemon.
9. If the sauce is not sour enough, add juice from one lemon.

title: Kachna divoká (Wild Duck)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild duck
- salt
- bacon
- wine vinegar
- water
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pluck the wild duck except for the head.
2. Clean it and prepare for roasting; optionally lard with bacon.
3. Place on a baking sheet and baste with wine vinegar mixed with water.
4. Add pepper and other spices as desired.

title: Kachna s Houbami (Duck with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- One cleaned and gutted duck
- Rendered bacon or lard
- Onion, sliced
- Peppercorns
- Strong broth

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned and gutted duck in a roasting pan on rendered bacon or lard.
2. Add sliced onion and several peppercorns and let it braise a little.
3. Baste the duck with a few tablespoons of strong broth, cover the pan with a lid and continue braising.

title: Kachna s houbami (Duck with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and plucked duck
- rendered lard or bacon
- sliced onion
- peppercorns
- strong broth
- two or three dozen small peeled mushroom heads or other fresh mushrooms
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned and plucked duck in a pot on rendered lard or bacon
2. Add sliced onion and a few peppercorns and let it braise for a while
3. Pour several spoonfuls of strong broth over the duck
4. Cover the pot with a lid and let it braise again, turning the duck at least once
5. When the duck is more than halfway done, add two or three dozen small peeled mushroom heads or other fresh mushrooms
6. Let everything braise until tender
7. Remove the duck to a dish and surround it with mushroom heads
8. Strain the sauce, mix it with a little tomato sauce and pour it over the duck

title: Kachní paštika (Duck Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Plucked and parboiled duck
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Pâté dough

## Directions

1. Cut the duck along the backbone.
2. Remove the bones, cut the breasts, lard them.
3. Season thoroughly inside and out with salt and pepper, nutmeg, and cloves.
4. Place in pâté dough form and bake.
5. This pâté is very tasty when served cold.

title: Kakao (Cocoa)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole cocoa beans
- water or milk
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Roast whole cocoa beans like coffee with their shells
2. Pound them finely
3. Boil in water or milk
4. Mix with cream and sweeten like coffee
5. Use one teaspoon of cocoa per cup, though this can be adjusted to taste

title: Kakao Creme (Cocoa Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- four to six decagrams ground cocoa
- cold cream
- three deciliters cream
- sugar
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix the ground cocoa with cold cream
2. Add three deciliters of cream
3. Add a little sugar and six egg yolks
4. Mix well

title: Kapaná polévka (Soup with Dropped Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- salt
- water
- eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. Let a piece of fresh butter heat up and pour as much boiling salted water on it as you need soup
2. Put two eggs in a cup and only as much flour as to make a thin dripping dough
3. Mix well and drip slowly into the boiling soup, which you must constantly stir so that large lumps do not form
4. If the dripping is too thin it will thicken by adding flour, if too thick it will thin by adding a few drops of water
5. You can also prepare this soup in another way by browning the salted water with butter roux and a piece of onion, finely chopped and browned until golden
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Kapoun s račími ocásky nebo mušlemi (Capon with Crayfish Tails or Mussels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- 1 liter and 4 deciliters water
- 6 dekagrams butter
- parsley root
- piece of celery
- carrot
- salt
- clove flower
- shells and claws of cooked crayfish
- oysters or mussels cooked in wine, snails, or stuffed frogs

## Directions

1. Clean the capon and divide into 4 pieces
2. Place on a baking dish with 1 liter and 4 deciliters water, 6 dekagrams butter, parsley root, piece of celery, carrot, salt and clove flower
3. Braise until the capon is completely tender, then divide into smaller pieces
4. Make a pale butter roux, strain it into the sauce
5. Crush the shells and claws of cooked crayfish and strain through a sieve into the sauce
6. Simmer the sauce, place the capon in it for a moment, then arrange on a platter
7. Pour the sauce over it and arrange crayfish tails around it
8. Instead of crayfish tails, oysters, mussels cooked in wine, snails or stuffed frogs may be used

title: Kapr marinovaný (Marinated Carp)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- butter
- onion
- rosemary
- pepper
- thyme
- bay leaves
- allspice
- ginger
- capers
- lemon peel
- celery (optional)
- parsley (optional)
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Clean the carp and salt it
2. Place on a baking pan, supporting with sticks so it does not touch the bottom
3. Brush with butter in the oven until cooked through
4. Slice onion into thin pieces and mix with rosemary, several peppercorns, a sprig of thyme, 3 bay leaves, allspice, a piece of whole ginger, a handful of nice capers, lemon peel cut into small sticks, and optionally celery and parsley
5. Mix everything together
6. Cut the fish into pieces and layer them one on top of another, sprinkling with the mixture between layers, or arrange them side by side
7. Cover the dish well with a tight-fitting lid or plate, ensuring the lid is not made of tin, copper, or other metal
8. Place in cold storage
9. The marinated fish will keep for two months in winter
10. When serving, add vinegar and oil

title: Kapr na modro (Blued Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Salt
- Vinegar (wine)
- Celery
- Parsley
- Onion
- Garlic cloves
- Whole pepper
- Allspice
- 5 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- Ginger
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Clean the carp without scaling, remove entrails and cut into pieces
2. Arrange on a platter
3. Heat 1 pint wine vinegar with a little salt
4. Pour the hot vinegar over the carp, which will turn a nice blue color
5. Drain the vinegar into a pot and add more vinegar
6. Add celery, parsley, onion (all cut into slices), 3-4 garlic cloves, whole pepper, allspice, 5 cloves, 1 bay leaf, a piece of ginger, and lemon peel cut into thin strips
7. Salt and simmer for half an hour
8. Arrange the carp in the pot
9. Add enough additional vinegar to completely cover the fish
10. Cover tightly with a plate and lid so steam cannot escape
11. Cook gently for half an hour, being careful the fish does not burn
12. Carefully remove the fish and arrange on a platter
13. Strain the sauce through a sieve over the fish
14. Sprinkle with lemon peel cut into thin strips
15. Cover with celery leaves
16. Cover well and set in a cold place
17. When serving, provide a bowl with vinegar, oil and pepper

title: Kapr na modro (Carp Blue)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- live carp
- vinegar
- water
- onion
- carrot
- parsley
- celery
- bay leaf
- thyme
- allspice
- pepper
- ginger
- fish bladder (optional)

## Directions

1. Kill and clean the carp without scraping scales, removing all blood
2. Cut into neat pieces and arrange on a baking dish
3. Salt the pieces
4. Heat vinegar mixed with water until boiling and pour over the carp
5. In a separate baking dish, prepare vinegar and water with sliced onion, carrot cut into slices, parsley and celery, bay leaf and thyme, allspice, pepper, and a piece of whole ginger
6. Arrange the fish neatly in this mixture
7. Cover and decorate around with the vegetables that were cooked together
8. Optionally add a piece of fish bladder to help it set
9. Strain the mixture over the fish and place on ice to chill

title: Kapr na Modro (Carp in Blue)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Good wine vinegar
- Water
- Onion
- Carrot
- Parsley
- Celery
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- New spices
- Pepper
- Piece of ginger
- Salt

## Directions

1. Tear open the carp and don't scrape it (usually a fresh, scaleless carp is taken), remove all blood, cut into neat pieces, arrange on a baking sheet, salt it.
2. Cook good wine vinegar with water, and when the mixture is boiling, pour it over the fish.
3. In the meantime, put vinegar with water on a baking sheet, cut onion into slices, add a nice carrot also cut into slices, parsley and celery, bay leaf and thyme, new spices, pepper, a piece of whole ginger.
4. Neatly arrange the fish in this, cook it, carefully place on a platter, arrange decoration around it from the vegetables which are cooked together.

title: Kapr na Másle (Carp with Butter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- New butter
- Salt
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the carp, place sticks crosswise on a pan or baking sheet so it doesn't fall to the bottom.
2. Put a piece of new butter in a cup, coat the carp with it, salt it, add lemon cut into quarters.
3. This method prepares the fish most quickly.
4. Can be baked either on stove or in oven, but if baking in oven, take care that it is not undercooked inside.

title: Kapr neb losos v rosolu (Jellied Carp or Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups vinegar
- 2 cups white wine
- Water
- 1 large onion sliced into rounds
- 1 clove garlic
- 20 grains pepper
- 20 grains allspice
- Ginger
- 6 cloves
- 1½ sprigs thyme
- 1½ bay leaves
- Lemon peel
- Gelatine
- Salt
- 4 pounds fish
- 2 whole eggs with shells
- White muslin (for salmon)
- Lemon slices
- Capers
- Mayonnaise (for salmon)

## Directions

1. In a large stone pot, combine vinegar, white wine and enough water to make it pleasantly sour
2. Season appropriately with salt
3. Add sliced onion, garlic, pepper, allspice, ginger, cloves, thyme, bay leaves, lemon peel, and a little gelatine
4. Cover and simmer gently for half an hour, then strain into another pot
5. When it returns to a boil, add the cut fish (about 4 pounds) and cook 15-20 minutes
6. For salmon, wrap each piece in white muslin to keep the nice pink color
7. Remove fish and arrange neatly on a deep platter
8. Break 2 whole eggs with shells into the sauce, simmer a little and strain through a thick cloth or flannel bag into a clean pot
9. Pour this jelly over the prepared fish and let cool in a cold place
10. When serving, dip the mold in hot water and turn out onto a flat platter
11. Garnish around the fish with lemon slices, capers, etc.
12. Serve mayonnaise with salmon

title: Kapr pečený s kyselou smetanou (Carp with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- ground pepper
- butter
- bay leaves
- sour cream
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the carp and cut into pieces, sprinkle with salt and ground pepper.
2. Grease a baking pan with butter and place two bay leaves in it.
3. Arrange the cut carp in the pan.
4. Pour a little good sour cream over it, add juice from one lemon.
5. Bake in the oven until golden, frequently basting with sour cream.
6. Carefully remove from the pan onto a dish, strain the sauce and serve in a small bowl.

title: Kapr pečený s kyselou smetanou (Carp with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- 2 bay leaves
- Sour cream
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the carp, cut into pieces and sprinkle with salt and ground pepper
2. Butter a baking pan and place 2 bay leaves in it
3. Arrange the cut carp in the pan
4. Pour a little good sour cream over it
5. Add the juice of 1 lemon
6. Bake in the oven until golden, basting frequently with sour cream during cooking
7. Carefully remove from the pan and arrange on a platter
8. Strain the sauce and serve in a small bowl

title: Kapr s hnědou omáčkou a se smržettii (Carp with Brown Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- butter
- salt
- ground pepper
- ground cloves
- onion
- thyme
- white flour
- broth
- wine vinegar
- anchovies
- morel mushrooms

## Directions

1. Grease a pot well with butter, arrange the cleaned and cut into pieces carp in it, sprinkle with salt, ground pepper and two ground cloves.
2. Add a large onion finely chopped, a piece of thyme and let it stew.
3. Place a piece of butter in the pot and make a brown roux with some white flour.
4. Pour in a pint of broth or some meat soup and a little wine vinegar, mix well, let it cook and strain through a sieve.
5. Pour this sauce over the carp, add three cleaned and finely chopped anchovies, about twenty well-cleaned whole morel mushrooms and let it simmer a little.
6. Arrange the fish on a dish, surround it with morels and serve the sauce in a small bowl.
7. If the sauce is too thick, you can add a little soup or hot water.

title: Kapr s Kmínem (Carp with Caraway)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned carp
- Caraway
- Salt
- New butter
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. The cleaned carp is seasoned with caraway or caraway is cooked and it is poured with just caraway water, salted, placed on a pan.
2. Add a piece of new butter, cover with a lid, braise until soft.
3. Carefully place on a platter with a spoon and add the sauce to it.
4. Usually grated horseradish is added to it.

title: Kapr s Černou Omáčkou (Carp with Black Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Good wine vinegar
- Sweet beer
- Good wine vinegar
- Carrot
- Onion
- Celery
- Parsley root
- Handful of nutmeats
- Handful of raisins
- Salt
- Grated gingerbread
- Piece of white sugar
- Piece of burned sugar

## Directions

1. Tear open the carp, remove entrails, carefully remove gall, discard intestines, save roe or soft roe.
2. Rinse the carp inside with good wine vinegar, save the blood in a separate cup, save all blood during preparation.
3. Remove scales from the carp, place on a baking sheet and pour sweet beer over it until it floats.
4. Add a little good wine vinegar, add carrot cut into slices, onion, a whole small or half large celery, parsley root, a handful of nutmeats and a handful of raisins.
5. Salt and cook well for a quarter hour, flame should only act on one side.
6. Carefully place the fish on a platter.
7. Add grated gingerbread, a piece of white sugar and a piece of burned sugar to the mixture in which the fish was cooked—pour in the blood.
8. Cook everything for a while, then remove parsley, carrot and celery, pour the remaining mixture over the carp.

title: Kapr Vařený (Boiled Carp)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-fat carp
- Salt
- Water
- Piece of butter
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the well-fat carp, salt it, place on a baking sheet, pour water over it, add a piece of butter and braise it.
2. Carefully place on a platter, garnish with lemon cut either into slices or quarters.

title: Kapucínka (Capucin Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart white wine
- a piece of sugar
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- lemon peel
- six egg yolks or three whole eggs
- one tablespoon flour
- one small cup water
- one tablespoon butter
- half pound black bread, grated
- sugar
- spices

## Directions

1. Boil one quart of white wine with a piece of sugar, a piece of whole cinnamon and lemon peel
2. remove from heat, remove scum and strain
3. in another pot beat well six egg yolks or three whole eggs with one tablespoon of flour and one small cup of water
4. pour into boiling wine
5. heat one tablespoon of butter until red
6. fry half pound of grated black bread in it
7. add a little sugar and spices to it
8. let it cool and turn out into a soup bowl
9. heat the wine soup mentioned above and pour it into the bowl over the prepared bread
10. if you want a weaker soup, use half wine and half water
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Kapusta (Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- pale roux
- smoked meat

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves and slice the cabbage into cubes, pour water over it and scald it.
2. Rub two cloves of garlic with salt, add a little pepper, a piece of fresh butter, and braise until the cabbage is soft.
3. Strain very little roux through a sieve, chop a piece of smoked meat and add to it, just enough so the cabbage gets the taste of smoked meat, which is placed in the middle of the platter and cabbage around it.

title: Kapusta (Colewort)
body:
## Ingredients

- kapusta
- voda - studená a vařící
- máslo nebo sádlo
- cukr - kousek
- mouka
- pepř - tlučený
- zázvor - bílý ustrouhaný
- česnek - utřený
- sůl

## Directions

1. Ober s kapusty svrchní lupeny a rozkroj ji na čtyry čtvrtky
2. Vypeř ve studené vodě a urovnej na mísu
3. Polej vařící vodou, kterou po chvilce sceď
4. Dej na kuthan kousek másla neb sádla, přidej spařenou kapustu
5. Přidej trochu soli a kousek cukru a nech to do měkka dusit
6. Udělej z másla hnědou jíšku a rozmíchej ji horkou vodou nebo polévkou
7. Přidej trochu tlučeného pepře a ustrouhaného bílého zázvoru
8. Dle libosti také spárek neb dva s máslem a solí utřeného česneku

title: Kapusta - jiný způsob (Cabbage - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- garlic
- caraway seeds
- pepper
- beef broth
- rose hip puree

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage, separate each leaf, cut out the stem, pour hot water over it to scald it, drain the water.
2. Rub 3 cloves of garlic, add a little caraway seeds and pepper, pour good beef broth; braise until the cabbage is soft, and add rose hip puree.

title: Kapustěnka (Cabbage and Cauliflower Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two cabbages
- 2 heads cauliflower
- beef soup
- handful mushrooms
- butter
- parsley
- pepper
- liver and heart of 3 chickens
- roll
- milk
- flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the cabbages and cauliflower in beef soup
2. When cooked, carefully remove them, cut off the cauliflower florets and chop the cabbage into strips
3. Slice the mushrooms, rinse them, add butter, fresh chopped parsley, pepper and stew
4. Chop the chicken livers and hearts that were boiled in beef soup
5. Slice the roll into patties, dip in milk, then flour, then egg
6. Mix the livers, hearts and mushrooms, place on a roll patty, top with another slice
7. Roll in grated roll and fry until golden
8. Mix with cauliflower and cabbage, place in a soup bowl and pour with good beef soup

title: Kapustěnka (Colewort Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cabbages
- two cauliflower florets
- beef broth
- handful of mushrooms
- butter
- chopped green parsley
- pepper
- liver and heart from three chickens
- bread roll
- milk
- flour
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook two cabbages and two cauliflower florets in beef broth, then carefully remove them, cut off the cauliflower florets and cut the cabbage into thin strips
2. Chop a handful of mushrooms, clean them well and add to them a piece of butter, a little chopped green parsley, a little pepper and let them stew
3. Chop the liver and heart from three chickens cooked in beef broth
4. Slice the bread roll, soak in milk, dip in flour and then in egg
5. Mix the liver, heart and mushrooms, place some of this mixture on one bread slice, cover with another slice, coat in grated bread crumbs and fry until golden
6. Put in a bowl with the sliced cabbage and cauliflower and pour good beef broth over it
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Karbanátky (Meatballs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts beef, calf and pork meat
- fat or butter
- eggs
- salt
- grated bread roll
- nutmeg
- grated zest of half a lemon
- crushed rusks
- butter for frying
- water and sour cream for sauce

## Directions

1. Mix equal quantities of beef, calf and pork meat with fat or butter, eggs, salt, grated bread roll, nutmeg and grated lemon zest
2. Form meatballs, roll in crushed rusks and fry in butter, basting with butter meanwhile
3. A little water and sour cream may be added to the sauce

title: Karbanátky (Meatballs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram ground or chopped pork
- 1/4 kilogram ground beef
- salt
- spices
- pepper
- 2 cloves garlic
- onion
- lemon zest
- 60 grams rendered pork fat
- grated bread roll
- 2 cheese eggs
- lard

## Directions

1. Mix meat with salt, spices, pepper, minced garlic, finely chopped onion, lemon zest, rendered fat, grated bread, and eggs
2. Form small balls from the mixture, flatten, and fry in lard
3. Usually serve with potato salad

title: Karbanátky na jiný způsob (Codfish Balls Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked and cooled codfish
- parsley
- red pepper
- lemon
- cream or milk
- butter
- flour
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- lard
- green parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the cooked and cooled codfish and chop the meat finely
2. Add a spoonful of finely chopped parsley, a little red pepper and the juice of one lemon and mix well
3. Heat a cup of cream or milk
4. Mix a spoonful of butter with two spoonfuls of flour and add to the boiling cream, let it boil for three minutes
5. Mix this sauce into the codfish and mix well, spread on a dish to cool
6. Once cooled, form into patties, dip in beaten egg, coat in grated white bread or crushed crackers and fry in hot lard
7. Garnish with green parsley

title: Karbanátky z hovězího masa se zemáky (Beef Meatballs with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram beef
- kidney fat
- fried onion
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 kilogram cooked cold potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop beef with kidney fat, fried onion, and cooked potatoes
2. Mix with eggs, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
3. Form meatballs, coat with breadcrumbs
4. Fry in butter

title: Karbanátky z tresky (Codfish Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- Soaked and cooked codfish
- potatoes
- butter
- eggs
- pepper
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the soaked and cooked codfish and chop the meat finely with a chopper
2. Take an equal amount of potatoes and mix them together
3. Add a spoonful of butter, one egg, a little pepper and mix well
4. Form into small patties, coat in flour and fry on a griddle in half lard and half butter

title: Karbanátky z uzeniny (Patties from Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Half kilogram of cooked marbled smoked meat
- Half kilogram of cooked mashed potatoes
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons of flour
- Pepper
- Flowers
- Rusks, crushed
- Butter

## Directions

1. Chop half kilogram of cooked marbled smoked meat.
2. Mix with half kilogram of cooked mashed potatoes, 3 eggs, 2 tablespoons of flour, pepper and flowers.
3. Form patties from the mixture, sprinkle with crushed rusks and fry in butter.
4. Usually they are served with vegetables.

title: Karbanátky ze Zbytků (Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover meat
- Three eggs
- Pepper
- Mixed spices
- Ginger
- Caraway seeds
- Lemon peel
- Salt
- Lard
- Potatoes
- Milk
- Egg yolks
- Lard

## Directions

1. Chop all leftover meat finely
2. If you have about three cups of meat, add three eggs, pinch of pepper, mixed spices, ginger, a little caraway seed, chopped lemon peel and salt to taste
3. Mix well and shape into small round pieces
4. Fry in lard until reddish
5. Serve with potato mash made as follows: peel potatoes, salt and boil; drain well and add sweet milk, two egg yolks and a tablespoon of lard; mash until thick; arrange on a dish and pour over with browned butter

title: Karbanátky ze Šunky (Ham Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups cooked ham or marbled smoked meat
- Four cups cooked potatoes
- Two tablespoons flour
- Three eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour or breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Chop two cups cooked ham or marbled smoked meat
2. Add four cups cooked potatoes pressed through a sieve or strainer
3. Add two tablespoons flour, three eggs, salt, pepper and flour to taste
4. Mix well together
5. Shape into croquettes
6. Coat in breadcrumbs
7. Fry in butter or lard until golden
8. Serve with vegetables, especially salad with sour cream

title: Karbonátky nebo klobásy z rybího masa (Fish Cutlets or Sausages)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish meat scraps
- garlic clove
- marjoram
- green parsley
- flower
- salt
- 4 eggs
- 3 soaked bread rolls
- parsley root piece
- bread crumbs
- optional lemon

## Directions

1. Remove all small bones from the fish meat scraps and chop finely.
2. Add a clove of garlic, a bit of marjoram, green parsley, flower, salt, 4 eggs and 3 soaked bread rolls.
3. Form cutlets from this mixture, inserting a piece of parsley root instead of a bone.
4. Coat in egg and grated bread.
5. Serve with sliced lemon or any vegetable.
6. The mixture can also be made into small sausages.

title: Karbonátky z rybího masa (Fish Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish meat scraps
- onion
- parsley
- thyme
- butter
- various spices
- water
- wine
- vinegar
- white sugar
- burnt sugar

## Directions

1. Remove bones from the fish meat scraps and place on a baking sheet with onion, parsley, thyme, butter, various spices and a spoonful of water.
2. Braise until brown.
3. Pour a little wine and vinegar over it and make a liaison.
4. Add a piece of white sugar and a piece of burnt sugar.

title: Kardinální polévka (Cardinal Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish butter
- 2-3 tablespoons roux
- strong meat soup
- fine groats or rice
- egg yolks
- cooked crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Make a roux from crayfish butter with 2-3 tablespoons roux
2. Cook it with strong meat soup, add fine groats or rice
3. When serving, beat in a few egg yolks and add cooked crayfish tails

title: Karfiol (Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- butter
- sugar
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cut cauliflower and carefully separate the florets, inspect well to remove any insects, rinse in several waters
2. Pour clean cold water over it, salt and cook
3. When completely tender, drain the water and add a piece of butter, sweeten to taste and add half a cup of good sweet cream, or retain a little of the cooking water
4. Make a thin roux from a spoonful of butter, brown it and sweeten to taste
5. The version with cream is more delicious
6. A few florets of cauliflower can always be added to broth to make it very tasty

title: Karfiol pro Zimní Potřebu (Pickled Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- young tender cauliflower
- salt
- water
- white vinegar
- sugar
- mixed spices

## Directions

1. Take young, tender cauliflower (which is most flavorful).
2. Clean it thoroughly and cut it into small pieces, about half an inch to an inch long.
3. Salt it well and let it sit for 16 hours.
4. Pour off the salt water and replace with fresh water, repeating this until all the salt is rinsed out.
5. Spread it on a colander to drain the water.
6. Meanwhile, prepare good white vinegar in an enameled pot and place it on the stove.
7. For every cup of vinegar, add an equal amount of sugar.
8. For every four cups of vinegar, add one spoon of mixed spices.
9. Bring everything to a boil.
10. Add the drained cauliflower (which must not be waterlogged) and boil well for 15 minutes.
11. Pack it hot into jars and seal well.
12. Alternatively, cauliflower preserved in mustard is also good: prepare as above but use less sugar and when it comes to a boil and has cooked for about 10 minutes, thicken it with mustard powder mixed in vinegar in a cup (to prevent lumps), and preserve like a sauce. This will be thicker. For one head of cauliflower use two cups of vinegar, one cup of sugar, a small teaspoon of mustard, and two large spoons of mustard powder.

title: Karfiol s Omáčkou (Cauliflower with Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- butter
- beef broth
- lemon juice
- parsley
- nutmeg
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook cleaned cauliflower in salted water and when done, remove with a skimming spoon to a dish
2. Pour butter sauce over it mixed with juice of one lemon and a little cauliflower water
3. To prepare the sauce: make a good butter roux, mix with beef broth in the desired amount, add a little parsley and nutmeg, let the sauce simmer and pour over prepared cauliflower
4. Alternatively: cook cauliflower in salted water and when almost done, drain the water and pour a little good milk over it to reach about the same level as the cauliflower, add butter and pepper, and finish cooking
5. Before removing from heat, thicken with a little flour roux mixed in milk

title: Karfiol se Smetanou (Creamed Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- egg yolks
- flour
- sweet cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean cauliflower, keep longer stems, rinse in cold water and cook in salted water, not too soft
2. Drain on a sieve
3. Mix 8 egg yolks with 2 tablespoons flour smoothly in two cups of cream and blend with the yolks
4. Place the bowl in another bowl with hot water and whisk with a whisk until everything thickens
5. Then pour through a sieve and whisk until cooled
6. Arrange cauliflower neatly on a dish well-buttered and pour the cooled cream over it
7. Place the dish in the oven on a trivet and bake
8. Brush with butter once more during baking
9. Bake for half an hour total

title: Karfiol se Sýrem (Cauliflower with Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower florets
- Parmesan cheese
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook whole cauliflower florets until tender
2. Place on a dish
3. Sprinkle with finely grated Parmesan cheese
4. Pour melted, reddish-brown butter over the top

title: Karfiol Smažený (Fried Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cauliflower
- Flour or beaten egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Lard

## Directions

1. Prepare cooked cauliflower by slicing it into cutlets
2. Coat in flour or beaten egg
3. Coat in breadcrumbs
4. Fry in equal parts butter and lard until golden brown

title: Karfiol Smažený (Fried Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cauliflower
- flour
- egg

## Directions

1. Fry cauliflower like fish
2. Cut cooked cauliflower into slices and coat in flour or beaten egg, then coat in breadcrumbs

title: Karfiol Zadělávaný (Creamed Cauliflower)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower florets
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Pale roux
- Spices
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower florets and remove the green leaves
2. Cook in salted water to which fresh butter has been added
3. When tender, place on a dish
4. Make a pale butter sauce, strain it through the water in which the cauliflower was cooked, add spices, squeeze juice from one lemon and simmer until thickened
5. Place the cauliflower in the sauce

title: Karfiolový Salát (Cauliflower Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower, divided into small florets
- salted water
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Divide cauliflower into small florets
2. Cook in salted water until tender
3. Drain well
4. Dress with oil, vinegar, pepper and salt

title: Karlovarský suchar (Carlsbad Twice-Baked Cookie)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams finely beaten, sifted sugar
- 8 eggs
- lemon zest, grated
- 200 grams finely beaten almonds

## Directions

1. Together and with constant stirring, roast 250 grams finely beaten sifted sugar, 8 eggs, grated lemon zest, and 200 grams finely beaten almonds on a warm surface for about half an hour
2. Knead everything and place into buttered molds
3. Bake slowly with gentle heat until golden
4. The next day, cut the cookie into thin slices
5. Carefully toast on both sides until brown

title: Kartiolová omáčka (Cauliflower Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams butter
- flour
- nutmeg
- water
- egg yolks
- crayfish butter
- cauliflower
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Brown 125 grams of butter
2. Add two spoonfuls of flour and a little grated nutmeg
3. Pour in 1/4 liter of water in which cauliflower has been cooked
4. Let all the liquid simmer with frequent stirring
5. Add two to three egg yolks to the sauce
6. Season with crayfish butter
7. Pour over cauliflower cooked until soft in water
8. Alternatively add a little sour cream to the egg yolks, blend together and omit crayfish butter

title: Karty (Cards)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 ounces fine sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 2 ounces flour
- a little ground cinnamon
- cloves
- flower
- a little finely grated lemon zest
- egg white
- split almonds
- fine sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Cream 2 tablespoons butter until frothy
2. Add 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks
3. Add 2 ounces fine sugar, 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract, 2 ounces flour, a little ground cinnamon, cloves, flower, and a little finely grated lemon zest
4. Mix everything well, work into dough on a board
5. Roll out to the thickness of a straw and cut into square cards
6. Brush with egg white, border with split almonds, sprinkle with fine sugar
7. Bake on a buttered sheet until pale yellow
8. Alternatively, you can bake them first, then coat with a glaze and then border with almonds

title: Karty (Cards)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- one whole egg
- two egg yolks
- two ounces fine sugar
- half teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub two tablespoons butter until it froths
2. Add one whole egg, two egg yolks, two ounces fine sugar and half teaspoon vanilla

title: Kaše krupičná (Farina Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 2 small cups semolina
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- hot melted butter

## Directions

1. Pour a quart of milk into a pot and bring it to a boil
2. Gradually sprinkle in two small cups of fine semolina while stirring constantly
3. Move the pot to the side where it won't boil vigorously and let it cook for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring frequently
4. Salt it, stir, and turn out onto a dish
5. Sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon and dress with hot melted butter

title: Kaše krupičná (Farina Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart milk
- two cups fine farina
- salt
- sugar and cinnamon
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Pour one quart of milk into a pot and when it begins to boil, gradually sprinkle two cups of fine farina into it with constant stirring
2. Move it to the side where it will not boil vigorously and let it cook 10 to 15 minutes, stirring frequently
3. Then salt it, mix and pour onto a platter, sprinkle with sugar mixed with ground cinnamon and grease with melted butter

title: Kaše kukuřičná (Cornmeal Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup cornmeal
- cold water
- 2 quarts milk or half milk and half water
- salt
- sweet cream, milk, or syrup

## Directions

1. Mix one small cup of cornmeal in cold water to make a thin batter like leaven
2. If cooking in a double boiler, bring two quarts of milk or half milk and half water to a boil
3. If cooking on direct heat, use only two quarts of water as milk would easily burn
4. When the milk or water begins to boil, slowly pour in the cornmeal batter and let it cook for about two hours
5. Serve hot with sweet cream, milk, or syrup

title: Kaše ovesná (Oatmeal Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk, or half water and half milk
- 1 teaspoon butter
- salt
- oatmeal
- sweet cream or milk

## Directions

1. Take a quart of milk or half water and half milk, add a teaspoon of butter, salt it and bring to a boil
2. When it boils, sprinkle in the oatmeal
3. If cooking in a double boiler, it requires no stirring
4. If cooking on direct heat, stir frequently
5. Cook for half an hour or longer, either in a double boiler or on gentle heat
6. Serve with sweet cream or milk

title: Kaše pražená (Dried Farina Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons fresh butter
- two small cups farina
- hot well water
- salt

## Directions

1. Put about two tablespoons of fresh butter on a pan to heat, pour two small cups of farina into it and let it roast until golden, you must constantly stir so it does not burn and no lumps form
2. Then pour in enough hot well water until you have a thick mush, stirring constantly, salt it and let it boil for a while, then pour hot melted butter over the mush

title: Kaše rýžová (Rice Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 17.5 deciliters milk (5 tablespoons)
- 5.25 deciliters rinsed rice (1.25 tablespoons)
- salt
- fresh butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Add 5.25 deciliters of rinsed rice to 17.5 deciliters of milk, add a little salt, a piece of fresh butter, a piece of sugar, and stir over the stove until it thickens
2. Place on a plate and sprinkle with sugar or honey

title: Kaše rýžová - Jiný způsob (Rice Porridge - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram rice
- 14 dekagrams sugar (8 lots)
- 4 egg yolks, beaten
- 7 deciliters sour cream or good milk (2 tablespoons)
- salt
- grated roll crumbs

## Directions

1. Rinse 1/2 kilogram of rice in cold water, add 14 dekagrams of sugar, 4 beaten egg yolks, 7 deciliters of sour cream or good milk and a little salt
2. Mix well, pour onto a buttered, grated roll crumb-sprinkled baking sheet and bake until golden
3. Turn out onto a plate and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Kaše z brášku (Pea Purée)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peas, somewhat firm
- butter
- broth
- salt
- sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Put somewhat firm peas in a pot with butter, cover and braise for a while, shaking often
2. After a quarter hour, add broth, press through a sieve
3. Thin the porridge with broth, salt it, add a piece of sugar and a little flour

title: Kaše z brášku - Jiný způsob (Pea Purée - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- salt water
- butter or bacon
- butter or cracklings
- smoked meat or sausages

## Directions

1. Cook peas in salted water and press through a sieve so that only the indigestible skins remain on the sieve
2. Put the pressed porridge in a baking dish with melted butter or sliced bacon, stir while cooking, pour with butter or cracklings
3. Smoked meat or sausages can be added to it
4. If hard water must be used or if the peas do not want to cook, add a little soda, which softens any water

title: Kaše z jahlí (Barley Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter barley
- 1 liter milk
- 10 egg yolks
- salt
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) sugar
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Scald 1/4 liter barley
2. Cook in 1 liter milk and press through a sieve
3. When cooled, add 10 egg yolks, salt, 9 decagrams sugar, lemon zest rubbed with sugar

title: Kaše z jahlí (Barley Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter barley
- 1 liter milk
- 10 egg yolks
- salt
- 9 dekagrams sugar (6 lots)
- lemon zest, rubbed with sugar
- 6 dekagrams ground almonds
- 10 egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Scald 1/4 liter of barley
2. Cook in 1 liter of milk and press through a sieve
3. When cooled, add 10 egg yolks, salt, 9 dekagrams of sugar, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, 6 dekagrams of ground almonds, and stiffly beaten snow from 10 egg whites
4. Bake in a greased mold

title: Kaše z krupice (Barley Grits Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 1 small cup barley grits or torn grits, rice, or oat grits
- salt
- melted butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Barley grits, torn grits, rice, oat grits, etc. are best cooked in a double boiler, where the lower pot contains water and the upper pot is set into it for cooking
2. In such a pot, the food will not burn or become scorched, as easily happens when cooked directly on heat
3. For a quart of milk, take one small cup of grits or torn grits
4. Rinse them in several waters, the last time in hot water, then add to the milk
5. Cover and let cook for two hours
6. When cooked, salt it and stir, turn out onto a dish and pour hot melted butter over it
7. You can also sprinkle it with sugar and cinnamon

title: Kaše z mouky (Flour Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters milk
- piece of fresh butter
- piece of sugar
- salt to taste
- flour, as much as needed to make a thick porridge

## Directions

1. Mix milk, butter, sugar and salt
2. Add flour while stirring to make a thick porridge
3. Sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Kaše z pohanky (Buckwheat Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- milk or water
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- buckwheat

## Directions

1. Place milk or water either on the stove or in the oven, add a piece of butter, sugar (this can also be omitted), and salt
2. Pour buckwheat into it and stir until it thickens
3. Finally add a little milk and let it bake in the oven for a while until cooked
4. Divide into portions or brush the whole thing with butter and sprinkle with sugar

title: Kaše zemáková (Potato Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram potatoes
- 6 dekagrams butter (4 lots)
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- fried onion
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook 1 kilogram of potatoes, peel them, mash them and mix with 6 dekagrams of butter, 2 tablespoons of sour cream, an egg yolk, salt, fried onion and pepper
2. Put it with the dish in the oven, make criss-cross marks with a fork on the surface, and bake for a while

title: Kaše Zemáková s Fazolemi (Potato Mash with Beans)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled potatoes
- beans (half the volume of potatoes)
- soft water
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- fried onion

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, cut them into pieces, and boil them.
2. Cook the beans in soft water until tender.
3. Mash the beans in their cooking water.
4. Press through a sieve.
5. Mix with the potatoes.
6. Add beef broth, salt, and pepper.
7. Before serving, sprinkle with fried onion.
8. Peas may be used instead of beans.

title: Kaše Zemáková s Hovězí Polévkou (Potato Mash with Beef Broth)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled potatoes
- salted water
- beef broth
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, cut them into pieces, and boil them in salted water.
2. Grate and mash the potatoes.
3. Thin the mixture with beef broth.
4. Press through a sieve.
5. Season with butter, pepper, and salt.
6. Stir over heat for a short time.

title: Kaše zemáková s hovězí polévkou (Potato Porridge with Beef Broth)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled potatoes
- salt water
- beef broth
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut peeled potatoes, boil in salted water, grate and mash them, thin with beef broth, press through a sieve, butter them, pepper and salt
2. Stir them over the fire for a while longer

title: Kaše zemáková s mlékem (Potato Porridge with Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- milk
- 15 dekagrams butter (10 lots)
- salt
- 5-6 eggs
- cheese, grated
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut raw potatoes, scald them and immediately drain the water, in its place add milk so that the potatoes are covered, finish cooking in it, press through a sieve, let cool
2. Mix in 15 dekagrams of butter, salt, 5-6 eggs and place the mixture in a greased mold
3. Brush with snow, sprinkle with grated cheese and pieces of butter, and bake for half an hour in the oven

title: Kaše Zemáková se Slaninou (Potato Mash with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- butter
- flour
- bacon, cut into cubes
- onion, chopped
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and grate them.
2. Squeeze out the water and press through a sieve.
3. Stir with butter and a few spoonfuls of flour over heat.
4. Add the chopped bacon, onion, pepper, and salt.
5. Stir the mash over heat for half an hour.

title: Kaše zemáková se slaninou (Potato Porridge with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- bacon
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Peel raw potatoes, grate them, squeeze out the water, press through a sieve, butter them with a few tablespoons of flour over the fire

title: Kaštanová nádivka (Chestnut Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- about one kilogram peeled and cooked chestnuts
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Salt the chestnuts and stuff them into the stomach of the turkey
2. When the turkey is roasted, remove the chestnuts
3. Brown sugar in butter until golden, sprinkle with flour and add broth
4. Stew the chestnuts in this mixture until all liquid is absorbed
5. Use the chestnuts to garnish the turkey

title: Kaštanový kompot (Chestnut Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- sugar
- sweet orange juice

## Directions

1. Bake or stew the chestnuts until tender
2. Peel and mash into small cakes
3. Place on a baking dish
4. Pour over with boiled sugar
5. Warm again in a warm oven without boiling
6. Remove to a dish and pour over with boiled sugar to which sweet orange juice has been added

title: Kaštanový kompot se smetanou (Chestnut Compote with Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- baked chestnuts
- egg yolks
- sweet cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the baked chestnuts and cut into small cakes
2. Mix egg yolks with sweet cream and slowly pour into sugar cooked to the eighth degree
3. Allow to cook together with constant stirring
4. Strain onto beautifully arranged cooked chestnuts on a dish

title: Kdoulová huspenina (Medlar Preserve)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- medlars
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the medlars and rub off the hairs
2. Cut into thin pieces and cook slowly, but do not let them break apart
3. Drain the water, cover the vessel so the medlars cook in steam
4. When hot, press the juice from them
5. Add crushed sugar equal to the amount of juice
6. Cook together with constant stirring until completely thickened
7. Constantly skim off the foam and when thickened, pour into containers

title: Kdoulová marmelada (Quince Marmalade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Sugar - equal measure to the pulp

## Directions

1. Clean and rub quinces
2. Cook until soft
3. Press the pulp through a fine sieve
4. Add sugar in the same measure as the pulp
5. Cook sugar to the sixth degree
6. Add quince pulp to it
7. Boil several times
8. Carefully skim the foam
9. Store the preserve in containers

title: Kdoulová Marmeláda (Quince Marmalade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and rub the quinces and cook until soft.
2. Press the pulp through a fine sieve.
3. For the amount of pulp, use an equal amount of sugar, which is cooked to the sixth degree, then add the quince pulp to it.
4. Mix everything and cook several times, carefully removing the foam, and pour the mixture into containers.

title: Kdoulová Paštička (Quince Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and cook quinces
2. Peel and press through a sieve
3. Dry the quince mass
4. Take equal amounts of sugar as mass and cook to the sixth degree
5. Mix together and cook several more times
6. Spread onto slate tablets and when dry, cut into small, oblong or square pieces

title: Kdoulová svařenina (Quince Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe quinces, sliced and peeled
