- water
- sugar (three-quarters cup per cup of pulp)
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cook sliced and peeled ripe quinces in water until soft, then strain and press through a sieve.
2. Add three-quarters cup of sugar per cup of pulp and juice from one lemon.
3. Let everything cook down to a thick consistency, pour the jam into jars while hot, and seal only after it has cooled completely.

title: Kdoulová Šťáva (Quince Juice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar
- lakmus

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces, press out the juice while hot and cook with plenty of sugar until thickened, then pour into bottles while still hot.
2. If a little lakmus is added during the preservation of the juice, it will be colored nicely red.

title: Kečap (Tomato Catsup)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh ripe tomatoes
- onions
- salt
- brown sugar
- mustard powder
- black pepper
- cinnamon
- cloves
- red paprika
- nutmeg
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Place fresh ripe tomatoes in a large clean pot, crush with a potato masher, and for each gallon of this pulp add three finely chopped or grated onions, 4 tablespoons of salt, 5 brown sugar.
2. Mix everything and let it cook completely.
3. Press through a sieve, pressing until only seeds and skins remain in the sieve.
4. Place the pressed pulp back on the stove and add the following spices: 4 tablespoons of mustard powder, 8 tablespoons of black pepper, 2 tablespoons of cinnamon, 2 small teaspoons of cloves, 1 teaspoon of red paprika, 1 grated nutmeg, 3.5 cups of vinegar.
5. Simmer slowly but not vigorously until it is thicker than syrup, stirring so it does not burn.
6. Fill bottles while hot, seal and wax them.

title: Kečap na jiný způsob (Tomato Catsup - Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bushel tomatoes
- onions
- green peppers
- vinegar
- sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- mixed spices
- nutmeg
- cloves

## Directions

1. Take half a bushel of tomatoes, scald and peel them, place in a glazed pot and let them cook well.
2. Then strain through a flour sieve.
3. Return to the pot, add 6 medium-sized onions and 3 green peppers and let them simmer down to a thick consistency.
4. Remove the seeds from the peppers before adding them to cook.
5. Then strain again through a sieve.
6. Add 2 cups of vinegar, half a cup of sugar, 2 teaspoons of salt, 1 teaspoon of cinnamon, 1 teaspoon of mixed spices, 1 teaspoon of grated nutmeg and half a teaspoon of cloves.
7. Let simmer for another 10 minutes.
8. Pour into bottles, cork and seal with wax.

title: Klobásy (Sausages)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw pork meat
- Wine
- Cream
- Cloves
- Salt
- Breadcrumbs soaked in milk
- Sheep intestines
- Flour
- Lard

## Directions

1. Remove all bones from raw pork meat; if you have a meat grinder, grind it; otherwise chop very finely
2. Mix with a little wine, cream, cloves, salt, and finely chopped breadcrumbs soaked in milk
3. Mix everything together and stuff into thin sheep intestines
4. Twist each piece in a cross, pierce with a skewer, dust lightly with flour, and fry in lard

title: Klobásy (Wine Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from the neck
- white bread soaked in sour cream
- ground white pepper
- clove
- lemon zest, finely chopped
- salt
- wine
- sheep intestines, cleaned

## Directions

1. Take a piece of short pork meat from the neck and chop it finely
2. For every three parts of meat, add one part of white bread soaked in sour cream
3. Season with ground white pepper, clove, and finely chopped lemon zest
4. Salt to taste
5. Add a little wine
6. Mix and work the mixture thoroughly for at least ten minutes
7. Fill the cleaned sheep intestines with the mixture
8. Alternatively, take two parts pork and one part veal, chop, and moisten with sour cream, then season and proceed as above
9. Shape the finished sausages into a coil
10. Pierce crosswise with two thin skewers to hold them together nicely
11. Store in a cool place until needed
12. Serve roasted with braised or fried cabbage, or only with mustard
13. Good dumplings or mashed potatoes with fried onions also go very well with them

title: Klobásy (Wine Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from the neck
- white bread soaked in sour cream
- white pepper
- allspice
- lemon zest, finely chopped
- salt
- wine
- sheep intestines

## Directions

1. Take a piece of pork meat from the neck and chop finely
2. For every three parts of meat, add one part white bread soaked in sour cream
3. Season with ground white pepper, allspice, and finely chopped lemon zest; salt properly
4. Add a little wine and mix thoroughly and knead for at least ten minutes
5. Fill into properly cleaned sheep intestines
6. Alternatively: take two parts pork and one part veal, chop, moisten with sour cream, season, and proceed as above
7. Roll the finished sausages into a coil and pierce across with two thin skewers to hold them together neatly

title: Kluzký ve smetané (Farina Quennelles in Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, 6
- Sugar, 4 tablespoons
- White breadcrumbs, grated, sufficient amount
- Salt
- Butter, melted and clarified
- Sour cream, 2 koflíky
- Sugar, 2 tablespoons
- Lemon rind, grated from 1 lemon
- Egg yolks, 5

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks with 4 tablespoons sugar until foamy
2. Add enough grated white breadcrumbs so it becomes thick like ordinary quennelles
3. Add salt and mix well
4. Shape small pieces with a coffee spoon and fry in hot clarified butter until golden
5. Boil 2 koflíky good sour cream
6. Add 2 tablespoons sugar and grated lemon rind from 1 lemon
7. Mix well and add the cooked quennelles to the cream

title: Kluzký ve Smetaně (Quenelles in Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- white bread, grated
- salt
- 2 cups good cream
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- grated zest of one lemon
- five egg yolks
- a little cold cream
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Beat together six egg yolks with 4 tablespoons sugar until foamy
2. Add enough grated white bread so that the mixture is as thick as ordinary quenelles, salt it, and stir well
3. Cut out small pieces with a coffee spoon and fry them golden in hot clarified butter
4. Boil 2 cups of good cream, add 2 tablespoons sugar and grated lemon zest
5. Beat five egg yolks with a little cold cream and pour into the boiling cream, stirring well
6. Arrange the quenelles in a deep porcelain dish, pour the prepared cream over them
7. Place on a baking sheet that you have sprinkled well with salt and put into a hot oven
8. When it is very hot, remove from the oven (do not let it bake) and bring to the table

title: Kminová polévka (Caraway Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters water
- A handful of caraway
- A piece of butter or lard
- Mushrooms
- Fried bread

## Directions

1. Salt 2 liters of water, add a handful of caraway and cook
2. Strain, add a piece of butter or lard and mushrooms
3. Cook again and pour over fried bread

title: Kmínka (Caraway Spirit)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter rye spirits or diluted good alcohol
- 70 grams caraway seeds
- 250 grams crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Add 70 grams of caraway seeds and 250 grams of crushed sugar to 1 liter of spirits
2. Let it sit on warm stove for fourteen days, stirring frequently
3. Strain the liqueur into bottles and seal well

title: Kmínka - Another Method (Caraway Spirit - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 liters good spirits (kořalka)
- 180 grams crushed caraway seeds
- 12 grams star anise

## Directions

1. Mix 3 liters of good spirits with 180 grams of crushed caraway seeds and 12 grams of star anise
2. Pour into a bottle and tie with a bladder
3. Place the bottle in a pot filled with water
4. Heat the water until it boils, then remove and let cool completely
5. Sweeten the liqueur with sugar, strain, and pour into clean bottles
6. Seal bottles firmly

title: Kmínová Likérka (Caraway Liqueur)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 grams alcohol
- 30 drops fennel oil
- 1 drop cinnamon oil
- 4 liters good spirits (kořalka)
- 750 grams sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the alcohol with the fennel oil and cinnamon oil
2. Mix with 4 liters of good spirits
3. Sweeten with 750 grams of sugar
4. Strain and pour into bottles
5. Seal bottles well to prevent evaporation

title: Kmínová omáčka (Caraway Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- brown coulis or brown roux
- strong meat broth
- caraway, cleaned and crushed
- salt

## Directions

1. Use brown coulis or brown roux and boil it with strong meat broth
2. Add the cleaned and crushed caraway
3. Season with salt
4. Boil several times
5. Serve with beef or mutton

title: Kmínová polévka (Caraway Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard rye bread
- Water
- Caraway
- Salt
- Butter
- Several egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut hard rye bread into cubes
2. Cook in water with caraway until it falls apart
3. Salt, strain, add butter and cook again
4. Beat in several egg yolks

title: Kmínová polévka (Caraway Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- About 3 tablespoons flour
- Butter
- 2-3 liters boiling water
- Various vegetables (kořínky)
- 1 tablespoon caraway
- Salt
- Bread or fried bread

## Directions

1. Fry about 3 tablespoons of flour in butter
2. Cook the roux in boiling water (2-3 liters) with various vegetables
3. Add 1 tablespoon of caraway, salt everything and pour into a bowl where bread or fried bread cut into pieces is located
4. Weak broth can be used instead of water for cooking and preparing the soup

title: Knedlíky Americké (American Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs well
2. Add 1 cup sweet milk and 1 tablespoon salt to it
3. Mix 1 teaspoon baking powder into 3 cups flour and add to the milk
4. Work this well on a plate
5. Shape five dumplings, roll them on a rolling pin to round them, and put them in water to boil

title: Knedlíky Linecké (Lined Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram sifted flour
- 2 to 3 whole eggs
- a piece of butter
- milk
- 2 bread rolls cut into cubes and fried
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 1 kilogram of sifted flour on a platter and salt it.
2. Mix 2 to 3 whole eggs in a separate bowl, add a piece of butter and as much milk as the flour will take.
3. Mix everything with the flour and knead well.
4. Mix in 2 bread rolls cut into cubes and fried, and beat the dough well.
5. There must be boiling water in the pot; dip the skimmer always in hot water and cut a piece of dough with it, from which a dumpling is formed.
6. Cut cooked dumplings into quarters, grease with hot butter and sprinkle with grated bread roll.
7. Serve with tomato sauce, horseradish with cream, or cabbage.

title: Knedlíky Linecké - Jiný Způsob (Lined Dumplings - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 kilogram flour
- 1.5 bread rolls cut into cubes and fried
- salt

## Directions

1. Cream 60 grams of butter.
2. Add 3 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs, salt.
3. Gradually add flour to the butter and eggs and beat the dough so it does not stick to the hand.
4. When the dough is well beaten, add the bread rolls.
5. Wrap everything in a cloth and place in boiling water.
6. To test if the dumpling is sufficiently cooked, pierce it with a stick; if the dough does not stick to it, it is done.
7. When sufficiently cooked, cut with thread into pieces, sprinkle with bread roll and grease.

title: Knedlíky Obyčejné (Plain Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram flour (type 00)
- salt
- 2 bread rolls
- 1 egg
- spring water

## Directions

1. Place 1 kilogram of flour on a baking sheet and salt it
2. Cut 2 bread rolls into cubes and fry them
3. Mix 1 egg with as much spring water as is needed for the flour
4. Mix the egg and water with the flour to form a stiff dough that does not stick to the hand
5. Add the fried bread cubes
6. Form dumplings
7. Boil in salted water

title: Knedlíky Opečené (Baked Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover plain dumplings
- butter
- 3 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut leftover plain dumplings into slices
2. Place a piece of butter underneath
3. On a baking sheet, stir constantly and bake well
4. When baked, beat about 3 eggs
5. Stir for a while with the eggs
6. Can be served as a standalone dish or added to beef

title: Knedlíky s broskvemi (Peach Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- těsto na knedlíky
- broskve
- smetana

## Directions

1. Udělej těsto jako na obyčejné švestkové knedlíky.
2. Rozkrájej je na větší kousky.
3. Do každého kousku zábal broskvi (pecky můžeš vyloupat; jsou-li broskve velké, můžeš dát pouze půlku).
4. Vař knedlíky.
5. Polej smetanou - chutná dobře i za studena.

title: Knedlíky s Uzeninou (Dumplings with Smoked Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked smoked meat, minced fine
- 3 slices bread cut into cubes and fried in butter
- 3 slices bread soaked in milk
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 cups good milk
- salt
- flour (as much as needed)

## Directions

1. Chop fine 1 pound of cooked smoked meat
2. Add to it 3 slices of bread cut into cubes and fried in butter and 3 slices of bread soaked in milk
3. Beat 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks into it
4. Add 2 cups of good milk, salt and mix well
5. Then add as much flour as needed to make thick dough, which you knead with a spoon
6. Make dumplings about the size of an apple and put them in boiling water; then stir them loosely at the bottom of the pot so they rise to the surface and let boil for half an hour
7. Take them out onto a plate, tear them in two with two forks, sprinkle with grated bread roll or bread crust and pour fresh melted butter over them

title: Knedlíky s Uzeným Masem (Dumplings with Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of fresh butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 3.5 deciliters sweet cream
- flour
- 2 bread rolls cut into cubes
- smoked meat
- salt

## Directions

1. Cream a piece of fresh butter finely.
2. Add 3 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs, salt.
3. Add 3.5 deciliters of sweet cream and enough flour so the beaten dough does not stick to the hand.
4. Mix in 2 bread rolls cut into cubes.
5. Form dumplings and place a piece of chopped smoked meat in the center of each.
6. Grease and sprinkle with grated bread roll.

title: Knedlíky s uzeným masem a slaninou (Dumplings with Smoked Meat and Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams (4 lots) of butter
- Salt
- 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk
- Flour (1 kilogram - 2 pounds)
- Milk
- 2 rolls
- One quarter kilogram (one quarter pound) of smoked meat
- 60 grams (4 lots) of bacon
- Water

## Directions

1. Cream 60 grams of butter with a mixer; salt it, add 2 whole eggs and an egg yolk, and beat again.
2. Mix with flour (1 kilogram), pour in milk and work the dough so it is as stiff as for buns.
3. Cut 2 rolls into cubes and fry in butter.
4. Similarly cut one quarter kilogram of smoked meat and 60 grams of bacon into cubes; mix everything with the dough and salt.
5. The water in which the dumplings are to be cooked must already be boiling and salted.
6. Wet a large spoon in this water, take dough with it and cook the dumplings for a quarter hour.
7. When cooked, halve them, grease and sprinkle with grated roll.
8. Usually cabbage is added to them.

title: Knedlíky se Slaninou (Dumplings with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth kilogram bacon
- 2 bread rolls cut into cubes
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into cubes and fry, or leave it raw.
2. Cut 2 bread rolls the same way and add to the bacon.
3. Mix 2 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs and milk as needed in a bowl, salt and mix everything well.
4. Drop dumplings into boiling salted water with a skimmer.
5. Serve these dumplings with cabbage and smoked meat.

title: Knedlíky se suchými švestkami (Dumplings with Dried Prunes)
body:
## Ingredients

- těsto na knedlíky
- velké kalifornské švestky (nejlépe Santa Clara)
- trochu cukru
- kousek citrónové kůry
- osmaženou žemličkou
- rozpálené čerstvé máslo

## Directions

1. Udělej těsto na knedlíky podle předešlého předpisu.
2. Uvař do měkka velké kalifornské švestky s trochem cukru a kouskem citrónové kůry.
3. Když jsou švestky vařené, vyndej z nich pecky a dávej po dvou (jsou-li menší, po třech) do každého knedlíku.
4. Vař knedlíky asi 15 minut.
5. Vyndej knedlíky, posyp osmaženou žemličkou a polej rozpáleným čerstvým máslem.
6. Alternativně: místo celých švestek můžeš naplnit knedlíky švestkovými povidly připravenými jako do koláčů.

title: Knedlíky se Škvarky (Dumplings with Cracklings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 2 bread rolls
- 1.5 deciliters cracklings
- 3 eggs
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and put it on a baking sheet.
2. Cut 2 bread rolls and fry them.
3. Add 1.5 deciliters of cracklings with the bread roll to the flour with 3 eggs.
4. Beat the dough.
5. Drop dumplings into a pot with a spoon.
6. Grease the cooked dumplings and serve with smoked meat on a platter.

title: Knedlíky z vařených brambor (Potato Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- dva krajíčky bílého chleba
- mléko
- pažitka
- tři vejce
- cibulka
- máslo
- libra vařených brambor

## Directions

1. Rozmočte dva krajíčky bílého chleba v mléce, trochu jej osolte.
2. Přidejte jemně rosekanou pažitku, tři vejce a jemně rosekanou a na másle osmaženu cibulku.
3. Přidejte libru vařených brambor.
4. Vše se dobře smíchá a propracuje.
5. Nadělej knedlíky a vař je ve slané vodě.

title: Knedlíky ze syrových brambor (Raw Potato Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- šest oškrabaných a opraných syrových brambor
- mouka
- sůl
- jedno nebo dvě celá vejce (volitelně)

## Directions

1. Ustrouhej na misku šest oškrabaných a opraných syrových brambor.
2. Vymačkej trochu vodu z nich skrze řídké pláténko, ne do sucha, ale přece z většiny.
3. Přidej k bramborům tolik mouky, aby povstalo hezky tuhé těsto, a osol.
4. Dobře propracuj a utvoř knedlíky.
5. Uvař je v prudce vařící vodě.
6. Chceš-li míti kypřejší, přidej jedno nebo dvě celá vejce.
7. Volitelně: knedlíky můžeš také vyrobit z polovice syrových a polovice vařených brambor.

title: Knedlíky ze syrových zemáků (Dumplings from Raw Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- cold water
- hot rice porridge or semolina porridge or barley porridge
- milk
- eggs
- 2-3 fried bread rolls cut into cubes

## Directions

1. Grate potatoes
2. Drain water several times
3. Let stand in cold water overnight
4. Squeeze out water
5. Mix with hot rice, semolina, or barley porridge made in milk
6. Add beaten eggs and fried bread roll cubes
7. Make dumplings
8. Cook in salted water until they float to surface
9. Serve with meat

title: Knedlíky Zemákové (Potato Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram cooked cold potatoes
- semolina or flour (as needed)
- salt
- 3 eggs
- 2 bread rolls
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Grate 1 kilogram of cooked cold potatoes
2. Mix with semolina or flour as needed
3. Season with salt
4. Beat 3 eggs and mix in
5. Knead until the dough does not stick to the hand
6. Cut 2 bread rolls into cubes and fry in fresh butter
7. Add to the dough
8. Knead thoroughly
9. Form dumplings the size of a medium apple
10. Boil in salted water
11. When cooked, cut in half, sprinkle with fried bread crumbs, and drizzle with melted butter
12. Serve hot

title: Knedlíky Zemákové s Houskou (Potato Dumplings with Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cold potatoes
- flour or semolina
- several eggs
- salt
- 2 or 3 bread rolls
- butter
- grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Grate the cold cooked potatoes
2. Mix with flour or semolina as desired
3. Add several eggs
4. Mix everything and knead into a stiff dough
5. Cut 2 or 3 bread rolls into cubes and fry in butter
6. Mix into the dough
7. Form dumplings of medium size
8. Boil in salted water
9. Cut in half, grease, and coat with grated bread crumbs
10. Must be eaten hot

title: Knedlíky Špekové (Dumplings with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 slices white bread
- ½ pound smoked bacon
- 2 cups milk
- 3 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- flour (as much as needed)
- grated bread roll or bread crust
- fresh butter
- 1 pound smoked meat
- braised cabbage

## Directions

1. Cut 4 slices of white bread and ½ pound smoked bacon into small cubes
2. Put on a saucepan and let fry until golden
3. Then put it in a bowl, pour 2 cups of milk over it
4. Beat 3 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks into it, salt and mix
5. Now add as much flour as needed to make thick dough, which you knead with a spoon
6. Make dumplings about the size of an apple and boil either in salted boiling water or in broth in which you have boiled 1 pound of smoked meat
7. Take the whole dumplings out onto a plate, cut them in half, sprinkle with grated bread roll or bread crust, pour melted fresh butter over them and serve with the smoked meat cut into neat pieces and braised cabbage

title: Knedlíky Švestkové (Plum Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram sifted flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 15 grams yeast diluted with milk, sugar and flour
- milk
- plums
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Heat a piece of butter.
2. Put the sifted flour on a baking sheet and salt it.
3. Add the heated butter, 2 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs.
4. Mix in 15 grams of yeast diluted with milk, sugar and flour.
5. Add milk as needed so the dough does not stick to the hand.
6. Work the dough thoroughly.
7. Cut the dough into pieces and wrap a plum in each piece.
8. Roll into balls properly.
9. After a quarter hour of rising, boil in salted water.
10. Grease with butter and sprinkle with either cheese or gingerbread.

title: Knedlíky Švestkové (Plum Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 1 kilogram flour
- salt
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 15 grams yeast (diluted with milk, sugar, and flour)
- milk
- prunes

## Directions

1. Heat a piece of butter
2. Place 1 kilogram flour on a baking sheet and salt it
3. Add heated butter, 2 egg yolks, and 2 whole eggs
4. Mix in 15 grams of yeast diluted with milk, sugar, and flour
5. Add as much milk as the dough can take
6. Knead thoroughly so it does not stick to the hand
7. Cut into pieces
8. Wrap a prune in each piece
9. Roll up well

title: Knedlíky Švestkové s Houskou (Plum Dumplings with Bread Roll)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram flour
- 3 grated bread rolls
- 3 tablespoons heated butter
- 15 grams pounded sugar
- milk or cream
- plums
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix half kilogram flour with 3 grated bread rolls.
2. Pour in 3 tablespoons of heated butter.
3. Add 15 grams of pounded sugar and mix everything.
4. Salt and make a stiff dough with milk or cream.
5. Make dumplings as ordinary plum dumplings, or roll the dough into 2 layers.
6. Remove pits from plums and place them by halves on one layer.
7. Cover with the second layer.
8. Grease a baking sheet, carefully place the entire dough on it and bake.

title: Knedlíčky na Zelený Hrášek (Dumplings with Green Peas)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish meat leftovers
- 2 bread rolls
- Milk
- 4 eggs
- Flour
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Finely chop the fish meat leftovers.
2. Soak 2 bread rolls in milk.
3. On a baking sheet, beat 4 eggs for a long time until a thick paste forms.
4. When cooled, crush the meat with the bread rolls, flour and salt in a mortar.
5. Press through a sieve.
6. Fry the dumplings or small flat cakes in butter.

title: Knedlíčky na zelený hrášek (Fish Dumplings with Green Peas)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish meat scraps
- 2 bread rolls
- milk
- 4 eggs
- flower
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop the fish meat scraps finely.
2. Soak 2 bread rolls in milk.
3. Mix on a baking sheet with 4 eggs until a thick paste forms.
4. When cooled, grind the meat with the bread rolls, flower and salt in a mortar.
5. Press through a sieve and fry the dumplings or small patties in butter.

title: Knedlíčky z brambor (Small Potato Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- tři lžíce sádla
- půl koflíčku pšeničné krupičky
- dvě lžíce cukru
- talíř vařených brambor
- jedno vejce
- sůl
- čtvrt koflíku mléka
- půl lžíce másla
- mouka

## Directions

1. Postavte hrnec s vodou na kamna.
2. Vezměte tři lžíce sádla, dejte na rendlík a přidejte půl koflíčku pšeničné krupičky a dvě lžíce cukru.
3. Míchejte, dokud není krupička do zlatova usmažená, ale ne spálená.
4. Mezi tím vezměte talíř vařených brambor, rozstrouhejte je, přidejte jedno vejce, sůl, čtvrt koflíku mléka a půl lžíce másla.
5. Přidejte mouky tolik, aby to bylo hezky tuhé, ale ne tvrdé.
6. Z těsta nakrájejte kousky a v rukou je upravte na šištičky asi jako půl prstu velké.
7. Dejte je do vařící vody.
8. Když vystoupí nahoru (musí se tím zamíchati), vyndejte je a dejte na cedník.
9. Když z nich voda okape, vysypte je do rendlíku a osmaž je.
10. Podávejte na stůl.

title: Knedlíčky z ryb (Fish Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or carp
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) fresh butter
- flower
- salt
- 2 whole eggs
- bread roll soaked in milk
- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) water
- lemon juice
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) butter
- parsley root
- whole celery
- light roux

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, remove skin and all bones, and chop the meat finely.
2. Mix 3 dekagrams (2 lots) of fresh butter with a bit of flower and salt.
3. Add 2 whole eggs and a bread roll soaked in milk.
4. Mix in the chopped meat and form small dumplings with a spoon.
5. The dumplings can be served in a meat broth or as a standalone dish.
6. For the standalone dish: mix 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) of water, lemon juice from one lemon, 3 dekagrams (2 lots) of butter, a bit of crushed flower, a piece of parsley root and whole celery.
7. Salt it and boil.
8. Add light roux, strain, and when boiling, add the previously prepared dumplings.
9. To this sauce, cooked fish prepared in white water can be added.

title: Koblihy (Doughnuts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) sugar
- lemon zest
- 2 tablespoons arrak or good rum
- 4 tablespoons diluted yeast
- flour
- warm sour cream
- butter
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 6 dekagrams sugar
2. Add chopped lemon zest, 2 tablespoons arrak or good rum, 4 tablespoons diluted yeast
3. Add enough flour (saving some for sprinkling) and warm sour cream to make a dough that does not stick to the hand when worked
4. Let rest; when risen, roll out, cut into circles for doughnuts, spread with jam, cover with another circle, trim again with a cutter and let rise a little
5. Put only as much butter on the baking sheet as absolutely necessary to give them a nice edge; as the butter is used up, add more by the piece
6. Cover the baking sheet with a lid, fry the doughnuts on both sides until golden and sprinkle with sugar while hot

title: Koblihy (Doughnuts) - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram grated, boiled potatoes
- 75 grams (5 lots) beaten butter
- 45 grams sugar
- flower
- salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 eggs
- flour
- jam
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix grated boiled potatoes with 75 grams beaten butter to which 45 grams sugar, a little flower and salt have been added
2. Mix in 3 egg yolks and 3 eggs
3. Add enough flour to make a dough
4. Roll out and cut circles with a form as for ordinary doughnuts
5. Spread jam on the bottom part, press on the second half, trim with a form and fry in butter
6. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Koblihy plnivané (Filled Fried Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces butter
- two whole eggs
- half ounce pressed yeast
- three tablespoons sifted sugar
- small piece lemon zest
- salt
- one pound warmed and sifted flour
- 1 tablespoon rum or wine
- 1 small cup milk or sour cream
- strawberry, raspberry or currant preserves, or poppy seed, plum or apricot filling
- flour for dusting

## Directions

1. Rub three ounces butter on a bowl until frothed
2. Mix into it two whole eggs dissolved with half ounce pressed yeast
3. Add three tablespoons sifted sugar, a small piece lemon zest, a little salt, one pound warmed and sifted flour, 1 tablespoon rum or wine (which is added so that they do not absorb too much fat), 1 small cup milk or sour cream
4. Beat the dough for a quarter hour constantly with a whisk in one direction
5. Dust it with flour and let it rise in warmth
6. Then take the dough out onto a floured board, roll it to little finger thickness
7. Cut out round circles from half with a cutter or glass, and mark circles on the other half but do not cut them out
8. Into the middle of each marked circle put a heap of strawberry, raspberry or currant preserves, or poppy seed, plum or apricot filling
9. Cover them with the cut out pieces, then cut out slightly smaller round cakes with a glass or cutter
10. Lay them on a warmed board covered with a floured cloth and let them rise
11. Then put them in rendered fat

title: Koblihy po americká (Doughnuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- two eggs
- 1 small cup sour milk with half teaspoon baking soda dissolved in water, or 1 small cup sweet milk with 1 teaspoon baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Put into a deep bowl 1 small cup sugar, 1 tablespoon butter and two eggs and rub well until frothed
2. Add 1 small cup sour milk into which you have poured half teaspoon baking soda dissolved in water, or add 1 small cup sweet milk and add 1 teaspoon baking powder to the flour
3. Add enough flour to make the dough about as stiff as for biscuits
4. Roll out to half a finger thickness and cut out rings, either with a special cutter which cuts out both the doughnut and the hole in the middle, or cut out the doughnut with a glass and cut out the middle with a large thimble
5. Put into prepared hot lard and fry on both sides

title: Koblihy s jahodami (Doughnuts with Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dekagrams (10 lots) flour
- salt
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) sugar
- 3 dekagrams fresh butter
- 2 egg yolks
- quarter liter warm milk
- egg
- sugared strawberries
- butter
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Prepare dough from 15 dekagrams flour, salt, 3 dekagrams sugar, 3 dekagrams fresh butter, 2 egg yolks and quarter liter warm milk
2. Roll out, brush with beaten egg, cut into wide strips
3. Lay heaps of sugared strawberries on the strips, cut out doughnuts and fry in butter
4. Sprinkle with sugar; can be eaten hot or cold

title: Kocmatice (Elderflower Fritters)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- elderflower with stem
- eggs
- salt or sugar
- bread crust and flour (mixed)
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the elderflower with the stem, clean it well so no bugs remain in the flower
2. Place salt or sugar into beaten egg, dip the flower in it, roll in mixed bread crust and flour and place in hot butter
3. Fry until golden pink
4. Arrange on a dish with stems pointing up, sprinkle with sugar and serve warm

title: Kocmatice (Fried Elderflower Blossoms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Elderflower blossoms with stems
- Egg
- Salt or sugar
- Bread crumbs mixed with flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the elderflower blossoms with stems and clean them thoroughly to remove any insects.
2. Beat an egg with either salt or sugar.
3. Dip the flower in the beaten egg, then roll in a mixture of bread crumbs and flour.
4. Fry in hot butter until pale pink in color.
5. Arrange on a platter with stems pointing upward, sprinkle with sugar, and serve hot.

title: Kokosové cukrovinky (Coconut Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet cream
- 1 teaspoon butter
- grated coconut
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook 2 cups white sugar with 1/2 cup sweet cream and 1 teaspoon butter
2. Cook for 15 minutes
3. Add grated coconut as desired
4. Mix in 1 teaspoon vanilla
5. Pour onto a plate
6. When cooled, cut into squares

title: Kokosové cukrovinky (Coconut Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- two small cups white sugar
- half a small cup sweet cream
- one teaspoon butter
- shredded coconut to taste
- one teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Bring two small cups of white sugar to a boil with half a small cup of sweet cream and one teaspoon of butter.
2. Let boil for 15 minutes, then stir in shredded coconut to taste.
3. Mix in one teaspoon of vanilla.
4. Pour onto a plate and when cooled, cut into squares.

title: Kokosové kopečky (Coconut Kisses)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound dried coconut
- one pound white sugar
- 3 egg whites (unbeaten)
- 10 tablespoons water
- flour
- egg white

## Directions

1. Mix dried coconut, white sugar, 3 unbeaten egg whites, and 10 tablespoons water thoroughly together
2. Cook in a hot pan in a pot with constant stirring for 15 minutes
3. Must stir constantly so it does not burn
4. Prepare a baking sheet, lightly brush with fat and sprinkle evenly with flour
5. Beat one egg white into stiff meringue
6. Quickly mix the cooked coconut with the meringue
7. Using two small spoons, form small heaps one next to the other, making two mounds from one full spoon
8. When the sheet is full, place in a not very hot oven
9. When they begin to turn yellow, open the oven and let them dry in the open oven for about 15 minutes
10. When removed from the oven, do not take from the sheet immediately, but wait until they cool, then they come away easily

title: Kokosový dort (Coconut Loaf Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 0.25 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup coconut

## Directions

1. Beat the 2 eggs well (reserve 1 egg white for frosting).
2. Add 1 cup sugar and 0.25 cup butter and cream together thoroughly.
3. Add 1 cup sweet milk, 2 cups flour, and 2 teaspoons baking powder and mix well until smooth.
4. Add 1 cup coconut and mix well.
5. Pour into a large mold and bake.
6. Beat the reserved egg white and spread on top, then sprinkle with coconut.

title: Kolník s vepřovým masem (Kohlrabi with Pork)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- pork meat
- vegetables
- pork lard, diced
- sugar
- pepper
- rose hip puree

## Directions

1. Clean the kohlrabi, slice it into discs or dice it, pour boiling water over it and scald it.
2. Cook the pork meat separately with vegetables.
3. Strain the broth over the kohlrabi, to which diced raw pork lard and sugar may be added, or these may be omitted and pepper added instead.
4. Cook everything until the rose hip puree is strained through a sieve; add the pork meat and serve hot.

title: Kolník s vepřovým masem (Kohlrabi with Pork)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat
- Kohlrabi
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sugar
- Flour
- Water
- Pork broth

## Directions

1. Rinse the pork meat and cook like beef with all vegetables, salt it and cook until done.
2. Meanwhile, peel the kohlrabi cleanly, cut into cubes or thin slices, cover with water and cook.
3. As soon as it begins to boil, drain the water to remove bitterness.
4. Pour pork broth over it, salt, and if it should be sweet, add sugar; otherwise pepper.
5. When softened, mix with pale flour, cook a bit, add the pork meat cut into neat pieces, and cook for a while.
6. If it should be dark, caramelize sugar.
7. In the same way, vodnice and brukve (kohlrabi) are prepared with pork.
8. If not all the broth is used for kohlrabi, it can be eaten as usual with a fried roll cut into noodles.

title: Koláč (Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- sugar (equal weight to eggs)
- flour (equal weight to eggs)
- butter
- lemon peel
- almonds
- raisins

## Directions

1. Weigh 6 eggs and measure equal amounts of sugar and flour.
2. Cream butter with egg yolks until foamy, then add the weighed sugar and beat briefly.
3. Fold in the flour mixed with lemon peel.
4. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites.
5. Spread white paper on a pan, spread the batter on it, and sprinkle with almonds and raisins.

title: Koláč librový (Pound Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram (1 pound) butter
- 12 yolks
- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- 1/2 kilogram flour
- 24 decagrams (16 lots) raisins
- snow from 12 whites

## Directions

1. Rub butter with yolks, add sugar and flour
2. Add raisins, mix well
3. Work dough, fold in snow from whites
4. Bake in greased form

title: Koláč na celý plech (Whole Sheet Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams (16 lots) butter
- 4 eggs
- 3 decagrams yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter warm milk
- 75 grams (5 lots) sugar
- Flour (as much as needed to make airy dough)
- Sugar, cinnamon and chopped almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Beat 24 decagrams butter until foamy with 4 eggs, 3 decagrams yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter warm milk, 75 grams sugar and enough flour to make airy dough
2. Spread the dough to the thickness of a finger across the entire baking sheet
3. Let it rise for a while in a warm place
4. Brush with egg, sprinkle with sugar, cinnamon and chopped almonds and bake slowly

title: Koláč s drobením (Cake with Crumb Topping)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams (8 lots) butter
- 6 egg yolks
- Salt
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) ground sugar
- 4 tablespoons diluted yeast
- Flour (as much as needed so dough is not too thick)
- 1/3 kilogram (1/4 pound) butter for crumb topping
- Flour for crumb topping
- Egg for brushing
- Sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Beat butter until foamy, add 6 egg yolks while beating, add a pinch of salt, 6 decagrams ground sugar, 4 tablespoons diluted yeast and enough flour so the dough is not too thick
2. Let it rise for a while, then spread out in a flat cake
3. Mix 1/3 kilogram butter with flour to make a crumb topping like breadcrumbs
4. Brush the cake around with beaten egg, sprinkle with crumb topping, bake, cut into pieces and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Koláč s jahodami (Cake with Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough (as for ordinary cakes)
- Strawberries
- Sugar
- Egg whites (alternative)

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as for ordinary cakes, roll out the dough, place on a baking sheet, make an edge and bake until pale
2. Cover the surface with strawberries that have either been poured with cooked sugar and stood for some time, or mixed with egg white foam beaten with sugar
3. Spread the strawberries across the cake and sprinkle with sugar, then bake the cake completely
4. Alternatively, instead of egg white, sour cream can be beaten and strained through a sieve onto the strawberries
5. Small cakes can be made the same way as large cakes

title: Koláč z lískových ořechů (Hazelnut Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams blanched hazelnuts
- 1 tablespoon sour cream
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) sugar
- zest from half lemon
- two yolks
- snow from 4 whites
- butter dough
- egg white for brushing

## Directions

1. Grind hazelnuts with sour cream, mix with sugar, lemon zest, yolks, and snow from whites
2. Spread mixture on butter cake dough
3. Make lattice from dough over it
4. Brush with white, sprinkle with sugar
5. Bake in oven

title: Koláče (Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) butter
- 4-5 egg yolks
- lemon or orange peel
- salt
- sugar (equal weight to yolks)
- 4 tablespoons yeast
- flour
- milk
- plum jam
- cheese curds
- poppy seed
- raisins
- almonds
- roasted semolina
- gingerbread

## Directions

1. Cream 6 dekagrams of butter with 4-5 egg yolks, lemon or orange peel, salt, sugar equal to the weight of yolks, 4 tablespoons yeast, flour and milk to form a dough that doesn't stick to the hands.
2. Allow to ferment.
3. Stretch the dough to the thickness of a pinky finger, spread with plum jam, cheese curds or poppy seed, and sprinkle with raisins, almonds, roasted semolina and sugar or gingerbread.
4. Make a rim around the dough, brush with egg, and bake.
5. When cooled, cut into squares or triangles.

title: Koláče bílkové (Egg White Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deciliters (8 tablespoons) flour
- 1 kilogram (2 pounds) fresh butter
- 6 tablespoons yeast (leavening)
- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) thick sour cream
- 3 tablespoons ground sugar
- 12 egg whites beaten stiff
- Salt
- Jam or preserves for filling
- Chopped almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Crumble 1 kilogram fresh butter into 28 deciliters flour, mix in 6 tablespoons yeast, 7 deciliters thick sour cream, 3 tablespoons ground sugar and stiff egg white foam from 12 egg whites
2. Work the dough so that it stretches
3. When risen for a while, salt it, make small hats, fill with jam, brush with egg white foam and sprinkle with chopped almonds

title: Koláče důlkové (Dimple Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram and 12 decagrams (1 pound and 6 lots) flour
- 15 decagrams (10 lots) butter
- 14 decagrams (about 9 lots) sugar
- 6 hard grated yolks
- raw eggs as needed for dough consistency
- raisin for filling
- chopped almonds for topping
- egg for brushing
- sugar and vanilla for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Sift flour on board, add butter, sugar, grated hard yolks, and raw eggs until dough does not stick to hands
2. Take a piece, form into ball, place on sheet
3. Make small indentation and place raisin in it
4. Brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with chopped almonds
5. Bake slowly in oven and sprinkle with sugar and vanilla

title: Koláče se slaninou (Tarts with Lard)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- egg
- salt
- caraway seeds
- lard cut into cubes

## Directions

1. Spread butter dough about half centimeter thick
2. Brush with egg
3. Sprinkle with salt and caraway seeds
4. Border with lard cut into cubes
5. Bake until golden

title: Koláče ze zemákové mouky (Cornstarch Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) sugar
- 1/2 tablespoon cornstarch flour
- zest from half lemon rubbed with sugar
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- salt
- sour cream
- butter pieces
- raisins

## Directions

1. Beat eggs, mix with sugar, cornstarch flour, lemon zest, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt in sour cream
2. Pour onto cake with bent edge
3. Sprinkle with butter pieces and raisins
4. Bake cake

title: Koláčky (Fruit Tarts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 4-6 egg yolks
- 1 piece fresh pressed yeast
- 4 egg whites
- 1 quart sweet sour cream
- sugar
- lemon zest
- salt
- 1 pound fine sifted flour
- filling (jam, cheese curd, or other filling)

## Directions

1. Cream butter
2. Add egg yolks one by one
3. Add broken piece of pressed yeast
4. Beat egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
5. Add sour cream
6. Add sugar
7. Add lemon zest and salt
8. Add flour and mix until dough leaves the spoon
9. Let dough rise
10. Scoop dough with a spoon and form small tarts
11. Top with jam, cheese curd, or other filling
12. Let rise again
13. Bake

title: Koláčky (Sugar Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kilogram butter
- 4 eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- ⅛ kilogram sugar
- ¼ kilogram fine flour
- grated lemon zest
- chopped almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Beat the butter with eggs and egg yolks until foamed
2. Add the sugar, fine flour, and grated lemon zest
3. Mix well
4. Spread the dough on a buttered baking sheet as small cakes
5. Sprinkle with chopped almonds
6. Bake slowly

title: Koláčky Cukrové (Sugar Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams flour
- 100 grams butter, torn into pieces
- 70 grams ground peeled almonds
- grated zest and juice of half a lemon
- 2 egg yolks
- salt

## Directions

1. Combine flour, butter pieces, ground almonds, lemon zest, lemon juice, egg yolks, and salt
2. Work into fine, crumbly dough
3. Work dough well and spread it out thinly
4. Cut cookies as desired
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Sprinkle with finely ground sugar and chopped almonds
7. Make a small indentation in the center with a finger
8. Bake in oven until reddish, slowly
9. After baking, place fine preserves or other jam in the center of each cookie

title: Koláčky důlkové (Dimple Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 5 yolks
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) ground sugar
- a little ground vanilla
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- flour as needed
- egg for brushing
- preserved fruit for filling

## Directions

1. Rub the butter, add yolks, sugar, vanilla, and lemon zest
2. Add flour as needed
3. Form dough into walnut-sized balls, flatten slightly
4. Make a small indentation in the center and fill with preserved fruit
5. Brush with egg and bake on a greased sheet

title: Koláčky malé (Small Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dough (as in preceding recipe)
- plum jam
- almonds
- egg
- gingerbread
- cheese curds
- poppy seed

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as in the preceding recipe.
2. Roll out evenly and cut cakes with a form.
3. Fill the center with either thinned plum jam and almonds, or roll out a piece of dough into four sides, fill the center with plum jam, gingerbread, cheese curds or poppy seed, fold the corners, brush with egg and sprinkle with chopped almonds.

title: Koláčky malé (Small Pastry Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough (as prepared in previous recipe)
- Stewed plums (povidla), almonds or similar filling
- Egg for brushing
- Pernik (gingerbread), curd cheese or poppy seed filling

## Directions

1. Prepare dough the same way as in the previous recipe
2. Roll out or stretch the dough evenly everywhere
3. Cut out small cakes with a form
4. In the center, place either thinned stewed plums, almonds, etc., and brush the edges with beaten egg
5. Alternatively, press a piece of dough into a square shape, place stewed plums, gingerbread, curd cheese or poppy seed in the center, fold the corners over, brush with egg and sprinkle with chopped almonds

title: Koláčky na Jiný Způsob (Tarts Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- pressed yeast
- sugar
- flour
- butter
- 2 egg yolks
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- salt

## Directions

1. Warm milk until lukewarm
2. Add yeast and sugar and mix well with milk
3. Add flour and beat well
4. Let rise in a warm place
5. Cream butter
6. Add egg yolks one by one
7. Add lemon zest and cinnamon
8. When starter rises, add to butter mixture
9. Salt and mix well
10. Add lukewarm milk and sifted flour
11. Add more flour if needed to reach proper consistency
12. Beat dough well
13. Dust with flour and let rise
14. When risen, dust surface with flour
15. Scoop pieces of dough with a spoon, shape into balls
16. Flatten slightly on work surface with fingers
17. Place on buttered baking pan
18. Brush with egg yolk mixed with milk
19. Top with cheese curd or jam
20. Let rise again
21. Bake until golden
22. Turn out onto plate and dust with sugar

title: Koláčky s Mákem neb Povidly (Tarts with Poppy Seed or Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough (prepared according to previous recipe)
- egg
- jam or poppy seed filling
- blanched almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare dough according to the previous recipe
2. Form into tarts
3. Brush the edge of each tart with beaten egg
4. Fill with prepared jam or poppy seed filling
5. Place one whole almond in the center of each tart and arrange sliced almonds around it in a star pattern
6. Bake in the oven
7. Turn out onto a work surface or rolling pin
8. Dust with sugar
9. Arrange cooled tarts on a shallow plate

title: Koláčky Skořicové (Cinnamon Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 500 grams sugar, crushed
- 16 grams crushed cinnamon
- salt
- 500 grams fine flour

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs with crushed sugar for at least half an hour
2. Add crushed cinnamon and a little salt
3. Add fine flour and work into dough
4. Form small cookies on a buttered baking sheet
5. Bake slowly until golden

title: Koláčky skořicové (Cinnamon Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- 1/2 kilogram ground sugar
- 16 grams ground cinnamon
- 1/2 kilogram fine flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat eight eggs with ground sugar for at least half an hour
2. Add ground cinnamon, salt lightly, and flour
3. Form small cookies on buttered sheet
4. Bake slowly until golden

title: Koláčky Skořicové - Druhý Způsob (Cinnamon Cookies - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams fine, sifted, slightly warmed flour
- 500 milliliters lukewarm sour cream
- 50-60 grams dissolved yeast
- 2 eggs and 2 egg yolks
- 200 grams fresh butter
- salt
- sugar to taste
- 2 fresh eggs
- 16 grams finely crushed cinnamon
- 180-200 grams crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Work flour, sour cream, yeast, eggs, egg yolks, butter, salt, and sugar into dough
2. Let dough rise
3. Roll out risen dough and cut out cookies
4. Let them rise again
5. Bake on a buttered sheet slowly until golden
6. Meanwhile, beat 2 fresh eggs with 16 grams finely crushed cinnamon mixed with 180-200 grams crushed sugar
7. Brush the hot freshly baked cookies with this mixture
8. Return to oven to dry

title: Koláčky skořicové - Jiný způsob (Cinnamon Cookies - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram fine sifted flour, slightly warmed
- 1/2 liter lukewarm sour cream
- 50-60 grams melted yeast
- two eggs and two yolks
- 200 grams fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour with warm sour cream
2. Add melted yeast, eggs, yolks, and butter

title: Koláčky z krupičné kaše (Semolina Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10.5 deciliters (3 tablespoons) good milk
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) butter
- 24 decagrams (16 lots) semolina and sugar
- 12 eggs mixed in milk
- Salt
- Chopped lemon zest
- Gingerbread and butter for brushing

## Directions

1. Cook a thick porridge from 10.5 deciliters good milk, 12 decagrams butter, 24 decagrams semolina and sugar
2. When cooled, mix with 12 eggs beaten in milk, add salt and chopped lemon zest
3. Bake small cakes on a baking sheet smeared with wax, some of which brush with gingerbread mixed with butter
4. Spread a layer across the entire bottom of a bowl, arrange a smaller layer with gingerbread on top and continue until all are stacked
5. These cakes can serve as a flour dish

title: Koláčky z lískových ořechů (Hazelnut Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams (8 lots) blanched hazelnuts
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) almonds
- 135 grams (9 lots) sugar
- firm snow from two whites
- several tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Grind hazelnuts with almonds, mix with sugar and snow from whites
2. Add flour to form stiff dough
3. Roll out and cut cookies with small form
4. Bake on greased sheet

title: Koláže ze žloutků (Cakes from Egg Yolks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 hard egg yolks
- butter
- 5 fresh egg yolks
- sugar
- flour
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Crush seven hard egg yolks, rub butter with them
2. Add 5 fresh egg yolks, the same amount of sugar and flour, finely chopped lemon peel
3. Form small cakes from the mixture
4. Bake on a floured, bread-crumb-covered baking sheet
5. Sprinkle the baked cakes with sugar

title: Kompot hroznový (Grape Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- five to six nice not too ripe bunches of blue or green grapes
- 200 grams sugar
- one quarter to one half liter water

## Directions

1. Remove the berries from five to six nice not too ripe bunches of blue or green grapes
2. Dissolve 200 grams sugar in one quarter to one half liter water and boil with it but the berries must not burst
3. Remove the berries carefully with a spoon
4. Continue boiling the sugar syrup until it thickens completely
5. Pour over the grape berries and let cool together with them

title: Kompot hruškový (Pear Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- nice ripe pears
- lemon juice
- sugar
- apple puree
- sugared syrup

## Directions

1. Peel the nice ripe pears and cut off the stems
2. Cut large pears into pieces, leaving small ones whole
3. Boil the pears in soft water to which a little lemon juice has been added
4. Place in boiled sugar and boil again together
5. Place nicely on dishes
6. Surround with apple puree and pour sugared syrup over

title: Kompot oranžový (Orange Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges and finely trim the peel
2. Separate the orange segments from one another
3. Arrange on a plate
4. When ready to serve, sprinkle with crushed sugar or pour over a thickly boiled sugar syrup and allow to cool

title: Kompot z brusnic (Cranberry Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries, cleaned
- sugar
- lemon peel
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned cranberries with sugar, lemon peel, and cinnamon for a quarter hour
2. Place on a plate
3. Remove the cinnamon and lemon peel
4. The cranberries can be added to baking

title: Kompot z mirabelí (Mirabelle Plum Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fully developed but not quite ripe mirabelle plums
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the fully developed but not quite ripe mirabelle plums
2. Prick in several places with a needle
3. Heat slowly and as soon as they begin to boil, remove them covered so they finish in steam
4. Remove and place in cold water
5. The next day when they have dried, bring them to a boil several times with boiled sugar
6. Arrange nicely on a dish and pour over with sugar boiled to the pearl stage

title: Kompot z pečených hrušek (Compote of Baked Pears)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- nice and tasty pears
- cinnamon
- sugar
- sugared syrup

## Directions

1. Carefully bake nice and tasty pears on a baking sheet
2. Remove the skin from them
3. Boil in dissolved sugar, cooking with it several times
4. Sugar and cinnamon may be added
5. Place the cooked pears on a dish and pour sugared syrup over

title: Kompot z renetek (Rennet Apple Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- rennet apples
- water
- sugar
- apple puree

## Directions

1. Peel the rennet apples and cut into quarters
2. Remove the seeds
3. Boil with water and sugar until completely soft and yellow
4. Place on a plate, level with a knife and sprinkle with sugar
5. Alternatively: boil the rennet apples separately and sugar separately
6. Let the cooked rennet apples cool on sieves
7. Arrange on dishes and pour over with sugar boiled to the sixth degree, or surround with apple puree

title: Koprová omáčka hnědá (Brown Dill Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of beef broth
- vinegar
- roux made from butter
- dill, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Simmer half a liter of beef broth with vinegar
2. Add roux made from butter
3. Strain
4. Add finely chopped dill and simmer for a while

title: Koprový ocet (Dill Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dill tops, while green
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut off the dill tops while they are green, wash them, place in a large jar, pour over with good vinegar, seal and set in the sun.
2. After some time the dill is placed among cucumbers, which are then poured over with the vinegar.
3. The vinegar can be preserved and used for preparing sauces.

title: Kornoutky (Filled Cornucopias)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- two tablespoons sugar
- two heaped tablespoons flour
- one tablespoon sweet milk
- butter for greasing
- three egg whites
- sugar for meringue

## Directions

1. Take two eggs, two tablespoons sugar, two heaped tablespoons flour and one tablespoon sweet milk
2. Whip until bubbles form
3. Grease a baking sheet well with butter, put on stove to heat
4. Take a teaspoon of dough and drip onto sheet so they do not touch each other
5. Bake only five minutes, just until freshly baked and not dried
6. Remove, shape like cornucopias
7. Whip three egg whites until stiff snow, add sugar and cook over heat (this is better) and fill cornucopias

title: Koroptve (Partridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- onion, sliced
- juniper berries
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Pluck the partridges down to the head; eyes may be removed or left in.
2. Cut off the lower jaw, remove the crop, scrape the beak and legs, cut off the spurs.
3. Clean, wash, cut open the gizzard and clean it out, and salt.
4. Truss like chickens and lard the breast in two rows.
5. Place on a baking dish with sliced onion according to the number of partridges.
6. Add several juniper berries and peppercorns, pour with water, cover with a lid, and braise until tender.
7. Cut into neat pieces, strain the sauce over them, and serve usually with cabbage.

title: Koroptve Jiný Způsob (Partridge, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- lard or bacon
- butter

## Directions

1. Wipe the partridges inside with a cloth without washing; salt them.
2. Lard or wrap with bacon.
3. Place in butter and fry first the breast, then the back, continuously basting with butter.
4. When fried, cook the juices with water or broth and add to the partridges.

title: Koroptve s Jalovcem (Partridge with Juniper)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- lard
- onion
- juniper berries
- bread roll, grated

## Directions

1. Salt the partridges and lard them, then brown on both sides.
2. When browned, braise briefly with onion, several juniper berries, and grated bread roll.

title: Koroptve s kyselým zelím (Partridges with Sour Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- lard
- sour cabbage
- onion
- broth
- wine
- partridge juices

## Directions

1. Braise the tougher partridges halfway in lard.
2. Meanwhile, braise the sour cabbage with fried onion, broth and wine.
3. Place the partridges into the cabbage and braise completely.
4. Pour the partridge juices over the cabbage and brown.
5. Surround with cut partridge pieces.

title: Koroptve s rejží (Partridges with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- rice
- broth
- onion
- cloves
- partridge juices

## Directions

1. Roast the partridges.
2. Sort the rice and braise it in broth with onion and two cloves until the grains remain whole.
3. Pour the partridge juices over the rice.
4. Place the roasted partridges on the rice.

title: Kotletky skopové (Mutton Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton ribs
- pepper
- salt
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Cut cutlets from nice ribs.
2. Beat them well, pepper and salt them.
3. Place in fresh butter on a pan or baking sheet.
4. Fry one to two minutes on one side, then on the other.
5. Drain the butter, pour strong beef broth over the meat.
6. Let it simmer, arrange nicely on a plate and pour over with baked sauce.
7. Mustard can be conveniently used with the cutlets.

title: Kořenné Makronky (Spiced Macaroons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet almonds to taste
- sugar
- vanilla
- cinnamon
- clove
- orange zest

## Directions

1. Crush sweet almonds to taste and strain through a fine sieve
2. To egg whites add sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and clove, all finely ground, and add chopped orange zest
3. Mix everything into stiff dough, spread on paper or wafers and bake gently
4. The baked macaroons can be brushed with glaze and finished in a warm oven

title: Kořenné Víno (Spiced Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter of good red wine
- 20 dekagrams (13 lots) of sugar
- A piece of cinnamon
- Cloves
- Lemon zest rubbed with sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 1 liter of good red wine with 20 dekagrams of sugar, a piece of cinnamon, cloves, and lemon zest rubbed with sugar, strain and pour into glasses.

title: Kořenný ocet (Spiced Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wine vinegar
- two onions
- nutmeg
- lemon
- bay leaf
- thyme
- pepper
- cloves
- garlic

## Directions

1. Prepare spiced vinegar from wine vinegar, to which are added two onions, nutmeg, lemon, bay leaf, thyme, pepper, cloves, garlic and similar ingredients.
2. Depending on what taste the vinegar is to have, the housewife adjusts the mixture of spices.
3. Let the vinegar remain with the spices in the sun or on warm stoves for at least three months, then strain and use as desired for preparing dishes.

title: Kořeňové víno (Spiced Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- red wine
- sugar
- lemon
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. To two bottles of red wine add half a pound of sugar, well-scraped rind from one lemon, a piece of cinnamon and several cloves
2. Place on warm stove and let stand for a long time so the flavors combine, but do not boil
3. Strain through a sieve and pour hot into glasses

title: Košíčky ze šato (Château Baskets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 3 teaspoons milk
- 3 teaspoons wine
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat 3 egg yolks and 1 whole egg until as thick as batter consistency
2. Add 3 teaspoons milk, 3 teaspoons wine, and a little sugar
3. When the dough is thick enough, dip a mold in hot butter, then in the dough, and fry quickly
4. Remove the basket from the mold and fill with château cream and serve

title: Krabi tvrdí (Hard Shell Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard shell crabs
- salt
- water
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Rinse crabs in fresh water
2. Boil in salted water for about fifteen minutes
3. Arrange on a dish
4. Serve with bread and butter

title: Krabi tvrdí (Hard Shell Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- salt
- water
- bread
- butter
- cooked rice
- salt
- paprika
- nutmeg
- thyme
- basil
- milk
- breadcrumbs or bread

## Directions

1. Rinse the crabs with fresh water and let them boil in boiling salted water for about fifteen minutes
2. Arrange them on a platter and serve with bread and butter
3. Alternatively, cut the boiled crabs, mix with half of cooked rice, salt, add a little paprika, nutmeg, thyme, basil, moisten with milk, fill the shells with this mixture
4. Sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs or bread, place a piece of butter on each shell, arrange on a baking sheet and let bake in the oven

title: Kraple (Kraple Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound flour
- one pound hard butter
- four egg yolks
- one whole egg
- salt
- one coffeepot sweet milk
- jam
- sugar

## Directions

1. Divide one pound of flour into two equal halves
2. Mix one pound of butter into one half of the flour
3. Beat the egg yolks and whole egg separately on a plate, then add to the other half of the flour along with salt and one coffeepot of sweet milk
4. Mix together and add just enough flour to make the dough rollable
5. Roll out the yellow (egg) dough slightly and place the white (butter) dough on top, rolling both to the same thickness
6. Fold together into a rectangle
7. Spread a clean cloth on the work surface, dust with flour, and roll out the entire dough as thin as possible
8. Fold like a handkerchief and place on a plate
9. Let rest overnight
10. Early the next morning, roll out again to about noodle thickness
11. Cut into strips about a hand's width long and across
12. Cut the rectangles into squares and fold each into a cross pattern with ends meeting at the center, creating a four-pointed shape
13. Place on a buttered baking sheet and bake in an oven
14. When baked, spread jam on top, place on a platter, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Krocan s nádivkou (Turkey with Sage Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- three bowls of grated white bread
- two eggs
- butter
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- sage

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey for roasting as described in the previous recipe
2. For the stuffing: Take three bowls of grated white bread, place in a bowl
3. Break two eggs into it, salt and pepper it
4. Add a piece of butter and enough milk to make the stuffing as thick as ordinary dumpling dough
5. Mix one spoonful of crushed and sifted sage into the stuffing
6. Fill the turkey with the stuffing, sew it up and bake according to the previous method

title: Krocan s nádivkou mandlovou (Turkey with Almond Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoonfuls of fresh butter
- two or three eggs
- several spoonfuls of sour cream
- quarter pound of peeled and sliced almonds
- a little ground flowers or herbs
- small piece of lemon rind, finely chopped
- salt
- sugar
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Beat two spoonfuls of fresh butter, crack two or three eggs into it
2. Pour in several spoonfuls of sour cream, a quarter pound of peeled and sliced almonds, a little ground flowers or herbs, a small piece of finely chopped lemon rind
3. Salt it, and if you want the stuffing slightly sweetened, add one spoonful of sugar
4. Add enough grated bread to make a sufficiently thick stuffing
5. Fill the cavity of a well-cleaned and eviscerated turkey with this, tie it up, salt it
6. Place the turkey on a roasting pan, pour several spoonfuls of broth underneath
7. Place pieces of butter on it and let it slowly roast on both sides until golden
8. However, you must frequently baste it with fresh butter
9. Then cut it, arrange neatly on a platter and pour over it the juices in which the turkey was roasted

title: Krocan s nádivkou z kaštanů (Turkey with Chestnut Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fine chestnuts
- water
- four spoonfuls of butter
- two spoonfuls of ground sugar
- beef broth or water

## Directions

1. Peel two pounds of fine chestnuts
2. Place them in boiling water and leave them there until you can remove the thin skin with a clean cloth
3. Wash them and let them drain
4. Put four spoonfuls of butter on a platter, add two spoonfuls of ground sugar
5. Place near the fire and stir until the sugar turns slightly brown
6. Pour a little beef broth or water over it
7. Put the chestnuts in it and let them stew, but not completely tender, and especially make sure the chestnuts remain whole
8. Remove them and let them cool a little
9. Wash the well-cleaned and eviscerated turkey
10. When cooking be careful not to tear the cavity
11. Now fill the cavity with the chestnuts, sew or skewer it shut
12. Salt the turkey and roast according to the previous method

title: Krocan s ústřicovou nádivkou (Turkey with Oyster Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- bread
- eggs
- butter
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- small onion
- two dozen fresh large oysters

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey and stuffing according to the previous method, but instead of sage add a small onion browned in butter
2. Mix two dozen beautiful fresh large oysters into the stuffing
3. Fill and roast according to the previous method

title: Kroketky nudlové (Noodle Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- flour
- milk
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- bitter macaroons, crushed
- lemon zest
- 7 egg yolks
- wine or vanilla sauce
- lemon foam

## Directions

1. Make fine noodles from 2 eggs and necessary flour
2. Cook in milk, butter, salt and sugar them
3. Mix in crushed bitter macaroons and lemon zest
4. Add 7 egg yolks, mix thoroughly and let cool
5. Form into balls, fry them and pour with wine or vanilla sauce or lemon foam
6. Serve to table

title: Kroketky z Drůbežích Zbytků (Chicken Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups meat from poultry leftovers, finely chopped
- 1 spoon butter
- 2 spoons flour
- 2 cups hot milk
- salt
- white ground pepper
- a little paprika
- a little grated nutmeg
- 1 spoon finely chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped onion
- egg
- bread crumbs
- hot fat (half butter and half lard)

## Directions

1. Chop finely two cups of meat from poultry leftovers
2. Rub one spoon of butter with two spoons of flour, mix it into two cups of hot milk and cook with constant stirring until it thickens into a thick sauce, being careful it does not burn
3. Salt the chopped meat, pepper it with white ground pepper and a little paprika, add a little grated nutmeg, one spoon of finely chopped parsley and one teaspoon of finely chopped onion
4. Mix everything well into the meat, add the sauce to the meat and mix well again and let cool
5. When cooled, form small patties smaller than the palm of the hand, dip them in egg, coat in bread crumbs and fry in hot fat—best is half butter and half lard

title: Kroketky z hovězího masa (Beef Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams cooked beef
- brown broth
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- sardines
- egg (beaten)
- breadcrumbs (grated)
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Finely mince the cooked beef
2. Mix with brown broth into which 3 egg yolks have been beaten
3. Season with salt and pepper, add a little chopped parsley and sardines
4. Mix thoroughly and allow to cool
5. Form into cone shapes from the cooled mixture
6. Dip in beaten egg, roll in grated breadcrumbs and fry well

title: Kroketky z Husích Jater (Goose Liver Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose livers
- chopped truffles
- butter
- roux (zápražka)
- gravy from the livers
- 4 egg yolks
- juice of half a lemon
- chopped livers
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Braise goose livers with chopped truffles in butter
2. When cooled, cut into cubes
3. Mix the roux with the gravy from the livers, 4 egg yolks
4. Cook this mixture
5. Add lemon juice and the chopped livers
6. Place on ice to set
7. Cut and form into small croquettes
8. Brush with egg
9. Roll in bread crumbs
10. Fry in butter

title: Kroketky z nakládaného lososa (Canned Salmon Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned salmon
- two eggs
- four tablespoons grated bread
- pepper
- lard
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the salmon from the can onto a dish, remove the small bones, beat in two eggs and four tablespoons of grated bread, pepper and mix well.
2. Place a piece of lard and a piece of butter on a pan and when hot, take spoonfuls of pieces the size of an oyster and fry on both sides until golden.

title: Kroketky z Telecího Okruží a Mozečku (Veal Sweetbread and Brain Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbread
- veal brain
- lukewarm water
- cold water
- mushrooms
- broth
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Wash the veal sweetbread and brain in lukewarm water
2. Cool in cold water
3. Braise with several mushrooms in broth
4. Add egg yolks to the juices and season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
5. Place on ice to set
6. Form into small croquettes
7. Fry in butter

title: Kroketky z Vajec (Egg Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- roux (zápražka)
- 0.5 liter sour cream
- pepper
- salt
- good broth (several tablespoons)
- 2 egg yolks
- hard boiled eggs (cut into cubes)
- cooked veal sweetbread
- minced garlic
- several tablespoons aspic
- butter for frying
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the roux with 0.5 liter of sour cream
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Add several tablespoons of good broth
4. Add 2 egg yolks and strain
5. Mix in cubed hard boiled eggs, cooked veal sweetbread, minced garlic, and several tablespoons of aspic
6. Place on ice to cool
7. When cooled, cut into elongated pieces
8. Shape into small croquettes
9. Fry in butter
10. Garnish with fried parsley

title: Kroketky Zemákové (Baked Potato Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 grams (5 lots) butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 small tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 6 tablespoons grated cooked potatoes
- egg for coating
- grated bread
- butter for baking

## Directions

1. Melt the butter
2. Slowly add 2 eggs
3. Mix in 1 small tablespoon of flour, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. Add 6 tablespoons of grated cooked potatoes
5. Form into small croquettes
6. Brush with egg
7. Coat in grated bread
8. Bake in butter

title: Kroketky Zemákové (Milk and Potato Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several floury potatoes
- 0.5 liter milk
- salt
- sugar
- vanilla
- 3 egg yolks
- egg for coating
- bread crumbs
- butter for frying
- sauce

## Directions

1. Cook several floury potatoes
2. Press through in 0.5 liter of milk with salt, sugar, vanilla, and 3 egg yolks
3. When the porridge cools, form small croquettes with a spoon
4. Coat with dough
5. Brush with egg
6. Coat with bread crumbs
7. Fry in butter
8. Usually served with sauces

title: Kroketky Zemákové (Potato and Milk Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several floury cooked potatoes
- 0.5 liter milk
- salt
- sugar
- vanilla (crushed)
- several egg yolks
- egg for coating
- bread crumbs
- butter for frying
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a porridge from several floury cooked potatoes, 0.5 liter milk, salt, sugar, crushed vanilla, and several egg yolks
2. Let it cool
3. Form small croquettes from the porridge
4. Brush with egg
5. Coat with bread crumbs
6. Fry in butter
7. Serve with wine sauce

title: Kroketky Zemákové (Potato Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 2 eggs
- a little flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 6 heaping tablespoons grated cooked floury potatoes
- egg for coating
- bread crumbs
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Cream the butter until it foams
2. Add 2 eggs
3. Mix in a little flour, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. Add 6 heaping tablespoons of grated cooked floury potatoes
5. Form into elongated croquettes
6. Brush with egg
7. Coat with bread crumbs
8. Fry in butter
9. Serves as a garnish for meat

title: Krokety Jablkové (Apple Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good apples
- white wine
- grated lemon zest
- chopped almonds
- vanilla (crushed)
- egg
- dry bread crumbs
- butter for frying
- sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a compote from good apples, white wine, and grated lemon zest
2. Strain it
3. Mix in chopped almonds and a little crushed vanilla
4. Spread the mixture on small omelets
5. Roll them, fold the ends, brush with egg
6. Roll in crushed bread crumbs
7. Fry in butter
8. Serve with sauce

title: Krokety Rýžové (Rice Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- 0.5 liter broth
- butter
- salt
- onion
- 2 cloves
- 3 egg yolks
- Parmesan cheese
- nutmeg
- minced ham or any meat
- egg for coating
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Braise the rice in 0.5 liter of broth
2. Grease it and season with salt
3. Mix with onion and 2 cloves
4. Remove the onion
5. Add 3 egg yolks, Parmesan cheese, and nutmeg
6. Let cool
7. Divide into equal portions and flatten
8. Sprinkle with minced ham or any meat
9. Roll up
10. Brush with egg
11. Roll in bread crumbs
12. Bake

title: Kroupová Polévka (Barley Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter medium barley
- butter
- beef broth
- 3 egg yolks
- toasted bread roll

## Directions

1. Cook the barley in water with butter
2. Add beef broth
3. Strain the broth
4. Add 3 egg yolks
5. Pour over toasted bread roll

title: Kroupová Polévka (Groat Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter liter of medium groats
- water
- butter
- beef broth
- 3 egg yolks
- fried bread roll

## Directions

1. Cook the groats in water with butter, pour in beef broth, strain, add 3 egg yolks and pour over fried bread roll

title: Kroupové Škubánky (Groat Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams medium groats
- water
- butter
- 4 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- grated bread roll
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil the groats in water with a piece of butter.
2. Let cool.
3. Add butter, 4 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks, grated bread roll, salt, and nutmeg.
4. Form dumplings.
5. Boil in broth.

title: Kroupy (Pearl Barley)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small cups pearl barley
- 1.5 quarts water
- salt
- a large pinch of caraway seeds
- melted butter or lard
- smoked meat or sausage

## Directions

1. Rinse two small cups of sorted barley
2. Pour 1.5 quarts of water over them, salt
3. Add a large pinch of sorted caraway seeds and let them cook with frequent stirring until tender and thick
4. Turn out onto a dish and dress with melted butter or lard
5. Surround with smoked sausage or smoked meat cut into pieces

title: Krupice (Semolina Pudding with Noodle Flakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) semolina
- butter
- 1.5 liters hot milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- egg dough (for noodles)
- stewed fruit

## Directions

1. Toast the semolina in butter
2. Add 1.5 liters of hot milk and salt
3. Bake for 0.5 hour in the oven
4. Roll out dough as for noodles and let it dry
5. Cut into flakes and boil in salted water
6. Drain the water from the flakes
7. Add the flakes to the semolina with sugar and cinnamon
8. Place in a buttered form, bake, and serve with stewed fruit

title: Krupice na mléce (Semolina in Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters milk
- salt
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) almonds
- large raisins
- sugar
- butter
- semolina
- pernik (spiced cake)

## Directions

1. Strain 2 liters milk, salt and cook on a baking sheet
2. Scald almonds, peel and coarsely chop; add equal amount of large raisins with sugar and butter to milk
3. Cook everything together
4. When milk comes to a boil, sprinkle appropriate amount of semolina
5. On the plate pour hot butter over it and sprinkle with pernik
6. Fine semolina, so-called children's semolina, is best for all semolina porridges; coarser is less tasty and requires more milk

title: Krupice pečená (Baked Semolina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams (16 lots) semolina
- 4 1/4 liters milk
- 12 decagrams (6 lots) butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) sugar
- lemon zest
- 45 grams (3 lots) ground almonds
- salt
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook 24 decagrams semolina in 4 1/4 liters milk and turn out to cool
2. Beat 12 decagrams butter until fluffy, add 8 egg yolks, 9 decagrams sugar, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, 45 grams ground almonds and salt
3. Mix semolina with snow from 8 egg whites into greased mold and bake for 1½ hours

title: Krupice pražená (Roasted Semolina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina
- water
- salt
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Place semolina on a baking sheet and stir constantly on the stove until it turns reddish
2. Pour as much water as is used for semolina porridge from milk
3. Salt it and stir until it is sufficiently thick
4. On the plate pour melted butter with onion over it

title: Krupice v mléce (Semolina in Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters milk
- salt
- piece of fresh butter
- sugar
- semolina
- cinnamon or honey or syrup

## Directions

1. Salt 2 liters milk slightly and add a piece of fresh butter and sugar
2. When boiling, stir it and with the other hand sprinkle semolina, not too much
3. Stir constantly until it thickens appropriately
4. On the plate sprinkle either cinnamon and sugar, or make indentations with a spoon and pour butter, or omit butter and coat with honey or syrup

title: Krupicový nákyp (Millet Pudding)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- millet
- 10 egg yolks
- sugar
- lemon zest
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil 3/4 liter of milk with 9 dekagrams (6 lots) of butter
2. When it cools, add millet, stir it until it does not stick to the baking sheet, and pour it onto a platter so the porridge cools
3. Rub butter with 10 egg yolks and sugar with grated lemon zest, with snow from egg whites bake the pudding for 3/4 hour

title: Krupicový nákyp - Second Method (Millet Pudding - Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- millet porridge
- butter
- jam

## Directions
