1. Cook the millet as before
2. Spread two fingers' worth of porridge on a greased baking sheet, spread with jam, and continue this way until the sheet is almost full
3. Bake the pudding for 3/4 hour

title: Krupicový nákyp - Third Method (Millet Pudding - Third Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- millet porridge
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) ground almonds
- 12 dekagrams butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- lemon zest
- salt
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix the millet porridge with 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of ground almonds, 12 dekagrams of butter, 10 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, snow from 10 egg whites, lemon zest, and salt
2. Mix everything well and bake in a greased form

title: Krupičná kaše (Semolina Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina
- salted boiling water
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Stir semolina into salted boiling water
2. Stir it until it thickens
3. Turn out onto a plate and coat with melted butter and onion

title: Krupičná polévka (Farina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of caraway seed
- green parsley
- celery
- salt
- boiling water
- farina
- 3 or 4 egg yolks or 2 whole eggs
- butter
- flowers
- ginger
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put into a pot a handful of caraway seed, a little green parsley and celery, add salt and pour boiling water on it
2. Let it boil a little
3. Strain it, and when it begins to boil again, add farina, stirring often so no lumps form
4. Then add 3 or 4 egg yolks or 2 whole eggs, depending on the amount of soup
5. Add a piece of butter, a little flowers, a little ginger, a little sweet cream
6. Mix it well and thicken the soup with it
7. This soup may be served in cups in place of barley soup, only it must be thin; not much farina must be cooked into it
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Krupičná polévka s mlékem (Semolina Soup with Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 litre cold milk
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) semolina
- 1-2 litres boiling milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 9 decagrams (6 lots) of semolina in 1/2 litre of cold milk
2. Pour in 1-2 litres of boiling milk
3. Salt it, sweeten with sugar, add cinnamon and stir for 10 minutes

title: Krupičné flíčky (Semolina Flakes - Method 1)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of semolina
- butter
- 1/4 liter of hot milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Toast semolina in butter
2. Add hot milk
3. Salt it
4. Bake 1/2 hour in oven
5. Roll out dough like noodles
6. Let dry
7. Cut into flakes
8. Cook in salted water and drain
9. Add flakes to semolina with sugar and cinnamon
10. Place in greased form and bake
11. Serve with stewed fruit

title: Krupičné flíčky (Semolina Flakes - Method 2)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Fry semolina in butter
2. Salt it
3. Bake 1/2 hour in oven
4. Cut dough into flakes like noodles
5. Cook in salted water and drain
6. Add flakes to semolina with sugar and cinnamon
7. Bake

title: Krupičné Knedlíky (Farina Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- one egg yolk
- two whole eggs
- half a small pot of farina
- salt

## Directions

1. Cream two tablespoons of butter until foamy
2. Add one egg yolk and two whole eggs to it
3. Add half a small pot of farina, salt slightly, and mix well
4. Let it stand for one hour
5. With a coffee spoon, shape small dumplings and drop them into boiling salted water
6. Let them cook for half an hour
7. Remove them with a skimming spoon, arrange on a dish
8. Sprinkle with grated bread roll or bread crust and pour melted fresh butter over them
9. You can serve them with braised pigeons, chickens, or braised veal

title: Krupičné Knedlíky na Jiný Způsob (Farina Dumplings, Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- two small pots of farina
- hot milk
- three eggs
- a little milk
- three slices of bread cut into small cubes and fried
- salt
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put two small pots of farina in a bowl
2. Pour it with one small pot of hot milk and stir until cooled
3. Add three eggs beaten with a little milk
4. Add three slices of bread cut into small cubes and fried
5. Salt and mix everything well
6. Wet your hands in cold water and shape round dumplings from the dough
7. Boil them in boiling water for half an hour
8. Remove them, tear apart with a fork, arrange on a dish
9. Sprinkle with grated bread roll or bread crust and pour melted fresh butter over them
10. You can also dress them with fried and chopped onion, or finely chopped and fried bacon
11. Instead of milk, you can scald the farina with hot water in which you have dissolved a piece of butter

title: Krupičné Knedlíky s Uzeninou (Farina Dumplings with Smoked Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- three eggs
- two small pots of fine farina
- half a pound of finely minced smoked meat
- salt

## Directions

1. Cream two tablespoons of butter with three eggs
2. Add two small pots of fine farina and half a pound of finely minced smoked meat
3. Salt slightly and mix well
4. With a spoon, take out pieces and drop into boiling salted water
5. Remove the dumplings with a skimming spoon, place on a dish
6. Sprinkle with bread roll or bread fried in butter and pour melted fresh butter over them

title: Krupičné Knedlíky v Krému (Farina Dumplings in Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- two small pots of fine farina
- two whole eggs
- fresh butter
- one teaspoon of ground sugar
- salt
- one small pot of sour cream
- five egg yolks
- one tablespoon of ground sugar

## Directions

1. Cook two small pots of fine farina into thick porridge
2. Remove from heat and let cool
3. Beat two whole eggs into it
4. Add a piece of fresh butter, one teaspoon of ground sugar, a little salt, and mix well
5. With a coffee spoon, take pieces and drop into boiling salted water
6. When cooked, let them drain on a clean cloth
7. Grease a mold with butter, sprinkle with grated bread roll crust and arrange the dumplings in it
8. Beat one small pot of sour cream with five egg yolks, one tablespoon of ground sugar, and mix well
9. Pour over the dumplings and let cook in steam for one hour
10. Place in a hot oven for a short while, then remove
11. Sprinkle with ground sugar and serve

title: Krupičné koláčky (Semolina Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough (as for ordinary cakes)
- Butter
- Semolina mixed with butter and chilled for several hours
- Sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Roll out dough to make a cake, make an edge, prick it, let it rise for a while in a warm place
2. Brush with butter and with semolina that has been mixed with butter and stood in the cold for several hours
3. Sprinkle thickly with sugar and cinnamon and bake

title: Krupiční koblihy (Semolina Doughnuts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina
- milk
- butter
- sugar
- butter for frying
- sugar with ground vanilla for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Boil semolina in milk with butter and sugar
2. Spread as thick as a finger on a sheet
3. Cut out small doughnuts, heat the sheet so they come off easily
4. Fry in butter and sprinkle with sugar mixed with ground vanilla

title: Krupičný Dort (Semolina Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) sugar
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) ground almonds
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- 195 grams (13 lots) semolina
- snow from 12 egg whites
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Beat 12 egg yolks with 24 dekagrams sugar
2. Add 9 dekagrams ground almonds and lemon zest rubbed with sugar
3. Add 195 grams semolina
4. Beat everything for half an hour
5. Slowly add snow from 12 egg whites
6. Pour into a greased form sprinkled with grated roll
7. Bake for one hour
8. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
9. Glaze by passing a hot spatula over it several times

title: Krupičný ježek (Semolina Hedgehog)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- butter
- semolina
- salt
- sugar
- lemon zest
- 6 egg whites for snow

## Directions

1. Cook semolina in milk with butter until a thick porridge forms
2. Spread out and let cool
3. Add salt, sugar, lemon zest and snow from 6 egg whites
4. Put in a greased mold, place in hot water and after two hours serve with a rolled sauce

title: Krupičný nákyp (Puffed Farina Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- half a pound (1.5 cups) of farina
- three tablespoons of butter
- six egg yolks
- six tablespoons of fine sugar
- rind of half a lemon, grated
- salt
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the milk and farina together into a thick porridge
2. Cream three tablespoons of butter until frothy
3. Gradually mix in six egg yolks
4. Add the cooked porridge
5. Add six tablespoons of fine sugar
6. Add the grated lemon rind
7. Season with a little salt
8. Gently fold in the stiff snow made from six egg whites
9. Pour into a buttered mold
10. Dust with sugar
11. Bake in a well-heated oven for three-quarters of an hour
12. Instead of farina, you may also use breadcrumb porridge, semolina porridge, or noodles cooked in milk

title: Krupičný nákyp se sýrem (Semolina Pudding with Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina porridge
- broth
- sour cream
- butter
- egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- egg whites
- breadcrumbs
- ham or other meat

## Directions

1. Cook semolina porridge with broth, sour cream and butter
2. Mix the cooled porridge with egg yolks, salt, nutmeg and snow, put into a greased form sprinkled with breadcrumbs and place it in hot water
3. Turn out the pudding onto the table, usually served with ham or other meat

title: Krupičný Svítek (Semolina Puff)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- sugar
- semolina
- milk

## Directions

1. Take 2 eggs, whip the whites so that stiff snow forms; then add 2 yolks, a pinch of salt, nutmeg and sugar to taste
2. Then lightly sprinkle semolina into it so that not too thick batter forms
3. It must not be thin, about like for pancakes
4. Grease a pie plate with butter and pour the puff into it and bake in the oven
5. It must bake quickly to golden color
6. When the puff is baked, cut it into cubes
7. In the meantime have milk on the stove, and as soon as it begins to boil, place the puff in it and let it come to a boil several times
8. Then remove each piece onto a designated plate, pour over it the milk in which the puff was boiled and serve

title: Krupičný Étere (Semolina Fried Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams semolina (12 lots)
- three-quarters liter hot milk
- 12 dekagrams butter (8 lots)
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix semolina into three-quarters liter hot milk and cook
2. When cooled, add butter and 6 eggs
3. Place on a greased baking pan, cover and when fried on one side, turn it
4. Break into pieces and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Krupičuá polévka (Farina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of caraway seeds
- a little fresh parsley
- celery
- salt
- boiling water
- 0.5 cup of farina
- 1 to 2 whole eggs
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- a little nutmeg
- a little ginger
- sweet sour cream

## Directions

1. Put a handful of caraway, a little fresh parsley and celery in a pot, salt it, pour boiling water over it and let it simmer briefly, then strain it.
2. When it begins to boil again, add half a cup of farina.
3. Stir often to avoid lumps; cook for a quarter hour.
4. Then beat 1 to 2 whole eggs, add a tablespoon of butter, a little nutmeg, a little ginger, and if desired some sweet sour cream, mix well and stir into the soup.

title: Kruplční nákyp s jablky (Barley Soufflé with Apples)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- butter
- barley
- egg yolks
- sugar
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- apples

## Directions

1. Mix butter and barley in 1 liter of milk, several egg yolks, sugar, lemon peel rubbed with sugar and snow from the egg whites
2. Grease a mold, place sliced apples without seeds on the bottom
3. Add the barley mixture to the mold, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
4. Bake slowly for one hour

title: Krupovka (Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound pearl barley
- cold water
- two ounces fresh butter
- two quarts good beef soup
- flower (seasoning)
- six egg yolks
- lukewarm soup or cooked cooled cream

## Directions

1. Clean the barley well and put in a pot with cold water to cook, being careful it does not burn.
2. When tender, transfer to another pot with two ounces fresh butter, rub well, pour two quarts of good beef soup over it, mix well.
3. Press through a strainer or sieve and season with flower.
4. In another pot, mix six egg yolks with a cup of lukewarm soup or cooked cooled cream and thicken the soup with this mixture.
5. Serve in cups at dinners or evening entertainments with a quarter slice of lemon to each portion.
6. If to be served at lunch, at least one third of the barley may be left in it.
7. Serves 12 persons.
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Krupovka (Purée of Barley)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of pearl barley
- a piece of fresh butter
- two quarts of hot beef broth
- six egg yolks
- a little lukewarm broth

## Directions

1. Cook one coffee cup of pearl barley. When it is soft, put it in a clean pot; add a piece of fresh butter and mix it well.
2. Then pour two quarts of hot beef broth over it, mix again, strain through a sieve and season with flowers.
3. In another pot, beat six egg yolks with a little lukewarm broth, pour this into the boiling broth, remove from heat and beat again and serve in coffee cups.

title: Královský Creme (Royal Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- whipped cream made from rice, semolina, sago or similar
- milk
- egg yolk
- sweet spiced cream

## Directions

1. Mix whipped cream with milk into a porridge
2. Add egg yolk and sweet spiced cream
3. Mix slowly and ensure neither ingredient is too hot or too cold
4. Optionally add fish bladder to help the cream thicken

title: Královský dort (Royal Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 kilogram sweet almonds
- some bitter almonds
- 2 eggs
- 0.25 kilogram finely crushed sugar
- 28 egg yolks
- 1 kilogram fresh butter
- vanilla
- cinnamon
- nutmeg flower
- lemon zest
- 0.25 kilogram finest flour

## Directions

1. Peel the sweet almonds and some bitter almonds
2. Rub them finely with 2 eggs
3. Add the 0.25 kilogram finely crushed sugar to which the 28 egg yolks are added
4. Mix everything for at least half an hour
5. Clarify 1 kilogram of fresh butter and add it slowly to the mixture
6. Finely crush vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg flower, and lemon zest and mix thoroughly
7. Slowly add 0.25 kilogram of finest flour while constantly stirring
8. Make snow from 28 egg whites and fold into the dough
9. Pour into low molds which are usually round, buttered, and spread with very thin batter
10. Bake slowly with hot wooden coals placed around the mold both underneath and on top so that it bakes evenly on all sides
11. Stack several layers baked to golden yellow on top of each other, layering with fine jam, and bake again in the oven
12. Brush the cooled cake with glaze and decorate

title: Královský dortík (Royal Small Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram butter
- 0.5 kilogram finely crushed sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 0.25 kilogram almonds rubbed with egg whites
- cinnamon
- cloves
- vanilla
- finely chopped lemon zest
- 0.25 kilogram fine flour
- 6 egg whites
- egg white for brushing
- chopped almonds
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Place 0.5 kilogram butter on a heated dish
2. Rub it with 0.5 kilogram finely crushed sugar and 12 egg yolks
3. Add the 0.25 kilogram almonds rubbed with egg whites, a little cinnamon, cloves, vanilla, finely chopped lemon zest, and 0.25 kilogram fine flour
4. Mix everything
5. Make snow from 6 egg whites and add to the mixture
6. Form heaps of dough on greased papers on baking sheets
7. Flatten them, brush with beaten egg white, sprinkle with chopped almonds and sugar, and bake gently

title: Královský Punč (Royal Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.75 kilograms sugar
- Tea water
- Juice of 6 lemons
- Burgundy wine
- Arrack
- Good Rhine wine
- 1 bottle champagne wine

## Directions

1. Chop 0.75 kilograms of sugar into pieces
2. Dissolve in tea water
3. Strain everything with cloth
4. Add juice from 6 lemons and mix
5. Add some Burgundy wine, arrack, good Rhine wine, and 1 bottle of champagne wine
6. Mix everything and serve cold on the table

title: Králík s Citrónovou Omáčkou (Rabbit with Lemon Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit, salted for 2-3 days
- beef broth
- fresh butter
- salt
- parsley root
- celery
- lemon peel
- flower (cloves)
- flour for roux
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Salt the rabbit for 2-3 days, otherwise it will be tough.
2. Wash the salted rabbit, cut it into pieces, and place on a baking dish.
3. Pour beef broth over it and add a piece of fresh butter, salt, parsley root, celery, lemon peel, and cloves.
4. Braise until completely tender.
5. Carefully remove the rabbit pieces.
6. Make a pale roux from butter and flour and add to the remaining broth to achieve appropriate thickness.
7. Strain the sauce over the rabbit and add the juice of one lemon.

title: Králík s Podpouštkou (Rabbit with Liver Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned rabbit, head, neck, and offal reserved
- butter
- finely chopped onion
- broth or boiling water
- parsley
- celery
- lemon juice
- liver (from reserved offal)

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned rabbit into pieces, reserving the head, neck, and offal.
2. Wash and dry the meat, salt it, dust with flour.
3. Fry in hot butter with finely chopped onion.
4. After a short time, add either broth or boiling water, parsley, and celery.
5. Add a little lemon juice and braise the meat in this sauce.
6. Strain the sauce and add the liver to it, season it, and pour over the rabbit meat.
7. This method can also be used for beef.

title: Králík Smažený (Fried Rabbit)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- flour
- egg
- grated bread roll

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into pieces, wash, and dry.
2. Coat in flour, dip in egg, and roll in grated bread roll.
3. Fry until golden brown.
4. Serve alone as a tasty dish or add to spinach or potato mash.

title: Krémová omáčka (Custard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 koflíky mléka
- 4 žloutky
- 1/2 koflíku cukru
- pár kapek vanilky

## Directions

1. Dej vařit dva koflíky mléka.
2. Rozetři 4 žloutky s půl koflíkem cukru, až se to hezky zpění a přidej k vařícímu mléku a míchej pilně až to zhoustne.
3. Ihned odstav, aby se to nesrazilo.
4. Přidej pár kapek vanilky, zamíchej a podej v mísecce.

title: Krůta (Roasted Turkey)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- edible chestnuts
- butter
- egg yolks
- sugar
- houska (bread roll)
- parsley
- chives

## Directions

1. Cut off the turkey's head and hold it by the legs so all blood drains out
2. Pluck, scald, and clean the turkey
3. Salt the turkey
4. Prepare stuffing: cook edible chestnuts in an oven, peel them, and stuff the turkey with them, or alternatively mix together 3 decigrams (2 lots) of butter, 8 grated edible chestnuts, a piece of crushed sugar, and 2 eggs
5. If the mixture is too thin, add more chestnuts or 2 grated houska rolls, 3 decigrams (2 lots) of butter, a little parsley and a little chives, and mix together
6. Fill the turkey with the stuffing and sew it shut
7. Spread butter on the turkey, roast until golden brown, and carve

title: Krůtí krev (Turkey Blood)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey blood
- salt
- spices

## Directions

1. When killing the turkey, catch the blood in a vessel
2. Salt and season the blood
3. Mix well
4. Roast it thoroughly

title: Kuba (Kuba)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter groats
- butter
- salt
- three-tenths deciliter (one tablespoon) dried mushrooms
- pepper
- allspice
- ginger
- clove
- two cloves garlic, grated
- pork lard

## Directions

1. Cook groats in water with a piece of butter until soft
2. Rinse dried mushrooms and cook in salted water
3. Strain mushrooms and chop finely
4. Mix groats with mushrooms
5. Add pepper, allspice, ginger, clove, and grated garlic
6. Grease a baking dish with pork lard
7. Bake groats with mushrooms in it

title: Kukuřičné Klasy (Sweet Corn on the Cob)
body:
## Ingredients

- young corn cobs (sweet corn variety)
- salt
- sugar
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the corn cobs and remove the silks, but keep the inner leaves
2. Boil them in hot salted water to which you can also add a little sugar
3. Cook for 20 minutes, then remove the cobs, remove the inner leaves and serve
4. Serve with fresh butter and salt

title: Kukuřičné lívance (Corn Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups corn flour
- hot water
- small teaspoon butter
- small teaspoon sugar
- small teaspoon salt
- five tablespoons sweet milk
- three broken egg yolks
- one teaspoon baking powder mixed with one tablespoon wheat flour
- three egg whites

## Directions

1. Put two cups of corn flour in a pot, pour with hot water, let it stand half an hour and when it has cooled, mix in a small teaspoon of butter, a small teaspoon of sugar and a small teaspoon of salt, five tablespoons of sweet milk and three broken egg yolks
2. Then mix one teaspoon of baking powder in one tablespoon of wheat flour, add it to the batter and mix well
3. At the end, mix in snow from three egg whites and bake

title: Kukuřičné lívance kynuté (Raised Corn Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup corn flour
- hot water
- two small cups milk
- two tablespoons leaven from dry yeast, or half a piece of soaked pressed yeast
- one teaspoon yellow sugar
- two beaten eggs
- salt
- half teaspoon baking soda dissolved in hot water

## Directions

1. In the evening, pour one cup of corn flour with hot water, but not into a thin batter, but only so that it is just soaked with hot water
2. When it cools, add two small cups of milk and two tablespoons of leaven from dry yeast, or half a piece of soaked pressed yeast, one teaspoon of yellow sugar and let it rise until morning
3. In the morning, add two beaten eggs, salt, dissolve half a teaspoon of baking soda in hot water, mix and bake

title: Kuráta se slanečkem (Spring Chicken with Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- two young chickens
- beef broth
- herring
- butter
- flowers or herbs
- lemon rind
- lemon juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Pluck two young chickens and cook them halfway in beef broth
2. Remove them and let them cool
3. Meanwhile, wash and cut the herring into elongated strips, like bacon
4. Thread the herring through the chickens on the breasts
5. Cut the chickens in half and place them on a platter
6. Pour over them the broth in which they were cooked
7. Add a piece of butter, some flowers or herbs, lemon rind, lemon juice, and remaining herring pieces
8. Cut the herring roe into cubes and add it as well
9. Brown this with a little flour and let the chickens simmer until tender
10. Remove them to a platter and strain the sauce through a sieve over them

title: Kuřata s Podpouštkou (Chicken with Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned chicken killed the day before
- butter
- salt
- broth from chicken head, neck, wings and innards
- bread cut into cubes
- pepper
- lemon juice
- egg yolks
- mushrooms
- asparagus
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Clean chicken, cut into quarters and salt them, let rest at least an hour
2. Make strong broth from chicken head, neck, wings and innards
3. Dry chicken quarters and braise on both sides in butter
4. Add prepared broth from other parts of poultry and braise chicken slowly in steam
5. At the same time cook bread cut into cubes together with chicken and remove to dish with chicken
6. Strain broth and thicken it, season with pepper and lemon juice, add several egg yolks
7. Pour sauce over chicken and bread cubes
8. Add browned mushrooms, asparagus, cauliflower and similar tender vegetables to sauce

title: Kuřata s Podpouštkou po Anglicku (English Style Chicken with Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- plucked and cleaned chicken
- boiling water
- good broth
- onion
- anchovies
- salt
- white pepper
- cream
- butter
- flour
- mushrooms
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scald plucked and cleaned chicken in boiling water, remove skin and cut into quarters
2. Cook with good broth, onion, anchovies, salt and white pepper until tender
3. Strain sauce with cream and butter mixed with flour
4. Cook finely chopped mushrooms in the sauce
5. Add egg yolks and lemon juice for taste
6. Pour this sauce over prepared chicken

title: Kuře s Celerem (Chicken with Root Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- beef broth or butter
- 2 heads of root celery
- piece of fresh butter
- several egg yolks
- flower (seasoning)
- white roux
- green celery greens

## Directions

1. Cook two chickens either in beef broth or braise them in butter
2. Let two heads of celery cook and when softened, slice one head into rounds and add to the chickens
3. The second head of celery, cooked until soft, rub through with a piece of fresh butter and several egg yolks
4. Season with flower, brown with white roux, strain through a sieve onto the chickens and let heat through
5. Place the chickens on a platter, pour the sauce over, surround with celery rounds and garnish with green celery greens

title: Kuře s citrónovou omáčkou (Chicken with Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- butter
- flour
- lemon
- sugar
- white wine

## Directions

1. Stew a nice chicken according to the previous method, remove it to a platter, cover it and place it in a warm place.
2. Make a white roux from a spoonful of butter and a spoonful of flour, thin it with the strained broth in which the chicken stewed, add juice from half a lemon, a little finely grated lemon zest and a small piece of sugar, if desired add a few tablespoons of white wine, cook it into a smooth sauce, and pour it over the cut up chicken.

title: Kuře s Houbami (Chicken with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-cleaned mushroom caps
- piece of fresh butter
- a little green parsley
- cleaned chicken, cut into pieces
- salt
- beef broth
- flour
- sweet sour cream

## Directions

1. Slice the well-cleaned mushroom caps and let them braise briefly in a piece of fresh butter and a little green parsley
2. Place cleaned chicken cut into pieces on top of them, salt them and let braise under a cover, being careful not to burn
3. When softened and the liquid begins to evaporate, dust lightly with flour, add a little beef broth and let it come to a boil
4. Strain and serve
5. To the sauce you can also add a little cooked sweet sour cream

title: Kuře s Karfiolem (Chicken with Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cleaned chickens
- 1 heaping spoon butter
- 1.5 spoons flour
- beef broth or salted water
- clean white cauliflower
- ground flower (seasoning)

## Directions

1. Cook two cleaned chickens well
2. On a griddle, let 1 heaping spoon of butter melt, add 1.5 spoons of flour, make a pale roux and brown the broth in which the chickens were cooked
3. Meanwhile, let clean white cauliflower cook in beef broth or salted water
4. Cut the chickens into neat pieces and place on the griddle
5. Pour the prepared sauce over them, season with ground flower and let simmer briefly
6. Place the chickens with sauce on a platter and surround them with cauliflower
7. Asparagus chickens are prepared the same way

title: Kuře s Kedlubnami (Chicken with Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- some fat
- rooster
- kohlrabi
- salt
- pepper
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon powder, sweet
- a little salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Put some fat in a pot; clean the rooster, cut it into pieces and place in the pot
2. Clean the kohlrabi, rinse them, cut into pieces and place on top of the rooster
3. Salt and pepper everything, cover and let it braise over gentle heat
4. When you see that everything will be ready in about half an hour, make a batter from two eggs, one cup milk, one teaspoon sweet powder, a little salt and flour for dumplings
5. Divide the batter into four parts with a spoon and place on the surface, letting everything braise
6. When the dumplings are half-cooked, turn them over with two forks to the other side
7. When done, remove them and add a little water, mix it with a little flour in the pot and thicken the sauce, then remove the rooster separately and the sauce and kohlrabi also separately

title: Kuře s knedličky (Chicken with Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- beef broth or fresh butter
- water
- new butter
- eggs
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- bread or crackers

## Directions

1. Boil two chickens in beef broth or stew on a pan in fresh butter with water added.
2. Meanwhile, prepare the dumplings as follows: Beat a spoonful of new butter in a deep plate until foamy, beat in two eggs, mix in a teaspoon of chopped fresh parsley, a little salt and pepper, and mix in enough grated bread or crumbled crackers so that dumplings the size of hazelnuts can be made from it, and cook them separately in salted water.
3. When the chickens are tender enough, remove them, strain the broth or liquid from them into a pot, and if there is not enough broth, add more, thicken it with a white roux, pour it back into the pan, place the chickens in it and let it cook.
4. Place the cooked dumplings on a platter with the chickens and pour the sauce over it.

title: Kuře s Majonézou (Spring Chicken with Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful cleaned chicken
- salt
- lemon juice
- butter
- green parsley
- beef broth
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 small glass good olive oil or heated fresh butter
- juice of 1 lemon
- salt
- pepper
- lettuce hearts
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Salt the cleaned chicken, lard it, rub the breast with lemon juice so it whitens nicely and let it braise in a griddle with butter, green parsley and a little beef broth
2. Cut it into neat pieces and let cool
3. Beat six egg yolks, add gradually a small glass of good olive oil or heated fresh butter, stirring constantly until it is smooth
4. Then add the juice of one lemon, salt and pepper it lightly
5. Dip the pieces of chicken in this sauce, arrange them on a platter, pour the remaining sauce over them and surround them with lettuce hearts and hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters

title: Kuře s paprikou (Chicken with Paprika)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- onion
- chicken
- salt
- flour
- beef broth
- sour cream
- paprika

## Directions

1. Place two spoonfuls of fresh butter in a pan, one thinly sliced onion, place two quartered chickens on it, salt it lightly, cover and let it stew for a while.
2. Then remove it to another pan, add a full spoonful of flour to the broth, mix it, pour a little beef broth and a few spoonfuls of sour cream and let it cook a bit more.
3. Strain this over the chicken, add about half a teaspoon of paprika and let it cook in the sauce.

title: Kuře s Rajskými Jablky (Chicken with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 tomatoes
- sour bread or white bread, grated and soaked in sour cream, or softened cracker
- beef broth
- flower (seasoning)
- small lemon zest
- piece of sugar
- cooked or braised chicken

## Directions

1. Cook 2-3 tomatoes in wine or water
2. Add grated bread or white bread soaked in sour cream, or softened cracker
3. Pour in a little beef broth, season with flower and small lemon zest
4. Add a piece of sugar, mix well and strain over cooked or butter-braised chicken

title: Kuře s Rýží (Chicken with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cleaned rice
- beef broth or hot water
- 3 egg yolks or 2 whole eggs
- piece of fresh butter
- beef broth
- flower (seasoning)
- 1 teaspoon green parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Rinse the cleaned rice first in cold water and then well in boiling water
2. Pour beef broth or hot water over it and let it cook until soft, being careful that it remains whole
3. In a small pot, beat together three egg yolks or two whole eggs with a piece of fresh butter and some beef broth
4. Season with flower, pour this over the rice and add the finely chopped green parsley, stirring gently
5. Place the cooked chicken on a platter, cover it with rice and serve

title: Kuře s rýží (Chicken with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- beef broth or water

## Directions

1. First pick over a pot of rice and rinse it in cold water, then rinse it well in boiling water.
2. Pour beef broth or hot water over it and let it cook until tender, but you must take care that it remains whole.

title: Kuře Zadělávané (Braised Chicken)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared chicken
- butter
- parsley root
- celery
- carrot
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut prepared chicken in half or in quarters and place on a baking dish or in a pot
2. Add a piece of good butter, parsley root, celery and carrot
3. Braise or cook until tender
4. Strain the broth from the chicken onto another baking dish
5. Make a light roux, add pounded flower, strain the roux through a sieve into the broth as needed
6. Add more butter if needed
7. Chop fresh parsley and mix it together with the chicken
8. Arrange the chicken on a dish like a mound and cover with sauce

title: Kvasová polévka (Bread Sponge Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- rye flour
- water
- salt
- butter
- onion
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 3 to 4 tablespoons of sour cream

## Directions

1. Have a special stone pot for fermenting ready.
2. In the evening, put 4 to 5 tablespoons or more of nice rye flour in it, as much as you wish to have plenty of soup, make a starter and cover the pot with a clean cloth and let it stand near the fire until morning so the starter ferments well.
3. In the morning, let water boil, season it well with salt, and when the water boils, with one hand pour the starter and with the other stir with a wooden spoon until it boils about three times.
4. It should not be thin, but also not thick, about like a thin sauce.
5. Then set it aside, put a nice piece of butter in a pan, and when it begins to sizzle, add finely chopped onion, and when it begins to redden, beat 2 to 3 eggs and throw into the onion, and when these begin to fry, mix lightly with the onion and add everything to the bread sponge soup along with 3 to 4 tablespoons of sour cream.

title: Kvíčaly (Guinea Fowl)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Guinea fowl
- Juniper berries
- Onion
- Lard
- Pepper
- Beef stock

## Directions

1. Prepare guinea fowl for roasting, save the stomach, liver and lungs
2. Crush about 6 juniper berries and rub the guinea fowl with them
3. Chop the onion and fry it in lard
4. When the onion is golden, place the guinea fowl on it
5. Add several peppers, pour in beef stock and roast well on both sides
6. Cook the saved organs at the same time with the guinea fowl

title: Kynutá štrudle s mákem (Raised Dough Roll with Poppy Seeds)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- 6 whole eggs
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 small pot good sour cream
- flour
- salt
- piece of soaked pressed yeast
- 2 small pots ground poppy seeds cooked in milk
- half a small pot ground sugar
- zest of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Rub 4 tablespoons butter
2. Beat in six whole eggs
3. Mix well
4. Add 1 tablespoon sugar
5. Add 1 small pot of good sour cream
6. Add enough flour to make a dough like for small pastries
7. Salt slightly
8. Add a piece of soaked pressed yeast
9. Beat well and let it rise
10. Dust the rolling pin with flour
11. Place the risen dough on it and roll it with a rolling pin to about the thickness of a stalk
12. Mix 2 small pots of ground poppy seeds cooked in milk
13. Half a small pot of ground sugar
14. Very finely chopped zest of half a lemon
15. Spread the rolled dough generously with this
16. Roll up into a strudel
17. Grease a pan with butter and let the strudel bake
18. Remove to a platter and sprinkle with ground sugar

title: Kynuté Biskety Bramborové (Raised Potato Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- about 6 medium potatoes
- 2 cups hot milk
- 2 heaping tablespoons butter or lard
- 1 small teaspoon salt
- 1 small teaspoon sugar
- ½ cup flour
- 1 cup liquid yeast or 1 piece of compressed yeast dissolved in 1 tablespoon lukewarm water
- additional flour as needed

## Directions

1. Peel about 6 medium potatoes, cook them, drain the water and mash them well with a wooden spoon.
2. Add to them 2 cups of hot milk, 2 heaping tablespoons of butter or lard, 1 small teaspoon of salt and 1 small teaspoon of sugar; add ½ cup of flour, mix everything well and set aside.
3. When it cools to lukewarm, add 1 cup of liquid yeast or 1 piece of compressed yeast dissolved in 1 tablespoon of lukewarm water, and enough flour to make a thick dough.
4. Beat this mixture for about 5 minutes until well beaten and it doesn't stick to the bowl or hands.
5. Cover, set in a warm place and let it rise for about 3 hours until well leavened.
6. Add more flour so that it becomes a soft dough, roll it quickly but lightly for 15 minutes, then roll out to about finger thickness and cut out biscuits.
7. Place them on a baking sheet greased with lard, cover and set in a warm place to rise.
8. When risen, bake in a hot oven and serve hot.

title: Kynuté Knedlíky (Raised Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup milk (for starter)
- 3 tablespoons flour (for starter)
- compressed yeast or dried yeast
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups milk
- salt
- flour (enough to make stiff dough like bread)

## Directions

1. In the morning make a starter from ½ cup milk, 3 tablespoons flour and a piece of compressed yeast, or from dried yeast overnight
2. When the starter has risen, beat 1 tablespoon butter and 2 eggs on a plate
3. Add the risen starter and 2 cups milk to it, salt and add enough flour to make dough as stiff as for bread and mix well in the bowl
4. Roll out on a rolling pin until the dough is nice and smooth and put it back in the bowl to rise
5. When it has risen so it is twice as much as before, put on rolling pin and cut dumplings the size of an apple, round them in your hands and let them rise again on the rolling pin
6. When they have risen again, put them in boiling water, cover well and let boil for half an hour
7. Take them out, tear each one with a fork and pour melted butter over them

title: Kynuté Knedlíky Jiným Způsobem (Raised Dumplings Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ pound flour
- ½ pound cottage cheese
- 3 eggs
- yeast
- milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Take ¾ pound flour, ½ pound cottage cheese, 3 eggs, a piece of yeast, milk as needed and salt, work everything together and let it rise
2. Make small round dumplings from it, let them rise again in the cold
3. Fry them in melted butter
4. Eat them hot immediately
5. Each person can sprinkle them with what they like

title: Kynuté piškoty (Raised Lady Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- four egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 4 bowls flour
- one bowl good fresh sour cream
- one small ball of yeast mixed with milk
- two egg whites
- flour
- butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Rub 4 tablespoons of butter with four egg yolks until foamy
2. Add 2 tablespoons of sugar, 4 bowls of flour, one bowl of good fresh sour cream, and one small ball of yeast mixed with milk
3. Mix everything well together
4. Finally fold in stiff meringue from two egg whites and let the dough rise
5. Put on a surface dusted with flour, roll out with a rolling pin and cut out lady fingers with a mold
6. Place on a baking sheet rubbed with butter
7. Let rise
8. Brush with beaten eggs
9. Sprinkle with finely pounded sugar and bake in the oven
10. Remove and arrange on a dish when cooled

title: Kynuté Švestkové Knedlíky (Raised Dough Prune Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of fresh butter
- three whole eggs
- six small pots of flour
- salt
- three tablespoons of yeast
- good milk or sour cream
- pitted prunes

## Directions

1. Cream one tablespoon of fresh butter
2. Beat three whole eggs into it and mix well
3. Add six small pots of flour, salt, and add three tablespoons of yeast, mix
4. Add as much good milk or sour cream as needed for dough thicker than for ordinary dumplings
5. Work well with a spoon and let rise
6. When risen, that is, when it has doubled in volume, remove the dough onto a board sprinkled with flour
7. Cut it into pieces about the size of a walnut, wrap a pitted prune in each piece
8. Place the dumplings on a board, let them rise a bit more, and cook as mentioned above

title: Kynuté Švestkové Knedlíky na Jiný Způsob (Raised Prune Dumplings, Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of fresh butter
- two eggs
- one small pot of milk
- one tablespoon of sugar
- salt
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- two small pots of flour
- beautiful ripe prunes

## Directions

1. Cream one tablespoon of fresh butter, beat two eggs into it, and beat well
2. Add one small pot of milk, one tablespoon of sugar, and salt
3. Mix one teaspoon of baking powder into two small pots of flour and add to the rest
4. Add flour as needed to work soft dough, about as stiff as for biscuits
5. With a spoon, scoop out pieces of dough, shape in your hands, place on a board, flatten slightly, and wrap a beautiful large and ripe prune in each piece
6. Boil in boiling salted water and cook for about 15 minutes
7. Do not overcook or they will fall apart
8. Dumplings with apples can be made the same way instead of prunes
9. Large apples are cut into quarters and small apples into halves; the core must of course be removed

title: Kysané zelí (Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- naložené zelí
- cibulka - prostřední
- cukr - štipec
- sádlo - lžíce
- cibule - půl malé
- mouka - lžíce
- voda - studená
- omastí z pečené husy nebo kachny

## Directions

1. Dej naložené zelí vařit do hrnku a přimíchej do něho jednu prostřední cibulku na drobno rozkrájenou
2. Přidej štipec cukru, přiklop a nech vařit půl hodiny
3. Do hrnečka dej lžíci sádla a půl malé rozkrájené cibule
4. Když cibulka zčervená, to přidej k zelí
5. Rozkverluj lžíci mouky ve studené vodě, přidej k zelí a zamíchej
6. Nech var obejít, můžeš také přidat lžíci omastku z pečené husy nebo kachny

title: Kysané zelí na jiný způsob
body:
## Ingredients

- cibule - prostřední
- sádlo - lžíce
- naložené zelí
- voda - vřelá
- mouka

## Directions

1. Rozkrájej drobounce cibuli prostřední velikosti a dej na rendlík se lžící sádla
2. Když začíná cibule červenat, dej do toho naložené zelí
3. Nech dusit 15 neb 20 minut, je-li to příliš vysmahlé, podlej vřelou vodou
4. Popraš moukou, zamíchej a nech ještě chvíli dusit

title: Kyselá hovězí pečeně (Sour Beef Roast)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef (choice cut)
- salt
- butter
- hot water
- onion
- vinegar
- mushrooms
- lemon (sliced)

## Directions

1. Pound the meat, salt it
2. Brown on both sides on butter
3. Add a little hot water and onion
4. When the roast is done, add vinegar, mushrooms, and a little chopped onion
5. Add lemon sliced into strips
6. Simmer and add to the roast

title: Kyselá čočka (Lentils with Sour Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- two small cups of good lentils
- soft water
- salt
- lard
- onion, grated
- thyme
- vinegar
- water
- eggs fried in butter or sausages

## Directions

1. Sort and wash two small cups of good lentils, add soft water and let them cook for three-quarters of an hour, then drain the water and pour them into another pot and salt them.
2. Put a piece of lard on the stove and stew the grated onion in it until it froths.
3. Add it to the poured-out lentils, sprinkle a little thyme into it, make a brown roux with vinegar and water and pour it onto the lentils and let them cook until soft.
4. Then pour onto a platter and garnish with fried eggs or sausages.

title: Kyselé Zelí (Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and cleaned cabbage heads
- salt
- caraway seeds
- dill seeds
- clean cabbage leaves
- clean cloth
- heavy stone

## Directions

1. Peel and clean the cabbage heads, cut them into quarters and remove the cores
2. Shred the cabbage on a grater and salt it in a large barrel or on a clean cloth spread on the floor
3. For fifty large heads, take one and a half pounds of salt, two tablespoons of cleaned caraway seeds and the same amount of dill seeds, and mix well
4. Cover the bottom of a clean wooden barrel or large stone crock with clean cabbage leaves
5. Place a layer of shredded cabbage in it, press it with a wooden mallet, add cabbage again, press again, and repeat until the barrel is full, but never add too large a layer at once or it cannot compress well
6. Place cabbage leaves on the surface, cover the barrel with a clean white cloth, place a well-fitting lid on it and put a clean washed heavy stone on top
7. Set the barrel in a warm place and let it stand about eight days until it sours
8. Then store it in a cold place protected from frost and cover with a clean cloth moistened in cold water, which should be washed and replaced after each use
9. From time to time you must clean the lid, cloth and stone thoroughly so the cabbage does not spoil
10. If the cabbage dries too much, you must add fresh water
11. When taking cabbage for cooking, scrape it from the surface with your fingers so the cabbage stays completely flat and cover and weight it again
12. Some places also add fennel, ground mustard, crushed pepper or juniper to the salt
13. Between individual layers you can also place peeled and cut sour apples without seeds, which gives the cabbage a particularly good flavor
14. If you layer the cabbage in a barrel with white wine, it will also acquire a very delicate flavor
15. Do not oversalt it, as oversalted cabbage will not sour for a long time
16. It is always better to use several smaller barrels than one large one, and if you want to use the cabbage soon, layer it in stone crocks and set it in a warm place so fermentation begins quickly
17. Many experienced housewives put shredded cabbage in a barrel, salt it, add caraway and then squeeze it with their hands for a long time until it releases water, then store it in a barrel and squeeze it quickly by hand; cabbage prepared this way lasts the longest

title: Kyselé zelí s vepřovou sekaniou (Sauerkraut with Sausage Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart sauerkraut
- one pound pork
- salt
- pepper
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Take one quart of sauerkraut and drain all the water from it
2. Place in a glazed pot or clay dish (not iron or tin as it will not taste good)
3. Add a bit of salt
4. Grind one pound of pork finely on a meat grinder
5. Mix the meat into the sauerkraut and season with pepper
6. Stir well
7. Cover with a lid and place in an oven to cook for about one hour
8. Remove and pour a cupful of sour cream over it, but do not stir
9. Return to the oven for another quarter hour
10. When the top is nicely reddened it is done

title: Kyselý zavařený meloun (Pickled Watermelon)
body:
## Ingredients

- firm part of watermelon
- vinegar
- 4 pounds watermelon flesh
- 3 pounds sugar
- 1/2 lemon
- several pieces of cinnamon
- about 8 cloves

## Directions

1. Cut the firm part of the watermelon into small pieces, removing the seeds
2. Place in a stone bowl
3. Pour over enough vinegar to make it slightly sour, not too sour
4. Let stand overnight
5. The next morning bring to a boil
6. Add sugar, lemon, cinnamon, and cloves
7. Simmer gently until it takes on a reddish color and becomes as thick as syrup, stirring frequently to prevent burning
8. When cooled, place in stone jars, seal well, and store in a cool place where it will keep for a long time

title: Káva (Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee beans, coarsely ground
- water
- egg white
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. For five cups of coffee, usually take three heaping tablespoons of coarsely ground coffee
2. Pour it into boiling water in a pot, let it come to a boil several times, remove from heat and let it settle
3. To clarify, pour a little cold water into it after boiling
4. Alternatively, mix a piece of egg white with a little water into the ground coffee, or add it to the water right after pouring in the ground coffee
5. When it settles, pour it into a coffee pot
6. Many also pour it from the pot in which it was boiled, of course through a strainer
7. It is not good to keep the sediment in the pot and reheat for more than a day or two if you want good coffee
8. The best coffee is achieved by steeping in the French way, but it requires more coffee
9. At ordinary occasions, milk is usually used in coffee, but if we want truly good coffee, we must use cream
10. In Bohemia milk or cream is boiled, here raw is used
11. Sweeten the coffee according to taste

title: Káva Na Jižní Způsob (Coffee Southern Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee beans
- sugar

## Directions

1. In southern regions where much coffee is drunk, never roast the coffee before the moment of preparation.
2. Roast the beans in a mortar, crush them, and cook them with sugar.
3. Do not wait for the coffee to settle; instead, pour it into small cups and drink it with its thick contents.

title: Káva Po Francouzku (Coffee French Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams of roasted coffee beans
- sugar
- milk
- 1/4 liter of boiling water

## Directions

1. For four larger cups, grind or crush sixty grams of roasted coffee beans.
2. Steep the coffee in a coffee machine with approximately 1/4 liter of boiling water.
3. Cover well so that no steam escapes, and let it stand on moderately warm stove for about two hours, without boiling again.
4. Remove from heat, let it settle, and serve with sugar and milk.

title: Káva po Francouzsku (French Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- ground coffee
- boiling water

## Directions

1. This coffee is prepared in a special pot equipped with a cloth filter made of firm thin linen, which should be washed and dried after each use
2. Place ground coffee in the filter, pour the required amount of boiling water over it, let it pass through and pour into cups
3. The filtered coffee can also be left for a while on the edge of the stove, but must not be boiled again
4. To achieve excellent black coffee, pour the filtered liquid once more over the ground coffee in the filter and let it pass through again
5. For this method of preparation, coffee should be ground very finely

title: Káva po Turecku (Turkish Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- black coffee, finely ground
- coffee beans
- sugar
- orange extract

## Directions

1. Place as much regular black coffee as you need in a pot with a handle, for each cup add a teaspoon of finely ground coffee sifted through a fine strainer and a piece of sugar, boil it for two minutes and pour boiling and unfiltered into cups
2. Serve pounded sugar so each person can sweeten to taste
3. You can also add a few drops of orange extract to it

title: Káva po turecku (Turkish Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary black coffee
- finely ground coffee
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put finely ground coffee sifted through a thick sieve into a pot with a handle, using one teaspoon per cup of coffee needed
2. Add a piece of sugar for each cup
3. Boil for two minutes
4. Pour boiling and unfiltered into cups
5. Serve with crushed sugar so each person can sweeten to taste
6. You can also add a few drops of orange extract

title: Kávová chladivka (Coffee Cooler)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilo of freshly roasted coffee
- three liters of boiling water
- one kilo of finely crushed sugar
- cognac

## Directions

1. Grind half kilo of freshly roasted coffee, place it in a machine, and pour over it three liters of boiling water
2. Add one kilo of finely crushed sugar to the prepared coffee, seal everything tightly, place in hot water in a warm place, and stir constantly so the sugar dissolves quickly
3. Strain the finished coffee through cloth, let it cool, pour it into an ice cream maker, and let it freeze similarly
4. Add cognac to it and serve in glasses on the table

title: Kávové Paštičky (Coffee Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee
- egg white
- sugar

## Directions

1. Brew strong coffee and strain it
2. Add egg white and strain everything
3. Add plenty of cooked sugar and boil again until no bubbles form
4. When everything is thick, pour onto paper or slate and let dry
5. The next day, dampen the paper underneath, carefully remove the pastes
6. Place between fine paper in boxes and store in a dry, cold room

title: Kávové pokroutky (Coffee Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- strong coffee
- fine pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Brew strong coffee and strain it through fine cloth
2. Add it to fine pounded sugar
3. Boil it strongly together until everything thickens
4. Everything else is as with all other brittle pieces

title: Kávové Polévání (Coffee Glaze)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 dekagrams (2-3 lots) coffee
- finely crushed, sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Brew 3-4 dekagrams (2-3 lots) coffee in a cup
2. Let cool
3. Mix with finely crushed, sifted sugar until very thick
4. Heat and apply to baked goods

title: Kávový dort (Coffee Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram almonds with skins, crushed
- 14 eggs
- 14 egg yolks
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) not too roasted ground coffee

## Directions

1. Mix 0.5 kilogram crushed almonds with skins, 14 eggs, 14 egg yolks, and 12 dekagrams ground coffee
2. Butter the mold and bake the cake

title: Křen mléčný (Horseradish with Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter beef broth
- three tablespoons flour
- piece of sugar
- finely chopped almonds
- horseradish
- good sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cook half liter beef broth with three tablespoons flour
2. Mix and strain
3. Add a piece of sugar
4. Add a small amount of finely chopped almonds
5. Add a piece of grated horseradish and a little good sweet cream
6. Cook and serve with beef or dumplings

title: Křen polévkový (Horseradish Sauce with Conserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- white flour
- lukewarm beef broth
- grated horseradish
- salt
- clove

## Directions

1. Put a piece of fresh butter in a pot with a little white flour and rub well
2. Pour lukewarm beef broth over it and stir slowly until it boils
3. Add grated horseradish to it, salt and season with clove

title: Křen polévkový s houskou (Horseradish Broth with Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter broth
- salt
- bread roll
- horseradish
- pepper

## Directions

1. Salt half liter of broth
2. Add grated bread roll to it
3. Stir constantly
4. Grate horseradish and add to it
5. Season with pepper

title: Křen s vejci (Horseradish with Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two hard egg yolks
- three tablespoons wine vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Crush two hard egg yolks
2. Mix with three tablespoons wine vinegar and salt

title: Křen se zemáky (Horseradish with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eight to ten starchy potatoes
- same amount of grated horseradish
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil eight to ten starchy potatoes
2. Peel and let cool
3. Press through a sieve
4. Mix with an equal amount of grated horseradish
5. Add oil and vinegar
6. Serve with beef

title: Křen smetanový (White Horseradish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- white flour
- blanched and well-pounded almonds
- powdered sugar
- lukewarm cream
- horseradish
- salt

## Directions

1. Rub a piece of fresh butter with a little white flour in a small pot
2. Add several peeled and well-pounded almonds and half a teaspoon of powdered sugar
3. Pour over it somewhat more than 1 cupful of lukewarm cream
4. Mix it well, place it on the fire and let it cook a little while stirring slowly constantly
5. When it has thickened sufficiently, add grated horseradish and a small pinch of salt
6. Mix well and serve

title: Křen studený (Cold Horseradish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wine vinegar
- sugar
- water
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Add sugar to wine vinegar
2. If the vinegar is too sharp, dilute with water
3. Add grated horseradish

title: Křepelky (Quail)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail
- Onion, sliced
- Pepper
- Allspice berries
- Juniper berries
- Water or stock
- Bread roll, fried and grated
- Salt

## Directions

1. Pluck the quail up to the head, being careful not to tear them
2. Slice the onion and add several peppers, several grains of allspice and several juniper berries
3. Pour in water or stock and braise until completely tender
4. Add some fried grated bread roll to the sauce to make it thicker
5. Arrange the quail on a dish and pour the strained sauce over them

title: Křepelky po myslivečku (Hunter-Style Quails)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quails
- salt
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- mushrooms
- red wine

## Directions

1. Prepare quails as for roasting and season with salt.
2. Place on a baking sheet with butter, onion, parsley, and mushrooms underneath.
3. Baste regularly and turn constantly during slow roasting.
4. When nearly done, pour red wine over them.
5. Continue roasting in this wine for a while longer.

title: Křepelky po vlaéeku (Quails in Parmesan Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams rice flour
- 1/2 liter strong broth
- 70 grams fresh butter
- pepper
- salt
- 2-3 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese
- roasted quails

## Directions

1. Bring the broth to a boil and add rice flour.
2. Add fresh butter, a little pepper, and salt.
3. Gradually bring to a thick paste, stirring constantly.
4. Add 2-3 tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese while cooking.
5. Arrange the roasted quails on a platter.
6. Pour the remaining sauce over the quails.

title: Křepelky s rýží (Quail with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail
- Rice
- Butter
- Parsley
- Onion
- Flour

## Directions

1. Prepare the quail for roasting or braising
2. Rinse the rice in several waters and drain
3. Add a piece of butter and flour to the rice and braise until tender
4. Meanwhile, braise the quail in butter with parsley and onion
5. Strain the liquid from the quail into the rice
6. Place the quail in the rice and let them rest together briefly on the stove

title: Křepelky v bílé omáčce (Quail in White Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail
- Onion, chopped
- Carrot root
- Butter (15 grams per quail)
- Flour
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the quail for roasting
2. Cut them into pieces or leave them whole and place in a pot with chopped onion, carrot root, butter and a little flour and salt
3. Braise until they turn reddish
4. Make a light roux, strain it into the sauce from which the quail have been removed
5. Simmer briefly, return the quail to the sauce
6. Add a piece of butter and adjust seasoning as desired

title: Křepelky v bílé omáčce (Quails in White Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quails
- onion
- carrot pieces
- butter (15 grams per quail)
- broth
- salt
- lemon (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare quails as for roasting; cut them into pieces or leave whole.
2. Place quails in a pot with chopped onion, carrot pieces, and butter (15 grams per quail).
3. Add a little broth and salt.
4. Braise until they turn red.
5. Make a pale roux and strain it into the sauce from which the quails were removed.
6. Boil briefly, add the quails back to the sauce.
7. Add a piece of butter and optionally squeeze lemon juice into the sauce.

title: Kůzlí kotlety (Kid Chops)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kid meat
- butter
- onion
- flour
- milk
- egg yolks
- egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Carefully cut the kid meat into chops, pound them, pepper and salt.
2. Braise them in butter with finely chopped onion, dust with flour, and add milk.
3. Add two or three beaten egg yolks and continue cooking on the stove, stirring frequently, until completely thickened.
4. Coat the chops thickly with this mixture.
5. Dip them in beaten egg, coat in breadcrumbs, and fry in butter until golden brown.

title: Kůzlí ragout (Kid Ragout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kid meat from loin, neck, breast, liver and kidneys
- butter
- onion
- spices
- flour
- meat broth
- sour cream
- paprika

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small pieces, salt it, and braise in butter with onion and spices until completely tender.
2. Dust with flour and add meat broth and good sour cream.
3. Sprinkle with paprika and continue braising, stirring frequently, until tender.
4. Carefully remove the cooked meat from the fat, which is allowed to settle, and serve with just the thick sauce poured over.

title: Laciný dort (Plain Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one full spoonful of butter
- one coffee cup of sugar
- two whole eggs
- two and a half coffee cups of flour
- one coffee cup of milk
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- vanilla or lemon to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the butter well with the sugar
2. add the two eggs and mix well
3. add the flour, milk, baking powder, and vanilla or lemon to taste
4. bake in a pan or on parchment plates
5. make a layered cake and fill with some fruit syrup

title: Lanýže po Vlašsku (Truffles in the Italian Manner)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and peeled truffles
- olive oil
- parsley, chopped
- thyme
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Slice the cleaned and peeled truffles into thin slices
2. Pour olive oil over them
3. Layer with chopped parsley, thyme, pepper, and salt
4. Braise slowly on warm stove
5. Add lemon juice
6. Serve to table

title: Lanýžová Nádivka (Truffle and Liver Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled truffles
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- cooked poultry livers

## Directions

1. Cut peeled truffles into fine pieces
2. Add finely chopped bacon
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Mix in finely beaten cooked poultry livers

title: Lanýžová Nádivka (Truffle Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- onion
- parsley
- mushrooms
- bacon
- salt
- pastry spice
- broth
- wine

## Directions

1. Wash the truffles, peel them, and chop them
2. Chop with onion, parsley, and mushrooms
3. Mix in bacon and salt
4. Add pastry spice
5. Bind with good broth and wine
6. Braise until all liquid evaporates
7. Cool before using for pheasants, capons, and similar birds

title: Lanýžová omáčka (Truffle Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- onion
- parsley
- fresh butter
- wine
- good gravy
- strong meat broth

## Directions

1. Clean and peel truffles
2. Slice them into thin rounds
3. Add finely chopped onion and chopped parsley
4. Add fresh butter and a little wine
5. Braise until truffles are completely soft
6. Add a little good gravy and strong meat broth
7. Boil thoroughly once more
8. Strain and serve

title: Lanýžový Salát (Truffle Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles, cleaned and rinsed
- salt
- pepper
- good vinegar or lemon juice
- good Provençal oil

## Directions

1. Peel cleaned and rinsed truffles
2. Cut into very fine slices
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Dress with good vinegar or lemon juice
5. Drizzle with good Provençal oil
6. Mix everything thoroughly again and arrange nicely on a dish

title: Lačené Okurky (Dill Summer Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers of medium size
- green dill
- grape leaves or cherry leaves
- water
- salt
- sugar
- rye bread crusts, dried

## Directions

1. Select cucumbers of medium size.
2. Prepare a stone pot.
3. Line the bottom of the pot with green dill and grape leaves (or cherry leaves if grape leaves are unavailable).
4. Arrange a layer of cucumbers in the pot, then add dill and leaves again, and continue layering until the pot is full.
5. Boil water and salt it until it tastes as salty as a well-salted soup.
6. Add a small amount of sugar, about one spoon per four-gallon pot.
7. Let the water cool and pour it lukewarm over the cucumbers.
8. Before weighting down with a stone, add two or three dried rye bread crusts to the cucumbers.
9. Let it sit for one or two days in a warm place until it ferments.
10. Then move it to a cool place such as a cellar.
11. By the fifth or sixth day the cucumbers will be properly aged and over the following 14 days they will be most delicious, though after that they become increasingly sour.

title: Ledvinková Pečené (Roast Fillet of Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 pound piece of veal with kidney
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- water
- grated bread roll or bread

## Directions

1. Take a 3 to 4 pound piece of veal with kidney, clean it thoroughly, salt it
2. Pepper both sides, place on a baking pan and top with butter
3. Add water and place in the oven
4. When browned on the top side, turn it over and baste with butter 4 to 5 times during roasting
5. Half an hour before serving, sprinkle the surface with grated bread roll or bread
6. If the juices have dried out, add more hot water and baste the top with pan juices before serving to help the bread crust adhere
7. When the bread coating is browned, place on a platter, pour pan juices over it and serve

title: Ledvinky (Veal Kidneys)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- flour
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Remove the fat from the veal kidneys, cut them into slices, and put them in hot butter.
2. Salt, pepper, and add chopped parsley.
3. Stir over high heat, dust with flour, add good beef broth, and stir until the sauce thickens.

title: Ledviny hovězí (Beef Kidneys)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef kidney
- Water
- Onion
- 20 grams butter
- Caraway
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the kidney into pieces and rinse it
2. Place in a pot without water and braise until the liquid from the kidney evaporates, then drain
3. Rinse the kidney again
4. Chop the onion and add 20 grams of butter, caraway and pepper
5. Braise until the kidney is tender

title: Lehounký dort (Light Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 eggs
- sufficient finely crushed sugar
- grated lemon zest
- finest flour
- butter for greasing mold
- paper for the mold edge

## Directions

1. Take 20 eggs, mix well and add sufficient finely crushed sugar
2. Heat the mixture slightly, let it cool, and repeat this process several times
3. Mix the grated lemon zest with the finest flour and add to everything
4. Mix everything again
5. Fill a buttered cake mold with the prepared dough
6. Since this cake tends to overflow from the mold and rises greatly, place paper very high on the rim of the mold to prevent the dough from overflowing

title: Lemon Glaze (Polévání z citronu)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon zest
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Rub the lemon zest against sugar
2. Crush the sugar
3. Mix with lemon juice
4. Warm before use
5. Coat the pastry and place on a warm spot for a short time

title: Lemon Ice Cream (Citrónová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Press lemons as desired
2. Strain the juice
3. Sweeten it
4. Dilute with water
5. Prepare ice cream from it

title: Lemon Ice Cream with Cream (Citrónová zmrzlina se smetanou)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- More than a liter of sour cream
- About three-quarters of a kilo of crushed sugar
- Zest of two lemons

## Directions

1. Beat the four eggs
2. Add more than a liter of sour cream
3. Add about three-quarters of a kilo of crushed sugar
4. Add grated zest from two lemons
5. Beat everything, bring to a boil
6. Proceed as described for simple ice cream

title: Lemon or Orange Candies (Alternative) (Cukrovinky citronové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8-10 egg whites
- juice from one lemon or orange
- 8-10 tablespoons ground sugar

## Directions

1. Make stiff snow from 8-10 egg whites
2. Beat in lemon or orange juice
3. Add 8-10 tablespoons ground sugar equal to number of whites used
4. Mix well
5. Clean and wax baking sheet
6. Place small pieces on sheet
7. Bake

title: Lemon Shavings (Hoblovačky citrónové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- finely ground sugar
- lemon juice
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a thick icing from finely ground sugar, lemon juice, and egg whites
2. Spread onto wafers cut into oblong strips
3. Bake slowly
4. Cool on a sieve

title: Lesklé Polévání (Shiny Glaze)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- finely crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Dust butter dough with finely crushed sugar
2. Bake in oven until sugar dissolves

title: Leštěné piškoty (Glazed Sponge Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 eggs
- beaten sugar
- 1500 grams finest flour
- glaze

## Directions

1. Beat 16 eggs in a bowl
2. Add beaten sugar
3. Heat everything several times and let cool again, stirring constantly throughout
4. Slowly mix in 1500 grams finest flour until dough forms
5. Place cookies on paper or wafers
6. Brush with glaze while baking, which gives them special shine and firmness

title: Likér Skořicový (Cinnamon Liqueur)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams cinnamon
- 1 liter pure water
- 750 grams sugar
- 3-4.5 liters good, fine spirits (kořalka)

## Directions

1. Crush 125 grams of cinnamon coarsely and boil it in 1 liter of pure water for about half an hour
2. Add 750 grams of sugar and let it settle
3. Decant the clear liquid and when it has cooled completely, add 3-4.5 liters of good, fine spirits
4. Seal everything well and place on warm stove for several days where excessive heat will not harm it
5. Strain everything into a bottle

title: Likérové pokroutky (Liqueur Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine pounded sugar
- any liqueur

## Directions

1. Pour any liqueur into fine pounded sugar
2. Boil it together and prepare everything else as with other brittle pieces

title: Likérový a rumový creme (Liqueur and Rum Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- four deciliters cream
- six egg yolks
- ten deka sugar
- three spoons of liqueur
- two spoons of rum or arrack

## Directions

1. Take four deciliters of cream, six egg yolks, ten deka sugar and mix everything
2. Add three spoons of liqueur and two spoons of rum or arrack

title: Limburský sýr (Limburg Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- warm morning milk
- warm evening collected milk

## Directions

1. This cheese is prepared from warm morning milk, and when it has been heated, the heated evening collected milk is added.

title: Limonáda (Lemonade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deci vody
- štáva ze tří citronů
- cukr

## Directions

1. Do 14 deci vody se vytlačí štáva z tří citronů
2. Přidá se dle potřeby cukru
3. Lze ji postavit k ledu
4. Pokud se voda dříve s cukrem svaří, vydrží limonáda na chladném místě po delší dobu

title: Limonáda (Lemonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- lemons
- sugar

## Directions

1. To a quart of water, squeeze the juice from three lemons and add six tablespoons of sugar
2. Let it stand covered for a while, then stir and pour into glasses
3. If the water is not cold enough, it can be cooled with ice
4. For the sick, add more water
5. For hot lemonade, use hot water instead of cold
6. Orange lemonade (orangeade) is prepared the same way

title: Limonáda fialková (Violet Lemonade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- půl litru čerstvé vody
- štáva fialková
- štáva z jednoho citronu
- cukr

## Directions

1. Smíchá se půl litru čerstvé vody s libovolným množstvím štávy fialkové
2. Přidá se štáva z jednoho citronu
3. Vše se dokonale smíchá
4. Dle chuti se přidá cukr

title: Limonáda jablková (Apple Lemonade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- šest jablek
- litr vařící vody
- 50 gramů medu
- štáva ze dvou citronů

## Directions

1. Vezme se šest jablek
2. Rozkrájí se v slupkách na kusy
3. Naleje se na ně litr vařící vody
4. Přidá se 50 gramů medu
5. Přidá se štáva ze dvou citronů
6. Vyhřívá se na teplých kamnech asi hodinu

title: Limonáda s citronem (Lemonade with Lemon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cukr
- citrony
- citrónová kůra
- voda
- víno

## Directions

1. Kůra se tence oloupá
2. S cukrem a vodou se svaří
3. Nechá se vychladnout
4. Přidá se citrónová šťáva
5. Sklenice vína se přileje

title: Limonáda s vínem (Claret Cup)
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon juice
- sugar
- water
- red wine
- raspberries or strawberries or fresh pineapple (optional)
- soda water (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare lemonade using half water and half red wine instead of all water
2. Instead of water, you can use soda water
3. Before pouring the lemonade into glasses, you can add a few raspberries, strawberries, or a piece of fresh pineapple

title: Linecký Dort (Linz Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10.5 deciliters (3 tablespoons) flour
- 42 dekagrams (28 lots) butter
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) almonds
- 18 dekagrams sugar
- 8 cooked egg yolks
- 2 raw egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix flour, butter, almonds, sugar, 8 cooked egg yolks, and 2 raw egg yolks into a dough
2. Make 2 cakes from the dough
3. Coat one with jam, cover with the other
4. Bake in a form as a cake and glaze

title: Lineský koláč (Linz Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram flour
- 6 hard yolks, grated
- 1/2 kilogram sugar rubbed with lemon
- 1/2 kilogram sugar crumbled
- 3-4 eggs
- preserved fruit for filling

## Directions

1. Sift flour on a board, grate in hard yolks
2. Add grated sugar rubbed with lemon and crumbled sugar
3. Mix in eggs and knead dough thoroughly in a cool place
4. Add flour if too thin
5. Roll out dough, set aside a piece for border
6. Bake on ungreased sheet in oven quickly
7. When baked and cooled, brush with fine preserved fruit
8. Or make latticed decoration from remaining dough and bake

title: Liqueur Ice Cream (Likérová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of sour cream
- Quarter kilo of crushed sugar
- Liqueur of choice

## Directions

1. Pour into one liter of sour cream a quarter kilo of crushed sugar
2. Add liqueur of choice, pouring enough to taste it in the ice cream
3. Beat everything into foam
4. Proceed as with other ice creams

title: Litá štrudle s tvarohem (French Pancake Roll with Cottage Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons flour
- 5 whole eggs
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- salt
- milk
- 1 pound cottage cheese
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 6 tablespoons sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- raisins
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix 8 tablespoons flour in a little milk
2. Add to it five whole eggs
3. 1 tablespoon sugar
4. Grated zest of one lemon
5. Salt slightly
6. Mix well
7. Add enough milk to make a batter like for pancakes
8. Coat a hot omelet pan or sheet with clarified butter
9. Pour the batter thinly
10. Bake on both sides and place on a clean cloth
11. Rub 1 pound cottage cheese with 1 tablespoon butter on a plate
12. Add to it five egg yolks
13. 6 tablespoons sugar
14. Grated zest of half a lemon
15. A handful of picked and rinsed raisins
16. Mix well
17. Coat the pancakes with this
18. Roll them up
19. Place them in a pan greased with butter
20. Sprinkle with sugar
21. Pour a little boiled milk
22. Let bake slowly in an oven at mild heat
23. Then remove piece by piece with a metal spatula and arrange on a platter
24. Sprinkle generously with sugar and serve

title: Litá štrudle s višněmi (French Pancake Cherry Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts cherries
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- zest of lemon
- milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. From 2 quarts of cherries remove the pits
2. Place cherries in a small pan
3. Add four tablespoons sugar
4. A piece of lemon zest chopped fine
5. Let it cook until the cherries are sufficiently thick
6. Place them on the pancakes prepared as before
7. Roll them up
8. Place in a small pan greased with butter
9. Sprinkle with sugar
10. Pour a little milk
11. Bake in the oven until golden
12. Then remove with a metal spatula and arrange on a platter
13. Sprinkle generously with sugar and serve warm

title: Litá štrudle s čokoládou (French Pancake Chocolate Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound chocolate
- 6 tablespoons white flour
- sugar
- 1 quart milk
- butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate quarter pound chocolate on a grater
2. Place in a small pan
3. Mix 6 tablespoons white flour and the same amount of sugar
4. Pour 1 quart milk into it
5. Mix well and cook on mild heat into a thick porridge
6. You must stir constantly while cooking
7. Pour the porridge into a platter
8. Let it cool
9. Add 2 tablespoons fresh butter
10. Eight egg yolks
11. Rub well
12. Then slowly mix in stiff snow from eight egg whites
13. Coat the pancakes prepared as before with this filling
14. Roll them up
15. Place one next to the other in a pan greased with butter
16. Sprinkle with ground sugar
17. Pour a little milk
18. Let bake slowly in the oven

title: Liver in Aspic (Játra v aspiku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large liver
- truffles
- bacon
- goose liver puree
- woodcock or wild duck puree
- game meat
- meat aspic

## Directions

1. Lard large liver with truffles, braise in pieces of bacon until tender, let cool and cut into pieces. Place in a small mold lined with truffles in layers, each layer covered with meat aspic. Or the liver can be braised until tender, mashed and mixed with puree of woodcock or wild duck and finely chopped roasted game meat, lined with truffles in molds with meat aspic layered on top, and chilled on ice.

title: Losos na holandský způsob (Salmon Dutch Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- salt
- butter
- sour cream
- lemon juice
- egg yolks, raw
- parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Wash the salmon and pour boiling water over it, leaving for half an hour
2. Scrape off scales, wash, and salt
3. Place on a baking sheet, add a piece of fresh butter and sour cream
4. Place in the oven and baste continuously with sour cream
5. When soft, transfer to a platter
6. Add juice from one lemon to the sauce and whisk in four raw egg yolks, then strain over the salmon
7. Sprinkle finely chopped fresh parsley on top
8. This method may also be used for catfish

title: Losos na modro (Salmon Prepared Blue)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- celery, sliced
- parsley
- carrot
- lemon peel
- pepper
- ginger
- allspice
- thyme
- bay leaf
- parsley, green

## Directions

1. Scale the salmon
2. Boil vinegar with water and pour over the salted salmon to turn it evenly blue
3. Place sliced celery, parsley, carrot, lemon peel, pepper, ginger, allspice, thyme, and bay leaf on a special baking sheet
4. Place the salmon on this bed, pour vinegar over it
5. Cook evenly on all sides
6. Transfer to a platter, surround with green parsley, the vegetables that were cooked with it, and lemon
7. Serve cold

title: Losos pečený (Baked Salmon with Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- fresh butter
- shallots
- capers
- sardines
- parsley root
- lemon juice
- pepper
- salt
- beef broth
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a nice piece of salmon into pieces, place several tablespoons of fresh heated butter in a pan
2. Add several finely chopped shallots, capers, four cleaned and finely chopped sardines, a piece of parsley root, lemon juice, season with ground pepper and salt
