3. Place the cut salmon in this and let it lie for twenty-four hours, turning it several times
4. Half an hour before serving, remove the salmon from the sauce and place on a well-buttered baking sheet
5. Add pieces of butter, place in the oven and bake at high heat for twenty minutes, basting frequently with the marinade sauce
6. Make a light butter roux, pour in a little beef broth and the remaining sauce in which the salmon was marinated, let it simmer a while
7. Arrange the baked salmon on a dish and surround with fried parsley, pour the sauce into the pan with the rest, let it boil for a minute and strain through a sieve into a small bowl

title: Losos s citrónovou omáčkou (Salmon with Lemon Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- bay leaf
- parsley
- vegetable roots, finely chopped
- white wine
- lemon, sliced
- cloves
- sugar
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Scale the salmon, remove bones, wash, and cut into pieces two fingers thick
2. Soak in water for half an hour, dry, salt, and sprinkle with vinegar for half an hour
3. Boil halfway in salted water
4. Place on a baking sheet lined with bay leaf, parsley, and finely chopped vegetable roots
5. Pour white wine over it, arrange lemon slices on top
6. Season with salt and add cloves and sugar
7. Cook until the salmon is tender
8. Strain the sauce, whisk in several egg yolks, and pour over the salmon

title: Losos s huspeninou (Salmon with Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- vinegar
- white wine
- lemon
- bay leaves
- onions
- vegetable spices
- salmon
- veal feet
- beef broth
- egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil salted water with vinegar, white wine, lemon sliced into rounds with centers removed, bay leaves, onions, and all vegetable spices
2. Add salmon pieces and boil for half an hour
3. Remove salmon from the broth
4. Add two to three cleaned veal feet and some beef broth to the cooking liquid, simmer until meat falls from the bones
5. Allow to cool, then next day remove fat and sediment
6. Add a little white wine and clarify with snow made from four eggs
7. Butter a mold and pour a finger's thickness of aspic into it, cool on ice
8. Decorate the bottom with cut lemon leaves, pour in a thin layer of warm aspic
9. Layer sliced salmon on top and alternate with aspic until the mold is nearly full
10. When the aspic is completely set, dip the mold in warm water and turn out onto a platter
11. Garnish the edge with parsley and boiled crayfish

title: Losos se smaženou houskou (Salmon with Fried Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- hard bread rolls, sliced
- milk
- salt
- egg
- smoked salmon
- grated bread
- butter
- sardine butter
- parsley, fried

## Directions

1. Slice hard rolls, soak in milk, salt, and drain
2. Dip in beaten egg
3. Slice the smoked salmon and place on bread slices
4. Top with another bread slice, coat with grated bread
5. Fry in butter
6. Brush with sardine butter and arrange on platter
7. Garnish with fried parsley

title: Loupáček Višňový (Cherry Puff)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cream
- good milk
- eighth of a kilogram fine flour
- a little milk
- ground sweet almonds
- fresh butter
- cinnamon
- sugar
- egg yolks
- fresh cherries

## Directions

1. Boil sour cream with good milk
2. Mix an eighth of a kilogram fine flour with a little milk
3. When the cream begins to boil, add the flour mixture to it, make a dough and pour into a form
4. Mix ground sweet almonds, fresh butter, cinnamon, and plenty of sugar, beat continuously while adding several egg yolks
5. Spread this mixture on the cake about a finger's thickness thick, arrange pitted cherries in it
6. Bake well

title: Loupáčkový Dort s Jahod (Pastry Cake with Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- layered pastry dough
- finely ground rusk
- cooked strawberries or plum preserves

## Directions

1. Make a layered pastry cake
2. Sprinkle with finely ground rusk
3. Coat the top with cooked strawberries or plum preserves
4. Decorate with pastry strips
5. Bake slowly

title: Loupáčkový Dort s Meruňkami (Pastry Cake with Apricots)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- layered pastry dough
- apricots
- sugar
- white paper
- apricot kernels

## Directions

1. Prepare a layered pastry cake
2. Put apricots into boiling water, remove the pits, and peel them
3. Place the peeled apricots on the cake
4. Sprinkle generously with finely ground sugar
5. Cover with white paper as large as the cake so the apricots do not burn
6. Place the cake in the oven and bake carefully
7. Peel the kernels, split them, and when the cake is baked, place them on top

title: Loupáčkový Dort s Višněmi (Pastry Cake with Sour Cherries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- layered pastry dough
- egg yolk
- pitted sour cherries or sweet cherries
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Roll layered dough into thin sheets
2. Cut into any desired size and place on a sheet brushed with egg yolk
3. Cut small noodles from the rolled dough and border the cake with them
4. Stuff pitted cherries between the openings thus formed
5. Sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon
6. When baked, cut into oblong pieces
7. During baking, brush the noodles that form the strips on the cake with beaten egg yolk

title: Loupáčky (Croissant Rolls)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram butter
- 5 1/2 kilogram flour
- several eggs
- piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Work the butter in water to remove buttermilk
2. Mix with flour, eggs, and sugar until the dough does not stick to the rolling pin
3. Let rest in a cold place for about half an hour
4. Shape into croissants (loupáčky)
5. Brush with beaten egg, milk, or butter
6. Sprinkle with sugar, poppy seeds, or chopped almonds
7. Bake slowly until golden

title: Lupiče s ústřicemi (Bass with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- bass
- salt
- butter
- oyster sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the bass well, gut it, cut it in half along the back, skin it, remove the bones and cut the meat into small thin pieces, salt it and let it stew slowly in hot butter.
2. Then drain the butter, arrange the fish nicely on a dish and pour oyster sauce over it.

title: Lístkový dort (Milfoil Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- beaten egg
- preserved fruit preserves
- white glaze
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Roll out puff pastry to the thickness of a finger
2. Cut five or six round layers using an inverted plate, each one slightly smaller than the last
3. Place them on papers
4. Brush with beaten egg and bake
5. When cooled, spread all layers except the smallest with preserves
6. Stack them on top of each other, starting with the largest on the bottom
7. Place the smallest layer on top
8. Cover the cake with white glaze
9. Sprinkle with chopped almonds

title: Lívance (Fried Yeast Pancakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 eggs
- 5 tablespoons diluted yeast
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- salt
- piece of lemon peel
- 4 dekagrams 5 grams (3 lots) sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix together milk, egg yolks, eggs, diluted yeast, butter, salt, lemon peel, and sugar well.
2. Add enough flour to make a batter like pancake batter and allow to ferment.
3. Place a piece of butter in each indentation of a special lívance pan, then drop a spoonful of batter and fry until golden on both sides.
4. Brush cooked lívance with thinned plum jam or sprinkle with gingerbread, cheese curds or poppy seeds; they may also be sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Lívance (Wheat Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- dva koflíky kyselého mléka
- dva žloutky
- trochu soli
- asi koflík mouky prosáté
- dva bílky

## Directions

1. Vezmi dva koflíky kyselého mléka, dej do něho dva žloutky a dobře rozmíchej.
2. Přidej trochu soli a asi koflík mouky prosáté a opět dobře zamíchej.
3. Je-li těsto příliš řídké (řidší než na kapánku), přidej ještě mouky a dobře to vytluc na hladké těsto.
4. Udělej z těch dvou bílků tuhý sníh a přidej jej k těstu.

title: Lívance (Wheat Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups sour milk
- two egg yolks
- salt
- about one cup sifted flour
- two egg whites
- small level teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in a little hot water

## Directions

1. Take two cups of sour milk and add two egg yolks and mix well
2. Add a little salt and about one cup of sifted flour and mix well again
3. If the batter is too thin, thinner than for a batter, add more flour and beat well to a smooth batter
4. Beat the two egg whites into stiff snow and add it to the batter with a small level teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in a little hot water, and mix gently
5. Heat a griddle, grease it with lard and bake the pancakes, taking a spoon of batter for each pancake and when cooked on one side, turn it
6. Serve with syrup or honey

title: Lívance bramborové (Potato Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- six large raw potatoes
- six tablespoons sweet cream
- two eggs
- four tablespoons flour
- a little salt
- butter for frying
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Take six large raw potatoes and grate them, then press through a clean cloth
2. Add six tablespoons of sweet cream, two eggs, four tablespoons of flour and a little salt, and mix it all together
3. Then put one tablespoon of butter on a heated pan and put the pancakes on the pan with a spoon, turn them and fry them until red, then put on a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Lívance kynuté (Raised Griddle Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups good milk
- three whole eggs
- one teaspoon ground sugar
- a piece of melted butter
- two or three tablespoons of leaven from dry yeast, or half a piece of pressed yeast
- salt
- flour
- melted butter for serving
- plum jam or cottage cheese
- ground cinnamon and sugar

## Directions

1. Beat three whole eggs into two cups of milk, add one teaspoon of ground sugar and a piece of melted butter
2. Add two or three tablespoons of leaven from dry yeast, or half a piece of pressed yeast, salt and mix well
3. Then add enough flour to make a thick batter and let it rise
4. Grease the griddle with melted butter, take batter with a scoop, pour the pancakes on it and bake on both sides until golden
5. Spread each pancake with melted butter, brush with plum jam, sprinkle with grated cottage cheese or with ground and sugar-mixed cinnamon and butter, and sprinkle with melted butter
6. You can also sprinkle them with ground cinnamon and sugar

title: Lívance na jiný způsob (Pancakes Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- two small cups sweet milk
- one teaspoon melted butter
- one cup flour
- one heaping teaspoon baking powder
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Put six egg yolks in a bowl and mix two small cups of sweet milk with them
2. Add one teaspoon of melted butter
3. Mix one heaping teaspoon of baking powder into one cup of flour, sift into the milk and mix nicely
4. Beat the six egg whites into snow and gradually mix into the batter
5. Quickly bake small pancakes on a lard-greased pan

title: Lívance z mouky připravené (Griddle Cakes from Prepared Flour)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups sweet milk
- about one cup self-raising flour

## Directions

1. Take two cups of sweet milk and mix about one cup of flour into it to make a completely thin batter
2. If you do not have milk on hand, add two cups of cold water into which you mix a beaten egg
3. No other preparation or salt is needed
4. Bake as usual

title: Lívance z podmáslí (Buttermilk Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one quart buttermilk
- small teaspoon baking soda dissolved in hot water
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs and add one quart of buttermilk to them
2. Add a small teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in hot water and a little salt
3. Add enough flour to make it thick, about like a batter
4. Fry on lard and serve with honey or syrup on the table

title: Majonéza (Mayonnaise Dressing)
body:
## Ingredients

- two egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- one cup olive oil or fresh melted butter
- two tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Beat two egg yolks
2. Add a pinch of salt and pepper to them
3. While beating constantly, gradually add one cup of olive oil or fresh melted butter
4. When all the oil or butter is beaten with the yolks, gradually add two tablespoons of vinegar while continuing to beat constantly
5. Do not add the vinegar earlier or it will curdle
6. If the sauce curdles, beat an egg yolk with salt and pepper in a separate bowl and gradually add the curdled mayonnaise in small amounts while beating constantly, continuing until you have a nice smooth sauce without shine
7. If the sauce becomes shiny, add a few drops of vinegar

title: Makaronky (Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound macaroni
- two tablespoons fresh butter
- salt
- Swiss cheese, grated
- new butter
- ham, sliced thin (optional)
- cream
- four egg yolks
- cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Break the pound of macaroni into pieces about two inches long
2. Boil in salted water, add two tablespoons fresh butter, salt lightly, and cook about half an hour
3. Drain the macaroni, grease a dish with butter, place a layer of cooked macaroni in it
4. Sprinkle with grated Swiss cheese and place pieces of fresh butter on top
5. Continue layering macaroni, cheese, and butter until all is used, ending with a layer of macaroni topped with pieces of butter
6. Let stand in a hot oven for about three quarters of an hour without baking
7. Remove, sprinkle generously with grated cheese and bring to the table
8. Optional: add thinly sliced ham to the layers
9. For baked macaroni: prepare as above in a baking dish, beat four egg yolks with cream in a bowl, pour over the macaroni, bake until golden in the oven, and sprinkle with cheese

title: Makaronky (Macaroons)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound blanched and peeled almonds
- four egg whites
- two small cups fine sugar
- zest of one lemon
- one more egg white

## Directions

1. Dry half a pound of blanched and peeled almonds in an oven, then pound them in a mortar with four egg whites.
2. Place in a bowl and add two small cups of fine sugar, grated zest of one lemon, and one more egg white.
3. Mix well and beat thoroughly.
4. Using a pastry bag or simply a teaspoon, form mounds about the size of a quarter on paper placed on a baking sheet, spacing them equally.
5. Bake at gentle heat until pale yellow.
6. The macaroons must rise during baking, the surface must crack, the inside must be white, and the bottom pale brown.
7. Instead of almonds, you can also use hazelnut kernels.

title: Maková buchta (Crown Cake with Poppy-seed)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup butter
- 8 yolks
- two pieces of yeast
- two tablespoons milk
- eight tablespoons sour cream
- half small cup finely chopped almonds
- zest of one lemon, chopped
- whites of eggs beaten to stiff snow
- 3 small cups flour
- 2 small cups ground poppy seed
- half small cup sugar
- salt
- butter for greasing
- almonds halved
- grated roll

## Directions

1. Put into a bowl 1 small cup butter, 8 yolks and rub well together
2. Dissolve two pieces of yeast with two tablespoons milk and eight tablespoons sour cream, put into the bowl
3. Add half small cup finely chopped almonds, zest of one lemon chopped and mix everything
4. Add stiff snow beaten from egg whites
5. Add 3 small cups flour, rub well into smooth batter
6. Add 2 small cups ground poppy seed, half small cup sugar and a little salt, mix everything well
7. Grease form with butter, line with almonds halved, sprinkle with grated roll
8. Put the batter into it, let it rise and bake

title: Maková nádivka (Poppy Seed Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 deciliters (žejdlík) poppy seeds
- 7 deciliters (2 žejdlíky) milk
- sugar equal to the milk amount
- 2 egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Pass the poppy seeds through a sieve, crush them, and place on the stove
2. Add milk and sugar in the proportions stated
3. Stir for a long time until the filling is sufficiently thick
4. Add egg yolks if desired

title: Maková nádivka - Fifth Method (Poppy Seed Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dek sugar
- 1 deciliter water
- crushed or ground poppy seeds
- vanilla
- cinnamon
- cloves
- lemon zest
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in water
2. Add crushed or ground poppy seeds until it thickens completely
3. Season with vanilla, cinnamon, and cloves
4. Add chopped lemon zest
5. Add abundant sour cream
6. Mix until a porridge forms

title: Maková nádivka - Fourth Method (Poppy Seed Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters poppy seeds
- warm sour cream
- sugar or 2 deciliters honey
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Thoroughly crush the poppy seeds or grind in a good coffee mill
2. Mix with warm sour cream into a thick dough
3. Sweeten with sugar or honey
4. Season with cinnamon, lemon zest, and vanilla

title: Maková nádivka - Second Method (Poppy Seed Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 deciliters honey
- crushed poppy seeds
- fragrant spices
- wine

## Directions

1. Heat and dissolve the honey
2. Add the crushed poppy seeds and fragrant spices to it
3. Mix with wine

title: Maková nádivka - Third Method (Poppy Seed Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dek sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 14 dek finely ground or crushed poppy seeds
- 5 dek melted butter
- 3 tablespoons sour cream
- 7 dek raisins
- a pinch of cinnamon
- vanilla
- snow from 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar with egg yolks
2. Add the finely ground poppy seeds, melted butter, and sour cream
3. Add raisins, cinnamon, and vanilla
4. Fold in the snow from beaten egg whites

title: Makový Pudding (Poppyseed Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons poppyseeds
- two tablespoons sugar
- two tablespoons ground almonds
- finely chopped zest of one lemon
- seven crushed cloves
- five egg yolks
- five egg whites
- butter
- grated rolls
- chocolate sauce

## Directions

1. Grind or pound four tablespoons of poppyseeds finely, place in a bowl, and add two tablespoons sugar, two tablespoons ground almonds, finely chopped zest of one lemon, and seven crushed cloves.
2. Stir for a quarter hour, then add snow made from five egg whites.
3. Place the mixture in a mold greased with butter and sprinkled with grated rolls.
4. Let it steam cook for one hour.
5. Then remove it onto a dish and pour chocolate sauce over it.

title: Makrela vařená (Boiled Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mackerel
- salt
- water
- butter
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Soak the mackerel as directed previously
2. Drain the water, pour hot water over it and let it soak on a hot spot for about five minutes
3. Drain the water again and place the fish on a heated oval dish
4. Place pieces of fresh butter around it and surround with small peeled potatoes boiled in salted water

title: Makrelka pečená (Baked Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mackerel
- salt
- water
- butter
- sweet cream
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the mackerel overnight in three quarts of water if preparing for breakfast
2. The next morning, drain the water and pour boiling water over the fish, let it sit for one or two minutes without boiling
3. Drain again and place the fish on a well-buttered baking sheet or pot, skin side up
4. Pour half a cup of sweet cream over it and season with pepper to taste
5. Place in the oven and let it brown
6. If preparing for lunch, soak the mackerel in the morning and let it soak for 6 hours

title: Malagová chladivka (Malaga Cooler)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- juice
- finely chopped peel of two lemons
- pinch of cinnamon
- one liter of sugar syrup at 18°
- one bottle of malaga
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Place on a dish juice and finely chopped peel from two lemons, a pinch of cinnamon, and one liter of sugar syrup at 18°
2. Let everything stand for three to four hours in a room, then strain the liquid and let it freeze
3. Before pouring, add one bottle of malaga and four egg whites to the liquid, work everything for at least ten minutes quite rapidly so the egg whites do not freeze but turn into snow, then pour immediately into glasses
4. Instead of malaga, any other good and preferred wine can be used

title: Malinová limonáda (Raspberry Lemonade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- čerstvé maliny
- litr štávy malinové
- dva litry vody
- štáva citrónová
- 24 dekagramy cukru

## Directions

1. Čerstvé maliny se vytlačí
2. Litr štávy se smíchá se dvěma litry vody
3. Přidá se štáva citrónová
4. Přidá se 24 dekagramy utlučeného cukru
5. Procedí se do sklenic
6. Není-li čerstvých malin, může se použít i zavařená štáva
7. Aby se chut zlepšila, může se přidat štáva citrónová nebo ryvízová

title: Malinová svařenina (Raspberry Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar (half cup sugar per cup of raspberries)

## Directions

1. Carefully pick over the raspberries and pick them clean, put them in a pot, set on fire and crush them in the pot.
2. When the mixture has come to a boil several times, add sugar—half cup of sugar per cup of raspberries.
3. Cook for half to three-quarters of an hour.

title: Malinová voda (Raspberry Water)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- čerstvé maliny
- voda
- citrónová štáva
- cukr
- malinový ocet

## Directions

1. Jsou-li čerstvé maliny, vylisují se
2. Co štávy, tolik se přilije vody
3. Přidá se citrónová štáva
4. Vše se osladí a procedí
5. Nejsou-li čerstvé maliny, vezme se stará malinová štáva a vodou se rozředí
6. Malinový ocet lze také k úpravě nápoje užít

title: Malinové cukrovinky (Raspberry Confections)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram peeled almonds
- egg white
- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- 1/4 kilogram raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Grind the peeled almonds with egg white very finely
2. Cook the sugar until it reaches the sixth degree, then add the finely ground almonds to it
3. Press raspberries through a fine sieve and add 1/4 kilogram of the juice to the prepared mixture
4. Stir constantly and heat slowly until it thickens
5. When cooled, place in molds as desired and bake again

title: Malinové Paštičky (Raspberry Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Press ripe raspberries through a sieve
2. Cook the mass with sugar to the sixth degree
3. Add what remained on the sieve during the pressing of the mass
4. Cook again with constant stirring until completely thick
5. Pour onto slate tablets to cool and dry
6. When dry, peel off the thin crust, cut and arrange as desired

title: Malinové pokroutky (Raspberry Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Instead of strawberries, raspberries can be used and very tasty brittle can be made using their juice
2. Prepare exactly as described for strawberry brittle

title: Malinové Polévání (Raspberry Glaze)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- finely crushed sugar
- stewed raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Mix finely crushed sugar with stewed raspberry juice
2. Apply to baked goods

title: Malinové Víno (Raspberry Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe raspberries
- finely ground sugar
- raspberry juice or ordinary white wine

## Directions

1. Crush the ripe raspberries
2. Press them and strain the juice through thick cloth
3. To each liter of juice, add finely ground sugar to taste
4. Mix everything and let stand peacefully for three days covered
5. Pour off the settled, clarified juice
6. Add to it as much raspberry juice, either the same amount or twice as much ordinary white wine
7. Transfer to bottles
8. After eight days it is drinkable

title: Malinový Dort (Raspberry Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine dough
- layer of cooked raspberries or ground rusk with fresh raspberries
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) finely ground almonds
- 18 dekagrams sugar
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- snow from 4 egg whites
- 15 grams (1 lot) flour

## Directions

1. Spread a finger's thickness of fine dough on the bottom of a form
2. Coat with a layer of cooked raspberries or sprinkle with ground rusk and fresh raspberries
3. Mix 18 dekagrams finely ground almonds, 18 dekagrams sugar, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, snow from 4 egg whites, and 15 grams flour
4. Pour the mixture over the raspberries
5. Bake at low heat for three-quarters of an hour

title: Malinový ježek (Raspberry Hedgehog)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-2 liters raspberry juice
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) ground sugar
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- several tablespoons ground Buchara
- 8 egg whites for snow

## Directions

1. Boil 1-2 liters raspberry juice with 18 dekagrams ground sugar
2. While it cools, beat 12 dekagrams butter with 8 egg yolks, 12 dekagrams sugar, lemon zest, lemon juice, several tablespoons ground Buchara and stiff snow from 8 egg whites
3. Put everything in a mold and boil for half an hour in water

title: Malinový kompot (Raspberry Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- water
- 1-2 liters raspberries

## Directions

1. Place 12 dekagrams sugar in water and boil until it thickens
2. Add 1-2 liters of raspberries
3. After a short time remove them and pour over with the boiled syrup

title: Malinový Nákyp (Raspberry Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) almonds
- 8 egg yolks
- 24 dekagrams crushed sugar
- lemon oil
- 3/4 kilogram grated cooked potatoes
- snow from 8 egg whites
- 1 liter sour cream foam
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) sugar
- 1/2 liter preserved raspberries
- 15 grams (1 lot) dissolved gelatin
- fresh raspberries

## Directions

1. Scald 24 dekagrams almonds, peel them, dry them, and crush them.
2. Beat with 8 egg yolks, 24 dekagrams crushed sugar, and lemon oil for 1 1/2 hours.
3. Add 3/4 kilogram grated cooked potatoes.
4. Slowly fold in snow from 8 egg whites.
5. Bake two cakes the thickness of a finger from the mixture.
6. When cooled, place one cake in a soufflé mold.
7. Brush with raspberry sauce made from 1 liter sour cream foam, 9 dekagrams sugar, 1/2 liter preserved raspberries, and 15 grams dissolved gelatin.
8. Put half the sauce on the bottom cake, brush the top cake with the other half, and place on top.
9. Place on a cold place and decorate with fresh raspberries.

title: Malinový ocet (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- good wine vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour two parts of good wine vinegar over three parts of raspberries, cover everything with paper and let it stand for at least two days, then press and strain.
2. Add an arbitrary amount of sugar to the vinegar, boil together, remove the foam, and when everything has cooled, strain and pour into containers.
3. Vinegar prepared this way is suitable not only for preparing dishes, but can also be diluted with a good amount of water, sweetened and used as a cooling beverage.

title: Malé Paštiky (Small Pastries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- beaten egg
- cooked fish
- soup
- butter
- lemon juice
- alternatively: fish or crayfish
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut puff pastry into small cakes, brush the edges with beaten egg
2. Fill with minced cooked fish that has been prepared with soup, butter, and lemon juice
3. Alternatively: Clean and cook fish or crayfish, finely chop and stew on a baking sheet
4. Fill the pastries with this filling and cover with a lid

title: Malí ptáčkové (Small Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- small birds, plucked
- butter
- onion
- bread crumbs, toasted and grated

## Directions

1. Cut off the heads of plucked small birds, clean them and salt them
2. Put a piece of butter on a small baking pan, add a piece of onion and place the birds on it breast-side down
3. Let the birds roast for about a quarter hour over brisk heat and when they begin to brown, sprinkle them with toasted grated bread crumbs
4. When the butter begins to sizzle, add several spoonfuls of broth and bring quickly to the table

title: Mandle v Županu (Almonds in Snow Coat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- crushed sifted sugar
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Whip egg whites into stiff snow
2. Add sufficient amount of crushed sifted sugar
3. Scald blanched almonds, dip in the snow
4. Place on paper and dry in oven

title: Mandlová nádivka (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dek finely crushed sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 10 dek almonds
- raisins
- citron peel
- snow from 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Thoroughly mix the finely crushed sugar with egg yolks and almonds
2. Add raisins and citron peel
3. Fold in the snow from beaten egg whites

title: Mandlová nádivka (Almond Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons sugar
- lemon zest
- 1/2 small cup finely pounded almonds
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Put 3 egg yolks in a bowl
2. Add 6 tablespoons of sugar and grated lemon zest
3. Add 1/2 small cup of finely pounded almonds and rub well
4. Fold in stiff snow made from 3 egg whites

title: Mandlová nádivka - Eighth Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 1 egg white
- 10 dek sugar
- 14 dek finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks and 1 egg white until frothy
2. Add sugar
3. Add finely chopped almonds

title: Mandlová nádivka - Fifth Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 14 dek sugar
- 4 dek citron peel
- 14 dek finely grated or chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs and 4 egg yolks
2. Add sugar until all is frothy
3. Add the peeled lemon zest
4. Add 4 dek citron peel and finely grated almonds

title: Mandlová nádivka - Fourth Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 28 dek sugar
- 28 dek finely chopped almonds
- 3 dek grated bread moistened with wine

## Directions

1. Take 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks
2. Mix with sugar and finely chopped almonds
3. Add grated bread moistened with wine
4. Mix into a pleasant filling

title: Mandlová nádivka - Second Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dek almonds
- 1 egg
- 7 dek butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 7 dek sugar

## Directions

1. Crush the almonds
2. Mix with one whole egg, butter, egg yolks, and sugar

title: Mandlová nádivka - Seventh Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 10 dek sugar
- lemon or vanilla flavoring
- 10 dek almonds
- snow from 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks with sugar
2. Add lemon or vanilla flavoring
3. Add almonds
4. Fold in snow from beaten egg whites

title: Mandlová nádivka - Sixth Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dek finely chopped almonds
- 3 eggs
- 10 dek sugar
- 7 dek raisins
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Mix finely chopped almonds, eggs, and sugar
2. Add raisins
3. Add finely crushed cinnamon and cloves

title: Mandlová nádivka - Third Method (Almond Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 14 dek sugar
- 9 dek almonds
- 5 dek grated bread moistened with wine
- snow from 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix and rub the egg yolks with sugar
2. Add almonds
3. Add grated bread moistened with wine
4. Fold in snow from beaten egg whites

title: Mandlové chrupavky (Almond Crisps)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams blanched, pounded almonds
- 20 dekagrams sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- flour
- butter
- sliced almonds
- lemon glaze

## Directions

1. Beat 12 dekagrams blanched, pounded almonds with 20 dekagrams sugar and 12 egg yolks
2. Beat 6 egg whites to stiff peaks and add 1 tablespoon flour
3. Make indentations from stiff paper, grease with butter, sprinkle with sliced almonds
4. Fill with the mixture and dry
5. When removed, coat with lemon glaze

title: Mandlové chrupavky – druhý způsob (Almond Crisps – Second Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams blanched, pounded almonds
- 20 dekagrams sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- flour
- butter
- sliced almonds
- lemon glaze

## Directions

1. Beat 12 dekagrams blanched, pounded almonds
2. Beat 20 dekagrams sugar with 12 egg yolks
3. Beat 6 egg yolks to stiff peaks with 1 tablespoon flour
4. Make oblong indentations from stiff paper, grease with butter, sprinkle with sliced almonds
5. Fill with the mixture and dry
6. When removed, coat with lemon glaze

title: Mandlové hoblovačky (Almond Shaved Wafers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 240 grams sweet almonds
- several bitter almonds
- juice of 2 lemons
- 240 grams sugar
- wafers

## Directions

1. Blanch and peel sweet almonds, adding some bitter almonds
2. Grate or pound them
3. Mix with lemon juice and 240 grams sugar to form a thick paste
4. Spread on wafers and let rest overnight
5. Cut into strips as thick as a finger
6. Bake in a warm oven, then bend on round wood

title: Mandlové koblihy (Almond Fritters)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram almonds
- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- chocolate
- 9 egg whites or 10 egg whites to form stiff meringue

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds
2. Add 1/2 kilogram sugar and chocolate
3. Fold in egg whites beaten to stiff peaks to form a moderately thick dough, adding more egg whites and sugar if needed to thin
4. Form fritters from the dough
5. Bake on a baking sheet lined with paper
6. Leave on the paper until cooled

title: Mandlové koláčky (Almond Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kilogram melted butter
- ¼ kilogram finely crushed sugar
- several bitter almonds beaten with egg white
- 4 eggs
- approximately ½ kilogram fine flour

## Directions

1. Mix the melted butter, crushed sugar, almonds beaten with egg white, and eggs
2. Beat until foamed
3. Gradually add the fine flour
4. Form into cakes
5. Bake gently

title: Mandlové koláčky (Almond Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram melted butter
- 1/4 kilogram finely ground sugar
- several bitter almonds with egg white, ground together
- four eggs
- approximately 1/2 kilogram fine flour

## Directions

1. Mix the butter, sugar, almonds, and eggs until foaming
2. Slowly add the flour
3. Shape the batter into cookies
4. Bake gently

title: Mandlové koláčky - Jiný způsob (Almond Cookies - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram melted butter
- 1/4 kilogram finely ground sugar
- three eggs and three yolks
- finely ground almonds
- 1/4 kilogram fine flour
- a little pleasant liqueur

## Directions

1. Rub the butter, add the finely ground sugar
2. Add three eggs and three yolks, mix until foaming
3. Add finely ground almonds, flour, and a little liqueur
4. Proceed as with previous cookies

title: Mandlové kyselo s moukou zemákovou (Almond Dessert with Potato Flour)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams (16 lots) sweet almonds
- 10 bitter almonds
- 1.5 litres water
- 24 decagrams (16 lots) sugar
- 1/4 litre potato flour
- fruit juice

## Directions

1. Parboil sweet and bitter almonds, grind them
2. Mix in 1.5 litres of water and strain
3. Mix some almond milk with the ground almonds and strain again
4. Boil with 24 decagrams (16 lots) of sugar and 1/4 litre of potato flour mixed in water until a thick paste forms
5. When cool, serve with fruit juice

title: Mandlové mléko (Almond Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- osmina kila mandlí
- voda
- cukr

## Directions

1. Osmina kila mandlí se opere
2. Utlouká se
3. Vodou se rozetírá
4. Přileje se vody
5. Smíchá se
6. Hustým ubrouskem se procezuje
7. Vše se osladí

title: Mandlové mléko (Almond Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled sweet almonds
- water
- sugar
- bitter almonds (optional, not for the sick)

## Directions

1. Pound a quarter pound of peeled sweet almonds
2. While pounding, sprinkle with water to prevent them from becoming oily
3. Put them in a clean pot
4. Pour a quart of water over them and stir
5. Let stand for a while
6. Strain through a clean cloth
7. Sweeten with two tablespoons of sugar
8. If desired, a few bitter almonds can be added for better flavor, but not for the sick

title: Mandlové mušle (Almond Shells)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams almonds
- 18 dekagrams sugar, finely pounded
- lemon peel, finely chopped
- clove
- cinnamon
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Blanch and dry the almonds, then chop finely
2. Place sugar on a baking sheet with finely chopped lemon peel, clove, and cinnamon
3. When the sugar is brown, add the almonds
4. Grease small molds with butter and bake the mixture in them

title: Mandlové oříšky (Almond Nuts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 grams butter
- 3 eggs
- 25 dekagrams sugar
- 25 dekagrams pounded almonds
- lemon peel rubbed with sugar
- 25 dekagrams flour

## Directions

1. Beat 75 grams butter
2. Add 3 eggs and 25 dekagrams sugar
3. Add 25 dekagrams pounded almonds and sugar-rubbed lemon peel
4. Mix for 1/4 hour
5. Add 25 dekagrams flour
6. Form small balls and bake slowly

title: Mandlové piškoty (Almond Lady Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound almonds
- two egg whites
- 10 tablespoons fine sugar
- fourteen egg yolks
- seven egg whites
- two tablespoons white flour
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Blanch and peel quarter pound almonds
2. Pound them finely in a mortar with two egg whites
3. Put on a bowl and add 10 tablespoons of fine sugar
4. Mix well and add fourteen egg yolks
5. Beat this for half an hour
6. Beat seven egg whites into stiff meringue
7. Slowly fold the meringue with two tablespoons of white flour into the remaining mixture
8. Butter small oblong grooved tin molds, dust with flour, and fill with the mixture, but not completely
9. Let bake slowly in the oven
10. Sprinkle with sugar, let cool completely and serve

title: Mandlové piškoty s čokoládou (Chocolate Almond Lady Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- almond mixture as in previous recipe
- 2 ounces grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare the mixture as in the previous method
2. Fold in 2 ounces of grated chocolate along with the meringue and flour
3. Bake as in the previous recipe

title: Mandlové rohlíky (Almond Crescents)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 76 dekagrams peeled dry almonds
- 59 dekagrams sugar mixed with vanilla or lemon
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Grind 76 dekagrams of peeled dry almonds like flour
2. Add 59 dekagrams of sugar mixed with vanilla or lemon
3. Mix well
4. Place a spoonful of the mixture in a mortar, add some egg white, and grind like dough
5. Continue until 4 egg whites are used
6. Mix everything well
7. Put the mixture in a pastry bag and pipe directly onto waxed paper in crescent shapes
8. Bake slowly

title: Mandlové rohlíky (Almond Crescents)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams almonds
- egg white
- 20 dekagrams sugar
- egg whites
- chopped almonds for rolling

## Directions

1. Grind 9 dekagrams of almonds with a cloth, pound with egg white
2. Mix 20 dekagrams of sugar with enough egg whites to form a firm dough
3. Roll on a surface sprinkled with chopped almonds

title: Mandlové rohlíky (Almond Crescents)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) almonds
- 20 dekagrams (14 lots) sugar
- egg whites, as needed for stiff dough
- chopped almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Rub the almonds with cloth and beat with egg white
2. Mix in the sugar and enough egg whites to form stiff dough
3. Roll out the dough and sprinkle with chopped almonds
4. Roll pieces of dough into crescent shapes
5. Bake until golden yellow

title: Mandlové skyvky (Almond Bread Slices)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three rolls
- eggs
- sour cream
- grated bread
- chopped almonds
- four egg whites
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut three rolls into slices, dip in eggs beaten with sour cream, roll in grated bread, chopped almonds, and four beaten egg whites.
2. Bake on both sides in butter, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm.

title: Mandlové suchary (Almond Twice-Baked Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 37.5 dekagrams (25 lots) flour
- 20 dekagrams sugar
- 5 eggs
- 4 grams crushed cinnamon
- 70 grams finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Mix 37.5 dekagrams flour, 20 dekagrams sugar, 5 eggs, and 4 grams crushed cinnamon to form dough
2. Cut pieces the thickness and length of a finger
3. Roll in 70 grams finely chopped almonds
4. Bake on a sheet at moderate heat

title: Mandlové taštičky (Darioles with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound butter for dough
- half small cup sugar
- lemon zest from one lemon
- four egg yolks
- three ounces peeled grated almonds
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Make butter dough from half pound butter
2. Roll out to the thickness of straw and cut into twelve square pieces
3. Put half small cup sugar in a bowl, add lemon zest from one lemon
4. Add four egg yolks and three ounces peeled grated almonds
5. Mix well and finally fold in stiff snow from four egg whites
6. Put a teaspoon of mixture into each square
7. Brush corners with egg, fold two opposite corners over each other
8. Place pouches on a butter-greased sheet and bake in the oven
9. While hot, dust generously with powdered sugar

title: Mandlové věnečky (Almond Rings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram flour
- ½ kilogram sugar
- butter dough
- egg for brushing
- chopped almonds
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Make butter dough from the flour and sugar and roll it out
2. Cut circles with a glass
3. Cut the center with a smaller glass or mold to form rings
4. Place the rings on a moist baking sheet
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Sprinkle with chopped almonds and sugar
7. Bake until golden

title: Mandlové Věnečky (Fancy Almond Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cupful of sugar
- sweet almonds
- 4 egg whites
- ½ teaspoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Take 1 small cupful of sugar
2. Blanch sweet almonds and cut into strips so there is ½ cupful
3. Beat 4 egg whites into stiff peaks
4. Add 1 large spoonful of sugar and beat the peaks longer until thickened by the sugar so it can be cut
5. Mix the remaining sugar with the almonds and sprinkle into the peaks
6. Also add ½ teaspoonful of vanilla and mix slowly
7. Before making the wreaths, wax a baking sheet
8. Place small heaps on it and spread them with a spoon in the middle so they form round wreaths
9. Bake until pale with moderate heat
10. They are very good, beautiful and keep in dry conditions for up to a month
11. They should be made in the cold

title: Mandlový chleb (Almond Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) sugar
- 4 eggs
- 24 dekagrams unpeeled almonds cut into thin strips
- 12 dekagrams candied lemon zest
- 3 dekagrams cinnamon
- 8 grams (½ lot) cloves
- ½ kilogram flour

## Directions

1. Rub the sugar with the eggs
2. Add the almonds cut into thin strips
3. Mix in the candied lemon zest, cinnamon, and cloves
4. Add the flour
5. Form the dough into oblong loaves
6. Bake on a floured baking sheet
7. While still warm, slice into thin slices
8. Dry both sides to golden color

title: Mandlový creme (Almond Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deka peeled or sugared almonds
- 3.5 deciliters boiling cream
- seven deka sugar
- seven egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take seven deka of peeled or sugared almonds and add to them 3.5 deciliters of boiling cream
2. Strain everything, sweeten with seven deka sugar and mix with seven egg yolks

title: Mandlový Dort (Almond Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram peeled ground almonds
- 4 eggs
- half kilogram sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar

## Directions

1. Mix half kilogram peeled ground almonds with 4 eggs, half kilogram sugar, 16 egg yolks, and lemon zest rubbed with sugar
2. Beat for half an hour
3. Slowly add snow from egg whites
4. Pour the mixture into a greased form
5. Bake at low heat for a quarter hour

title: Mandlový dort (Almond Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram peeled ground almonds
- 4 eggs
- half kilogram sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- egg white snow

## Directions

1. Mix half kilogram of peeled ground almonds with 4 eggs
2. Mix half kilogram sugar, 16 egg yolks, and lemon zest rubbed with sugar
3. Beat this mixture for half an hour
4. Slowly add the egg white snow
5. Pour the mixture into a greased baking form
6. Bake at moderate heat for a quarter hour

title: Mandlový dort (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound of sweet almonds
- one ounce of bitter almonds
- three egg yolks
- three-quarters pound of powdered sugar
- ten egg yolks

## Directions

1. Scald and peel three-quarters pound of sweet almonds and one ounce of bitter almonds
2. pound them in a mortar with three egg yolks
3. place in a deep bowl
4. add three-quarters pound of powdered sugar and ten egg yolks

title: Mandlový dort (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound sweet almonds
- one ounce bitter almonds
- three egg yolks
- three-quarters pound powdered sugar
- ten egg yolks
- a little flour
- one dried roll (žemlička)
- ten egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Scald and peel three-quarters pound sweet almonds and one ounce bitter almonds
2. Pound them in a mortar with three egg yolks
3. Place the pounded mixture in a deep bowl
4. Add three-quarters pound powdered sugar, ten egg yolks, and a little flour
5. Mix for one hour
6. Pound one dried roll in a mortar and add it to the mixture
7. Finally add stiff snow made from ten egg whites
8. Fill a form buttered and dusted with grated roll
9. Bake in an oven for one hour
10. Test the oven temperature by placing a piece of paper in it—if it browns, the oven is hot enough for baking the cake
11. When cooled, spread with white glaze applied thickly
12. Let it dry in lukewarm water

title: Mandlový Ježek (Almond Hedgehog Pudding)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) almonds
- 24 dekagrams sugar
- 10 eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- cinnamon
- finely chopped lemon zest
- clove
- brown grated roll

## Directions

1. Grind 24 dekagrams almonds with 24 dekagrams sugar and sieve
2. Add 10 eggs and 4 egg yolks
3. Add snow from 4 egg whites
4. Add cinnamon, finely chopped lemon zest and clove
5. Mix well
6. Bake in a form sprinkled with brown grated roll, or steam in boiling water
7. If steamed, make a wine sauce to serve

title: Mandlový Ježek - Jiný Způsob (Almond Hedgehog Pudding - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 12 dekagrams ground almonds
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) sugar
- 1 lemon
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 dekagrams butter with 4 egg yolks
2. Add 12 dekagrams ground almonds
3. Add 9 dekagrams sugar and lemon
4. Add 6 hard-boiled egg yolks and snow from 4 egg whites
5. Steam in a cloth

title: Mandlový Ježek - Jiný Způsob (Almond Hedgehog Pudding - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter kilogram cooked grated potatoes
- melted butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 60 grams (4 lots) peeled finely ground almonds
- lemon zest grated with sugar
- sweet sour cream
- 4 egg whites
- flour
- quarter liter milk
- 10 peeled almonds
- sugar
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Place cooked grated potatoes on a plate
2. Mix melted butter, 4 egg yolks, 60 grams peeled finely ground almonds, lemon zest grated with sugar and sweet sour cream
3. Fold in snow from 4 egg whites
4. Place mixture in a greased form and cook for half an hour in boiling water
5. After half an hour, turn out and pour the following sauce around it
6. Mix 1 spoonful flour with quarter liter milk
7. Grind 10 peeled almonds and strain with milk through cloth
8. Add sugar and lemon zest
9. Heat over fire, stirring until it thickens like barley pudding

title: Mandlový Nákyp (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a kilo of peeled and dried almonds
- egg whites
- lemon peel
- orange peel
- vanilla, finely ground
- cinnamon
- clove
- finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Grate half a kilo of peeled and dried almonds finely with egg whites.
2. Make snow from the egg whites, to which lemon and orange peel are added.
3. Add finely ground vanilla, cinnamon, clove, and finely ground sugar to the mixture.
4. Mix everything thoroughly and prepare as described for other soufflés.

title: Mandlový Nákyp (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram peeled and dried almonds
- egg whites
- lemon zest
- orange zest
- finely crushed vanilla
- cinnamon
- clove
- finely crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Finely grate 1/2 kilogram of peeled and dried almonds with egg whites
2. Make snow from egg whites, to which lemon and orange zest are added
3. Add finely crushed vanilla, cinnamon, clove, and finely crushed sugar to the soufflé
4. Mix everything well and prepare as described for other soufflés

title: Mandlový Nákyp - Fifth Method (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty five dekagrams (17 lots) butter
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sweet almonds, finely crushed
- 8 bitter almonds
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 dekagrams sugar
- lemon peel
- 3 rolls soaked in milk
- stiff snow from 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 25 dekagrams butter, then slowly add 12 dekagrams well-crushed sweet and 8 bitter almonds.
2. Add 8 egg yolks, 12 dekagrams sugar, lemon peel, 3 rolls soaked in milk, and stiff snow from 8 egg whites.
3. Grease a mold with butter and bake the soufflé.
4. Serve warm at table.

title: Mandlový Nákyp - Fourth Method (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) almonds
- 5 cooked egg yolks
- 24 dekagrams butter
- 9 raw egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- snow from 9 egg whites
- sugar
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Grease a mold and sprinkle with grated roll.
2. Beat together 24 dekagrams almonds, 5 cooked egg yolks, 24 dekagrams butter, 9 raw egg yolks, 3 whole eggs, snow from 9 egg whites with sugar and lemon peel added.
3. Bake the soufflé in the mold, or place the mold in boiling water. In the latter case, pour sauce made from wine over the soufflé.

title: Mandlový Nákyp - Second Method (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) almonds
- 24 dekagrams butter
- 4 whole eggs
- 12 egg yolks
- snow from 10 egg whites
- several handfuls grated roll
- lemon peel rubbed with sugar
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix 24 dekagrams almonds, 24 dekagrams butter, 4 whole eggs, 12 egg yolks, snow from 10 egg whites, several handfuls grated roll, lemon peel rubbed with sugar, and a little salt together.
2. Grease a mold with butter and sprinkle with grated roll.
3. Bake the mixture in the mold and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Mandlový Nákyp - Sixth Method (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) flour
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- 16 dekagrams sugar
- 12 dekagrams crushed sweet almonds
- several bitter almonds
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 more egg yolks
- lemon peel rubbed with sugar
- stiff snow from 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Dissolve 12 dekagrams flour in 1/4 liter sour cream.
2. Add 9 dekagrams butter, 16 dekagrams sugar, 12 dekagrams crushed sweet almonds, several bitter almonds, and 5 egg yolks.
3. Heat over low heat, stirring, until the mixture does not stick to the pan.
4. Let cool.
5. Add another 5 egg yolks, lemon peel rubbed with sugar, and stiff snow from 10 egg whites.
6. Bake in a greased mold.

title: Mandlový Nákyp - Third Method (Almond Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a kilo almonds
- 24 egg yolks
- quarter kilo (half pound) sugar
- lemon peel
- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) wine
- 8 egg yolks
- piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Scald half a kilo almonds, peel them, grate them on a grater. The leftovers that could not be grated are crushed in a mortar and set aside.
2. Add 24 egg yolks one by one to a bowl and constantly beat until they foam and thicken.
3. Crush quarter kilo sugar with which the peel of one lemon was rubbed, and slowly add it to the beaten egg yolks, stirring until it foams again.
4. Add the almonds to the mixture.
5. Pour the mixture into a buttered mold that has been sprinkled with grated biscuits, and bake slowly.
6. For the sauce: put 7 deciliters wine on the stove, add a piece of sugar and stir until it boils. When boiling, add 8 egg yolks and mix well.
7. Pour the sauce over the unmolded soufflé or serve it in a separate dish and sprinkle the soufflé with sugar.
8. The soufflé is also good when the almonds are neither scalded nor peeled. Hazelnuts can be used instead of almonds, which are also only grated without scalding or peeling. The main thing with this soufflé is that everything is well worked.

title: Mandlový Štrudel (Almond Strudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram fine flour
- 1 egg
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) butter
- pinch of salt
- half cup lukewarm water
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) butter
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) sugar
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) peeled ground almonds
- 4 egg whites
- lemon zest
- sweet sour cream
- egg white snow

## Directions

1. Make dough on a floured board from flour, 1 egg, 3 dekagrams butter, salt and half cup lukewarm water
2. Work the dough until bubbles form
3. Cover with a warm bowl and let rest for an hour
4. Spread cloth on table, flour it, lightly roll out dough, then stretch by hand on all sides as thinly as possible without tearing
5. Prepare filling: grate 6 dekagrams butter; gradually add 9 dekagrams sugar, 9 dekagrams peeled ground almonds, 4 egg whites, chopped lemon zest, several spoonfuls sweet sour cream and snow from egg whites
6. Spread filling on dough, slowly roll up strudel and bake on greased pan
7. Brush surface with sour cream and pour rest into pan
8. Bake slowly with gentle heat, baste with milk or sour cream during baking to prevent burning
9. Serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Marcipáti (Marchpane)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1/2 pound ground almonds
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 egg whites
- egg white for brushing
- whole almonds
- white or red glaze for decoration

## Directions

1. Cook 1 cup sugar with 2 tablespoons water until thick
2. Add 1/2 pound ground almonds and mix well
3. Cook, then squeeze juice from 1 lemon into the mixture
4. When slightly cooled, add snow from 2 egg whites
5. Brush wafers with egg white and spread the paste on them
6. Border with whole almonds
7. Bake over moderate heat on a buttered sheet
8. When done, decorate with white or red glaze

title: Marinované Koroptve (Marinated Partridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- older partridges
- salt
- bacon slices
- onion
- bay leaf
- pepper
- juniper berries
- broth
- wine
- calf's feet
- cloves
- lemon
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Salt the older partridges, wrap with bacon slices, and braise with onion, bay leaf, several peppercorns and juniper berries, several spoonfuls of broth, and a glass of wine.
2. For each partridge, take one calf's foot; cut them up and cook with onion, salt, pepper, several cloves, and lemon sliced into strips in water.
3. Strain; mix the juice from the partridges with the strained liquid, remove the fat, and acidify with vinegar.
4. Place the partridges in a pot and pour the warm juice from the feet over them.
5. Finally pour a little fat over to form a crust.
6. Thus preserved, the partridges will keep for a long time.

title: Marinované ryby (Marinated Fish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- flour
- Provençal oil
- parsley roots
- carrot
- celery
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- cloves
- spices

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces and coat with flour.
2. Roast in Provençal oil until golden.
3. Cook the parsley roots, carrot, celery, onion, thyme, bay leaf, cloves and all spices together.
4. Pour the mixture over the fish and let cool.

title: Marinovaní divocí holubi (Marinated Wild Pigeons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young wild pigeons
- vinegar
- spices
- onion
- butter
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Young wild pigeons should be larded at least one day before roasting; older pigeons must be tenderized and marinated for at least three days beforehand.
2. Salt the pigeons and marinate them in vinegar with appropriate spices and onion.
3. Roast them in butter.
4. Serve in tomato sauce.

title: Marinovaní Pstruzi (Marinated Brook Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small fried brook trout
- Onion, sliced
- Peppercorns, several grains
- Bay leaf
- Rosemary, a piece
- Lemon peel, peeled
- Good wine vinegar, enough to cover the trout
- Good olive oil, several spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Arrange small fried trout in a deep dish and let them cool completely
2. In a pot, arrange sliced onion, several peppercorns, a bay leaf, a piece of rosemary, and peeled lemon peel
3. Add good wine vinegar in enough quantity to completely cover the trout
4. Let this boil for one hour
5. When completely cooled, mix in several spoonfuls of good olive oil
6. Pour this over the trout
7. Let them lie in a cool place for several hours
8. Then arrange them on a dish
9. Pour the marinade over them and serve to the table

title: Marinovaný kapr (Marinated Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Salt
- Wine vinegar
- Pepper
- Allspice
- Onion
- Garlic cloves
- Ginger
- Cloves
- Lemon peel
- Oil

## Directions

1. Clean and scale the carp, cut into pieces and salt
2. Roast on a grill or in a pan, basting frequently with butter
3. Place in a deep dish and cover
4. In a pot, combine wine vinegar with several grains of pepper and allspice, onion cut into slices, 2 cloves garlic, a small piece of ginger, 3 cloves, and a piece of lemon peel
5. Heat the mixture and pour over the prepared fish, cover well and set in a cold place
6. When serving, provide a bowl with oil, vinegar and pepper

title: Marinovaný Slaneček (Marinated Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk herring, three
- Milk
- Wine vinegar
- Onion, sliced into rounds
- Lemon, sliced into rounds
- Bay leaves, several
- Peppercorns, several grains
- Allspice
- Capers, a little

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from three milk herring
2. Place them in milk and let them lie overnight
3. The next day, wash them thoroughly
4. Remove the milt to a small pot
5. Split the herring in half and remove the bones
6. Roll the meat into rounds and fasten with a small skewer
7. Mix the milt thoroughly and add vinegar by the spoonful until you have enough sauce to cover the herring
8. Continue beating until it thickens
9. Line the bottom of a deep container with sliced onion
10. Place the herring on top
11. Cover with lemon slices
12. Add several bay leaves, several peppercorns, allspice and a little capers
13. Pour the sauce over it
14. Let it lie for several hours

title: Marinování Pstruhů (Marinated Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- salt
- butter
- bay leaf
- rosemary
- pepper grains
- lemon
- cloves
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and wash the trout, salt well, let rest an hour, then dry
2. Brush with butter and bake
3. When cooled, place in a jar with bay leaf, rosemary, peppercorns, sliced lemon, and a few cloves
4. Pour cooked vinegar over them, seal with oil on top so no air can reach it, and cover the jar well

title: Marmelada malinová (Raspberry Marmalade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Most ripe raspberries
- Sugar - finely crushed, equal measure to juice

## Directions

1. Press the ripest raspberries through a sieve
2. Add finely crushed sugar equal to the amount of juice
3. Mix everything together
4. Cook with constant stirring, continuously skimming the rising foam
5. Allow cooked juice to cool
6. Let stand undisturbed until a crust forms on top
7. Carefully remove this crust
8. Pour marmalade into containers

title: Marmelada třešňová (Cherry Marmalade)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Very ripe cherries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean well-ripened cherries
2. Remove pits and stems
3. Place in a pot
4. Stir constantly and cook until completely soft
5. Press through a fine sieve
6. Cook sugar to the sixth degree
7. Pour juice into it
8. Cook everything together
9. Store in containers

title: Marmelada z broskvi neb merunek (Marmalade from Peaches or Apricots)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-ripened peaches
- Sugar - equal measure to the pulp

## Directions

1. Select well-ripened peaches
2. Halve them
3. Remove the pits
4. Press peaches through a fine sieve
5. Take sugar in the same measure as pulp, cook it to the sixth degree first
6. Cook together with peach pulp with constant stirring
7. Skim foam from the top
8. Pour marmalade into containers
9. When cooled, cover with paper and bladder

title: Marmelada z broskvi neb merunek - Jiný způsob (Marmalade from Peaches or Apricots - Alternative Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-ripened peaches
- Sugar - crushed, equal measure to the pulp

## Directions

1. Select well-ripened peaches
2. Remove pits from them
3. Press the pulp through a fine sieve
4. Add crushed sugar in the same measure as the pulp
5. Mix together
6. Boil several times
7. Store in containers

title: Marmelada Šípková (Rose Hip Jam)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe rose hips
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select very ripe rose hips and remove the seeds.
2. Place the rose hips on a baking sheet and mix them several times daily.
3. When soft, press through a sieve.
4. Add an equal amount of dissolved sugar to the pulp, mix and cook several times, then pour into containers and seal.

title: Marrow Soufflé (Nákyp z Morku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 kilogram (1/4 pound) marrow
- 6 egg yolks
- flour equal to the weight of the yolks
- flower water
- salt
- 6 egg whites
- butter or lard for greasing

## Directions

1. Remove all bones and membranes from 1/8 kilogram marrow
2. Beat it until it turns white
3. Add 6 egg yolks, flour equal to the weight of the yolks, flower water and salt
4. Make stiff snow from 6 egg whites and fold in slowly
5. Place in a mold greased with butter or lard
6. Bake in the oven

title: Marrow Soufflé - Another Method (Nákyp z Morku - Jiný Způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 kilogram (1/4 pound) white marrow
- 8 egg yolks
- flower water
- 1/8 kilogram (1/4 pound) white flour
- salt
- 8 egg whites
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Remove bones and membranes from 1/8 kilogram white marrow
2. Beat for 1/2 hour
3. Add 8 egg yolks, flower water, 1/8 kilogram white flour and salt
4. Add stiff snow made from 8 egg whites
5. Mix everything and either wrap in cloth or place in a greased closed mold
6. Place the mold in boiling water and cook until a stick comes out clean

title: Mashed Potatoes (Bramborová kaše)
body:
## Ingredients

- scraped potatoes
- hot cream or milk
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook scraped potatoes and strain the water from them
2. Mash the potatoes with a potato masher
3. Add a little hot cream or milk and a piece of butter
4. Salt and pepper everything and stir until the mash froths
5. Then stir it once more, place the mash on a dish and pour over it fresh butter in which you have fried a small piece of finely diced onion
6. American style is served without onion

title: Masová Nádivka (Meat Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- minced veal
- eggs
- bread rolls
- milk

## Directions

1. Mix minced veal with eggs and bread rolls soaked and crumbled in milk
2. Work everything together
3. Use for stuffing roasts

title: Masový Paj (Mince Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds beef from the neck
- 2 pounds raisins
- 2 pounds small raisins, cleaned and well washed
- 1/2 pound citron peel
- 3 nutmegs
- 1 1/2 pounds suet from the kidneys, finely chopped
- 8 large apples, peeled and finely chopped
- 1 pint brandy
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 pint apple must
- pastry for bottom and top crusts

## Directions

1. Take 3 pounds beef from the neck, cook well, salt, and chop finely
2. Add 2 pounds raisins, 2 pounds small raisins that have been cleaned and well washed, 1/2 pound citron peel, 3 nutmegs, 1 1/2 pounds suet from the kidneys finely chopped, 8 large peeled apples finely chopped, 1 pint brandy, 1 cup sugar, and 1 pint apple must
3. Mix everything together well and heat thoroughly on the stove until very hot, but do not let it boil
4. When it has cooled slightly, spread into forms, cover with top crust, and bake
5. This pie is always eaten warm

title: Masový sýr (Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from the thigh and head
- pork ears
- pork snout
- pork tongue
- salt
- ground pepper
- clove
- lemon, sliced
- prepared mustard
- oil and vinegar
- ground clove or dried sage (optional)

## Directions

1. Take pork meat from the thigh and head (trim away the fatty jaw), ears, snout, and tongue (it may also be beef)
2. Boil everything until tender
3. Remove bones and cartilage
4. Skin the tongue
5. Cut everything into neat, oblong pieces
6. Arrange in a round, flat mold
7. On the bottom, lay a layer of thigh meat
8. Sprinkle with a little salt, ground pepper, and clove
9. Then add a layer of ears, salt and season again
10. Then a layer of tongue and continue this way until the mold is full
11. Place a lid on the mold
12. Weight it down well so everything is compressed properly
13. Let it stand overnight in the cold
14. Turn out the cheese and cut into slices
15. Serve either with sliced lemon, prepared mustard, or with oil and vinegar
16. The meat must be placed in the mold quickly and while hot, otherwise proper cohesion will not be achieved and the cheese will crumble when cut
17. Before turning out the cheese, loosen it around the edges with a knife so it does not remain stuck inside
18. Instead of clove, you may also use ground clove or ground dried sage as you prefer
19. The mold may also be lined on the bottom and sides with fat-free boiled pork rind, which is also placed on top before the cheese is weighted down

title: Mastný sýr cihlový (Fat Brick Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh morning milk (not collected)
- collected evening milk
- cream (optional)
- rennet
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare from fresh morning milk not collected, to which collected evening milk is added, and if the cheese is to be especially fatty, cream is also added; everything is heated in a pot and rennet is added.
2. Sometimes only cream is added to morning milk, making the cheese fattier.
3. The fattier the cheese should be, the higher temperature must be given to the milk before rennet is added; for leaner cheese the temperature can be lower, thus the milk temperature before curdling is +26-30° R.
4. The cheese curdled in the pot is broken and cut with wooden knives, then placed into molds with skimming spoons.
5. Molds for brick cheese are made from four small wooden, perforated boards, bottomless, and are placed on little tables fitted with grooves so that the whey, expelled by the weight of the cheese, may drain well.
6. The cheese in molds is turned twice a day, flipped, and salted, which is repeated for about seven days, then the cheese is placed in cellars on shelves to ripen and mature.

title: Maštěný Karfiol (Buttered Cauliflower)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- butter
- bread crust
- hot melted butter

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower, carefully remove all greens so the florets do not break, rinse it, place it in salted water with a piece of butter, and cook until tender
2. Meanwhile, grate the bread crust and fry it in butter until golden
3. Carefully place the cooked cauliflower on a dish, sprinkle with the fried bread and pour hot melted butter over it

title: Maštěný Karfiol (Buttered Cauliflower)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower and carefully remove all green leaves so the florets do not break
2. Wash it and place in salted water with a piece of butter
3. Cook until tender
4. Meanwhile, grate the breadcrust and fry it in butter
5. Carefully place the cooked cauliflower on a dish
6. Sprinkle with the fried breadcrumbs and pour hot melted butter over it

title: Mechynkový kompot (Cranberry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- quart of cranberries
- water
- pinch of baking soda
- sugar (two cups)

## Directions

1. Sort through a quart of cranberries and pick them clean.
2. Put them in a granitware bowl, pour cold water over them until covered, and let them cook.
3. Add a pinch of baking soda and let cook until they burst.
4. Then add two cups of sugar and let cook for an hour.

title: Medové Vino (Honey Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters of honey
- 20 liters of water
- approximately 1/4 kilogram of hops
- Several dekagrams of yeast (to start fermentation)
- Optional: ginger, nutmeg flower, cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix 5 liters of honey with 20 liters of water, to which add approximately 1/4 kilogram of hops.
2. Let this mixture boil for about a quarter hour, carefully skimming and removing the foam that develops.
3. Add several dekagrams of yeast to the cooled liquid to begin alcoholic fermentation.
4. After fermentation, sugar, ginger, nutmeg flower, cinnamon and similar may be added as desired.
5. To hasten the clarification of the wine, strain through coarse cloth.
6. When the wine is well sealed in a barrel with a stopper, has rested for about a month, has reached its maturity, it can be siphoned into bottles.
7. Such honey wine or strong mead, reminiscent of malmsey wine taste, remains, if bottles were properly corked, unspoiled for years and becomes better the older it is.

title: Medvědí kýta (Bear Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- young bear meat from the thigh
- salt
- melted butter
- grated horseradish
- sardine sauce, mustard sauce, or other sharp sauce

## Directions

1. Meat from young bears can be eaten and tastes almost like beef
2. Cut nice steaks from the thigh, salt them, brush with melted butter
3. Roast either on a grill or in a roasting pan
4. Surround them with grated horseradish and serve with sardine sauce, mustard sauce or another sharp sauce

title: Melon Ice Cream (Melounová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripest melons
- Lemon juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the ripest melons
2. Remove fibers and seeds
3. Peel everything
4. Cut into pieces
5. Press through a sieve
6. Add lemon juice to the obtained juice
7. Sweeten generously
8. Prepare as other ice cream

title: Melons (Melony)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe but firm melons
- soft water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select ripe but still firm melons
2. Cut into oblong, thin pieces
3. Clean and peel them
4. Place in soft water and set on warm stove
5. As soon as they have cooked slightly, remove and place in thinly cooked sugar
6. If not all pieces are completely soft, keep covered so they finish cooking in the steam
7. Cook the melons with the sugar several times, skim off foam, and cover the vessel with paper
8. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, add the melons, cook together
9. Repeat this for at least two more days, each time cooking the drained sugar thicker and thicker, always skimming foam
10. Once completely candied and cooled, place in jars and bind

title: Meruňková omáčka (Apricot Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetened wine
- apricots or apricot pulp

## Directions

1. Boil the sweetened wine with apricots or apricot pulp
2. Use for pouring over flour dishes

title: Meruňkové a Podobné Dorty (Apricot and Similar Fruit Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- well-cleaned and halved fruit
- sugar
- cinnamon
- pastry strips

## Directions

1. Prepare apricot, peach, and other fruit cakes as you would prepare plum cake
2. Use well-cleaned and halved fruit, either peeled or unpeeled

title: Meruňkový kompot (Apricot Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fully developed but not quite ripe apricots
- sugar
- apple puree

## Directions

1. Take fully developed but not quite ripe apricots
2. Halve and peel them
3. Boil slowly in dissolved sugar
4. When cooled, place on sieves
5. Boil the sugar with a little apple puree to the pearl stage
6. Place the apricots into it
7. Arrange on a dish, pour over with sugar and sprinkle with finely ground sugar

title: Meruňky v cukru sušené (Apricots Dried in Sugar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe apricots of medium size
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Plunge ripe apricots of medium size into hot water until soft.
2. Remove them and place on a sieve to dry.
3. Peel them and remove the pits.
4. Boil a kilogram of sugar, let the apricots sit in it for a while, remove from heat, cover the baking pan with paper and cloth, and leave the apricots in the sugar for 24 hours.
5. On the second day, place them on a sieve to let the sugar drain off, press them, sprinkle with ground sugar, and dry in a warm oven.

title: Minced Hare Patties (Sekaná ze Zajíce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare front quarter meat
- 2 pounds of fatter pork meat
- pork fat or 6 ounces of scraped bacon
- bread roll soaked in wine and well squeezed
- half a grated bread roll
- one or two eggs
- salt
- pepper
- finely chopped lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cut the raw hare front quarter meat with 2 pounds of fatter pork meat.
2. If the pork meat is not fatty, add 6 ounces of scraped bacon, then one bread roll soaked in wine and well squeezed and half a grated bread roll, one or two eggs, necessary salt, pepper and a little finely chopped lemon zest.
3. Mix everything well together and form into a small hare shape.
4. Lard it with bacon, place on a baking sheet lined with butter and onion cut into slices.
5. Bake with frequent basting.
6. Alternatively, the minced meat can be covered with sour cream, in which case the lemon zest must be omitted.

title: Minestra (Minestra Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- malá cibule
- čerstvá slanina nebo máslo s hovězím tukem
- jeden celer
- jednu mrkev
- dva syrové brambory
- několik pěkných hub
- malá tvrdá hlávka zelí
- zelené srdíčko od celeru
- zelená petrželka
- šafrán
- květ
- tři unce vlaské rýže
- parmezánský sýr
- silná hovězí polévka
- uvařené plíčky
- račí klepeta a ocásky

## Directions

1. Rozkrájej malou cibuli na drobno
2. Smaž ji nějakou chvíli na čerstvé slanině na kostky nakrájené nebo na másle s hovězím tukem na pólo smíšeném
3. Rozkrájej jeden celer, jednu mrkev, dva syrové brambory a několik pěkných hub vesměs na kostky
4. Rozkrájej malou tvrdou hlávku zelí na nudle
5. Dej zelené srdíčko od celeru s jemně usekanou zelenou petruželkou a troškou šafránu a květu k cibuli
6. Nech to trochu spejchnout
7. Přidej smíšeninku a za částého míchání nech to asi deset minut smažit
8. Konečně přidej tři unce vlaské, za sucha utřené rýže a nastrouhaného parmazánského sýra
9. Chvíli to ještě smaž
10. Polej silnou hovězí polévkou
11. Nech to vařiti tak, aby rýže zůstala pěkně zrnitou a nepříliš měkkou
12. Můžeš k tomu přidat také kousek uvařených, na hrubší nudle nakrájených plíček, po případě i račí klepeta a ocásky
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mispulka Marmalade (Medlar Jam)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe medlars
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds from the ripe medlars.
2. Place the medlars on a dish and rub and stir them several times daily until they are completely soft.
3. Press the very soft medlars through a sieve.
4. Add an equal amount of crushed sugar to the pressed pulp.
5. Mix everything together and cook together, then pour into containers.

title: Mišpulkové marmelády (Medlar Marmalade - Fine Preserve)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-ripened medlars
- Sugar - crushed, equal measure to the pulp

## Directions

1. Remove seeds from well-ripened medlars
2. Place medlars on a dish
3. Rub and stir them several times daily so they become completely soft
4. Press very soft medlars through a sieve
5. Add crushed sugar equal to the pressed pulp
6. Mix everything together
7. Cook together
8. Store in containers

title: Mladý Hrášek s Rýží a Uzeninou (Young Peas with Rice and Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Young peas
- Butter
- Sugar
- Salt
- Rice
- Beef broth
- Smoked meat
- Lemon sauce and boiled chicken (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the young peas, place on a baking pan, salt, add a piece of butter and a piece of sugar, and stew until tender
2. Prepare the rice: wash it, place on another pan with butter and salt, and stew in beef broth until tender
3. Chop the smoked meat finely and mix with the peas into the rice
4. Serve as a dish by itself or add to boiled chicken in lemon sauce

title: Mladý Pór (Young Leek)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young leek
- salt
- butter
- bread crust

## Directions

1. Clean the young leek, cut off at the green leaves, tie several together, place in salted water with a piece of butter and cook until tender
2. Melt fresh butter, sprinkle the leek with grated fried bread crust and pour butter over it

title: Mladý zajíc (Young Hare)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Young hare legs, split head and sides
- Bacon, cut into pieces
- Butter
- Flour
- Red wine
- Broth
- Lemon peel
- Bundle of vegetables
- Juniper berries
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Onion
- Mushrooms
- Hare blood

## Directions

1. Fry bacon pieces cut into cubes in butter
2. Brown the hare pieces on both sides in the fat without bacon
3. Dust with flour and cover with bacon again
4. Pour in red wine and a little broth
5. Add lemon peel, a bundle of vegetables, juniper berries, salt, pepper and spices
6. Cover the pan and braise the meat
7. After half an hour, add onion fried in butter and sugar, and several mushrooms
8. When the meat is tender, remove it and add hare blood to the sauce and simmer briefly
9. Similarly rabbit and roe deer may be prepared

title: Mléčni Kroupová Polévka (Milk Barley Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams fine barley
- water
- butter
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- 2 and 1/2 liters hot milk
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the barley in water with butter
2. Add lemon peel and cinnamon
3. Add 2 and 1/2 liters hot milk
4. Add a little sugar and salt
5. Boil everything for a quarter hour

title: Mléčni Kroupová Polévka (Milk Groat Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams (8 lots) of fine groats
- water
- butter
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- 2.5 liters of hot milk
- a little sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the fine groats in water with butter, add lemon peel with cinnamon, 2.5 liters of hot milk, a little sugar and salt, then boil everything for a quarter hour

title: Mléčná omáčka (Milk Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 6 dekagrams sugar
- lemon peel
- 5 egg yolks
- potato or wheat flour
- salt
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 liter of milk with 6 dekagrams of sugar that has been rubbed on lemon peel
2. Add 5 egg yolks
3. Add a spoonful of potato or wheat flour
4. Add salt and several spoonfuls of cold milk
