title: Mléčná polévka (Milk Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 litres milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- salt
- several tablespoons roasted flour
- several egg yolks
- rolls, fried

## Directions

1. Boil 2 litres of milk with sugar, cinnamon and salt
2. Add several tablespoons of roasted flour, boil for a while
3. Mix several egg yolks and pour over fried rolls

title: Mléčná polévka s cibulí (Milk Soup with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- small white onions
- butter
- good milk
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- egg yolks
- whole eggs
- dried bread roll, sliced

## Directions

1. Peel small white onions, cut them into small pieces and stew them in butter until soft and completely white
2. Then place them on a sieve and let them drain
3. Boil as much good milk as you want soup, salt it, pepper it, add the onion and let everything cook together
4. After cooking strain the soup, add several beaten egg yolks or whole eggs, mix well and pour into a dish with dried sliced bread roll
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mléčná polévka s nudlemi neb flíčky (Milk Noodle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of milk
- noodles or noodle strips made from 1 egg
- a piece of butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Let 2 quarts of milk cook, but be careful that it does not burn.
2. As it cooks, add noodles or noodle strips made from one egg, add a piece of butter and salt, and let it simmer for a minute.

title: Mléčná polévka s rejží (Milk Soup with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams (8 lots) rice
- 2 litres boiling milk
- sugar
- cinnamon stick
- lemon peel
- several egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pick through and parboil 12 decagrams (8 lots) of rice and set in cold water
2. Boil it and let the water drain off, put it into 2 litres of boiling milk
3. Add sugar, unbroken cinnamon stick and lemon peel, boil and add several egg yolks

title: Mléčná polévka s rýží (Milk Soup with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- hot water
- cold water
- milk
- sugar
- vanilla
- salt
- egg yolks
- whole eggs
- milk
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Rinse a quarter pound of nice rice several times in hot water, then pour cold water on it and let it cool
2. Drain the water, put the rice in a deep pot, pour two quarts of good boiling milk on it
3. Add a quarter pound of sugar and if you wish you can also crush a piece of vanilla with it, salt lightly and let the rice cook until soft
4. Beat in a cup four or five egg yolks or two or three whole eggs with a little milk and a piece of fresh butter and pour into the cooked soup, which you must stir diligently
5. Pour into a dish and serve
6. For 6 people
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mléčná polévka s rýží (Milk Soup with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 cup of rice
- hot water
- 1 cup of hot water
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- a pinch of nutmeg
- 2 quarts of milk
- salt
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Wash half a cup of rice several times in hot water.
2. Place it in a pot and pour 1 cup of hot water over it, add a tablespoon of butter and a pinch of nutmeg.
3. Cover and let it cook.
4. When the water is almost evaporated, pour 2 quarts of milk over the rice, salt it, and when it comes to a boil, beat an egg yolk in it.

title: Mléčná polévka se ságem (Milk Soup with Sago)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago
- milk
- sugar
- lemon peel
- salt
- egg yolks
- whole eggs
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a quarter pound of nice white sago, pour two quarts of good milk on it
2. Add a piece of sugar over which you have grated lemon peel, salt lightly and let the sago cook until soft
3. Beat in a cup either six egg yolks or three whole eggs with a little milk and a piece of butter, pour into the soup, stir and serve
4. For 6 people
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mléčná polévka se žloutky (Monaco Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread rolls
- sugar
- salt
- milk
- egg yolks
- whole eggs

## Directions

1. Cut two bread rolls into thin slices, sprinkle with sugar, let them dry brown in the oven and put them in a dish with a bit of salt
2. Boil two quarts of good milk, add four beaten egg yolks or two whole eggs, mix well (but it must not boil anymore) and pour into the dish with the dried bread roll
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mléčné kyselo s moukou zemákovou (Milk Dessert with Potato Flour)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several bitter almonds
- 1.5 litres hot milk or cream
- sugar
- 1/4 litre potato flour, mixed in water

## Directions

1. Parboil several bitter almonds, peel and grind them
2. Pour 1.5 litres of hot milk or cream over them and strain
3. Sweeten it, add 1/4 litre of potato flour mixed in water
4. Boil, let cool and serve with sugar

title: Mléční račí polévka (Milk Crayfish Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15-20 small crayfish
- salted water
- roll toasted in oven
- 125 grams butter
- 2-2.5 liters fresh milk
- salt
- sugar
- roll cut into cubes and fried

## Directions

1. Select 15-20 small crayfish, cook in salted water, shell the meat from tails, remove intestines and gallbladder
2. Crush the rest completely in a mortar with toasted roll and 125 grams of butter
3. Place this mixture on a baking sheet, cook with 2-2.5 liters of fresh milk, salt and sweeten
4. Strain everything
5. Add roll cut into cubes and fried to the soup

title: Mléčný Chléb (Milk Sponge Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups boiling water
- one teaspoon sugar
- one quarter teaspoon salt
- one quarter teaspoon baking soda
- flour as needed
- milk for the second mixing

## Directions

1. Pour two cups of boiling water into a pitcher and add one teaspoon sugar, one quarter teaspoon salt, and one quarter teaspoon baking soda
2. Let it cool until you can hold your finger in it
3. Add flour as needed to make a nice thick starter and beat for two minutes
4. Place the pitcher in a pot of hot water, but not so hot that it scalds, and maintain the water at a steady temperature until it rises nicely
5. If you start early in the morning and watch it, you will have the starter risen by eleven o'clock
6. You can then proceed as with previously made starters, but use milk instead of water for the final dough making
7. Let the dough rise nicely, form it into loaves, place in baking pans and let rise again, but do not let them overrise or the bread will be full of holes and unpalatable
8. Bake by the previous method

title: Mléčný Štrudel (Milk Strudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 egg white
- 45 grams (3 lots) butter
- salt
- lukewarm water
- butter
- egg yolk
- sour cream
- sugar
- egg white snow
- raisins
- half a liter of boiling milk or sour cream
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Mix flour, 2 eggs, 1 egg white, 45 grams butter, salt and lukewarm water to form a firm dough
2. Work the dough until bubbles form in it
3. Let rest for half an hour
4. Prepare filling from grated butter, egg yolk, sour cream, salt, sugar and snow from egg whites
5. Stretch dough on a cloth, stretch as thinly as possible
6. Spread with filling; sprinkle with raisins
7. Roll up and place in a greased baking pan
8. Pour half a liter of boiling milk or sour cream on the pan
9. Brush strudel with sour cream
10. Serve with sweet milk or sour cream sauce

title: Moravská Polévka (Moravian Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 carrots
- 1 turnip (kelerub)
- 2 parsley roots
- 1 leek
- 1 celery root
- butter
- beef broth
- 1 sliced roll

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots, turnip, parsley roots, leek, and celery root into cubes
2. Braise the vegetables in butter for a quarter hour
3. Add the braised vegetables to beef broth and simmer for half an hour
4. Pour the soup over sliced roll in a bowl

title: Moravská Polévka (Moravian Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 carrots
- rutabaga
- 2 parsley roots
- leek
- celery
- butter
- beef broth
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots, rutabaga, parsley roots, leek, and celery into cubes
2. Braise in butter for a quarter hour
3. Add the chopped vegetables to beef broth
4. Cook for half an hour
5. Pour over sliced bread roll

title: Moučná kaše (Flour Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams (8 lots) flour
- cold milk
- 1 liter milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour with cold milk
2. Boil 1 liter milk with salt
3. Pour the thin prepared batter into it and cook for a quarter hour while constantly stirring
4. Sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon and pour butter over it

title: Moučné Knedlíky Ještě na Jiný Způsob (Flour Dumplings Yet Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping tablespoon lard or butter
- 2 whole eggs
- ½ pint sweet sour cream or good milk
- 1 pound flour
- 2 bread rolls cut and fried, or 4 slices white bread

## Directions

1. Beat a heaping tablespoon of lard or butter on a plate
2. Add 2 whole eggs and mix well
3. Add ½ pint of sweet sour cream or good milk
4. Mix in 1 pound of flour and work it well with a spoon so that the dough does not stick to the hand
5. Add 2 fried bread rolls or 4 slices of white bread, again mix well
6. Shape the dumplings, put them in boiling water and let them boil for half an hour
7. They go very well with cooked dishes and can also be served with cabbage or various sauces

title: Moučné Knedlíky na Jiný Způsob (Flour Dumplings Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ tablespoon fresh or clarified butter
- 3 eggs
- white flour (1 heaping tablespoon per egg)
- 6 tablespoons milk or sour cream
- salt
- ½ bread roll (žemličku)

## Directions

1. Beat about ½ tablespoon of fresh or clarified butter until foamy
2. Add 3 eggs to it, and after each egg add a heaping tablespoon of white flour and mix well
3. Then add 6 tablespoons of milk or sour cream, whether sour, and after each tablespoon of milk mix in a tablespoon of flour and salt it
4. Cut ½ bread roll into small cubes, fry it until golden and let it cool
5. Once the dough is well mixed, mix the cooled bread cubes into it
6. If the dough is too thin, add another tablespoon of flour
7. Boil them in salted boiling water

title: Moučný Svítek (Wheat Flour Puff)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- 6 whole eggs
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 2 small cups sifted good flour
- 3 tablespoons cream
- salt
- milk
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub 4 tablespoons butter until it foams, add to it 6 whole eggs, 4 tablespoons sugar, 2 small cups sifted good flour, 3 tablespoons cream, salt slightly and mix well
2. Grease a metal baking pan with butter, pour the batter about halfway up the pan and let it bake slowly in the oven
3. Then remove the puff from the oven, pierce it several times with a small knife all the way to the bottom, pour cold milk over it
4. After a while, when the milk has soaked into the puff, add as much milk as before, but you must sweeten the milk and add a little vanilla
5. Then when the puff is sufficiently soaked with milk, remove it from the heat, drain the remaining milk from it, turn it out onto a dish

title: Moučný Svítek s Mozečkem (Puff with Calf's Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons fresh butter
- 3 full tablespoons flour
- 2 cups boiling milk
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 egg whites
- butter for greasing the pan
- 1/2 calf's brain
- salt water
- small piece of butter
- parsley
- onion, finely chopped
- ground pepper
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Melt 3 tablespoons fresh butter, add 3 full tablespoons flour, let it foam
2. Gradually pour in 2 cups boiling milk while constantly stirring
3. Let it cook until it becomes a fine paste, then let it cool
4. Add 6 egg yolks and mix well, season with salt
5. Gently fold in snow made from 3 egg whites
6. Grease a shallow pan with butter and bake 2/3 of the prepared paste until golden
7. Meanwhile, cook 1/2 calf's brain in salted water, remove veins, cut into small slices
8. Place the slices in a pan, add a small piece of butter, chopped parsley, finely chopped onion, season with ground pepper and seasonings, let it stew briefly
9. Grease a pan with butter, cut the baked puff into 3 parts, place one layer in the pan, cover with a portion of brain mixture, place the second part of puff, cover again with brain, place the third part of puff, cover again with brain, top with the fourth part of raw paste
10. Place the pan in a larger pan with boiling water and let it steam for three-quarters of an hour
11. Turn out onto a platter and serve quickly

title: Moučný Svítek s Vanilkou (Vanilla Puff)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- 2 cups milk
- salt
- flour
- 5 egg whites
- butter
- vanilla
- sugar
- 2 cups sour cream

## Directions

1. Put 5 egg yolks, 2 teaspoons sugar, 2 cups milk into a bowl, season lightly, add enough flour to make it thick like batter and mix well
2. Gently fold in snow made from 5 egg whites
3. Spread a piece of butter in a pan, pour the mixture into it and bake in the oven
4. Boil a piece of vanilla with a piece of sugar in 2 cups sour cream and place the cut puff into this sauce
5. Alternatively: whisk 5 egg yolks and 1 teaspoon flour in this sour cream, stir on the stove until it thickens and serve with the puff separately in a bowl

title: Mozečkem nadívané žemličky (French Rolls with Calves Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dvě unce másla
- Cibule
- Telecí mozek
- Sůl
- Pepř
- Zelená petruška
- Dvě vejce
- Strouhaná houska
- Mléko
- Vejce

## Directions

1. Nech zpěniti dvě unce másla s usekanou cibulí
2. Vlož do toho dobře očištěný telecí mozek
3. Osol a opepři
4. Přidej trochu usekané zelené petružele
5. Přidej dvě celá vejce
6. Dobře vše promíchej
7. Jakmile to trochu ztuhne, odstav od ohně a nech vychladnout
8. Nakrájej strouhanou housku na tenké řízky
9. Polej je trochu mlékem a ihned slij, aby se nerozpadly
10. Pomaž jeden lístek housky mozkem
11. Druhý nan přimáčkni
12. Omoč v rozkloktaném vejci
13. Obal ve strouhané žemli
14. Usmaž do zlatová v rozpáleném másle
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mozečková Polévka (Calf Brain Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brain
- egg
- bread roll
- butter
- cauliflower
- salt
- toasted bread roll

## Directions

1. Clean the veal brain and remove membranes
2. Cut into pieces and roll in egg and breadcrumbs
3. Fry in butter with chopped bread roll
4. Pound in a mortar
5. Place with broth on a roasting pan and cook
6. Strain and add a little cauliflower, butter, and salt
7. Pour over toasted bread roll

title: Mozečkové koláčky (Brain Patties)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Two veal brains
- Salt water
- Pale sauce
- Cream
- Onion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Three eggs
- Butter
- Truffle sauce, caper sauce or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the brains in salted water.
2. To pale sauce add a little cream to make a paste, let it cool, mix with chopped brains, fried onion, salt, pepper and 3 eggs, then form patties and fry in butter, top with an egg and serve with truffle, caper or anchovy sauce.

title: Mořští rackové (Shrimps)
body:
## Ingredients

- live and well-cleaned shrimps
- butter, hot
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place live, well-cleaned shrimps briefly in boiling water and they are done
2. Alternatively, place in hot butter, keep for a moment, shake gently several times
3. Arrange on a dish and pour butter over them
4. Canned or pickled shrimps should be soaked briefly in fresh water before use
5. Both types can be prepared with butter as described above

title: Mošt Ovocný (Fruit Must)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lesser quality fruit, unripe or rotted fruit
- water

## Directions

1. Crush the fruit
2. Leave it in a container for several days
3. Strain and press the fruit
4. Dilute the juice with water
5. Pour into wine barrels
6. When fermentation is complete, transfer to other containers
7. Care for it like wine
8. The must gains a pleasant taste with various spice and wine additions

title: Mramorový dort (Marble Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- For white dough: egg whites from seven eggs, three coffee cups of sugar, one coffee cup of butter, one coffee cup of sour milk, small teaspoon of baking soda, four coffee cups of flour
- For dark dough: seven egg yolks, three coffee cups of brown sugar, one coffee cup of butter, one coffee cup of sour milk, four coffee cups of flour, small teaspoon of baking soda, one large spoonful of mixed spices, cinnamon and cloves

## Directions

1. Prepare both doughs separately
2. layer them in a greased pan by alternating one spoonful of dark dough with one spoonful of light dough until the pan is full
3. bake for one hour and a quarter

title: Mrkev (Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- butter
- fresh parsley
- sugar
- salt
- beef broth
- flour
- paprika

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the carrots and cut them into small sticks
2. Place on a saucepan with a piece of foamed butter, add some chopped fresh parsley, a piece of sugar, and a little salt
3. Let them stew in their own juice for a while, stirring frequently to prevent burning
4. Add a little beef broth
5. When soft, dust with flour and season with a pinch of paprika

title: Mrkev s uzeným masem (Carrots with Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Water or broth
- Smoked meat scraps
- Flour roux
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Scrape, rinse and cut carrots into cubes.
2. Cook in water or broth, then add chopped pieces of smoked meat.
3. Mix in a flour roux, pepper, vinegar and salt; cook everything well together.
4. Serve on a dish with cooked potatoes.

title: Mrkev se zeleným hráškem (Carrots with Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- small red carrots
- cold water
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- flour
- beef broth
- sour cream
- dill
- green peas
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Peel the small red carrots cleanly, cut into thin sticks and rinse in cold water
2. Place on a small saucepan with a piece of fresh butter and a piece of sugar, having salted them first
3. Stew until tender, adding broth or water as needed to prevent burning
4. Dust with a little flour, add a little beef broth and a little sour cream, season with dill and let stew a bit longer
5. On another small saucepan place a piece of butter and a quart of fine green peas, add a piece of sugar and let stew until soft
6. Add a piece of finely chopped fresh parsley, dust with flour and salt lightly
7. Add a little beef broth and let stew a bit longer
8. Place the peas in the center of a dish and arrange the carrots around them in a wreath

title: Mulberries (Moruše)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar, one and a half kilos
- beautiful, selected, ripe mulberries

## Directions

1. Cook one and a half kilos of sugar until it reaches the sixth degree of thickness.
2. Add beautiful, selected, ripe mulberries to it and cook everything again.
3. Pour onto a dish, cover with paper, and let it sit undisturbed.
4. On the second day, drain off the sugar and cook it thicker.
5. Add the mulberries to it, skim off the foam, and place the preserve in containers.

title: Mullagatawny (Mullagatawny Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken cut into pieces
- a piece of veal bone
- three quarts water
- a bundle of fresh parsley
- one bay leaf
- a stroke of garlic
- a pinch of ground pepper
- cloves
- mace
- salt
- one quarter pound carrots
- one quarter pound turnip
- one quarter pound onion
- one ounce celery root
- two apples
- two ounces ham
- three ounces butter
- three tablespoons flour
- three teaspoons Indian curry
- a piece of sugar
- cooked rice

## Directions

1. Place cut chicken and a piece of veal bone in a pot with three quarts of water and let cook
2. Skim the foam and add a bundle of fresh parsley, one bay leaf, a stroke of garlic, a pinch of ground pepper, cloves and mace
3. Salt and let cook until the chicken is tender, then remove it and cut into pieces
4. Remove all fat from the broth and strain through a sieve
5. Chop one quarter pound carrots, one quarter pound turnip, one quarter pound onion, one ounce celery root, two apples and two ounces of ham, each separately
6. Place three ounces of butter on a pan and let the chopped onion brown on it, then add carrots, turnip, ham, celery, apple and chopped chicken meat and let braise
7. Then add three tablespoons of flour, three teaspoons of Indian curry, a piece of sugar, pour in the broth and mix well
8. Let cook a while, then remove the meat, strain the broth through a sieve, return the strained broth with the meat to the pot and stir over the fire until it begins to boil
9. Place in a soup bowl and serve at table with cooked rice in a separate dish
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mustard (Hořčice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- black mustard (ten decagrams)
- white mustard (twenty decagrams)
- hot wine must

## Directions

1. Grind the mustard
2. Mix about ten decagrams of black and twenty decagrams of white mustard until a thick paste forms
3. Dilute the mustard with hot wine must

title: Muzika (Stewed Fruit Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams (16 lots) plums
- 28 dekagrams dried apples
- 28 dekagrams dried pears
- a little sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Rinse the plums, dried apples and pears
2. Place on a stove with enough water so the mixture is just submerged
3. Add a little sugar

title: Muškatelky (Medlars)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Small medlars
- Soft water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the medlars gently and remove the stems
2. Pierce the pears several times
3. Cook in soft water until they can be easily crushed with fingers
4. Transfer to cold water
5. When cooled, place on a plate and cover with thinly dissolved sugar
6. Cover with paper and let sit quietly
7. Each day pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, add the medlars and bring to a boil, continuing until the fifth day when everything is candied
8. On the fifth day, remove the medlars to containers, place between papers and store in a cool place

title: Muškátové pokroutky (Nutmeg Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- finely pounded and sieved nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare the same as cinnamon brittle, only substitute finely pounded and sieved nutmeg for cinnamon

title: Muškátový meloun (Musk Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Melons
- Sugar or salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chill melons on ice or in a cool place before serving to make them very cold
2. Clean and cut into halves or quarters
3. Arrange on grape leaves or other large leaves
4. Serve sprinkled with sugar or salted and peppered
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Mušle (Mussels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, closed mussels
- wine
- fresh butter
- flour (optional)
- lemon juice
- horseradish or lemon

## Directions

1. Dry fresh, still-closed mussels with a cloth, place on a baking sheet with wine and good fresh butter.
2. If desired, a little flour can be added.
3. Braise until they open.
4. Arrange on a serving dish, pour over the juice, add lemon juice, and serve either with horseradish or lemon.

title: Mušle - Jiný Způsob (Mussels - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- water
- wine
- parsley
- onion

## Directions

1. Wash the mussels with water.
2. Cook the closed mussels in wine with parsley and onion until they open.
3. Remove one half of the shell, keep the other half full and add to the sauce.
4. Use to garnish many dishes.
5. From large types of mussels, remove the cartilage after cooking.

title: Mušle se smetanou (Clams with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- clam liquid
- clams
- butter
- onion, finely chopped, one
- salt
- pepper, ground
- wine, one glass
- brandy, one tablespoon
- egg yolks
- cream
- butter, fresh, a piece
- bread crumb slices in shape of small hearts

## Directions

1. Boil the clam liquid and let clams thicken slightly in it
2. Remove clams and let drain
3. Fry the finely chopped onion in butter until white
4. Add the clams to the onion
5. Add a pinch of salt and ground pepper
6. Add one glass of wine and one tablespoon of brandy
7. Let everything heat well
8. Thicken with egg yolks mixed with cream and a piece of fresh butter
9. Keep stirring but do not boil
10. Pour onto dish
11. Garnish with bread crumb slices shaped like small hearts

title: Mušle v mléce (Clam Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- clams, 50
- clam liquid
- water, two cups
- salt
- pepper
- butter, two tablespoons fresh
- milk, two cups
- crackers, several, finely crushed

## Directions

1. Cook the liquid from 50 clams
2. Meanwhile, chop the clams finely
3. Remove foam from the boiling liquid and add two cups of water
4. When it returns to boiling, add the chopped clams
5. Season with pepper
6. Let simmer very slowly for 5 minutes
7. Add two tablespoons fresh butter
8. Finally add two cups of milk and let heat through, but do not boil
9. Remove from heat, add several finely crushed crackers
10. Serve hot

title: Máselné Rohlíčky (Butter Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece of fresh yeast (about 2 ounces)
- 1 cup milk
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- ½ cup butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- salt
- flour to make a firm dough like for a roll

## Directions

1. Let a piece of fresh yeast soak in 1 cup of milk and make a thin batter and let it rise.
2. When the batter is risen, beat 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks and add to the batter.
3. Then rub ½ cup of butter with 2 tablespoons of sugar, add to the batter, mix everything well with a fork and add a little salt.
4. Add enough flour to make a firm dough like for a roll, roll it out, put it in a warm place to rise, then roll it again and make rolls this way: Roll out the dough to finger thickness, cut it into small pieces with a small glass or round cutter, roll each piece with a rolling pin into a long shape about 6 or 8 inches long and then roll it up.
5. Hold one end, twist the other end and roll it with your hand, then press the end down with tongs.
6. When placing on a baking sheet, bend each into a roll shape, grease with warmed butter and let rise well.
7. When well risen, brush the top with beaten egg and put into a nicely warm oven to bake nicely.

title: Máslová kolečka (Butter Rings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram flour
- fresh butter
- yeast
- sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 yolks
- salt
- sweet sour cream
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare dough from flour, butter, yeast, sugar, egg, yolks, salt, and sour cream
2. Let dough rise for a while
3. Roll out and form into rings
4. Let rise again on stove
5. Brush with yolk, sour cream, and butter, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
6. Bake until golden

title: Máslová omáčka (Butter Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 grams butter
- flour
- salt
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- cold water
- 120 grams fresh butter
- lemon juice
- pepper

## Directions

1. Melt 70 grams of butter in a pan
2. Add a spoonful of flour
3. Season with salt
4. Add nutmeg and grated cinnamon
5. Moisten with a little cold water
6. Stir and let simmer
7. Add 120 grams of fresh butter
8. Add lemon juice and finely ground pepper

title: Máslová omáčka (Drawn Butter Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 cupfuls water
- parsley
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mix four tablespoons of butter smoothly with two tablespoons of flour on a deep plate
2. Put two cupfuls of water in a pot and when it boils, add the butter and flour mixture with constant stirring
3. When it begins to boil again, add chopped parsley, pepper and salt and remove from heat

title: Máslová omáčka s kapary (Butter Sauce with Capers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 grams butter
- flour
- water
- lemon juice
- capers

## Directions

1. Mix 24 grams of butter with a spoonful of flour and 1/4 liter of water
2. Place bowl in hot water
3. Beat the mixture until it foams
4. Add lemon juice and capers
5. Can be served with fish, chicken and veal

title: Máslová zmrzlina (Butter Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- deset žloutků
- litr vody
- cukr
- citrón nebo pomeranč
- půl kila sladkého másla
- různé koření, skořice, vanilka

## Directions

1. Deset žloutků se s litrem vody smíchá
2. Cukr o citrón nebo pomeranč se otře
3. Přidá se půl kila sladkého másla
4. Při stálém šlehání se několikrát zahřeje a zase vychladnout nechá
5. Rozvařeným cukrem se hojně osladí
6. Přidáním různého koření, skořice, vanilky atd. lze zmrzlině přidat rozmanité chuti

title: Máslové Těsto (Butter Dough)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram fine flour
- half kilogram butter
- one eighth finely beaten sugar
- grated lemon zest
- several eggs

## Directions

1. Make a firm dough from all ingredients
2. Knead the dough thoroughly
3. Roll out and cut as desired
4. Layer the butter dough in thin sheets
5. Fill with various preserves as desired
6. Brush with egg yolk
7. Bake quickly in a well-heated oven
8. Always prepare the dough in a cold room; if to be added to game, omit the sugar

title: Míchaná vejce (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- salt
- chives

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter in a saucepan, pour the required number of beaten eggs into it, salt them a little and slowly stir them so they do not stick to the bottom
2. When they thicken a little, remove them to a plate and sprinkle with chopped chives
3. You can also add a little milk or cream to the beaten eggs, one tablespoon per two eggs, a piece of

title: Míchaná vejce (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- salt
- milk or sour cream (optional, one spoon per two eggs)
- chives (chopped)
- pepper (ground)

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter in a pan
2. Pour in the required number of beaten eggs
3. Season lightly with salt
4. Slowly stir the eggs so they do not stick to the bottom
5. When they thicken slightly, turn them out onto a plate
6. Sprinkle with chopped chives
7. Optional: add a little milk or sour cream, chopped chives or parsley, or ground pepper to the beaten eggs

title: Míchaná vejce s houbami (Scrambled Eggs with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh mushrooms
- Fresh butter
- Caraway seeds
- Fresh parsley
- Salt
- Sour cream
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and slice fresh mushrooms
2. Put them in a saucepan, add a piece of fresh butter and a little caraway seed
3. Let them stew gently
4. Add finely chopped fresh parsley, salt and continue stewing until the liquid evaporates
5. Add another piece of butter
6. Beat several eggs lightly in a little sour cream
7. Pour into the mushrooms and stir as for ordinary scrambled eggs

title: Míchaná vejce se sardelemi (Scrambled Eggs with Sardeles)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- sour cream (two spoons)
- flour (one coffee spoon)
- salt
- pepper
- sardeles or small fish (cleaned and cut into thin long pieces)

## Directions

1. Beat several eggs with two spoons of sour cream
2. Add one coffee spoon of flour
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Add cleaned sardeles or small fish cut into thin long pieces
5. Stir everything together in a pan with a piece of heated butter
6. Alternatively: finely chop the sardeles and cook them in butter with chopped green parsley, then mix into the scrambled eggs

title: Míchaná vejce se sýrem (Scrambled Eggs with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- Swiss cheese (cut into cubes)
- butter
- grated cheese (for sprinkling)

## Directions

1. Beat several eggs in a small bowl with a pinch of salt, pepper, and a piece of Swiss cheese cut into cubes
2. Pour into a pan with melted butter
3. Stir until it thickens
4. Turn quickly onto a plate
5. Sprinkle with grated cheese
6. Optional additions: cooked asparagus tips, stewed mushrooms, lightly fried onion, or smoked salmon

title: Míchaná vejce se šunkou (Scrambled Eggs with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked ham (finely chopped)
- butter or lard
- chives (finely chopped) or green parsley
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Crush the cooked and finely chopped ham in butter or lard with a little finely chopped chives or green parsley
2. Season with pepper
3. Mix it into the scrambled eggs prepared according to the previous recipe
4. Alternatively: add finely chopped cooked ham directly to the beaten eggs and stir everything together in a pan with butter

title: Mřenky na Modro (Bluefish in Blue Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned bluefish
- Vinegar
- Salted water
- Spices
- Vegetables
- Onion
- Bay leaf
- New spices
- Oil

## Directions

1. Clean the bluefish and remove guts.
2. Pour vinegar over them and cover with paper.
3. Let sit for half an hour.
4. In a large baking sheet, put spices, vegetables, onion, bay leaf and new spices in salted water.
5. Add the bluefish with vinegar.
6. Boil quickly and serve with vinegar and oil.

title: Na másle dušené křepelky (Stewed Quail with Spice Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and prepared quail
- salt
- bacon sliced into strips
- butter
- small onion
- small carrot
- bay leaf
- cloves
- peppercorns
- meat juices
- white wine
- string

## Directions

1. Salt the cleaned quail, wrap them with bacon strips and tie with string
2. Butter a pan, arrange the quail in it
3. Add one small onion sliced into rounds, a small carrot, one bay leaf, two cloves, several peppercorns, some meat juices and a small glass of white wine
4. Cover well and let braise gently for a quarter hour over moderate heat
5. Remove the quail, press the sauce through a sieve, coat the quail with it
6. Arrange them in a circle either on braised green peas, braised cabbage or regular cabbage

title: Nadivaný pečený kapr (Stuffed Baked Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- carp roe
- water
- lemon juice
- butter
- bread or roll soaked in milk
- eggs
- boiled roe
- egg yolks
- ground flower
- ground pepper
- salt
- anchovy fillets
- mushrooms or truffles

## Directions

1. Clean the carp well, remove gall and intestines from the roe.
2. Place roe in a pot, pour well water and lemon juice on it, let it simmer for about five minutes, then remove.
3. Beat four tablespoons of butter, add one roll soaked and squeezed out in milk or two slices of bread, two beaten eggs, chopped boiled roe, six egg yolks, a bit of ground flower and some ground pepper, salt everything and mix well.
4. Fill the cleaned and salted carp with this mixture and sew it shut.
5. Grease a baking pan well with butter, place onion sliced into rounds, lay the salted carp on it, place pieces of fresh butter on it.
6. Bake and toward the end sprinkle with cleaned and finely chopped anchovies and bake until golden.
7. Frequently baste the carp with its baked juices during baking.
8. If desired, you can use anchovy butter instead of ordinary butter in the stuffing, and cook finely chopped mushrooms or truffles in butter instead.

title: Nadívaná holoubata (Stuffed Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned, plucked, and rinsed squabs
- butter
- eggs and egg yolks
- milk
- salt
- lemon zest
- chopped fresh parsley
- grated bread roll

## Directions

1. Cut off the heads of well-cleaned, plucked and rinsed squabs
2. Carefully remove the giblets and nicely lift the skin on the breasts for the stuffing
3. Rub butter, beat one egg and one yolk into it
4. Add two spoonfuls of milk, a pinch of salt, a little finely chopped lemon zest, a little chopped fresh parsley, and four spoonfuls of grated bread roll
5. Mix everything well
6. Stuff the squabs with this mixture
7. Tie the giblets shut with thread
8. Tie the legs to the tail ends
9. Place the squabs on a baking sheet on a piece of butter
10. Add a little water and roast them with frequent basting with the roasted juices until nicely done
11. Cut them into quarters, arrange nicely on a dish and pour the roasted juices over them

title: Nadívaná jablka (Stuffed Apples)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large tasty apples
- fine plum puree (apricot or peach)
- dissolved sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the large tasty apples
2. Carefully cut out the cores
3. Fill the center of the apples with fine apricot or peach puree
4. Place side by side on a baking sheet
5. Pour dissolved sugar over them
6. Bake gently in an oven until soft

title: Nadívaná Rajská Jablíčka (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- White grated and fried bread
- Fresh parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the top like a lid and set it aside.
2. Remove the inside of the tomatoes with a small spoon using the indentations and place on a plate.
3. To this add white grated and fried bread, finely chopped parsley, salt and pepper, mix and fill the tomatoes with this and finally cover each with the lid again and place on a buttered and breadcrumb-coated baking pan, top with several pieces of butter and put in a not too hot oven.
4. Cover the top with ordinary thick paper, because they like to burn.
5. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Nadívaná Rajská Jablíčka Jiným Způsobem (Stuffed Tomatoes, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Meat or poultry leftovers
- Onion
- Grated bread
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare the tomatoes in the previous manner, chop finely any leftover meat or poultry, chop onion very finely and fry until golden, add the chopped meat to it, add two spoonfuls of grated bread, salt and pepper and let it stew for about a minute.
2. Then add an egg yolk, mix well, fill the tomatoes with this and place them in a deep dish, sprinkle with grated bread, place pieces of butter and let bake.

title: Nadívaná skopová kýta (Stuffed Leg of Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- shallots
- anchovies
- white bread
- sour cream
- eggs
- salt
- lard
- onions
- carrots
- water
- butter
- sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from the leg and cut out additional meat to create a proper opening
2. Melt lard and fry finely chopped shallots and anchovies
3. Add the cut-out meat, four tablespoons grated white bread, four tablespoons sour cream, three whole eggs, and salt
4. Mix well
5. Stuff the leg with this mixture
6. Sew it shut carefully, ensuring it keeps its original shape
7. Place two sliced onions and two carrots on a baking pan
8. Place the leg on them
9. Pour a little water and melted butter under it
10. Roast in the oven, basting with sour cream during roasting
11. When cooked, strain the sauce and remove all fat from it
12. Remove the thread from the leg before serving
13. Pour sauce over it

title: Nadívaná vejce (Deviled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs (about one dozen)
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- butter (heated)
- vinegar (two spoons)

## Directions

1. Boil about one dozen eggs hard
2. Let them cool in cold water
3. Peel them and cut in half so that the halves of whites remain whole
4. Remove yolks and mix finely
5. To the mixed yolks add a little mustard, pepper, salt, heated butter, two spoons of vinegar and mix well
6. Put the mixture back into the whites, taking care to make them look nice
7. Put about as much filling as would fit into two whites and pour more vinegar into that
8. Mix and pour over the eggs
9. These eggs are good for outings

title: Nadívané Brambory (Stuffed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized potatoes
- cooked or roasted beef and pork (usually leftover)
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- beef broth
- lard

## Directions

1. Choose medium-sized potatoes, peel them, trim them and wash them well
2. Using a spoon, carve a small cavity in each potato so they look like little cups
3. Take cooked or roasted beef and pork, usually leftover, chop it finely and add an appropriate amount of onion, also finely chopped
4. Season well with pepper and salt; if needed, add some beef broth, and the stuffing is ready
5. Take a baking pan, put a little lard on it, place the hollowed potatoes with the stuffing at hand, and using a spoon, fill and arrange them on the pan one next to the other so the stuffing does not spill
6. Cover with a lid and put in the oven to bake
7. When the potatoes are soft, remove the lid and let them brown, then serve hot

title: Nadívané brukve (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- veal
- lard
- egg yolks
- butter
- soaked bread roll
- rose hip puree
- cauliflower rosettes
- sausage
- beef ribs

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi and cook until half done; when cooled, hollow them out and fill with the following forcemeat: chop veal with lard, place on a baking dish with two egg yolks, butter, soaked bread roll, let it soften on the stove.
2. When cooled, press everything through a sieve, add a raw egg yolk.
3. Fill the kohlrabi with this forcemeat, place in sauce and let steam until soft, then place on a platter hollow side up, which is covered with a cauliflower rosette, and surround alternately with a slice of sausage and beef ribs.

title: Nadívané brukve - jiný způsob (Stuffed Kohlrabi - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- butter
- bread roll
- hard-boiled eggs
- pork and beef meat
- pepper
- spices
- flower
- ginger
- caraway seeds
- garlic
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi and hollow them out to create a cavity; grate or finely chop the hollowed-out insides, place in butter on a small baking dish.
2. Add grated bread roll, chopped hard-boiled eggs, finely chopped pork and beef meat, a little pepper, new spices, flower, ginger, caraway seeds, one clove of garlic, and braise until everything is soft.
3. Coat the hollowed kohlrabi with butter, stand them side by side on a baking dish hollow side up, pour beef broth over them so they do not burn, and braise until soft.
4. Then fill with the forcemeat and sprinkle with fried bread roll.

title: Nadívané buchtičky (Stuffed Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of dry flour
- one cupful of melted cooled butter
- one small cupful of fine sugar
- four egg yolks or two whole eggs
- a little pounded flower
- a piece of finely cut lemon zest
- a little salt
- a piece of pressed yeast
- lukewarm cream or milk
- curd cheese, poppy seed, fruit preserves, sugar with cinnamon, fruit or jam for filling

## Directions

1. Sift two quarts of dry flour onto a board, make a hollow in the middle, pour into it one cupful of melted cooled butter, add one small cupful of fine sugar, four egg yolks or two whole eggs, a little pounded flower, a piece of finely cut lemon zest, a little salt, a piece of pressed yeast dissolved with as much lukewarm cream or milk to make a loose dough.
2. Work it well until the dough no longer sticks to the spoon, let it rise in a warm place, then cut small pieces from it, roll them out, fill with either curd cheese, poppy seed, fruit preserves, sugar with cinnamon, fruit or jam, wrap them, place one next to the other in a buttered pan, let them rise a little more and then bake in the oven.

title: Nadívané Cibule (Stuffed Onions)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large round onions
- salt
- water
- veal
- pork
- butter
- good meat broth

## Directions

1. Clean and peel large round onions, cook them in salted water for fifteen minutes, then place in fresh water to cool
2. Hollow out the cooled onions and stuff with chopped veal and pork
3. Place them close to each other in a pot, pour good meat broth with butter and salt added over them and stew slowly

title: Nadívané jehněčí hrudí (Stuffed Brisket of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb brisket
- stuffing (same as for veal brisket)
- salt
- white ground pepper
- onion
- carrot
- fresh melted butter or lard
- beef broth or water

## Directions

1. Wash the lamb brisket well and dry it
2. Carefully trim the breast and fill with the same stuffing as used for veal brisket
3. Sew it closed
4. Salt on both sides and sprinkle with white ground pepper
5. Place diced onion and carrot in the roasting pan and put the brisket on top
6. Pour fresh melted butter or lard over it
7. Add a little beef broth or water
8. Roast for about one hour with frequent basting with the meat juice
9. Remove the brisket to a platter
10. Let the gravy simmer with a little beef broth, remove all fat and pour it over the roasted meat

title: Nadívané jehněčí neb kůzlečí hrudí (Stuffed Lamb or Kid Breast)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or kid breast
- bread
- butter
- eggs
- egg yolk
- salt
- herbs (parsley or chives optional)
- milk
- water

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the breast, salt it.
2. If breast is small, take one loaf, otherwise 2 loaves of bread, grate them, press through a sieve, dry on the stove and crush.
3. Beat 30 grams of butter, 2 eggs, 1 yolk, a little salt, a little herbs.
4. Add grated bread to everything.
5. If the stuffing is too thick, add milk or an egg; the stuffing should be the consistency of porridge.
6. Fill the cleaned breast with the stuffing using a syringe or spoon and sew it shut.
7. Add 2 large spoonfuls of water and 30 grams of butter to the breast and bake until reddened.
8. Cut into neat pieces and serve.

title: Nadívané Kedlubny (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- young kohlrabi
- veal
- cooked smoked pork
- kohlrabi pulp
- eggs
- white bread or crackers
- ground spice
- fresh butter
- salt
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Take small kohlrabi of equal size, peel them, cut off the tops, carefully hollow them out and place in cold water
2. Remove to a clean cloth, place at an angle to let water drain, and stuff with veal forcemeat
3. Finely chop raw veal, cooked smoked pork, and the hollowed kohlrabi pulp
4. Mix in three eggs, three tablespoons of grated white bread or crushed crackers, ground spice, a piece of fresh butter, and salt
5. Mix well and fill the kohlrabi
6. Cover each with its cut-off top, grease a pan with butter, place stuffed kohlrabi in it, add beef broth to about half the height of the kohlrabi, and braise until tender
7. Arrange on a shallow dish and pour a little brown sauce over

title: Nadívané koblihy (Filled Doughnuts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- white flour
- 2 eggs
- sour cream
- salt
- jam
- raisins
- chopped almonds
- curd cheese
- sugar
- egg yolks
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix white flour with 2 eggs, sour cream and salt
2. Work the dough, beat with a roller and let rest for an hour under a warm bowl
3. Roll out, pull out as for strudel, cut into wide strips
4. Spread jam on the strips, sprinkle with raisins, chopped almonds or curd cheese mixed with sugar and egg yolks
5. Stack the strips one on top of another, cut out doughnuts and fry in butter until pale

title: Nadívané koroptve žampiňony (Partridges Stuffed with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of small mushrooms
- salt
- water
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) butter
- pepper
- partridges
- lard

## Directions

1. Rub the mushrooms with salt, rinse in water and dry.
2. Dissolve the butter and add the mushrooms, salt and pepper.
3. Let cool.
4. Stuff the partridges with the mushroom mixture.
5. Wrap in lard, tie and roast or fry.

title: Nadívané Kuře (Stuffed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons butter
- two eggs
- half small onion
- half cup milk
- white bread or breadcrumbs or crackers
- cloves

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken for roasting and salt inside and outside
2. Beat two tablespoons butter until it foams
3. Add two eggs, half a small onion browned to yellow, half a cup of milk, salt, pepper, and a pinch of cloves
4. Mix in enough grated white bread, breadcrumbs, or crushed crackers to make a batter like for dumplings
5. Stuff the chicken with this mixture, sew it closed
6. Place on a baking pan, cover the surface with butter, add water
7. Bake, frequently basting with the pan juices during cooking

title: Nadívané lívance (Stuffed Fried Pancakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roast meat
- beef marrow
- onion
- grated roll
- salt
- butter
- broth
- egg

## Directions

1. Make a filling by chopping together leftover roast, beef marrow, onion, and grated roll with salt, cooking briefly with butter and broth over heat.
2. Allow filling to cool.
3. Spread cooled filling on prepared lívance, roll them up, cut into pieces, coat with egg, roll in grated roll, and fry in butter.
4. Serve with vegetables.

title: Nadívané Okurky (Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized cucumbers
- veal
- pork
- rendered lard
- raw eggs (2-3)
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- flower (garnish)
- nutmeg
- bread (1-2 pieces soaked in milk)
- beef broth
- good wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut cucumbers lengthwise and hollow them out
2. Finely mince equal quantities of veal and pork, add a piece of rendered lard, 2-3 raw eggs, a little salt, pepper, ginger, flower, a little nutmeg for flavor, and 1-2 pieces of bread soaked in milk and squeezed out, mix everything together
3. Fill the cucumber halves with this mixture and close them
4. Place on a baking sheet, pour beef broth and good wine vinegar over them, and either bake in the oven or cook in the oven
5. Serve the prepared cucumbers after the soup course

title: Nadívané omeletky masité (Omelette with Meat Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked ham, or braised sweetbreads, kidneys, brains, goose liver, mushrooms, oysters, crayfish or fish meat
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare omelettes according to the previous recipe, but instead of bacon fill them with either cooked ham, braised sweetbreads, kidneys, brains, goose liver, mushrooms, oysters, crayfish or fish meat
2. The meat must be finely chopped and braised in butter
3. When the omelette begins to set, spread the braised filling over it, then fold the omelette

title: Nadívané sandviče (Stuffed Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken meat
- celery stalks
- tarragon leaves
- mayonnaise
- salt
- pepper
- small bread rolls

## Directions

1. Chop chicken meat into small cubes
2. Cut celery stalks into small strips, using as much as there is meat
3. Add tarragon leaves and enough mayonnaise to bind everything together
4. Season with salt and pepper and mix well
5. Take small bread rolls, cut off the top
6. Carefully scoop out the interior
7. Fill rolls with the mixture
8. Cover with the cut-off pieces and arrange on a napkin-covered plate
9. Veal roast, crab, pickled salmon and similar may be used instead of chicken
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Nadívané zemáky (Stuffed Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several same-sized potatoes
- 2-3 cooked potatoes
- chopped onion
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) butter
- bacon cut into cubes
- 1 egg
- chopped parsley
- chives
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash, peel, and split potatoes lengthwise
2. Scoop out centers
3. Mash cooked potatoes with chopped onion, butter, bacon cubes, egg, chopped parsley, chives, pepper, and salt
4. Pound everything in a mortar
5. Grease split potatoes with butter
6. Fill with stuffing
7. Close potatoes together
8. Place in greased mold and bake

title: Nadívané zemáky, jiný způsob (Stuffed Potatoes, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- brain
- water or milk
- chopped ham
- onion
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- chopped lemon zest
- potatoes
- beef broth
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak brain in water or milk for a time
2. Remove membranes
3. Chop and mix with chopped ham, onion, eggs, salt, pepper, and chopped lemon zest
4. Partially cook potatoes
5. Cut off tops and set aside
6. Scoop out centers
7. Fill with stuffing
8. Cover with cut-off tops
9. Stick together with egg
10. Cook in beef broth after braising in butter

title: Nadívané žampiony (Stuffed Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- lemon juice
- mushroom stems, finely chopped
- fresh parsley
- meat
- butter
- breadcrumbs, grated and fried
- salt
- flower (cloves?)
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel the mushrooms, remove the stems, scrape out the insides and put them in water strained through lemon juice.
2. Finely chop the stems with a piece of fresh parsley, add a little meat and let it stew in butter; then add a tablespoon of fried grated breadcrumbs, a little salt and a small amount of flower, two egg yolks, mix it into a fairly thick stuffing, fill the hollowed mushrooms to the top, put them on a shallow, butter-coated baking pan and let them brown nicely in the oven.
3. You can serve them either alone or use them to garnish various roasts.

title: Nadívaný Nákyp (Filled Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- soufflé dough
- cherries
- jam

## Directions

1. Pour soufflé dough into a mold
2. When baked, place cherries and other preserves on top
3. Place a second soufflé dough on top and bake everything again slowly

title: Nakládaná divoká husa (Marinated Wild Goose)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 2 carrots
- 1/2 lemon
- parsley
- thyme
- 4-5 cloves
- 8 peppercorns
- 3 juniper berries
- 1 liter good vinegar
- wild goose, interlarded with bacon

## Directions

1. Slice the onions, carrots, and lemon into pieces.
2. Boil them with parsley, thyme, cloves, peppercorns, and juniper berries in 1 liter of good vinegar.
3. Allow the mixture to cool completely.
4. Place the heavily bacon-interlarded goose in the cooled marinade.
5. Cover well and let it marinate for 3-4 days.
6. Roast the marinated goose in abundant butter for 3-4 hours, or braise it in butter with added onion, spices, lemon, vegetables, and abundant bacon.
7. Remove the fat from the sauce and take out the goose.
8. To the strained sauce, add lemon juice and sour cream.
9. Boil again, thicken with flour, add red wine, and pour over the goose when it is soft-cooked or braised.

title: Nakládaná rajská jablíčka (Pickled Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe but not overripe tomatoes
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Select ripe but not overripe tomatoes, clean them thoroughly, and place on a large plate so the water drains
2. Take a stone jar of about 3 gallons capacity, clean and dry it, and arrange the tomatoes in it without crushing them
3. Boil good vinegar and pour it hot over the tomatoes
4. Cover with a plate so it cools as slowly as possible
5. When the steam escapes, seal well and store in the cellar
6. This way they can be kept until fresh with minimal effort and are more reliable than in cans
7. In winter they are good for sauces, and if not too sour vinegar is used, also for the table, though they have a sour taste
8. The skin comes off easily

title: Nakládaná rajská jablíčka (Pickled Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe but not too soft tomatoes
- wine vinegar
- peppercorns
- allspice
- cloves
- salt
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Wash the tomatoes in cold water and let them dry on clean cloth
2. Arrange them in jars or stone jars
3. Meanwhile boil wine vinegar with a few peppercorns, allspice, cloves, and a little salt
4. When completely cooled, pour over the tomatoes
5. The vinegar must completely cover the tomatoes and the surface is sealed with melted butter or lard
6. Seal the containers tightly or tie them up and store in a cool place
7. Alternatively, you may use equal parts vinegar and water, but in that case use more salt and proceed otherwise as before

title: Nakládané hovězí (Boiled Corned Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- corned beef
- cold water
- hot water

## Directions

1. Clean the meat well; if it is too salty, place it in cold water, which when it comes to a boil, after about 10 minutes, drain and replace with fresh boiling water.
2. If it is not too salty, you can place it in hot water.
3. Skim off the foam diligently and boil about half an hour for each pound of meat, so 4 pounds for about two hours.
4. If it is to be served cold, it is better not to remove it immediately after cooking, but rather set it aside and leave it in the water in which it was boiled until it has almost cooled.
5. Then place the meat in a shallow dish, lay a board over it, and weigh it down with bricks.
6. The compressed meat cuts better.

title: Nakládané Okurky (Pickled Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers of any size
- water
- salt
- dill fronds or dill seed (optional)
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick cucumbers of any desired size and place them in an earthenware pot.
2. Boil water and add plenty of salt to it.
3. Pour the hot water over the cucumbers so they are completely submerged.
4. If the cucumbers rise to the surface, place a plate on top and weigh it down with a stone.
5. Add dill fronds or dill seed if desired for flavor.
6. Let the cucumbers soak in the salt water for 3-4 days so they absorb the brine well and develop proper flavor.
7. Remove the cucumbers and pack them into bottles.
8. Pour boiled vinegar over them.
9. Screw the bottles shut as you would for preserving.
10. These cucumbers will keep until the new harvest.

title: Nakládané okurky (Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers of any size
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Pick cucumbers of any desired size and place in an earthenware jar
2. Boil water with plenty of salt
3. Pour boiling water over the cucumbers so they are completely submerged
4. If cucumbers rise, a plate weighted with a stone can be placed over them
5. Additional spices may be added to cucumbers prepared this way

title: Nakládané vinné hrozny (Pickled Grapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, completely healthy grapes, preferably of firm grain
- vinegar
- pepper
- allspice
- cinnamon
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, completely healthy grapes, preferably of firm grain, and place them in a stone jar
2. Boil vinegar with whole pepper, allspice, and cinnamon, and sugar to taste
3. After simmering gently for half an hour, remove from heat
4. Pour over the grapes
5. When cooled, tie up the jar and store in a cool place
6. The grapes must be completely submerged and lightly weighted down with something such as a small plate and a stone

title: Nakládaný losos (Canned Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned salmon
- lemon slices
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. Remove the lid from the can and carefully place the salmon on a dish so that the largest pieces remain intact and remove the bones.
2. Pay attention as the salmon in the can is cooked and therefore fragile.
3. Arrange on the dish with lemon slices and serve vinegar and spices alongside.

title: Nakládaný losos (Canned Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned salmon
- lemon
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. Remove the lid from the can and carefully take the salmon out onto a dish so that the largest pieces remain as intact as possible and remove the bones
2. Be careful as the salmon in the can is cooked and therefore fragile
3. Arrange on a dish surrounded by lemon slices and serve with vinegar and spices

title: Nakládaný losos na jiný způsob (Canned Salmon Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned salmon
- several eggs
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Remove the salmon from the can onto a dish in the manner mentioned above.
2. Hard boil several eggs, peel them and cut them lengthwise in half.
3. Arrange the salmon and pour mayonnaise over it.

title: Nakládaný losos na jiný způsob (Canned Salmon with Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned salmon
- eggs
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Remove the salmon from the can onto a dish in the manner described above
2. Cook several eggs hard, peel and cut lengthwise into halves
3. Arrange the salmon and pour mayonnaise over it

title: Nakládáni brusnic (ruský způsob) (Preserved Cranberries, Russian Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cranberries
- apples or pears
- hard water

## Directions

1. Clean, rinse and dry ripe cranberries and layer them alternately with wrapped apples or pears in stone containers
2. Pour hard water over them, press down with a heavy object and place in a cool location
3. Cranberries prepared this way will last the whole winter; when needed, they are prepared with sugar

title: Nakládáni hub v octě (Pickling Mushrooms in Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- salt
- water
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil cleaned mushrooms in salted water, place in a container
2. Pour fresh good vinegar over them
3. After eight days, pour off the vinegar, boil it, let it cool, strain and pour again over the mushrooms, which must be covered
4. Close the containers well and store

title: Nakládáni jahod (Layered Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries with stems
- sugar (equal weight to strawberries)
- rum

## Directions

1. During dry weather, gather strawberries with stems and lay them on a shallow dish so they don't touch each other and sprinkle with sugar
2. Press through a sieve 1 liter of ripe strawberries, add as much sugar as the strawberries weigh, and boil over mild heat
3. Strain through muslin, boil the juice again and remove foam
4. When the juice cools, place the sugared strawberries in it
5. Bring to heat but do not allow to boil and let cool
6. Repeat this about four times
7. Carefully remove strawberries and place in jars
8. Boil the juice once more, let cool and pour over the strawberries

title: Nakládáni jahod do rumu (Strawberries Preserved in Rum)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe strawberries gathered in dry weather
- pounded sugar
- good rum

## Directions

1. Place ripe strawberries gathered in dry weather with pounded sugar in jars
2. Pour enough good rum over them to cover the strawberries
3. Close and seal the jars

title: Nakládání hovězího masa do jíchy (Corning of Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon saltpeter
- 1 gallon water
- salt

## Directions

1. Place a tablespoon of saltpeter into a gallon of water and bring to a boil for about a quarter hour, seasoning it well with salt. Let it cool completely.
2. Pour this brine over the meat. The meat will keep throughout the whole summer and remain as if fresh. Pork can also be preserved this way, but requires more salt.

title: Nakládání hovězího masa na jiný způsob (Corned Beef, Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 pounds meat
- 6 to 7 pounds salt
- 1 box mixed spices
- 2 cloves garlic

## Directions

1. Grind the mixed spices on a mill and add 2 cloves of garlic that have been peeled and finely crushed. Mix all of this together well.
2. Season the fresh meat with this mixture and layer it in a pot or barrel so there are no empty spaces between the pieces. Weight it down with a board or stone. This preserves the meat for a considerable time.

title: Nakládání hovězího masa na kyselo (Pickled Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- vinegar
- water
- ground spices

## Directions

1. Parboil the meat so no blood remains, then remove the bones. Boil vinegar with water in equal parts, adding ground spices as used for gravy.
2. Let the meat cool so it does not steam, then the next day cut it into small pieces, about 2 to 3 pounds each. Place in a container and pour the cooled brine over it. Such meat will keep for a whole year.

title: Nakládání meruňky (Preserved Apricots)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Half-ripe apricots
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel half-ripe apricots.
2. Boil the sugar until it thickens completely.
3. Place the apricots in jars, pour sugar over them, seal well, and place in a pot with hay.
4. Boil for a while, then place in a cold location.

title: Nakládání Mladých Fazolí na Zimu (Canned Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- young bean pods
- salt
- water
- hay
- soft water

## Directions

1. Select only very young bean pods.
2. Blanch them once in salted water by bringing to a boil.
3. Quickly dry them on a sieve and store in boxes. Or, prepare them this way for better flavor: Fill bottles with beans and screw them loosely shut.
4. Place the bottles in a basin on the bottom of which you have placed some hay.
5. Fill the basin with soft water and bring it to a boil.
6. Remove the basin from the heat, let the bottles cool completely, then screw them tightly shut.
7. Store them in a dry, cool cellar.

title: Nakládání okurek (Pickling Cucumbers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- štíhlé, zdravé okurky (slender, healthy cucumbers)
- trnkové lupení nebo lupení z kyselých višní či réví (sloe leaves or sour cherry or vine leaves)
- kopor (dill)
- nejtvrdší studničná voda (hard well water)
- sůl (salt)
- dýnko (wooden disk)
- čistý kámen nebo pálenými cihlami (clean stone or fired bricks)

## Directions

1. Štíhlé, zdravé okurky se vyperou a otrou, všechny věci k nakládání se také čistě vyperou
2. Nejprv se dá vrstva trnkového lupení, aneb není-li toho, vrstva lupení z kyselých višní neb réví promíchaná koprem
3. Na to se naklade vrstva okurek, pak opět vrstva lupení atd. až je sud plný
4. Na poslední vrstvu okurek dá se hodná vrstva lupení a kopru
5. Vezme se nejtvrdší studničná voda, rozpustí se v ní hodně soli a naleje se plný sud
6. Vezme se dýnko poněkud nižší než sud a položí na okurky, obtěžká se čistým kamenem neb pálenými cihlami
7. Když okurky dobře ukysají, sebere se pěna, dýnko se omeje, přiloží se ještě vrstva lupení, a na ně dýnko, kteréž se opět obtěžká a sud slanou vodou doleje
8. Sud se teď přikryje čistým plátnem, a na ně se položí dno, které sud na prst přesahuje, aby vzduch přístupu neměl

title: Nakládání okurek (Pickling of Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- fresh cold water
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- spices
- dill

## Directions

1. Pick cucumbers in the evening and place in a bucket, covering with fresh cold water and let soak briefly
2. Prepare salt water so saturated that an egg floats in it
3. Remove cucumbers from plain water and place in salt water where they stay overnight
4. In the morning remove and dry with cloth, which gives them a nice green color
5. Pack cucumbers into a five-gallon barrel with one end removed, alternating layers of cucumbers and dill until the barrel is full
6. Seal the barrel
7. Boil water and vinegar using three-quarters of a pail of water to one-quarter pint of vinegar
8. Add some spices during boiling
9. When cooled, pour through the bung hole
10. Seal tightly
11. The barrel must be full enough to fill the container completely; a five-gallon barrel requires two pails of cucumbers
12. Cucumbers keep for years in both barrels and bottles if kept airtight and will remain nicely green

title: Nakládání okurek - Jiný způsob (Pickling Cucumbers - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- okurky (cucumbers)
- studená voda (cold water)
- dobrý ocet (good vinegar)
- voda (water)
- višňové nebo vinní listí (cherry or vine leaves)
- kopor (dill)
- sůl (salt)
- zázvor (ginger)
- nové koření (allspice)
- křen (horseradish)
- paprika (paprika)
- prkénko (board)
- kámen (stone)

## Directions

1. Okurky se na obou koncích oříznou a na dvě hodiny vloží do studené vody
2. Zatím se svaří dobrý ocet s vodou
3. Do nádoby (nejlépe dřevěné nebo hliněné) se vloží dospodu višňového nebo vinního listí a kopru, na to vrstva okurek, posolí se
4. Nasype zázvoru, nového koření, na koláčky nakrájeného křenu a opět listí
5. Když je nádoba plna, nalije se s vodou svařený teplý ocet, okurky přiloží prkénkem a kamenem
6. Takto naložené okurky se již třetího dne mohou vybírali

title: Nakládání zelí z vodnice (Pickling Kohlrabi)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- vodnice (kohlrabi)
- sůl (salt)
- kmín (caraway)
- míšeňská jablka nebo malé okurky (Meissen apples or small cucumbers)
- vinné nebo višňové listí (vine or cherry leaves)
- prkénko (board)
- kámen (stone)

## Directions

1. Vodnice se očisti, oloupá a postrouhá na patřičném struhadle, dobře se prosype soli a kmínem
2. Dá do nádoby ve které má již zůstati naložené; může se složiti buď několik míšeňských jablek, malých okurek, vinního neb višňového listí
3. Utlačí se hodně, a dá se na místo vlažné na několik dní, nahoře se udělá otvor a odvádí přebytečná voda
4. Přiloží prkénkem a obtíží kamenem; po několika dních se postaví do chladného sklepa

title: Nakládání červené řípy (Pickling Beets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- červená řípa (beets)
- hrnec (pot)
- vinný ocet (wine vinegar)
- voda (water)
- kořínek křenu (horseradish)
- fenykl (fennel)

## Directions

1. Červená řípa prostřední velikosti se očisti a v hrnci do měkká uvaří; nechá vychladnouti, oloupá, na koláčky tence nakrájí
2. Dobrý vinní ocet se svaří (je-li silný, přileje se vody)
3. Očištěný kořínek křenu se nakrájí na koláčky
4. Do hrnce nebo sklenice se širokým hrdlem se střídavě klade vrstva řípy s vrstvou křenu a posype fenyklem
5. Když je nádoba naplněna, polije se vychladlým, svařeným octem, zaváže buď měchýřem nebo k tomu upraveným pergamenovým papírem a postaví na chladné místo

title: Naložená Ledvinová Pečené (Roast Pickled Fillet of Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 onion, chopped
- pepper berries
- whole allspice
- cloves
- water
- vinegar
- veal fillet with kidney
- salt
- bacon or lard
- vegetables (carrot, parsnip, celery, onion)
- pan juices
- 3 egg yolks
- beef broth
- sour cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Put a chopped onion, pepper berries, whole allspice and cloves in a pot
2. Add water and vinegar, bring to a boil and let simmer for half an hour, then cool
3. Place cleaned veal fillet in a roasting pan, pour the cooled pickling liquid over it, and let it rest in a cool place for 3 to 4 days, turning it daily
4. Remove the fillet and lard it with thin strips of bacon or lard, salt it
5. Place on a baking pan with chopped vegetables underneath
6. Add half a cup of the pickling liquid and roast until cooked through
7. Remove from oven, slice into cutlets and place on a heated platter
8. Beat 3 egg yolks with a little broth in a small cup
9. Pour this into the simmering strained sauce, stirring over heat until the sauce begins to rise, then pour over the meat
10. For basting you may use sour cream instead of wine, and serve with potato croquettes or sliced and fried dumplings

title: Naložená Nadívaná Paprika (Stuffed Pickled Peppers)
body:
## Ingredients

- large bell pepper pods
- salt
- salted water
- onion
- red cabbage
- tomatoes
- carrots
- corn kernels
- small cucumbers
- horseradish
- whole mustard
- celery seed
- Spanish pepper (curry powder)
- ground cloves
- cinnamon
- allspice
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Select large pepper pods, cut each one on the side and carefully remove the seeds
2. Pour salted water over them, let them lie for six days and renew the salted water several times
3. Finely chop onion, red cabbage, tomatoes, carrots, corn kernels from the cob and small cucumbers, grate a piece of horseradish and mix well
4. Add whole mustard, celery seed and a little Spanish pepper (curry powder)
5. Remove the pepper pods from the water, let them drain, dust them inside with ground cloves, cinnamon and allspice, fill them with the filling so they regain their original shape and sew them carefully
6. Arrange them in a stone crock, pour over them all the spices boiled and cooled vinegar, cover the crock well and set in a cold place
7. The proportion of individual components of the filling should be adjusted according to individual taste

title: Naložená rajská jablíčka na kyselo (Pickled Tomatoes in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes of medium size
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash, dry, and salt the tomatoes
2. Let stand for about 4 hours
3. Boil good vinegar and when cooled, pour over the tomatoes and let stand until the next day
4. On the second day, drain the vinegar, boil it again, and when cooled pour over the tomatoes
5. On the third day, boil fresh vinegar
6. Place tomatoes in jars and pour the fresh boiled and cooled vinegar over them until the jars are about two inches from full
7. Stand the jars close together in water so they do not bump each other during cooking, and let them boil in steam for about five minutes
8. Remove jars to table, attach rubber seals, tighten lids well, and return to the same water and let cool completely
9. Stored this way, pickled tomatoes will keep for over two years and remain fresh even if air reaches them

title: Naložená skopová kýta (Leg of Mutton with Cream Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- onion
- cloves
- allspice
- pepper
- bay leaf
- ginger
- garlic
- bacon
- lemon peel
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and salt the leg of mutton
2. Boil one and a half cups vinegar with two cups water
3. Add one large sliced onion, six cloves, ten grains of allspice, ten peppercorns, bay leaf, a piece of ginger, and two garlic cloves
4. Cover and boil for fifteen minutes
5. Remove from heat and let cool
6. Place the salted leg in a deep container
7. When the liquid cools, pour it over the leg
8. Keep in cold place for five to six days in winter, two to three days in summer, turning once or twice during this time
9. When ready to roast, place on a board and lard with bacon
10. Place on baking pan
11. Pour about half the marinade and one cup water under it
12. Add a piece of lemon peel and a piece of butter on top
13. Roast until half done
14. Pour five to six tablespoons of good thick sour cream over it
15. Turn once during roasting and baste as needed
16. When done, transfer to a dish

title: Naložené Cibulky (Pickled Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- small white onions
- salt
- water
- sweet milk
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. Peel small white onions and salt them slightly.
2. Boil them for ten minutes in equal parts water and sweet milk.
3. Remove and let them drain.
4. Pack them into jars and pour spiced hot vinegar over them, but do not add any new spices that would darken them.
5. Screw shut while still hot.

title: Naložené Houby (Pickled Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fresh mushrooms
- cold water
- vinegar
- onion
- peppercorns
- spices
- salt
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the stem off young fresh mushrooms and place them in cold water.
2. Pour vinegar into a pot and add one onion sliced thin, several peppercorns, and spices.
3. Salt it and bring to a boil.
4. Add the mushrooms and let them boil for five minutes.
5. Remove the mushrooms and let them drain.
6. Pack them into bottles and pour cooled wine vinegar over them in which you have boiled several peppercorns and several cloves.
7. Screw tightly and store in a cool, dry place.

title: Naložené hovězí s hlávkou (Corned Beef with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately three pounds corned beef
- water
- cabbage

## Directions

1. Wash a piece of about three pounds of corned beef in hot water, place it in a pot, pour water over it until it is submerged, bring it to a boil and skim.

title: Naložené hovězí s hlávkou (Corned Beef with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds corned beef
- 1 head of cabbage
- 4 to 5 potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Rinse a piece of corned beef, about three pounds, in hot water. Place it in a pot, pour water over it until submerged, bring to a boil and skim off the foam. Cover the pot well and let it simmer over gentle heat.
2. After cooking for about 1.5 hours, prepare the cabbage: Quarter the head of cabbage, remove the core, place in cold water and let it sit for about a quarter hour. Drain well on a sieve and place into the pot with the meat, along with 4 to 5 potatoes.
3. After a quarter hour, add 4 to 5 peeled larger potatoes and let cook until the potatoes are tender. Remove the meat onto a platter and arrange the cooked vegetables around it.

title: Naložené Okůrčičky na Sladko (Sweet Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- small cucumbers
- water
- wine vinegar
- sugar
- salt
- alum powder
- cinnamon bark
- whole cloves

## Directions

1. Take small cucumbers, the smaller the better.
2. Pour boiling water over them and let them sit for 4 hours.
3. Boil wine vinegar with sugar and spices: for one gallon of vinegar add one cup of sugar, one cup of salt, one teaspoon of alum powder (which helps preserve color and firmness), one ounce of broken cinnamon bark, and one spoon of whole cloves.
4. Let everything boil for about 15 minutes.
5. Remove the cucumbers from the water and pack them into jars.
6. Pour the hot vinegar and spices over them and screw the jars shut while hot.

title: Naložené vejce (Pickled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dozen eggs
- Two cups of vinegar
- Six to eight peppercorns
- A piece of ginger
- About four cloves
- A slice of garlic

## Directions

1. Boil two dozen eggs until hard and peel them carefully without breaking them
2. Place them in a stone jar
3. Take two cups of vinegar and add six to eight peppercorns, a piece of ginger, about four cloves, and a slice of garlic
4. Boil this mixture for about five minutes
5. Pour the boiling mixture over the eggs
6. Store in a cool place
7. Use after two weeks

title: Naložené vepřové nožičky (Pickled Pig's Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- onion
- spices

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the pig's feet well
2. Place them in a pot
3. Pour enough water over them so they are completely covered
4. Let them cook
5. After about one hour of cooking, salt them and let them continue cooking until tender
6. Pig's feet require four to five hours to cook completely
7. Arrange them in a clay pot
8. Pour boiling vinegar over them, simmered with onion and all spices
9. Let stand for two days

title: Naložený Kopr (Pickled Dill)
body:
## Ingredients

- young dill leaves
- wine vinegar
- peppercorns
- cloves

## Directions

1. Place young washed dill leaves into a clean pot.
2. Add enough wine vinegar to completely cover them.
3. Add several peppercorns and cloves.
4. Let it boil for several minutes.
5. Remove from heat and let it cool.
6. Pour into jars, seal well, and store in a dry, cool place.
7. The dill must always be completely covered with vinegar; if it dries out, add some boiled vinegar.

title: Nejjemnější Creme (Most Delicate Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken gizzards
- best cream
- vanilla, coffee, tea, chocolate or similar flavorings
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean chicken gizzards thoroughly, rinse them, and dry them slowly
2. When ready to use, crush them coarsely
3. Heat the best cream with various suitable additions such as vanilla, coffee, tea, or chocolate, sweetened well and not too hot
4. Pour over the crushed gizzards, which gradually dissolve
5. Proceed as with other cooked creams
6. Serve in the same containers in which it was made

title: New Potatoes (Nové brambory)
body:
## Ingredients

- young potatoes
- fresh water
- salt
- caraway
- fresh parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Scrape young potatoes and wash them well in cold water
2. Place them in a pot and pour fresh water over them
3. Add salt, caraway, and a piece of green parsley
4. When cooked, strain the water from them
5. Remove the parsley and turn the potatoes out onto a dish
6. Leave them uncovered for about five minutes in the back of the stove
7. Then dress them with fresh hot butter

title: Nougats (Století koláčky)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound fresh butter
- one pound fine sugar
- grated zest from one lemon
- fifteen egg yolks
- one-quarter pound peeled grated almonds
- a little ground flower
- salt
- one pound white dry flour
- preserved sour cherries
- egg yolks for brushing
- finely chopped almonds
- ground sugar

## Directions

1. Beat one pound fresh butter until foamy
2. Mix in one pound fine sugar, grated lemon zest, and fifteen egg yolks one by one
3. Add one-quarter pound peeled grated almonds, a little ground flower, and a pinch of salt
4. Mix well and add one pound white dry flour
5. Stir for half an hour
6. Drop by spoonfuls in mounds the size of a walnut on a clean buttered baking sheet, spacing them well apart
