4. When cooled, add one glass of wine, one tablespoon of mustard, a small teaspoon of crushed sugar, and pepper
5. Add four beaten egg yolks
6. Place in boiling water and beat with a whisk until it froths

title: Omáčka k rybě (Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of chopped parsley
- egg yolks from three eggs
- red pepper
- half a teaspoon of salt
- one spoon of vinegar
- half a cup of fresh heated butter
- one spoon of finely chopped small pickles

## Directions

1. Place one spoonful of chopped parsley in a bowl and pound it into a paste
2. Add egg yolks from three eggs and mix well
3. Season with red pepper
4. Add half a teaspoon of salt
5. Thin with one spoon of vinegar
6. Gradually mix in half a cup of fresh heated butter
7. Add one spoon of finely chopped small pickles

title: Omáčka k studenému masu (Cold Meat Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- half a coffee cup of fine olive oil
- three cleaned sardines, finely chopped
- one spoon of finely chopped parsley
- two spoons of fine mustard
- one spoon of ground sugar
- a knife point of ground white pepper
- one coffee cup of wine vinegar
- a little water
- a knife point of salt

## Directions

1. Push four hard-boiled egg yolks through a wire sieve
2. Add to this half a coffee cup of fine olive oil and stir until a fine paste is formed
3. Add three cleaned, finely chopped sardines, one spoon of finely chopped parsley, two spoons of fine mustard, one spoon of ground sugar, a knife point of ground white pepper, one coffee cup of wine vinegar, a little water and a knife point of salt
4. Mix well
5. Serve with ham, smoked tongue, cold roast or fish
6. This sauce will keep in the cold for eight days or longer

title: Omáčka k Žeberkám (Sauce for Chops)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- garlic
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- rose roux
- broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop onion, garlic, bay leaf, and marjoram
2. Stew briefly in rose roux
3. Cook with broth and season with lemon juice
4. This sauce is excellent with veal chops

title: Omáčka kaparová (Caper Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter beef broth
- vinegar
- roux
- capers

## Directions

1. Mix beef broth with vinegar and roux
2. Simmer
3. Strain
4. Chop capers
5. Mix in
6. Simmer and strain the sauce

title: Omáčka koprová (Dill Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- white flour
- beef broth
- white wine vinegar
- dill leaves
- sour cream or sweet cream

## Directions

1. Make a white thin roux from fresh butter and a little white flour
2. Mix it with as much beef broth as you need sauce
3. Add a little white wine vinegar and let it boil
4. When ready to serve, add finely chopped dill leaves and several tablespoons of good sour cream to the sauce
5. If you do not have sour cream, you can use half a cupful of sweet cream, but you must add a little vinegar

title: Omáčka koprová, bílá (White Dill Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter liter of beef broth
- vinegar
- salt
- roux or three tablespoons of flour
- dill, finely chopped
- quarter liter of good sour cream
- two raw egg yolks

## Directions

1. Simmer a quarter liter of beef broth with vinegar and salt
2. Add roux or flour, stirring so it is evenly mixed
3. Strain everything
4. Finely chop the dill, add it to a quarter liter of good sour cream in which two raw egg yolks have been mixed
5. Pour to the sauce

title: Omáčka kyselá, houbová (Sour Mushroom Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter beef broth
- vinegar
- roux
- pepper
- dried mushrooms
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cook half liter beef broth with vinegar, roux and pepper
2. Wash dried mushrooms and cook in salted water
3. When cooked, add to the sauce and cook a short time longer

title: Omáčka marjánková (Marjoram Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter beef broth
- five cloves of garlic
- salt
- pinch of marjoram
- pepper
- roux

## Directions

1. Add five cloves of garlic rubbed in salt to half liter beef broth
2. Add a pinch of marjoram that has been rubbed in the hand and a little pepper
3. Cook with roux and strain

title: Omáčka Meruňková neb Broskvová (Apricot or Peach Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of apricot or peach preserve
- 1 cupful of white wine
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- rind from half a lemon

## Directions

1. Put 1 cupful of apricot or peach preserve into a small pot
2. Slowly pour 1 cupful of white wine over it
3. Add a piece of whole cinnamon and rind from half a lemon
4. Place over heat and stir constantly
5. When it begins to boil, remove from heat
6. Strain through a fine sieve, sweeten to taste, cover with a lid and let stand in a warm place

title: Omáčka na salát (Salad Dressing)
body:
## Ingredients

- three egg yolks
- one teaspoon of mustard
- two teaspoons of salt
- one half spoon of paprika
- two spoons of sugar
- two spoons of heated butter or good olive oil
- half a cup of sour cream
- one half cup of hot vinegar
- three egg whites, half-beaten

## Directions

1. Take three egg yolks, one teaspoon of mustard, two teaspoons of salt, one half spoon of paprika, two spoons of sugar, two spoons of heated butter or good olive oil, half a cup of sour cream, one half cup of hot vinegar and three egg whites that have been half-beaten
2. Mix everything together in order as written
3. Cook over low heat in a double boiler, similar to how oat meal is cooked, until it thickens like sauce
4. Pour into a sauce dish and allow to cool
5. This preparation will keep up to 14 days in a cool place and is excellent on head lettuce, potatoes, cooked beans in pods, cauliflower, etc.

title: Omáčka na saláty (Salad Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled egg yolks, grated and rubbed
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- mustard to taste

## Directions

1. Combine grated hard-boiled egg yolks with vinegar, oil, and salt
2. Add mustard to taste
3. Use to dress watercress or head lettuce

title: Omáčka okurková (Cucumber Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter liter of beef broth
- pepper
- pale roux
- vinegar
- pickled cucumber

## Directions

1. Mix a quarter liter of beef broth with pepper, pale roux and vinegar
2. Simmer everything
3. Cut the pickled cucumber either thinly into slices or into cubes
4. Simmer for a while longer

title: Omáčka papriková (Paprika Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of butter
- sliced bacon
- onion
- herb roots and vegetables
- several mushrooms
- two pods of paprika
- several grains of spice
- half liter broth
- lemon juice
- pale roux
- several tablespoons cream
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry a piece of butter, sliced bacon, onion, herb roots, vegetables, several mushrooms, two pods of paprika and several grains of spice
2. Pour over half liter broth
3. Add lemon juice and cook for half an hour
4. Strain the broth and remove fat
5. Add pale roux, several tablespoons cream and two egg yolks

title: Omáčka Pomerančová neb Citrónová (Orange or Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- rind from half an orange (or lemon)
- 2 cupfuls of boiling cream
- 4 spoonfuls of sugar
- 1 spoonful of flour
- a little cold milk
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut off the yellow rind from half an orange with a sharp knife so that no white rind remains on it, put into a pot
2. Pour 2 cupfuls of boiling cream over it, add 4 spoonfuls of sugar, cover the pot and let it cool
3. Mix 1 spoonful of flour with a little cold milk and 4 egg yolks
4. Add to this the cooled cream with the orange
5. Place over heat and stir until it begins to boil
6. Quickly remove the sauce from heat, strain through a sieve and serve immediately at the table
7. For lemon sauce, use lemon rind instead of orange rind. Otherwise the recipe is the same.

title: Omáčka s kaparami (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- brown broth
- lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon capers

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter in a pan and fry a little flour until it turns yellowish
2. Pour brown broth over it and cook well
3. Add a little lemon juice and a tablespoon of capers
4. Remove from heat and do not let it boil anymore

title: Omáčka s rumem (Brandy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lžíce másla
- 1 lžíce mouky
- 2 lžíce cukru
- vřelá voda
- 3 lžíce rumu nebo brandy
- muškátový oříšek

## Directions

1. Utři lžíci másla se lžicí mouky a dvěma lžícemi cukru.
2. Přilej pak tolik vřelá vody, aby to bylo přihoustlé a nech to varem obejít.
3. Přidej pak tři lžíce rumu neb brandy a nastrouhej do toho muškátového oříšku.

title: Omáčka sardelová (Sardine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sardines
- fresh butter
- beef broth
- brown butter roux
- lemon zest (optional)

## Directions

1. Rinse two sardines in cold water, remove the bones and mash them with fresh butter
2. Put into a pot, pour broth over it and cook
3. Brown with a brown butter roux and mix well
4. Let it cook and pour into a bowl
5. If desired, you can also add finely chopped lemon zest

title: Omáčka se sekaným masem (Sauce with Minced Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- meat remnants
- roux
- broth
- onion
- bay leaf
- spices
- bunch of vegetables
- vinegar
- lemon juice
- sour cucumbers or mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare sauce from roux with broth, onion, bay leaf, spices and a bunch of vegetables
2. Strain the sauce
3. Add vinegar and lemon juice
4. Add sliced sour cucumbers or mushrooms
5. Add cut meat and heat

title: Omáčka Smetanová s Mandlemi (Cream Almond Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of blanched sweet almonds
- 4 or 5 bitter almonds
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 spoonful of flour
- sugar to taste
- a piece of vanilla or lemon rind
- ½ cupfuls of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pound 2 ounces of blanched and peeled sweet almonds and about 4 or 5 bitter almonds in a mortar
2. Mix 4 egg yolks with 1 spoonful of flour
3. Sweeten this mixture to taste with sugar
4. Add a piece of vanilla or lemon rind to it
5. Mix in the pounded almonds and ½ cupfuls of sweet cream
6. Place over heat and stir constantly until it thickens, but do not let it boil
7. Remove the vanilla or lemon rind, pour the sauce into a dish and serve at the table

title: Omáčka Smetanová s Vanilkou (Cream Vanilla Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 coffee spoonful of flour
- a little cream
- 5 egg yolks
- sugar to taste
- a little vanilla extract
- 1 pint of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put 1 coffee spoonful of flour and a little cream into a pot, mix well
2. Add 5 egg yolks, mix everything well again
3. Sweeten to taste, add a little vanilla extract and 1 pint of sweet cream
4. Place over heat and stir slowly until it thickens, but it must not boil

title: Omáčka smržová (Morel Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh morels
- butter
- pepper
- parsley
- salt
- beef broth
- roux

## Directions

1. Slice fresh morels and braise until water evaporates
2. Add a piece of butter, pepper, green parsley and salt
3. Braise with the morels
4. Cook beef broth with roux and add to the morels

title: Omáčka vinní (Wine Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter white wine
- quarter liter vinegar and water
- sugar
- eighth liter good sour cream
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix half liter white wine with quarter liter vinegar and water
2. Cook for fifteen minutes
3. Add sugar and one eighth liter of good sour cream
4. Mix three egg yolks and cook for a short time

title: Omáčka Viňuá na Jiný Způsob (Wine Sauce Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoonful of white flour
- a little white wine
- 4 egg yolks
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- rind from half a lemon, finely cut
- 2 cupfuls of white wine
- ½ cupful of sugar

## Directions

1. Put a spoonful of white flour into a pot, add a little white wine and mix well
2. Add 4 egg yolks and mix again
3. Add a piece of whole cinnamon and finely cut rind from half a lemon
4. Pour 2 cupfuls of white wine over it, add ½ cupful of sugar
5. Place over heat and stir slowly until it thickens
6. When the sauce begins to boil, remove from heat
7. Strain through a sieve and serve immediately at the table

title: Omáčka z Brusnic (Cranberry Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- canned cranberries
- water
- wine
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- nutmeg
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook canned cranberries in water with wine, sugar, cinnamon, lemon zest, and nutmeg
2. Strain and thicken with flour

title: Omáčka z Bylin (Herb Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of fresh parsley
- spinach
- onion
- roux
- broth
- lemon juice
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Pound parsley, spinach, and onion in a mortar
2. Press through a cloth
3. Add roux to broth with salt and juice from half a lemon
4. Simmer for a quarter hour
5. Add herb juice and one egg yolk

title: Omáčka z mlíčí (Soft Roe Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three and one-half deciliters of beef broth
- four tablespoons of wine vinegar
- ground pepper
- four tablespoons of Provençal oil
- soft roe
- four grated egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix three and one-half deciliters of beef broth with four tablespoons of wine vinegar
2. Add a little ground pepper and four tablespoons of Provençal oil
3. Clean the soft roe of all membranes
4. Clean it
5. Rub it with oil and four grated egg yolks
6. Mix everything together
7. When it has cooled, pour it over the remaining fish

title: Omáčka z mrkve (Carrot Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots, grated
- three and one-half deciliters of beef broth
- sugar
- saffron
- salt
- roux
- parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Grate the whole carrot and squeeze the juice from it
2. Mix the juice with three and one-half deciliters of beef broth
3. Add a piece of sugar, a little saffron and salt
4. Mix in roux
5. Simmer and strain
6. Mix in finely chopped green parsley

title: Omáčka z Míšeňských Jablek (Meissen Apple Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Meissen apples
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- 3½ deciliters (spoonful) sour cream

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and place on a baking sheet with sugar, cinnamon, and lemon zest
2. Stew until soft
3. Press through a sieve into a pot
4. Add 3½ deciliters of sour cream
5. Add more sugar if not sweet enough
6. Mix well and place in cold location
7. For those who do not like sweet sauces, Austrian wine can be used instead of sour cream, and either sauce can be used with cold game

title: Omáčka z naložené okurky (Pickle Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled cucumber
- beef broth
- white wine vinegar
- green dill
- brown roux

## Directions

1. Slice the pickled cucumber into thin pieces
2. Put it in a small pot and pour a little beef broth and some white wine vinegar over it
3. Add a piece of finely chopped green dill and let everything boil
4. Brown the sauce with a brown roux, mix it well and let it cook

title: Omáčka z rajských jablek (Tomato Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter liter of beef broth
- three fresh or pickled tomatoes or tomato preserve
- two finely chopped onions
- eight peppercorns
- four grains of allspice
- two cloves
- roux
- sugar
- wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Place three fresh, pickled, or preserved tomatoes in a quarter liter of beef broth
2. Add two finely chopped onions
3. Add eight peppercorns, four grains of allspice, two cloves
4. Add roux and a piece of sugar
5. A little wine can also be mixed in

title: Omáčka z vinních jahůdek (Sauce from Grape Berries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- berries from sweet grapes
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- orange peel
- clove
- sugar
- toast from rolls
- boiling liquid

## Directions

1. Remove the berries from the grapes
2. Stew for about half an hour with cinnamon, lemon peel, orange peel, and clove
3. Add sugar and toast from rolls
4. Pour with the boiling liquid
5. Strain through a sieve

title: Omáčka z čerstvých hub (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- beef broth
- pale butter roux or sour cream and flour
- clove

## Directions

1. Clean and chop fresh mushrooms and cook them for about a quarter hour in beef broth
2. Brown the sauce with pale butter roux or thicken it with sour cream and flour
3. Season with a little clove
4. Let it cook and pour into a bowl with the mushrooms

title: Omáčka z červeného vína (Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 koflík červeného vína
- 1/2 koflíku vody
- hrst pěkných velkých očištěných hrozinek
- kousek celé skořice
- kousek citrónové kůry
- cukr (dle potřeby)
- čerstvé máslo
- trocha bílé mouky

## Directions

1. Dej do hrnku koflík červeného vína a půl koflíku vody, přidej hrst pěkných velkých očištěných hrozinek, kousek celé skořice, kousek citrónové kůry a tolik cukru, aby to bylo dostatečně sladké.
2. Nech to čtvrt hodiny vařit.
3. Udělej z čerstvého másla a z trochu bílé mouky bledou jíšku.
4. Proceď víno skrze sejtko, zapraž je tou jíškou, nech ještě čtvrt hodiny povařit a proceď skrze sejtko do mísečky.

title: Omáčka ze chmelnlčku (Hop Sprout Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh hop sprouts
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- beef broth
- roux

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the fresh hop sprouts
2. Pour vinegar and water over them
3. Braise them, drain
4. Chop them
5. Make a brown roux
6. Drain beef broth and mix it with the water in which the hop sprouts were cooked
7. Caramelize a piece of sugar and add the chopped hop sprouts to it

title: Omáčka ze Slanečka (Sprat Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 sprat heads
- half liter beef broth
- pale roux
- sour cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Put sprat heads in a pot with half liter of beef broth
2. Let cook until soft
3. Add pale roux and strain
4. Alternatively, sour cream and flour can be used instead of roux, then simmer

title: Omáčka ze Srstek (Berry Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- wine
- water
- sugar
- onion
- thyme

## Directions

1. Stew the berries until almost all water evaporates
2. Strain, removing seeds and skins
3. Add wine, water, a piece of sugar, a piece of chopped onion, and thyme
4. When cooked, strain again
5. Add more sugar if not sweet enough

title: Omáčka ze suchých hub (Dried Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried mushrooms
- fresh water
- meat broth
- butter roux
- clove

## Directions

1. Rinse dried mushrooms nicely in fresh water
2. Put them in a pot and pour meat broth over them and cook until soft
3. Strain the broth and chop the mushrooms finely
4. Pour the broth they were cooked in back over them
5. Brown with butter roux and add a pinch of clove
6. Let it cook a little longer
7. If you want the sauce sour, add a little white wine vinegar

title: Omáčka ze suchých hub (Sauce from Dried Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dried mushrooms
- pale roux
- two tablespoons beef broth
- parsley

## Directions

1. Scald dried mushrooms and drain the water
2. Finely chop the mushrooms
3. Add pale roux and two tablespoons beef broth
4. Cook the sauce
5. Add chopped parsley if desired

title: Omáčka ze šalvěje (Sage Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sage
- three and one-half deciliters of beef broth
- sauce from veal roast or roux from 30 grams of butter
- juice from half a lemon

## Directions

1. Place a piece of sage in three and one-half deciliters of beef broth
2. Add sauce from veal roast; if not available, use roux made from 30 grams of butter
3. Strain
4. Add juice from half a lemon

title: Omáčka ze žampionů (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced mushrooms
- butter
- lemon juice
- brown roux
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Stew the sliced mushrooms in butter on thin slices
2. Add lemon juice
3. Add brown roux
4. Season with pepper
5. Pour in broth
6. Simmer until cooked

title: Omáčka ze žampionů (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms sliced into strips
- butter
- lemon juice
- brown roux
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Braise mushrooms sliced into strips in butter
2. Add lemon juice and brown roux
3. Season with pepper
4. Add broth and cook

title: Omáčka zemáková - studená (Cold Potato Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two boiled cold potatoes
- wine vinegar
- water
- good beef broth
- butter or olive oil
- fish roe from herring

## Directions

1. Grate two boiled cold potatoes
2. Pour in wine vinegar
3. Add 1/8 liter water and the same amount of good beef broth
4. Mix well
5. Add two spoonfuls of melted fresh butter or two spoonfuls of olive oil
6. Remove the fish roe from herring of all membranes
7. Rub through
8. Add to sauce and mix well
9. Sauce is served with leftovers of roasted meat

title: Omáčka česneková (Garlic Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three cloves of garlic, chopped
- beef broth
- pepper
- roux (pink)

## Directions

1. Chop the three cloves of garlic
2. Simmer in beef broth with pepper
3. Add pink roux, simmer for a while
4. Strain

title: Omáčka česneková (Garlic Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- white flour
- beef broth
- 2 garlic cloves
- butter
- marjoram (optional)

## Directions

1. Make a roux from fresh butter and a little white flour
2. Pour beef broth over it, salt and let it cook
3. Take about two garlic cloves, rub them well with a piece of good butter
4. When ready to serve, put the garlic butter into the sauce and mix well
5. If desired, a little pounded marjoram can be added

title: Omáčka žlutá cibulová (Yellow Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 onion
- beef broth
- 2 slices white bread
- butter
- saffron
- cumin

## Directions

1. Chop half an onion finely, place in a pot, pour beef broth over it and let it simmer briefly.
2. In another pot, place two slices of white bread soaked in water and squeezed dry, a piece of fresh butter, a little dried saffron ground to powder, mix well.
3. Pour the broth with onion over this mixture, mix well and let it simmer a little longer.
4. Finally brown the sauce with a roux and let it simmer a little longer.
5. If desired, you may also add a little ground caraway.

title: Onion Soup with Milk (Cibulová polévka s mlékem)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- small white onions
- butter
- good milk
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- bread, cut into cubes and toasted

## Directions

1. Peel small white onions and cut into small pieces
2. Stew in butter until soft and completely white
3. Place on a sieve so they drain completely
4. Take as much good milk as needed for the soup
5. Bring milk to a boil, salt and pepper it
6. Add the onions and bring everything to a boil together
7. After boiling, strain through a sieve
8. Beat a few egg yolks into it
9. Pour the soup prepared this way onto a plate into which bread cut into cubes and toasted has been placed

title: Orange Ice Cream with Cream (Pomerančová zmrzlina se smetanou)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- More than a liter of sour cream
- Quarter kilo of sugar
- Zest of two oranges

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs
2. Beat them well
3. Pour in more than a liter of sour cream
4. Add a quarter kilo of sugar
5. Grate zest from two oranges
6. Bring everything to a boil
7. Prepare as other ice creams

title: Orange Shavings (Pomerančové hoblovačky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several oranges
- sugar
- orange zest
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Grate oranges onto sugar
2. Finely chop orange zest
3. Add orange juice
4. Make an icing with ground sugar
5. Spread onto wafers
6. Bake slowly as with other wafer cookies

title: Oranžová huspenina (Orange Preserve)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three to four sweet oranges
- fine sugar
- isinglass
- water

## Directions

1. Rub three to four sweet oranges onto fine sugar
2. Crush isinglass and add with water
3. Cook everything together until half has boiled away
4. Add crushed sugar to taste, stirring constantly
5. Strain through clean thin cloth onto a dish
6. Let cool

title: Oranžová Paštička (Orange Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- orange water
- orange peel

## Directions

1. Cook sugar with orange water
2. Add sugar rubbed with orange peel in abundance
3. Cook everything to the grain stage
4. Proceed as with the previous pastes

title: Ordinary Clear Beef Soup (Beef Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beef
- fresh cold water
- salt
- vegetables in season: celery, celery leaves, parsley root, parsley leaves, piece of onion, leek, cabbage, piece of carrot, piece of turnip or cauliflower
- alternatively in winter or early spring: small handful of peas, clove of garlic, pinch of caraway, piece of onion, pinch of dried mushrooms
- optionally: piece of beef liver fried in butter with onion, or raw beef liver
- beef kidney and bones

## Directions

1. Rinse 2 pounds of beef in clean cold water and pound it with a wooden mallet
2. Do not pound the meat before rinsing, as it will lose much of its juices
3. Place the meat in a pot filled with clean fresh water and let it simmer slowly
4. When the water stops foaming, salt it and add vegetables proportionally to the amount of meat
5. In summer add: celery, celery leaves, parsley root, parsley leaves, piece of onion, leek, cabbage, piece of carrot, piece of turnip or cauliflower
6. If vegetables are not available, as in winter or early spring, add: small handful of peas, clove of garlic, pinch of caraway, piece of onion, pinch of dried mushrooms
7. These ingredients may be varied according to taste or preference
8. For improvement, optionally add a piece of beef liver fried in butter with onion, or raw beef liver
9. Beef kidney and bones will also enhance the flavor
10. Once vegetables are added, let the meat simmer slowly for about 2.5 to 3 hours, but not vigorously as this makes the soup unpalatable
11. If water evaporates during cooking, replenish with boiling water so the soup continues to boil without interruption
12. When the meat is tender, strain the soup into a smaller pot and add garnishes according to the following recipes
13. This recipe serves 6 people

title: Osmažený Chléb (Fried Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- stale white bread
- three eggs
- half a cup of milk
- salt
- four tablespoons lard

## Directions

1. Cut stale white bread into slices.
2. Beat three eggs, add half a cup of milk and salt.
3. Put four tablespoons of lard in an iron pan and when it is heated, dip the bread in the egg mixture and fry nicely to a golden color on both sides.
4. Serve hot with syrup or honey.

title: Ostružinový kompot (Blackberry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Blackberries
- Water
- Sugar
- A piece of cinnamon
- Oranges
- Red wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Let rinsed and drained blackberries cook in water with sugar and a piece of cinnamon
2. Arrange them on a dish and pour thickly boiled juice over them
3. Sprinkle with sugar and line with oranges cut into thin pieces
4. Alternatively, you can use equal parts water and red wine
5. Instead of cinnamon, you can also use lemon peel

title: Oukrop (Garlic Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread
- Butter
- 3 cloves garlic
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut bread into small slices
2. Fry in butter until golden
3. Crush 3 cloves garlic with butter and salt
4. Boil briefly with water
5. Pour over the fried bread
6. Alternatively, onion may be used instead of garlic

title: Ovar (Head and Neck Stew)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of pig's head
- piece of pig's neck
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Take a piece of the head and neck of the pig and boil them.
2. While still hot, cut them into neat pieces and serve with grated horseradish.

title: Ovoce scukrňatělé na hnědo (Brown Sugared Fruit)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- sugar
- lemon juice
- preserved nuts, cherries, sour cherries, apples, pears or other fruit

## Directions

1. Roast chestnuts, peel them, and pierce them on thin pointed sticks on both sides
2. Chestnuts can be pierced on these sticks in any quantity desired
3. Besides chestnuts, preserved nuts from sugar can be cleaned, cut lengthwise and placed on sticks either in pieces or whole
4. The same applies to preserved sour cherries, cherries, apples, pears and other fruit
5. Boil the finest sugar until it thickens completely, then add a few drops of lemon juice
6. Dip the pierced fruit into this prepared liquid, immediately withdraw it and turn it around in the juice
7. Repeat this as long as the sugar thickens completely and hardens and dries on the fruit
8. When this is done, place or better lay the sticks with fruit in a place where they can dry comfortably without touching each other
9. Such sugared fruit can be used for decoration and assembly of various columns, rocks, etc.

title: Ovocná limonáda (Fruit Lemonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cold water
- raspberry, strawberry, cherry, currant or other fruit juice
- lemon juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. To a quart of fresh cold water, add one cup of raspberry, strawberry, cherry, currant or other fruit juice
2. Add strained juice from two lemons
3. Sweeten adequately with pounded sugar and mix well

title: Ovocná omáčka (Fruit Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- preserved or freshly pressed fruit juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the fruit juice with sugar while constantly stirring
2. Use for flour dishes

title: Ovocné knedlíky (Fruit Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- mouky na kvart
- dvě lžičky prášku na pečení
- oloupaná a bez pecky oloupaná broskev na jeden kousek
- cukr
- skořice
- máslo nebo smetana

## Directions

1. Udělej těsto jako na biskety: na kvart mouky dej dvě lžičky prášku.
2. Nadělej kousky těsta, rozválej do kulatá a dej po jedné oloupaté a bez pecky zbavené broskvi do každého kousku.
3. Zavij do kulatá a kladu na plechovou mísu jednu vedle druhé těsně.
4. Když je mísa plná, posyp vrchem cukrem a skořicí.
5. Nalej třetinu mísy vařící vody pod knedlíky a hned je vstrč do trouby.
6. Druhou mísu použij k přikrytí.
7. Po desíti minutách sejmi vrchní mísu a nech knedlíky povrchu zčervenat asi za 10 nebo 15 minut.
8. Podávej ihned na stůl se sladkou podlívkou, máslem nebo smetanou.

title: Ovocný dort (Fruit Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of sugar
- one coffee cup of butter
- seven egg yolks
- half coffee cup of black syrup (molasses)
- half teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in warm water
- seven egg whites
- one quart of sifted flour
- one pound of small currants
- one pound of large raisins with seeds removed
- half pound of citron chopped finely
- approximately one-quarter coffee cup of flour
- one pound of sliced almonds
- one teaspoon of grated nutmeg
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- half teaspoon of cloves
- half teaspoon of mixed spices

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar with the butter
2. add seven egg yolks
3. add the molasses mixed with the baking soda dissolved in warm water
4. beat the seven egg whites and add them
5. fold in the sifted flour
6. clean the small currants and large raisins and chop the citron finely
7. mix all together and dust with about one-quarter coffee cup of flour and mix again so the flour coats everything (this is done so the raisins do not sink to the bottom)
8. add to the batter along with the sliced almonds
9. season with nutmeg, cinnamon, cloves, and mixed spices
10. grease the pan with butter and line with paper, grease the paper as well
11. pour the batter into the pan so it is about three-quarters full
12. bake in a moderately warm oven for two hours
13. if it bakes too quickly, cover with paper
14. when baked, turn out and coat with white icing

title: Ovocný ocet (Fruit Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good or bad fruit, green or rotten fruit
- warm water (not boiling)

## Directions

1. Crush the fruit intended for vinegar production in large vessels
2. Pour warm—but not boiling—water over it; do not use too much water as the vinegar would be very weak
3. Allow the fruit pulp made in this manner to rest in special vessels for 2-3 weeks, stirring thoroughly several times daily so the layers are completely worked through

title: Ovocný ocet (Fruit Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good or poor fruit
- green or rotten fruit
- warm water
- honey (optional)

## Directions

1. Crush the fruit designated for vinegar production in large containers and pour warm—but not boiling—water over it; do not use too much water as the vinegar would be very weak.
2. Let the pulp made this way lie in special containers for two to three weeks, stirring well several times daily so that the layers work through completely.
3. After this time, press the pulp thoroughly, and transfer the obtained liquid into barrels and containers in a warm room, in such a way that dust and other impurities cannot contaminate it.
4. Cover these containers with light cloth for this purpose.
5. The warmer the room in which the expressed liquid is stored, the faster the vinegar will develop, as heat strongly promotes fermentation and all kinds of decomposition.
6. To obtain vinegar quite quickly, it is good to warm a portion of the pulp over moderate heat every few days and pour the warmed portion into the barrel, which greatly speeds up and promotes fermentation.
7. The warming must not occur rapidly and too strongly, as boiling damages the fruit liquid and one would wait in vain for good vinegar.
8. To make the fruit vinegar have particularly delicate taste and beautiful color, it is good to add a little honey to the fruit liquid, preferably adding the honey always to that fruit liquid which is being warmed.
9. During fermentation the fruit liquid throws off a quantity of impurities, which can be removed several times daily with a flat perforated spoon, and this is repeated until the liquid becomes completely clear and no longer clouds, nor throws off any impurities.
10. As soon as the liquid has cleared, it gradually transforms into vinegar, which soon acquires a pleasant sharpness and tartness, and then it is time to draw it off and decant it into good, odor-free and clean barrels, and the decanted vinegar is stored in a dry, well-ventilated and also cold room, because in heat it readily spoils.

title: Ovocný rosol (Fruit Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe fruit
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Fruit jelly is juice from ripe, but not overripe fruit, cooked with sugar
2. First cook the fruit with a little water so it releases its juice
3. Strain the juice either through a thick linen strainer or a bag; if you first strain through a strainer and then through a bag, the jelly will be clearer
4. The jelly must be cooked on a strong fire and since it rises high during cooking, do not put much in the pot; when it finishes cooking, it does not rise as much and you must watch that it does not overcook; as soon as it thickens, it must be put immediately into jars, otherwise it will jell in the pot
5. After cooling, first place on top a piece of paper soaked in rum and close the jars
6. During cooking, stir constantly and carefully collect the foam, otherwise the jelly would become cloudy, would thicken on the jarring place, and would not keep long

title: Ovocný Syrup (Fruit Syrup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of strawberries, raspberries, currants, and other fruit
- one and a half times as much sugar as juice

## Directions

1. For each part of juice, add one and a half times as much sugar
2. Boil everything together several times
3. Skim the foam
4. Let cool
5. Pour the finished syrup into bottles

title: Ořechová nádivka (Walnut Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 deciliters boiled sugar or good syrup or pleasant honey
- finely chopped peeled walnuts
- cinnamon
- allspice
- vanilla
- cloves
- orange zest
- lemon zest
- a tablespoon of good rum and wine

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar, syrup, or honey with constant stirring
2. Add finely chopped peeled walnuts until everything thickens
3. Season with cinnamon, allspice, vanilla, cloves, orange zest, and lemon zest
4. Add a tablespoon of good rum and wine

title: Ořechová nádivka - Fifth Method (Walnut Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dek butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 10 dek sugar
- vanilla flavoring
- 2 dek finely chopped citron peel
- 10 dek finely shredded or chopped walnuts
- raisins
- several tablespoons of good wine

## Directions

1. Rub butter with egg yolks and sugar
2. Add vanilla flavoring
3. Add finely chopped citron peel, walnuts, and raisins
4. Mix with several tablespoons of good wine

title: Ořechová nádivka - Fourth Method (Walnut Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 14 dek sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 25 dek finely shredded peeled walnuts
- vanilla
- lemon zest
- 4 tablespoons rum
- approximately 5 dek raisins

## Directions

1. Boil sugar in water until completely thick
2. Add finely shredded peeled walnuts
3. Add crushed vanilla
4. Add finely shredded or chopped lemon zest
5. Add rum and raisins

title: Ořechová nádivka - Second Method (Walnut Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dek sugar
- 1 deciliter water
- 20 dek finely crushed peeled walnuts
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- orange zest
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar with water
2. Add finely crushed peeled walnuts, cinnamon, and finely chopped lemon and orange zest
3. Add some vanilla for pleasant taste

title: Ořechová nádivka - Sixth Method (Walnut Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dek small peeled walnuts
- egg whites
- 2 eggs
- 10 dek sugar

## Directions

1. Crush the small peeled walnuts
2. Mix with egg whites and 2 eggs to which sugar has been added

title: Ořechová nádivka - Third Method (Walnut Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 dek finely chopped walnuts
- boiling sour cream
- 2 deciliters honey
- 2 tablespoons rum
- vanilla
- fine spices

## Directions

1. Scald the finely chopped walnuts with boiling sour cream
2. Add honey
3. Add rum, vanilla, and fine spices

title: Ořechová nádivka do cukrovinek (Walnut Filling for Candies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- 1/8 liter water
- 20 dekagrams (13 lots) walnut kernels, beaten with cream
- a little cinnamon or vanilla
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) candied lemon peel

## Directions

1. Clarify the sugar in the water
2. Add the walnuts beaten with cream, a little cinnamon or vanilla, and the candied lemon peel
3. Mix everything well
4. Use the mixture as a filling for various candies

title: Ořechové kopečky (Hazelnut Mounds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams fresh hazelnuts
- 9 dekagrams pounded sugar
- 7-8 egg whites
- sugar
- pounded cinnamon
- powdered sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Blanch and peel fresh hazelnuts, chop coarsely, and toast with 9 dekagrams pounded sugar
2. Beat 7-8 egg whites to stiff peaks
3. Mix with sugar and pounded cinnamon and the prepared nuts
4. Form small mounds with a spoon on sugar-sprinkled paper
5. Bake in a warm oven

title: Ořechové Makrony (Walnut Macaroons)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams walnuts
- 500 grams sugar
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Select walnuts, crack them, remove the kernels, scald and peel
2. Crush 500 grams walnuts and mix with egg whites and more than 500 grams beaten sugar
3. Mix everything into stiff dough, place on wafers on paper and bake slowly

title: Oříškový creme (Nut Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 deciliters boiling cream
- peeled, crushed or sugared nuts
- five egg yolks
- seven deka sugar

## Directions

1. Add peeled, crushed or sugared nuts to 3.5 deciliters of boiling cream
2. Strain everything and mix with five egg yolks and seven deka sugar

title: Oříškový dort (Hazel-Nut Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound shelled hazel-nut kernels
- half pound sifted sugar
- eight egg yolks
- four egg whites
- two tablespoons flour
- butter
- grated roll
- lemon or orange glaze
- preserved nuts or fruit for decoration

## Directions

1. Grate one-quarter pound of cleaned hazel-nut kernels
2. Place them on a dish with half pound sifted sugar
3. Add eight egg yolks and mix for at least half an hour until it froths and thickens
4. Gradually add stiff snow made from four egg whites
5. Add two tablespoons flour
6. Butter a form and dust it with grated and sifted roll
7. Pour the batter into the form and let the cake bake slowly
8. When cooled, turn it out and let it cool completely
9. Cover the cake with lemon or orange glaze
10. Let it dry in a lukewarm oven
11. Decorate with preserved nuts or fruit
12. The same method can be used for walnut cake

title: Paj s Jablkovými Povidly (Apple Custard Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- three coffee cups of cooked apples strained through a sieve
- one coffee cup of sugar
- six well-beaten eggs
- one quart of milk
- a little grated nutmeg
- dough

## Directions

1. Take three coffee cups of cooked apples strained through a sieve, one coffee cup of sugar, six well-beaten eggs, one quart of milk, and a little grated nutmeg
2. Mix everything together and pour onto prepared dough
3. When baked, spread with snow from egg white and place in the oven to dry

title: Paj se Zavareninou (Preserve Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough
- preserve
- jam or marmalade

## Directions

1. Line a form with dough
2. Make a thicker border
3. Fill it with some preserve, jam or marmalade
4. Cover with latticed strips of dough

title: Paj z Kyselé Smetany (Sour Cream Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sour cream
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup small raisins
- pinch of cinnamon
- clove
- nutmeg
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 pastry crust on a plate

## Directions

1. Take 1 cup sour cream, 1/2 cup sugar, 2 eggs, 1/2 cup small raisins, a pinch of cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, and 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
2. Mix everything well together
3. Pour onto prepared crust on a plate and bake
4. Make the pie in a single crust
5. It is good but lasts only one day

title: Paj Švestkový (Plum Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- dough
- two eggs
- three tablespoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Take picked and softly cooked plums and mix them well
2. Line a pie plate with dough
3. Spread with these plum preserves, about half the plate full, and bake
4. When baked, spread with snow from two eggs sweetened with three tablespoons of sugar
5. Return to the oven and leave there until the snow browns

title: Pavézky z mozku (Calf's Brain Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf brain
- salt
- pepper
- chives
- parsley
- lemon peel
- bread or rolls
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the brain thoroughly and poach it
2. Chop the brain very finely
3. Salt and season with ground pepper
4. Add finely chopped chives, finely chopped parsley, and a bit of lemon peel
5. Mix everything together
6. Cut thin slices from hardened bread or rolls
7. Spread generously with the brain mixture
8. Cover with another slice of bread or roll
9. Dip in beaten eggs
10. Coat in breadcrumbs
11. Fry in clarified butter

title: Pařenky na Jiný Způsob (Bread Puffs Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard rolls, buns, or white bread
- hot milk
- fresh butter
- poppy seed
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut hard rolls, buns, or white bread into cubes.
2. Place in a clay dish and pour over with hot milk; one cup of milk is sufficient for half a gallon of bread.
3. Cover with a metal plate and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour.
4. Remove, shake it together, turn out onto another dish, pour over with heated fresh butter, and sprinkle with poppy seed mixed with sugar.

title: Pařenky ze Suchého Chleba (Milk Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups milk
- about one cup good butter
- stale bread

## Directions

1. Bring two cups of milk to a boil; have about one cup of good butter heated.
2. Cut the bread into thin slices.
3. As the milk boils, add one slice at a time.
4. When sufficiently softened, place on a plate.
5. Pour over with a spoon of the heated butter.
6. Mix together ground poppy seed with a bit of sugar and sprinkle over the slices.
7. Do not add the bread to the milk all at once; add only three or four slices at a time.

title: Paštika z Holubů (Pigeon Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pastry dough
- Squabs
- Good broth
- Butter
- Spices
- Onion
- Lemon
- Squab gizzards and lungs
- Cooked milk
- Mushrooms
- Crayfish
- Salted water
- Good meat broth
- Champagne wine
- Butter
- Capers
- Egg yolks
- Flour

## Directions

1. Prepare pastry from the previously described dough
2. The day before preparation, select squabs, pluck them, clean them and cut into quarters, wash them
3. Place with some good broth, butter, spices on onion and lemon on a baking sheet and let braise
4. Let the braised squabs cool
5. Chop the uncooked gizzards and lungs, mix with cooked milk and chopped mushrooms
6. Cook crayfish in salted water, shell them and chop the meat
7. Place this mixture on the bottom of the pastry and layer everything together, cover with good meat broth
8. Cover the top with a dough lid and bake for at least an hour over mild heat
9. Pour a sauce made from pigeon broth, champagne wine, a little butter and capers mixed with egg yolks, cooked and sprinkled with flour over the baked pastry

title: Paštika z Hovězího Jazyku (Beef Tongue Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful beef tongue
- Salted water
- Butter pastry dough
- Butter
- Wine
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in salted water until done, skin it
2. When cooled, cut into small slices
3. Prepare pastry from butter dough
4. Place butter and similar items on the bottom as mentioned for pork tongue pastry
5. Prepare sauce from wine, butter, sugar and cinnamon
6. Cook the sauce, strain it
7. When the pastry is baked, pour the sauce over it

title: Paštika z husy (Goose Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Pâté dough

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as duck pâté.
2. Serve cold.

title: Paštika z kapouna (Capon Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough
- Capon
- Flour
- Butter
- Lard, finely chopped
- Onion, finely chopped
- Nutmeg flower, crushed
- Lemon zest, finely chopped
- Wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare a pâté from butter dough and bake it.
2. Prepare the capon as for ragout.
3. Brown some flour with butter, add the finely chopped lard and mix everything together.
4. Place pieces of capon on top, cover with finely chopped onion, crushed nutmeg flower, and finely chopped lemon zest.
5. Pour wine over, season with salt and pepper, and let it simmer.
6. Pour lemon juice over the finished pâté.

title: Paštika z Kapounu (Capon Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry dough
- Capon
- Flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake a pastry from butter dough
2. Prepare the capon as for ragout with a little flour and butter browned

title: Paštika z kuřat (Chicken Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- four to five pound chicken
- one spoonful of butter
- piece of chopped onion
- two bowls of hot water
- half pound of ham, cut into small pieces
- potatoes
- puff pastry
- salt
- pepper
- one egg white
- fresh parsley
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken into pieces at the joints
2. Put one spoonful of butter in a pot, and when the onion begins to turn yellow, put the chicken in the pot
3. Pour two bowls of hot water over it and let it stew
4. If desired, you can add half a pound of ham cut into small pieces
5. Trim and cut potatoes into squares, boil them for a quarter hour and drain them
6. When the chicken is stewed, cut the meat from the larger bones and remove them
7. Line a shallow two-quart dish with puff pastry
8. Lay a layer of the cut potatoes at the bottom, then a layer of meat
9. Place on top the pieces of pastry cut from the edges of the dish that protruded over the rim
10. Add a spoonful of chopped fresh parsley, salt, pepper, and a few small pieces of butter the size of a walnut
11. Cover with the top puff pastry, which you have slit in the middle
12. Press down and trim the edges
13. Brush the top with beaten egg white and bake in a hot oven for half an hour until the crust is fully baked
14. Meanwhile prepare the following sauce: Put a small spoonful of butter in a pot and when it melts, mix in two level spoonfuls of flour, stir but do not let it brown
15. Pour one bowl of the broth in which the chicken was cooked and one bowl of milk, stirring constantly until it begins to boil
16. Remove from heat, salt and pepper it, add the beaten yolk of one egg and one spoonful of finely chopped parsley
17. Pour this into the pie when it is baked, using a funnel through the hole made in the top pastry, or serve the sauce in a bowl so each person can help themselves
18. Serve hot at the table

title: Paštika ze pstruhů (Trout Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Trout
- Vinegar
- Onion
- Anchovies
- Capers
- Parsley
- Butter
- Flour
- Wine
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Lemon slices
- Salt
- Butter dough

## Directions

1. Clean the trout and cut them up, pour boiling vinegar over.
2. Stew onion, anchovies, capers, and parsley in butter, brown with flour, and add to the sauce.
3. Pour vinegar and wine with onion, cloves, bay leaf, and lemon slices over the trout, season with salt, and simmer until soft.
4. Place the pâté on a baking sheet lined with butter, line with butter dough, and bake well.

title: Paštika ze zvěřiny (Venison Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison
- Onion
- Bacon
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Butter dough
- Butter
- Egg yolk
- Flour for roux
- Good meat broth
- Wine vinegar
- Sugar
- Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Simmer the venison until tender, remove bones, add onion, bacon, cloves, and pepper.
2. Either pound the venison well or cut into pieces.
3. Spread butter on the bottom of pâté dough, add meat and forcemeat.
4. Brush edges with beaten egg, cover with pâté dough, and bake at strong heat with sauce.
5. For the sauce: make a brown roux with butter, add good meat broth and a little wine vinegar, add crushed sugar, chopped onion, crushed pepper and cloves, simmer and strain.
6. Add lemon slices, chopped mushrooms, capers, and a piece of butter, simmer everything together once more, and pour over the pâté through a sieve.

title: Paštika ze štiky (Pike Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike
- Pike liver
- Fish meat
- Melted butter
- Eggs
- Grated bread roll
- Vinegar
- Lemon slices
- Mushrooms, chopped
- Capers
- Lemon
- Crayfish tails
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Butter dough
- Egg yolk
- Strong broth
- Caper sauce with lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the pike, tear it apart, gut it, and cut into pieces.
2. Remove all bones from the meat, place meat in vinegar, and interlace with lemon slices.
3. Prepare forcemeat from pike liver, fish meat, melted butter, several eggs, and grated bread roll, season with salt and nutmeg.
4. Spread the forcemeat on a platter, place the pike brushed with butter on top.
5. Top with chopped mushrooms, capers, lemon, and crayfish tails, season with salt.
6. Add another layer of forcemeat, then fish, and so on until the dish is full.
7. The last layer is made of forcemeat, covered with strong broth, covered with leaf pastry and butter dough, brushed with egg, and baked at strong heat for about 1.5 hours.
8. Serve with caper sauce with lemon juice.

title: Paštika Šunková (Ham Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned ham
- Warm water
- Fresh cold water
- Chopped onion
- Bay leaf
- Fine spices
- Crushed pepper
- Clove
- Nutmeg flower
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Beef meat from the haunch
- Tendons
- Fat from kidneys
- Cleaned sardelles
- Capers
- Grated rye bread
- Lemon zest, finely chopped
- Several egg yolks
- Chopped truffles
- Mushrooms
- Brown butter roux
- Good broth

## Directions

1. Clean the ham and place in warm water, cook slowly until soft
2. Remove bones and place ham in fresh cold water for several days
3. Remove ham from water and place in a pot
4. Sprinkle with chopped onion, bay leaf, fine spices, crushed pepper, clove and nutmeg flower
5. Pour wine and vinegar over until ham is fully covered
6. Cover and let rest for several days
7. Seal the pot completely so no steam can escape
8. Cook the ham
9. Take beef from the haunch, remove tendons and chop with onion, kidney fat, cleaned sardelles and capers
10. Add grated rye bread, finely chopped lemon zest, several egg yolks, chopped truffles and mushrooms, crushed pepper and nutmeg flower
11. Prepare pastry as previously described
12. Place chopped meat on the bottom, then the ham on top and arrange everything as with other pastries
13. Prepare sauce from brown butter roux, add good broth
14. When cooked, strain through a fine sieve
15. Add finely chopped sardelles, capers, lemons and capers to the strained sauce
16. Cook again, strain and pour over the pastry

title: Paštiky z drůbeže (Poultry Pâtés)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- poultry scraps
- sardels without bones
- spoon of capers
- 2 rusks soaked in broth
- 4-6 egg yolks
- snow from 4 egg whites
- salt
- nutmeg
- fine dough

## Directions

1. Chop the poultry scraps, add chopped sardels without bones, a spoon of capers, 2 rusks soaked in broth, 4-6 egg yolks, snow from 4 egg whites, salt and nutmeg
2. Mix well
3. Put fine dough in small molds, place the mixture on the dough and bake for a quarter hour

title: Paštiky z Husích Jater (Goose Liver Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose livers
- White pepper, finely crushed
- Warm milk
- Pastry dough
- Paper
- Eggs
- Serviette
- Lemon
- Butter
- Bacon
- Onion
- Nutmeg flower
- Egg yolks
- Wine

## Directions

1. Wash the goose livers completely
2. Sprinkle with white finely crushed pepper
3. Pour warm milk over and let sit for one to two days, replacing the milk several times daily with fresh warm milk
4. Roll out cooled pastry dough thinly, cut into pieces as desired and lay on paper
5. Cut strips from another rolled dough to line the pastry walls and press firmly onto the base
6. Brush the bottom and walls with beaten eggs
7. Prepare a third piece of dough for the top cover
8. Fill the pastry with serviette so it does not collapse
9. Cover with paper and bake slowly, preferably the day before filling
10. Decorate the pastry with various ornaments added before filling and baking
11. Place sliced lemon, butter and bacon on the bottom of the dough, add onion patties
12. Pour a little wine over, place the prepared livers, sprinkle with nutmeg flower and cover with prepared dough lid
13. Bake over mild heat
14. While baking, beat several egg yolks into milk, perforate the pastry lid and pour the mixture through the hole, then seal the hole completely with the same dough so steam does not escape and the pastry does not lose flavor

title: Paštiky z Kuřete (Chicken Patties)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large chicken, young hen or capon
- 1/8 kilo (1/4 pound) smoked meat
- half that amount of lard
- new spices
- a small onion, finely chopped
- a pinch of marjoram
- cloves
- 2 cloves of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- cooking twine
- salted water
- aspic or horseradish

## Directions

1. Skin a large chicken, young hen or capon; cut along the neck and pull out the meat, handling the wings the same way, then sew the skin on the wings and legs.
2. Finely chop the meat, and for one chicken take 1/8 kilo (1/4 pound) smoked meat, half that amount of lard; cut both into small cubes.
3. Salt and pepper it, add new spices, finely chopped small onion, a pinch of marjoram, cloves, two cloves of garlic; mix everything together.
4. Mix well with the raw meat, smoked meat and lard, fill the skin with this mixture through the neck, sew it closed, wrap it in cooking twine and boil for half an hour in salted water.
5. Press it, let it cool, remove the twine, slice the cooled mixture into thin pieces and serve aspic or horseradish around it.

title: Paštiky z Vepřového Jazyku (Pork Tongue Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tongue
- Salted water
- Finely chopped almonds
- Raisins
- Chopped bacon
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Pepper
- Lemon zest
- Butter pastry dough

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in salted water until soft
2. Remove the skin and chop the meat finely
3. Mix finely chopped almonds, raisins, chopped bacon, sugar, cinnamon, pepper and lemon zest
4. Salt the mixture
5. Fill into butter pastry dough
6. Add a pleasant sauce as described for other pastries

title: Paštinák (Parsnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- fresh parsley
- milk
- egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Scrape parsnips, wash, cut into rounds or sticks and boil in boiling water until tender
2. Place on a saucepan with a piece of butter, salt, pepper, add some chopped fresh parsley and let stew with frequent stirring for a while
3. Place on a hot plate, pour over with milk mixed with a beaten egg or a spoonful of flour and a piece of butter
4. You can also cut it lengthwise and fry it in bacon
5. Cooked parsnips can also be pressed through a sieve, seasoned to taste with salt and pepper, mixed with a piece of butter and a little sour cream and reheated well

title: Paštička Broskvová neb Meruňková (Peach or Apricot Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches or apricots
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Press sieved fruit mass into a container on the stove so all moisture evaporates
2. Slowly add finely powdered sugar with constant stirring
3. Cook together until completely thick
4. Pour onto slate tablets to cool

title: Paštička Řepíková (Turnip Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Press sieved turnip mass through a sieve
2. Mix with finely powdered sugar
3. Cook until thick
4. Spread thinly onto slate tablets in a warm place
5. When dry, peel and cut, then layer on top of each other

title: Paštičky síňkové (Ham Pâtés)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham leftovers or freshly baked and cooled ham
- Meat
- Ribs, crushed in mortar
- Butter
- Truffles, finely chopped
- Coulis
- Good wine
- Butter dough

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from leftover or fresh ham, chop finely.
2. Stew the crushed ribs in butter and finally press through fine sieve with the meat.
3. Mix everything again with finely chopped truffles, coulis, and good wine, and stew.
4. Place pâtés in butter dough and serve hot.

title: Paštičky z Černého Ryvízu (Black Currant Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- black currants
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove berries from their stems
2. Place in containers, cover with bladder and put in boiling water
3. Cook for 20 to 30 minutes
4. Press through a sieve
5. Mix the pressed mass with juice and sugar
6. Slowly bring to a boil several times with constant stirring
7. When completely thick, spread onto slate tablets
8. Peel and stack together

title: Pažitková omáčka (Chive Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- chives
- butter
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- flower
- cream
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Let chopped chives stew in butter.
2. Add broth, salt, pepper, season with ground flower.
3. Brown with a light roux, let it simmer a little, and pour into a container.
4. You may also finish it with cream and egg yolks.

title: Peach Ice Cream (Broskvová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripest peaches
- Kernels from the pits
- Water
- Wine

## Directions

1. Select the ripest peaches
2. Peel them and split them in half
3. Press through a sieve
4. Grind the kernels from the pits with water and wine
5. Press and strain
6. Add this milk to the peach juice
7. Sweeten everything
8. Transform into ice cream

title: Peanutsové Kukesy (Peanut Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of butter
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 cupful of peanuts, finely cut
- ½ cupful of milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Take 1 cupful of butter, 1 cupful of sugar and 1 cupful of peanuts finely cut (they may also be crushed with a roller if you don't want to chop them), ½ cupful of milk, 2 eggs and 1 teaspoonful of baking powder
2. Mix in only as much flour as is needed so the cookies can be rolled

title: Pear Ice Cream (Hrušková zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Tastiest and ripest pears
- Wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the tastiest and ripest pears until soft
2. Peel and cut them
3. Press through a sieve
4. Dilute the obtained juice with wine
5. Sweeten generously
6. Proceed as with other ice creams

title: Pentlový skládaný dort (Ribbon Layer Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sugar
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon extract
- 7 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- milk
- flour
- baking powder
- figs, halved

## Directions

1. Bake 4 cakes as for silver layer cake, but omit the extract.
2. For yellow dough: use 0.5 cup butter, 1 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon cinnamon extract, 7 yolks, 1 whole egg, 0.5 cup milk, 0.5 cup flour, and 0.5 teaspoon baking powder.
3. Pour part of this dough into 2 plates, cover with halved figs, dust lightly with flour, cover with remaining dough, and bake.
4. Make 2 cakes by placing 1 yellow cake between 2 white cakes, with cinnamon frosting between them.
5. Cover the cakes with cinnamon frosting.
6. Instead of figs, you may use other fresh or preserved fruits, such as strawberries, raspberries, pineapples, peeled and quartered oranges, and so forth.

title: Pepřná omáčka (Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three dozen pods of red pepper
- two heads of cabbage
- one horseradish root
- one cup of mustard seed
- a little cloves
- a little sugar
- two quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook in two quarts of vinegar for a quarter hour
2. Allow the cooked mixture to stand for three hours
3. Strain and pour into bottles

title: Pepřová omáčka (Black Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of butter
- one sliced onion
- one spoon of whole pepper
- one teaspoon of ground pepper
- several grains of new spice
- a piece of ginger
- a slice of garlic
- two cloves
- two bay leaves
- a piece of thyme
- about two spoons of wine vinegar
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Make a brown roux from two spoons of butter
2. Add to it one sliced onion, one spoon of whole pepper, one teaspoon of ground pepper, several grains of new spice, a piece of ginger, a slice of garlic, two cloves, two bay leaves and a piece of thyme
3. Add about two spoons of wine vinegar
4. Pour in as much beef broth as you need sauce
5. When everything has cooked together for a little while, strain the sauce into a dish
6. If the sauce is too thick, add more broth; if not sour enough, add a little vinegar

title: Perník Polský (Polish Gingerbread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram rye flour
- 1 kilogram honey
- 2 tablespoons alcohol
- 1 dekagram soda dissolved in a spoonful of water
- a little new spice
- orange zest cut finely
- 5 grams cardamom

## Directions

1. Roast 1 kilogram rye flour until golden yellow
2. Sieve the roasted flour through a thick sieve
3. Heat 1 kilogram honey and scald the flour with the boiling honey, pouring only 1/4 of the honey at first, adding the rest when the dough is formed
4. Add to the dough 2 tablespoons alcohol, 1 dekagram soda dissolved in a spoonful of water, and spices: a little new spice, finely chopped orange zest, and 5 grams cardamom
5. Knead the dough thoroughly on a board until it is pliable
6. Take pieces of dough the size of a walnut and form them into small cakes
7. Place them a finger's width apart on a greased baking sheet and bake in an oven until golden

title: Perníková nádivka (Gingerbread Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- gingerbread
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Grate several pieces of gingerbread
2. Put it in a small pot with a piece of butter and a little sugar
3. Let it fry a little
4. You can use it either on German cakes or in small buns

title: Perníkové placičky (Molasses Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 koflík sádla
- 1/2 koflíku cukru
- 1 koflík melasy
- 1 koflík kyselé smetany
- 2 vejce
- 1 lžička mletého fenyklu
- 1 lžička zázvoru
- 1/2 lžičky kmínu
- 1/2 lžičky skořice
- 1/2 lžičky hřebíčku
- 1/2 lžičky nového koření
- 1/2 lžičky soli
- 1 muškátový oříšek
- 1 lžička sody do pečení
- 2 lžíce horké vody
- mouka (dle potřeby)

## Directions

1. Vezmu jeden koflík sádla, půl koflíku cukru, jeden koflík melasu, jeden koflík kyselé smetany, dvě vejce, jednu lžičku mletého fenyklu, jednu lžičku zázvoru, půl lžičky kmínu, půl lžičky skořice, půl lžičky hřebíčku, půl lžičky nového koření, půl lžičky soli a jeden muškátový oříšek, to všechno semleté.
2. To vše dohromady promíchám.
3. Vezmu lžičku sody do pečení, dám do koflíku a naleju na ni asi dvě lžíce horké vody, rozmíchám a vleju to do toho ostatního těsta a zas rozmíchám, až mi to jde vzhůru.
4. Přidám mouky, aby bylo těsto tuhé, dám na vál a vykrájím jako jiné suchárky, peču pozvolna a když schladnou, složím do hrnce a vy drž jí dlouho čerstvé.

title: Perníčky (Spice Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg honey
- flour
- 2 tablespoons liquor
- 1 dekagram potash (potassium carbonate) dissolved in a teaspoon of water
- a little allspice
- orange peel, finely chopped
- 5 grams cardamom

## Directions

1. Heat the honey and mix in flour with the boiling honey, pouring in only 1/4 of the honey at once, adding the remaining 1/4 as the dough forms
2. Add 2 tablespoons liquor, 1 dekagram potash dissolved in a teaspoon of water, and spices: a little allspice, finely chopped orange peel, and 5 grams cardamom to the dough
3. Knead the dough thoroughly on a work surface until it is pliable
4. Form pieces of dough the size of walnuts into small cookies
5. Place them a thumb's width apart on a greased baking sheet
6. Bake in an oven until golden
7. Store in a dry place in a container tied with parchment

title: Petruzelové máslo (Parsley Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound fresh butter
- Two tablespoons chopped fresh parsley
- Juice from one lemon
- Pinch of pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. To half pound of fresh butter take two tablespoons of chopped fresh parsley, juice from one lemon, a pinch of pepper and a little salt, mix everything well and store the butter in a cool place.
2. This butter is added to various roasts instead of sauce; small pieces of butter are spread on the surface of the roast and allowed to melt from the heat.

title: Petruželová omáčka (Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley roots
- fresh parsley
- bread crumbs
- beef broth
- cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean parsley roots, cut them into very thin slices, chop fresh parsley finely, and place both with fried grated bread crumbs in a pan.
2. Pour beef broth over it, let it simmer, season as desired, and finally mix in a little cream and a piece of butter.

title: Petruželová voda (Parsley Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts water
- 1 celery root sliced into rounds
- 2 parsley roots
- handful of peas
- green parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Put into 2 quarts of water 1 celery root sliced into rounds, 2 parsley roots, a handful of peas, and green parsley
2. Salt it and let it simmer slowly for about 2 hours, carefully skimming the foam
3. Remove from heat and let stand quietly for about half an hour
4. Slowly strain through a thick cloth into an earthenware pot without clouding it
5. Set in a cool place
6. Where fish bouillon cannot be prepared, this stock works very well for preparing fasting soups and sauces
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Pečeni Pstruzi (Baked Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- gutted and cleaned trout
- salt
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Salt the gutted and cleaned trout and let rest for at least an hour
2. Coat in butter mixed with flour and bake on both sides

title: Pečená bělice (Baked White Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- white fish
- salt
- grated white bread
- milk
- butter
- walnut
- pepper
- corn flour
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the white fish well, wipe it with a cloth and salt it inside.
2. Make a stuffing from grated white bread soaked in milk, add a piece of butter the size of a walnut, salt and pepper it.
3. Stuff the white fish and sew it shut.
4. Sprinkle with corn flour, place on a baking pan, pour with water and bake.
5. During baking baste with the juice from the pan.
6. Bake for about an hour. Lake trout or pike are baked similarly.

title: Pečená divoká kachna (Roast Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 part vinegar
- 2 parts water
- several grains of allspice
- 1 onion
- a piece of carrot
- a piece of lemon peel
- salt
- wild duck
- lard, thinly sliced
- butter
- sour cream
- marinade

## Directions

1. Boil together in a pot one part vinegar, two parts water, several grains of allspice, one onion, a piece of carrot, a piece of lemon peel and some salt
2. Let this cool, then pour it over well-cleaned wild duck placed in a deep clay vessel and let it lie there for several days
3. When ready to prepare, dry the duck, salt it, lard it on the breast with four to six rows of thinly sliced lard, place it on a baking pan, put pieces of butter on it and let it roast in the oven until tender
4. During roasting, baste it frequently with sour cream and the marinade in which it was marinated
5. When roasted, arrange it on a platter and pour the baked sauce over it

title: Pečená divoká kachna (Roast Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar (one part)
- water (two parts)
- spices

## Directions

1. Boil in a pot one part vinegar and two parts water with several spices

title: Pečená Granátová Dýně (Baked Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggplant
- Crackers
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the eggplant until soft; when cooked, cut it in half, scrape out the flesh, mix it well, and for each cup of flesh add one spoonful of rolled crackers and one spoonful of butter, salt and pepper, mix everything well and put in a bowl.
2. Beat eggs, spread half of it over the mixture and sprinkle with rolled crackers, then coat with the other half of the egg and sprinkle with crackers again and let it bake until golden.

title: Pečená Husa (Roast Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- One cleaned and gutted goose
- Salt
- Water
- Caraway seeds, sifted

## Directions

1. Wash the well-cleaned and gutted goose in cold water.
2. Salt it on the surface and inside.
3. Sprinkle the inside with a little sifted caraway seeds.
4. Place it on a baking pan and add a little water.
5. Put it in the oven and let it roast slowly.
6. Turn it and frequently baste it with the roasted drippings.
7. When nearly done, drain off the fat and finish roasting the goose on high heat until brown.
8. If during roasting you prick the skin on the back and under the wings with a fork, the fat will cook out better and the skin will become crisp and more appetizing.
9. Serve with dumplings with cabbage, stewed cabbage, or various prepared potatoes.

title: Pečená husa (Roast Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- water
- salt
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Clean and eviscerate the goose thoroughly
2. Wash it in cold water, salt the surface and inside
3. Sprinkle the inside with a little caraway seeds
4. Place it on a roasting pan, pour a little water underneath
5. Put it in the oven and roast

title: Pečená Kachna (Roast Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- One duck
- Three onions
- One teaspoon sage leaves
- Two tablespoons grated bread
- One egg
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Duck is roasted in exactly the same way as goose.
2. It can also be stuffed as follows: finely chop three onions, roast and sift one teaspoon of sage leaves, add two tablespoons of grated bread, one egg, a piece of butter, salt and pepper, mix well, stuff the duck and sew it closed.
3. Roast as you would a goose.

title: Pečená nadívaná vejce (Baked Stuffed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- raw eggs (two)
- lemon zest (small piece)
- green parsley (chopped)
- salt
- milk
- bread crumbs (grated)
- sardeles (optional)
- pike or crayfish meat (optional)
- goose liver (optional)
- sweetbreads (optional)
- mushrooms (optional)
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs lengthwise into halves
2. Remove yolks and crush in a bowl with a piece of butter
3. Add two raw eggs
4. Add a small piece of lemon zest
5. Add a little chopped green parsley
6. Add salt and milk and enough grated bread crumbs to make a thick filling
7. Optional: add crushed sardeles, chopped pike or crayfish meat, stewed goose liver, sweetbreads, or mushrooms
8. Mix well
9. Fill the egg whites with this mixture
10. Put the eggs back together
11. Place on a greased baking sheet
12. Mix the remaining filling with a little sour cream
13. Pour under the eggs
14. Bake for half an hour
15. Serve alone on a plate or surrounded with various vegetables

title: Pečená rýže (Baked Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams rice (16 lots)
- cold water
- 1 liter milk
- 9 dekagrams butter (6 lots)
- sugar, rubbed with lemon
- salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 egg yolks
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Soak 24 dekagrams of rice in cold water for a quarter hour, stirring often, then drain
2. When the rice has dried, braise it for a quarter hour in 1 liter of milk, add 9 dekagrams of butter, sugar rubbed with lemon, salt and cook for a quarter hour
3. Mix in 4 egg yolks and turn out onto a plate
4. Add 2 more egg yolks and stiffly beaten snow from all the egg whites, put in a greased mold and bake for an hour with gentle heat
5. Serve the baked rice with wine sauce

title: Pečená rýže (Baked Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup rice
- 3 pints milk
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- salt
- sweet cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse a small cup of rice, put it in a deep dish and pour three pints of milk over it
2. Add four tablespoons of sugar and a little salt
3. Bake for three hours at moderate temperature
4. As the milk bakes down, replenish it with boiling milk kept ready on the stove
5. Serve with sweet cream and sugar

title: Pečená rýže na jiný způsob (Baked Rice, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 small cup rice
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons fine sugar
- salt
- 1 quart milk
- cinnamon and sugar

## Directions

1. Sort and rinse half a small cup of rice well
2. Put it in a form or baking dish
3. Add three tablespoons of butter, two tablespoons of fine sugar, a little salt and a quart of milk
4. Put it in the oven and let it bake until all the milk is baked out
5. Then sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and serve

title: Pečená sekanina (Baked Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat leftovers
- 6 large raw potatoes
- 2 to 3 eggs
- onion
- 2 tablespoons flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the cold meat, peel about six large raw potatoes and grate them. Add 2 to 3 eggs, chop some onion, add 2 tablespoons of flour and season to taste with salt and pepper. Mix well and bake in a well-buttered pan.

title: Pečená skopová kýta (Roast Leg of Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- salt
- garlic
- water
- butter or lard
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the leg of mutton thoroughly, remove skin and fat
2. Salt well on both sides
3. Insert garlic generously on both sides
4. Place on baking pan
5. Pour a little water under it
6. Let it roast, basting frequently with butter or lard
7. Baste at the end with the pan juices to give it good color
8. If the meat is older, pound well before salting
9. If very old, braise covered first, then roast in the oven when tender
10. Serve with boiled or roasted potatoes
11. Small potatoes may be scraped and added to the pan half an hour before serving to roast together

title: Pečená Telecí Kýta (Roast Leg of Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg, white and fine
- salt
- pepper
- 4 cloves
- 1/2 onion, chopped
- 1 small bay leaf
- water
- 1/2 cup butter
- grated bread roll or bread

## Directions

1. Clean a fine white veal leg thoroughly, dry it with a cloth
2. Salt and pepper both sides
3. Place on a baking pan, insert 4 cloves into the leg, add chopped onion and a small bay leaf
4. Add a little water and 1/2 cup butter
5. Place in the oven and roast for two hours
6. Turn it over, sprinkle with grated bread roll or bread, top with butter
7. Roast at frequent intervals, basting with pan juices until golden brown
8. Place on a platter and pour strained sauce over it

title: Pečená treska (Scalloped Codfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Codfish
- butter
- bread crumbs
- sardines
- lemon
- nutmeg
- cream

## Directions

1. Cook the codfish until tender, drain and cool
2. Carefully remove the bones and flake the meat
3. Generously butter a baking dish or mold and dust with grated bread crumbs
4. Place a layer of codfish at the bottom, then a layer of grated bread crumbs mixed with chopped sardines, well rubbed with lemon zest and nutmeg
5. Continue alternating layers until the dish is full
6. Top with a layer of bread crumbs, pour a little cream over it, place pieces of butter on top and bake in the oven
7. Either turn out onto a serving plate or serve in the baking dish

title: Pečená Vepřová Svíčka (Roasted Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloins
- Salt
- Caraway seeds
- Flour
- Lard
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the pork tenderloins
2. Arrange lengthwise on a roasting pan
3. Sprinkle with salt, caraway seeds and a little flour
4. Place a piece of lard on top
5. Pour a little water
6. Roast in oven, basting frequently with pan juices until sufficiently cooked

title: Pečená štika (Baked Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- onion
- carrot
- celery
- leek
- butter
- white flour
- broth
- vinegar
- wine
- pepper
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Clean the pike, remove entrails, scrape it well, clean and salt it inside, cover with cloth and let it lie for two hours.
2. Wipe it with cloth and make several cuts across both sides.
3. Place one large onion, one carrot, half celery, one leek all sliced thinly into a pot, add a piece of butter and let it fry to golden, then add two tablespoons of white flour, let it stew for another minute.
4. Pour a pint of broth, a coffee cup of vinegar, a glass of wine on it, add several grains of pepper, one bay leaf and let it cook well for half an hour.
5. Grease a baking pan well with butter, place a piece of cut celery and parsley in it, lay the pike on it and bake in the oven, frequently brushing with butter.
6. When the pike is baked, carefully remove it, cut into neat pieces and arrange on a dish.
7. Push the prepared sauce through a sieve into the baking pan, let it cook for another minute in the oven and then strain through a sieve over the cut pike.

title: Pečené krupičné věnečky (Baked Semolina Rings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams fine semolina
- 1 1/4 liters milk
- 75 grams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- salt
- grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook semolina in milk
2. Let cool
3. Mix in butter and egg yolks
4. Add salt
5. Roll in grated bread crumbs
6. Shape into rings
7. Fry on both sides in butter
8. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Pečené Kuře (Roast Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned chickens
- salt
- pepper
- fresh parsley
- butter
- beef broth or water

## Directions

1. Wash well-cleaned chickens, salt inside and on the surface
2. Truss them, place several fresh parsley leaves and a piece of butter inside
3. Dust the inside lightly with ground pepper
