4. Put them on a baking pan with a piece of fresh butter and a little beef broth or water
5. Roast them on both sides until golden, frequently brushing with butter
6. Baste every 10 minutes with the pan juices
7. Place them whole or cut into quarters on a plate
8. Pour the sauce over them and serve

title: Pečené křepelky (Roast Quail)
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- salt
- bacon
- grape leaves
- butter
- beef broth
- string
- optional filling
- optional: onion, parsley, mushrooms, red wine

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean quail, sprinkle with a little salt
2. Wrap them in bacon and if available, grape leaves, and tie with string
3. Arrange them on a roasting pan, place pieces of butter, baste with beef broth
4. Roast in the oven until golden
5. Untie the strings, remove the leaves from the quail, arrange nicely on a platter and pour the strained juices over them
6. You can also fill them with a fine stuffing
7. You can also place sliced onion, parsley and mushrooms on the pan under the quail and when they are nearly done, pour in a glass of red wine

title: Pečené mandle (Baked Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 kilogram (quarter pound) almonds, grated
- equal amount sugar
- equal amount flour
- 4 egg yolks
- butter
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Mix grated almonds, sugar, and flour
2. Add 4 egg yolks and make a stiff dough
3. Press almonds through a small mold
4. Either brush with butter or bake on a baking sheet in an oven
5. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
6. Store in a dry place

title: Pečené měkké mušle (Baked Soft Clams)
body:
## Ingredients

- soft clams in shells
- salt
- pepper
- parsley stems, finely chopped, half a teaspoon
- bacon, six tiny cubes
- fresh butter, thin layer

## Directions

1. Place one large or two smaller clams on each shell
2. Season lightly with salt and pepper
3. Add half a teaspoon of finely chopped parsley stems
4. Add six tiny cubes of bacon
5. Cover with a thin layer of fresh butter
6. Bake in the oven until golden
7. Arrange neatly on a dish on folded cloth
8. Garnish with green parsley or watercress

title: Pečené nudle (Baked Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 1/2 deciliters (1 žejdlík) of smetana (sour cream)
- butter
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of sugar
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) of ground almonds
- 6 egg yolks
- a little lemon zest
- butter dough for base and top
- almonds
- sugar mixed with cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make noodles from 3 yolks and 2 whole eggs
2. Cook in salted water and drain
3. Place on baking pan
4. Pour sour cream over noodles, add butter, sugar and ground almonds
5. Bake until sour cream is absorbed
6. Let cool
7. Mix 6 egg yolks with lemon zest and pour over noodles
8. Cover baking pan bottom with butter dough
9. Place almonds on dough, then noodles, then cover with dough
10. Bake until noodles are done
11. Sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon

title: Pečené okruží (Roasted Organ Meats)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- organ meats
- salt
- fat

## Directions

1. Clean the organ meats and remove all membranes and blood
2. Salt them, place on a baking pan, baste occasionally with fat and turn until golden brown
3. May also be fried after boiling first

title: Pečené omeletky (Omelette Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- grated lemon zest
- 4 egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Put 4 egg yolks in a bowl, add 4 tablespoons of sugar and a bit of grated lemon zest, and beat well for a quarter hour
2. Gently fold in stiffly beaten egg whites from 4 eggs
3. Pour into a deep dish or pan greased with butter
4. Bake over moderate heat for at most ten minutes
5. Serve immediately to the table

title: Pečené Podsvinče (Roast Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young pig
- Salt
- Pepper
- Caraway seeds
- Bread
- Butter or lard
- Beer or cider
- Lemons
- Mustard or cabbage

## Directions

1. Clean the pig thoroughly
2. Rub inside with ground pepper and salt
3. Sprinkle with caraway seeds
4. Insert a slice of bread
5. Sew closed
6. Tie the legs and cover the ears with greased paper cones
7. Place on a shallow roasting pan over thin sticks that are not sooty
8. Position pig leg-side up so it does not touch the pan
9. Pour several spoonfuls of beer or cider over it
10. Place in oven and brush frequently with fresh butter
11. When the skin is crispy and reddened, turn and continue roasting, brushing with butter
12. When nearly done, brush several times with beer to make the skin crispier
13. When done, make an incision around the neck to allow steam to escape and prevent the skin from becoming soft
14. Place on a dish and surround with lemon slices
15. Place a lemon slice in the mouth
16. Serve with either cabbage or mustard on the side

title: Pečené s kyselou smetanou (Roast Beef with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cutlets from the loin or tenderloin
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- vinegar
- water
- bay leaves
- sour cream
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut nice thick cutlets from the loin or tenderloin, pound them well, pierce with bacon, and dust with salt and pepper
2. Place cut bacon and onion in a saucepan, lay the cutlets on top, cover with a lid, and let braise until the expressed juice is reabsorbed
3. Add a little vinegar, a little water, and one or two bay leaves, and let braise until completely tender
4. Remove the cutlets, strain the juice, mix with sour cream, return the cutlets to the saucepan, pour the gravy over them, let simmer briefly
5. Place on a serving dish, make a wreath of roasted or fried potatoes around them, and serve

title: Pečené sluky (Roasted Woodcocks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- butter
- red wine
- juniper berries
- red wine

## Directions

1. Roast the woodcocks briefly in butter to which red wine and crushed juniper berries have been added.
2. Strain the sauce again with red wine and simmer.
3. Pour over the roasted woodcocks until tender.

title: Pečené telecí okruži (Baked Veal Haslet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal mesentery
- butter or lard
- bread rolls or bread
- eggs
- green parsley or chives
- leek
- thyme
- lemon zest
- pepper
- caul fat

## Directions

1. Boil cleaned mesentery and chop it finely
2. To the chopped meat add a piece of butter or lard, 2 grated bread rolls or 4 slices of bread, 3 eggs, a little chopped green parsley or chives, or just a piece of leek, a little thyme, a piece of finely chopped lemon zest and a pinch of pepper
3. Mix everything well
4. Grease a casserole with butter or lard
5. Sprinkle with grated bread roll
6. Put the mixture in it
7. Cover with caul fat which you keep whole for this purpose and let it bake

title: Pečené telecí osrdí (Baked Veal Pluck)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal pluck (heart and lungs)
- butter or lard
- eggs
- lemon zest
- marjoram
- pepper
- garlic
- bread or bread rolls
- milk

## Directions

1. Boil pluck in salted water until tender
2. Slice it, remove all sinews and marrow and chop finely
3. Cream 3 spoonfuls of butter or lard on a plate
4. Add 3 eggs, finely chopped lemon zest, a little chopped and rubbed marjoram, a pinch of pepper, a slice of garlic, the chopped pluck, two slices of bread or a bread roll soaked and squeezed out in milk, half a grated bread roll and mix everything well
5. Grease a mold with butter
6. Sprinkle with grated bread roll
7. Put the mixture in it and let bake in the oven for an hour
8. Turn it out onto a plate and serve with a sardine or caper sauce in a separate bowl

title: Pečené vemeno (Baked Udder)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Udder
- Pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Vegetables

## Directions

1. Clean the udder and cook it
2. Peel it and slice into pieces
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Bake on a baking sheet in butter until browned
5. Serve with vegetables

title: Pečené vepřové jazyky (Roast Pork Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongues
- lard
- salt
- white wine
- vinegar
- sugar
- lemon peel
- cloves
- allspice

## Directions

1. Blanch the pork tongues
2. Remove the outer coarse skin from them
3. Lard them with bacon on both sides
4. Salt them
5. Roll them slightly and pass wooden skewers through both ends
6. Brush them with melted butter and roast until golden on butter
7. Meanwhile make a sauce from equal parts wine and vinegar
8. Add a piece of sugar, a piece of lemon peel, several cloves and a bit of allspice to the sauce
9. Let it simmer
10. Thicken lightly with flour and pour the sauce over the tongues

title: Pečené zelí se zemáky (Baked Cabbage with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- slanina (bacon)
- zelí (cabbage)
- vepřové sádlo (pork lard)
- sůl (salt)
- vařící voda (boiling water)
- bílé víno (white wine)
- zemáky (potatoes)
- máslo (butter)
- žloutky (egg yolks)

## Directions

1. Na pekáček dá se v malých kouskách slanina, na tu zelí, polité vepřovým sádlem, vše se osolí
2. Poleje vařící vodou a přikryté pozvolna se vaří
3. Když se již dovařuje, přileje se k němu bílého vína a zelí dále se dusí
4. Měkce uvařené zemáky se oloupají, rozstrouhají, v másle a raléc udusí
5. Načež vrstevnatě se zelím na pekáček se kladou
6. Vrchem se sloje žloutky rozkvedlené, vše až do červena se upeče

title: Pečené řepachy (Broiled Smelts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smelts
- butter
- parsley butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the smelts as in the previous method
2. Dry them with a cloth
3. Salt and pepper them
4. Brush with softened butter
5. Broil either on a double grill or on a baking sheet
6. Arrange on a dish, pour melted parsley butter over them, and surround with sliced lemon

title: Pečený beránek (Roasted Lamb)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb hindquarters
- garlic
- salt
- fresh butter
- water
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Remove membranes and fat from lamb hindquarters.
2. Cut the legs at the joints and skewer them together.
3. Wash it clean, split the hindquarters and remove the intestines found there.
4. Rub garlic with salt and grease with this and fresh butter.
5. Add water and roast until golden.
6. Can be sprinkled with grated bread before it is completely roasted to brown it.
7. Those who do not like garlic may omit it.

title: Pečený jehněčí zadek (Roast Hind Saddle of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb hind saddle
- butter or lard
- salt
- bread crumbs
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Wash the lamb hind saddle well and make small incisions on the underside of the legs so they bend and fit in the roasting pan
2. Salt it and place it meat-side down in the roasting pan on a piece of butter or lard
3. It is best to use a roasting pan with a grate at the bottom, or place two clean unstained wooden spoons on the bottom
4. Roast it with frequent basting and buttering
5. During roasting, add a little broth or hot water
6. Turn it over, sprinkle with bread crumbs, surround with pieces of fresh butter and roast until golden
7. Roast for one and a half to two hours depending on size
8. Cut into neat pieces, arrange on a platter and pour the strained meat juice over it
9. Lamb saddle of kid can be roasted the same way

title: Pečený Jeseter (Baked Sturgeon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh sturgeon
- Salt
- Butter, melted
- Bread crumbs, grated
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Skin the sturgeon
2. Wash the meat from fresh sturgeon and salt it
3. Place it in a baking pan in melted butter
4. Coat the surface generously with butter
5. Sprinkle with grated bread crumbs
6. Bake in the oven
7. Place on a dish
8. Pour hot butter mixed with lemon juice over it
9. Serve to the table

title: Pečený kapoun (Roasted Capon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- butter
- bacon
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald the capon, pluck it, singe it, draw it, salt it and truss it
2. Either roast it in butter or lard it with bacon and roast in the oven until nicely browned

title: Pečený Kohout (Roast Chicken with Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- rooster
- egg
- breadcrumb
- fat
- caraway
- onion
- flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Clean the rooster well, cut into pieces
2. Coat in egg and then in breadcrumbs
3. Place on a pan, add a little fat, sprinkle with caraway and onion, cover
4. Bake gently until cooked
5. Remove the rooster
6. To the fat in the pan, add flour as desired for thickness
7. Brown until golden
8. Add milk to the pan as desired for sauce consistency
9. Serve the sauce with the rooster

title: Pečený krocan (Roast Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey, not older than one year
- pepper
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. The best turkey is not older than one year
2. After slaughtering, pluck the turkey well, singe the down, clean it, wash it, salt it, and let it lie in a cold place for at least two to three days in winter and at least twenty-four hours in summer before roasting
3. Place it on a roasting pan, dust it with pepper inside and on the surface
4. Insert and place pieces of butter on top—altogether about one bowl of butter
5. Pour one bowl of water underneath
6. Bake it
7. Every fifteen minutes, baste it with the rendered juices at a steady but not strong heat
8. Roasting takes one to one and a half hours, depending on the size of the turkey
9. When roasted, cut it into neat pieces, place on a platter and pour the juices over it
10. Serve with cranberry marmalade

title: Pečený losos s majonézou (Baked Salmon with Mayonnaise Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- salt
- fresh butter
- lemons
- lemon rind
- capers
- new spice
- pepper
- cloves
- parsley
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. From a nice piece of salmon cut small pieces about two fingers wide
2. Salt them and bake in fresh butter
3. Place on a dish, pour fresh heated butter over them
4. Squeeze juice from several lemons over them, sprinkle with finely chopped lemon rind and capers
5. Crush a few grains of new spice, pepper and four cloves, sprinkle this over the salmon and let it lie for several hours
6. Remove the salmon, arrange on a dish, pour mayonnaise sauce in the middle and make a border of parsley

title: Pečený skopový hřbet (Roast Saddle of Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice saddle from a young mutton
- sour cream or vinegar
- lard for larding
- salt
- pepper
- water
- butter
- paper for covering

## Directions

1. Let a nice saddle from a young mutton lie for three days in sour cream or vinegar and turn it frequently.
2. Remove all the skin and fat from it.
3. Lard it well with lard.
4. Place it on a baking pan, salt it, pour some water and the liquid in which it was marinated over it.
5. Add plenty of butter and cover with greased paper so that it does not brown too quickly.
6. Let it roast for three hours with frequent basting with the roasted juices.
7. Then place it on a platter and serve with dumplings or boiled potatoes.

title: Pečený Slaneček (Baked Char)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- char
- water
- flour
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Place char in water, remove skin, tear open lengthwise, remove bones, slice diagonally, dry
2. Coat with flour, dip in egg and grated bread, and bake in butter

title: Pečený Uzený Špek (Baked Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked bacon
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Prepare smoked bacon as for fried bacon
2. Place on a baking sheet
3. Bake in a hot oven until done
4. Serve with fried potatoes

title: Pečený zaječí zadek (Roasted Hare Hindquarters)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare hindquarters
- Bacon
- Onion
- 5-6 whole peppers
- Bacon
- Bread
- Milk

## Directions

1. Lard the hindquarters with bacon
2. Slice onion, add 5-6 whole peppers and roast on a pan with sliced bacon
3. Soak bread in milk, squeeze it out, add to the sauce when the hare is placed on a platter, and simmer with it
4. Arrange the remaining bacon around the hare and pour with sauce

title: Pečený Zajíc (Roast Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- One young rabbit
- Fresh bacon
- Salt
- Three onions
- Thyme
- One bay leaf
- About ten grains of allspice
- Eight grains of pepper
- Two cloves
- Garlic
- Bacon scraps
- Rabbit scraps
- Wine vinegar
- Water
- Butter
- Sour cream
- Flour
- Potatoes for frying

## Directions

1. Skin a beautiful young rabbit, separate the front from the back, wash it clean and remove the skin.
2. Lard it densely with fresh bacon and salt it.
3. Put three onions sliced into rounds on a baking pan, add a pinch of thyme, one bay leaf, about ten grains of allspice, eight grains of pepper, two cloves, a piece of garlic, bacon scraps and rabbit scraps.
4. Place the rabbit on the pan, add a little wine vinegar underneath, some water, and a piece of butter.
5. Roast the rabbit briskly; when it is nearly done, turn it and finish roasting, basting it very frequently with good sour cream in which you have dissolved a piece of fresh butter.
6. When the rabbit is tender enough, cut it into neat pieces and arrange them on a platter.
7. Dust the gravy with a little flour, stir it, press it through a sieve, add a little more sour cream, let it simmer for a few minutes longer and pour into a small bowl.
8. Serve fried potatoes alongside the rabbit. Instead of sour cream, you may also baste the rabbit with butter and pour over the roasting juices.

title: Pečený Zajíc v Kyselé Smetaně (Roast Rabbit with Sour Cream Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- One rabbit
- Sour cream
- Vinegar
- Water
- One large onion
- Thyme
- Bay leaves
- Whole pepper
- Whole ginger
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Butter
- Two fresh onions
- Lard for larding

## Directions

1. After skinning the rabbit, separate the back from the front, trim all the skin from the back, wash it and salt it, place on a baking pan.
2. Into a cup of vinegar and the same amount of water, put one large sliced onion, a little thyme, bay leaves, whole pepper, whole ginger, allspice and several cloves.
3. Bring this to a boil and pour the cooled mixture over the rabbit.
4. The rabbit may rest in this mixture for 3-4 days, or even 8 days in winter, turning it daily.
5. When ready to roast, remove the rabbit to a board, lard it nicely with lard, put a piece of butter on a baking pan with two fresh sliced onions, place the rabbit on it with the lard side down, add some of the liquid in which it was resting.
6. During roasting, turn the rabbit frequently, basting it each time with thick sour cream, and place pieces of butter on top until the rabbit browns nicely on the surface and the gravy thickens.
7. If the gravy browns too much, add a little of the strained liquid and some sour cream to it.
8. Place the rabbit on a platter and pour the strained gravy over it.

title: Pečený úhoř (Roast Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- butter
- sage
- rolls, cut into slices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Skin the eel and clean it
2. Cut it into pieces, salt it and let it rest covered for an hour
3. Put a piece of butter in a pan and place it near the fire
4. When the butter is hot, add a little sage, cover it and let it rest in a warm place for a quarter hour
5. Dry the salted eel with a cloth
6. Strain the prepared butter through a sieve into a shallow iron pan
7. Place the eel in it along with two rolls cut into slices and let both brown on both sides
8. Remove it, arrange the eel in the center of a platter
9. Surround it with the browned rolls
10. Sprinkle with lemon juice
11. Serve hot

title: Pickled Bean Pods (Nakládání lusků)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- bean pods
- salt (half kilogram per ten kilograms of pods)

## Directions

1. Cut the bean pods into noodles
2. Mix with salt
3. Layer the cut pod noodles into stone vessels or barrels
4. Weight down the top with a board and stone
5. Arrange everything as explained in the pickle cabbage method

title: Pickled Bean Pods - Another Method (Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- bean pods
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the bean pods into fine noodles
2. Scald with boiling water
3. Allow to drain
4. Dry
5. Salt
6. Pour with cold well water
7. Every fourteen days, clean, turn them over, and add more liquid if necessary

title: Pickled Cucumbers (Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Cold water
- Good vinegar
- Water
- Cherry or grape leaves
- Dill
- Ginger
- Allspice
- Horseradish, sliced into cakes
- Stone for pressing
- Wood or clay container

## Directions

1. Trim the cucumbers at both ends and soak in cold water for two hours
2. Meanwhile, boil good vinegar with water
3. In a container (preferably wooden or clay), place cherry or grape leaves and dill at the bottom
4. Add a layer of cucumbers, salt them, sprinkle with ginger, allspice, sliced horseradish cakes, and add leaves again
5. When the container is full, pour the boiled warm vinegar mixed with water over the cucumbers
6. Weight down the cucumbers with a board and stone
7. The pickled cucumbers can be removed on the third day
8. If desired, add a few pieces of paprika

title: Pickled Cucumbers Russian-Style
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized, firm, green cucumbers
- Stone pots
- Good vinegar
- Yellow mustard seed
- Onion
- Garlic
- Horseradish, cut up
- Cherry and grape leaves
- Dill greens
- White mustard seed

## Directions

1. Select medium-sized, firm, green cucumbers, clean and rinse them, and place in stone pots, pour salted boiled vinegar over them
2. To three liters of good vinegar add twenty decagrams of yellow mustard seed, fifty decagrams of onion, five decagrams of garlic, cut up horseradish, cherry and grape leaves, and dill greens
3. On top, put white mustard seed in linen bags so the cucumbers are completely well covered, then seal the pots completely
4. After fourteen days, pour off the vinegar from the cucumbers, boil it again, and if needed add more; let it cool and pour it cold over the cucumbers

title: Pickled Red Cabbage Russian-Style (Ruské zelí)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-aged sour cabbage
- Mustard
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Raisins

## Directions

1. Press out the well-aged, sour cabbage and place on a dish
2. Pour mustard, oil, vinegar and sugar over it
3. Mix everything together
4. Add raisins

title: Pickling Cucumbers in Vinegar
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, green, firm cucumbers without seeds
- Water
- Salt
- Dill, sliced
- Cherry leaves
- Horseradish, sliced
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Stone container

## Directions

1. Rinse the cucumbers and salt them for 12 hours, then dry them
2. Layer cucumbers with sliced dill, cherry leaves, sliced horseradish, salt and pepper in a stone pot
3. Pour vinegar to cover the cucumbers completely
4. After 12-14 days, pour off the vinegar, boil it, let it cool, and pour it back over the cucumbers
5. Cover with slate, weight with a stone, tie up the pot, and place in a cold location
6. For small cucumbers, clean with a brush, soak in salted water for 4 hours, dry, and layer similarly
7. After 3-4 days, pour off and boil the vinegar; repeat this process two more times
8. Some add paprika pods, fennel and other spices to the cucumbers

title: Pickling of Peas, Parsley, and Lemon Zest (Nakládání hrášku, petružele a citrónové kůry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh peas
- water
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- peppercorns (whole grains)
- parsley hearts
- lemon

## Directions

1. Place fresh peas in boiling water
2. Spread on a sieve so water drains
3. Simmer with butter, water, salt, and sugar until half softened
4. Or cook in salted water, and when water drains, pour into jars; per half-liter of peas add 15 grams of crushed sugar, pour with warm water, tie with parchment paper and bladder, wrap the jars with straw, place in a pot on hay and cook for three quarters of an hour
5. Peas can also be stored completely cooked in vessels; per liter of peas add one liter of water, a piece of butter, 25 grams of sugar, a small teaspoon of salt, and cook until peas soften; cover with the water in which they were cooked, seal and cook slowly in water for only half an hour
6. Peas can also be preserved raw with salt; add whole peppercorns, place in jars, cover with butter, tie with bladder and place in a cool location
7. Parsley can be preserved by the last method; remove the hearts, salt the parsley lightly, place in a vessel and cover with butter and tie
8. Lemon zest is peeled from the lemon, freed from white pith, finely cut, salted, filled into a vessel and tied; this way it lasts even a quarter of a year

title: Pickling Peppered Cucumbers
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Healthy, firm, preferably small cucumbers
- Best quality clean vinegar
- Soft water
- Salt
- Cherry or grape leaves
- Dill greens
- Green paprika
- Plum brandy (optional)
- Fat for sealing (optional)

## Directions

1. Select healthy, firm, rather small cucumbers and the best quality clean vinegar; dilute the vinegar with soft water and add salt; to three liters of vinegar add one liter of water and six tablespoons of salt
2. Pickling cucumbers must be gathered dry; early cucumbers are better than late ones
3. Wash the cucumbers in cold water, clean and dry them, then place in glass jars or barrels
4. The barrels must be completely clean and without any smell; place cherry or grape leaves and dill greens at the bottom, then arrange the cucumbers well, and always add some green paprika with each layer
5. For each layer of cucumbers, add a layer of leaves and dill
6. Pour the boiled and cooled vinegar over everything, continuing layer by layer until the container is full, then seal completely so no air can enter
7. If the vinegar dries out, refill with new vinegar prepared the same way
8. Some pour a little plum brandy over the cucumbers in jars to keep them fresher and longer
9. It is also possible to seal them on top with fat so air cannot easily enter
10. For quick sour cucumbers, cut them at both ends and keep them for two or three days in a warm room, then store in a cold place

title: Pickling Plums in Honey (Nakládání švestek do medu)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- ground cinnamon
- cloves
- honey

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the plums and place them in a clean barrel.
2. Sprinkle with ground cinnamon and cloves.
3. Pour honey over them and seal the barrel well.
4. Roll the barrel and then store it in the cellar.
5. Shake the barrel every day.
6. Let them be preserved for several months.

title: Pikantní omáčka (Piquant Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- not quite one cup of wine vinegar
- one bay leaf
- a sprig of thyme
- three shallots or one small onion
- a pinch of salt and pepper
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Boil not quite one cup of wine vinegar with one bay leaf, a sprig of thyme, three shallots or one small onion, a pinch of salt and pepper down to half
2. Pour beef broth over this
3. Allow to simmer about half an hour
4. Strain it
5. Make a roux, sweeten with a piece of sugar
6. Allow to simmer again and pour into a sauce boat

title: Pineapple Ice Cream (Ananasová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe pineapple
- Lemon juice
- Water
- Dissolved sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the ripe pineapple into pieces
2. Grate on a grater
3. Squeeze lemon juice onto it
4. Dilute with water
5. Sweeten with dissolved sugar
6. Proceed as with other ice creams

title: Pivní Chlebová Polévka (Beer Bread Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter beer
- 80 grams sugar
- lemon peel finely chopped
- piece of butter
- salt
- caraway
- ginger
- grated bread
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Add 80 grams sugar, finely chopped lemon peel, a piece of butter, salt, caraway, ginger, and grated bread to a liter of beer
2. Simmer until everything dissolves
3. Strain
4. Add 2 egg yolks

title: Pivní polévka (Beer Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart beer
- two ounces sugar
- a pinch of salt
- six egg yolks or three whole eggs
- one small cup cream
- one tablespoon flour
- a piece of butter
- a pinch of flowers (spice)
- fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Boil one quart of beer with two ounces of sugar and a pinch of salt
2. in a pot beat six egg yolks or three whole eggs
3. add to them one small cup of cream, one tablespoon of flour, a piece of butter and a pinch of flowers
4. beat well together
5. pour into boiling beer and beat well once more over heat
6. pour the soup into a bowl over fried bread cubes, or over bread cut into cubes, or serve plain in cups
7. for ordinary household use you can use half beer and half water
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Pivní Polévka na Jiný Způsob (Beer Soup, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beer
- Sugar
- Milk
- Salt
- 1, 2 or 3 eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Boil beer and add 1 spoon sugar
2. Boil milk separately (use less milk than beer) and salt according to milk's needs
3. Slowly pour the milk into the beer, stirring constantly to prevent curdling
4. Beat 1, 2 or 3 eggs (depending on soup quantity) in about 2 spoons water and beat well
5. Pour the egg mixture into the hot soup while stirring, being careful not to let it curdle
6. Add sugar according to soup quantity and desired sweetness

title: Pivní polévka s chlebem (Beer Soup with Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of sliced bread
- two quarts beer or one quart beer and one quart water
- a pinch of caraway
- salt
- six egg yolks or three whole eggs
- a piece of butter
- two ounces sugar
- one or two small cups cream
- lemon juice or vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Put about one plate of sliced bread in a pot
2. add two quarts of beer or one quart beer and one quart water
3. add a pinch of caraway and salt
4. let it boil until the bread dissolves
5. in another pot beat six egg yolks or three whole eggs
6. add a piece of butter, two ounces sugar and one or two small cups of cream
7. beat well and pour into the soup
8. let it simmer for a while longer while stirring constantly
9. pour into a bowl
10. if you want it sour, you can add lemon juice or several tablespoons of vinegar
11. this soup can also be made with water instead of beer, omitting the sugar, and is then called beggar's soup (serves 12 people)
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Piškotová buchta (Bohemian Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound (1 cup) fine sugar
- zest of orange or lemon, grated
- six eggs and six yolks
- 2 small cups sifted flour
- fresh butter for greasing the form
- finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Put into a bowl half pound fine sugar, grated orange or lemon zest, six eggs and six yolks, mix until well frothed
2. Add 2 small cups sifted flour and mix well again
3. Grease form with fresh butter
4. Sprinkle with finely chopped almonds
5. Put the prepared batter into it and bake for three-quarters of an hour

title: Piškotová zmrzlina (Biscuit Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- šest žloutků
- litr smetany
- osmina kila cukru
- třetina kila piškotu

## Directions

1. Šest žloutků, litr smetany, osmina kila cukru se smíchá
2. Stále se šlehá
3. Několikrát se zahřeje a zase vychladnout nechá
4. Přidá se třetina kila jemně utlučeného piškotu
5. Smíchá se a v zmrzlinu se přemění

title: Piškotové těsto (Sponge Cake Batter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) beaten sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- lemon
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) flour
- 7 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat together 18 dekagrams beaten sugar, 7 egg yolks, lemon, 9 dekagrams flour, and stiff snow from 7 egg whites
2. Spread on waxed paper with a knife
3. Let it set in the oven
4. Cut into rectangular pieces
5. Spread one half with preserves, cover with the other half, and glaze with icing

title: Piškotový dort (Sponge Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 dekagrams (24 lots) sugar
- 13 egg yolks
- 13 egg whites
- 36 dekagrams flour
- zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Add 13 egg yolks one by one to 36 dekagrams sugar
2. Beat everything
3. Mix in the snow from 13 egg whites
4. Mix in 36 dekagrams flour and lemon zest
5. When everything is beaten, bake in a buttered mold dusted with flour

title: Piškotový dort (Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- half pound sifted sugar
- eight egg whites
- two small cups flour
- butter
- grated and sifted roll

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks in a deep bowl
2. Add half pound sifted sugar and beat for half an hour
3. Add stiff snow made from eight egg whites and mix for another half hour
4. Then add two small cups flour and mix a little longer
5. Butter a form and dust it with grated and sifted roll
6. Pour the batter into the form so it remains two fingers from the top
7. Bake very slowly in the oven
8. Test doneness by piercing with a wire—if it comes out dry, the cake is done
9. Turn out the cooled cake carefully
10. Cover with glaze and decorate with preserved fruit

title: Piškotový dortík (Sponge Cake Small)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- fine lemon zest
- 0.5 kilogram finest potato flour
- egg white snow from remaining egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 0.5 kilogram sugar and add 20 egg yolks one by one
2. Mix and beat everything together
3. Add fine lemon zest
4. Prepare stiff snow from the remaining egg whites and add to the above dough
5. Add 0.5 kilogram finest potato flour
6. Spread the dough in buttered molds and bake slowly and carefully

title: Piškotový Nákyp (Biscuit Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) flour
- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 10 egg yolks
- snow from egg whites
- lemon peel
- 3 1/2 deciliters sour cream
- 3 3/4 deciliters milk

## Directions

1. Beat 18 dekagrams flour, 24 dekagrams sugar, 2 whole eggs, 10 egg yolks, snow from egg whites, and lemon peel together.
2. Grease a mold, sprinkle with flour, and bake the soufflé.
3. Mix 3 1/2 deciliters sour cream and 3 3/4 deciliters milk.
4. Pour this mixture over the soufflé and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Piškotový závin (Jelly Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one small cup sugar
- one and one-half small cups flour
- two teaspoons baking powder
- four teaspoons hot water
- salt
- jelly

## Directions

1. Place three eggs in a bowl
2. Add one small cup sugar and mix well
3. Add one and one-half small cups flour mixed with two teaspoons baking powder
4. Add four teaspoons hot water while continuing to mix
5. Pour onto a pan and bake, but not too thickly—about the thickness of a finger, otherwise it will crack when rolled
6. Prepare a towel, dampen it with warm water, and spread it on a table
7. Turn out the baked pastry onto the towel
8. Spread with jelly and roll using the towel
9. Leave covered for about ten minutes
10. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve

title: Piškoty (Lady Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound fine granulated sugar
- eight egg yolks
- eight egg whites
- two bowls sifted flour

## Directions

1. Put half pound of fine granulated sugar in a bowl
2. Add eight egg yolks and beat until foamy
3. Fold in stiff meringue from eight egg whites and two bowls of sifted flour
4. Pour batter into small molds dusted with flour, or if you do not have these, pour onto paper into finger-length pieces
5. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and bake in a moderately warm oven

title: Piškoty (Sponge Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg whites
- egg yolks
- 36 dekagrams (24 lots) beaten sugar
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) flour

## Directions

1. Beat stiff snow from 12 egg whites
2. Add egg yolks and 36 dekagrams beaten sugar
3. Mix on a warm surface until it warms
4. Remove from heat but continue stirring until it cools again
5. Add 18 dekagrams flour and mix
6. Form cookies, sprinkle with sugar, and bake on a paper-covered sheet

title: Piškoty (Sponge Cookies) - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg whites
- 12 egg yolks
- 250 grams finely beaten sugar
- 250 grams fine flour

## Directions

1. Make snow from 12 egg whites
2. Add 12 egg yolks, 250 grams finely beaten sugar, and beat everything well
3. Place on a moderately heated surface and beat constantly
4. Let cool and heat again, repeating this several times with constant beating
5. Mix in about 250 grams fine flour
6. Spread the dough on paper as desired, sprinkle with sugar, and bake at high heat
7. Do not open the oven before the cookies are baked, or they will fall and shrink
8. Cut the baked cookies carefully
9. Cinnamon, vanilla, cloves, chocolate, crushed almonds, or nuts can be added for flavoring

title: Piškoty karlovarské (Carlsbad Sponge Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 eggs
- 750 grams beaten sugar
- flour (amount not specified)
- lemon zest, finely grated

## Directions

1. Break 20 eggs into a bowl
2. Beat as if making snow from them
3. Add 750 grams beaten sugar and mix
4. Heat several times and let cool again, beating constantly throughout
5. Slowly add flour and finely chopped lemon zest
6. Proceed as with other cookies

title: Placky (Hoe Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 2 eggs
- 6 tablespoons pork or goose lard
- salt
- ginger
- milk

## Directions

1. Place flour on a work surface and make a well in the center
2. Add eggs to the well
3. Add lard and salt
4. Season with ginger
5. Mix with a knife using several tablespoons of milk
6. Work into completely smooth dough with hands
7. Form into thick cakes about the size of a walnut
8. Score with a knife in a cross pattern
9. Arrange on a baking pan
10. Bake slowly in the oven

title: Pletenice (Braided Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kilograms flour
- 3 eggs
- 3/4 liter warm milk
- prepared yeast
- butter

## Directions

1. Make dough from flour, eggs, warm milk, and yeast
2. Let rise for three quarters of an hour
3. Divide in half and roll out
4. Spread thinly sliced butter on one portion and cover with the other
5. Fold in three and roll out three times
6. Divide in two portions
7. Roll out lengthwise
8. Braid together
9. Press ends together
10. Brush with egg
11. Bake at moderate heat

title: Plums in Vinegar (Švestky v octě)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe plums picked by hand
- crushed cinnamon
- cloves
- good wine vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take very ripe plums that were picked by hand, cut off the ends of the stems, place plums in a container
2. Sprinkle with crushed cinnamon and cloves
3. Pour good wine vinegar mixed with sugar, cook together and skim off the foam
4. When the vinegar has cooled, pour it over the plums and seal the container tightly
5. After eight days, pour off the vinegar again, cook it anew, skim off the foam, and when cooled, pour it over the plums and seal the container tightly

title: Podpouška na Kuřata (Asparagus and Crayfish Sauce with Chicken)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned chicken
- butter
- salt
- bundle of asparagus
- salted water
- crayfish
- chicken broth or broth from chicken bones
- 60 grams butter
- flour
- nutmeg flower
- sweet cream
- parsley
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Clean chicken and cut into quarters, salt them
2. Brown chicken on both sides in butter
3. Cook a bundle of asparagus in salted water until soft
4. Cook crayfish in salted water and remove meat from tails and claws
5. To half a liter of crayfish broth add 60 grams of butter mixed with flour, add powdered nutmeg flower and a little sweet cream
6. Boil everything well and add finely chopped parsley
7. If using chicken bone broth instead, thicken it slightly with flour, mix with several spoonfuls of cream, crayfish butter and eggs
8. When serving, arrange crayfish meat in the center, surround with asparagus with heads pointing inward, arrange browned chicken around these
9. Cover crayfish and asparagus with sauce, moisten chicken with its own sauce, garnish the edge with fried bread roll

title: Podpouštka Husí (Goose Fricassee)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted goose
- vinegar
- water or beef broth
- lemon peel
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaves
- pepper
- spice
- salt
- pale roux
- burnt sugar
- Austrian wine
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roasted goose into neat pieces
2. On a baking pan, put part vinegar and part water or beef broth
3. Add a piece of sliced lemon peel, a whole onion, thyme, several bay leaves, a little pepper, spice, and salt
4. Boil everything together; then press pale roux into it, add a piece of burnt sugar and a little Austrian wine
5. Boil again; place the sliced goose on another baking pan and strain the sauce over it
6. Let simmer for half an hour; finally arrange on a platter surrounded by fatty potatoes

title: Podpouštka na telecí maso (Braised Calf Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- calf meat
- boiling water
- butter
- flour
- onion
- celery
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- asparagus
- cauliflower
- bread
- butter
- flour for roux
- 60 grams butter
- lemon juice
- nutmeg
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the washed meat into small pieces, blanch in boiling water, drain and dry
2. Place in hot butter, sprinkle with flour and brown on both sides
3. Pour over with enough strong broth to cover the meat
4. Add onion, celery, parsley, salt, pepper and bay leaf, cover the pot and braise for about two hours
5. Cook asparagus cut into pieces and cauliflower in salted water, as well as bread and meat pieces
6. Make a roux from flour and 60 grams butter, simmer with strained broth from the meat, add lemon juice, nutmeg and several egg yolks
7. Cook the asparagus, cauliflower, bread and meat together with this sauce, season and serve

title: Podpouštka na telecí maso s raky (Braised Calf Meat with Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kilograms calf brisket
- 125 grams butter
- broth
- spices
- root vegetables
- crayfish
- salt
- 120 grams butter for crayfish butter
- bread
- cauliflower
- asparagus
- flour
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the brisket into pieces, blanch and bake in 125 grams of hot butter, then simmer slowly with broth, spices and root vegetables until tender
2. Boil crayfish in salted water, remove the meat, crush the shells with 120 grams butter to make crayfish butter
3. Fill cleaned crayfish shells with chopped meat or bread, cook in crayfish broth
4. Add cauliflower and asparagus cooked in salted water
5. Make a roux, simmer with broth, color with crayfish butter, add nutmeg and pour over the meat, vegetables and bread, garnish with stuffed crayfish shells

title: Podpouštka s mozečkem (Brains in Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 6 calf brains
- cold water
- salted water
- flour
- grated nutmeg
- butter
- onion
- salt
- good broth
- lemon juice
- egg yolks
- parsley

## Directions

1. Rinse calf brains in cold water, remove membranes
2. Cook in boiling salted water, cool in cold water, dry, dust with flour and grated nutmeg
3. Place in butter with sliced onion, season with salt and pour over with good broth and lemon juice
4. Make a sauce by stirring in several egg yolks and chopped parsley, pour over the brains

title: Podpoušťka ze zbytků kachních (Duck Ragout from Leftovers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onion
- flour
- reserved duck stock from the previous day
- broth or water
- several tablespoons of vinegar
- glass of red wine
- sour cucumber, sliced into strips
- bay leaf
- several peppercorns
- new spice
- duck leftovers

## Directions

1. Sauté the chopped onion until translucent, add a little flour
2. Add the reserved duck stock from the previous day, broth or water, several tablespoons of vinegar, a glass of red wine
3. Add the sour cucumber sliced into strips, bay leaf, several peppercorns, and new spice
4. Boil everything for a while
5. Warm the duck leftovers in the sauce

title: Podsvinče (Roast Suckling Pig)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- salt
- bread or breadcrumbs
- caraway seeds
- butter
- beer
- lemon

## Directions

1. Place the not too young suckling pig in cold water, then sprinkle with coarse salt and place in hot water, turning it so it is evenly scalded.
2. Clean it, especially the eyes, ears, and tongue which are rubbed with salt.
3. Cut under the neck and pull out the entire windpipe to avoid unpleasant odor and difficulty with subsequent cleaning.
4. Discard the intestines; save the lungs, liver, and heart; carefully remove the bile.
5. Rinse the pig in several waters, dry it, salt and season with caraway.
6. Cut the trotters to the lower joints.
7. Stuff with bread or breadcrumbs, then sew closed.
8. Place cross-sticks on a baking pan, dissolve fresh butter in a pot, coat the pig well with it, and roast slowly in the oven, basting frequently.
9. To prevent the tail and ears from burning, they may be wrapped in paper.
10. When nearly done, coat it with beer instead of butter to give it shine.
11. Before serving, remove the stuffing; place lemon or something green in its mouth.

title: Podsvinče rosolované (Suckling Pig in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned suckling pig
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- new spices
- onion
- veal trotters

## Directions

1. Salt the cleaned pig.
2. Pour onto a baking pan enough water mixed with 3½ deciliters (half a tablespoon) of good vinegar so that the pig, when placed on the pan, will be submerged.
3. Add pepper, thyme, bay leaf, a piece of lemon zest, new spices, and plenty of onion to the water.
4. To rosolate better, add veal trotters.
5. Boil everything.
6. Place the pig on a platter and pour part of the hot broth over it; pour the rest onto a baking pan.
7. When cooled, carefully roast the pig in that broth on both sides.
8. When cooked, it can either be chopped or placed whole in a mold decorated with various vegetables.
9. Slowly strain the aspic through cloth onto the pig and place on ice to cool; then unmold onto a platter and add pepper and vinegar.
10. If it does not unmold well, wrap the form in a warm cloth.

title: Pohankové Knedličky (Buckwheat Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter buckwheat flour
- one eighth liter boiling water
- bacon cut into cubes
- salt

## Directions

1. Pour half liter of buckwheat flour with one eighth liter of boiling water and mix well.
2. Fry diced bacon with the flour.
3. Form small dumplings from the dough and boil in salted water.

title: Pohankové lívance (Buckwheat Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups lukewarm water
- two cups sweet milk
- one ounce pressed yeast, or one piece of dry yeast
- buckwheat flour
- one teaspoon salt
- two tablespoons syrup
- half teaspoon baking soda dissolved in lukewarm water

## Directions

1. In the evening, take two cups of lukewarm water and the same amount of sweet milk, dissolve one ounce of pressed yeast in it, or soak one piece of dry yeast and gradually add enough buckwheat flour to make a thin batter, cover and let it stand overnight
2. In the morning, add one teaspoon of salt, two tablespoons of syrup and half a teaspoon of baking soda dissolved in lukewarm water, mix well and bake the pancakes on a hot fire
3. If you do not have milk, you can use water, but you can then add two eggs per quart of water, which you beat and add before baking with salt and soda, so that the milk is replaced
4. Syrup is added so the pancakes get a better color
5. From prepared buckwheat flour, pancakes are baked in the same way as from wheat flour

title: Pokroutky hřebíčkové (Clove Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dark clove leaves
- clove spice
- boiling water
- clove essence
- refined fine sugar

## Directions

1. Take dark clove leaves and place them with clove spice in a closed pot
2. Pour enough boiling water to completely submerge the leaves
3. Seal the pot well and let it sit on warm stove for several hours
4. Press out the entire contents completely
5. Add clove essence
6. Boil this liquid with refined fine sugar until completely and firmly thickened
7. Pour it onto paper and when cooled, peel it off and cut into small brittle pieces

title: Pokroutky Hřebíčkové (Clove Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Dark clove leaves
- Clove spice
- Boiling water
- Clove essence
- Fine refined sugar
- Paper

## Directions

1. Take dark clove leaves and place them with clove spice in a sealed container
2. Pour in boiling water until the leaves are completely submerged
3. Seal the container well and leave it on warm stove for several hours
4. Completely press out all the contents
5. Add clove essence to the liquid
6. Cook this liquid with fine refined sugar until it becomes completely thick and firm
7. Pour it onto paper and when cooled, peel it off and cut into small pieces

title: Pokroutky jasmínové (Jasmine Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- jasmine water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare jasmine brittle the same as rose brittle, only do not use any coloring and use jasmine water instead of rose water

title: Pokroutky Oranžové (Orange Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pure refined sugar
- Fine grated orange peel

## Directions

1. Cook the pure refined sugar
2. Slowly add the fine grated orange peel to it
3. Proceed with all else as described for chocolate brittle

title: Pokroutky Růžové (Rose Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crushed sugar
- Rose water
- Carmine
- Rose water

## Directions

1. Cook crushed sugar with rose water until it thickens
2. Add carmine mixed with rose water until everything turns red
3. Proceed as with chocolate brittle

title: Pokroutky Čokoládové (Chocolate Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar
- Grated vanilla chocolate

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar
2. Add grated vanilla chocolate to it
3. Mix slowly and continue cooking with constant stirring until no bubbles appear
4. Pour the thickened liquid onto paper
5. When it hardens, cut it into pieces
6. To remove the paper, first dampen the bottom with a damp sponge

title: Pokroutky čokoládové (Chocolate Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablets of chocolate
- 3 tablets worth of sugar
- 3 tablets worth of raw grated almonds
- flour equal in weight to chocolate
- 3 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- wafer rounds
- 1 blanched almond per piece

## Directions

1. Grate 6 tablets of chocolate
2. Mix it with sugar equal to 3 tablets in weight, the same amount of raw grated almonds, and flour equal in weight to the chocolate
3. Mix everything well, then beat in 3 eggs and 1 egg yolk
4. Work this into a batter like for dripping, so it is not too stiff
5. Place round pieces cut from wafers on waxed baking sheets
6. Pour batter onto each piece, place a blanched, peeled almond in the middle
7. Dry the brittle in the oven

title: Pokroutky čokoládové - s vanilkou (Chocolate Brittle with Vanilla)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- vanilla chocolate

## Directions

1. Cook sugar, add grated vanilla chocolate to it
2. Mix slowly and with constant stirring

title: Polní Salát (Field Lettuce Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- field lettuce leaves
- salt
- wine vinegar
- olive oil or fresh melted butter
- celery salad or watercress

## Directions

1. Separate each leaf of field lettuce individually
2. Rinse each
3. Season with salt
4. Pour wine vinegar over
5. Add olive oil or fresh melted butter
6. Mix well
7. Arrange on a plate around the edge
8. Place celery salad or watercress in the center
9. Serve as a side dish with roasts

title: Polská hovězí polévka (Polish Beef Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef soup
- carrots
- leeks
- whole cabbage
- parsley roots
- celery
- 4 large potatoes
- 0.25 liter water from red beets
- 0.5 liter finely chopped red beets
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- flour grits

## Directions

1. Strain beef soup in which much carrot, leek, whole cabbage, parsley roots, celery and potatoes were boiled
2. Set the vegetables aside, chop the cabbage finely and cut the remaining vegetables into strips, add back to soup
3. Add water from red beets and finely chopped red beets, season with salt and pepper
4. If not sour enough, add vinegar
5. When the soup boils, add a little flour grits, boil a bit more and serve

title: Polská omáčka k hovězímu masu (Polish Sauce for Beef)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion, cut into slices
- slices of smoked meat
- peppercorns
- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- saffron
- wine
- sardine butter
- small piece of lemon peel
- juice from half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the onion into slices and braise it for a while with slices of smoked meat and a few peppercorns in butter
2. Sprinkle with flour and pour beef broth over it
3. Add a pinch of saffron
4. Strain
5. Add wine
6. Mix in sardine butter
7. Add a small piece of lemon peel and finally juice from half a lemon

title: Polský Chlodnik (Polish Cold Beet Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- red beets with greens
- water
- dill
- sour cream (1 liter)
- kvass (0.5 liter)
- hard-boiled eggs
- crayfish tails
- cucumbers
- lemon slices
- pepper
- salt
- ice cream

## Directions

1. Cook red beets with their greens in water, strain, chop the vegetables and mix with dill, pour one liter of sour cream and half liter of kvass
2. When serving, decorate with hard-boiled eggs, crayfish tails, cucumbers cut into cubes, lemon slices, season with pepper and salt, and surround with ice cream

title: Polský Salát (Polish Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast beef
- lettuce hearts
- chopped onion
- a little vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cut cold roast beef into small pieces
2. Add lettuce hearts and chopped onion
3. Season with a little vinegar, mustard, pepper and salt
4. Top with hard-boiled eggs

title: Polévka bobová (Bean Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- Salted water
- A piece of garlic
- A little marjoram
- Salt
- A little pepper
- A piece of celery
- Parsley
- Pink roux (růžovou jíškou)
- Fried bread or bread

## Directions

1. Cook white beans in salted water until completely soft
2. Add a piece of garlic, a little marjoram, salt, a little pepper, a piece of celery, and parsley
3. When everything has cooked, brown with pink roux
4. Strain and pour over fried bread or bread

title: Polévka Bramborová (Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Fresh or clarified butter
- Green parsley
- Marjoram leaves
- Cooked potatoes
- Broth (recipe no. 38)
- Salt
- White butter roux
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Stew onion, green parsley, and marjoram leaves (all finely chopped) in fresh or clarified butter
2. Add cooked potatoes cut into rounds
3. Pour in the broth
4. Season with salt and cook
5. Make a white butter roux and use to thicken the soup
6. Add a little ginger
7. Mix well and simmer a little longer
8. Strain through a fine sieve and serve

title: Polévka Bramborová s Houbami (Potato Soup with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large potatoes
- 2 quarts cold water
- Salt
- 1/2 celery root
- A handful of fresh or dried mushrooms
- 2 spoons butter
- 1 spoon flour
- Garlic
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter 4 large potatoes
2. Add 2 quarts cold water and bring to boil
3. Season with salt and add 1/2 celery root and a handful of mushrooms
4. When potatoes are cooked, remove the celery
5. Make a roux from 2 spoons butter and 1 spoon flour and mix into the soup
6. Crush garlic with salt and add to the soup
7. Add a pinch of marjoram

title: Polévka bylinní (Herbal Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- A piece of butter
- A little salt
- A little flower (kvetu)
- White roux (bílou jíškou)
- Sweet or sour cream
- Sorrel leaves
- Purslane (opence/oponky)
- Daisy leaves
- Strawberry leaves
- Young nettle leaves
- Dandelion leaves
- Salted water
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter, a little salt, and a little flower into water
2. When cooked, brown with white roux and strain
3. Add either sweet or sour cream, mix and cook for a while
4. Cook the tender leaves of sorrel, purslane, daisy, strawberries, young nettles, and dandelion in salted water
5. Drain the water, chop the leaves finely and add to the soup in which 2 egg yolks have been mixed

title: Polévka celerová (Cream of Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- several celery stalks
- salted water
- four ounces of butter
- pepper
- four ounces of pale roux
- two quarts of beef broth
- four egg yolks
- a spoonful of sour cream
- two ounces of butter

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare several celery stalks, let them boil five minutes in salted water, immerse in cold water and when cooled remove and let drain
2. Chop them finely, put in a saucepan with four ounces of butter, salt, pepper and let stew until soft
3. Press through a strainer, thicken with four ounces of pale roux, add two quarts of beef broth, mix well, let boil fifteen minutes and then press through a fine sieve
4. Put back in a pot on the stove and stir frequently, it must be very hot but must no longer boil
5. Before the soup is served, thicken with a liaison of four egg yolks, a spoonful of sour cream and two ounces of butter and pour into a bowl over sliced bread fried pale in butter
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka drobečková (Goose Giblet Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose giblets (excluding liver)
- water (1.5 liters per giblets from one goose)
- vegetables of all kinds
- salt
- herbs
- butter (if goose is lean)
- rye
- thin noodles
- mushrooms or cauliflower (optional)
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the goose giblets, chop them into small pieces, rinse them and place in water
2. Add all kinds of vegetables, salt and herbs
3. If the goose was lean, add a piece of butter and let it simmer for a while
4. Meanwhile, simmer rye in butter and pour the giblet soup over it; or make thin noodles
5. In the latter case, strain the soup, add flour and if there are mushrooms or cauliflower, add them with parsley

title: Polévka dršťková (Tripe Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- about 3 kilos tripe
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- 4 cloves garlic
- parsley
- butter (for roux)
- beef soup (for second version)

## Directions

1. Clean the tripe thoroughly
2. Boil for an hour, rinse again after the water is drained, and cook until completely soft
3. Strain the soup and add marjoram, 4 cloves of garlic, pepper, salt and a handful of finely chopped parsley
4. Add a butter roux to it
5. Strain everything, cut the tripe into strips, boil for a bit longer and season
6. Alternative: drain the second water and use good beef soup instead

title: Polévka Dršťková (Tripe Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- about 3 kilos of tripe
- marjoram
- 4 cloves of garlic
- pepper
- salt
- a handful of finely chopped parsley
- butter roux

## Directions

1. Clean the tripe thoroughly; after it has boiled for an hour, wash it again once the water has been drained, and cook until completely soft
2. Strain the soup and add marjoram, 4 cloves of garlic, pepper, salt and a handful of finely chopped parsley; make a butter roux and add it to the soup
3. Strain everything, cut the tripe into noodles, boil for a while longer and season with salt
4. Alternatively: drain the second water and use good beef broth instead

title: Polévka Hnědá s Bažantem (Consommé of Pheasants)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh bacon sliced into strips
- onions sliced into rounds
- beef and veal slices
- poultry necks
- livers and gizzards from pheasants and partridges
- three parsley roots
- one large celery
- two carrots
- one leek
- beef broth
- one small celery
- parsley root
- three hot salted water-blanched cabbage halves
- one young pheasant salted
- two egg whites
- bread or croutons

## Directions

1. Line a pot or pan with bacon slices, then make a layer of sliced onions, then arrange beef and veal slices, poultry necks, livers and gizzards from pheasants and partridges
2. Add three parsley roots, one large celery, two carrots, one leek, all sliced, cover with a lid and let braise until brown
3. Occasionally check and pour a few spoons of beef broth to prevent burning
4. In a second pot, line the bottom with bacon, sprinkle with thinly sliced onions, add one small celery and parsley root both sliced into small pieces, about three hot salted water-blanched cabbage halves, and place a salted young pheasant on top (if serving more than 12 people, two pheasants are needed)
5. Cover with a lid and let braise until brown, frequently baste with beef broth to prevent burning
6. Remove the cabbage and pheasant, pour the liquid and braised contents from the first pot into a pot, add good beef broth and let cook about half an hour
7. Strain it and wait until it begins to boil again, then add about two egg whites' worth of snow and let cook a moment to clarify the broth
8. Strain either through a thick cloth sieve or through a thin cloth
9. Slice the cabbage into strips and the pheasant neatly and arrange on a platter, around it make a wreath of the cabbage strips and sprinkle with bread cubes fried to golden color
10. This clear broth can be poured into a dish or carried around in pitchers, likewise the pheasant
11. In case of lack of pheasants, partridges work very well for this broth
12. The bread may be cut into half-moons or triangles instead of cubes and fried
13. Good hard bread cubes fried in butter also serve well
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka Hrachová (Pea Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of peas
- soft water
- 2 liters of beef broth
- pepper
- marjoram
- garlic ground with salt
- pale butter roux
- fried bread roll or bread
- or groats or rice cooked in butter

## Directions

1. Cook the peas in soft water until they fall apart into a porridge
2. Pour 2 liters of beef broth over them, add a little pepper, marjoram, garlic ground with salt and a little pale butter roux
3. Strain everything, let it boil for a while and pour it over fried bread roll or bread; alternatively, groats or rice cooked in butter can be used instead of bread

title: Polévka kapaná (Dropped Dough Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 litre salted water
- a piece of fresh butter
- thin dough
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Put a piece of fresh butter into 1 litre of salted water
2. When the water boils, drop in thin dough and add a little chopped parsley

title: Polévka kardinální (Cardinal Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 20-30 crayfish
- salted water
- 150 grams butter
- flour
- 2.5-3 liters clear light meat soup or fish, pea or vegetable soup for fasting
- fried roll, sliced and lightly pounded
- salt
- fried roll cut into cubes
- crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Cook 20-30 crayfish in salted water, shell the tails, crush the rest after removing intestines and gallbladder in a mortar
2. With 150 grams of butter on a low flame, brown with a spoonful of flour
3. Add 2.5-3 liters of clear light meat soup or fish, pea or vegetable soup, cook everything together with fried sliced and lightly pounded roll, salt and strain
4. Add to the soup fried roll cut into cubes with crayfish tails

title: Polévka Krupková se Sušenými Houbami (Barley Soup with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup good torn barley grains
- 1 spoon new butter
- Salted water
- 3 handfuls dried mushrooms
- Green parsley
- Broth (recipe no. 38)
- Water from cooked mushrooms
- Light butter roux
- Salt
- A little pepper
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Cook 1 small cup of torn barley grains with 1 spoon new butter in salted water until soft
2. Cook 3 handfuls of dried mushrooms in a separate pot
3. Drain water from mushrooms and chop them finely with fresh parsley
4. Pour broth (recipe no. 38) and mushroom water over the chopped mushrooms
5. Add the cooked barley grains
6. Thicken with a light butter roux
7. Season with salt
8. Add a little pepper and ginger
9. Let simmer

title: Polévka kyselá ze smetany (Sour Cream Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 litre good sour cream
- 1/2 litre whey
- 2 tablespoons flour
- a little flower
- salt
- fried eggs or 4 beaten egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 litre of good sour cream and 1/2 litre of whey on the stove
2. Add 2 tablespoons of flour, a little flower and salt
3. Stir until completely cooked, then add either fried eggs or 4 beaten egg yolks

title: Polévka mléčná (Milk Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 litre milk
- 2 handfuls flour
- sugar (optional)
- lemon juice (optional)
- roll, fried and diced
- butter

## Directions

1. Place 1 litre of milk on the stove
2. When it begins to boil, mix in 2 handfuls of flour
3. Add sugar if you prefer it sweet, or lemon juice if you prefer it sour
4. Fry a roll in butter, dice it and add to the soup

title: Polévka Mléčná (Milk Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several potatoes
- onion
- chopped parsley
- butter
- bay leaf
- hot water or broth
- half a liter of boiling milk
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- fried bread roll

## Directions

1. Slowly cook several potatoes, drain the water and stew them; meanwhile, stew onion and chopped parsley in butter
2. Press the potatoes through a sieve and add them to the onions, mix well, add one bay leaf and pour in the necessary amount of hot water or broth to make a thin porridge
3. Add half a liter of boiling milk, season with pepper, salt and nutmeg; let the soup boil for a while and pour over fried bread roll

title: Polévka panádlová (Bread Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- six white rolls
- milk
- 8 egg yolks
- herbs
- salt
- beef soup

## Directions

1. Soak the white rolls in milk and press them well
2. Mix the egg yolks with the rolls thoroughly, add a little herbs
3. Add salt and pour 4 glasses of beef soup
4. Boil for about a quarter hour, pour into cups and serve
5. If it is still too thin, add another egg yolk

title: Polévka panádlová (Panada Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- bread rolls, finely broken
- egg yolks
- whole eggs
- butter
- cream
- salt

## Directions

1. To two quarts of boiling water add half a pound of finely broken bread rolls and let it cook for about three quarters of an hour with frequent stirring
2. Beat in a cup four egg yolks or two whole eggs with two tablespoons of butter and a spoonful of cream and pour into the cooked soup, which you must stir diligently
3. Season with salt as desired, pour into a dish and serve
4. Instead of water you can also use milk, which will also make a very tasty soup
5. For 8 people
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka Pivní (Beer Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter beer
- sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 6 egg yolks
- a little smetana (sour cream)

## Directions

1. Pour sugar into a liter of beer and bring to boil
2. When boiling, add 1 tablespoon flour, 6 egg yolks, and a little smetana

title: Polévka Pivní (Beer Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart beer
- 1 quart water
- About 2 spoons sugar
- 1 small spoon butter
- A little nutmeg
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 cup sour cream or milk

## Directions

1. Boil beer with water
2. Once boiling begins, add about 2 spoons sugar, 1 small spoon butter, and grated nutmeg
3. Beat 3 egg yolks with 1 cup sour cream or milk
4. Slowly pour the egg mixture into the boiling soup, stirring constantly to prevent curdling
5. Once the soup begins to rise, remove from heat

title: Polévka Pivní s Chlebem (Beer Soup with Rye Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rye bread
- Water
- Beer
- Salt
- Caraway seeds
- Garlic
- Butter
- 1 cup good sour cream
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut rye bread into a pot, about enough to fill a deep plate
2. Pour half water and half beer (1 quart each)
3. Season with salt and add a little caraway seed
4. Add garlic crushed with a piece of butter
5. Simmer for half an hour
6. Beat 4 egg yolks in 1 cup sour cream
7. Add this mixture to the boiling soup, stirring constantly to prevent curdling
8. Pour into a serving dish

title: Polévka pražená s krupicí (Roasted Semolina Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 litres water
- a little caraway seeds
- a piece of butter
- salt
- children's semolina

## Directions

1. Put a little caraway seeds, a piece of butter and a little salt into 2 litres of water
2. When it boils, strain the soup
3. Meanwhile, roast children's semolina until brown

title: Polévka pražená s krupicí (Roasted Soup with Semolina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters water
- A little caraway
- A piece of butter
- A little salt
- Children's semolina
- A little chopped fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Put a little caraway, a piece of butter, and a little salt into two liters of water
2. After boiling, strain the soup
3. Meanwhile, roast the children's semolina until brown
4. When the soup is boiling, add the semolina and a little chopped green parsley

title: Polévka Rybí (Fish Broth)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water
- beef
- 45 grams fish bone
- salt
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Remove bones from the fish, chop finely, and place in water with beef and at least 45 grams fish bone
2. Add salt and vegetables as in other broths
3. Cook and strain through a sieve so it strains slowly
4. Place on heat and cook
5. Chop fish innards and add to broth, or alternatively leave uncooked like ordinary beef broth and add toasted bread roll

title: Polévka rýžová na způsob krupovky (Rice Soup in the Manner of Barley Groats)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 kilo rice
- 0.5 liter good beef soup
- 7 grams (5 lots) butter
- herbs
- salt
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice and let it soak for 1/8 hour
2. Pour 0.5 liter of good beef soup over it, add butter, herbs and salt
3. Boil with constant stirring until the rice softens and becomes porridge
4. Let it cool, add 8 egg yolks and mix well
5. Pour in beef soup, boil for a quarter hour, strain and pour into cups
6. Best served in the evening

title: Polévka s bramborovými knedličky (Soup with Potato Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of butter
- five egg yolks
- one whole egg
- six tablespoons of grated potatoes
- a heaping spoon of nice flour
- a bit of chopped green parsley
- salt
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Beat three ounces of butter on a plate, add five egg yolks and one whole egg
2. Add six tablespoons of grated potatoes, a heaping spoon of nice flour, a bit of chopped green parsley, and salt
3. Mix and beat everything well together
4. Drop small spoonfuls of the mixture into boiling strained broth as quenelles
5. Let them cook for several minutes then pour into a serving bowl
6. If desired, brown the soup slightly with white roux
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka s chlebem (Consommé Colbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard bread roll, sliced thin
- clarified butter
- onion, sliced into noodles
- green chives, chopped
- parsley, chopped
- beef soup
- flowers
- ginger
- poached eggs

## Directions

1. Slice a hard bread roll thinly and fry it in clarified butter until brown
2. Similarly fry onion sliced into noodles until nicely brown—it must not burn
3. Place both in a soup bowl with chopped green chives and parsley, pour good beef soup over it, season with flowers and ginger
4. Carefully place as many poached eggs as there are people at table into the soup
5. Alternatively the eggs may be served on a separate plate; then neither onion nor chives go into the bowl, but the eggs are placed on the plate, sprinkled with chopped chives and surrounded with a wreath of fried onion
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka s Drštkami (Tripe Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe, well cleaned and rinsed
- cold water
- salt
- beef broth
- green parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- pepper
- garlic
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Put well-cleaned and rinsed tripe in a pot, pour cold water over and simmer half an hour
2. Remove, clean again, rinse in cold water, put in another pot, pour fresh cold water over, salt and simmer until tender
3. Remove, slice into strips and put in a pot
4. Pour beef broth over, add finely chopped green parsley, fry in a small amount of butter, season with ground pepper, add a clove of garlic and a pinch of marjoram
5. Let simmer a while longer and pour into a soup bowl

title: Polévka s dršťkami (Tripe Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- water
- salt
- beef soup
- flower
- pepper
- ginger
- parsley, finely chopped
- white roux for thickening

## Directions

1. Boil the tripe thoroughly in several changes of water and let it cook for about half an hour
2. Remove it onto a board, scrape each piece with a knife or old sharp spoon, trim all the edges, rinse the tripe thoroughly again, place it in another pot, pour clear water over it, salt it and let it cook until tender
3. When sufficiently tender, cut it into noodles and place it in beef soup thickened with white roux, season with ground flower, pepper and ginger, add some finely chopped parsley and serve
4. If the tripe is still tough, let it cook in the unthickened soup long enough until it softens, then thicken the soup
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka s jaterními knedlíčky (Consommé with Calf's Liver Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of calf's or beef liver
- two ounces of beef marrow fat or lard
- marjoram
- lemon peel, finely chopped
- two cloves of garlic rubbed with salt
- flower
- pepper
- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks
- salt
- bread roll, grated
- beef soup
- green parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Chop or grind half a pound of calf's or beef liver, remove all the sinews and membranes and rub it well with two ounces of beef marrow fat or lard
2. Add to it a little marjoram, a little finely chopped lemon peel, two cloves of garlic rubbed with salt, a pinch of flower, a pinch of pepper, beat two whole eggs and two yolks into it
3. Salt it properly and mix in grated bread roll until the mixture is neither too thin nor too thick
4. Mix everything well together and cook it into two quarts of boiling beef soup using a coffee spoon
5. Let it cook for about ten minutes and pour the soup into a bowl over chopped green parsley
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka s Jaterními Knedlíčky (Soup with Calf's Liver Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound calf's or beef liver
- 2 tablespoons carrot fat or butter
- marjoram
- lemon peel, finely chopped
- garlic, minced and mixed with salt
- clove
- pepper
- 2 whole eggs
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Chop or mince the liver and remove all veins and sinews
2. Mix well with the fat or butter
3. Add marjoram, lemon peel, garlic, clove, and pepper
4. Beat in two whole eggs
5. Salt appropriately and mix in enough grated bread so the mixture is neither too thin nor too thick
6. Mix everything well together and form into quenelles
7. Cook in 2 quarts of boiling beef broth
8. Let simmer about ten minutes and serve in a bowl on chopped fresh parsley. Goose liver may be used instead of calf's or beef liver.

title: Polévka s kapankou (Consommé with Dough Drops)
body:
## Ingredients

- two whole eggs
- one teaspoon of melted butter
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Break two whole eggs into a small cup
2. Add one teaspoon of melted butter and just enough flour to make a thin batter
3. Beat it well
4. When the broth is boiling, slowly drip this mixture into the broth and stir frequently so that large lumps don't form
5. Let it cook for a few more minutes
6. If eggs are expensive, you can replace half an egg with milk
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka s Kapáními (Dough Drops Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 teaspoons flour
- 1 egg
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 4 teaspoons flour in a cup, beat an egg into it, salt and beat well until like thin porridge
2. Slowly pour this into boiling beef broth, stirring with a fork as it flows in and simmer about five minutes

title: Polévka s knedličky z racích ocásků (Soup with Crawfish Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pairs of livers and breasts from chickens
- half and one ounce of beef fat
- ten cleaned shelled crawfish tails
- two beaten eggs
- half a grated roll soaked in cream and squeezed out
- crawfish shells
- one whole egg
- one egg yolk
- salt
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Chop all the chicken livers, breasts, and crawfish tails finely
2. Add two beaten eggs, half a grated roll soaked in cream and squeezed out, and mix everything well together
3. Make crawfish butter from the crawfish shells, place a spoonful on a plate, add the chopped mixture
4. Add one whole egg and one egg yolk to this, add a bit of salt and beat it well
5. Mix in the other half of the grated roll
6. Make quenelles and cook them in two quarts of beef broth
7. In a small cup, beat six egg yolks, add them to the boiling broth
8. Finish the soup with crawfish butter
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Polévka s knedlíčky masitými (Soup with Veal Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of cooked veal, preferably leftover
- three slices of white bread
- milk
- two spoons of butter
- two whole eggs
- one egg yolk
- salt
- grated bread or crackers
- lard
- strong hot beef broth

## Directions

1. Chop half a pound of cooked veal into small pieces, add three slices of white bread soaked in milk, put everything in a mortar and mash into a paste, or rub in a bowl.
2. Then rub two spoons of butter until it froths, beat in two whole eggs and one egg yolk, add the mashed mixture, salt it and mix well; if it is still too thin, add a little grated bread, make small dumplings from it, coat them in grated bread or crackers and fry them nicely to a golden color in lard.
3. Place them in a soup bowl and pour boiling strong beef broth over them.

title: Polévka s knedlíčky z bramborů (Soup with Potato Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large floury potatoes
- two spoons of butter
- fresh green parsley
- one whole egg
- three egg yolks
- salt
- grated bread or crackers
- lard or butter
- beef or potato broth

## Directions

1. Cook four large floury potatoes, peel them and grate them on a grater.
2. Rub two spoons of butter until it froths, add finely chopped fresh green parsley, one whole egg, three egg yolks and a little salt. Mix everything together well with the potatoes.
3. Then make dumplings the size of a walnut, coat them in grated bread or crackers, fry them nicely to a golden color in lard or butter, place in a soup bowl and pour beef or potato broth over them (according to recipe number 37).

title: Polévka s knedlíčky z hovězího morku (Soup with Marrow Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of beef marrow
- two whole eggs
- salt
- flowers
- a piece of grated lemon peel
- grated white bread or crackers

## Directions

1. Take two spoons of beef marrow and beat it so long until it whitens, add two eggs one after the other, add a little salt and flowers, a piece of grated lemon peel, mix everything well; then add as much grated white bread or crackers as necessary to make the dough properly firm for dumplings.
2. As a test, cook one dumpling and if it is not sufficiently firm, add a little more bread. Then form dumplings from it.
3. About a quarter hour before serving the soup, cook the dumplings in ordinary beef broth, cover the pot and let it cook slowly for about ten minutes.

title: Polévka s knedlíčky z mozku (Soup with Calf's Brain Quenelles)
body:
