- salt

## Directions

1. Heat milk and cook butter in it
2. Add potato flour mixed with cold milk
3. Stir constantly over heat until dough no longer sticks to the pan
4. Cool completely
5. Add eggs, flour, and salt
6. Form small dumplings
7. Cook in salted water
8. Serve in soup

title: Potato Dumplings with Bacon (Zemákové knedlíky se slaninou)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram grated potatoes
- 90 grams (6 lots) bacon, cut into cubes
- 1 grated roll
- 1 roll, cut into cubes
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 4-5 eggs

## Directions

1. Fry the grated potatoes in the diced bacon
2. When fried for a while, let it cool
3. Add salt, eggs, and form dumplings
4. Flour the dumplings
5. Cook in salted water until they float to the surface
6. Serve with sauces or roasted bread crumbs browned in butter

title: Potato Dumplings Without Eggs (Zemákové knedlíky bez vajec)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- salt
- lemon peel rubbed with sugar
- nutmeg
- sugar
- fried bread, cut into pieces
- flour

## Directions

1. Grate potatoes and mix with butter and salt
2. Add lemon peel rubbed with sugar, nutmeg, sugar, fried bread pieces, and flour
3. Form small dumplings
4. Cook in salted water
5. Serve with braised fruit or sauce

title: Potato Dumplings, Another Method (Zemákové knedlíky - Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 grams (10 lots) butter
- 4 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 500 milliliters good sweet cream
- 120 grams (8 lots) curd cheese
- salt
- several large cooked grated potatoes

## Directions

1. Beat butter with eggs and egg yolks
2. Mix in cream and curd cheese
3. Add salt and grated cooked potatoes
4. Form dumplings
5. Cook in salted water
6. Garnish with grated roll crumbs

title: Potato Nuky (Potato Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, grated
- Egg yolks
- Whole eggs
- Flour
- Cooked potatoes, grated
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Mix 6 decagrams of grated butter with two egg yolks, 2 eggs, a tablespoon of flour, 8 tablespoons of cooked grated potatoes, salt, nutmeg and chopped parsley
2. Shape into nuky with a spoon, cook in salted water, and add to broth

title: Potato Nuky - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Potato flour
- Hot sour cream

## Directions

1. Rub a piece of butter with 75 grams of potato flour and a quarter liter of hot sour cream, until it no longer sticks to the pan

title: Potato Pasties (Zemákové paštiky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12-15 mealy potatoes
- salt
- nutmeg
- onion, chopped
- butter
- 6 egg yolks
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes and pass through a sieve
2. Fry with salt, nutmeg, and chopped onion in butter
3. When the mixture no longer sticks to the pan, transfer to a platter
4. Add egg yolks
5. Roll dough thickly on a board
6. Cut out circles with a form or small glass
7. Brush both sides with beaten egg
8. Sprinkle with grated roll crumbs
9. Mark a circle on the pasties with a smaller glass
10. Bake on butter until golden on both sides
11. Cut out the marked circle, deepen the hole with a spoon
12. Fill with any meat mixture
13. Serve as a main dish or with beef soup

title: Potato Pasties, Another Method (Zemákové paštiky - Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several nice potatoes
- parsley
- onion, chopped
- butter
- salt herring, soaked and deboned
- yeast dough
- cream
- egg
- sardelova sauce

## Directions

1. Slice potatoes and braise with parsley and chopped onion in butter
2. Chop the salt herring into small pieces
3. Line a form with yeast dough
4. Layer chopped herring, cream-covered potatoes, and salt herring
5. Cover with dough
6. Bake for one hour
7. Remove skin with a knife
8. Serve with sardelova sauce

title: Potato Pockets (Zemákové tašky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams (1 pound) cooked grated potatoes
- 2 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- thick sour cream
- sugar
- butter
- egg

## Directions

1. Mix cooked grated potatoes with eggs, egg yolk, flour, and salt
2. Form small mounds and roll into pieces the thickness of a finger
3. Brush with thick sour cream and sprinkle with sugar
4. Cut into wide strips and fold
5. Press edges so cream doesn't leak out
6. Place in hot butter until golden on both sides
7. Brush with sour cream mixed with beaten egg and sugar
8. Bake until a crust forms
9. Serve warm on a platter

title: Potato Puff (Míchané brambory pečené)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups cooked and mashed potatoes
- one egg yolk
- one spoon butter
- 0.5 cup milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take 1.5 cups of cooked and mashed potatoes, preferably leftover
2. Mix in the yolk from one egg, a spoon of butter and half a cup of milk
3. Salt and pepper and finally whip the white from one egg and gently fold it in
4. Butter a small dish, place the mixture in it and bake

title: Potato Soufflé (Nákyp z Brambor)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 egg yolks
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) ground sugar
- a piece of vanilla or lemon zest
- 15 egg whites
- dried potato flour
- 1/2 kilogram (1 pound) potato flour
- butter for greasing
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 15 egg yolks with 1/4 kilogram of ground sugar and a piece of vanilla or lemon zest for 1/4 hour
2. Add stiff snow (beaten egg whites) made from 15 egg whites
3. Sift dried potato flour and add it by the spoonful, 1/2 kilogram total
4. Mix the mixture and bake in a mold greased with butter and sprinkled with grated breadcrumbs

title: Potato Soufflé (Zemákový nákyp)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several potatoes
- salt
- 90 grams (6 lots) butter
- lemon peel, grated
- 120 grams (8 lots) sugar
- cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- bread roll, grated

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in salted water
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Mix over heat with butter, lemon peel, sugar, and cream
4. Remove from heat
5. Add egg yolks
6. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
7. Pour into buttered form sprinkled with grated bread
8. Bake for half an hour, preferably in hot ashes surrounded by coals

title: Potato Soufflé - Another Method (Nákyp z Brambor - Jiný Způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- flour
- sugar
- cinnamon
- 60 grams (4 lots) butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- butter for greasing
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Allow cooked potatoes to cool, peel and grate them
2. Mix equal parts flour and sugar so the mixture weighs half the weight of the potatoes
3. Mix everything and add a little cinnamon
4. Beat 60 grams of butter with 10 egg yolks
5. Add stiff snow made from 10 egg whites, folding in carefully
6. Grease a mold with butter and sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs
7. Bake the soufflé

title: Potato Soufflé - Another Method (Nákyp z Brambor - Jiný Způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) butter
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) almonds
- 3 whole eggs
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- 12 dekagrams sugar
- a plate of grated cooked potatoes
- flower water

## Directions

1. Beat together 24 dekagrams butter, 12 dekagrams almonds, 3 whole eggs, 10 egg yolks, 12 dekagrams sugar and a plate of grated cooked potatoes
2. Add flower water
3. Bake the soufflé in a mold

title: Potato Soufflé - Another Method (Nákyp z Brambor - Jiný Způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- 1/4 kilogram flour
- 1/4 kilogram potatoes
- 1/4 kilogram sugar
- 6 hard, cooled egg yolks
- lemon zest from one lemon
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- 6 whole eggs
- 1.5 deciliters (1/2 žejdlík) wine
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Cook, cool and grate potatoes
2. Mix 1/4 kilogram flour, 1/4 kilogram grated potatoes, 1/4 kilogram sugar
3. Grate 6 hard cooled egg yolks and mix in lemon zest
4. In a separate bowl, beat 9 dekagrams butter
5. Add 6 whole eggs and 1.5 deciliters wine
6. Mix everything together
7. Place on a greased baking pan and bake slowly

title: Potato Soufflé with Cheese (Zemákový nákyp - Jiný způsob se sýrem)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several cooked potatoes
- several egg yolks
- cheese, grated
- salt
- pepper
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate cooked potatoes
2. Mix with egg yolks, grated cheese, salt, and pepper
3. Pour into buttered form
4. Bake for about quarter hour
5. Cover with stiffly beaten egg whites
6. Bake until surface browns

title: Potato Soufflé, Another Method (Zemákový nákyp - Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- baked peeled potatoes
- 240 grams (16 lots) base amount
- 60 grams (4 lots) sugar
- salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 30 grams (2 lots) blanched pounded almonds
- lemon peel, grated
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mash baked peeled potatoes
2. Add sugar, salt, egg yolks, almonds, and lemon peel
3. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Pour into form
5. Bake for half an hour

title: Potatoes with Bacon (Brambory se špekem)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh bacon
- one small onion
- cooked peeled potatoes
- salt
- white ground pepper

## Directions

1. Dice the fresh bacon and one small onion finely and fry them
2. Add cooked, peeled potatoes cut into slices
3. Salt and season with a little white ground pepper
4. Fry the potatoes until golden brown

title: Potažené třešnč a višnč cukrem (Cherries and Sour Cherries Coated with Sugar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries or sour cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Trim the stems halfway.
2. Place the cherries on a small baking pan and pour thickened, strained sugar over them, in which they soak well.
3. Remove them and coat them several times in powdered, fine sugar, then place them on a sieve to dry.

title: Potápka zadělávaná (Stuffed Diver)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- diver
- bacon
- pepper
- allspice
- 2 onions
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the diver and cut off the head and legs.
2. Lard the cleaned bird with bacon, or wrap it in bacon strips.
3. Add a few peppercorns, allspice, and 2 finely chopped onions.
4. Season with salt and roast until golden, turning it so it browns evenly on both sides.
5. Chop and pour sauce over it, then sprinkle with fried breadcrumbs or make a wreath of fried breadcrumbs.
6. Alternatively, make a dark sauce as for game and place the diver in it.

title: Povidla (Plum Preserve Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- plum preserve or preserve from other fruit
- water or wine
- fine spices

## Directions

1. Dilute the preserve with water or wine
2. Before use, boil it
3. Season with fine spices

title: Povidla z bezinek (Elderberry Preserves)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe elderberries
- Butter - a piece
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Wine (optional)
- Sweet cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Strip ripe elderberries
2. Sprinkle with water
3. Stew on a baking pan with butter, sugar, and cinnamon
4. Those who wish may add a little wine
5. If to be stored, cover well
6. If to be eaten soon, add a little sweet cream

title: Povidla z rajských jablek (Preserves from Tomatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe tomatoes
- Sugar - 14 dekagrams per 30 tomatoes

## Directions

1. Mash ripe tomatoes in a container
2. Cook until soft
3. Strain
4. Add calculated sugar
5. Stir until preserves are completely thickened
6. These preserves can be stored and used for making sauce or as filling

title: Povézky (Bread and Plum Sweetmeat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- hard rolls
- one kilogram dried plums
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- milk
- egg
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Cut hard rolls into small flat cakes.
2. Cook one kilogram dried plums in water to which sugar, a little cinnamon, and lemon zest have been added.
3. When soft, strain through a sieve so water drains off.
4. When cooled, chop the plums, dust with sugar, add sugar-rubbed lemon zest, and rapidly boiled fruit syrup.
5. Brush the bread cakes on one side, place two together, arrange on a shallow dish and pour milk over.
6. Before serving, dip in egg, roll in grated bread, fry on both sides in butter, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Povézky (Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- hard rolls cut into cakes
- one kilogram dried plums
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- eggs
- grated roll
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut hard rolls into cakes
2. Cook one kilogram dried plums in water with a piece of sugar, a little cinnamon and lemon zest
3. When soft, place on a sieve to drain
4. When cool, chop, sprinkle with sugar, add lemon zest rubbed with sugar and rapidly boiled juice
5. Brush roll cakes on one side, put two and two together, pour milk over on a shallow plate
6. Before serving, dip in egg, roll in grated roll, fry on both sides in butter

title: Povézky s mozečkem (Bread and Brains Sweetmeat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two fresh brains
- warm water
- hot water
- butter
- salt
- eggs
- hard rolls
- milk

## Directions

1. Soak two fresh brains in lukewarm water, remove all membranes, cook in hot water, and strain through a sieve so water drains.
2. Melt butter in a baking pan, place the brains in it, salt, and fry with two eggs over moderate heat until a thick paste forms.
3. Remove from heat and let cool.
4. Remove crusts from hard rolls, cut into small flat cakes, dip in milk, spread with the brain mixture, place two cakes together, dip in egg, roll in bread, and fry in butter.
5. May be served after beef broth.

title: Pražená polévka (Brown Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- caraway seeds
- salted water
- flour for brown roux
- toasted bread cubes or croutons
- noodles, or drops of batter

## Directions

1. Let a lot of caraway cook in salted water.
2. Meanwhile, make a brown roux, strain the water from the pot into it and let it cook well.
3. Pour into a bowl with toasted bread cubes, or you can add noodles, or drops of batter to it.

title: Pražená polévka s kmínem (Soup of Caraway Seed)
body:
## Ingredients

- plenty of caraway seed
- salt
- water
- flour for roux

## Directions

1. Let plenty of caraway seed boil in salted water
2. Meanwhile make a brown roux
3. Strain the water from the pot into the roux and let boil well
4. Pour into a tureen on fried bread cubes, or you can add groats, noodles, or dumplings to it
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Pražené mandle (Roasted Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- 1/4 liter water
- 1/2 kilogram grated almonds

## Directions

1. Boil half kilogram of sugar in quarter liter of water
2. Add half kilogram of grated almonds
3. Stir over heat until they begin to crack
4. Remove from heat and stir until dry
5. Stir again over heat until they shine
6. Lay one beside another on a baking sheet to cool completely

title: Pražské koláče (Prague Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams (25 lots) flour
- 4 egg yolks mixed in sour cream and salted
- 20 decagrams (almost 13 lots) butter
- 4 egg whites
- Ground sugar
- Vanilla
- Almond slivers for topping

## Directions

1. Place flour on a work surface, make a depression in the middle, pour in 4 egg yolks mixed with sour cream and salted
2. Knead the dough with 20 decagrams butter
3. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a knife, cut out round cakes, place on a floured baking sheet
4. Make snow from 4 egg whites, mix in ground sugar and vanilla
5. Brush the cakes with this mixture, sprinkle with almond slivers and bake at moderate heat

title: Pražské kotletky (Veal Cutlets, Prague Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound veal meat from the ribs
- one quarter pound beef marrow
- one shallot or small onion
- fresh parsley
- lemon zest, finely chopped or grated
- salt
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- clarified butter or lard
- rib bones
- lemon juice
- fried potatoes or vegetables

## Directions

1. From one pound of veal meat from the ribs, remove the bones
2. Remove sinews and tendons and chop finely with one quarter pound of beef marrow, one shallot or small onion, some fresh parsley, and finely chopped or grated lemon zest
3. Salt it
4. Mix well
5. Form into cutlets three fingers wide and as long as a finger
6. Dip in beaten eggs
7. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs
8. Insert a well-scraped rib bone into each one
9. Fry in hot clarified butter or lard
10. Arrange on a plate
11. Drizzle with lemon juice
12. Surround with fried potatoes
13. You may also surround with vegetables

title: Preclíčky cukrové (Sugar Pretzels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- 125 grams fresh butter, torn into pieces
- 100 grams crushed sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 egg yolks
- sour cream as needed
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix flour, butter, sugar, egg, egg yolks, and salt
2. Add sour cream as needed and work into light dough
3. Roll out completely
4. Form into small braided strips
5. Make small pretzels from these strips
6. Place on flour-dusted baking sheet
7. Brush with beaten egg whites
8. Sprinkle with coarsely crushed sugar
9. Bake slowly until golden brown

title: Preclíčky klášterní (Monastery Pretzels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams fine flour
- 50 grams butter
- 70 grams sugar
- egg
- yolk
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix flour, butter, sugar, egg, yolk, and salt
2. Work into a firm dough
3. Let rest for at least half an hour
4. Roll out
5. Shape into pretzels
6. Brush with beaten yolk
7. Bake on buttered baking sheet

title: Preserved Chestnuts (Zavařené kaštany)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful chestnuts
- water
- lemon juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the most beautiful chestnuts, throw them into boiling water and let them cook strongly, then remove
2. In another container in boiling water finish cooking them completely
3. Completely cooked chestnuts are cleaned, peeled again, placed in lukewarm water with lemon juice
4. When cooled, rinse in water, dry and place on a dish, pour over with dissolved sugar
5. Cook together several times and let cool
6. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it more concentrated up to the sixth degree, add the chestnuts, and when they have been cooked several times, place them in containers

title: Preserved Green Grapes (Sušené zelené hrozny)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes, green and undeveloped
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the grapes as described in the previous section
2. On the second day, pour off the sugar and cook it to the sixth degree
3. Add the grape berries to it, cook everything together
4. Skim off the foam and select the grapes to dry
5. Dust them finely with pounded sugar, coating them thoroughly
6. Dry again and turn frequently
7. Once completely dried, store between fine paper in boxes

title: Preserved Green Grapes (Zavařené zelené hrozny)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large, well-developed, still green grapes
- sugar
- soft water

## Directions

1. Select large, undeveloped, still green grapes
2. Remove the seeds and cook the grapes in soft water several times
3. Cover the vessel and when the berries are soft enough to crush with fingers, remove to a sieve to dry
4. For equal weight of grape berries, take sugar and cook to the sixth degree
5. Add the wine berries to it and cook everything together
6. Skim off foam, pour the preserve onto a dish and cover with paper
7. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, add the berries, cook together several times, skim foam, and pour into jars after cooling
8. Alternatively, on the second day place berries in jars and pour the thickened, well-cooked sugar over them once cooled

title: Preserved Medlars (Zavařené mišpulky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large, ripe medlars
- soft water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select large, ripe medlars, cut them in half, remove the seeds
2. Blanch in soft water, and when cooled, place on a dish
3. Pour over with cooled dissolved sugar, in which they are allowed to rest quietly until the next day
4. After two days, always pour off the sugar, cook it more concentrated, and when cooled, pour it over the medlars
5. On the last day, cook together, skim off the foam and place the preserve in containers

title: Preserved Medlars (Zavařené mišpulky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large, ripe medlars
- soft water
- cold sugar

## Directions

1. Select large, ripe medlars
2. Cut them in half
3. Remove the seeds, cook in soft water and when cooled, place on a dish
4. Pour cold sugar over them

title: Preserved Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds firm ripe peaches that cling to the pit
- 4 pounds sugar
- 2 ounces whole cinnamon
- 1 quart vinegar
- 2 cloves per peach

## Directions

1. Clean the peaches well with cloth
2. Combine sugar, cinnamon, and vinegar and bring to a boil, cooking for five minutes
3. Insert two cloves into each peach
4. Cook the peaches in the liquid until soft enough to pierce with a fork
5. Remove peaches and place in jars
6. Boil the liquid down to half its volume
7. Pour over the peaches
8. Follow general preservation rules for canning

title: Preserved Quinces (Zavařené kdoule)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe, large quinces
- soft water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select very ripe, large quinces, cut them into long, narrow pieces, remove the seeds, peel the quinces
2. Cook loosely in soft water, remove softened pieces, let the remaining pieces cook further until soft, then remove and place on a sieve to cool and dry
3. Arrange on a dish, pour over with dissolved sugar
4. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it more concentrated than the day before, add the quinces, cook slowly with it
5. Repeat this for three more days, and when the quinces are completely candied and the sugar turns to pearls, place in containers, and when dry, wrap them
6.

title: Preserved Redcurrant Berries (Jahůdky ryvízové zavařené)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned, ripe redcurrant berries
- sugar, cooked to thickness

## Directions

1. Place cleaned ripe redcurrant berries into boiled sugar when it is at its peak boiling.
2. Skim the foam, pour everything into a pitcher, cover with paper, and let sit undisturbed overnight.
3. On the second day, drain the syrup and boil until thickened.
4. Add the berries to it, stir until the liquid becomes completely thick.
5. Then place in containers; when cooled, seal with a bladder.

title: Preserved Rosehips (Zavařené šípky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, large, fully ripe rosehips
- sugar, cold and thin at first, then progressively thicker

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, large, completely ripe rosehips.
2. Cut them lengthwise and clean them completely.
3. Cook them in fresh river water until soft.
4. Place in cold water to cool completely.
5. Arrange on a baking sheet and pour over them a cold, thin sugar syrup.
6. On the second and third days, drain off the sugar each day and cook it progressively thicker and thicker, then pour the cooled sugar back over the rosehips.
7. Continue this process until the fourth day, then pour hot sugar over the rosehips and cook together several times.
8. Skim off the foam and place the preserve in containers.

title: Preserved Rosehips, Another Method (Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- red, ripe rosehips, not yet soft
- sugar, half a kilogram per half kilogram of rosehips
- water, one quarter liter per half kilogram of sugar
- lemon juice, from two lemons
- cinnamon, optional

## Directions

1. Wipe red, ripe rosehips that are not yet soft with a cloth.
2. Trim the stem and flower remnants with a small stick without cutting open the rosehips.
3. Remove the seeds, wash them, drop them into boiling water, and cook until half-done.
4. Cool in cold water and place on a sieve to drain.
5. For each half kilogram of rosehips, use half a kilogram of sugar cooked in one quarter liter of water.
6. When the foam is skimmed off, pour the hot sugar over the rosehips.
7. On the second day, cook the sugar again and cook the rosehips with it once; repeat this on the third and fourth days.
8. On the last day, add juice from two lemons, pour thick sugar over the rosehips, and when cooled in jars, seal them.
9. Sometimes cinnamon is added to the sugar, which must be removed before sealing.

title: Preserved Seedless Redcurrant Berries (Zavařené bezpecké jahůdky ryvízové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- the most beautiful and largest redcurrant berries
- sugar, thickly cooked

## Directions

1. Pick the most beautiful and largest berries from the bunches.
2. Carefully remove the small seeds from them using a pointed stick.
3. Pour the prepared berries into thickly cooked sugar.
4. Cook everything slowly and place in small containers, preferably jars.
5. When cooled, seal with paper and a bladder.

title: Preserved Walnuts (Zavařené ořechy)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- well-developed but unripe walnuts
- fresh water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Select well-developed but unripe walnuts
2. Wash them and cut off the tips from top and bottom
3. Pierce them three to four times completely through
4. Place in fresh water and soak for fourteen days, changing the water daily
5. Then squeeze them by hand until soft
6. Place softened walnuts in fresh water and let sit quietly
7. On the second day, pour off the water, pour purified, very thin sugar over them and let sit quietly overnight
8. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, let it cool, and pour back over the walnuts
9. Repeat this for fourteen days until the sugar completely crystallizes
10. Add ground cinnamon and cloves
11. Layer in jars, bind well, and store in a dry, cool place

title: Preserved Walnuts, Alternative Method (Jiný způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts with soft shells
- cold water

## Directions

1. In July, when the shell is still soft, pick the walnuts
2. Pierce them with a needle or sharp stick
3. Soak in cold water for 12-14 days, renewing the water daily
4. Then cook slowly

title: Preserving Bilberries with Apples (Zavařování brusnic s jablky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- bilberries
- sugar, 1 kilogram per 2 liters of bilberries
- cinnamon, a few pieces
- apples, 20 of good quality
- or sweet pears instead of apples

## Directions

1. Sort through and clean the bilberries, scald them, and drain the water.
2. Add 1 kilogram of sugar and a few pieces of cinnamon per 2 liters of bilberries.
3. Boil the mixture, skim off the foam, and when the individual berries begin to burst, remove from heat and cover with half the syrup.
4. Cut 20 apples of good quality into halves, remove the seeds, place in the remaining half of syrup, sprinkle with sugar, and cook until dissolved.
5. Combine the bilberries with the apples, mix well, place in glazed containers, and store sealed.
6. Instead of apples, sweet pears may be used.

title: Preserving Plums in Vinegar (Zavařování švestek v octě)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sugar
- ground cinnamon
- cloves, several
- good wine vinegar, two cups

## Directions

1. Pit the plums and place them in a pot.
2. Cover with sugar and add ground cinnamon and several cloves.
3. Pour in two cups of good wine vinegar.
4. Place the pot in cold water so that the water reaches halfway up the sides of the pot.
5. Tie the pot with paper and boil the water for a full hour.
6. Then tie the pot with a bladder and let it cool in the water.
7. For the first 14 days, shake the pot occasionally so the plums sink to the bottom.
8. It is best to seal the pot with some fat so that air cannot enter.

title: Pstruzi (Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- salt
- wine vinegar
- water
- thyme
- bay leaf
- onion
- whole pepper
- new spices
- ginger
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Scale the trout, eviscerate, and leave whole
2. Salt them
3. Boil good wine vinegar mixed with water
4. Pour the boiling vinegar mixture over the trout
5. Add thyme, bay leaf, onion cut into thin strips, whole pepper, new spices, a piece of ginger, and lemon zest cut into thin strips
6. Simmer for a quarter hour
7. Place on a platter and add all the ingredients that were cooked with them

title: Pstruzi - Another Method (Trout in Water)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- water
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- onion

## Directions

1. Clean the trout, eviscerate, and place in boiling water
2. After 10 minutes, place on a platter
3. Add oil, vinegar, pepper, and a small whole onion

title: Pstruzi na modro (Blued Brook Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- vinegar
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- white wine
- spices for carp

## Directions

1. Pour boiled salted vinegar over sliced trout so they turn a nice blue and then arrange them in a kettle, add to them all the spices as for carp, pour over them one part fresh water, one part vinegar and one part white wine, only enough so that the trout are covered, salt them and let them boil for a quarter hour.
2. Then remove the fish onto a dish, strain the sauce over them and let them cool.

title: Pstruzi Nadívaní (Stuffed Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large trout
- carp meat
- mushrooms
- truffles
- parsley
- eggs
- onion
- white bread
- milk
- pepper
- nutmeg
- thyme
- white wine
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and eviscerate larger trout and rinse
2. Make stuffing from carp meat, mushrooms, truffles, parsley, eggs, onion, white bread soaked in milk, pepper, nutmeg, and thyme
3. Season with salt and cook with white wine
4. Cool the mixture and drain
5. Fill the trout with the stuffing
6. Dip in beaten egg, coat with grated breadcrumbs
7. Fry in butter
8. Serve with tomato sauce

title: Pstruzi Pečeni (Baked Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned trout
- salt
- white pepper
- lemon juice
- olive oil
- breadcrumbs
- anchovy sauce or crayfish sauce or caper sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and eviscerate trout, rinse, dry, cut into pieces
2. Salt and season with white pepper
3. Marinate in lemon juice for half an hour
4. Dip in good olive oil, coat with grated breadcrumbs
5. Bake until brown
6. Serve with anchovy, crayfish, or caper sauce

title: Pstruzi Pečeni (Fried Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and gutted trout
- salt
- white pepper
- lemon juice
- good olive oil
- grated bread
- sardine sauce, crayfish sauce, or caper sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the trout, wash and dry them, cut into pieces
2. Salt and sprinkle with white pepper, then place in lemon juice
3. After half an hour, dip in good olive oil, coat in grated bread, and bake until brown
4. Serve with sardine sauce, crayfish sauce, or caper sauce

title: Pstruzi po Švýcarsku (Trout à la Suisse)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter wine
- 1 liter broth
- salt
- rye bread
- onion
- parsley
- 2 slices lemon
- pepper
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix wine with broth and salt
2. Add fried rye bread, onion, parsley, two slices of lemon, and a little pepper
3. Boil everything together thoroughly and strain
4. Place trout in this sauce and braise
5. Strain the sauce, brown with flour, and serve with the trout

title: Pstruzi s Majonésou (Trout with Mayonnaise)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large trout
- mayonnaise
- egg
- anchovies
- aspic
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean and cut larger trout into pieces
2. Cook in court-bouillon
3. Remove from skin, remove bones
4. Arrange in mayonnaise on a platter, neatly arranged with the sauce
5. Garnish variously with eggs, anchovies, aspic, or capers

title: Pstruzi s Omáčkami (Trout with Sauces)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- oyster sauce or caper sauce or anchovy sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the trout and remove the bones
2. Cut into pieces, dip in beaten egg, coat with grated breadcrumbs
3. Fry in butter
4. Serve with oyster, caper, anchovy, or tomato sauces

title: Pstruzi s Račí Omáčkou (Trout with Crayfish Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned trout
- salt
- crayfish butter
- crayfish sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and cut trout into pieces
2. Salt and arrange in a small baking dish greased with crayfish butter
3. Braise until completely soft
4. Pour crayfish sauce over and serve

title: Pstruzi s vinem (Brook Trout in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- salt
- shallots or onion
- burgundy wine
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Clean the trout, gut them, salt them and let them lie for an hour.
2. Line the bottom of a pan with sliced shallots or onion, place the prepared trout on top, pour a pint of burgundy wine over them, add several bay leaves to them and let them boil for a quarter hour.
3. Then remove them onto a dish, pour the sauce over them and bring them quickly to table.

title: Pstruzi s Vínem (Brook Trout in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brook trout
- Shallots or onion, sliced
- Burgundy wine, one pint
- Bay leaves, several

## Directions

1. Clean, scale, salt the trout and let them lie for one hour
2. Line the bottom of a pot with sliced shallots or onion
3. Place the prepared trout on top
4. Pour one pint of Burgundy wine over them
5. Add several bay leaves
6. Let them cook for a quarter hour
7. Remove the trout to a dish
8. Pour the sauce over them and serve quickly to the table

title: Pstruzi se Zeleninou (Trout with Vegetables and Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large trout
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- coulis
- lemon juice
- anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Eviscerate larger trout, rinse, cut into pieces, skin, and remove bones
2. Salt and braise in butter with finely chopped onion, parsley, and mushrooms on both sides, being careful not to brown the delicate vegetables
3. Drain the butter, season, add a little coulis
4. Finish with lemon juice and anchovy butter
5. Cook gently and arrange neatly on a platter

title: Pstruzi se Zeleninou (Trout with Vegetables)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- young peas
- asparagus

## Directions

1. Prepare trout according to the previous method
2. Serve with young peas, asparagus, and other vegetables

title: Pstruzi Smaženi (Fried Trout)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- small trout
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- butter
- parsley
- vegetables
- lettuce
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean, gut, wash, and salt the small trout and let rest at least half an hour in a cold place
2. Dry in clean cloth
3. Coat in flour and beaten eggs, then sprinkle several times with finely grated bread crumbs
4. Fry carefully in hot butter
5. Garnish with green parsley, fine vegetables, lettuce, or lemon cut into quarters

title: Pstruzi v Aspiku (Trout in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- small trout
- vinegar
- water
- vinegar for cooking
- bay leaf
- pepper
- onion
- lemon
- salt
- lemons for decoration
- truffles
- crayfish tails
- hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- aspic

## Directions

1. Clean, gut, and wash the trout carefully
2. Pour boiling vinegar over them, cover with paper, and let rest until they turn completely blue
3. Prepare only as much water as needed to cover the trout, add vinegar, bay leaf, pepper, onion, lemon, and salt, then cook
4. Add the trout to this liquid but do not boil; after a quarter hour remove them and let cool
5. Decorate molds with lemons, truffles, crayfish tails, hard-boiled eggs, and capers
6. Pour aspic over the decorations, and when set, arrange the trout sliced lengthwise and sparsely throughout the mold
7. Pour aspic over again and layer in stages, repeating until the mold is full
8. Place the full mold on ice to set the aspic
9. Unmold and arrange the trout in various ways, tastefully decorated, and serve

title: Pstruzi v Tokijském Víně (Trout in Tokay Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned trout
- salt
- Tokay wine
- bay leaf
- rosemary
- saffron
- flour
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make incisions on both sides of the cleaned trout
2. Salt and pour Tokay wine over
3. Layer with bay leaf, rosemary, and a little saffron
4. Cook until tender
5. Strain the sauce, mix with flour and butter, and adjust with lemon juice
6. Pour over the fish and serve

title: Pstruzi v Červeném Víně (Trout in Red Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned trout
- salt
- onion
- pepper
- parsley
- red wine

## Directions

1. Cut cleaned trout into pieces and salt
2. Arrange on a base of onion, pepper, and parsley
3. Cover with red wine
4. Simmer for a quarter hour
5. Remove the trout
6. Strain the sauce, brown with flour, and pour over the fish

title: Pstruzi Špikovaní (Trout with Crayfish Tails)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- crayfish tails
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- lemon zest
- onion
- clove

## Directions

1. Braise trout until not completely soft
2. When cooled, insert crayfish tails into the meat
3. Make a special sauce from 70 grams of melted butter, 1 spoon flour browned, bouillon, lemon zest, onion, and clove
4. Simmer together
5. Strain and pour over the trout

title: Ptáčky Bez Kostí Na Jiný Způsob (Boneless Birds, Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of lean pork meat
- 2 tablespoons of lard
- 1 large onion
- paprika (a knife tip)
- raw rice (1 coffeepot)
- meat gravy from the cooked meat
- cabbage or vine leaves
- half-boiling water or broth

## Directions

1. Cut the two pounds of lean pork meat into small pieces and place 2 tablespoons of clean lard in a pot
2. Chop one large onion and add it to the lard along with a knife tip of paprika
3. When the onion begins to yellow, add the meat and salt it; stir and cover with a lid
4. Let it braise over gentle heat until half-cooked without adding any water, as the meat will give off enough juice on its own
5. When the meat is half-tender, remove it to a board and chop it finely
6. Place the chopped meat in a bowl and add one coffeepot of raw rice as well as the gravy from the meat; mix everything together well
7. Lay this mixture on cabbage or vine leaves and roll it up like an envelope
8. Layer these rolls in a pot and pour half-boiling water or broth over them
9. Let it braise for one hour

title: Punch Glaze (Polévání punčové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- lemon juice
- rum
- water

## Directions

1. Mix sifted sugar with juice from half a lemon, half a teaspoon of rum, and a few drops of water
2. Apply to pastry while lukewarm

title: Punč z Vajec (Egg Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle white wine
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Lemon zest
- 8-10 eggs
- 30 dekagrams (20 lots) sugar
- Warm arrack

## Directions

1. Mix 1 bottle of white wine, lemon juice, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, 8-10 eggs, and 30 dekagrams of sugar
2. Place the bowl in hot water
3. Beat the mixture until it froths
4. Finally add warm arrack

title: Punč z Vajec - Another Method (Egg Punch - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- Juice of 3 lemons
- Juice of 2 sweet oranges
- 3 bottles red wine
- 1 bottle rum
- 1 kilogram sugar

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks, lemon juice, red wine, and sugar
2. Heat and beat until foaming
3. Remove from heat
4. Slowly add rum
5. Serve on the table

title: Punč z Vajec - Another Method II (Egg Punch - Another Method II)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 whole eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 liter water
- 1 liter wine
- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- Zest of several lemons
- 0.5 liters good rum
- Juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Crush 6 whole eggs and mix with 6 egg yolks using a whisk
2. Slowly add 1 liter of water and 1 liter of wine
3. Add 0.5 kilogram of sugar that has been rubbed with lemon zest
4. Continuously beat the mixture and heat slowly
5. Remove from stove
6. Slowly add 0.5 liters of good rum and juice from 3 lemons

title: Punčovina (Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams sugar
- Zest of 3 lemons
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 0.75 liters boiling water
- 0.5 liters good rum

## Directions

1. Rub the sugar with zest from 3 lemons
2. Crush the sugar and squeeze juice from 2 lemons into it
3. Pour in 0.75 liters of boiling water
4. Mix everything together thoroughly until the sugar dissolves completely
5. Add 0.5 liters of good rum
6. The punch is ready to serve

title: Punčová omáčka (Punch Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- orange sauce (sherbet)
- good rum

## Directions

1. Make an orange sauce (sherbet)
2. Add good rum to taste

title: Punčová zmrzlina (Punch Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cukr
- kůra ze dvou citronů
- hořký pomeranč
- štáva z osmi citronů
- štáva z jednoho pomeranče
- voda
- arák

## Directions

1. Na cukr se otrou kůry z dvou citronů a hořkého pomeranče
2. Vylisuje se štáva z osmi citronů a jednoho pomeranče
3. Přidá se trochu vody
4. Vše se osladí, procedí a dá umrznouti
5. Při upravování na zmrzlinu se přileje trochu araku

title: Punčové Polévání (Punch Glaze)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sifted sugar
- juice from half a lemon
- half tablespoon of rum
- several drops of water

## Directions

1. Mix sifted sugar with juice from half a lemon
2. Add half tablespoon of rum and several drops of water
3. Apply lukewarm to baked goods

title: Punčový Nákyp (Punch Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of cream
- 95 grams (6 lots) butter
- 180 grams (12 lots) sugar
- 180 grams flour
- 10 egg yolks
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- juice from 2 lemons
- half a liter of rum
- a little salt
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook half a liter of cream, 95 grams (6 lots) of butter, and 180 grams (12 lots) of sugar together
2. Sprinkle in 180 grams of flour and stir over gentle heat until a paste forms
3. Let cool
4. Mix in 10 egg yolks, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, juice from 2 lemons, half a liter of rum, and a little salt
5. Fold in stiff egg white snow
6. Bake for one hour

title: Punčový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob (Punch Soufflé, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bottle of wine
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) beaten sugar
- 16 peeled Meissen apples
- 1 bottle of punch
- finely beaten sugar

## Directions

1. Cook half a bottle of wine with 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of beaten sugar and 16 peeled Meissen apples on a baking sheet with constant stirring
2. When completely softened, pour in 1 bottle of punch and sprinkle with finely beaten sugar
3. Pass a red-hot spatula several times over the sugar to form a glaze
4. Serve the soufflé while it is still warm

title: Pupencové zelí (Brussels Sprouts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- poupata (sprouts)
- česnek (garlic)
- sůl (salt)
- marjánkové plevy (oregano)
- pepř (pepper)
- nové máslo (fresh butter)
- hovězí polévka (beef broth)
- bledá jíška (pale roux)
- cukr upálený (caramelized sugar)
- hovězí maso (beef)

## Directions

1. Poupata se olámou, očistí, dají na pekáček, spaří se a po pěti minutách se voda slije
2. 2 stroužky česneku se utrou v soli, přimíchá marjánkových plev, pepře, nového másla, všechno se přidá k poupatům a pod poklicí se dusí
3. Aby se nepřipálila, podlévají se hovězí polévkou
4. Bledá jíška se protlačí na poupata, udělá se trochu omáčky, přidá kousek upáleného cukru a dává kolem hovězího masa

title: Purée z Holubů (Purée of Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from four roasted pigeons
- bones from the pigeons
- two quarts beef broth
- one onion
- one parsley root
- one celery stalk
- one carrot
- salt
- pinch of cumin
- pinch of marjoram
- several cloves
- a piece of mace
- several grains of pepper
- a spoon of boiling Madeira wine
- six hard-boiled egg yolks
- a piece of butter

## Directions

1. Remove meat from four roasted pigeons and break the bones, place them in a pot with two quarts of beef broth
2. Add one onion, one parsley root, one celery stalk, and one carrot, all cut into small pieces
3. Salt it and let it cook for one hour, then strain through a cloth into another pot
4. In a small cup, place a pinch of cumin, a pinch of marjoram, several cloves, a piece of mace, and several grains of pepper, pour a spoon of boiling Madeira wine over it, cover and let it steep
5. Pound the pigeon meat in a mortar with six hard-boiled egg yolks and a piece of butter
6. Mix it well into the broth, strain once more through a cloth and place on a hot surface, but it must not boil anymore
7. Strain the wine into the broth, add a piece of good butter, stir well and pour onto a serving dish with fried bread cubes or small croutons
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Purée ze slepice (Purée of Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from roasted or boiled chicken
- butter
- small onion
- beef broth
- roll
- hard-boiled eggs
- grated roll or pastry

## Directions

1. Chop the meat from roasted or boiled chicken finely
2. Stew it about a quarter hour on butter with a finely chopped small onion
3. Pound it in a mortar into a paste
4. Depending on the amount of meat, let one or half a roll cook soft in one quart of beef broth
5. Add four to six hard-boiled crushed egg yolks and the pounded meat
6. Mix everything well and press through a sieve
7. Thin with beef broth as needed
8. Pour it into a soup bowl on fried grated roll cubes or on pastry
9. Other meats can also be used for this soup
10. Alternative method: Cook an old hen in beef broth until soft, cut out the breasts and chop them finely
11. Pound the rest of the hen in a mortar, put it in a pot, add chopped parsley, celery and carrot, pour in beef broth and let it cook for one hour
12. Then pour it onto the roll or pastry and the chopped breasts
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Pálené mandle (Burnt Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful selected almonds
- sugar
- water
- ground cinnamon
- vanilla or other fine spices (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and rub the beautiful selected almonds
2. Put sugar in a pot or pan as desired
3. Add a little water and mix
4. Add the almonds and heat everything well so the sugar dissolves quickly
5. Continuously stir and cook and turn so long until the almonds begin to crack
6. Remove the almonds, coat them in crushed cinnamon
7. Mix again in a pan with sugar and heat strongly until everything is completely dried
8. When the sugar has completely adhered to the almonds, cover the pan with clean cloth and let stand for some time, by which a special shine is achieved
9. Instead of cinnamon, vanilla or other fine and desired spices can be added to the almonds

title: Pěnivá Čokoláda (Foamy Chocolate)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- 1 liter milk or water
- 250 grams chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs thoroughly
2. Add 1 liter of milk or water
3. Add 250 grams of chocolate
4. Place on warm stove and beat the chocolate constantly until serving time

title: Pěněný nákyp (Foamy Pudding)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- stewed raspberries
- sugar
- 12 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix stewed raspberries with ground sugar with snow from 12 egg whites so the mixture becomes fluffy
2. Grease the form, put the mixture into it, sprinkle with sugar, place it in hot water for a quarter hour and serve on the table with wine sauce

title: Přední nožičky z mladých zajíců neb králíků (Front Legs of Young Hares or Rabbits)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare or rabbit shoulders
- Salt
- Bacon
- Onion, finely chopped
- Whole peppers
- Chopped bacon
- Bread crust
- Bread

## Directions

1. Wash the hare or rabbit shoulders, make an incision above the elbow and skewer them
2. Salt them, lard with bacon and place on a baking pan with finely chopped onion, several whole peppers, and chopped bacon
3. Moisten, braise for a while and then bake in the oven until almost pink
4. Fry grated bread crust, let it steam for a while and partly sprinkle the meat with it
5. Pour with strained sauce and partly garnish with bread around it

title: Přípravování vinního octa v domácnosti (Homemade Wine Vinegar Preparation)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter of good wine vinegar
- piece of fermented leaven, salted and peppered
- ½ liter of weak wine (added every other day)
- alternatively: fallen fruit, water, 1 kilogram of sugar
- alternatively: evaporated vinegar and ½ liter of good vinegar or 1 liter of alcohol

## Directions

1. Take a stone vessel and pour half a liter of good wine vinegar into it
2. Wrap a piece of salted and peppered fermented leaven in clean cloth and suspend it in the vessel
3. Every other day, add ½ liter of weak wine until the vessel is full
4. Remove the leaven and after 8-10 days the vinegar is ready
5. After consumption, continue adding weak wine to maintain the vinegar
6. Alternatively, to prepare vinegar from fallen fruit: crush the fruit, place it in a barrel, pour almost as much water as there is fruit pulp, add 1 kilogram of sugar, allow it to ferment, strain after 6 days, pour into a barrel of evaporated vinegar, add ½ liter of good vinegar or 1 liter of alcohol if stronger vinegar is desired, and after 6-8 weeks it becomes very palatable but cannot be kept long

title: Quark Cheese Strudel (Tvarohový Štrudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) flour
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- water
- quark cheese
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg whites
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- lemon zest rubbed with half the sugar
- 9 dekagrams sugar
- 15 grams (1 lot) ground almonds
- cinnamon
- raisins
- sweet cream or milk

## Directions

1. Mix 24 dekagrams flour with 2 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk, salt and water
2. Work the dough thoroughly, cover with a warm pan and let rest briefly
3. Roll out, then stretch as thinly as possible on a floured cloth
4. Brush the stretched dough with butter
5. Prepare filling by mixing quark cheese, 2 eggs, 2 egg whites, 9 dekagrams butter, lemon zest rubbed with half the sugar, 9 dekagrams sugar, 15 grams ground almonds, cinnamon and raisins
6. Spread the filling on the dough
7. Roll up, place in a greased mold and pour sweet cream or milk over it

title: Quince Bread (Kdoulový chleb)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- quince pulp
- sugar (equal amount to the quince pulp)
- crushed cinnamon
- cloves
- lemon or orange peel (cut into small pieces)

## Directions

1. Rub the hairs from the quince with cloth, cut off the ends, cook in soft water, place on a sieve to dry
2. Press the pulp, collect the flesh and place on a sheet or glass
3. Take as much crushed sugar as there is quince flesh—cook everything together with constant stirring vigorously
4. For better flavor, add crushed cinnamon and cloves, also lemon or orange peel cut into small pieces, mix everything thoroughly
5. Spread on sheets and let dry in a cool place, and when completely dry, place the quince bread between papers in boxes

title: Rabbit with Cream Sauce (Zajíc s Bílou Omáčkou)
body:
## Ingredients

- young cleaned rabbit
- salt
- piece of sliced bacon
- two sliced onions
- lemon zest
- sprig of thyme
- bay leaf
- several peppercorns
- three tablespoons of wine vinegar
- one tablespoon of butter
- one and a half tablespoons of flour
- two small cups of thick good sour cream
- vinegar
- beef broth or hot water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned young rabbit into equal pieces, wash it, salt it and place on a pan with a piece of sliced bacon.
2. Add two sliced onions, a piece of lemon zest, a sprig of thyme, a bay leaf, several peppercorns.
3. Pour over three tablespoons of wine vinegar and let it braise until tender.
4. Remove the meat to a dish and strain the sauce.
5. On the pan put one tablespoon of butter and one and a half tablespoons of flour, and when it dissolves, add two small cups of thick good sour cream and the sauce in which the rabbit was cooked.
6. If needed, add a little more vinegar and beef broth or hot water, let it simmer a little longer, and if the sauce is too sour, add a piece of sugar.
7. Pour the sauce over the rabbit and serve.

title: Raci (Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water
- Salt
- Caraway
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Brush the crayfish, rinse in several waters.
2. When removing the intestine, be careful not to let them fall apart.
3. Salt the water, add caraway and green parsley, and when boiling, throw in the crayfish for 5 minutes.
4. Remove and place on a platter.

title: Raci po Anglicku (Crayfish Cooked English Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned crayfish
- salted water
- good oil

## Directions

1. Place cleaned crayfish in boiling, well-salted water
2. Cook for ten to twelve minutes
3. Remove from water
4. Coat with good oil
5. Arrange tastefully on a plate and serve

title: Raci po Rusku (Crayfish Cooked Russian Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- salted water
- dill
- cleaned crayfish

## Directions

1. Boil plenty of salted water with dill
2. When at a rolling boil, add cleaned crayfish
3. Cook again for about a quarter hour
4. Serve at table

title: Raci Smažení (Fried Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salted water
- flour
- salt
- parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- cleaned crayfish shells

## Directions

1. Cook crayfish in salted water
2. Carefully remove the meat from the shells
3. Mix flour with salt and finely chopped parsley
4. Coat the crayfish meat in this mixture
5. Fry in butter
6. Place the meat back into the cleaned shells
7. Serve on a plate garnished tastefully with fried parsley

title: Raci v Sladké Smetaně (Crayfish in Sweet Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet sour cream
- butter
- cumin
- salt
- white wine
- cleaned crayfish

## Directions

1. In sweet sour cream, add a little butter, cumin, salt and white wine
2. Add cleaned crayfish
3. Cook for about a quarter hour
4. Remove and serve with strained sauce at table

title: Raci v Smetaně (Crayfish in Sour Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cream
- parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- cumin
- cleaned crayfish
- white wine or vinegar
- good meat broth

## Directions

1. Add finely chopped parsley, browned in butter, to sour cream
2. Season with salt and cumin
3. Add cleaned crayfish
4. When the sour cream comes to a boil, add either white wine or vinegar
5. After cooking, pour good meat broth over the crayfish and serve

title: Raci vaření (Boiled Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs, cleaned
- caraway seeds
- pepper, ground
- salt
- water
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the crabs thoroughly
2. Place in a pot and add caraway seeds and a little ground pepper
3. Pour boiling salted water over them, enough to cover
4. Cover tightly with a lid
5. Let boil for about a quarter hour
6. Drain the water
7. Arrange the crabs neatly on a dish
8. Garnish with green parsley
9. Serve

title: Ragout ze sluk (Ragout of Woodcock)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Two woodcock
- Thirty grams butter
- One hundred grams lard, cut into cubes
- Three hundred grams mushrooms, cleaned and sliced
- Flour
- Half liter white wine
- Pepper
- Salt
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the woodcock but do not cook them
2. Melt thirty grams butter with one hundred grams diced lard
3. Add three hundred grams cleaned mushrooms cut into slices
4. Place the woodcock in this to braise for at least ten minutes
5. Sprinkle with flour and pour in the fat
6. Pour half a liter of boiling white wine over, season with pepper and salt
7. Mix everything well, simmer and serve garnished with chopped parsley

title: Rajská Jablíčka na Topinkách (Tomatoes on Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 medium tomatoes
- 2 small onions
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 small cup sweet milk or sour cream
- Butter
- Toast

## Directions

1. Scald, peel and cook together four medium tomatoes and two small finely chopped onions for three quarters of an hour.
2. Then drain all the water, salt and pepper to taste, add a small cup of sweet milk or sour cream, add a piece of butter and mix well.
3. In the meantime prepare several slices of toast from white bread and pour the tomatoes over them.

title: Rajská omáčka černá (Black Tomato Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- sugar
- butter
- wine
- spices
- bay leaf
- black gingerbread or dark beets
- three tomatoes
- roux
- wine

## Directions

1. Chop the whole onion and caramelize it with sugar and butter
2. Mix in one-third liter of wine
3. Add all spices, bay leaf and black gingerbread or dark beets
4. Add three tomatoes and roux
5. Add a little wine
6. Simmer
7. Strain and serve to the table

title: Raspberry Candies (Malinové cukrovinky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo peeled almonds
- egg white
- 1/2 kilo sugar
- 1/4 kilo raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Rub 1/2 kilo peeled almonds very finely with egg white
2. Boil 1/2 kilo sugar to the sixth degree
3. Add finely rubbed almonds to syrup
4. Push raspberries through fine sieve
5. Add 1/4 kilo strained juice to mixture
6. Stir constantly and heat slowly until it thickens
7. When cooled, place in molds as desired
8. Heat again until thickened

title: Raspberry Glaze (Polévání malinové)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- preserved raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Mix finely crushed sugar with preserved raspberry juice
2. Stir over moderate heat
3. Spread on pastry

title: Raspberry Ice Cream (Malinová zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe, cleaned raspberries
- Wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Press ripe, cleaned raspberries through a sieve
2. Cover with wine
3. Sweeten generously
4. Allow to freeze as ice cream

title: Raspberry Ice Cream with Cream (Malinní zmrzlina se smetanou)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Two eggs
- One and a half liters of sour cream
- Two and a half liters of raspberry juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs
2. Beat them well
3. Pour in one and a half liters of sour cream
4. Bring to a boil while constantly stirring
5. When the juice has cooled, add it to the remaining two and a half liters of raspberry juice
6. Sweeten generously
7. Transform into ice cream

title: Raspberry Juice, Alternative Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place fresh raspberries in a pot with warm water on the stove.
2. Crush the raspberries while cooking and strain through clean cloth.
3. For half a kilogram of juice, add 375 grams (25 lots) of sugar, cook together and set aside so it continues to cook until the foam stops rising.
4. Let cool, pour into jars, seal well, place in boiling water for 5 minutes and store in a cool place.

title: Raspberry Mousse with Potato Starch (Malinové kyselo s moukou zemákovou)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram raspberries, currants, blueberries and similar berries
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- 1 liter juice
- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) sugar
- 1/4 liter potato starch flour dissolved in water or wine
- sugar
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pick through 1/2 kilogram of raspberries, currants, blueberries and similar
2. Cook with cinnamon and lemon zest
3. Strain through cloth
4. Cook 1 liter of juice with 24 dekagrams (16 lots) of sugar and 1/4 liter of potato starch flour mixed in water or wine
5. Stir constantly until a thick porridge forms
6. Pour into a moistened mold to cool
7. Turn out onto a plate
8. Serve at table with sugar and sweet cream

title: Račí Kobližky (Crayfish Fritters)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of crayfish butter
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) crayfish tails and claws
- 6 eggs
- 2 soaked rolls
- a little flour
- salt
- butter dough
- sugar or fried green parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Cream together a piece of crayfish butter with chopped crayfish tails and claws
2. Beat well with 6 eggs
3. Add 2 soaked rolls, a little flour, and salt
4. Spread butter dough on a baking sheet
5. Place spoonfuls of the mixture on the dough as small mounds
6. Wrap each individually and fry
7. Sprinkle with sugar or fried green parsley
8. Typically served at larger meals after beef

title: Račí Kroketky (Crayfish Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 90 grams hot butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 onion
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- finely chopped crayfish meat
- fish milt (soft roe)
- egg
- breadcrumbs, grated
- green parsley

## Directions

1. In hot butter, brown 2 tablespoons flour and 1 onion
2. Add 1/4 liter sour cream and cook the sauce until it thickens completely
3. Strain the sauce
4. Add finely chopped crayfish meat and fish milt to it
5. Place on ice and when completely thickened, turn out onto a rolling surface
6. Roll into small cones or cylinders
7. Dip in egg
8. Coat in grated breadcrumbs and fry
9. Garnish with green parsley and serve after soup at table

title: Račí Omáčka (Crayfish Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon fine flour
- 100 grams crayfish butter
- quarter liter good meat broth
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- crayfish tails
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry the flour in crayfish butter
2. Cook with quarter liter good meat broth
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Add lemon juice
5. Mix in finely chopped crayfish tails
6. Strain the sauce and add two egg yolks

title: Račí Omáčka - Second Method (Crayfish Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons flour
- 125 grams crayfish butter
- half liter good meat broth
- lemon juice
- anchovy butter
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry flour in crayfish butter
2. Cook with half liter good meat broth, stirring constantly
3. Make more pleasant with lemon juice
4. Before serving, add anchovy butter and two egg yolks

title: Račí Omáčka k Chřestu neb Květníku (Crayfish Sauce for Asparagus or Cauliflower)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 125 grams crayfish butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- half liter meat broth

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks
2. Mix in a spoonful of flour
3. Add crayfish butter, salt, and nutmeg
4. Add half liter meat broth
5. Mix everything well and cook slowly with constant stirring
6. Pour over prepared vegetables

title: Račí polévka (Crayfish Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salted water
- crayfish intestines
- beef bones, half kilo
- vegetables
- salt
- 1 liter and 4 deciliters water
- pale roux
- crayfish butter
- pepper
- cauliflower
- ginger
- roll cut into noodles

## Directions

1. Clean crayfish with a brush, remove intestines and throw into boiling salted water for 6 minutes
2. Remove meat from claws and tails, crush everything else in a mortar
3. Crush half kilo of beef bones, add all vegetables, salt and cook in 1 liter and 4 deciliters of water
4. When you have a good strong soup, strain it and add pale roux
5. Press crayfish butter through a sieve into the soup, add meat from claws and tails with pepper, cauliflower and ginger
6. Fry roll cut into noodles and pour soup over it

title: Račí polévka - Kardinální polévka (Crayfish Soup - Cardinal Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 crayfish
- salted water
- 200 grams butter
- leek
- parsley
- celery
- flour
- soup
- crayfish meat
- crayfish tails
- asparagus
- cauliflower
- fried roll cut into cubes

## Directions

1. For 3 liters of crayfish soup, cook 30 crayfish in salted water
2. Shell the cooked meat from tails and claws, remove intestines and gallbladder, crush everything else in a mortar with 200 grams of butter
3. Add finely chopped leek, parsley and celery, fry everything in butter, brown with flour
4. Slowly cook with soup for about an hour, strain, beat in a few egg yolks
5. Add shelled crayfish meat, tails, asparagus, cauliflower, or fried roll cut into cubes

title: Račí polévka II (Crayfish Soup II)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 25-30 crayfish
- 250 grams butter
- finely chopped onion
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 3 liters soup
- egg yolks
- crayfish meat
- fried roll cut into cubes
- green peas braised in butter
- asparagus
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Cook 25-30 crayfish, shell the meat, crush the rest of the body with 250 grams of butter
2. Add finely chopped onion, 3 tablespoons flour, fry while stirring constantly
3. Pour with 3 liters of soup, cook together for an hour, strain and beat in egg yolks
4. Add crayfish meat cut into cubes, fried roll cut into cubes, peas, asparagus and cauliflower to the soup

title: Račí Salát (Crayfish Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salted water
- eggs
- anchovies, cleaned and boneless
- capers
- smoked salmon, sliced thinly
- green parsley
- piquant, salad, or mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Cook crayfish in salted water
2. Remove meat from tails and claws
3. Arrange tastefully on a plate
4. Surround with quarters of hard-boiled eggs
5. Around those arrange crayfish tails garnished with cleaned and boneless anchovies, capers, and thinly sliced smoked salmon, and green parsley
6. Pour piquant, salad, or mayonnaise sauce over everything

title: Rebarborový kompot (Rhubarb Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb stalks
- sugar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash nice rhubarb stalks clean and chop into small pieces.
2. Put them in a dish, sprinkle generously with sugar, cover with a plate, and bake in an oven.
3. Bake about 25 minutes and add grated nutmeg.
4. If needed, add more sugar to taste.

title: Rebarborový Paj (Rhubarb Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb stalks
- sugar
- flour
- pie crust dough

## Directions

1. Rinse and peel the rhubarb stalks
2. Cut into pieces one inch long
3. Sweeten generously with sugar
4. Fill a pie pan lined with dough with the rhubarb
5. Dust with flour
6. Cover with top dough
7. Make several slits in it
8. Bake the pie in the oven
9. Before putting rhubarb in the pie, you must first dust the bottom crust lightly with flour

title: Red Cabbage English-Style (Červené zelí po anglicku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard heads of red cabbage
- Salted boiling water
- Meat broth
- Bacon
- Good vinegar
- Butter mixed with flour
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut hard heads of red cabbage in half, remove the core and cut the cabbage into quarters
2. Cook once in salted boiling water and remove to a strainer
3. Pour meat broth over it on a baking sheet, add bacon and good vinegar, as well as butter mixed with flour, pepper and salt
4. Cook slowly and serve with partridges or cured meats
5. Remove the bacon from the cabbage before serving

title: Red Cabbage Russian-Style (Červené zelí po rusku)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned cabbage heads
- Finely chopped onion
- Parsley
- Celery
- Butter
- Good meat broth
- Flour
- Vinegar
- Cloves
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Finely shred or chop the cleaned cabbage heads
2. Mix the cabbage with finely chopped onion, parsley and celery that have been previously sautéed in butter
3. Braise everything in good meat broth
4. When the cabbage has softened considerably, brown it lightly with flour, add vinegar, cloves and a bit of sugar
5. Mix thoroughly and cook again

title: Rejžová polévka (Rice Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pot of rice
- warm water
- salt
- cloves
- fresh butter
- green parsley
- saffron
- beef broth
- sour cream
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Pick over half a pot of rice, rinse it several times in lukewarm water, put in a pot, pour a pot of hot water over it, salt, add cloves, a piece of fresh butter, let it cook slowly for half an hour until the rice is soft.
2. If the water cooks out before the rice is soft, add a little more water.
3. Be careful that the rice does not burn and does not break apart when stirring.
4. When the rice is cooked, put it in a soup bowl, add a little chopped green parsley, saffron powder and pour hot strained beef broth over it.
5. Alternatively, beat one egg yolk in good sour cream (about as much as fits in a coffee pot), pour it into the cooked broth, quickly beat this, pour into a soup bowl and serve immediately.

title: Rejžová polévka na způsob krupovky (Cream of Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of rice
- two spoons of flour
- fresh water
- good beef broth
- half an old hen (if possible)
- salt
- a piece of fresh butter
- four egg yolks
- three spoons of sour cream

## Directions

1. Pick over one coffee cup of rice and clean it several times in boiling water.
2. Dissolve two spoons of flour in fresh water, add this to the rice, then pour good beef broth over it, in which (if possible) half an old hen has been cooked.
3. Now place the pot on the fire and stir diligently until the broth comes to a boil. Then season with salt and add a piece of fresh butter, and let it cook slowly for about an hour, adding a little beef broth meanwhile.
4. Then strain the broth through a fine sieve and place it back on the fire until it comes to a boil again; before bringing to the table, beat four egg yolks in three spoons of sour cream and mix them in.

title: Rejžová polévka s kuřaty (Rice Soup with Spring Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of nice rice
- two or three coffee cups of hot water
- a piece of butter
- salt
- two cleaned spring chickens
- beef broth
- a small piece of nutmeg
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Pick over one coffee cup of nice rice and clean it thoroughly, put it in a pot and pour two or three coffee cups of hot water over it, add a piece of butter and salt and let it cook until the rice softens.
2. Take two cleaned spring chickens and let them cook; when they are cooked tender, remove them and let them cool, then cut them neatly and place them in a soup bowl.
3. Strain the broth in which the chickens were cooked through a fine sieve, skim all the fat from it, grate a small piece of nutmeg and add a little fresh parsley and pour it over the previously cooked rice; then put this in the soup bowl with the prepared chickens and bring to the table.

title: Rejžová polévka s kuřaty (Rice Soup with Spring Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pot of nice rice
- warm water
- a piece of butter
- salt
- two cleaned spring chickens

## Directions

1. Pick over one pot of nice rice and rinse it clean, put in a pot and pour two or three pots of hot water over it, add a piece of butter and salt and let it cook until the rice is soft.
2. Take two cleaned spring chickens and let them cook...

title: Remuláda (Remoulade Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as mayonnaise, except use hard-boiled egg yolks instead of raw ones
2. The hard-boiled yolks must first be rubbed with salt, pepper and a little oil into a fine paste

title: Revízová omáčka z mouky zemákové (Currant Sauce with Potato Flour)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- currant juice
- water or wine
- sugar
- potato flour

## Directions

1. Press the currants through a sieve
2. Dilute with water or wine
3. Add sugar and potato flour mixed in wine

title: Revízové pokroutky (Currant Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe currant clusters
- sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully comb out ripe currant clusters and clean them
2. Press them out
3. Mix the juice with sugar and cook like other brittle pieces until it hardens
4. Place on paper to harden and cool

title: Revízový Dort (Review Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cooked egg yolks
- 1 raw egg
- 14 dekagrams (8 lots) ground almonds
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 12 dekagrams butter
- 28 dekagrams (16 lots) flour
- piece of lemon zest

## Directions

1. Mix 4 cooked egg yolks, 1 raw egg, 14 dekagrams ground almonds, 14 dekagrams sugar, 12 dekagrams butter, 28 dekagrams flour, and lemon zest
2. Make a cake two fingers thick from the dough
3. Coat with jam, make a lattice from the dough, place on the jam
4. Bake the cake

title: Revízový ježek (Reviewer's Hedgehog)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram sliced rolls
- 1/4 liter milk
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 15 dekagrams (10 lots) sugar
- lemon zest
- 10 egg whites for snow
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut sliced rolls into thin slices, soak in 1/4 liter milk and stir over heat with 12 dekagrams butter until a thick porridge forms
2. When cooled, add beaten 10 egg yolks, 15 dekagrams sugar and lemon zest
3. When snow from 10 egg whites is mixed in, put everything in a mold in layers: a layer of sugared Revíz, then the mixture, then another such layer
4. Boil the hedgehog for 2 hours in hot water

title: Revízový Nákyp (Currant Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter milk
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) sugar
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 12 dekagrams butter
- 1 liter redcurrant juice
- sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- lemon peel
- snow from 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Dissolve 9 dekagrams sugar and 4 tablespoons flour in half liter milk.
2. Add 12 dekagrams butter and cook until a thick porridge forms.
3. Let cool.
4. Cook 1 liter redcurrant juice with sugar.
5. Pour several tablespoons of the redcurrant juice into a greased mold.
6. Mix the cooled porridge with 6 egg yolks, salt, lemon peel, and snow from 6 eggs.
7. Pour over the redcurrant juice in the mold.
8. Bake for one hour.

title: Revízový Nákyp - Second Method (Currant Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter redcurrant berries
- sugar
- 1 liter sour sour cream
- 9 eggs
- 4-5 tablespoons grated roll
- lemon peel rubbed with sugar
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter redcurrant berries with sugar into a mold.
2. Dissolve 1 liter sour sour cream with 9 eggs, 4-5 tablespoons grated roll, lemon peel rubbed with sugar, and 12 dekagrams crushed sugar.
3. Add the currant mixture.
4. Bake the soufflé for half an hour.
5. When removing from the oven, prick with a fork to prevent it from collapsing.

title: Ripe Mirabelles (Zralé mirabelky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe but not soft mirabelles
- River water
- Fresh water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Select ripe but not soft mirabelles
2. Place in river water and warm them—but do not boil
3. Transfer to fresh water
4. When cooled, place on a sieve to dry
5. Put in containers and cover with dissolved sugar
6. On the second day, pour off the sugar and cook it thicker, skim the foam, and pour hot sugar over the mirabelles
7. On the following day, cook the drained sugar further (if the sugar is insufficient to cover the mirabelles, add more dissolved and cooked sugar as needed), place the mirabelles in it, cook everything slowly, skim the foam, and cover the container
8. On the fourth day, cook the drained sugar to the sixth degree, add the mirabelles and cook with them several times, skim the foam, let everything cool, and place in containers

title: Ripe Plums (Zralé švestky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Shorten the plum stems
2. Pierce the plums several times
3. Wash in water and place on a plate
4. Pour over with dissolved sugar and cover with paper
5. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, skim the foam, and when cooled, pour over the plums; repeat this until the fifth day
6. Place candied plums in containers between papers

title: Ripe Redcurrants with Sugar (Vyzrálý ryvíz scukrovatělý)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- best ripe redcurrant bunches
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select the best ripe redcurrant bunches (about three or four), tie them together by the stems, and place in stone containers.
2. Cook as much sugar as needed for the amount of redcurrants being prepared.
3. Pour the boiling sugar over the redcurrants.
4. Cover the container with paper and let sit undisturbed.
5. On the second day, carefully drain all the syrup, cook it thick, add the redcurrant berries, and cook everything together.
6. Then place in containers or jars and seal tightly with a bladder.

title: Risolky z Kuřete (Rissoles of Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover meat from roasted or cooked chicken
- 4 spoons chopped braised mushrooms (per 2 cups meat)
- 4 spoons brown sauce (per 2 cups meat)
- 4 egg yolks (per 2 cups meat)
- salt
- pepper
- flower (seasoning)
- a little chopped green parsley
- lemon juice
- nice noodle dough
- egg white
- beaten eggs
- fat for frying
- fried green parsley

## Directions

1. Chop finely the leftover meat from roasted or cooked chicken
2. To two cups of meat add 4 spoons of chopped braised mushrooms, 4 spoons of brown sauce, 4 egg yolks, a little salt, pepper and flower
3. Heat this well with constant stirring, add a little chopped green parsley, finish with lemon juice, remove to a plate and let cool
4. From nice noodle dough, cut round leaves with a small glass or mold
5. Place a little of the mixture on each, brush the edges with egg white, cover with another leaf and press the edges
6. Place the rissoles on a floured board; then dip them in beaten eggs, fry in hot fat, arrange on a platter, garnish with fried green parsley and serve a suitable sauce separately
7. Rissoles from other meat, poultry and game are prepared the same way, and various leftovers can be used very advantageously in this manner

title: Roasted Goose Liver (Pečená játra husí)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large goose liver
- milk
- salt
- butter
- truffle sauce

## Directions

1. Place large goose liver in milk and let it rest. Cut into round slices, salt them, place on a baking sheet lined with butter in which they roast for several minutes, turning frequently. Arrange attractively on a plate and pour truffle sauce over them.

title: Rohlíky (Bread Rolls)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 liters flour
- 6 eggs in 1/4 liter sour cream
- 45 grams yeast (dissolved in milk)
- 24 decagrams butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix flour, eggs in sour cream, yeast dissolved in milk, butter, and salt
2. Work the dough well
3. Let the dough rise for a while
4. Roll out and cut into square pieces
5. Roll the squares into rolls
6. Let rise on a baking sheet
7. Bake

title: Rohlíky (Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lukewarm milk
- 2 egg yolks
- ½ cup butter
- 1 piece of compressed or dried yeast
- salt
- flour to make a dough like for dumplings

## Directions

1. Take 2 cups of lukewarm milk, put into it 2 egg yolks and ½ cup of butter, 1 piece of compressed or dried yeast, salt and add flour so that the dough is about like for dumplings.
2. Let it rise, then dust the work surface with flour, turn out the dough and work it so that it is stiffer than for cake.
3. When you have worked the dough, roll it out to about finger thickness, cut into square pieces, cut off one corner from each and roll them so that the cut side is rolled to the inside; place on a baking sheet greased with lard, bend them, let them rise, brush with beaten egg and let them bake.
4. You can also sprinkle them with poppy seeds or caraway seeds and salt.

title: Rolled Strudel (Válený Štrudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 2 eggs
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- salt
- quark cheese
- egg or cream
- sugar
- raisins
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Make a well in flour on a board
2. Place in the well 2 eggs, 12 dekagrams butter and salt
3. Work the dough so it doesn't stick to the board
4. Let rest in a cool place
5. Meanwhile, thin the quark cheese with egg or cream
6. Add sugar and raisins
7. Roll out the dough thinly
