8. Sprinkle with melted butter
9. Coat with prepared quark cheese
10. Carefully roll up or fold into quarters
11. Place on a greased pan, brush with butter on top and bake slowly
12. Serve sprinkled with sugar, either sliced or whole

title: Rolled Strudel - Another Method (Válený Štrudel - Jiný Způsob)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 large tablespoon lukewarm water
- salt
- jam or raisins, quark cheese, apples, or other filling
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Crumble 9 dekagrams butter into flour on a board
2. Add 2 eggs
3. Add 1 large tablespoon lukewarm water
4. Salt to taste
5. Work the dough almost like noodle dough
6. Let rest briefly
7. Repeat the folding process several times
8. Roll out like thick noodles
9. Coat with jam or sprinkle with raisins, quark cheese, apples or other filling
10. Roll up and bake on a greased pan

title: Rosol masový (Meat Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- four pork legs
- two veal legs
- one kilogram pork meat
- veal meat
- skin from ham and pork meat
- six liters water
- lard
- onion
- carrot
- parsley
- celery
- bay leaf
- pepper
- allspice
- vinegar
- burned sugar

## Directions

1. Take four pork legs, two veal legs, one kilogram pork meat, veal meat, and skin from ham and pork meat, cleaned and chopped, and cover with six liters of water and cook
2. Fry onion, carrot, parsley and celery (cut into pieces) in lard
3. Put into pot, add bay leaf, pepper and allspice and cook several hours on gentle heat
4. When the pork skin is completely soft, salt the aspic, add good vinegar and wine, and add burned sugar for color
5. Cook all for about half an hour, set aside, strain and let the aspic set in a cool place or with ice

title: Rosol oranžový (Orange Jelly)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe juicy apples
- water
- sugar
- zest of two oranges
- juice of two oranges

## Directions

1. Peel ripe juicy apples, cut into thin slices
2. Pour a little water over them and cook
3. During cooking, mash the apples so all the juice flows out easily
4. When well cooked, press through a sieve and pour the juice into a separate container
5. Cook the juice with sugar that has been rubbed with the zest of two oranges until thickened
6. Add the juice of two oranges and cook again until completely thickened
7. Pour the thickened juice into containers and seal tightly

title: Rosolovaný kapoun (Jellied Capon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned capon
- carrot
- parsley
- celery
- 2 halved onions
- 4 peppercorns
- new spice
- juice of 2 lemons or good wine vinegar
- 2 calf feet

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned capon on a baking dish with carrot, parsley, celery, 2 halved onions, 4 peppercorns, new spice and juice of 2 lemons or good wine vinegar
2. Simmer until the capon is tender
3. Allow to cool, and instead cook 2 calf feet in the broth
4. Cut the carrot, celery and parsley in which the capon was cooked into stars
5. Place in a mold, decorate with green parsley, arrange the capon on top
6. Strain the broth over it and place on ice

title: Rosolové skládanky (Layer Jelly Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons sweet baking powder
- 2 cups sugar
- jelly for filling

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites into stiff snow.
2. Mix in 2 cups flour and 2 teaspoons sweet baking powder.
3. In another bowl, beat the yolks well with 2 cups sugar.
4. Combine both mixtures together and mix well.
5. Divide among six metal plates and bake.
6. When cooled, layer with jelly. The most delicious jelly is lemon jelly, made as follows: Take 3 eggs, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup butter, 3 lemons (zest and juice), and cook while stirring constantly until it thickens.

title: Rostený Slaneček (Broiled Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh herring
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Oil
- Sour cream sauce

## Directions

1. Scale, clean and wash fresh herring
2. Make lengthwise cuts on both sides of each
3. Place them in a deep dish
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
5. Season with lemon juice
6. Pour oil over them
7. Let them lie for one hour, turning occasionally
8. Broil them on a grill
9. Arrange on a dish
10. Serve with sour cream sauce
11. Alternatively, arrange hot broiled herring on a heated dish and place pieces of parsley butter on top
12. Fresh herring may also be prepared like perch

title: Rozpouštěni vyziho měchýře (Dissolving Fish Bladder)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good fish bladder
- fresh water

## Directions

1. Good fish bladder, made from bladder, must be clean, smooth, white and transparent
2. Put the fish bladder in clean paper and crush with a wooden mallet as much as needed for aspic
3. For two dekagrams of crushed fish bladder, pour about two deciliters of fresh water
4. Let it sit several hours on warm stove
5. Place the container in boiling water and slowly bring to a boil until the fish bladder is completely dissolved
6. Skim foam carefully during cooking, stir constantly
7. Strain through cloth
8. The liquid is fine, transparent and whitish

title: Rozsekaný šil (Stuffed Pike)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, cleaned
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion
- chopped meat
- sardine butter
- grated Parmesan cheese
- bacon, thinly sliced
- broth
- flour for thickening
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the pike and split it along the backbone, removing all bones
2. Wash the meat again and place it in lemon juice seasoned with salt and pepper, along with parsley and sliced onion, for several hours
3. Remove the meat, dry it with a clean cloth
4. Fill the pike with a mixture of chopped meat, sardine butter, and grated Parmesan cheese
5. Sew the pike closed
6. Place thinly sliced bacon on the bottom of a casserole and lay the pike on top
7. Pour broth and thin flour sauce over it, add vinegar, lemon juice, and salt
8. Cook thoroughly, basting frequently with the cooking liquid during preparation
9. Serve hot with oyster sauce or another piquant sauce

title: Roštěná (Roasted Beef - Alternate Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from ribs (250 grams per serving)
- salt
- pepper
- beef fat (15 grams/1 lot)
- beef broth (2 tablespoons)

## Directions

1. Pound the meat, salt and pepper it
2. Place on 15 grams of fat and braise under a lid until reddened
3. Pour 2 tablespoons of good beef broth over it
4. Braise for a quarter hour

title: Roštěná (Roasted Beef)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from ribs
- salt
- pepper
- onion (chopped)
- pepper (about 4 grains)

## Directions

1. Pound the meat, salt it, place on baking sheet
2. Add chopped onion and about 4 grains of pepper
3. Braise until tender
4. Serve on a plate with the juices that remain from braising

title: Roštěné Kuře (Broiled Spring Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- salt
- pepper
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Clean young chickens well, remove the insides, split them in half along the back
2. Sprinkle with salt and ground pepper
3. Place them in wire tongs (broiler)
4. Broil over hot coals, first the inside and then the outside until nicely browned
5. Place on a plate in the oven so they do not cool
6. When all are broiled, cover with fresh butter and serve

title: Roštěné křepelky (Broiled Quail on Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- dry buttered toast

## Directions

1. Clean quail nicely and split them along the back
2. Place them skin side down on well-buttered wire tongs (broiler) and roast slowly until nicely browned
3. Salt and pepper them and brush generously with butter
4. Serve on dry buttered toast

title: Roštěné ústřice (Broiled Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- large oysters
- heated butter
- salt
- pepper
- bread, buttered and slightly moistened with milk

## Directions

1. Let the oysters drain on a sieve
2. Dip them one by one into heated butter so they don't stick to the wire tongs for broiling
3. Broil them quickly over glowing coals
4. When nicely browned on both sides, pepper and salt them, arrange with butter
5. Place on hot buttered bread slightly moistened with milk
6. Serve very hot

title: Roštěné ústřice (Broiled Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- large oysters
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- bread, sliced and buttered
- milk

## Directions

1. Let nice large oysters drain on a sieve
2. Dip them one by one into heated butter so they do not stick to the wire tongs for broiling
3. Broil them quickly over glowing coals
4. When they are nicely brown on both sides, pepper and salt them
5. Arrange them on hot buttered toast that has been moistened slightly with milk
6. Serve very hot as they are only good this way

title: Ruskie Pirogy s Masem (Russian Meat Pastries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- minced meat from leftover roast
- broth
- pepper
- salt
- dough
- egg
- grated bread roll
- butter

## Directions

1. Brown the onion
2. Add minced meat from leftover roast to the onion
3. Pour in broth, season with pepper and salt
4. Allow to cool
5. Roll out dough as for noodles
6. Sprinkle the minced meat mixture on the dough
7. Cut out round pieces of dough
8. Shape them into four-sided pieces
9. Brush with egg
10. Roll in grated bread roll
11. Fry in butter

title: Ruské jídlo z kornové mouky se škárkami (Corn Mush with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- cornmeal
- salt
- bacon bits
- grated cheese or firm curd cheese

## Directions

1. Bring water to a boil in a clean iron pot
2. Sprinkle in cornmeal with one hand while stirring
3. Salt to taste
4. Sprinkle generously with bacon bits and stir
5. Cover the pot well and bake in an oven or stove for 1.5 hours
6. When done, turn out the hot mixture onto a shallow dish
7. Cut into slices with a strong thread
8. Grease as desired and sprinkle with grated cheese or firm curd cheese
9. When turned out, it looks like a loaf of bread

title: Ruské pirohy s jáhlím (Russian Pirogs with Millet)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- millet
- milk
- butter
- yeast
- 6 egg yolks
- flour
- 6 egg whites
- salt
- minced meat
- grated roll

## Directions

1. Scald millet, boil in milk and press through a sieve
2. Add butter, a little yeast and let stand in a warm place to rise
3. Add 6 egg yolks, a little flour, snow from 6 egg whites and salt
4. Work well and let rise again
5. Roll out thick dough, cut round pieces, sprinkle with minced meat, fold, brush with egg, coat in grated roll and fry in butter

title: Ruské pirohy s vejci (Russian Pirozhki with Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- noodle dough
- hard cooked eggs
- dill or parsley
- egg for brushing
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as for noodles, but do not roll too thin
2. Cut pieces with a mold or glass
3. Sprinkle with chopped hard eggs, dill or parsley
4. Fold the edges sealed with pressure until they stick
5. Brush with egg, roll in grated breadcrumbs
6. Fry in butter

title: Ruské Pirohy se Zeleninou (Russian Pirozhki with Vegetables)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- cabbage
- pepper
- salt
- beef broth
- fish
- butter
- mushrooms
- dough
- bread crust

## Directions

1. Fry onion, add cabbage, season with pepper and salt, stew for a while, can be moistened with beef broth occasionally
2. Cut fish into slices, remove bones and skin, fry in butter and add to the cabbage with fried chopped mushrooms
3. Wrap in dough, roll in bread crust and fry in butter

title: Ruský chlebíček k čaji (Russian Tea Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine flour
- lukewarm water
- yeast
- dissolved butter
- crushed sugar
- finely chopped lemon zest
- several eggs
- chopped almonds
- raisins

## Directions

1. Prepare a thick dough from fine flour, lukewarm water, and yeast
2. Let it rise
3. Add dissolved butter, crushed sugar, finely chopped lemon zest, several eggs, chopped almonds, and raisins
4. Work everything with necessary flour into a stiff dough
5. Place in a greased mold
6. Brush with beaten egg
7. Bake slowly

title: Ruský creme (Russian Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- three eggs
- sugar with vanilla
- two deciliters cream
- a little rum or liqueur

## Directions

1. Mix six egg yolks and three eggs
2. Add sugar mixed with vanilla
3. Process everything until it foams
4. Add two deciliters of cream and a little rum or liqueur

title: Ruský koláč (Russian Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ten hard yolks
- 25 decagrams (17 lots) sugar
- 25 decagrams butter
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- 2 raw eggs
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) flour

## Directions

1. Pass hard yolks through sieve, add sugar
2. Add butter, lemon zest, raw eggs, and flour
3. Beat for a whole hour
4. Place on sheet with greased paper
5. Bake at gentle heat
6. Decorate with preserved fruit

title: Ruský koláč mandlový (Russian Almond Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 decagrams (17 lots) almonds
- 12 hard yolks, sifted
- 25 decagrams sugar
- snow from 6 whites

## Directions

1. Blanch and grate almonds
2. Mix with hard yolks passed through sieve, sugar, and snow from whites
3. Bake mixture on floured sheet
4. Brush with preserved fruit

title: Ruský koláč s vínem (Russian Cake with Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter sour cream
- 1/2 kilogram ground sugar
- wine
- 4 eggs
- juice and lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- 1 kilogram flour
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Make cold dough from sour cream, sugar, wine, eggs, juice, lemon zest, and flour
2. Knead slightly and let rest in cool place for 2 hours
3. Form into large square cake
4. Brush with wine, sprinkle with sugar and chopped almonds

title: Ruský Lanýžový Salát (Russian Truffle Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles, carefully cleaned and peeled
- Madeira wine
- salt
- pepper
- good oil
- parsley, finely chopped
- garlic
- Siberian wormwood
- mayonnaise sauce
- mustard

## Directions

1. Peel truffles carefully and place on a shallow baking sheet
2. Season with salt and drizzle with a little Madeira wine
3. Braise gently for about three to four minutes
4. Cut into very fine thin slices
5. Season slices with salt and pepper
6. Drizzle with good oil
7. Let rest in a cool place for at least ten minutes
8. Sprinkle with finely chopped parsley, garlic and Siberian wormwood
9. Mix in mayonnaise sauce and add mustard

title: Ruský Štrudel (Russian Strudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) flour
- 4 eggs
- sour cream
- 28 dekagrams (16 lots) butter
- filling

## Directions

1. Mix flour, 4 eggs and sour cream to make a dough thinner than for noodles
2. Roll out and thicken with knife blade using 28 dekagrams butter
3. Fold 4-5 times
4. Add any desired filling

title: Ryba na černo (Fish with Black Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart water
- 1 pint vinegar
- 2 medium onions
- 1 celery root
- Parsley root
- Carrot root
- 1 clove garlic
- 2 bay leaves
- Thyme
- 15 grains allspice
- 15 cloves
- 2 small pieces ginger
- 2 pounds prunes
- 3 pints beer
- ½ lemon peel
- ½ pound rye bread
- ½ pound gingerbread
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup nuts
- Juice of ½ lemon
- ½ cup sugar

## Directions

1. Put water and vinegar in a pot and bring to boil
2. Add chopped onions, celery, parsley root, carrot root, garlic, bay leaves, thyme, allspice, cloves, ginger
3. Simmer for about a quarter hour
4. Place fish in the broth
5. While fish cooks, prepare the sauce: cook 2 pounds of prunes until soft, drain the water into a cup
6. Strain the prunes and chop finely, return to the prune water
7. Add 3 pints beer, long-cut lemon peel, diced rye bread, grated gingerbread, butter, raisins, nuts, lemon juice, and sugar
8. Mix everything together with the prunes
9. When fish is cooked, remove it and strain the broth
10. Return broth to heat and add the sauce
11. Make a light roux with butter, add a spoonful to the sauce and simmer
12. Arrange fish on a platter and pour sauce over it
13. Carp is most commonly prepared this way, but sturgeon and other fish that do not fall apart can also be prepared this way

title: Ryba se Smetanovou Omáčkou (Fish with Sour Cream Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Salt
- Sticks for baking sheet
- Good sour cream
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish as usual for cooking and salt it.
2. Place sticks crosswise on a baking sheet, lay the fish on them so it doesn't touch the bottom.
3. Pour good sour cream over it until the fish is baked.
4. Carefully place on a platter, pour sauce over it and garnish with lemon.
5. This method can be used for carp, catfish, perch and pike.

title: Ryba se Sárdelemi (Fish with Sardels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Salt
- 14 decagrams (1/4 pound) sardels
- 14 decagrams butter
- Lemon juice
- Lemon wedges
- Mustard
- Sticks for baking sheet

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish and leave it whole, wipe with cloth, salt it.
2. Clean the sardels, remove bones and rub with 14 decagrams butter.
3. Coat the fish inside and out with the sardel mixture.
4. Place on sticks in a baking sheet placed crosswise so the fish doesn't fall to the bottom.
5. Squeeze juice from one lemon into the baking sheet.
6. Bake the fish well, place on a platter, strain the juice over it.
7. Can be garnished with lemon cut into quarters or serve mustard on a separate plate.
8. Carp, pike and catfish can be prepared this way.

title: Ryba Smažená (Fried Fish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Salt
- Flour
- Egg
- Grated bread roll
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, rinse it, dry it with a clean cloth.
2. Salt it, coat with flour, dip in egg, coat with grated bread roll.
3. Slowly fry it golden brown in butter.
4. All types of fish can be prepared this way. Small fish are fried whole, larger ones are cut into equal pieces.

title: Ryby s podpouštkou (Fish with White Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon, pike, zander or perch
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- bay leaf
- optional spices
- light roux
- fish broth
- capers
- sardine butter
- lemon juice
- egg yolks
- potato flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Kill the fish, clean, and cut into pieces.
2. Salt the pieces and let sit for several hours.
3. Dry the pieces and place in water with salt, pepper, onion, bay leaves and optional spices.
4. Boil together, continuously skimming foam.
5. When the fish is cooked, remove it, peel off the skin, remove bones, arrange nicely on a dish and pour white sauce over it.
6. For the white sauce: make it from light roux cooked with fish broth, capers and sardine butter, with lemon juice added.
7. Alternatively, make the sauce from several egg yolks mixed with potato flour and good vinegar, mixed with boiling fish broth.

title: Ryby v aspiku (Fish in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- clear gelée made from fish broth
- truffles
- crayfish tails
- hard eggs
- capers
- parsley leaves
- trout or other fish
- dark aspic

## Directions

1. Pour clear gelée into a round mold.
2. Line it with carved truffles, crayfish tails, sliced hard eggs, capers and parsley leaves.
3. Cover again with gelée and set on ice to harden.
4. Cook trout or other fish "au bleu" and remove from bones.
5. Place the fish in the center on the hardened gelée in the mold.
6. Pour more gelée over it, arrange a new layer of fish, cover again with gelée and set on ice to harden.
7. Turn out the hardened fish with aspic onto a dish and decorate with dark red aspic.

title: Rybí omáčka (Fish Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- fish broth
- two tablespoons mustard
- one tablespoon Provençal oil
- chopped parsley
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Rub butter with flour and fish broth
2. Add two tablespoons of mustard, one tablespoon of Provençal oil, and chopped parsley
3. Add juice from one lemon
4. Simmer over moderate heat
5. If needed, thin with fish broth

title: Rybí omáčka (Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- piece of cleaned carp or pike
- carrots, sliced into rounds
- celery
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- ginger
- saffron
- water
- flour for roux
- wine
- four hard-boiled egg yolks, grated

## Directions

1. Place butter in a pan
2. Add piece of cleaned carp or pike, carrot slices, and celery
3. Season with salt, pepper, cloves, ginger, and saffron
4. Allow to braise; periodically add water so it does not burn and to ensure sufficient liquid
5. Strain the liquid
6. Make a light butter roux and fry the strained liquid in it
7. Add a little wine and four grated hard-boiled egg yolks
8. Mix well and allow to cool
9. This sauce is typically served with pike or trout

title: Rybí Omáčka (Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- carp or pike, cleaned, cut into rounds
- carrots and celery, sliced into rounds
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- ginger
- saffron
- water

## Directions

1. Place a piece of butter on a pan
2. Add a piece of cleaned carp or pike and carrots and celery sliced into rounds
3. Season with salt, pepper, thyme, ginger and saffron
4. Let it simmer, pouring in water occasionally so it doesn't burn and there is enough broth
5. Then strain it

title: Rybí polévka (Fish Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter and 4 deciliters water
- piece of celery
- parsley
- cabbage
- cauliflower, crushed
- pink roux
- salt
- piece of fresh butter
- chopped green parsley
- fish milt and roe
- roll cut into noodles

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter and 4 deciliters of water to boil
2. Add a piece of celery, parsley, cabbage, and crushed cauliflower
3. After it has boiled for a while, add pink roux and cook again
4. Strain it, salt it, add a piece of fresh butter and chopped green parsley
5. Add fish milt and roe, place roll cut into noodles in the soup bowl and pour the soup over it
6. Fried roll may be served on a separate plate

title: Rybí polévka (Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- entrails of two fish, one male and one female
- stock (recipe 38)
- piece of green parsley, finely chopped
- salt
- white butter roux
- a bit of crushed flower
- white grated ginger
- fried bread roll or white bread cut into cubes

## Directions

1. From the entrails of two fish, one male and one female, carefully remove the intestines and gall bladder
2. Place the entrails in a pot and pour stock (recipe 38) over them
3. Add a piece of finely chopped green parsley and salt
4. Let it cook for an hour
5. Thicken it with a white butter roux
6. Add a bit of crushed flower and white grated ginger
7. Let it cook a little longer
8. Pour the broth into a bowl over fried bread roll or white bread cut into cubes

title: Rybí polévka II (Fish Soup II)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram of fish
- salted water
- 2 hard egg yolks
- 2 rolls soaked in milk and wrung out
- roux
- soup from peas and vegetables
- fried roll

## Directions

1. Cook half kilogram of fish in salted water and remove bones
2. Pound 2 hard egg yolks and 2 rolls soaked in milk and wrung out in a mortar
3. Mix this mixture with roux and soup from peas and vegetables, cook, strain
4. Pour over fried roll

title: Rybí polévka III (Fish Soup III)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish meat
- flour
- butter
- soup
- parsley
- fried roll

## Directions

1. Remove bones from the meat, coat in flour and fry in butter
2. Add soup to this, let it braise for a while, strain it
3. Mash the fish meat, add parsley and pour over fried roll

title: Rybí polévka s knedlíčky (Fish Soup with Fish Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pieces of fish
- stock (recipe 39)
- three hard-boiled egg yolks
- two tablespoons of butter
- one whole egg and one yolk
- a bit of flower
- a bit of white grated ginger
- a piece of lemon peel, finely chopped
- salt
- bread roll or white bread, grated

## Directions

1. Prepare the broth according to recipe 39
2. From two pieces of fish, trim the skin and remove all bones
3. Chop the fish meat finely and pound it with three hard-boiled egg yolks in a mortar into a paste
4. Cream two tablespoons of butter, beat in one whole egg and one yolk
5. Add a bit of flower, a bit of grated white ginger, a piece of lemon peel finely chopped, and salt
6. Add the pounded fish meat and mix well
7. Add enough grated bread roll or white bread so that quenelles can be made from it
8. Pour them into the above-mentioned broth, but you must not thicken this broth until the quenelles are cooked

title: Rybí rosol (Fish Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fish scraps, heads
- onion
- turnip
- lemon peel
- bay leaves
- thyme
- vinegar
- various spices
- white wine
- water
- fish bladder
- large fish stock
- saffron
- stewed roots

## Directions

1. Cook fish scraps, heads, onion, turnip, lemon peel, bay leaves, thyme, vinegar, various spices, white wine and water together, with the solid parts submerged
2. Add dissolved fish bladder and stock from a large fish
3. Color the aspic with saffron and a solution from stewed roots
4. Strain everything and let it rest
5. For clear fish aspic, strain through heated cloth repeatedly until completely translucent and clean

title: Rybí sekanina (Fish Hash)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or carp
- 5 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 bread rolls soaked in milk
- sage
- marjoram
- flower
- 4 cloves garlic
- half a small onion browned in butter
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) butter
- piece of lemon peel
- 4 raw eggs
- pepper
- vinegar
- olive oil
- optional mussels
- wine

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the fish, remove skin and bones, and chop finely.
2. Add the hard-boiled eggs and bread rolls soaked in milk.
3. Add a piece of sage and marjoram rubbed in the hand, a bit of flower, 4 cloves of garlic, and half a small onion browned in butter on 3 dekagrams (2 lots) of butter.
4. Add a piece of finely chopped lemon peel and 4 raw eggs.
5. Mix everything well on a dish.
6. Grease a small baking pan with butter, sprinkle with grated bread and bake in the oven.
7. Cut the mixture into equal pieces, add pepper, vinegar and olive oil.
8. If there are mussels, add them cooked in wine.

title: Rybízová svařenina (Currant Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the currant berries, wash them, put them in a pot, crush and let them cook.
2. When the boil has gone over several times, add sugar, three-quarters of the amount.

title: Rybízová svařenina (Currant Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- sugar (three-quarters cup sugar per cup of currants)

## Directions

1. Remove currant berries from their stems, wash them, put them in a pot, crush them and let them cook.
2. When the mixture has come to a boil several times, add sugar—three-quarters cup of sugar per cup of currants.
3. Crush the berries with a wooden spoon or masher and cook over moderate heat for half an hour.

title: Rybízové Víno (Currant Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe currant berries
- water
- sugar
- currant wine (when fermented)

## Directions

1. Strip the very ripe currant berries from their stems
2. Press them
3. Add as much water as juice obtained
4. To each liter of this juice, add half a kilogram of sugar if the wine is to last long, or less sugar if the wine is to be drunk soon
5. Pour the prepared juice best into a wine barrel, but not filling it completely, best if a few liters still need to be added
6. Place the barrel in a cellar where it rests peacefully
7. The wine ferments in a short time, and when fermentation is complete, top up with fermented currant wine and close the upper opening so that air has access inside, then after a time hammer the cork in firmly
8. The wine remains in the barrel until February, when it is carefully drawn off, not from the tap but always so that only completely clear wine is obtained
9. All vessels must be very clean, well rinsed with wine
10. Fill bottles up to the neck; the cork must not be pressed in firmly or they easily burst
11. Bottles must be protected from frost or they readily crack
12. Transfer the wine, so that it is more certainly clear, through thick cloth before pouring into bottles

title: Rybízové Víno - Jiný Způsob (Currant Wine - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe currants
- sugar
- wine
- liquor

## Directions

1. On a dry, sunny day, pick the ripe currants, place in a wooden container and crush and mash them completely in it
2. Let stand to sour
3. Strain the soured juice and press it
4. Add sugar to taste, depending on how sweet you want the wine
5. Mix everything, add a little wine and good liquor, and let stand for at least six weeks again
6. As soon as the juice clarifies, pour into bottles, or if it is cloudy, transfer to other barrels, and only when it is completely settled, pour into bottles

title: Rybízový rosol (Currant Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Currant jelly is prepared in exactly the same way as grape jelly.

title: Rye Bread (Traditional Rye Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart lukewarm water
- 1/4 teaspoon baking soda
- salt to taste
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Pour the lukewarm water with baking soda mixed in to the starter
2. Salt the mixture
3. Add flour as needed and work the dough thoroughly with a wooden spoon so it does not stick to the bowl or hands
4. Dust with flour again, cover with a warm cloth and let rise for about half an hour
5. Roll out the dough on a floured surface, shape into a loaf, place on a baking sheet and let rise again
6. Place in a well-heated oven and bake for at least two hours

title: Ryvizová povidla (Currant Preserves)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully ripe currants
- Sugar as needed

## Directions

1. Select fully ripe currants
2. Remove all seeds using a pointed tool like a feather
3. Place currants on a baking pan
4. Add sugar as needed
5. Cook until thickened
6. Proceed as with other preserves

title: Ryvizová povidla - Jiný způsob (Currant Preserves - Alternative Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully ripe currants
- Sugar - 500g per kilogram of juice

## Directions

1. Select fully ripe currants
2. Mash in a container
3. Press through a sieve
4. Place juice on a baking pan
5. Add sugar according to taste and need
6. Cook slowly
7. Constantly skim the foam while cooking
8. Cook until thickened, taking care not to scorch
9. Store in stone containers covered with cloth and bladder

title: Ryvizový rosol (Currant Jelly)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe currants
- crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the ripest currants and press to extract the juice
2. Add crushed sugar equal to the amount of juice
3. Cook in several strong boils for at least three hours without stopping until completely thickened
4. Fill into small glasses and store on ice

title: Ryvízová Šťáva na Zmrzlinu (Currant Juice for Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- currant juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. If currant juice is to be used for making ice cream, it must be sweetened as much as possible; the rest is done as with other ice creams.

title: Rýže s houbami (Rice with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- salt
- butter
- parsley, chopped
- pepper
- beef broth
- eggs
- cheese

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms, slice them, salt and braise until all the liquid is reabsorbed, then braise briefly with butter, chopped parsley and pepper
2. Meanwhile cook rice in beef broth
3. Put a layer of rice in a greased mold, a layer of mushrooms, until the mold is full
4. Brush the top rice layer with egg, butter it, sprinkle with cheese and bake for 3/4 hour
5. Usually served with roasted beef

title: Rýže s uzeninkou (Rice with Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter kilogram of rice
- One quarter kilogram (one quarter pound) of butter
- Beef broth or water
- One quarter kilogram of smoked meat
- Pepper
- Young peas
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice, place on a baking sheet, mix with one quarter kilogram of butter.
2. Pour beef broth or water over, and braise until softened.
3. Strip all bones from one quarter kilogram of smoked meat, chop finely, carefully mix with rice and pepper.
4. If there is young peas, add it, which was braised in butter with a piece of sugar.
5. Usually this is used to surround beef meat.

title: Rýže s uzeninou (Rice with Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- butter
- salt
- Parmesan cheese
- smoked meat, chopped
- eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Cook the rice, butter it, salt it, mix with Parmesan cheese
2. Put the rice in a greased dish, sprinkle with chopped smoked meat, pour with eggs beaten in milk
3. Sprinkle rice on top, brush with egg on the outside, sprinkle with cheese and bake

title: Rýžová kaše (Boiled Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup rice
- cold water
- 1 quart milk
- salt
- cinnamon
- sugar
- fresh melted butter
- dried prunes, cooked and pitted

## Directions

1. Sort one small cup of rice and rinse it twice in cold water
2. Pour a quart of milk over it and let it cook with frequent stirring
3. When cooked, salt it, turn out onto a dish
4. Sprinkle with ground cinnamon and sugar and pour hot fresh melted butter over it
5. In some places, cooked and pitted dried plums are also added to rice mush

title: Rýžová nádivka (Rice Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- finely chopped smoked meat or tongue
- strained peas
- eggs
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Braise the rice
2. Mix with finely chopped smoked meat or tongue and strained peas
3. Bind with eggs and mushrooms
4. Use for stuffing young pigs, etc.

title: Rýžová polévka (Consommé with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces rice
- lukewarm water
- two quarts clear beef soup
- salt
- flower (seasoning)
- piece of fresh butter
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Pick over the rice and rinse it several times in lukewarm water.
2. Put in a pot, pour two quarts of clear beef soup over it, salt, add a little flower and a piece of fresh butter.
3. Let simmer slowly for half an hour until rice softens, taking care that the rice does not burn and does not become mushy when stirring.
4. If desired, serve with a plate of grated Parmesan cheese on the side.
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Rýžová polévka (Rice Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Lemon zest
- White wine
- Juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice, parboil it in water, salt it and butter it
2. Add lemon zest
3. Make a roux, add white wine, water, and juice of half a lemon
4. Cook everything, sweeten and add the rice

title: Rýžová polévka (Rice Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- An eighth of a kilogram (quarter pound) rice
- Soft water
- A piece of new butter
- A little flower (kvetu)
- Pepper
- Salt
- 2 liters water
- Mushrooms
- A piece of butter
- A little cream
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Soak rice in soft water for about a quarter hour (in which case the rice will cook in half an hour)
2. Drain the water and pour in fresh water
3. Add a piece of new butter, a little flower, pepper, and salt
4. Braise the rice so it doesn't burn, pouring water over it
5. When soft, pour 2 liters of water over it
6. Slice mushrooms and add a piece of butter, a little cream mixed with 2 egg yolks

title: Rýžová polévka na způsob krupovky (Rice Soup in the Manner of Barley Groats)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- cold water
- soup
- butter
- salt
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice in cold water and boil it halfway
2. Drain the water and finish cooking in soup with butter
3. Press the rice through a sieve and add soup
4. Season with salt, boil with constant stirring, add two egg yolks and thicken as desired

title: Rýžový nákyp (Puffed Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cleaned and well-rinsed rice
- milk or cream
- two tablespoons of butter
- eight egg yolks
- half a cup of sugar
- a little vanilla
- a little grated lemon rind
- a pinch of salt
- eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in milk or cream into a thick porridge
2. Let it cool
3. Add two tablespoons of butter
4. Add eight egg yolks
5. Add half a cup of sugar
6. Add a little vanilla
7. Add a little grated lemon rind
8. Add a pinch of salt
9. Stir for fifteen minutes
10. Gradually fold in the stiff snow made from eight egg whites
11. Pour immediately into a mold
12. Dust with sugar
13. Bake in the oven for three-quarters of an hour
14. Dust with sugar again and bring to table
15. Sago pudding is prepared in the same way

title: Rýžový nákyp (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of well-rinsed rice
- water
- one heaping tablespoon of butter
- salt
- one cup of sweet milk
- a little nutmeg, grated
- two tablespoons of sugar
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Bring water to boil and add one cup of well-rinsed rice
2. Cook for 10 minutes
3. Drain the water
4. Let stand uncovered on the stove for a while to evaporate the moisture
5. Add one heaping tablespoon of butter
6. Mix in with a fork
7. Add a little salt
8. Add one cup of sweet milk
9. Let simmer slowly for half an hour until the rice is soft
10. Turn the rice out onto a dish
11. Grate a little nutmeg over it
12. Add two tablespoons of sugar (more may be used according to taste)
13. Add the yolks of four eggs, beaten
14. Finally add the stiffly beaten snow made from four egg whites
15. Pour into a well-buttered mold
16. Bake in a very hot oven
17. When baked, sprinkle with sugar and bring to table

title: Rýžový nákyp ještě na jiný způsob (Rice Pudding, Yet Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- a small cup of rice
- one quart of milk
- hard wheat bread
- butter
- five eggs
- raisins
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook a small cup of rice in one quart of milk
2. When cooked, let it cool
3. Toast finely grated hard wheat bread in butter until golden
4. Beat five eggs
5. Butter a mold for a bábovka cake with cold butter
6. Sprinkle the bread around the inside so the rice does not stick
7. Put a two-finger-thick layer of rice around the bottom
8. Add a layer of beaten eggs
9. Then add a layer of raisins, cinnamon, and sugar
10. Grease with butter and start layering rice again
11. Repeat this three times
12. Bake slowly in the oven

title: Rýžový nákyp na jiný způsob (Rice Pudding, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- hot water
- boiling milk
- five eggs
- sugar
- lemon juice or lemon rind
- butter
- white bread or hardtack

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice in hot water
2. Pour boiling milk over it
3. Let it cook slowly on a low heat until it becomes a thin porridge, about half-cooked
4. When it cools, beat five eggs and separate the yolks from the whites
5. Beat the yolks separately with sugar
6. Mix with the porridge
7. Add lemon juice or just the rind
8. Finally beat the egg whites separately into snow and gently fold into the porridge
9. Pour into a mold which has been previously buttered and sprinkled with white bread or hardtack
10. Place the mold in another dish with boiling water, cover it, and let it cook slowly on low heat for three-quarters of an hour
11. Then put it in the oven for a quarter hour so the top becomes golden
12. Bring every pudding immediately from the oven to table, as when it sits for a long time it becomes watery

title: Růžice (Rosettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- four whole eggs
- one egg white reserved
- eight tablespoons sugar
- six tablespoons sweet cream
- a bit of salt
- four tablespoons wine (optional)
- flour

## Directions

1. Reserve one egg white in a bowl for later use
2. Mix together the remaining eggs, sugar, sweet cream, and salt
3. If available, add wine; if not, it can be omitted but the result will be less flavorful
4. Mix well and add enough flour to make the dough slightly thinner than noodle dough
5. Roll out very thin, like noodle dough
6. Use five different cutters of graduated sizes, each noticeably smaller than the next, to cut out circles
7. Place on a cloth dusted with flour
8. Beat the reserved egg white until stiff peaks form
9. Brush the center of the largest circle with beaten egg white and stack the remaining four circles on top, largest to smallest, pressing gently in the center with a finger moistened in egg white to create a dimple and help the petals hold together
10. Be careful not to apply egg white to a large area, or the petals will not separate properly
11. Heat lard in a pan and fry the rosettes carefully until they are red but not burnt
12. Beat one egg white into stiff peaks
13. Place a piece of stiff beaten egg white in the center of each rosette, top with a few grains of red sugar, or fill with jam instead
14. The petals should rise beautifully during frying to resemble an actual rose

title: Růžová chladivka (Rose Cooler)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of sugar syrup
- two ounces of rose water
- several small glasses of alcohol

## Directions

1. Take one liter of sugar syrup, add two ounces of rose water, and let everything freeze
2. Pour several small glasses of alcohol into it, mix everything thoroughly, and serve in glasses

title: Růžová omrzlina (Rose Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- růžová štáva
- růžová voda
- citrónová šťáva
- cukr

## Directions

1. Růžová štáva se rozředuje růžovou vodou
2. Přidá se citrónová šťáva
3. Vše se ocukruje a svaří
4. Upraví se jako ostatní zmrzliny

title: Růžová zmrzlina se smetanou (Rose Ice Cream with Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- šest žloutků
- litr sladké smetany
- růžová štáva
- cukr

## Directions

1. Vrazí se šest žloutků do litru sladké smetany
2. Přidá se růžová štáva, až se dostatečně osladí a chut růžovou dostane
3. Několikrát se svaří a vychladne
4. Při tom se stále šlehá
5. Zařizuje se jako jiná zmrzlina

title: Růžová Šťáva (Rose Hip Juice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned rose petals
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place cleaned rose petals in a heated bottle and pour with sugar cooked to the sixth degree and still very warm.

title: Růžová Šťáva (Rose Petal Syrup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned rose petals
- sugar cooked to the sixth degree
- hot water

## Directions

1. Place cleaned rose petals into a heated bottle
2. Pour sugar cooked to the sixth degree and still very hot over them
3. Tie the bottle opening with a bladder
4. Leave the bottle on warm stove for at least a week
5. Strain the syrup and filter it
6. Store the syrup
7. Use for making confectionery, ice cream, etc.

title: Růžové Koblihy (Fried Rosettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as for Boží Milosti
- egg white
- clarified butter or lard
- sugar
- jam or cream

## Directions

1. Make and roll out dough as for Boží Milosti
2. Cut round, scalloped-edged flowers with a small cutter to make petal-like pieces
3. Brush the center of each petal with egg white
4. Stack three petals on top of each other and press down in the center with a finger
5. Fry in heated clarified butter or lard, using enough fat so the rosettes float
6. Place jam or cream in the center, sprinkle generously with sugar, and arrange on a platter

title: Růžové Mandle (Rose Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- sugar
- rose water
- carmine dye dissolved in gum
- water

## Directions

1. Scald and blanch almonds, then dry
2. Add rose water to boiled sugar
3. When almonds are at least partially coated with sugar, add carmine dye dissolved in gum and water
4. This gives the almonds a nice red color

title: Růžové Paštičky (Rose Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- refined sugar
- rose water
- carmine

## Directions

1. Cook refined sugar to the grain stage with rose water
2. Color with carmine
3. Proceed as with the first paste

title: Sago Soup (Sago Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago
- ordinary beef soup
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse sago several times in lukewarm water and finally in hot water
2. Place it in a pot and pour ordinary beef soup over it
3. Cook until soft, which takes only a few minutes
4. Salt it, flavor with a little pepper and serve
5. Calculate one spoonful of raw sago per person

title: Sago Soup with Yolks (Sago Soup with Yolks)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago
- strong beef soup
- 4 egg yolks
- soup broth for tempering

## Directions

1. Rinse sago as in the previous recipe and place it in a pot
2. Pour strong beef soup over it and let it cook until the sago softens
3. Before removing from heat, mix 4 egg yolks tempered in lukewarm soup into the pot for 6 people and immediately remove the soup from heat
4. If the yolks cook, they will curdle

title: Salmi síňkové (Woodcock Salmi)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock (2-3 per person)
- Butter
- Juniper berries, crushed
- Red wine
- Stock
- Bread, fried
- Puff pastry
- Truffles
- Goose liver

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcock and roast them in butter
2. Add crushed juniper berries and red wine
3. Let the roasted woodcock cool, then cut off the breasts whole
4. Pound everything else except the head and stomach in a mortar
5. Pour in stock and some red wine, press through a sieve
6. Cook down into a thick sauce and place the reserved breasts in it to heat through
7. Arrange the salmi artfully on a dish and garnish with fried bread or puff pastry around the edges
8. Also garnish with truffles or braised goose liver

title: Salmy z koroptví (Partridge Salmi)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- raw egg
- two eggs soaked in milk
- squeezed roll
- flour
- ginger
- pepper
- quarter onion
- lard
- 1/8 kilo (1/8 pound) lard

## Directions

1. Pluck the partridges and carefully remove the skin, keeping only the wing tips and lower part of the legs with skin.
2. Chop the meat and pound it in a mortar, do the same with the bones.
3. Add to the mortar four tablespoons of chopped meat, one raw egg and two eggs soaked in squeezed milk, a little flour, ginger, pepper, a quarter chopped onion and a piece of lard.
4. When pounded, push the mixture through a fine sieve.
5. Add chopped lard cubes to the pressed mixture and mix everything together.
6. Stuff the skin with the mixture and sew it closed.
7. Boil for half an hour.
8. Slowly press until all juice flows out.
9. Let cool, then cut into thin slices.

title: Salmy z křepelek (Quail Salmi)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 20-25 quails
- 250 grams foie gras, cleaned, peeled and sliced thin
- fresh butter
- 2-3 onions
- 125 grams smoked ham, cut into cubes
- pepper
- parsley
- 1/2 to 3/4 liter red wine
- quail bones and carcasses
- strong beef and poultry broth

## Directions

1. Remove the breasts from the ribs of well-cleaned quails.
2. Heat fresh butter in a casserole and add sliced foie gras.
3. Add the peeled onions, smoked ham cut into cubes, pepper, parsley, and red wine.
4. Braise on hot coals.
5. Crush the quail carcasses finely, braise in fresh butter, and cook in very strong beef and poultry broth.
6. Strain everything and keep warm.
7. Arrange the quail portions on a platter, pour the sauce over them, and decorate variously for serving.

title: Salsify dušené (Stewed Oyster Plant)
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify roots
- cold water
- salt
- milk
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Scrape the roots and place in cold water to prevent them from losing color
2. When you have scraped the desired amount, slice into rounds about half an inch thick
3. Pour water over them and cook until tender
4. When cooked, strain the water and pour milk over them, salt and let cook for a while
5. Take one spoon of butter and one spoon of flour, mix well and add to the salsify

title: Salt-Pickled Bean Pods (Do soli naložené lusky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- tender green pods
- salt (half the weight of the pods)

## Directions

1. Trim both ends of the tender green pods
2. Mix with salt
3. Place into jars for preservation
4. Tie them up and let them rest for several days
5. After a few days when they have settled, refill the jars from other jars with pickled pods, repeating this from time to time
6. Before use, rinse the pods in clean water
7. Drizzle with vinegar
8. Add oil and pepper

title: Salted Cucumbers
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Green, medium-sized, firm cucumbers
- Fresh well water
- Salt
- Cherry and grape leaves
- Grape leaves
- Dill greens
- Black rye bread or sourdough starter in a wine leaf (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak green, medium-sized, firm cucumbers in fresh well water for 24 hours, then remove and dry them
2. Rub the barrel where the cucumbers will be stored with salt
3. Line the bottom with cherry and grape leaves, and dill greens
4. Add the cucumbers and cover them again with the same leaves
5. Layer cucumbers and leaves alternately until the barrel is full
6. Fill the barrel completely with salted well water prepared like broth, ensuring it is completely filled
7. Do not over-salt the water as this would interrupt fermentation
8. Seal the barrel completely; keep it in a warm kitchen in the first days, and when cucumbers begin to ferment, move to a cold room
9. To speed up fermentation, add either a piece of black rye bread or sourdough starter wrapped in a wine leaf
10. Daily, rock the barrel in the cellar where it was placed at the beginning of fermentation, and turn it over for eight days
11. When the liquid dries out, add new fresh liquid through the hole where the cork is located
12. The cucumbers will be tasty, keep for a long time, and during use must always remain in the liquid, otherwise they will easily become moldy

title: Salámy (Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salami, cervelat, or mortadella
- Parsley
- Vinegar
- Onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel salami, cervelat, or mortadella
2. Slice thinly
3. Arrange in the middle of a dish
4. Garnish around with a wreath of chopped parsley
5. Can also be drizzled with vinegar and sprinkled with finely chopped onion
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Salát celerový (Celery Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- beef broth or salted water
- wine vinegar
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the celery thoroughly, wash it, and carefully remove and clean the green center, setting it aside in cold water
2. Cook the celery in either beef broth or salted water until it softens
3. Place the cooked celery in cold water to cool
4. Slice the cooled celery into rounds, salt and pepper it
5. Pour wine vinegar and olive oil over it
6. Place the removed green heart in the center

title: Salát celerový (Root Celery Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- several celery heads
- salt
- watercress
- wine vinegar
- olive oil
- pepper

## Directions

1. Scrape several celery heads, cut off the top with the greens and put in cold water
2. Cook the heads in salted water, then remove and put in cold water
3. Scrape them clean with a knife and slice thinly
4. Stand the tops that you cut off in the center of a salad bowl and arrange the sliced celery around it
5. Make a border of well-cleaned and washed watercress, sprinkle with salt, pour with wine vinegar and good olive oil, and sprinkle with ground pepper

title: Salát Hlávkový (Head Lettuce Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- head lettuce
- three hard-boiled egg yolks
- Provençal oil
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove outer leaves and cut into quarters
2. Rinse thoroughly
3. Rub three hard-boiled egg yolks
4. Pour Provençal oil and wine vinegar onto the yolks and mix well
5. Add the lettuce and mix
6. Arrange on a plate
7. Surround with sliced hard-boiled eggs

title: Salát Holandský (Dutch Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- celery
- small onions
- cabbage
- cleaned and rinsed herring, cut into small pieces
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Cook each ingredient separately: potatoes, celery, small onions, and cabbage
2. Cut potatoes, celery and onions into slices
3. Mix with cabbage cut into small pieces, cleaned and rinsed herring
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Dress with vinegar and oil

title: Salát Okurkový (Cucumber Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- vinegar
- oil
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers and slice or cut thinly with a knife
2. Place slices on a plate and cover with the plate
3. Drain the water
4. Salt the cucumbers
5. Mix well with forks
6. Pour vinegar and good oil over
7. Season with pepper

title: Salát okurkový (Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- vinegar
- thick sour cream
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel nice cucumbers and either slice them thinly or grate them on a grater, salt them, pour vinegar and good thick sour cream over them, mix well and sprinkle with ground pepper

title: Salát Se Slanečkem (Salad with Herring)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes in their skins
- well-soaked herring
- pickled cucumbers
- several tablespoons of capers
- finely chopped onion
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in their skins
2. Peel and cut into slices
3. Soak herring well, remove bones and skin, and cut into slices
4. Cut pickled cucumbers into slices
5. Mix potatoes with several tablespoons of capers, finely chopped onion, oil, salt, pepper and vinegar

title: Salát z Humry, Raku neb Ryb (Lobster, Crab or Fish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from cooked fresh lobster or canned lobster
- mayonnaise
- lobster roe (if available)

## Directions

1. Tear the meat from cooked fresh or canned lobster into pieces with a fork
2. Prepare salad the same way as chicken salad
3. If the lobster had roe, you can use it to garnish the top of the salad
4. The same preparation is used for salad made from cooked fish, crayfish and crabs
5. Another fish salad can be made from shredded fish meat mixed with chopped sardines and capers and layered with sliced potatoes and beetroot; it can be dressed with either mayonnaise, remoulade, or simply oil and vinegar and garnished in the usual way

title: Salát z karfiolu (Cauliflower Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- several nice white cauliflower florets
- salt
- fresh butter
- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- four tablespoons of olive oil
- five tablespoons of wine vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean several nice white cauliflower florets and cook them in salted water with a piece of fresh butter until tender, being careful to keep the florets whole
2. When the cauliflower cools, place it on a clean cloth and let it drain
3. Either break it into small pieces or arrange the whole head neatly in a pyramid on a salad platter
4. Press four hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve, mix them well with four tablespoons of olive oil, add five tablespoons of wine vinegar, salt and a little pepper, mix well and pour over the prepared cauliflower

title: Salát z kuřat (Chicken Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- one small head of cabbage
- leaf celery stalks
- twelve eggs
- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one tablespoon of mustard
- one small cup of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop finely the meat from one chicken, also a small head of cabbage and the same amount of leaf celery stalks, each separately
2. Cook twelve eggs hard, chop the whites finely and rub the yolks with two tablespoons of butter, two tablespoons of sugar and one tablespoon of mustard
3. Add to this one small cup of wine vinegar, mix well, then mix all the chopped ingredients and pour the sauce over it

title: Salát z kuřat na jiný způsob (Chicken Salad - Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted or boiled cold chicken
- white celery stalks
- mayonnaise
- lettuce leaves
- hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- olives

## Directions

1. Slice the meat from roasted or boiled cold chicken into small pieces, mix it with the same amount of white celery stalks cut into noodles and three tablespoons of mayonnaise
2. Arrange it in a pyramid on a platter lined with lettuce leaves, pour mayonnaise over it, garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters, capers or olives
3. Instead of celery you can also use other vegetables cut into noodles

title: Salát z Kuřat na Jiný Způsob (Chicken Salad Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from roasted or boiled cold chicken
- equal amount of white celery stalks cut into thin strips
- three tablespoons mayonnaise
- lettuce leaves
- hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters
- capers
- olives
- alternatively: sour pickles cut into thin strips
- alternatively: tender lettuce leaves torn into pieces
- alternatively: vinegar mixed with oil, salt and pepper instead of mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from roasted or boiled cold chicken into small pieces
2. Mix with equal amount of white celery stalks cut into thin strips and three tablespoons mayonnaise
3. Arrange on a platter lined with lettuce leaves
4. Pour mayonnaise over top
5. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters, or with capers and olives
6. Alternatively, use sour pickles cut into thin strips or tender lettuce leaves instead of celery
7. Alternatively, use vinegar mixed with oil with a bit of salt and pepper instead of mayonnaise
8. The same method can be used for salad made from sweetbread, goose liver and other meats, and many leftovers can be used this way quite appropriately and deliciously

title: Salát z Lososa (Salmon Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound can of salmon
- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- one teaspoon sugar
- one teaspoon salt
- one teaspoon mustard powder
- one tablespoon overheated fresh butter
- two tablespoons vinegar
- equal amount of as white lettuce head as possible, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Take one pound can of salmon and remove the bones
2. Chop the meat finely
3. Chop equal amount of as white lettuce head as possible
4. Rub four hard-boiled egg yolks together
5. Add one teaspoon sugar, one teaspoon salt, one teaspoon mustard powder, one tablespoon overheated fresh butter and two tablespoons vinegar
6. Mix well and pour this mixture over the salmon meat

title: Salát z mrkve (Carrot Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned carrots
- salted water
- wine vinegar
- oil
- cooked prunes

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned carrots in salted water and let them cool
2. Slice the carrots into rounds
3. Pour wine vinegar and oil over them
4. Arrange cooked prunes around the salad

title: Salát z rajských jablíček (Tomato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice tomatoes
- one medium onion
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Scald nice tomatoes, peel off the skin, slice into rounds and put on a platter
2. Chop one medium onion finely and add to them, salt, pepper and pour with vinegar mixed with water, sweetened to taste

title: Salát z rajských jablíček s majonézou (Tomato Salad with Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve tomatoes
- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- one raw egg yolk
- one small teaspoon of salt
- half a teaspoon of paprika
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one tablespoon of butter
- two tablespoons of mustard
- one small cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice twelve tomatoes into rounds and put on a platter
2. Mash four hard-boiled egg yolks, add one raw egg yolk, one small teaspoon of salt, half a teaspoon of paprika, one tablespoon of sugar, one tablespoon of butter, two tablespoons of mustard and one small cup of vinegar
3. Mix everything well, pour over the tomatoes and keep on ice until ready to use so they are very cold

title: Salát ze stonků naťového celeru (Celery Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- leaf celery stalks
- salt
- pepper
- mustard powder
- oil
- vinegar
- mayonnaise or remoulade
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Remove the upper hard and stringy stalks, rinse the inner white and tender stalks well, dip them briefly in cold water, then slice them into pieces about one and a half inches long and cut these again into thin noodles
2. Rinse them once more, then dry, salt, season with pepper and a little mustard powder, pour with oil and vinegar, shake well and put on a platter lined with lettuce leaves
3. Instead of oil and vinegar, the salad can first be mixed with a little mayonnaise, arranged in a pyramid on a platter lined with lettuce leaves and poured over with remaining mayonnaise or remoulade

title: Salát ze Syrového Masa (Chopped Meat Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound beef from the round, scraped or ground
- salt
- pepper
- onion, very finely chopped
- vinegar
- oil
- alternatively: sardines
- alternatively: capers
- alternatively: hard-boiled eggs, sliced

## Directions

1. Scrape with a sharp metal spoon or grind in a meat grinder one pound of beef from the round into a paste
2. Make four mounds of it on a plate
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and very finely chopped onion
4. Pour vinegar and oil over it
5. Let it stand at least half an hour so that the flavor of the ingredients is absorbed into the meat
6. You can also use sardines, capers and hard-boiled eggs, sliced

title: Salát Ze Zeleniny (Vegetable Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- asparagus
- small celery
- green beans
- rutabaga
- cauliflower
- salted water
- hard-boiled eggs, cut into quarters
- sauce made from grated egg yolks, oil, mustard, vinegar, pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Clean carrots, asparagus, small celery, green beans, and rutabaga
2. Cut asparagus and beans into pieces
3. Divide cauliflower
4. Cut rutabaga and carrots into slices
5. Cook everything in salted water
6. Drain the water
7. Arrange cauliflower in the middle of a dish and the remaining vegetables around it
8. Pour sauce made from grated egg yolks, oil, mustard, vinegar, pepper and salt
9. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters

title: Salát ze zelné hlávky (Cold Slaw)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage head
- hot water
- onion
- good vinegar
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the cabbage head into small strips, pour hot water over them which you then squeeze out and drain, and if desired add a little onion cut into strips
2. Cook good vinegar on a pan, add a piece of butter, salt and put the blanched cabbage in it, let it braise under a lid on high heat for about ten minutes, stirring frequently
3. Remove the braised cabbage to a dish, pepper it and let it cool

title: Salát ze zelné hlávky na jiný způsob (Cold Slaw Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage head
- bacon, quarter pound
- onion
- wine vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the cabbage head as described previously
2. Cut a quarter pound of nice bacon into small cubes and put on a pan, add to it one onion also cut into small cubes and let fry until golden
3. Then add a pint of wine vinegar, put the cabbage into it, salt and let it braise for ten minutes, then sprinkle the salad with ground pepper and serve warm at table

title: Salát ze zelné hlávky s kyselou smetanou (Cold Slaw with Sour Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one nice firm cabbage head
- one tablespoon of butter
- three tablespoons of boiling water
- one egg
- half a tablespoon of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- half a small cup of sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut a nice firm head into fine noodles and put on a grater with one tablespoon of butter
2. When it dissolves, add the cabbage to it, add three tablespoons of boiling water, cover and let it sit for a quarter hour at the edge of the stove in warmth, but do not let it boil
3. Then beat one egg and mix it into the cabbage, add half a tablespoon of vinegar, salt and pepper
4. Finally mix in half a small cup of sour cream
5. Let it cool and serve cold on the table

title: Salát ze zelné hlávky se sladkou smetanou (Cold Slaw with Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh crisp head of cabbage
- salt
- half an onion
- two tablespoons of good butter
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- one small cup of sweet cream
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice a nice fresh crisp cabbage head and put the cabbage in a wooden bowl
2. Sprinkle with salt, add half a finely chopped onion and pound it with a wooden pestle for 5 minutes
3. Put it in a bowl and pour the following sauce over it: Heat two tablespoons of good butter in a small pot
4. When it turns red, but before it begins to burn, add one tablespoon of vinegar
5. When it begins to boil, pour in one small cup of sweet cream with constant stirring so it does not curdle
6. Add a pinch of pepper
7. Pour this sauce over the salad

title: Salát ze zelné hlávky se sladkou smetanou (Cold Slaw with Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh crisp cabbage head
- salt
- onion
- good butter
- vinegar
- sweet cream
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice a nice fresh crisp cabbage head, put the cabbage in a wooden dish, sprinkle with salt, add half an onion finely chopped and pound it with a wooden mallet for 5 minutes
2. Then put it into a dish and pour the following sauce over: In a small pan melt two spoonfuls of good butter
3. When it reddens but before it begins to burn, add a spoonful of vinegar and when it begins to boil, pour a cup of sweet cream into it with constant stirring so it does not curdle and add a pinch of pepper
4. Pour this sauce over the salad

title: Salát ze štěrbáku (Endive Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- endive
- powdered sugar
- vinegar
- olive oil
- salt

## Directions

1. In autumn endive is most beautiful; remove all the green leaves from it, rinse the yellow ones well in cold water and let them drain well
2. Put it on a platter, sprinkle with powdered sugar, mix several tablespoons of wine vinegar with olive oil and a little salt
3. Pour this over the endive leaves and mix well, then arrange on a platter

title: Salát Ze Žampionů (Mushroom Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms, cut into slices
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- sardines
- capers

## Directions

1. Cut mushrooms into slices
2. Braise in oil
3. When cooled, drizzle with vinegar
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Surround with sardines and capers

title: Salát Řeřichový (Watercress Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- salt
- wine vinegar
- Provençal oil

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse watercress
2. Place on a plate
3. Season with salt
4. Pour wine vinegar with Provençal oil over it
5. Can also be prepared like head lettuce as described previously

title: Salátové sandviče (Salad Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound chicken breast
- one-quarter pound cold cooked beef tongue
- vinegar
- celery salt
- paprika
- mayonnaise
- lettuce leaves
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop chicken breast and cold cooked beef tongue into pieces
2. Pound together in a mortar
3. Moisten with several drops of vinegar
4. Add celery salt, paprika and four or five tablespoons of mayonnaise
5. Mix well together
6. Place in cold bowl and keep on ice until ready to use
7. Spread bread slices with this mixture
8. Place a crisp lettuce leaf moistened in tarragon vinegar on one piece
9. Cover with another buttered bread slice
10. Press together gently and serve
11. Ham combined with veal roast also makes a very good salad sandwich
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Saláт Po Švýcarsku (Swiss-Style Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- celery, cooked in salted water until soft
- red beets
- corn salad with indented leaves
- salt
- vinegar
- oil
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook celery and red beets in salted water until soft
2. Cut into thin slices
3. Mix with corn salad with indented leaves
4. Season with salt
5. Dress with vinegar and oil
6. Add pepper

title: Sandvice (Sandwich)
body:
## Ingredients

- white bread, preferably two days old
- butter
- meat, cheese, or other filling

## Directions

1. Cut white bread into equal slices about one-quarter inch thick
2. Remove crusts so that nice pieces remain, about four inches square
3. Slice meat thinly and equally, ensuring it is prime, tender, and juicy, well-roasted or boiled
4. Ensure sandwich edges are straight and filling does not protrude
5. Arrange finished sandwiches on a plate covered with a napkin
6. Garnish with fresh parsley or watercress
7. Serve with a small dish of mustard so each person may help themselves as desired
8. If sandwiches dry out before use, moisten a napkin in cold water, wring it out, and cover the sandwiches
9. Alternatively, each sandwich may be wrapped in thin waxed paper
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče (Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- two-day-old white or rye bread
- meat, tongue, or cheese

## Directions

1. A sandwich consists of two thin slices of bread with a thin slice of meat, tongue, cheese, etc. between them.
2. It is best to use two-day-old bread without holes, either white or rye bread, cut into equal slices about a quarter inch thick.
3. The meat must be tender, juicy, well-roasted or cooked, and cut into thin, equal slices.
4. The edges of the sandwiches should be even and the contents should not protrude.
5. If the sandwiches become dry before serving, dampen a cloth in cold water, wring it out well, cover the sandwiches with it, or wrap each sandwich in thin waxed paper.
6. Mustard is served with sandwiches.

title: Sandviče po francouzsku (French Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- prime ham, finely chopped
- fresh butter
- tarragon vinegar
- ground white pepper

## Directions

1. Finely chop sufficient amount of prime ham
2. Add a piece of fresh butter and several drops of tarragon vinegar
3. Season with ground white pepper and mix well
4. Spread this mixture on prepared bread slices
5. Cover with another slice
6. Press together gently
7. Cut finished sandwiches crosswise into two triangular halves and serve
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče s kaviárem (Caviar Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- caviar
- onion, finely chopped
- butter
- lemon juice
- rye bread

## Directions

1. For each sandwich use one tablespoon caviar
2. Add half a teaspoon finely chopped onion
3. Add a piece of butter the size of a walnut
4. Add juice from half a lemon
5. Mix well together
6. Spread on prepared rye bread slices
7. Press together gently
8. Cut sandwich crosswise into two triangular halves and serve
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče s kaviárem (Caviar Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Kaviár
- Drobná usekná cibule
- Máslo
- Citrón
- Žitný chléb

## Directions

1. Na každý sandvič vezmi jednu lžičku kaviáru, půl lžičky drobné usekané cibule a kousek másla zvící vlaského ořechu
2. Přidej šťávu z půl citronu
3. Vše dobře promíchej
4. Namaž směs na připravené kousky žitného chleba
5. Zlehka kousky dohromady přimáčkni
6. Rozkroj sandvič příčně na dvě tříhranné půlky
7. Podlož
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče s okurkami (Pickle Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- butter
- sour pickle
- veal roast

## Directions

1. Butter prepared bread slices
2. On one slice place thinly sliced sour cucumber
3. Then place a thin slice of roasted veal
4. Cover with another slice
5. Press together gently and serve
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče s olivami (Olive Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Šest vyloupaných oliv
- Máslo
- Paprika

## Directions

1. Vezmi šest vyloupaných oliv
2. Dobře je usekej
3. Přidej kousek másla zvící vlaského ořechu
4. Přidej špetičku papriky
5. Dobře vše promíchej
6. Připrav sandvič dle obvyklého způsobu
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče s vejci (Egg Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Vejce vařená na tvrdo
- Sůl
- Pepř
- Chléb
- Máslo

## Directions

1. Rozkrájej na tvrdo vařená studená vejce na tenké lístky
2. Opepři a osol vejce
3. Polož vždy čtyři lístky vajec na jeden máslem namazaný řízek chleba
4. Druhým řízkem přikrej
5. Zlehka přimáčkni
6. Podlož
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče se sardinkami (Sardine Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- butter
- French mustard
- sardines
- lettuce leaves
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Butter one bread slice and spread the other very thinly with French mustard
2. Halve two sardines and remove the backbone
3. Arrange sardine halves on the buttered bread
4. Sprinkle with a little lemon juice
5. Place a crisp lettuce leaf on top
6. Cover with the other slice
7. Press together gently and serve
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče se sýrem Brie (Brie Cheese Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brie cheese
- fresh parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Finely chop a little fresh parsley and mix with Brie cheese
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče se sýrem Brie (Brie Cheese Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Zelená petruška
- Máslo
- Chleb
- Sýr Brie

## Directions

1. Usekej na drobno trochu zelené petruželky
2. Smíchej ji s kouskem másla
3. Pomaž připravené kousky chleba touto směsí
4. Polož mezi kousky chleba tenké řízky sýra Brie
5. Zlehka kousky dohromady přimáčkni
6. Podlož
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče se sýrem Roquefort (Roquefort Cheese Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dvě unce sýra Roquefort
- Máslo
- Chlebové řízky

## Directions

1. Dvě unce sýra Roquefort smíchej s kouskem másla
2. Potři směsí připravené chlebové řízky
3. Přikrej svrchními řízky
4. Zlehka kousky dohromady přimáčkni
5. Rozkroj sandviče na čtyři čtvrtky
6. Podlož
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče se zvěřinou (Game Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- butter
- French mustard
- hard-boiled eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- cold game meat
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Butter one bread slice and spread the other thinly with French mustard
2. Slice hard-boiled eggs into rounds, remove yolks and mash them with oil, vinegar, salt and pepper
3. Place four egg white rounds on the buttered bread
4. Chop half a pound of cold game meat finely
5. Season with salt and pepper, add mashed yolks and a little tarragon vinegar and mix well
6. Fill the egg white rounds with this mixture
7. Cover with the other bread slice, press gently and serve
8. Poultry and other fine meats may be used instead of game
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče se švýcarským sýrem (Swiss Cheese Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Žitný chléb
- Máslo
- Švýcarský sýr

## Directions

1. Nakrájej žitný chléb na tenké řízky
2. Udělej z nich kousky stejné velikosti jako obyčejné sandviče
3. Pomaž chléb máslem
4. Dej mezi dva kousky chleba několik lístků švýcarského sýra
5. Zlehka kousky dohromady přimáčkni
6. Rozkroj sandvič příčně na dvě tříhranné půlky
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče z drůbeže (Fowl Sandwich)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted chicken, roasted squab, or roasted turkey
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove skin and fat from roasted poultry
2. Slice meat very thinly
3. Sprinkle with oil and vinegar
4. Prepare as other meat sandwiches
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sandviče ze syrového masa (Raw Beef Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- prime tender beef meat
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Finely chop a piece of prime tender beef meat with onion
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Spread on one bread slice
4. Cover with another bread slice
5. Press together slightly and serve
6. Onion may be omitted if desired
7. Butter and mustard are not used with these sandwiches
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Saratoga Chips (Saratogské brambory)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw peeled potatoes
- salt water
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut peeled raw potatoes into slices with a kitchen mandoline, or if you do not have one, carefully with a knife so that all slices are as equal and thin as possible
2. Place them in salt water for half an hour
3. Remove them and dry them well with a cloth; the better you dry them, the sooner they will be fried and the lighter they will be
4. Heat plenty of lard in a pot as for frying doughnuts and when it is quite hot, fry the dried potatoes until golden, which takes only a few moments
5. Remove the fried potatoes with a skimmer and place in a dish; when all are fried, if they are not salty enough, salt them

title: Sardele na Stůl Předkládati (Anchovies Served at Table)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- best and largest anchovies
- capers
- lemon
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Select the best and largest anchovies, wash, clean, remove bones, cut lengthwise
2. Arrange in a star shape on a plate
3. Garnish with capers and lemon sliced into rounds
4. Pour vinegar and oil over according to taste

title: Sardele Smažené (Fried Anchovies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large anchovies
- flour
- wine
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Select large anchovies, wash clean, cut off tails and fins, tear in half, remove bones, dry in clean cloth
2. Sprinkle with flour, dip in batter made from flour and wine, fry in hot butter
3. Serve garnished with green parsley after soup
4. Alternative: Anchovies may also be dipped in beaten egg and coated in grated bread, then fried the same way as other fish

title: Sardelová Omáčka (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 anchovies
- 2 tablespoons capers
- 2 hard egg yolks
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) butter
- good broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Finely chop the anchovies, capers, and hard egg yolks
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Add good broth and cook with the butter

title: Sardelová omáčka (Sardine Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two sardines
- two tablespoons of capers
- two hard egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- good broth
- 60 grams of sardine butter

## Directions

1. Finely chop two sardines, two tablespoons of capers and two hard egg yolks
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Pour good broth over
4. Simmer with 60 grams of sardine butter
5. This sauce is suitable for veal, mutton, poultry and fish

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Eighth Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 anchovies
- half liter beef broth
- rose roux

## Directions

1. Add the anchovies to half liter of beef broth
2. Let simmer
3. Add rose roux and mix everything well

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Fifth Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- butter
- flour
- finely chopped lemon zest
- onion
- strong meat broth
- white wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the anchovies, chop into small pieces
2. Place on a baking sheet with one or two egg yolks, a little butter, a spoonful of flour, finely chopped lemon zest, and onion
3. Cook with constant stirring and adding strong meat broth and good white wine, along with lemon juice
4. When everything has cooked sufficiently, strain and use as a sauce

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Fourth Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- egg yolks
- wheat flour
- finely chopped lemon zest
- finely cleaned anchovies
- good meat broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Beat butter until creamed
2. Add egg yolks and a little wheat flour
3. Add finely chopped lemon zest
4. Add finely cleaned anchovies
