5. Mix everything with good meat broth to which some white wine has been added
6. Cook everything and use as a sauce

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Second Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter white coulis
- 60 grams anchovy butter
- 2 egg yolks
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cook the white coulis with anchovy butter
2. Blanch two egg yolks in white wine and add to the sauce

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Seventh Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned, washed, and deboned anchovies
- peeled onion
- brown roux
- strong meat broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Finely chop the anchovies with the peeled onion
2. Cook with brown roux for several minutes, stirring constantly
3. Cook until almost all liquid has evaporated
4. Add strong meat broth
5. Add lemon juice to taste if desired

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Sixth Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large selected anchovies
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the anchovies and remove the bones
2. Pound them with fresh butter in a mortar
3. Press through a fine sieve
4. Place in a cold location
5. Cook brown coulis with strong beef broth
6. Remove fat and foam
7. Mix frequently and combine the sauce with the anchovy mixture prepared above

title: Sardelová Omáčka - Third Method (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- light roux
- strong meat broth
- cleaned anchovies without bones, finely chopped
- onion
- 2 egg yolks
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cook the light roux with strong meat broth
2. Add cleaned and finely chopped anchovies and onion
3. Simmer for one hour
4. Strain the mixture
5. Simmer again and while stirring constantly, add two egg yolks blanched in white wine

title: Sardelová omáčka - variant (Sardine Sauce - variant)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of white coulis
- 60 grams of sardine butter
- two egg yolks
- white wine

## Directions

1. Simmer half a liter of white coulis with 60 grams of sardine butter
2. Beat in two egg yolks that have been bloomed in white wine

title: Sardelové máslo (Sardelle Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces sardelles
- Half pound fresh butter
- Paprika

## Directions

1. Clean two ounces of sardelles, remove the bones and crush the sardelles with a pinch of paprika in a mortar into a paste; then work it on a clean board with half pound of fresh butter until smooth, like butter again, put it on a dish and place in a cool place.

title: Sardelové teplé máslo (Anchovy Warm Butter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 180 grams fresh, melted butter
- 8-10 cleaned, deboned, finely chopped anchovies

## Directions

1. Take 180 grams of fresh, melted butter
2. Add 8-10 cleaned, deboned, finely chopped anchovies to it
3. Stew them in the butter until they turn yellow and firm, stirring constantly
4. Use hot instead of sauce for fish

title: Sardinky (Canned Sardines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- canned sardines
- green parsley
- capers
- good Provençal oil

## Directions

1. Open the can with the special tool designed for this purpose
2. Remove sardines from the can just before serving
3. Arrange sardines tastefully on a dish, garnish with green parsley and capers, pour over with good Provençal oil

title: Sardinky (Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin slices of rye bread
- Sardines
- Lemon
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of rye bread into heart-shaped pieces the size of a palm
2. Place two sardines on each piece
3. Sprinkle with their oil
4. Place a piece of lemon on top
5. Garnish on the sides with a piece of parsley
6. Present one at a time on small plates
7. Sardines must be kept on ice and removed from storage only before preparation
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Saská kaše (Saxon Porridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter fresh, cold milk
- 6 tablespoons fine flour
- 250 grams butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 100 grams finely ground sugar
- lemon zest from 1 lemon
- salt
- 12 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- wine or fruit sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Mix 6 tablespoons of fine flour into 1/2 liter of cold milk, stirring well so no lumps remain
2. Place on the stove, stir constantly until it boils
3. Pour onto a plate and let cool
4. Meanwhile, cream 250 grams of butter until fluffy, beat in twelve egg yolks, 100 grams of finely ground sugar, grated zest from one lemon, salt everything
5. Pour into the lukewarm porridge, add the stiffly beaten snow from twelve egg whites
6. Grease a mold with butter, fill it, and bake for about an hour with gentle heat
7. Serve the baked porridge with wine or fruit sauce

title: Saské koláče (Saxon Tarts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams fine flour
- 100 grams fresh butter
- six egg yolks
- 90 grams sugar
- lukewarm sour cream
- 40 grams dissolved yeast

## Directions

1. From fine flour, fresh butter, egg yolks, sugar, lukewarm sour cream, and dissolved yeast, prepare dough and season with salt
2. Work the dough well and roll it out
3. Cut into thin sticks
4. Let rise on a greased baking sheet
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Bake until brown
7. While baking, brush the tarts on the stove with dissolved sugar and cinnamon

title: Sauce from Duck Leftovers (Podpoušťka ze zbytků kachních)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- flour
- duck sauce or gravy from previous day
- broth or water
- vinegar
- red wine
- sour cucumber
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- new spice
- duck leftovers

## Directions

1. Simmer chopped onion, add a little flour, reserved duck sauce or gravy from the previous day, broth or water, several tablespoons vinegar, a glass of red wine, sour cucumber cut into slices, bay leaf, several peppercorns and new spice. Cook everything briefly, then reheat the duck leftovers in the sauce.

title: Scukernatělá skořice (Sugared Cinnamon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- tragacanth flour
- crushed sugar
- crushed cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix tragacanth flour with crushed sugar and a bit of crushed cinnamon
2. Work into a dough
3. Shape small thin sticks from the dough
4. Let dry completely
5. Place in a pot or small pan and sugar as described for almonds
6. Sugared cinnamon can also be colored in various ways

title: Scukernatělé třešně (Sugared Cherries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- preserved cherries
- crushed sugar
- gum dissolved in water

## Directions

1. Take preserved cherries, squeeze them in your hands and shape nicely round
2. Place them in crushed sugar in which they become well coated
3. Place on a sieve so they do not touch each other
4. Let them dry
5. Dampen the dried cherries with dissolved gum
6. Dust again with finely crushed sugar
7. Let dry on warm stoves
8. After a few hours when completely dry, repeat everything described here again, which can be repeated several times depending on how heavily sugared the cherries should be

title: Scukernatělý hřebiček (Sugared Clove)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram cloves
- 1 kilogram sugar
- finest potato flour

## Directions

1. Crush quarter kilogram of cloves with kilogram of sugar
2. Sift and mix with finest potato flour
3. Mix together with dissolved sugar to form a dough
4. Shape the dough to resemble cloves
5. Place on warm stoves to dry and harden

title: Scukernatělý oranž (Candied Orange)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- orange
- water
- sugar
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Using a sharp, pointed knife, carefully peel the orange skin in small pieces, such that alternating strips of peeled and unpeeled skin remain on the orange.
2. Pierce the orange in several places with a thin pointed knife.
3. Place the whole orange in clean fresh water.
4. After several hours, remove the orange from this water and place it in fresh water, in which it should be cooked until soft.
5. Then place it in thin sugar syrup and leave it soaking overnight.
6. On the second day, remove the orange, prepare thick sugar syrup, place the orange in it and boil again. Repeat this on the third and fourth days, until the sugar cooks down into solid crystalline pieces.
7. On the fifth day, the sugar is completely hard and may be transferred to containers.
8. The orange must gradually be cooked according to this method over several days, first in weakly sugared, later in strongly sugared water, so that if it were fully cooked in strongly sugared water on the first day, it would shrivel greatly, whereas in this way it retains its complete shape.
9. A few drops of lemon juice may be added to prevent the sugar from hardening too much.

title: Sekanina Husí Faširovaná (Stuffed Goose Forcemeat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover goose meat
- bread soaked in milk
- hard-boiled eggs
- flour
- pepper
- garlic
- marjoram
- butter or goose fat
- eggs

## Directions

1. Remove any bones from the leftover goose and chop very finely
2. Add finely chopped bread soaked in milk (according to the amount of leftover meat), finely chopped hard-boiled eggs, flour, pepper, garlic, marjoram, and either butter or goose fat
3. Add raw eggs as needed; mix everything well
4. Place on a baking pan greased with butter or fat and bake

title: Sekanina Telecí (Veal Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cooked veal, about 2 cups chopped finely
- piece of fresh butter
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 1 teaspoon chopped onion
- fresh parsley, chopped finely
- good beef broth
- juice from roasted meat
- lemon rind, chopped finely
- lemon juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop the leftover cooked veal very finely into about two cups
2. Melt a piece of fresh butter on the stove
3. Add one teaspoon of flour and one teaspoon of chopped onion and brown it until the onion turns slightly yellow
4. Add a little finely chopped fresh parsley
5. Dissolve with good beef broth
6. Add a little juice from the roast and let simmer for about ten minutes
7. Add the chopped meat, season lightly with salt
8. Sprinkle with very finely chopped lemon rind
9. Cover the pot and let it stand on the fire for about ten minutes, but do not boil
10. Pour lemon juice over it and serve

title: Sekanina z telecích plic (Calf Lungs Patty)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- calf lungs
- broth
- 2 onions
- butter
- parsley
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak and cook calf lungs in broth, cut into pieces
2. Fry 2 onions in butter with parsley, add the lungs, good broth and salt
3. Simmer for a quarter hour, then add a mixture with lemon juice

title: Sekanina z Telecích Plic (Minced Veal Lungs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- veal lungs
- butter
- parsley
- 2 onions
- good broth
- several egg yolks
- lemon juice
- salt
- fried bread or biscuit for serving

## Directions

1. Soak veal lungs and boil them in broth
2. Cut lungs into cubes
3. Fry 2 onions in butter with parsley
4. Add lungs and good broth, salt
5. Boil for a quarter hour
6. Mix in several egg yolks pounded with lemon juice
7. Serve surrounded by fried bread or biscuit

title: Sekanina z telecího masa (Calf Meat Patty)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 195 grams (13 lots) butter
- 3-4 eggs
- 240 grams (16 lots) bread roll soaked in milk
- ½ kilogram finely chopped calf meat
- 195 grams bacon
- salt
- ground nutmeg
- several tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Cream butter with 3-4 eggs
2. Mix in bread roll soaked in milk, finely chopped calf meat, bacon, salt and ground nutmeg
3. Add several tablespoons of sour cream

title: Sekanina z Telecího Masa (Minced Veal Loaf)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 195 grams (13 lots) butter
- 3-4 eggs
- 240 grams (16 lots) bread soaked in milk
- half kilogram finely minced veal
- 195 grams bacon
- salt
- ground nutmeg
- several tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Rub butter with 3-4 eggs
2. Mix in bread soaked in milk, minced veal, bacon, salt, and ground nutmeg
3. Add several tablespoons of sour cream

title: Sekanina ze skopového masa (Minced Mutton)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted mutton leg
- onions
- flour
- beef broth
- meat juice
- red wine
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Remove skin and fat from leftover roasted mutton leg, cut into strips and set aside.
2. Braise 2 onions with a spoonful of flour.
3. Add half a liter of good broth, a little meat juice and a glass of red wine.
4. Cook the sauce, remove fat, strain, pepper and salt it.
5. Add the meat, heat it in the sauce and can be served with mashed potatoes.

title: Sekanina ze zbytků (Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover beef, pork or veal
- potatoes
- butter
- onion
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the meat finely with a chopper in a wooden bowl. At the same time chop the potatoes separately, using about half as much potato as meat.
2. Place two tablespoons of butter and one finely chopped onion in a skillet and let fry until it begins to yellow. Add the meat and potatoes, pour in about two tablespoons of water, salt and pepper, and over brisk heat with constant stirring, cook for about 15 minutes.

title: Sekaná Telecí Pečené (Veal Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds veal meat
- ½ pound salt pork, or ¼ pound butter
- 3 eggs
- 2 slices bread, rolled out
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Chop the veal meat and salt pork finely by hand or with a grinder
2. Add three eggs, two slices of rolled bread, two tablespoons of sour cream, one tablespoon of butter
3. Season with salt and pepper and mix well
4. Form into an oblong loaf
5. Grease a baking pan with butter and place the loaf in it
6. Place a piece of butter on top
7. Pour approximately two tablespoons of water over it
8. Bake for about two hours, basting frequently with the pan juices
9. When cooled after baking, slice into neat pieces

title: Sekaná vepřová pečené (Pork Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds pork
- 4 slices white bread soaked in water or milk, or 6 crackers
- half a grated onion
- a piece of finely chopped lemon peel
- three eggs
- a bit of white wine
- salt
- ground pepper
- allspice
- grated bread for topping

## Directions

1. Chop three pounds of pork
2. Add 4 slices of white bread soaked in water or milk, or 6 crackers
3. Add half a grated onion
4. Add a piece of finely chopped lemon peel
5. Add three eggs and a bit of white wine
6. Salt it
7. Season with ground pepper and allspice
8. Mix well with your hands
9. Form into an oblong loaf
10. Grease a baking pan with butter or lard
11. Place the meatloaf in it
12. Sprinkle with grated bread
13. Bake until nicely browned
14. Baste frequently during baking with melted lard or butter
15. Remove to a platter
16. Slice into equal pieces
17. Pour over either the pan drippings or a lemon sauce

title: Sekaná vepřová uzená kýta (Deviled Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked ham hock with skin
- water
- flour
- paprika or pepper
- allspice
- salt

## Directions

1. In the evening, place the ham hock with skin in a container
2. Pour enough water over it so it is completely submerged and hold it down with a stone so it stays submerged
3. In the morning, rinse it well in about two changes of water
4. Mix flour with water and knead into a large dough patty
5. Spread it over the entire ham hock, pressing it well everywhere
6. Place on a baking pan and bake at moderate heat for 3-4 hours depending on the size of the ham hock
7. When done, remove the dough and skin
8. When cooled, cut the meat into small pieces
9. Put through a meat grinder and grind finely
10. Season to taste with paprika or pepper, allspice, and salt if not salty enough
11. Mix everything well with the meat
12. Put into glass jars, seal well and store in a cool place
13. It will keep as long as it is not eaten

title: Silná Polévka ze Zvěřiny (Strong Game Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- old hare
- wild rabbit
- old partridges
- veal
- salt
- onion
- clove
- carrot
- celery
- leek
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Cook the hare, rabbit, partridges, and veal together slowly for three to four hours
2. Add salt, onion, clove, carrot, celery, leek, and bay leaf to the soup
3. Strain the soup
4. Remove the fat from it
5. Serve at table

title: Skládaný dort s ovocnými rosoly (Layer Jelly Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 0.5 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 3 egg whites
- raspberry or currant or other fruit jelly
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put 0.5 cup butter in a deep bowl and beat well
2. Gradually add 1 cup sugar in small pieces and beat well with the butter
3. Add 3 egg yolks one by one, mixing well each time
4. Sift 2 cups flour with 2 teaspoons baking powder
5. Pour 0.5 cup milk into batter and add half the sifted flour
6. Add another 0.5 cup milk and remaining flour, beat well
7. Add 1 teaspoon vanilla extract and gently fold in stiff peaks from 3 egg whites
8. Divide among three greased plates and bake
9. When cooled, spread each layer with raspberry, currant or other fruit jelly and stack together
10. Leave top layer unspread with only powdered sugar or cover with sugar glaze

title: Skládaný kokosový dort (Coconut Layer Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour
- 3 egg whites
- 1 cup powdered sugar
- shredded coconut

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup sugar and 0.5 cup butter well
2. Add egg yolks from 3 eggs
3. Add 1 cup sweet milk
4. Add 2 teaspoons baking powder and enough flour to make batter slightly thicker than pancake batter
5. Divide into three plates and bake
6. Beat 3 egg whites into stiff peaks
7. Add about 1 cup powdered sugar
8. When cake is baked and slightly cooled, stack individual layers together
9. Spread some of the beaten egg whites on each layer and sprinkle with shredded coconut, including on top

title: Skládaný čokoládový dort (Chocolate Layer Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- two-thirds cup water
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 cups flour
- vanilla
- 0.5 cup sugar
- 0.5 cup syrup
- 0.5 cup milk or cream
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 5 tablespoons grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Mix 1.5 cups sugar well with 0.5 cup butter
2. Beat egg whites and yolks separately
3. Add yolks to sugar and butter
4. Add two-thirds cup water and 2 teaspoons baking powder mixed into 3 cups sifted flour
5. Finally add egg whites and a little vanilla
6. Bake in thin layers on metal plates
7. For the chocolate coating: boil 0.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup syrup, 0.5 cup milk or cream, and 1 teaspoon butter for five minutes
8. Add 5 tablespoons grated chocolate
9. Cook until thick and when cooled add vanilla
10. Spread the chocolate coating between layers and on top

title: Skládaný čokoládový dort na jiný způsob (Chocolate Layer Cake, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 0.25 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 0.5 cup milk
- 3 tablespoons grated chocolate
- 1.5 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 0.5 teaspoon baking soda
- 0.5 teaspoon vanilla
- chocolate for frosting
- egg whites
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup sugar and 0.25 cup butter well
2. Add 2 eggs and mix well
3. Add 0.5 cup milk and 3 tablespoons grated chocolate and mix again
4. Take 1.5 cups flour, add 1 teaspoon cream of tartar, mix well and add to chocolate batter
5. Add 0.5 teaspoon dissolved baking soda and 0.5 teaspoon vanilla and mix all together
6. Alternatively use 3 teaspoons baking powder instead of cream of tartar and soda
7. Bake in thin layers on metal plates
8. For frosting: melt chocolate and mix into egg whites
9. Add sugar and continue mixing until thick enough
10. Spread frosting between layers and on top, making three layers

title: Skopová kýta (Mutton Leg)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton leg
- garlic
- salt
- potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Remove bones, fat and all membranes from the mutton leg.
2. Beat, wash and salt it.
3. Pierce it and fill the holes with garlic.
4. Place on a baking sheet, cover and add water.
5. Braise slowly in the oven, turning until both sides are evenly cooked.
6. Cut into neat pieces and arrange with potatoes.

title: Skopová kýta nakládaná (Marinated Mutton Leg)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton leg
- salt
- pepper (coarsely crushed)
- juniper berries
- new spices
- thyme
- bay leaves
- onion
- good vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Remove membranes and fat from the mutton leg, salt it.
2. Mix roughly crushed pepper, juniper and new spices, sprinkle on the leg.
3. Add a piece of thyme, about 4 bay leaves and sliced onion.
4. Boil one liter of good vinegar with 1 liter and 4 deciliters of water.
5. Pour this over the leg, place in a wide container, weigh down with a stone to keep it well compressed; keep in winter up to 14 days.
6. When ready to prepare, wash it and place on a baking sheet.
7. Pour part of the pickling liquid over it, dilute the remainder with water and vinegar until about half submerged.
8. Add several peppercorns, thyme and 2 whole onions, bake well on both sides.
9. Make either white or brown sauce as for game.
10. Cut the roast and arrange with neat pieces of puff pastry.

title: Skopová s Kapary (Mutton with Capers)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton roast flattened and cleaned
- salt
- butter
- hot water
- chopped onion
- 2 tablespoons capers
- small roasted potatoes

## Directions

1. Salt dried roast and brown it on both sides in butter
2. Pour 2 cups hot water into the sauce
3. Add chopped onion with 2 tablespoons capers to the sauce and simmer briefly
4. Serve roast with small roasted potatoes around it

title: Skopová s Kmínem (Mutton with Caraway)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- salt
- vegetables
- gravy
- wine
- bay leaf
- lemon
- 2 tablespoons crushed caraway seeds
- fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Salt mutton and boil with vegetables
2. Cut meat into pieces
3. Cook gravy with gravy roux, add wine, bay leaf, lemon, and 2 tablespoons crushed caraway seeds
4. Serve on platter with fried potatoes

title: Skopová s Omáčkou (Mutton with Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton breast, neck, or ribs
- salt
- parsley root
- celery
- carrots
- 3 peppercorns
- sardines
- 15 grams (1 lot) butter
- 3 cloves garlic
- marjoram flowers
- half a grated bread roll
- lemon cut into slices or quarters

## Directions

1. Place mutton on a baking pan, salt it
2. Add parsley root, celery, carrots, and peppercorns
3. Braise until meat is tender
4. Strain broth over meat on another pan
5. Clean 4-5 sardines and remove bones
6. Rub sardines with butter, add 3 cloves garlic, marjoram flowers, and half grated bread roll
7. Add this mixture to the meat and braise briefly
8. Arrange on a platter and surround with lemon slices or quarters

title: Skopová sekanina s vejci (Stewed Mutton Hash with Poached Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted mutton leg with bone
- brown gravy
- beef broth
- salt
- lemon juice
- butter
- poached eggs

## Directions

1. From nice leftover roasted mutton leg, chop the meat with the bone finely and place on a dish.
2. Add to the dish as much brown gravy as needed (similar to what is prepared for calf sweetbreads), then add half as much beef broth as you added gravy.
3. Mix well, season with salt, squeeze lemon juice into it, strain the gravy through a sieve onto the hash, mix well, place pieces of butter on top, pour a little brown broth over it, and set it in a warm place.
4. Just before serving, bring it to a hot fire, stir until the hash is boiling, arrange it on a dish, surround it with poached eggs.
5. Prepare the poached eggs as follows: into boiling water mixed with vinegar, quickly drop fresh eggs, and as soon as they rise to the surface, remove them with a skimmer, sprinkle them with a little pepper and salt, and arrange them around the meat.

title: Skopové hrudí s dušenou rýží (Mutton Brisket with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton brisket
- salt water
- beef broth
- large cup of rice
- broth from the brisket
- water
- onion
- two cloves
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean a nice mutton brisket well, cut off a small lower bone, let it boil for a quarter hour in salt water, then cool.
2. Now trim the brisket neatly and let it cook slowly in beef broth until tender.
3. Meanwhile, pick over a large cup of nice rice, place it on a shallow pan, pour two cups of broth from the brisket and two cups of water over it, add an onion into which you have pressed two cloves, a piece of butter, salt it.
4. Cover the pan and let the rice braise slowly until it is tender and all the liquid is absorbed into it.
5. Now place the rice on an oblong platter, lay the brisket cut into neat pieces on it, and pour a little of the broth in which you cooked the brisket over it.

title: Skopové Hrudí Stočené (Rolled Mutton Breast)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton breast
- salt
- onion chopped
- sardines cut up
- twine
- broth
- spices
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Remove bones from breast and salt it
2. Sprinkle with chopped onion and cut-up sardines
3. Roll and tie with twine
4. Pour in enough broth to cover
5. Add spices and vegetables to cover
6. Braise slowly
7. Remove fat from sauce and pour over roast

title: Skopové jazyky s citrónovou omáčkou (Sheep's Tongues with Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton tongues
- salted water
- celery
- parsley
- carrot
- onion
- butter
- flour
- sugar
- lemon
- wine
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook several mutton tongues until tender in salted water with a piece of celery, parsley, carrot, and half an onion.
2. Peel the tongues, cut each one lengthwise into two halves, and place them on a serving dish.
3. In a small pot, put one tablespoon of butter, one tablespoon of flour, a piece of sugar on which you have grated the zest of one lemon, and add the juice of that same lemon, mix well.
4. Pour in a skimmer-full of boiling broth from the tongues mixed with a glass of wine, mix well, and pour over the tongues and let it simmer.
5. You can also thicken the sauce with two egg yolks.

title: Skopové kotletky (Mutton Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- young mutton ribs
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- gravy

## Directions

1. Prepare cutlets from young ribs with the rib bone attached
2. Trim neatly
3. Clean the meat from the bone ends so cutlets can be held conveniently
4. Scrape the bone clean
5. Salt and dust with ground pepper on both sides
6. Place on hot iron pan with melted butter
7. Roast quickly on both sides over high heat
8. Cook five to ten minutes until done
9. Arrange on a heated dish
10. Pour good gravy over them

title: Skopové ledvinky (Mutton Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton kidneys
- cold water
- onion
- butter
- caraway seeds
- breadcrumbs or flour
- pepper
- salt
- fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Slice the mutton kidneys lengthwise into thin strips and rinse in cold water.
2. Chop the onion finely and fry it in a piece of butter until golden.
3. Add the kidneys to it, sprinkle with a little caraway seed, and let them stew until tender.
4. Then season with salt and pepper, add either a little grated breadcrumbs or dust with flour, mix, and let stew a little longer.
5. Remove them onto a dish and surround with fried potatoes.

title: Skopové maso (Mutton) - Another Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton breast
- vegetables
- salt
- raw potatoes
- pepper
- garlic
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Cook mutton breast with all vegetables.
2. When half-cooked, add raw potatoes cut into slices and continue cooking until everything is tender.
3. Cut the meat into neat pieces and make a sauce.
4. Strain the broth in which the potatoes were cooked, add pepper, crushed garlic, and a little marjoram.
5. Mix well and simmer for about a quarter hour.
6. Strain the potatoes and sauce onto the meat.

title: Skopové Maso s Chřestem (Mutton with Asparagus)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton off the bone
- carrots
- parsley root
- cabbage
- 15 grams (1 lot) good butter
- asparagus

## Directions

1. Braise mutton with carrots, parsley root, cabbage, and butter until meat is tender
2. Strain pale gravy over the meat
3. Cut asparagus at the point where it becomes woody
4. Braise asparagus briefly with meat until tender
5. Arrange asparagus around the meat or in the center on a platter

title: Skopové Maso s Kapustou (Mutton with Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton meat
- vegetables
- cabbage
- cabbage broth
- 3 cloves garlic
- pepper
- marjoram
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. Wash mutton and boil it with vegetables
2. Remove outer leaves of cabbage, clean and cut into quarters or remove ribs from each leaf separately
3. Boil prepared cabbage leaves, drain water
4. Pour mutton broth over it, add 3 cloves minced garlic, pepper, and marjoram
5. Braise until tender and thicken with brown gravy
6. If cabbage was cut into quarters, arrange carefully on plate so it does not break, and strain gravy into the cooking liquid
7. Warm meat in this, arrange neatly in the center of platter, and cabbage around it

title: Skopové maso s marjankovou omáčkou (Mutton with Marjoram Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds young mutton from the shoulder
- butter
- flour
- broth from cooking the meat
- garlic, rubbed with salt
- fresh parsley, finely chopped
- marjoram
- cloves
- small boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook three pounds of young mutton from the shoulder and cut it into neat pieces.
2. Make a light roux from one tablespoon of butter and one and a half tablespoons of flour, thin it with the broth in which the meat was cooked, and place the meat in it.
3. For each pound of meat, add a piece of garlic rubbed with salt, a little finely chopped fresh parsley, a little marjoram, and a pinch of cloves.
4. Peel small boiled potatoes, add them, let it simmer a little, then arrange the meat on a dish, surround with potatoes, and pour the gravy over it.
5. Alternatively, you can scrape raw potatoes, cut them into pieces, and when the meat is half-cooked, add them to the broth and cook them together with the meat.

title: Skopové Maso s Marjánkovou Omáčkou (Mutton with Marjoram Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton breast
- vegetables
- salt
- handful of marjoram
- pepper
- 4 cloves garlic
- mutton fat
- brown gravy
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash mutton breast and boil with all vegetables and salt
2. When cooked, cut into neat pieces and place on platter
3. Rub a handful of marjoram and add to the broth with pepper and 4 cloves garlic
4. Boil and add enough mutton fat to brown gravy to thicken it
5. Boil, strain through a sieve, add mutton meat
6. Leave briefly on a baking pan and pour over boiled potatoes

title: Skopové maso s marjánkovou omáčkou (Mutton with Marjoram Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton breast
- vegetables
- salt
- marjoram
- pepper
- four cloves of garlic
- mutton fat
- flour
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash the mutton breast and cook it with all vegetables and salt.
2. When cooked, cut it into neat pieces and set aside on a plate.
3. Crush a handful of marjoram and add it to the broth with pepper and four cloves of garlic, bring to a boil.
4. Add enough mutton fat to flour prepared in a roux so that it thickens.
5. Boil and strain through a sieve, add the mutton meat, leave for a while longer on the baking sheet.
6. Pour over boiled potatoes.

title: Skopové Maso s Mrkví (Mutton with Carrots)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton ribs
- water
- all vegetables
- salt
- carrots
- pepper
- piece of sugar
- pale gravy

## Directions

1. Place mutton ribs on a baking pan and pour water over them
2. Add all vegetables and salt lightly
3. Boil until meat is tender
4. Meanwhile wash and cut carrots into thin noodles
5. Season with pepper and add a piece of sugar
6. Braise carrots, continually basting with broth until completely tender
7. Make pale gravy and press enough of it through to thicken properly
8. Cut meat into neat pieces, add to carrots, caramelize a piece of sugar to give it a pink color

title: Skopové Maso s Rýží (Mutton with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- vegetables
- rice
- broth from mutton

## Directions

1. Boil mutton with all vegetables
2. Meanwhile wash rice and pour a little mutton broth over it
3. Braise rice, gradually adding broth until rice is tender
4. Cut mutton into pieces, place on platter
5. Arrange rice on top

title: Skopové s Cibulí (Mutton with Onion)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- onion leaves
- crushed caraway seeds
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Boil mutton
2. Remove foam
3. Add several onion leaves and crushed caraway seeds
4. Boil together
5. Add fried bread to the sauce

title: Skopové s kedluby (Mutton with Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton ribs
- small kohlrabi
- butter
- pepper
- sugar
- young tender leaves from kohlrabi root
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Cook mutton ribs; meanwhile peel small kohlrabi and cut into noodles.
2. Place on a shallow pan with a small piece of butter, pepper, add a piece of sugar, also young tender leaves from the kohlrabi root finely chopped and let braise until tender.
3. When both the meat and kohlrabi are tender, make a light roux, dilute it with broth in which the meat was cooking and pour into the kohlrabi.
4. Add a pinch of marjoram and mix and add the already cooked meat and serve.

title: Skopové s knedlíky (Pot Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of mutton from the leg
- half a small onion
- garlic clove
- salt
- water
- two cups of flour
- small spoon of butter
- small spoon of baking powder
- one egg yolk
- small cup of milk

## Directions

1. Place two pounds of mutton from the leg in a pot, add half a small onion cut into pieces, a clove of garlic rubbed with salt, salt, pour water to cover the meat, cover and let it braise.
2. When the meat is tender, prepare dough as follows: Take two cups of flour, crumble into it a small spoon of butter, salt, add a small spoon of baking powder, one egg yolk and a small cup of milk, about enough so that the dough is like for biscuits.
3. Sprinkle the rolling board with flour, place the dough on it, work it a little with your hands, roll it out to a finger's thickness and cut into squares about three fingers wide and long.
4. Check that there is enough broth on the meat; if the meat is not covered with broth, pour hot water over it and then cover the meat with these squares, one next to the other.
5. Cover well so that no steam escapes and let it boil for 10 minutes.
6. Then remove the dough and meat with a fork onto a platter and make a gravy from the remaining broth.
7. Mix a spoon of flour in half a cup of milk and pour into the broth in the pot; if not salty enough, salt and pepper it and pour over the dumplings and meat.

title: Skopové s mrkví (Mutton with Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- salted mutton ribs with all vegetables
- 6 to 8 carrots
- butter
- pepper
- sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook salted mutton ribs with all vegetables until tender.
2. Meanwhile, peel 6 to 8 carrots and cut them into strips.
3. Place them on a shallow pan with a piece of butter, pepper, add a spoonful of sugar and let them braise with frequent basting with the broth in which the meat is cooking until tender.
4. Then make a light roux from a spoonful of butter and a heaping spoonful of flour to thicken sufficiently.
5. Add the cooked ribs cut into neat pieces and a piece of caramelized sugar.
6. Mix everything well and let it simmer once more.
7. Mutton with cabbage is prepared the same way, with cabbage cut into quarters; one can also use half cabbage and half carrots.

title: Skopové s Zemáky (Mutton with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton breast
- vegetables
- salt
- raw potatoes

## Directions

1. Boil mutton breast with all vegetables
2. When half cooked, cut raw potatoes into slices and add to it
3. Boil until everything is tender

title: Skopové se zeleným hráškem (Mutton Stew with Green Pease)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of mutton from the shoulder
- flour
- lard
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- water
- green peas

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of mutton from the shoulder into large cubes.
2. Sprinkle with flour and brown them to a golden color in half lard and half butter.
3. Salt and pepper and pour water into the pan so that the meat is covered.
4. Cover and let it braise until tender.
5. When the meat is tender, remove it and skim all the fat from the gravy.
6. Before serving at the table, pour the gravy into the green peas, which you have previously cooked, and mix the prepared meat into it.

title: Skopové Zadělávané na Divoko (Mutton Braised Wild Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- wine vinegar
- water
- thyme
- bay leaf
- pepper
- allspice
- onion
- lemon zest
- elder preserves or dark gingerbread
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash mutton, place on a baking pan
2. Add wine vinegar, water, thyme, bay leaf, pepper, allspice, onion, and lemon zest
3. Braise until meat is tender
4. Cut meat into pieces and make a pink gravy
5. Use either elder preserves or dark gingerbread and cook with the gravy
6. If bitter, add a piece of sugar
7. Strain sauce over meat and serve with potatoes around it

title: Skopové řízky (Mutton Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton loin or back meat
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut thick chops from the loin or back
2. Pound them with a mallet
3. Trim neatly with a knife
4. Salt the chops
5. Brush with melted butter
6. Roast on a grill or baking pan on both sides quickly
7. Cook for ten to twelve minutes until done

title: Skořicová omáčka (Cinnamon Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cream or milk
- a piece of cinnamon
- sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon flour
- cold cream
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the cream or milk with a piece of cinnamon
2. Add a little sugar and let it stand
3. In another vessel, mix half a teaspoon of flour with cold cream into which 4 egg yolks have been beaten
4. When the cinnamon mixture has cooled completely, pour into this sauce
5. Mix everything again
6. Slowly thicken over gentle heat
7. Strain; it is excellent for flour dishes

title: Skořicová Paštička (Cinnamon Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- cinnamon oil

## Directions

1. Cook sugar to the grain stage
2. Add a few drops of cinnamon oil
3. Proceed exactly as with the coffee paste

title: Skořicová poleva (Cinnamon Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- white frosting base
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon extract or 1 teaspoon finely ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare frosting as previously mentioned
2. Fold in 1 teaspoon of cinnamon extract, or 1 teaspoon of finely ground cinnamon

title: Skořicové hvězdičky (Cinnamon Stars)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound almonds
- egg white
- one pound powdered sugar
- half ounce ground cinnamon
- sugar glaze
- cinnamon for glaze

## Directions

1. Grind half pound of almonds finely with egg white
2. Knead well with one pound powdered sugar and half ounce ground cinnamon into a thick dough
3. Roll out the dough on paper to the thickness of a straw
4. Cut out stars with a metal star-shaped mold
5. Place them on a baking sheet lined with paper
6. Bake slowly
7. When removed from the oven, brush with sugar glaze mixed with a little cinnamon
8. When the stars cool, remove them from the paper

title: Skořicové hvězdičky (Cinnamon Stars)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound almonds
- egg white
- one pound powdered sugar
- half ounce ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pound half pound almonds finely with an egg white
2. Knead well with one pound powdered sugar and half ounce ground cinnamon

title: Skořicové koláče (Cinnamon Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- 125 grams fresh melted butter
- 70 grams finely ground sugar
- two teaspoons ground and sifted cinnamon
- good white wine

## Directions

1. Mix flour, butter, sugar, and cinnamon
2. Mix with white wine to form dough
3. Knead thoroughly, roll out
4. Shape cookies, brush with white, sprinkle with sugar
5. Bake slowly on greased sheet

title: Skořicové Mandle (Cinnamon Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- sugar
- finely crushed cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare as described for white sugared almonds
2. When almonds are completely coated with sugar, add finely crushed cinnamon to the warm sugar
3. Stir all together again
4. Place almonds on sieves to dry completely

title: Skořicové obloučky (Cinnamon Arches)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 1/2 kilogram pounded and sifted fine sugar
- juice and grated peel of 1 lemon
- 1/2 kilogram pounded almonds
- 1 tablespoon finely pounded cinnamon
- butter or pure wax
- sugar water or egg yolks for glazing

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Fold in 1/2 kilogram pounded and sifted fine sugar
3. Add lemon juice and grated peel from 1 lemon
4. Add 1/2 kilogram pounded almonds and 1 tablespoon finely pounded cinnamon
5. Mix thoroughly and work well
6. Roll out on a board and cut into long thin pieces
7. Curve into arches as desired
8. Brush with melted butter or pure wax
9. Bake on a sheet slowly to golden over gentle heat
10. While baking, brush with sugar water or egg yolks for a nice sheen

title: Skořicové pokroutky (Cinnamon Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- finely ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook sugar, add a little finely ground cinnamon
2. Cook together until completely thickened
3. Spread on paper and when it hardens, peel off and cut into pieces

title: Skořicové pokroutky (Cinnamon Lozenges)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup fine sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 coffee spoon cinnamon
- 1/2 finely chopped lemon zest
- 1/4 pound flour
- almonds cut into noodles
- fine sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 cup fine sugar, 1 whole egg, and 1 egg yolk in a bowl and stir for half an hour
2. Add 1 coffee spoon cinnamon, 1/2 finely chopped lemon zest, and 1/4 pound flour
3. Mix well
4. Coat a sheet with wax, drop small mounds of dough onto it
5. Cover with almonds cut into noodles
6. Sprinkle with fine sugar and bake slowly

title: Skořicové preclíky (Cinnamon Pretzels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams well-creamed butter
- 1 egg
- 250 grams sugar
- 4 grams finely ground cinnamon
- 375 grams sifted fine flour
- 1 gram ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Take 125 grams of well-creamed butter
2. Gradually beat in 1 egg, 250 grams of sugar, 4 grams of finely ground cinnamon, 375 grams of sifted fine flour, and 1 gram of ground vanilla
3. Knead into perfect dough
4. Roll out and form into pretzel shapes
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Bake on a baking sheet slowly at gentle heat

title: Skořicový chlebíček (Cinnamon Little Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16-20 grams ground and sifted cinnamon
- 1/2 kilogram unpeeled, ground almonds
- 1/2 kilogram flour
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 kilogram of sugar with 8 egg yolks and beat
2. Add 16-20 grams of ground and sifted cinnamon, 1/2 kilogram of unpeeled ground almonds, and 1/2 kilogram of flour
3. Knead into dough, roll out, and form into thin sticks
4. Cut into pieces about ten centimeters long and about five centimeters wide
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Bake slowly on a baking sheet in a gently heated oven

title: Skořicový dort (Cinnamon Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams sugar
- 250 grams unpeeled almonds
- 12 crushed and well-beaten egg yolks
- 24 grams ground cinnamon
- lemon zest shavings
- candied citron zest finely cut
- egg white snow from 6 to 8 whites

## Directions

1. Take 250 grams sugar and 250 grams unpeeled almonds
2. Beat 12 egg yolks well and add to the almonds
3. Add 24 grams ground cinnamon, lemon zest shavings, and finely cut candied citron zest to everything
4. Add stiff egg white snow from 6 to 8 whites
5. Pour into a baking form
6. Bake slowly
7. Cover the top with sugar glaze

title: Skořicový Nákyp (Cinnamon Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients as for lemon soufflé
- finely crushed cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare this soufflé like lemon soufflé, but add finely crushed cinnamon to the dough
2. In this way one can also prepare vanilla, clove, and other soufflés

title: Sladká polévka z mléka (Sweet Milk Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 quarts of good milk
- 1 tablespoon of sugar
- salt
- 3 egg yolks
- a little cold milk
- a piece of crushed sugar
- lemon zest
- bread crust
- finely crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1.5 quarts of good milk in a pot and let it cook, add a tablespoon of sugar and salt it lightly.
2. In another pot, beat 3 egg yolks with a little cold milk, add a piece of crushed sugar mixed with lemon zest.
3. Pour this into a soup tureen and add the cooked milk to it, stirring as you pour.
4. Cut bread crust into cubes, place on a baking sheet, sprinkle generously with finely crushed sugar, put in the oven and let it bake.
5. Put the bread cubes on a plate and serve separately with the soup.

title: Sladká Polévka z Mléka (Sweet Milk Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 quarts good milk
- 1 spoon sugar
- Salt
- 3 egg yolks
- Cold milk

## Directions

1. Heat 1.5 quarts good milk
2. Add 1 spoon sugar and a little salt
3. In a separate bowl, beat 3 egg yolks with a little cold milk

title: Sladká račí omáčka (Sweet Crayfish Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12-15 crayfish
- 125 grams crayfish butter
- 1 teaspoon fine flour
- 1/2 liter cream or warm milk
- salt
- sugar
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish
2. From the shells prepare crayfish butter
3. Mix 125 grams of this butter with 1 teaspoon fine flour
4. Boil with 1/2 liter cream or warm milk
5. Season with salt and sugar
6. Beat in 2 egg yolks

title: Sladká Vinní Polévka (Sweet Wine Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wine
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- large sultana raisins
- fried grated bread

## Directions

1. Add a little water to wine as needed
2. Add sugar, cinnamon, and finely chopped lemon peel
3. Simmer together
4. Add cleaned and picked large sultana raisins and fried grated bread
5. Allow everything to simmer together
6. Serve at table

title: Sladké Brambory Pečené (Baked Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well, put them in the oven to bake and leave them in the oven according to size—about half an hour for small ones if the oven is nice and hot, and up to an hour for large ones
2. When they are soft enough to be mashed in the hand, they are baked
3. Serve with butter, salt and pepper

title: Sladké Brambory Smažené (Fried Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- lard or butter
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash sweet potatoes well and cook them
2. When they are soft, remove them, peel and split lengthwise into halves
3. Put about two spoonfuls of lard or butter in a pan, salt the potatoes, coat in flour and fry on both sides in hot fat until reddened
4. Note: sweet potatoes that are merely boiled tend to be too watery

title: Sladké okurky (Sweet Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet gherkins
- Onion
- Paprika peppers
- Shallots
- Tarragon leaves
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Arrange sweet gherkins cut into rounds on a dish
2. Intersperse with sliced onion and paprika peppers cut into rounds
3. Surround with several shallots
4. Sprinkle with chopped tarragon leaves
5. Pour vinegar over all
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Sladký Zavařený Vodní Meloun (Preserved Water Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- water melon
- 4 small pots of sugar
- 1 small pot of water
- 1 large lemon or 2 small lemons
- 2 handfuls of raisins

## Directions

1. When we eat the red flesh from melons, trim the green rind, cut the white flesh into cubes not too large and not too small; then take 4 small pots of sugar and 1 small pot of water, make a syrup from this and add the melon to boil in it.
2. Boil it for a full 4 hours, then add one large lemon cut into slices or two small ones, 2 handfuls of raisins and let it boil 1.5 hours longer.
3. When the melon is boiled, it should not be white but transparently yellow.

title: Slanečková Omáčka (Sprat Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onion
- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- finely chopped cleaned sprat
- wine or vinegar
- capers
- pepper
- garlic
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry chopped onion in butter with a spoonful of flour until golden
2. Cook with beef broth
3. Mix with finely chopped cleaned sprat, wine or vinegar
4. Add capers, pepper, and garlic if desired
5. Add blanched egg yolks before serving

title: Slanečky (Herrings)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herrings
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Red beets
- Hard-boiled egg whites
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Soak herrings for two days in cold water or milk
2. Split them and remove the backbone
3. Cut halves into pieces one inch long
4. Arrange two halves on an oblong flat dish so they appear uncut
5. Place the split head at one end and tail at the other
6. Drizzle with a little oil and vinegar
7. In the middle between the two herring halves, place a small heap of chopped hard-boiled egg yolk
8. Beside the yolk, between the herring halves, place a heap of chopped red beet
9. Add a heap of chopped hard-boiled egg white
10. Finally add a heap of chopped parsley
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Slanina s fazolemi (Pork and Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of dried beans
- Cold water
- Kitchen soda
- Salt
- Half a can of tomatoes or three to four fresh tomatoes
- One onion
- One tablespoon of butter
- About one pound of salted pork
- One tablespoon of syrup
- One tablespoon of sugar

## Directions

1. In the evening, soak two cups of beans in cold water
2. In the morning, put them on the stove with that water, add half a teaspoon of kitchen soda and a little salt and let cook for about half an hour
3. In a pan, put half a can of canned tomatoes or three to four fresh tomatoes, add one chopped onion, one tablespoon of butter and let braise for about half an hour
4. Then wash well about one pound of salted pork, add to the beans and strain the tomato sauce into them
5. Add one tablespoon of syrup and one tablespoon of sugar, salt to taste, cover well and bake slowly for two to three hours

title: Slané rohlíčky k čaji (Salted Tea Rolls)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams fresh, heated butter
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- 375-400 grams fine flour

## Directions

1. Rub butter until foamy
2. Beat in eggs and sour cream
3. Work in flour thoroughly
4. Let rise
5. Roll out dough and shape into thin long pieces
6. Brush with beaten egg
7. Salt
8. Place on buttered baking sheet
9. Bake until golden and crispy

title: Slepice (Hen)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hen
- parsley
- cabbage
- celery
- salt
- 1-2 eggs
- cloves
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. For the hen to be tasty, it must be salted and marinated for at least 2 days before preparation; then cut it into at least 4 parts; add parsley, cabbage and celery to it; cook until completely tender.
2. Meanwhile, make thin noodles from one or two eggs, strain the broth when cooking, put the noodles in the broth, add cloves and cauliflower.
3. Young hen may also be roasted, but must be marinated in salt longer for that purpose.

title: Slezový nápoj (Salsify Drink)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- slezový kořen
- voda
- 14 dkg cukru

## Directions

1. Slezový kořen se nakrájí na kostky
2. Vaří se tři čtvrti hodiny ve vodě
3. Kořínek se vyhodí
4. Na tři a půl decilitru scezené vody se počítá 14 dkg cukru
5. Vaří se tak dlouho, až je to úplně husté
6. Dá se do láhviček a pečlivě se uzavře

title: Slokové máslo (Roasted Meat Butter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from a roasted, fatty, juicy sole, cooled
- approximately 250 grams of butter
- lemon juice
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. From a roasted, fatty, juicy sole that has cooled, cut off the meat
2. Pound it together with approximately 250 grams of butter in a mortar
3. Push through a fine sieve
4. Salt it, sprinkle with lemon juice and pepper
5. Let it cool

title: Sluky (Woodcocks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- salt
- lard
- peppercorns
- juniper berries
- lemon zest
- onion
- pepper
- flour
- nutmeg
- bread soaked and fried
- marjoram
- garlic

## Directions

1. Clean the woodcocks and remove innards; save the liver, stomach and heart.
2. Salt the woodcocks and lard them like partridges.
3. Add several peppercorns, juniper berries, a piece of lemon zest and chopped onion.
4. Braise the woodcocks in this.
5. Finely chop the saved innards and add pepper, flour, nutmeg, a piece of fried onion in butter, fried grated bread, some marjoram and a stroke of garlic.
6. Mix everything with the innards.
7. Grease a small pan, spread the mixture on it and fry it.
8. Straighten the woodcocks and arrange the fried mixture around them, or place the mixture in the middle and the woodcocks around it.

title: Sluky dušené (Braised Woodcocks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- ham
- lard
- butter
- salt
- onion
- juniper berries
- pepper
- brown coulisse
- good wine
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Place perfectly plucked and cooked woodcocks on a baking pan.
2. Wrap with ham and lard, place tightly next to each other.
3. Pour butter over, salt, wrap with lard and braise with onion, juniper berries and pepper.
4. Add a quarter liter of brown coulisse, good wine and a little lemon zest.
5. Braise the woodcocks in this sauce until tender.
6. Strain the sauce and pour over the woodcocks when serving.

title: Sluky paštiky (Woodcock Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Butter
- Veal meat
- Ham
- Bread roll
- Eggs
- Sour cream
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Pâté mold
- Mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Pluck and cook the woodcock, remove neck and breast
2. Roast legs, head and wings in butter and pound them as finely as possible
3. Braise the woodcock in butter for a quarter hour until completely soft
4. Prepare a pâté mold, grease it with butter and line it with fine minced veal, ham, bread roll and eggs
5. Pour good sour cream over this, salt, pepper and spice it, and brown in butter
6. Place the braised woodcock tightly on this base, coat with butter
7. Work the pounded meat in with butter, cover the top with butter dough
8. Bake together for at least an hour and a half
9. Serve with mushroom sauce

title: Sluky po polsku (Woodcock Polish Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Salt
- Melted lard
- Onions, cut into pieces
- Truffles
- Mushrooms
- Bay leaf
- Ham, finely sliced
- Vegetables
- Spices
- White wine
- Coulis
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcock but do not cook it, salt it
2. Place it on a roasting pan in melted lard
3. Lay several pieces of sliced onion, truffles and mushrooms under it, along with bay leaf, finely sliced ham, vegetables and spices
4. Braise the woodcock in this mixture for about ten minutes
5. Pour good white wine and coulis over it, season with pepper and braise completely
6. Remove the fat, strain the sauce and adjust the taste with lemon juice

title: Sluky po polsku (Woodcocks à la Polonaise)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- salt
- lard
- onion
- truffles
- mushrooms
- bay leaf
- ham
- herbs
- spices
- white wine
- coulisse
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcocks but do not cook them.
2. Salt and place on a baking pan in melted lard.
3. Lay underneath several pieces of chopped onion, truffles and mushrooms, as well as bay leaf, finely chopped ham, herbs and spices.
4. Braise the woodcocks in this mixture for about ten minutes.
5. Pour good white wine and coulisse over, add pepper and braise everything completely.
6. Skim off the fat, strain the sauce and finish with lemon juice.

title: Sluky po vlašsku (Woodcocks à l'Italienne)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh woodcocks
- lard
- eggs
- sage
- sauce
- butter

## Directions

1. Pluck the fresh woodcocks, remove innards and prepare for roasting.
2. Wrap in lard and brush with beaten egg.
3. Place sage underneath.
4. Roast with constant basting of the sauce and melted butter until completely tender.

title: Sluky s lanýžemi (Woodcocks with Truffles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- truffles
- salt
- butter
- brown coulisse
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcocks, remove innards, clean and cut them.
2. Salt and stuff with finely chopped truffles.
3. Place on a baking pan in boiling butter and braise about a quarter hour.
4. Pour a glass of butter over and roast again for about a quarter hour, turning on all sides.
5. Finally add about a quarter liter of brown coulisse.
6. Simmer the sauce, make it more flavorful with lemon juice, strain and pour over the woodcocks.

title: Sluky se smetanou (Woodcock with Sour Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Plucked woodcock
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Onion
- Juniper berries
- Pepper
- Bay leaf
- Butter
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcock and cook it, then salt it
2. Place it in vinegar with onion, juniper berries, pepper and bay leaf
3. When it has lain in all of this, roast it in butter
4. Braise it completely in sour cream mixed with vinegar

title: Sluky se smetanou (Woodcocks with Sour Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- salt
- vinegar
- onion
- juniper berries
- pepper
- bay leaf
- butter
- sour cream
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcocks and cook them.
2. Salt and place in vinegar with onion, juniper berries, pepper and bay leaf.
3. When marinated, roast in butter and braise completely in sour cream mixed with vinegar.

title: Sluky v gelée (Woodcock in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Bones from woodcock
- Butter
- Fine minced game or chicken
- Lard
- Truffles, chopped
- Smoked tongue
- Aspic

## Directions

1. Pluck and cook the woodcock, remove the bones
2. Pound the bones in a mortar, braise them in butter, press through a fine sieve
3. Mix with fine minced game or chicken and use for stuffing the woodcock
4. Sew up the stuffed woodcock, wrap it in lard, roast it buttered
5. When roasted, place it on ice, cover it with chopped truffles and smoked tongue
6. Pour aspic over everything and let it set
7. Arrange decoratively for serving

title: Slívová svařenina (Plum Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe plums
- water
- sugar (half cup or more per cup of pulp)
- crushed cinnamon
- crushed cloves

## Directions

1. Wash nice ripe plums, remove the pits if they come away easily, and cook with a little water.
2. If the pits do not come away easily, cook with the pits in.
3. When soft, press through a sieve and put back in the pot.
4. After cooking for a while, add sugar—half cup or more per cup of pulp—along with a little crushed cinnamon and cloves.
5. Cook with frequent or constant stirring until the desired thickness is reached.

title: Slívový rosol (Plum Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums, wild or cultivated
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Shell the plums, wild or cultivated, put them in a pot, add a little water and cook until soft.
2. Then strain the juice and cook it with sugar, as with grape jelly.
3. If they are sweet, they do not require as much sugar.

title: Smażená kukuřičná kaše (Fried Cornmeal Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts water
- 1 quart cornmeal
- cold water
- salt
- 1 egg
- bread crumbs or flour
- hot lard
- syrup

## Directions

1. Bring two quarts of water to a boil in a large pot or earthen bowl
2. Mix one quart of cornmeal in cold water to make a thin batter
3. Slowly pour this batter into the boiling water while stirring constantly
4. Salt it and let it cook loosely for two hours, stirring frequently to prevent burning
5. When the mush is cooked, pour it onto a shallow dish no thicker than two inches
6. The next day or when cooled, cut it into slices
7. Dip them in beaten egg and coat in bread crumbs or flour
8. Fry in hot lard
9. Serve with syrup

title: Smaženec (Friture)
body:
## Ingredients

- six yolks
- two teaspoons of sugar
- four full tablespoons of flour
- salt
- one cup of good milk
- egg whites
- two tablespoons of butter

## Directions

1. Put six yolks, two teaspoons of sugar, four full tablespoons of flour, a little salt into a cup.
2. Add one cup of good milk and mix well.
3. Beat the egg whites into snow and fold gently into the batter.
4. Heat two tablespoons of butter on a shallow pan, pour the batter into it and fry until it gets a nice crispy bottom, then carefully turn it, mash with a spoon, sprinkle with sugar and put on the table.

title: Smaženec Krupičný (Farina Friture)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- two tablespoons of sugar
- a piece of fresh butter
- salt
- one cup of nice semolina
- six whole eggs

## Directions

1. In one quart of milk boil two tablespoons of sugar, add a piece of fresh butter, salt it a little and cook one cup of nice semolina into it, stir frequently and let it become very thick.
2. Remove the porridge onto a plate and when it is half cooled, mix into it six whole eggs and a piece of fresh butter.
3. Heat three tablespoons of butter on a pan, put the porridge into it, cover the pan with a lid and bake until it gets a nice crispy bottom, then turn it and bake well, mash with a spoon, smooth onto a plate, sprinkle with sugar and bring to the table.

title: Smaženec Rýžový (Rice Friture)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of nice rice
- one quart of good milk
- two tablespoons of sugar
- salt
- four eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Rinse about one cup of nice rice several times in hot water, cook it in one quart of good milk, add two tablespoons of sugar, salt it a little and cook until soft.
2. Remove the porridge onto a plate, let it cool and mix it with four eggs and a piece of butter.
3. Heat four tablespoons of butter in a pan, put the mixed porridge into it and bake like the previous friture.

title: Smaženec s Jablky (Apple Friture)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve nice apples
- butter
- grated bread or rolls
- powdered sugar
- raisins
- white wine

## Directions

1. Peel twelve nice apples and slice them thinly.
2. Grease a pan with butter and sprinkle with grated bread.
3. Put a layer of sliced apples about a finger thick, sprinkle with powdered sugar and cleaned and washed raisins, then cover with a layer of grated bread and continue alternating these layers until everything is used; the last layer must be of grated bread or rolls, which then place pieces of fresh butter on; put it in the oven and bake.
4. Meanwhile pour in little by little one cup of ordinary white wine and when it is baked, turn it onto a plate, sprinkle with sugar and bring to the table.

title: Smaženec Žemličkový (Crumb Friture)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices from white bread or rolls
- three whole eggs
- two cups of milk
- salt
- one and a half tablespoons of butter
- powdered sugar
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Slice a piece of white bread or rolls into thin slices.
2. Beat three whole eggs in two cups of milk, add a little salt and pour this over the sliced bread and let it stand for a while until it softens.
3. Heat one and a half tablespoons of butter on a pan, put the soaked bread into it and fry until it has a nice golden crispy bottom, mash it with a spoon and fry again.
4. When ready to serve, sprinkle generously with powdered sugar and powdered cinnamon.

title: Smaženky (Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one cup milk
- half teaspoon salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs, or beat the whites and yolks separately.
2. Add one cup of milk, half teaspoon of salt, and enough flour to make the batter.

title: Smaženky (Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one cup milk
- half a teaspoon of salt
- flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- lard

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs, or beat the whites and yolks separately.
2. Add to them one cup of milk, half a teaspoon of salt, and enough flour to make a thin batter, thinner than for pancakes, so that it flows from a spoon.
3. Beat until smooth.
4. Prepare a deep pan and heat good lard in it.
5. Add two teaspoons of baking powder to the batter and mix well.
6. Drop spoonfuls of this batter into the hot lard.
7. When browned on one side, turn over, then remove with a skimmer and place hot on the table.
8. Do not pierce them with a fork, as the steam would escape and they would become heavier.

title: Smaženky s Jablky (Apple Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- batter

## Directions

1. Peel apples, remove the core, and slice them into not too thin slices so they do not fall apart.
2. Make batter as described in the previous recipe.
3. Dip the apple slices in it and fry on both sides.
4. In the same way, prepare fritters from dried plums, which you have previously cooked and removed the pits from.

title: Smažená cibule (Fried Onion)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon
- four medium onions
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into cubes and render it
2. When the bacon is rendered, remove the cracklings and into the hot fat cut four medium onions very thinly
3. Cover, let stew for 15 minutes and stir frequently
4. Season with pepper and salt as needed

title: Smažená cibule (Fried Onion)
body:
## Ingredients

- slanina
- cibule - čtyry prostřední

## Directions

1. Rozkrájej slaninu na kostky a dej škvařit
2. Když slanina zhnedle, vylej škvarky
3. Do škvařícího sádla pokrájej tenounce čtyry prostřední cibule
4. Přiklop a nech dusit 15 minut
5. Během dušení často zamíchej
6. Opepři a dle potřeby osol

title: Smažená Granátová Dýně (Fried Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggplant
- Salt water
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs or cracker crumbs
- Lard

## Directions

1. Peel the eggplant, cut it into slices half an inch thick, and let them soak for an hour in salted water.
2. Then dry them, dip the slices in beaten egg, coat in breadcrumbs or rolled crackers and fry in lard until soft.

title: Smažená Granátová Dýně na Jiný Způsob (Fried Eggplant, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggplant
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard

## Directions

1. Cook the eggplant until soft, cut it open and scrape out the flesh from inside.
2. Salt and pepper it; take some of it on a spoon, coat with beaten egg and roll in breadcrumbs and fry in hot lard.

title: Smažená nadívaná vejce (Fried Stuffed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- egg yolks
- green parsley (chopped)
- sardeles (cut into pieces)
- sour cream
- bread crumbs
- salt
- egg (beaten)
- flour-mixed bread crumbs
- butter (for frying)
- flour (pale, thin sauce)
- pea water or broth
- sardeles (cut into pieces)
- green parsley or chives
- lemon juice
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard and cut a piece from one side of each so the egg can stand on a plate
2. Cut a larger piece from the other end to form a lid
3. Remove yolks and crush in a mortar
4. Cream a piece of butter and add the crushed yolks
5. Add chopped green parsley, sardeles cut into pieces, several spoons of sour cream, a little grated bread crumbs, salt and mix well
6. Fill the egg whites with this mixture
7. Press the lid on
8. Dip in beaten egg
9. Coat in bread crumbs mixed with flour
10. Fry in heated butter and arrange on a hot plate
11. Make a pale thin sauce, dilute with a little pea water or broth
12. Add sardeles cut into pieces and a little green parsley or chives to the sauce
13. Season with lemon juice
14. Add several spoons of sour cream
15. Cook everything together
16. Pour the sauce over the fried eggs and serve

title: Smažená Rajská Jablíčka (Fried Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small tomatoes
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Eggs
- Butter
- Flour
- Crackers
- Green parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel small tomatoes as noted in the previous recipe, cut them into thicker cakes, salt, pepper, season with lemon juice, dip them in thin batter made from eggs, butter and flour and fry in hot butter.
2. Or dip the prepared cakes in beaten eggs, coat in rolled crackers and fry in hot fat; you can also mix a little chopped fresh parsley into the eggs.
3. They are served either alone or as a garnish to various roasts.

title: Smažená sekanina (Fried Sausage Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound ground pork
- 3 eggs
- ground pepper
- finely chopped onion
- salt
- 4 slices grated white bread

## Directions

1. Put one pound of ground pork in a deep bowl
2. Beat in 3 whole eggs
3. Add a bit of ground pepper
4. Add a bit of finely chopped onion
5. Salt it
6. Add 4 slices of grated white bread
7. Mix it well
8. Form the mixture into small patties
9. Fry them quickly in lard

title: Smažená Sekanina (Fried Sausage Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound ground pork
- Three eggs
- Pepper
- Onion
- Salt
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard

## Directions

1. Place one pound ground pork in a deep bowl
2. Add three whole eggs
3. Add ground pepper, finely chopped onion, salt
4. Add four slices of grated white bread
5. Mix well
6. Shape into small patties
7. Fry quickly in lard

title: Smažená vejce (Fried Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons lard
- one tablespoon good butter
- eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a clean and smooth saucepan and melt three tablespoons of lard with one tablespoon of good butter
2. When the fat sizzles, break one egg after another into it
3. If you are not sure that all eggs are fresh, do well to break each one into a cup and only then put it in the saucepan
4. This will protect you from spoiled eggs getting in with the others
5. As the eggs fry, continuously pour the sizzling fat over the surface so that the upper part of the egg is also fried, without loss of neatness, as happens when the egg is turned
6. When the eggs are fried to your liking, remove them with a spoon onto a plate
7. Usually they are made with fried ham, which is fried at the same time in thin slices, which are arranged with the eggs and thus served

title: Smažená Vepřová Svíčka (Fried Pork Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloins
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Sage powder
- Lard

## Directions

1. Boil pork tenderloins thoroughly
2. Remove sinews and veins
3. Cut into 4 pieces by slicing lengthwise and then crosswise
4. Pound well with a wooden mallet until nicely flattened
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Dust with flour
7. Dip in beaten egg
8. Coat in breadcrumbs mixed with sage powder
9. Fry in hot lard

title: Smažená ztracená vejce (Fried Poached Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs or crushed crackers
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook eggs using the previous poaching method
2. Let them cool
3. Coat with beaten egg
4. Sprinkle with grated bread or crushed crackers
5. Fry quickly in heated butter

title: Smažená želva (Fried Turtle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- killed turtle
- salt
- water
- flour
- wine
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the killed turtle thoroughly
2. Boil in salted water for fifteen minutes
3. Peel the turtle, removing the skin
4. Eviscerate it
5. Cut the meat into pieces
6. Coat in flour and wine
7. Fry in butter

title: Smažené houby (Fried Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- larger mushroom caps
- salt
- pepper
- egg, beaten
- breadcrumbs, grated
- melted butter
- fresh parsley, chopped (optional)
- caraway seeds (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the caps of larger mushrooms, slice them into thicker pieces, salt, pepper them, dip them in beaten egg, coat them in grated breadcrumbs and fry them to a golden color in melted butter.
2. You can also mix a little chopped fresh parsley or a little picked and crushed caraway seeds into the eggs.

title: Smažené housky (Fried Rolls)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eight rolls
- hard egg yolks
- preserved fruit
- sliced almonds
- lemon
- lemon juice
- sugar
- three and one-half deciliters red wine
- cherry juice
- cinnamon
- cloves
- lemon zest
- one large spoon cooked raisin

## Directions

1. Grate eight rolls; from other rolls cut out a piece, hollow out the center, dry, pound in mortar, and sift.
2. To this add four hard egg yolks, one heaping spoon of preserved fruit, two spoons chopped almonds, lemon and lemon juice, and sugar as needed.
3. Fill the hollowed rolls with this stuffing, cover with a lid dipped in egg white, and place on a baking sheet.
4. Meanwhile boil three and one-half deciliters red wine with a little cherry juice, a piece of cinnamon, several cloves, a piece of lemon zest, one large spoon cooked raisin, and a piece of sugar; strain over the rolls.
5. Bake the rolls for a while, then arrange on a serving dish, place a piece of preserved fruit on each roll and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Smažené jehněčí (Fried Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb neck, shoulder and breast
- salt
- ground pepper
- flour
- egg
- bread crumbs
- clarified butter
- parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb neck, shoulder and breast into equally large neat pieces
2. Salt them and sprinkle lightly with ground pepper
3. Sprinkle lightly with flour
4. Dip in beaten egg
5. Roll in bread crumbs
6. Fry in clarified butter until golden
7. Sprinkle lightly with ground salt
8. Arrange on a platter and surround with parsley sprigs

title: Smažené kluzký krupičné (Fried Farina Quennelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Farina, 2 koflíky
- Lard, 2 tablespoons
- Salt
- Water, boiling
- Eggs, 2 whole
- Butter for frying

## Directions

1. Place 2 koflíky farina in a bowl
2. Heat 2 tablespoons lard in a pan and pour it over the farina
3. Add salt and pour enough boiling water so that when mixed well it becomes like soft dough
4. Let cool
5. Add 2 whole eggs and mix well
6. Dip a spoon in water and shape large quennelles from the dough
7. Drop into boiling salted water
8. When cooked, drain them well
9. Fry them in butter until brown
10. Serve hot at the table

title: Smažené Koroptve (Fried Partridge)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young partridges
- melted butter
- grated bread roll
- egg, beaten
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the young partridges and halve them.
2. Dip in melted butter, roll in grated bread roll.
3. Dip in beaten egg and roll in bread roll again.
4. Quickly fry in hot butter until golden.
5. Serve with fried parsley as garnish or with vegetables on the table.

title: Smažené Kuře (Fried Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens, one to one and a half pounds each
- salt
- flour
- egg
- fresh parsley
- breadcrumbs or bread
- crackers
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Use young chickens weighing about one to one and a half pounds each
2. Cut into quarters or joint pieces
3. Salt them
4. Coat in flour, dip in beaten egg mixed with finely chopped fresh parsley
5. Coat with breadcrumbs or bread, or if desired, crushed crackers
6. Fry until golden in hot butter or lard
7. For larger chickens, coat only in flour since larger chickens do not fry through well with breadcrumb coating

title: Smažené ledvinky (Fried Kidneys)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- veal, pork, or lamb kidneys
- butter
- red wine
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- lemon

## Directions

1. Wash the kidneys, salt them, and braise in butter with red wine until tender.
2. Let them cool, cut not too thinly, dip in egg, coat in breadcrumbs, and fry on both sides until brown.
3. Serve with lemon cut into quarters and can be served right after the soup course.

title: Smažené Mušle (Fried Mussels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- water
- salt
- onion
- egg yolks
- flour
- pepper
- butter
- lemon juice or lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and soak the mussels, place in a pot with cold water, salt, and several onions.
2. Boil over high heat.
3. When the shells open, remove the mussels and let the water drain.
4. Clean the animals and remove from the shells.
5. Coat them in a batter made from egg yolks, flour, salt and pepper.
6. Fry on butter, add lemon juice or fresh lemon slices.
7. If the onion that was added turns black, it is best not to eat the mussels, as it may indicate the presence of something harmful.

title: Smažené měkké mušle (Fried Soft Clams)
body:
## Ingredients

- large soft clams
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- egg, beaten
- bread crumbs, finely grated
- lard, heated
- lemon
- parsley, fried

## Directions

1. Remove clams from shells
2. Remove the hard muscles from the clams
3. Let drain on cloth
4. Salt and pepper the clams
5. Dust with flour
6. Dip in beaten egg
7. Roll in finely grated bread crumbs
8. Fry in heated lard until golden
9. Arrange on dish
10. Garnish with sliced lemon and fried parsley

title: Smažené nudle (Fried Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- butter

## Directions

1. Make noodles in ordinary way
2. Let dry
3. Fry in boiling butter until golden
4. Add to soup

title: Smažené okurky (Fried Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- flour
- melted fat

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers, slice them into thicker slices, remove the cores with a special tool, salt them
2. Let them stand for a quarter hour, then drain, dry, coat in flour and fry in hot melted fat

title: Smažené rybí řízky (Fried Fish Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh fish fillets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- Flour or thin batter
- Lard for frying
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Cut fillets from any fresh fish
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Dip in thin batter made from eggs
4. Fry in hot lard
5. Arrange on a platter and garnish with fried parsley and lemon slices

title: Smažené salsify (Fried Salsify)
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify roots
- butter

## Directions

1. Scrape the roots, cut lengthwise and fry in butter on both sides

title: Smažené skopové kotletky (Breaded Mutton Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- young mutton ribs
- salt
- pepper
- butter or lard
- breadcrumbs
- sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare cutlets from young ribs with bone attached as described previously
2. Salt the cutlets
3. Dust lightly with pepper
4. Dip in melted butter or lard
5. Coat in breadcrumbs
6. Fry in hot lard until golden
7. Arrange on a heated dish
8. Pour sauce over them or serve sauce separately on the side

title: Smažené Telecí Brzlíky (Fried Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- salt
- warm water
- 2 eggs
- piece of heated butter
- grated roll or bread
- fresh butter for frying
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the veal sweetbreads well and place them in lukewarm water for a quarter hour to blanch them
2. Put them in boiling salted water and boil for five minutes to firm them up
3. Place them again in cold water and let them cool
4. Split each one lengthwise in half
5. Season both sides with salt
6. Beat two eggs with a piece of heated butter
7. Dip the sweetbreads in this mixture and sprinkle both sides with grated roll or bread
8. Melt fresh butter on the stove and fry the sweetbreads on it until brown, not too quickly
9. Arrange them on a dish and sprinkle with lemon juice
10. Serve; you can add carrots, spinach, or other vegetables

title: Smažené telecí hrudí (Fried Veal Breast)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients
