3. Cook for half an hour

title: Třešňový kompot (Cherry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries (one pound)
- Sugar (one quarter pound)
- Water (one small cup)
- A piece of whole cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Red wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook one quarter pound sugar with one small cup of water, a piece of whole cinnamon and lemon peel
2. Add cherries with stems removed and washed in cold water
3. Let them cook on high heat and then cool
4. Remove the cherries with a slotted spoon to a dish
5. Let the juice cook a little longer and pour it over the cherries
6. You can also add a little red wine to the sugar
7. Prepare compote from sour cherries similarly

title: Třešňový kompot (Sweet Cherry Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram ripe cherries
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) sugar dissolved in water
- lemon juice
- cinnamon
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pour 9 dekagrams sugar dissolved in water, or mixed with lemon juice, cinnamon and lemon peel, over half a kilogram of ripe cherries
2. Shake several times and boil for half an hour

title: Třešňový Pudding (Cherry Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- three rolls
- boiling milk
- three tablespoons melted fresh butter
- finely chopped lemon zest
- three tablespoons fine sugar
- two tablespoons ground almonds
- six egg yolks
- one pound cherries
- wine
- sugar
- six egg whites
- butter
- grated rolls
- cherry sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the crumb of three rolls in boiling milk, place in a mortar with three tablespoons melted fresh butter and stir until it becomes smooth dough.
2. Set aside, add a bit of finely chopped lemon zest, three tablespoons fine sugar, and let it cool.
3. Then add two tablespoons ground almonds, six egg yolks, and mix well together.
4. Take one pound of cherries, remove the pits, place the cherries in a mortar with a little wine and sugar, let them cook briefly, and place on a sieve so the juice drains, as otherwise the pudding would be heavy.
5. Add the cherries to the remaining mixture, mix carefully, and fold in snow made from six egg whites.
6. Grease a mold with butter, sprinkle with grated rolls, and let the pudding steam cook for three-quarters of an hour.
7. Remove onto a dish and pour cherry sauce over it.

title: Třešňový rosol (Cherry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe cherries with pits
- Currant juice, one-fourth part

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from ripe cherries, crush them with the pits, let them stand overnight.
2. Then press them, add one-fourth part currant juice, strain once more.
3. Then cook as with grape jelly.

title: Uherské jelito (Hungarian Intestines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams rendered pork lard
- 4 onions, finely chopped
- 250 grams internal pork fat, diced
- 250 grams breadcrumbs, diced
- Cream
- 1 liter pork blood
- Half liter cream
- Marjoram
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Ginger
- Bay leaf
- Nutmeg
- Intestines

## Directions

1. Heat the rendered pork lard and fry the finely chopped onions in it
2. Add the diced internal fat and the breadcrumbs softened with cream
3. Mix everything with 1 liter pork blood and half liter cream
4. Season with marjoram, pepper, cloves, ginger, bay leaf, and nutmeg
5. Mix well and stuff into intestines
6. Boil for quarter hour
7. Bake the intestines
8. Serve with grated radish

title: Uherské zelí (Hungarian Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- zelí (cabbage)
- voda nebo vepřová polévka (water or pork broth)
- jíška (roux)
- sůl (salt)
- pepř (pepper)
- šafran (saffron)

## Directions

1. Zelí se uvaří buď ve vodě neb ve vepřové polévce, zapraží se jíškou, osolí, opepří a ošafraní, pozvolna se dusí

title: Uherské zelí - Jiný způsob (Hungarian Cabbage - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- zelí (cabbage)
- sádlo (lard)
- mouka (flour)
- vepřová polévka (pork broth)
- smetana dobrá (good sour cream)

## Directions

1. Zelí se sádlem se až do žlutá udusí, moukou posype, vepřovou polévkou zaleje a pozvolna vaří, až úplně změkne
2. Tu a tam přidávají i trochu dobré smetany, vše se dokonale dohromady svaří

title: Uherský dort (Hungarian Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram pure butter
- four whole eggs
- eight egg yolks
- 375 grams sugar
- finely grated lemon zest
- 250 grams potato flour
- 250 grams fine wheat flour
- stiff snow made from eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat half kilogram of pure butter until creamy
2. Gradually add four whole eggs, eight egg yolks, 375 grams sugar and finely grated lemon zest
3. Mix to one side for at least three quarters of an hour
4. Mix with 250 grams potato flour and 250 grams fine wheat flour
5. Finally add stiff snow made from eight egg whites
6. Bake the dough slowly in the form of cakes
7. Turn these onto a sieve, and when cool
8. Layer them on top of each other
9. Between the cake layers spread optional preserves and cover everything with glaze

title: Uherský guláš (Hungarian Gulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef meat, cut into pieces
- mutton meat, cut into pieces
- pork meat, cut into pieces
- bacon
- onion, chopped
- cumin
- pepper
- flour
- beef broth or water
- salt
- paprika
- several grains of allspice
- wine (optional)
- boiled or fried potatoes
- potatoes, peeled and chopped (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut beef, mutton, and pork meat into pieces.
2. Brown the chopped onion in bacon fat, add a little cumin and pepper.
3. Add the beef meat and let it braise while stirring frequently.
4. After a while, add the mutton meat and finally the pork.
5. Dust with flour, add beef broth or water, salt, paprika, several grains of allspice, and a little wine if desired.
6. Let it braise until everything is sufficiently tender.
7. Place the gulash in a deep platter and serve with boiled or fried potatoes.
8. Alternatively, peeled and chopped potatoes can be cooked with the meat.
9. For six people, 2-3 pounds of all meats combined is sufficient.

title: Unripe Melons (Nevyzrálé melony)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- developed but not fully ripe melons
- soft water
- alum
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut developed but not fully ripe melons into oblong pieces
2. Clean and soak in soft water in which a small amount of alum has been dissolved
3. Cook the melon slices until soft, then place in fresh cold water
4. Remove to a dish, layer them and pour filtered sugar over them
5. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it stronger, and preserve it with the melons
6. On the third day, cook the sugar thicker again, add the melons and cook together
7. Repeat this until the fifth day in the same manner
8. Skim off the foam that forms, place completely candied melons in clean jars, cover with sugar syrup and bind

title: Upravování Čaje (Tea Preparation)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Tea leaves
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Rinse the container in which the tea is to be steeped with boiling water to heat it.
2. Pour out the water and add the necessary amount of tea to the container.

title: Uschování čerstvých hub (Preservation of Fresh Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh young mushrooms
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean fresh, young mushrooms, leave small ones whole, cut larger ones into thick slices
2. Boil in salted water, remove with a skimmer and put into tin boxes, shake well
3. Pour salted water over the mushrooms until all are submerged, do not fill the boxes completely
4. Close well and seal with pitch or wax
5. Let cool, later they can be fried in butter like fresh ones

title: Uschování čerstvých hub v másle (Preservation of Fresh Mushrooms in Butter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean mushrooms, let them dry on a sieve
2. Stew on hot butter until the absorbed butter flows out clear again
3. Salt well, shake, pour hot into stone jars
4. The next day pour melted butter over them and seal with cloth

title: Usmažení Cibule (Fried Onion)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- butter or bacon
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the onion, cut into small fine cubes and place in lightly browned butter or bacon, stir constantly until completely reddened
2. Use for flavoring many dishes

title: Usušené zralé a zelené broskve (Dried Ripe and Green Peaches)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches preserved without apple pulp
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Place preserved peaches on a dish and dry them.
2. Place them on a fine sieve so the sugar drains.
3. Sprinkle them finely with ground sugar.
4. When the peaches are coated on all sides, dust them off and pack between paper into boxes.

title: Uzenice (Bologna Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 parts beef meat
- 1 part pork meat
- salt
- pepper
- fresh intestines

## Directions

1. Take two parts beef meat and one part pork meat and chop finely or grind well through a mill
2. Season well with salt and pepper and mix thoroughly
3. Fill into fresh intestines
4. On the second day, smoke it and smoke for two days
5. When ready to use, bake for half an hour in a water bath

title: Uzeničky (Smoked Meat Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 5 eggs
- salt
- smoked meat broth

## Directions

1. Salt flour and add eggs
2. Make dough like for noodles
3. When rolled-out dough is dry, cut into square pieces
4. Boil in salted water
5. Drain with cold water so they do not stick together
6. Alternatively, pieces of dough may be boiled in the broth in which smoked meat was cooked
7. Finely chop the meat
8. Mix with dough
9. Add 4 eggs beaten with milk and butter
10. Add piece of sugar or pepper
11. Salt

title: Uzeničky (Smoked Meat Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- eggs
- water
- smoked meat
- milk
- butter
- sugar or pepper

## Directions

1. Salt the flour, add eggs and make dough like for noodles
2. When the rolled-out dough has dried, cut it into square pieces
3. When salted water is boiling, cook the cut pieces in it, then rinse with cold water so they do not stick together
4. Alternatively, the cut dough pieces can be cooked in the broth in which smoked meat was boiled
5. Finely chop the smoked meat and mix with the dough, add 4 eggs mixed with milk and butter, and either a piece of sugar or pepper, salt it, crumble 6 dekagrams (4 lots) of butter into it, mix well, spread on a greased and breadcrumb-sprinkled baking sheet, on which it is placed on the table or turned out

title: Uzeničky (Smoked Meat Noodles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Half kilogram smoked meat, bones removed
- 8 eggs
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- 3 eggs
- 150 milliliters good milk or cream
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs, grated

## Directions

1. Finely chop the smoked meat after removing bones
2. Beat 8 eggs and mix with flour to make a dough like noodles
3. Salt and allow to dry
4. Cut into square pieces
5. Boil in salted water, drain, and rinse with cold water so they do not stick
6. Place on a baking pan with the chopped meat
7. Add 3 beaten eggs, 150 milliliters milk or cream, and a little pepper
8. Mix everything together
9. Grease a baking form and sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs
10. Pour the mixture into the form
11. Dot with butter on top
12. Bake in the oven until golden

title: Uzeničky zemákové (Smoked Meat with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Half kilogram cooked smoked meat
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 4-5 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes, peel, and cut into slices when cooled
2. Chop the cooked smoked meat
3. Grease a baking pan with butter
4. Place the mixture of smoked meat and potatoes on the pan
5. Salt and pepper
6. Beat 4-5 eggs and pour over the mixture
7. Crumble butter on top
8. Bake in the oven
9. Serve on the table in the baking pan wrapped with a cloth

title: Uzenková Nádivka (Sausage Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sausage meat
- grated bread roll
- marjoram
- thyme

## Directions

1. Mix sausage meat with grated bread roll
2. Add marjoram and thyme
3. Use for stuffing turkey poultry

title: Uzená Drůbež (Smoked Fowl)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young fat geese
- Salt
- Saltpeter (salpeter)
- Finely chopped lemon zest
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Juniper berries
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Kill, pluck and wash young fat geese, then cut off the neck and wings close to the body.
2. Split the geese lengthwise along the back, carefully remove the innards and bones.
3. Rub the inside and outside thoroughly with salt mixed with saltpeter and finely chopped lemon zest.
4. Fold both halves together so the inner meat adheres, making it appear whole.
5. Place on a platter, cover with a board and tie tightly.
6. Let rest for eight days, turning daily and basting with the liquid that runs out.
7. Remove, dry somewhat, sprinkle inside with pepper and cloves, fold the halves together again, wrap in paper and smoke for 8 to 10 days over gentle heat.
8. Similarly prepare ducks, capons, poulardes, young hens, partridges and pheasants: pluck well, split lengthwise along the back, remove innards and carefully separate all bones from meat, cut off head and wings.
9. Rub with salt and let rest for 2 days.
10. Dry and rub coarsely with ground pepper, juniper berries, cloves and a little nutmeg.
11. Sew up the rear, sew the prepared fowl into coarse linen and let rest under weight for several hours.
12. Smoke for 8 days over juniper wood smoke.

title: Uzené maso (Smoked Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Salt
- Saltpeter
- Pepper
- Bay leaves
- Juniper
- Spices

## Directions

1. Hang the meat in a chimney into which smoke from incompletely burned wood flows.
2. Meat intended for smoking is usually cured, which helps it last longer and gain better flavor.
3. The flavor of meat depends entirely on the curing process.
4. Usually the meat is salted and rubbed with saltpeter, which makes it red.
5. Along the bone, make a cut and insert crushed pepper, and between individual layers place bay leaves, juniper, and spices.
6. Polinate the meat daily with the liquid that forms, or fill a container with salt solution, then no pouring is necessary.
7. Besides pork, eels, geese, and tongues are smoked.

title: Uzené Maso (Smoked Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked meat
- Horseradish
- Rye bread
- Dumplings with cabbage or cooked peas with pickled cucumbers (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil smoked meat in several changes of water until tender
2. Slice into neat pieces
3. Arrange on a heated dish
4. Serve with grated horseradish and rye bread, or with dumplings and cabbage, or cooked peas with pickled cucumbers

title: Uzené Maso s Rýží (Smoked Pork with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound cooked smoked meat
- Half pound washed rice
- Butter
- Beef broth
- Fresh or dried mushrooms
- Flour

## Directions

1. Chop one pound cooked smoked meat into small pieces
2. Cook half pound washed rice in broth strained from the meat
3. Grease a mold with butter
4. Layer rice, then smoked meat, then rice again, alternating
5. Place rice on top, add pieces of butter
6. Pour with a spoon of beef broth
7. Bake in an oven for half an hour
8. If fresh mushrooms are available, stew them in butter, add to the mixture and sprinkle with flour; if using dried mushrooms, boil them in beef broth, chop and add to the smoked meat

title: Uzersky (Sausages)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat
- Beef meat
- All spices
- Intestines

## Directions

1. Chop a portion of pork and a portion of beef meat
2. Mix in all spices
3. Stuff into intestines
4. For home smoking: place sticks in a barrel, pile up oak shavings and twigs and light them so they only smolder, cover the barrel and let smoke for half an hour

title: V pivě vaření raci (Crayfish in Beer)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Sweet beer
- Salt
- Caraway
- Parsley
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Place cleaned crayfish in a vessel filled with sweet beer.
2. The beer must be salted with the addition of caraway, parsley, and pepper.
3. The crayfish cook quickly in beer.

title: V polévce vařeni raci (Crayfish in Broth)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water
- Salt
- Caraway
- Parsley
- Strong broth
- Grated bread roll
- Butter
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Throw cleaned crayfish into boiling salted water to which caraway and parsley have been added.
2. Simmer for ten to twelve minutes depending on size, then remove meat from tails and claws.
3. Cover with strong broth and mix with grated bread roll, crayfish butter, and lemon slices.
4. Simmer everything once more gently and slowly, then serve immediately.

title: V Páře Dělaný Creme, Anglický (Steamed Cream, English)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good cream
- vanilla, orange, tea, roasted coffee or similar
- four whole eggs
- eight egg yolks
- 20 to 28 decagrams sugar
- salt
- flavored cream

## Directions

1. Steep vanilla, orange, tea, roasted coffee and similar items in good cream, then strain
2. Let cool
3. Add four whole eggs and eight egg yolks
4. Add 20 to 28 decagrams of sugar and salt
5. Mix thoroughly
6. Pour the prepared liquid into containers placed in a larger pan of water and steam
7. Top with whipped flavored cream

title: Vaječná omáčka (Egg Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three spoons of butter
- two spoons of flour
- salt
- ground pepper
- nutmeg
- one pint of boiling water
- two egg yolks
- one quarter pound of butter
- juice of one lemon
- two finely chopped hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Mix three spoons of butter with two spoons of flour
2. Season with salt, ground pepper and nutmeg
3. Add one pint of boiling water and beat well with a whisk to make it smooth
4. Allow to simmer a little
5. Remove from heat and stir in two egg yolks, one quarter pound of butter and juice of one lemon
6. Strain through a fine sieve or cloth
7. Finally, add two finely chopped hard-boiled eggs

title: Vaječná polévka (Egg Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour (for roux)
- butter
- soup
- four eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- roasted roll

## Directions

1. Make a light roux and let it cool
2. Pour soup over it in which four eggs have been beaten
3. Season with salt and nutmeg, pour over a roasted roll

title: Vaječná polévka (Egg Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- 35 grams (2 lots) butter
- 3 spoonfuls grated roll
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat together eggs, butter, grated roll, salt and nutmeg
2. Slowly pour the mixture into the soup and boil it

title: Vandličky z masa (Meat Patties)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram beef topside
- ½ kilogram calf meat
- ½ kilogram ham
- 3 eggs
- butter
- 2 tablespoons chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the beef, calf meat and ham, mix with 3 eggs, butter and 2 tablespoons chopped parsley
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Place in greased molds and bake slowly, serve with various vegetables

title: Vanilková omáčka (Vanilla Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter cream or milk
- two eggs
- sugar
- several drops of vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs with 1/4 liter of cream or milk
2. Add sugar and several drops of vanilla essence
3. Place the mixture in a bowl on warm stove or in a vessel filled with hot water
4. Stir often and boil slowly until it thickens

title: Vanilková omáčka (Vanilla Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- about 15 dekagrams sugar
- 3 egg whites
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar to the fourth degree
2. While constantly stirring, pour gradually into stiff snow from 3 eggs, to which vanilla has been beaten and added

title: Vanilková omáčka vinná (Wine Vanilla Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla bean
- water
- half liter white wine
- potato flour
- sugar
- four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Finely chop the vanilla and dissolve it in a little water
2. Let it stand for at least an hour on a warm place, well covered
3. Add half a liter of white wine
4. Add potato flour and sugar to taste
5. Add four beaten egg yolks, mix everything well
6. Heat slowly and preserve it, stirring constantly

title: Vanilková Paštička (Vanilla Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Dry vanilla, finely powder it and cook in a little water
2. Strain through clean cloth
3. Add plenty of sugar and cook together until sugar forms a grain
4. Proceed as with the previous paste

title: Vanilkové Mandle (Vanilla Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine large almonds
- refined sugar
- vanilla, finely crushed
- water

## Directions

1. Clean fine large almonds
2. Boil in soft water
3. Remove the skin and dry on a sieve until completely dry
4. Dissolve pure refined sugar in water
5. As described for white sugared almonds, pour warm sugar onto the almonds and stir
6. When partially coated with sugar, add a little finely crushed vanilla mixed with boiled sugar
7. Continue until almonds are completely coated with sugar

title: Vanilkové pokroutky (Vanilla Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla
- soft boiling water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pound vanilla finely and sieve it
2. Place it in a closed pot
3. Add enough hot soft water according to the amount of vanilla
4. Seal the pot tightly and let it sit overnight
5. In the morning strain the contents and press out the vanilla completely
6. Boil the juice with sugar until thickened
7. Pour onto paper, spread it out and when hardened and dried, carefully peel off and cut into pieces of any size

title: Vanilkové preclíčky (Vanilla Pretzels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 250 grams sugar
- vanilla
- 375 grams fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat eggs with sugar
2. Add crushed vanilla
3. Gradually add flour and mix until well-worked dough forms
4. Shape into small pretzels
5. Brush with beaten egg yolks
6. Place on buttered baking sheet
7. Bake slowly
8. Sprinkle with sugar
9. Serve

title: Vanilkový Creme (Vanilla Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three and one-half deciliters cream
- vanilla
- seven decagrams sugar
- six to eight egg yolks or one tablespoon flour and five egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil three and one-half deciliters of cream with vanilla
2. Let cool and strain
3. Add seven decagrams of sugar and six to eight egg yolks or one tablespoon flour and five egg yolks
4. Beat on warm stove

title: Vanilkový Dort (Vanilla Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram butter
- 16 to 18 egg yolks
- half kilogram ground sugar
- ground vanilla
- half kilogram fine flour
- salt
- firm snow from egg whites

## Directions

1. Take half kilogram butter and beat it thoroughly
2. Add 16 to 18 egg yolks, half kilogram ground sugar, and ground vanilla
3. Add half kilogram fine flour and salt
4. Mix with firm snow from egg whites
5. Bake in cake forms
6. Fill with vanilla cream and coat with vanilla glaze

title: Vanilla Ice Cream (Vanilková zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- One-eighth kilo of sugar
- Finely crushed vanilla
- One liter of sweet sour cream

## Directions

1. Finely crush one-eighth kilo of sugar
2. Add finely crushed vanilla
3. Pour in one liter of sweet sour cream
4. Beat the sour cream until foamy
5. Collect the foam and strain through a sieve
6. Continue beating the remaining sour cream until all becomes foamy and transforms into snow
7. Place the snow in a container with ice
8. It will be frozen within an hour
9. Unmold the ice cream by first placing it in warm water and then immediately turning the mold onto a plate

title: Vanilla Liqueur (Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Vanilla
- 3 liters good spirits (kořalka)
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Crush or finely chop the vanilla and place it in a bottle with about 3 liters of good spirits
2. Seal the bottle well and leave it in a warm place for at least two weeks
3. Shake and stir daily
4. Add sugar to taste and let it sit for several more days
5. Strain and it is ready to drink

title: Vanilla Suchar (Vanilla Rusk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams sugar
- egg yolks from 18 eggs
- egg white from 6 eggs
- a little vanilla
- 750 grams flour

## Directions

1. Mix 500 grams sugar with the egg yolk from 18 eggs, egg white from 6 eggs, a little vanilla, and 750 grams flour
2. Spread the dough thinly on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper
3. Brush with a beaten egg yolk and bake quickly until golden
4. Once cooled, cut into pieces and sprinkle with sugar and vanilla

title: Vařená bílá poleva (Boiled Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 small cups fine sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 2 egg whites
- vanilla or lemon juice to taste

## Directions

1. Cook 1 1/2 small cups of fine sugar with 2 tablespoons of water until it threads
2. Meanwhile beat the whites of 2 eggs and fold the hot sugar into them, stirring until it cools
3. Then add vanilla or lemon juice to taste

title: Vařená bělice (Boiled White Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- white fish
- salt
- lemon juice
- water
- onion
- green parsley
- parsley butter

## Directions

1. Clean the white fish and let it boil in salted water seasoned with lemon juice with sliced onion and a bunch of green parsley.
2. Place the boiled fish on a hot dish, pour melted parsley butter over it and arrange with sliced lemon and green parsley.

title: Vařená hlávka (Boiled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- hlávka
- voda - studená a slaná
- mouka - lžíce
- cukr
- pepř

## Directions

1. Hlávku rozčtvrť a vyřezej košťály
2. Dej do studené vody a ponech v ní asi čtvrt hodiny
3. Dobře osákni na sítku a dej vařit do slané vody
4. Když se pět minut povařila, slij vodu a dej jinou vodu horkou
5. Když je hlávka měkká a voda vyvařená, udělej jíšku ze lžíce mouky
6. Přidej trochu cukru a opepři a zadělej tím hlávku

title: Vařená Husa (Boiled Goose)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt
- vegetables (carrots, celery, parsley, cabbage, cauliflower)
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Clean the goose and cut it up
2. Place in a pot, salt it, add all vegetables and cook until soft
3. Strain the broth; arrange the goose on a platter and serve with horseradish

title: Vařená lupiče (Boiled Bass)
body:
## Ingredients

- bass
- salt
- fresh butter
- lemon juice
- toasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the bass, scale it, salt it and let it lie for about an hour; if too large, cut it into pieces.
2. Let plenty of salted water boil on a pan, place the bass in it and let it boil for a quarter hour.
3. Meanwhile mix a good piece of fresh butter with lemon juice and let it heat.
4. When the bass is arranged on a dish and all the water that dripped from it has been removed from the dish, pour this butter over it and sprinkle with toasted bread crumbs.

title: Vařená maso v kořeňovém náleve (Beans in Spiced Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- water
- baking soda
- salt
- tomatoes
- onion
- butter
- salt pork
- tomato sauce
- syrup
- sugar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place the beans with water on the stove, add half a teaspoon of baking soda and a little salt and let them cook for about half an hour.
2. In a pan, put half a three-pound can of tomatoes, or 3-4 fresh ones, add one chopped onion, a tablespoon of butter, and let them stew for about half an hour.
3. Then wash well about a pound of pickled salt pork, add it to the beans and strain the tomato sauce into them.
4. Add a tablespoon of syrup and a tablespoon of sugar, salt to taste, cover well and bake slowly for two to three hours.

title: Vařená ryba (Boiled Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Any large fish with firm meat
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and cut it into pieces
2. Pour boiling salted water over it
3. Cook gently for half an hour
4. Serve with butter sauce or fish sauce

title: Vařená ryba (Boiled Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish with firm meat
- salted water

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and cut it into pieces
2. Pour boiling salted water over it and cook gently for half an hour
3. Serve with butter or fish sauce

title: Vařená ryba s omáčkou (Boiled Fish with Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Water
- Salt
- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon salt
- ½ teaspoon mustard
- Fish roe (if available)
- A little milk

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and tie it in clean cloth
2. Place in a pot and pour boiling water over it, salt with a tablespoon of salt
3. Cook for half to three-quarters of an hour
4. Finely chop the 6 hard-boiled eggs
5. Mix together the eggs, butter, pepper, salt, mustard, fish roe if available, and a little milk
6. Cook this mixture and pour it over the fish after removing it from the cloth and arranging it on a platter

title: Vařená ryba s omáčkou (Boiled Fish with Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water
- salt
- 6 hard-boiled eggs, finely chopped
- 2 spoonfuls of butter
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard
- fish roe, if available
- a little milk

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and wrap it in clean cloth
2. Place in a pot and pour boiling water over it, salt with a spoonful of salt and cook for half to three-quarters of an hour
3. Take 6 hard-boiled eggs, finely chopped, 2 spoonfuls of butter, 1 teaspoon pepper, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon mustard and fish roe if available and a little milk for the sauce
4. Cook this mixture together and pour it over the fish

title: Vařená čokoládová poleva (Boiled Chocolate Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 tablet bitter chocolate
- 1/2 small cup milk or cream
- 1/2 small cup sugar

## Directions

1. Grate 1/2 tablet of bitter chocolate
2. Cook it with 1/2 small cup of milk or cream and 1/2 small cup of sugar
3. Cook until it thickens into a porridge

title: Vařená šunka (Boiled Ham)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Cold water
- Vegetables

## Directions

1. Rinse the ham, scrub well and soak in cold water for several hours to easily remove all impurities.
2. Bring the thus prepared ham to boiling in cold water slowly, and when softened - remove from heat and let cool in its own cooked broth either completely or partially, depending on whether it is to be served hot or cold.
3. The skin is removed and the hot ham is placed on a dish - surrounded by various vegetables (spinach, lentil or pea purée, potatoes, etc.), or the cold ham is decorated in various ways and garnished with aspic.

title: Vařená šunka (Boiled Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Place a well-soaked and washed ham in a large pot, add enough water to cover it and let it cook until tender, which depending on its size may take 2 to 3½ hours.
2. If it is very salty, drain the water when it is half-cooked and pour fresh boiling water over it.
3. Turn it several times during cooking and add fresh boiling water.
4. Remove the skin while hot, sprinkle it with marjoram and ground pepper, cover it again with skin and let it cool.

title: Vařená šunka po meklenburgsku (Mecklenburg-Style Boiled Ham)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned ham
- Black bread, grated
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Lard

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned ham until tender in its own broth
2. Allow it to cool in its own cooking liquid
3. Remove the skin
4. Sprinkle the ham with grated black bread, sugar, cinnamon, and cloves
5. Grease with lard
6. Bake in the oven

title: Vařené zelí z hlávek (Boiled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- zelná hlávka
- voda
- ocet
- sůl
- kmín
- mouka
- hovězí polévka
- cukr
- sádlo nebo máslo nebo omastí

## Directions

1. Dobře obranou zelnou hlávku zbavenou vrchních zelných listů rozkrájej na čtyry kusy
2. Košťál vykroj a lupení na jemné nudličky rozkrájej
3. Dej to do hrnku, přitlač, nalej vody a octa
4. Osol a posyp kmínem
5. Nech vařit, když je skoro uvařeno, zapraž je jíškou
6. Nech dovařit, kdyby bylo příliš husté, přilej trochu hovězí polévky
7. Dle chuti přidej octa a kdyby bylo příliš kyselé, přidej kousek cukru
8. Omasti je pak buď vepřovým neb husím sádlem, aneb máslem, aneb omastkem z pečené anebo drůbeže

title: Vaření raci (Cooking Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water
- Salt
- Caraway
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Butter

## Directions

1. Rinse the crayfish thoroughly in fresh water.
2. Bring salted water to a boil, throw in the crayfish and cook quickly without much trouble in about a quarter hour.
3. Add caraway, salt, finely chopped parsley, and butter to the water.
4. Stir the crayfish frequently and let them remain in the pot a while after cooking.
5. Such prepared crayfish are very tasty.

title: Vaření Raci po Francouzsku (Crayfish Cooked French Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned crayfish
- water or wine with vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- bay leaf
- thyme
- clove
- onion, sliced

## Directions

1. Remove the intestine from the tail of cleaned crayfish
2. Place crayfish in a container filled with water mixed with vinegar or wine
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Add butter, bay leaf, thyme, clove, and sliced onion
5. Cook vigorously for about 20 minutes
6. Remove from heat and serve the crayfish either warm or chilled

title: Vaření raci po francouzsku (French Style Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water or wine
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Cloves
- Onion

## Directions

1. Remove the intestine from the cleaned crayfish.
2. Place crayfish in a vessel filled with water and vinegar or wine, season with salt and pepper, add butter, bay leaf, thyme, cloves, and chopped onion.
3. Simmer vigorously for about 20 minutes, remove from heat, and serve the crayfish warm or cold.

title: Vaření raků (Cooking Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cook cleaned crayfish in water to which vinegar, butter, salt, and pepper have been added.
2. Serve on a platter so that the claws stick out, placing fresh green parsley between the claws.
3. Since crayfish stain napkins and such stains do not come out easily, use special napkins or paper napkins.

title: Vařený hovězí jazyk s omáčkou, aneb s křenem (Boiled Beef Tongue with Sauce or Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef tongue
- Capers
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Boil the cleaned and rinsed beef tongue as you would beef, until tender
2. Remove the outer skin and slice the tongue lengthwise
3. Sprinkle with capers
4. You can serve with caper sauce, anchovy sauce, or grated horseradish

title: Vařený losos na jiný způsob (Boiled Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- hot water
- sardine or fish sauce
- lemon

## Directions

1. When choosing salmon for cooking, be careful that it is not too dry
2. Wrap the salmon in white thin cloth (cheesecloth), pour hot water over it and let it simmer gently
3. When cooked, remove the cloth, place the salmon on a hot dish and serve with sardine or fish sauce and sliced lemon

title: Vařený losos s omáčkou (Boiled Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- vinegar
- onion
- bay leaf
- salt
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- fresh butter
- parsley
- shallots
- mustard
- pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse a nice piece of salmon in cold water, place in a pan
2. Pour a little vinegar over it, add onion cut into slices, bay leaf, salt and pour water until the salmon is completely covered
3. Cover and cook until tender
4. Press four hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve, add six tablespoons of fresh heated clean butter and stir until a fine sauce forms
5. Add finely chopped parsley and several finely chopped shallots, a tablespoon of mustard, salt and season with a little ground pepper
6. Add vinegar until the sauce is thick, mix well, pour into a small bowl
7. Serve with the cooked and buttered salmon

title: Vařený okoun (Boiled Perch)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot
- parsnip
- celery
- leek
- fresh butter
- green parsley
- peppercorns
- salt
- water
- perch

## Directions

1. Cut a well-cleaned carrot, parsnip, celery and leek into small strips, place in a pot with a piece of fresh butter, add green parsley, several peppercorns, salt it and let it fry for a while.
2. Then pour a quart of water over it and let it cook for a while.
3. Meanwhile scale and gut the perch, salt them and place in a kettle and pour the cooked strained vegetables over them, cover well and let them stew until tender.
4. Place them in a deep dish and pour the sauce with vegetables over them.

title: Vařený Šil (Boiled Sheatfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sheatfish
- salt
- water
- coriander
- onion
- bay leaf
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Cook the sheatfish in salted water in which coriander, onion, bay leaf, and vegetables have been added
2. Some prefer to cook all these ingredients first, strain the mixture, and when cooled, pour it over the raw fish, in which it is then cooked
3. Whole cooked fish is placed on a platter surrounded by various dishes (potatoes, vegetables, etc.) and decorated variously

title: Vdolky (Bohemian Biscuits with Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lukewarm milk
- a piece of butter
- 2 whole eggs
- a little ground cardamom
- a little salt
- a piece of compressed or dried yeast dissolved in milk
- white flour to make a thinner dough than for dumplings
- jam or preserves
- curd cheese, poppy seeds, or grated gingerbread for topping
- melted fresh butter for pouring

## Directions

1. In 2 cups of lukewarm milk, dissolve a piece of butter, crack into it 2 whole eggs, add a little ground cardamom, a little salt, a piece of compressed or dried yeast softened in milk, and enough white flour to make a thinner dough than for dumplings.
2. Work the dough until it is completely smooth, let it rise, then take it out onto a floured work surface, make small dimples, let them rise well, and bake them on a dry or butter-greased sheet on both sides.
3. Or you can fry them in lard.
4. Then spread them with jam, sprinkle with either curd cheese, poppy seeds, or grated gingerbread and pour melted fresh butter over them.

title: Vdolky (Filled Yeast Pastries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh yeast (beer or pressed)
- water
- sugar
- milk
- flour (1 kilogram)
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- grated lemon peel
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) salt
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) yeast
- plum jam

## Directions

1. Prepare the yeast: if using beer yeast, rinse with water until clear, then add a piece of sugar, a little milk and flour, and set in a warm place to ferment; if using pressed yeast, add water, milk, sugar and flour, and set in a warm place.
2. Sift 1 kilogram of flour. Cream 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of butter and add grated lemon peel.
3. Add most of the flour, incorporating 3 dekagrams (2 lots) of yeast, and add as much warm milk as the flour will absorb, working until the dough does not stick to the spoon.
4. Scrape down the mixing spoon and bowl, dust the dough with flour, set in a warm place and allow to ferment for about an hour.
5. Roll out the dough and cut vdolky with a small form or shape by hand.
6. Bake on a stove or on a greased sheet, or fry in butter like pastries.
7. Thin plum jam with water, add sugar, cinnamon or lemon peel, simmer briefly, and use to spread on the vdolky.
8. Sprinkle jam-topped vdolky with poppy seeds, gingerbread or cheese curds, and add a dollop of sour cream in the center.

title: Vejce Míchaná (Scrambled Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several eggs
- salt
- fresh butter
- chives (optional)
- pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs into a bowl and add salt.
2. Melt a piece of fresh butter, pour in the eggs, place over heat and stir constantly until they reach the desired thickness.
3. Finely chopped chives may be added directly to the pan or served on a separate plate.
4. Pickled cucumbers are usually served with them.

title: Vejce na kyselo (Sour Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 deciliters good sour cream
- 30 grams fresh butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- Ham, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Crumble 30 grams fresh butter into 3.5 deciliters sour cream seasoned with salt and pepper.
2. Pour onto a baking sheet.
3. When the butter is melted, crack enough eggs onto it to cover the entire surface.
4. Sprinkle the entire surface with finely chopped ham.
5. Bake in the oven until the eggs are cooked.
6. Cut around the edges with a knife and carefully transfer to a plate so the top eggs remain undamaged.

title: Vejce na kyselo (Sour Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh butter
- Flour
- Vinegar
- Good milk
- Pinch of flower (herb)
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of fresh butter in a saucepan
2. Let a large spoonful of flour brown on it
3. Add several spoonfuls of vinegar and mix
4. Gradually pour in good milk while constantly stirring until a thickened sauce forms
5. When the sauce is cooking, add a pinch of herb
6. Quickly drop eggs one by one into the sauce
7. Let them set in the sauce and serve immediately

title: Vejce na kyselo (Sour Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour (one large spoon)
- vinegar (several spoons)
- milk (good quality)
- salt (pinch of flowers)
- eggs

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of fresh butter in a pan
2. Let one large spoon of flour brown on it
3. Add several spoons of vinegar
4. Mix and gradually pour in good milk while constantly stirring until a thickened sauce forms
5. When the sauce is cooking, add a pinch of salt flowers
6. Quickly drop eggs one by one into the sauce
7. Let them set in the sauce and serve immediately

title: Vejce na Měkko (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Place eggs in boiling water for 5 minutes.
2. Soft-boiled eggs are a very easily digestible and nourishing dish.

title: Vejce na Tvrdo (Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- boiling water
- salt
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Place eggs in boiling water and keep them there for half an hour if they are to be completely hard.
2. May be eaten with salt and horseradish, though they are very difficult to digest.

title: Vejce na Zvláštní Způsob (Eggs in Special Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- eggs
- sardines
- parsley or chives
- grated bread roll
- butter

## Directions

1. Oil a regular dish and place it on the stove.
2. Pour 1/4 liter of sour cream into it.
3. Carefully beat in the eggs.
4. Sprinkle with chopped sardines, chopped parsley or chives, grated bread roll and small pieces of butter.
5. Place the dish in the oven so the eggs bake.

title: Vejce pečená (Baked Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter (two spoons fresh)
- egg yolks (five)
- flour (one small spoon white)
- salt
- vinegar (small amount)
- sour cream (two cups thick)
- pepper (ground white, small amount)
- hard-boiled eggs (ten, cut into small cubes)

## Directions

1. Cream two spoons of fresh butter until foamy
2. Add five egg yolks and one small spoon of white flour
3. Salt and mix well
4. Add a small amount of vinegar and two cups of thick sour cream
5. Add a little ground white pepper
6. Mix thoroughly
7. Slowly fold in ten hard-boiled eggs cut into small cubes
8. Grease a baking dish with butter
9. Pour the mixture into it
10. Pour melted butter over the top
11. Bake in the oven until done

title: Vejce pečená (Baked Stuffed Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- About 8 eggs
- 30 grams fresh butter
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Mushrooms (porcini, champignons or morels), finely chopped and fried in butter
- Crayfish tails or 3 grated potatoes (if mushrooms are unavailable)
- Butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Hard boil about 8 eggs, plunge into cold water, cool, peel, and cut in half.
2. Carefully remove the yolks and rub them with 30 grams fresh butter, salt, nutmeg, finely chopped parsley, and finely chopped mushrooms that have been fried in butter.
3. If mushrooms are not available, crayfish tails or 3 grated potatoes may be added to the mixture.
4. Mix everything well and fill the egg whites with the mixture.
5. Place the halves on a greased baking sheet with the filling side up and bake, or join two halves together, soak in milk, coat in bread crumbs, and fry.
6. If asparagus or cauliflower is available, prepare it in butter, arrange on a plate, and surround with the eggs.

title: Vejce s brambory (Eggs with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard boiled eggs
- Cooked cooled potatoes
- Butter
- Chives
- Pepper
- Four egg yolks or two whole eggs
- Sour cream or sweet milk
- Grated bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Slice several hard boiled eggs and an equal amount of cooked cooled potatoes into thin slices
2. Grease a baking dish with butter
3. Put a layer of potatoes on the bottom
4. Sprinkle with chopped chives and pepper
5. Alternate layers of eggs and potatoes until the dish is full, ending with a layer of potatoes
6. Beat four egg yolks or two whole eggs with a glass of good sour cream or sweet milk
7. Pour this mixture over the contents of the baking dish
8. You may also sprinkle with grated bread and dot with small pieces of butter
9. Bake in the oven

title: Vejce s honbami (Eggs with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Pepper
- Caraway seeds
- Salt
- Eggs
- Milk
- Flour
- Bread crumbs
- Pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Clean mushrooms, slice them thinly, and rinse.
2. Place on a baking sheet with butter, pepper, caraway seeds, and salt.
3. Stew until tender.
4. Use about half as many eggs as mushrooms by weight.
5. Add eggs to the mushrooms and mix over heat.
6. When thickened, place on a plate and surround with pickled cucumbers.
7. Alternatively, slice bread thinly, soak in milk, coat in flour, then egg, then bread crumbs, fry in butter, and arrange around the eggs.
8. All edible mushrooms can be used, but the best are: porcini, champignons, or morels.
9. For morels, drain the liquid that releases during cooking before adding butter.
10. If there are plenty of mushrooms, use only the caps; stems may be used but must be carefully inspected for worms.

title: Vejce s houbami (Eggs with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh mushrooms
- Eggs
- Butter
- Fresh parsley, chopped
- Pepper
- Salt
- Pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Slice fresh mushrooms thinly and rinse them.
2. Salt the mushrooms and place them on a baking sheet with chopped parsley, fresh butter, and pepper.
3. Fry for a while.
4. Meanwhile, on another baking sheet, place eggs with butter, chopped chives, salt, and pepper.
5. When both are ready, arrange the mushrooms in the center and around the eggs, or arrange them in a cross pattern with pickled cucumbers between the arms.
6. Use the first arrangement when mushrooms are scarce, the second when there are plenty.

title: Vejce s kyselou smetanou (Eggs with Sour Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 to 6 eggs
- 4 raw egg yolks
- 3.5 deciliters good thick sour cream
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Hard boil 5 or 6 eggs.
2. When cooled, peel and slice into halves, quarters, or cubes.
3. Whisk 4 raw egg yolks into 3.5 deciliters of good thick sour cream.
4. Grease a baking sheet with butter.
5. Place the sliced hard eggs on it and salt.
6. Pour the whisked yolks in cream over the eggs.
7. Place in the oven and let thicken slowly.
8. Turn out onto a plate and serve with pepper sprinkled over.
9. If the cream is not thick enough, flour may be added.

title: Vejce s kyselou smetanovou omáčkou (Eggs with Sour Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of water
- Two spoonfuls of vinegar
- Salt
- Pinch of sugar
- Two or three spoonfuls of chopped fresh dill
- One cup of good thick sour cream
- One spoonful of flour
- Five or six eggs
- Boiled beef

## Directions

1. Bring two cups of water to boil with two spoonfuls of vinegar, salt, a pinch of sugar, and two or three spoonfuls of chopped fresh dill
2. Take one cup of good thick sour cream and one spoonful of flour and mix them well together
3. Pour the cream mixture into the boiling water and let it come to a boil
4. Drop five or six eggs into it and when they are set, serve with boiled beef

title: Vejce s kyselou smetanovou omáčkou (Eggs with Sour Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- water (two cups)
- vinegar (two spoons)
- salt
- sugar (pinch)
- green dill (two or three spoons chopped)

## Directions

1. Bring two cups of water to boil
2. Add two spoons of vinegar
3. Add a little salt
4. Add a pinch of sugar
5. Add two or three spoons of chopped green dill

title: Vejce s omáčkou z hořčice (Eggs with Mustard Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Boil eggs for about 5 minutes until soft-boiled.
2. Cool in cold water.
3. Cut and serve with mustard sauce.

title: Vejce s vínem (Eggs with Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Beat eggs with wine and sugar, beating continuously until the wine begins to rise.
2. Alternatively, boil half a liter of wine with half the amount of water and 6 decagrams of sugar.
3. Whisk 2 egg yolks in water and slowly add the wine while continuously stirring.
4. Pour the wine into glasses and serve as a pleasant beverage.

title: Vejce se Sardelemi (Eggs with Sardines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- sardines
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs into a bowl and mix well.
2. Clean the sardines, remove all bones, and cut them into strips or mash them with butter.
3. Place on a pan, stir over heat until reaching the desired thickness.
4. Pepper is usually added.

title: Vejce se Slaninou (Eggs with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon, cut into cubes
- eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into cubes and fry on a baking sheet until pink.
2. Add beaten, mixed, salted, and peppered eggs.
3. Stir constantly until sufficiently thick.

title: Vejce se Sýrem (Eggs with Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- grated cheese (one third the weight of the eggs in their shells)
- butter (one sixth the weight of the eggs)
- milk (1 spoonful per egg)
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For one person, weigh 2 eggs in their shells.
2. Add one third their weight in grated cheese and one sixth in butter.
3. Add 1 spoonful of milk per egg, some salt and plenty of pepper.
4. Stir until the egg white sets.
5. Serve on a warm plate.

title: Vejce se zemáky (Eggs with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 hard eggs
- Equal weight of cooked, peeled, cooled potatoes
- Butter for greasing
- Chives, chopped
- Pepper
- 4 egg yolks
- 3.5 deciliters good sour cream

## Directions

1. Peel 8 hard eggs and slice them.
2. Slice the same weight of cooked, peeled, cooled potatoes.
3. Grease a baking sheet and layer potatoes.
4. Sprinkle with chopped chives and pepper.
5. Add a layer of eggs.
6. Continue alternating layers until the sheet is full, with the top layer being potatoes.
7. Whisk 4 egg yolks into 3.5 deciliters good sour cream and pour over.
8. Bake in the oven.

title: Vejce se Švýcarským Sýrem (Eggs with Swiss Cheese)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dekagrams Swiss cheese, grated
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 liter cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix the grated cheese with 3 eggs and 1/2 liter of cream.
2. Place the mixture on heat with butter.
3. Stir constantly until it begins to boil and rise.
4. Serve on a heated plate.

title: Vejce sekaná (Chopped Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 to 10 hard eggs
- 45 grams fresh butter
- 30 grams white flour
- 3.5 deciliters good sweet cream
- Butter for greasing
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Peel 8 to 10 hard eggs, cut in half, and remove the yolks.
2. Cut the whites into thin strips.
3. Rub 45 grams fresh butter and add the removed yolks and 30 grams white flour, grinding them together.
4. Pour in 3.5 deciliters good sweet cream.
5. Pour onto a baking sheet and stir over heat until thickened.
6. Let cool.
7. Add the sliced whites to the cooled mixture.
8. Place everything on a greased baking sheet sprinkled with bread crumbs and bake in the oven.

title: Vejce vařená (Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. For soft-boiled eggs where the white is partially cooked but not hard and the yolk is completely liquid, put clean washed eggs into boiling water, remove the pot from the heat so the water does not boil further, cover well and leave for five minutes
2. The best and safest method to achieve the desired degree of cooking is that the eggs are completely heated through and the white is nicely gelatinous and not hardened
3. Must be carefully watched and not left in water longer than five minutes
4. Another good method is to put eggs in cold water, bring to the fire and let the water come to a boil
5. As soon as the water begins to boil, the eggs are just right and the cook cannot be mistaken in this case
6. The most common method is to put eggs in boiling water and let them boil for three minutes
7. In this time they are soft-boiled
8. If we let them boil for five minutes, the white is cooked and the yolk is semi-liquid, whereas in 8 to 10 minutes they are hard-boiled

title: Vejcová Paštika (Egg Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Six hard-boiled and peeled eggs
- Peeled and cored apples
- Necessary fat from kidneys
- Small raisins
- Sugar
- Bread roll soaked and squeezed in milk
- Wine
- Salt
- Crushed clove
- Cinnamon
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop the six hard-boiled peeled eggs with the peeled and cored apples
2. Add the necessary fat from kidneys and mix well
3. Add small raisins, sugar, bread roll soaked and squeezed in milk, wine, salt, crushed clove, cinnamon and nutmeg
4. Mix everything together
5. Fill the filling into small pastries
6. Bake

title: Vejcová Paštika (Egg Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- six hard-boiled eggs
- peeled and pitted apples
- kidney fat
- small raisins
- sugar
- bread crumbs soaked in milk
- wine
- salt
- cloves
- cinnamon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop six hard-boiled peeled eggs with peeled and pitted apples
2. Add necessary kidney fat and mix well
3. Add small raisins, sugar, bread crumbs soaked in milk and squeezed, wine, salt, crushed cloves, cinnamon, and nutmeg
4. Bake this together
5. Fill small pastries with this filling

title: Vejcová polévka (Egg Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- About 5 eggs
- A piece of new butter
- A piece of chives (pažitky)
- 2 liters water
- All kinds of vegetables
- A piece of butter or a piece of paprika-flavored bacon
- Roux (jemnou jíškou)
- Bread (housky)
- Milk
- Bread crumbs
- More chives or parsley

## Directions

1. Mix about 5 eggs, a piece of new butter, and a piece of chives
2. Stir this over the fire until a thick porridge forms
3. Put all kinds of vegetables into 2 liters of water, add a piece of butter or paprika-flavored bacon and brown with fine roux
4. When everything has cooked, strain
5. Cut bread into rounds, soak in milk, dip in egg and bread crumbs, and fry
6. Spread one side with the prepared porridge, cover with another round
7. Pour soup over it, to which chopped chives or parsley has been added

title: Vejcové koláče s kmínem (Egg Cakes with Caraway)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams grated white bread
- 1 tablespoon flour
- ½ liter milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon crushed caraway
- egg white snow

## Directions

1. Take 125 grams grated white bread, 1 tablespoon flour, half a liter of milk, three egg yolks, mix and salt everything
2. Add 1 tablespoon crushed caraway and snow from egg whites
3. Fry the cakes made from all these ingredients on both sides in lard
4. Serve with smoked meat, ham, sausages or salad

title: Vejcové řízky (Egg Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kilo fine flour
- 16 eggs
- sweet sour cream as needed
- hot butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix ¼ kilo fine flour with sixteen eggs and as much sweet sour cream as needed to make a thick mixture
2. Pour into a cake mold and bake
3. When cooled, cut into long narrow pieces
4. Fry them in hot butter and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Vejcový dort (Egg Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- about half kilogram good butter
- about 16 egg yolks
- lemon zest finely shaved
- finely ground cinnamon
- clove
- half kilogram ground sugar
- half kilogram finest flour

## Directions

1. Heat the baking pan
2. Spread about half kilogram good butter on it
3. Beat 16 egg yolks into the butter
4. Beat and mix everything together for at least half an hour
5. Add finely shaved lemon zest, finely ground cinnamon, clove, half kilogram ground sugar, and half kilogram finest flour
6. Mix and beat everything together
7. Make stiff snow from the remaining egg whites and mix into the described dough
8. Pour the prepared dough into greased baking forms
9. Bake slowly and gently
10. The cakes must be carefully removed from the forms while still hot, but never immediately after they come from the oven, as they are prone to collapse and fall

title: Vejcový Paj (Custard Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one quart of milk
- one teaspoon of ground vanilla
- one coffee cup of sugar
- one tablespoon of corn starch
- a pinch of salt
- dough

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs with one quart of milk
2. Add one teaspoon of ground vanilla, one coffee cup of sugar, one tablespoon of corn starch, and a pinch of salt
3. Mix everything well
4. Fill two dough-lined forms with this mixture and bake
5. Alternatively, you can use only yolks for the cream and make stiff snow from egg whites and sugar, spread it on the baked pies and let it set slightly in a hotter oven

title: Velikonoční bochánek (Easter Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- eggs
- milk
- yeast
- sugar
- salt
- lemon zest
- ground cloves or anise

## Directions

1. Make the dough in the same way as for Christmas twist
2. Make two larger or three smaller loaves
3. Brush them with beaten egg
4. Make a cross on each surface with a knife
5. Let them rise and then bake slowly in the oven

title: Velké Máslové Fazole (Lima or Butter Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint large butter beans
- Salt water
- 1 small cup sour cream or milk
- 1 spoonful butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Shell one pint of large butter beans from their pods and cook in salted water until soft.
2. Then drain them, pour a small cup of sour cream or milk over them, add one spoonful of butter, salt and pepper, let it simmer a bit more and serve.

title: Velmi Chutní Raci (Delicious Crayfish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cream
- salted and diluted beer
- parsley
- cumin
- butter

## Directions

1. Pour sour cream over live crayfish, which eat it gladly and thus lose their watery taste
2. Cook the crayfish in salted and water-diluted beer, to which parsley and cumin have been added
3. After cooking, pour hot melted butter over the crayfish from time to time before serving at table

title: Vemeno smažené (Fried Cow's Udder)
body:
## Ingredients

- Kravské vemeno
- Sůl
- Mouka
- Vejce
- Strouhaná žemle
- Máslo

## Directions

1. Dobře očištěné kravské vemeno vař v několika vodách až změkne a vodu více nekalí
2. Ořezej z něj všechny kůžičky
3. Rozkrájej je na stejná a nepříliš tlustá kolečka nebo obyčejné kousky
4. Popraš je drobátko solí
5. Obal je nejprve v mouce
6. Pak obal v rozkloktaném vejci
7. Konečně obal ve strouhané žemličce
8. Usmaž je v rozpáleném másle do zlatová barvy
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Vemeno smažené (Fried Udder)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cow udder
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- Bread roll
- Butter
- Salads or sardine or mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the udder and cook it, allow to cool
2. Slice into rounds, salt and pepper
3. Dip in beaten egg on all sides
4. Coat in grated bread roll and fry in butter
5. Serve with various salads or sardine or mustard sauce

title: Vepřová kýta (Pork Ham)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ham
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Wash the ham, pound it, remove all bones, sprinkle with caraway, and when it is half-roasted, cut the skin, and roast until completely golden.

title: Vepřová kýta (Pork Ham) - Second Method
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ham
- onion
- carrot
- celery
- parsley root
- bay leaf
- thyme
- pepper
- new spices
- lemon zest
- capers

## Directions

1. Remove all membranes from the ham.
2. Slice the onion, carrot, celery, add parsley root, bay leaf, thyme, pepper, new spices, lemon zest, and capers.
3. Pour everything onto a baking pan and let the ham lie in it for a full day.
4. On the second day, roast it.
5. When roasted, slice it and make a brown sauce; place it in the sauce in which the ham was roasted, and braise for another 10 minutes.

title: Vepřová nakládaná kýta (Pickled Leg of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- onion
- bay leaves
- thyme
- ginger
- peppercorns
- cloves
- allspice
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove skin and lard from the pork leg, wash it cleanly, salt it, and place it in a deep dish.
2. Have two cups of vinegar, the same amount of water, one chopped onion, several bay leaves, some thyme, a piece of ginger, several grains of pepper, cloves, and allspice simmer in a pot, and when cooled, pour over the leg.
3. Let it lie this way for three days, turning it twice daily; then place it on a roasting pan, strain a little of the liquid over it, and let it roast, basting it frequently with gravy.
4. When nearly done, mix a tablespoon of flour with a little sour cream in a cup, add it to the leg, let the sauce thicken a little, then remove the leg to a platter and pour the sauce over it.

title: Vepřová pečené ledvinová (Roast Loin of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- A nice piece of loin near the kidney
- Salt
- Caraway seeds
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean a nice piece of loin, salt it, sprinkle with a little caraway, place on a baking sheet skin side down, pour a little water over it and bake on that side until the skin is tender
2. Then turn the meat, cut the skin into squares with a sharp knife, sprinkle lightly with salt and bake at high heat until nicely golden
3. Pork leg can be cooked the same way
4. If the meat is very fatty, remove the skin and all the lard
5. Bread dumplings with cabbage are best served with this roast

title: Vepřová Sekanina (Sausage Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork from shoulder with some fat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sage
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Take pork from shoulder with some marbling and chop finely, preferably with a meat grinder
2. For five pounds of meat add two heaping tablespoons salt, one level small teaspoon pepper, one level tablespoon sage, half small teaspoon ginger
3. Mix thoroughly and store in a cool place where it will keep for days
4. Fry as croquettes or thin with warm water and stuff into sheep casings to make pork sausages

title: Vepřová žebírka se zelím (Spare Ribs with Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds spare ribs
- sauerkraut
- onion
- flour
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of spare ribs and cut them so that there are always 2-3 ribs together.
2. Parboil and salt them and let them cook.
3. When they have cooked for half an hour, add a quart of pickled sauerkraut and half an onion chopped, cover and let cook until both meat and sauerkraut are tender.
4. Mix a tablespoon of flour in a little water and thicken the sauerkraut with it.

title: Vepřové divoké po myslivečku (Wild Boar Hunter's Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar meat
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- spices
- aromatic vegetables
- bay leaf
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Salt a piece of wild boar meat.
2. Boil it in water, vinegar, spices, aromatic vegetables and bay leaf.
3. Serve with grated horseradish.

title: Vepřové Jaterničky s Pivem (Pork Sausages with Beer)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of fatty pork meat
- salt
- ground pepper to taste
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop one pound of fatty pork meat, salt it and season to taste with ground pepper, then form it into small rolls in the manner of sausages, coat them in breadcrumbs and place on a buttered baking pan.
2. When they begin to turn red on top, baste them with small amounts of beer while turning them; then arrange them on a platter, pour the pan drippings over them and serve.
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Vepřové jaterničky s pivem (Sausages with Beer)
body:
## Ingredients

- Libra libového vepřového masa
- Sůl
- Pepř
- Strouhaná houska
- Máslo
- Pivo

## Directions

1. Usekej libru libového vepřového masa
2. Osol a okořeň dle chuti tlučeným pepřem
3. Udělej z toho malé válečky na způsob jaterniček
4. Obal je ve strouhané housce
5. Vlož na kuthan máslem vymazaný
6. Když počínají po vrchu červenati, podlévej je při obracení troškami piva
7. Narovnej je na mísu
8. Polej je šťávou z kuthanu
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Vepřové kotletky (Pork Chops)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from the kidney or neck with bone
- flour
- egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Pound the meat with a wooden mallet, salt it, coat in flour, then in egg, and then in breadcrumbs.
2. Fry until golden brown.

title: Vepřové kotletky (Pork Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork cutlets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- White bread or crackers
- Butter or lard
- Onion
- Sage
- Parsley butter
- Potato croquettes

## Directions

1. Neatly trim and pound the pork cutlets
2. Salt, pepper, dip in beaten eggs, coat in grated white bread or crumbled crackers and fry in hot butter or lard until golden
3. You can mix a little finely chopped onion and sage into the bread
4. Arrange on a hot platter, top with pieces of parsley butter and surround with potato croquettes
5. You can also serve with the same sauces as veal cutlets or garnish with various vegetables

title: Vepřové kotletky na jiný způsob (Pork Cutlets Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young pork ribs
- Salt
- White ground pepper
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Four onions
- Flour
- Hot water
- Meat extract
- Pepper
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare cutlets from young pork ribs
2. Salt both sides and lightly dust with white ground pepper
3. Dip in melted butter, coat in grated breadcrumbs, and about a quarter hour before serving, bake on a grill or in a pan
4. Meanwhile, slice four onions into thin rounds, put a piece of fresh butter on a small pan, place the sliced onion in it and fry until golden
5. Add one tablespoon of flour, then pour in one cup of hot water and add one and a half teaspoons of meat extract, salt, add a few grains of pepper and a piece of sugar
6. Let simmer until the onion is completely soft, then press this mixture through a fine sieve, put it hot on a platter and arrange the cutlets around it in a wreath

title: Vepřové maso na pivě (Pork in Beer)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- salt
- caraway seeds
- beer

## Directions

1. Salt the pork meat and season with caraway seeds.
2. Place it on a baking pan to which half a liter of beer has been added.
3. Braise it on the stove until it becomes tender.

title: Vepřové maso s kukuřičnou moukou (Scrapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat (head, liver, heart, and other offal)
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- marjoram
- cornmeal
- water

## Directions

1. Any pork meat can be prepared this way, even leftovers from roasted meat in small quantities, but it is best made from pork head, liver, heart, and other offal that you do not wish to use otherwise.
2. Put all of this in a pot with salt, pepper, garlic, and marjoram to cook until tender, then remove the meat and strain the water and pour it back into the pot.
3. Slowly stir in enough cornmeal to make a nice porridge and let it cook for half an hour.
4. In the meantime, chop the meat finely and mix it in; there should be approximately equal quantities of both.
5. Pour while hot into molds and let cool.
6. When you wish to use it, cut it into slices and fry on both sides and serve warm.

title: Vepřové po polsku (Pork, Polish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin meat from the ribs
- salt
- butter
- flour
- pork broth
- vinegar
- lemon zest
- allspice
- cloves
- blanched almonds
- raisins without pits
- syrup
- gingerbread

## Directions

1. Salt and let a nice piece of pork loin from the ribs cook.
2. Make a light brown roux (from 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon flour for 2 pounds), thin it with broth from the meat, add a little vinegar, a piece of fine lemon zest, a little allspice, three crushed cloves, blanched almonds, raisins without pits, a little syrup, and half a cup of grated gingerbread and let it simmer.
3. Place the meat, cooked until tender and cut into larger pieces, into this sauce, let it simmer for another minute, and serve.

title: Vepřové s brukví (Pork with Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- vegetables
- kohlrabi (kedlubny)
- water
- pork broth
- salt
- sugar
- pepper
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Parboil the pork and cook with all vegetables as you would beef.
2. Meanwhile, peel the kohlrabi (kedlubny) cleanly, cut into cubes or thin slices, pour water over them and place by the fire.
3. When it begins to boil, drain the water, pour pork broth over it, salt to taste, and add sugar if it should be sweet, otherwise pepper.
4. When softened, fry it with a light roux, let it simmer a little, add the pork meat cut into neat pieces, let it simmer a bit longer, pour onto a platter and serve.
5. If you want it dark, you can add a piece of caramelized sugar.
6. In the same way, pork with carrots, celery, beets, or cabbage is also prepared.

title: Vepřové s brukví (Pork with Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat
- Various vegetables

## Directions

1. Clean the pork meat and cook it with all the vegetables as you would beef

title: Vepřové závitky s rýží (Pork Rolls with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg
- rice
- onion
- butter
- salt
- beef broth

## Directions

1. From pork leg, cut across about as thick as a finger, slices, beat them well and salt them.
2. Shred several onions and let them foam in butter, then spread them on the prepared slices.
3. Place on each a full tablespoon of well-rinsed rice, roll them up, wrap each with string, arrange them in a buttered roasting pan and let them brown somewhat on high heat.
4. Then add beef broth to them so that the rice can soften and let them then stew until tender.

title: Vepřové řízky (Pork Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork chops
- Salt
- Caraway seeds
- Hot water
- Flour

## Directions

1. Clean the chops and arrange them in a pan one beside the other
2. Salt and season with caraway seeds and pour over three or four tablespoons of hot water
3. Cover and let braise
4. When the water evaporates, the lard usually renders from them, but if not, add a piece of lard
5. When they turn red on one side, turn them so they also turn red on the other side
6. When done, remove them to a platter, pour hot water into the rendered fat and add about half a tablespoon of flour to make a gravy, salt if needed and pour over the chops
7. They can also be baked on a baking sheet in an oven

title: Vepřový drob na kyselo (Pork Offal in Sour Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pork offal (liver, lungs, heart, kidneys)
- salt
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- new spices
- pepper
- lemon zest
- vinegar
- water
- flour
- burnt sugar or gingerbread
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse the offal, salt it, place on a baking pan, add sliced onion, thyme, bay leaf, new spices, pepper, and a piece of lemon zest.
2. Cook in vinegar diluted with water.
3. When the offal is cooked, remove it and leave it either whole or cut into pieces.
4. Add flour to the sauce and cook either with burnt sugar or gingerbread, strain over the offal, and if not sweet enough, add sugar.

title: Vepřový drob na kyselo (Pork Offal in Sour Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork offal (liver, heart, lungs, kidneys)
- Salt
- Onion
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Allspice
- Pepper
- Lemon zest
- Vinegar
- Water
- Flour
- Sugar or gingerbread
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Rinse the offal that was removed before roasting, salt it, and place on a baking sheet.
2. Chop onion and add thyme, bay leaf, allspice, pepper, and a piece of lemon zest.
3. Cook in vinegar diluted with water.
4. When the offal is cooked, remove it and either leave it whole or cut it into pieces.
5. Add flour to the sauce and cook either with caramelized sugar or gingerbread.
6. Strain the sauce over the offal, and if not sweet enough, add sugar.
7. Potatoes may be added to the sauce.
8. The liver can be used to make liver dumplings: grate the liver, add half a grated roll, 1 egg, 15 grams (1 lot) of butter, salt, a pinch of flowers, a little pepper, and marjoram; form small dumplings with a spoon and cook in the soup; if too thick, add more roll.

title: Vepřový ovar s křenem (Boiled Pork with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of pork head
- piece of meat from the neck
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of the head and a piece of meat from the neck
2. Slice the meat into neat pieces
3. Serve hot on a preheated platter
4. Serve with grated horseradish

title: Vepřový rosol (Pork Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram pork meat
- one kilogram veal
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- new spices
- thyme
- bay leaf
- onion
- carrot
- parsley
- celery
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook the pork and veal in water with vinegar, pepper, new spices, thyme, bay leaf, onion, carrot, parsley, celery, and lemon zest.
2. Finely chop the cooked meat.
3. Strain the liquid through cloth; place the meat in a mold and add just enough of the liquid to cover it.
4. Season with pepper and place on ice.
5. If it does not unmold easily, wrap the form in a thin cloth and it will slip out easily.

title: Vinná omáčka (Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls parsley
- 3 egg yolks
- wine
- ginger
- pepper
- nutmeg
- flower

## Directions

1. Chop two handfuls of parsley very finely, place in a bowl, add three egg yolks and mix well.
2. Pour warm wine over it, as much as you want sauce to be of appropriate thickness.
3. Season with ginger, pepper, nutmeg and flower.
4. Simmer slowly and stir constantly for about five minutes.

title: Vinná omáčka (Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two handfuls of clean picked parsley
- three or five egg yolks
- lukewarm wine
- cloves
- ginger
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pound two handfuls of clean picked parsley in a mortar
2. Beat in three or five egg yolks and mix well together
3. Pour lukewarm wine over this, but not too much so it is not too thin
4. Strain through cloth
5. Season with cloves, ginger, pepper and nutmeg
6. Allow to simmer slowly, stirring constantly so the sauce becomes a nice color

title: Vinná Polévka (Wine Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter wine
- 3/8 liter water
- lemon juice
- 100 grams sugar
- a little ground cinnamon
- 1 1/2 tablespoons potato flour mixed in cold water

## Directions

1. Mix wine, water, lemon juice, sugar, and cinnamon together
2. Bring to a good boil
3. While stirring constantly, slowly add 1 1/2 tablespoons potato flour mixed in cold water
4. Simmer until done
5. Pour the soup over fried roll in a bowl and serve at table

title: Vinná polévka (Wine Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart wine
- a piece of sugar
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- a piece of lemon peel
- six egg yolks or three whole eggs
- one small cup cream
- a small teaspoon flour
- a little cold wine
- a pinch of salt
- fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Put one quart of wine in a pot with a piece of sugar, a piece of whole cinnamon and a piece of lemon peel
2. let everything boil together, remove scum and strain
3. in another pot beat six egg yolks or three whole eggs with one small cup of cream, a small teaspoon of flour, a little cold wine and a pinch of salt
4. pour boiling wine over this
5. mix well and let it stand on warm heat but do not let it boil
6. serve either in cups or pour over fried bread cubes
7. if you want a weaker soup, you can boil it with one quart of wine and one pint or even one quart of water
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Vinné Sodo (Chaudeau - Wine Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls of sugar
- rind from one lemon, cut up
- a piece of cinnamon
- 1 pint of white wine

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks, 4 spoonfuls of sugar, and cut-up rind from one lemon together well in a large pot
2. Add a piece of cinnamon
3. Pour 1 pint of white wine into it
4. Place over moderate heat and whisk with a wire whisk until it froths
5. Do not let it boil. When it thickens, pour into a dish and serve immediately at the table

title: Vinné Řízky (Wine Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roll or white bread, grated
- a handful of peeled and chopped almonds
- lemon zest
- orange zest
- a little cinnamon
- two egg yolks
- two egg whites, beaten to snow
- unsalted butter or good lard for frying
- sugar
- white or red wine

## Directions

1. Grate one roll or white bread.
2. Mix together a handful of peeled and chopped almonds, lemon zest, orange zest, a little cinnamon, two egg yolks, and the beaten egg whites from two eggs, adding the egg white snow last.
3. Spread this mixture on 15-16 slices of white bread.
4. Fry in unsalted butter or good lard with the spread side down.
5. Turn and fry the other side very lightly so it does not burn.
6. Dust with sugar as desired and pour over with white or red wine.

title: Vinní Chlebová Polévka (Wine Bread Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water
- hard bread grated
- lemon peel
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- 1/4 liter white wine
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Boil grated hard bread in a liter of water with lemon peel
2. Season with salt and sugar, add cinnamon
3. Blend well
4. Strain and simmer again
5. Add 1/4 liter of white wine and 1 egg yolk

title: Vinní Kroupová Polévka (Wine Barley Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams barley
- 1 and 1/2 liters water
- butter
- wine
- lemon juice
- cinnamon
- sugar
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the barley with water and butter
2. Add wine
3. Add lemon juice, cinnamon, and sugar
4. Boil everything for a quarter hour
5. Strain the broth
6. Add 2 egg yolks

title: Vinní Nákyp (Wine Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 rolls
- red wine
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- 8 egg yolks
- sugar
- 4 tablespoons arak

## Directions

1. Cut 4 rolls into cubes and soak in red wine.
2. Beat 12 dekagrams butter and mix with 8 egg yolks, sugar, 4 tablespoons arak, and the soaked rolls.
3. Grease a mold and bake the soufflé in it over moderate heat.

title: Vinní ocet (Wine Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wine remnants from barrels
- good vinegar
- heat at 17-20° R.

## Directions

1. Drain the wine remnants from the barrels so they transform into vinegar
2. Add good vinegar to them and place everything in a warm or sunny location where air and heat have access
3. Heat to 17-20° R.
