4. Decant the prepared vinegar into vessels, which must always be topped up so the vinegar does not spoil and become ruined

title: Vinní omáčka (Wine Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon flour
- 2 dekagrams butter
- 10 dekagrams sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- a little salt
- 1 glass good wine, preferably Madeira

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, butter, sugar, egg yolks, and salt
2. Pour in a glass of good wine, preferably Madeira
3. Stir thoroughly and constantly on warm stove until it boils and cooks
4. Stir for a while longer then remove from heat

title: Vinní Polévka (Wine Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle white wine
- 3/8 liter sugar water
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- fine potato flour
- 4 egg yolks
- fried roll

## Directions

1. Boil white wine with 3/8 liter of sugar water to which a little cinnamon, lemon peel, and fine potato flour have been added
2. Simmer everything together in the soup
3. Whisk 4 egg yolks and add to the soup
4. Once it has simmered again, pour over fried roll in a bowl

title: Vinní Polévka (Wine Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 grams butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 liter boiling water
- juice of 1 whole lemon
- peel of half a lemon
- 100-125 grams sugar
- piece of cinnamon
- 1 bottle white wine
- 4 egg yolks
- fried roll cut into cubes

## Directions

1. Brown 2 tablespoons flour in 70 grams of butter until yellow
2. Add 1/2 liter of boiling water
3. Add lemon juice and peel from half a lemon, 100-125 grams sugar, a piece of cinnamon, and a bottle of white wine
4. Simmer for about ten minutes while stirring constantly
5. Whisk 4 egg yolks and add to the soup
6. Once it has simmered again, pour over fried roll cut into cubes in a bowl

title: Vinní Polévka se Suchary (Wine Soup with Rusks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- about 8 rusks pounded to powder
- 1/2 bottle wine
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil water with cinnamon, finely cut lemon peel, and about 8 rusks pounded to powder for at least a quarter hour
2. Strain through fine sieve
3. Add half a bottle of wine
4. Sweeten to taste
5. Bring to a boil again
6. Finally add several whisked egg yolks

title: Vinní Šodó (Wine Sabayon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three and one-half deciliters good wine
- 14 decagrams sugar
- seven to eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Add 14 decagrams of dissolved sugar to three and one-half deciliters of good wine and boil together
2. Let cool
3. Add seven to eight egg yolks
4. Mix together constantly until foamy

title: Vinný Čokoládový Creme (Wine Chocolate Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter red wine
- 250 grams chocolate
- 200 grams sugar
- 30 grams dissolved fish bladder
- egg white from four eggs

## Directions

1. Boil one liter of red wine with 250 grams of chocolate and 200 grams of sugar
2. Mix in 30 grams of dissolved fish bladder
3. Sieve the crushed chocolate
4. Add snow from four egg whites
5. Pour into molds and place on ice

title: Violet Ice Cream (Violková zmrzlina)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Violet juice
- Lemon juice
- Plenty of sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Take violet juice described elsewhere
2. Add lemon juice
3. Add plenty of sugar
4. Dilute slightly with water
5. Proceed to make ice cream

title: Višně zavařené (Preserved Sour Cherries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Trim the ends of the stems.
2. Boil the sugar until it reaches the sixth degree.
3. Add the sour cherries to it.
4. Proceed with everything else as has been described before.

title: Višňovka (Cherry Liqueur)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe, cleaned cherries
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Cinnamon
- Allspice
- Lemon peel
- Orange peel
- Sugar
- Good spirits (kořalka)

## Directions

1. Remove pits from ripe, cleaned cherries and place them in large bottles
2. On each layer of cherries, add various spices: cloves, pepper, cinnamon, allspice, lemon peel, and orange peel, with plenty of sugar
3. Pour good spirits over everything
4. Cover the bottle opening with clean cloth and leave in the sun for some time
5. Strain the cherry liqueur into special bottles and seal firmly to prevent evaporation

title: Višňovka (Cherry Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe sour cherries without pits
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves
- allspice
- cherry pits
- wine alcohol or good rye liquor

## Directions

1. Fill a large glass bottle halfway with ripe sour cherries without pits
2. Add sugar according to desired sweetness—generally half a pound of fine sugar per two pounds of cherries
3. Add broken cinnamon, some cloves, half an ounce of allspice and some kernels from the cherry pits
4. Fill the bottle with wine alcohol or good rye liquor
5. Seal the bottle well and place where it gets the most sun
6. Stir it frequently so everything mixes
7. It will be ready in three weeks

title: Višňová omáčka (Cherry Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- red wine
- cherries
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil red wine with cherries, sugar, and cinnamon
2. Strain everything
3. Serve

title: Višňová omáčka k černé zvěřině (Sour Cherry Sauce for Game)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cherries
- cherry pits
- crushed cherry pits
- bread crust
- cinnamon
- clove
- all kinds of spices
- wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Sort the sour cherries and crush the pits in a mortar
2. Place the crushed pits with the pitted cherries on a baking pan
3. Add bread crust, cinnamon, clove, and all kinds of spices
4. Boil until completely broken down
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Add wine and a sufficient amount of sugar
7. Boil until the sauce thickens properly
8. Serve in a special dish with roasted game

title: Višňové a třešňové pokroutky (Cherry and Sour Cherry Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- sour cherry or cherry juice

## Directions

1. Pound sugar finely
2. Mix in sour cherry or cherry juice
3. Prepare everything else as described for other brittle pieces

title: Višňové Vino (Cherry Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe cherries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the ripe cherries, remove the stems, crush the cherries and press them through a fine sieve.
2. Add sugar to the juice, mix everything together and pour the wine into a barrel or bottles (if there is little of it) so that it can ferment.
3. Cork the fermented wine, let it rest peacefully for three months, then siphon it into bottles.

title: Višňový a třešňový kompot (Cherry and Sweet Cherry Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries and sweet cherries
- sugar
- currant juice

## Directions

1. Either pit the cherries and sweet cherries, or place them whole in sugar boiled to the sixth degree
2. Boil several times with this
3. Remove the cherries and sweet cherries carefully with a spoon and place on a sieve
4. Boil part of the syrup with a little currant juice into a puree and arrange it so that when cold it can be poured over the cherries

title: Višňový Dort (Cherry Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) scalded almonds
- 12 dekagrams sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 eggs
- snow from 4 egg whites
- lemon zest
- several grated rolls
- picked pitted cherries

## Directions

1. Mix 12 dekagrams scalded almonds, 12 dekagrams sugar, 4 egg yolks, 3 eggs, snow from 4 egg whites, lemon zest, and several grated rolls into a fairly stiff dough
2. Place half in a form and bake for about a quarter hour
3. Spread picked pitted cherries on top
4. Cover with the remaining dough
5. Bake and glaze the cake

title: Višňový Paj (Cherry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- pitted cherries
- sugar
- brandy (optional)
- flour
- pie crust dough

## Directions

1. Pit the cherries
2. Sprinkle with sugar
3. Sprinkle with brandy if desired
4. Dust with flour
5. Prepare a pie in the same manner as apple pie
6. Pies made from strawberries, raspberries, blueberries and grapes are prepared the same way
7. The amount of sugar should be adjusted according to the sweetness of the fruit

title: Vlašské šalami (Italian Salami)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kilograms fine pork meat
- 2.5 kilograms juicy beef meat
- 2 kilograms bacon, diced
- Good red wine
- 200 grams salt
- 50-60 grams finely ground pepper
- Garlic
- Beef intestines

## Directions

1. Finely chop 5 kilograms pork and 2.5 kilograms beef meat completely
2. Place in cloth, tie firmly, and hang for 24 hours to allow the juices to drain, preventing early spoilage
3. Mix the chopped meat with 2 kilograms bacon cut into small cubes
4. Moisten with good red wine (2-3 small glasses)
5. Salt with about 200 grams salt and pepper with 50-60 grams finely ground pepper
6. Moisten again with a little (one small glass) red wine mixed with garlic pressed through a sieve
7. Mix thoroughly and work together well
8. Clean beef intestines completely and soak in wine with various spices for 24 hours
9. Stuff with the prepared meat mixture
10. Smoke and store in a cool place

title: Vlašský dort (Walnut Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram finely crushed sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- finely sifted flour
- stiff snow (beaten egg whites)
- butter for greasing cake molds
- finely grated bread crumbs for dusting molds

## Directions

1. Take half kilogram of finely crushed sugar and beat egg yolks into it
2. Mix everything thoroughly
3. Gradually add sifted fine flour and stiff snow
4. When everything is worked together, pour into cake molds greased with butter and dusted with finely grated bread crumbs
5. Bake slowly
6. The cakes may also be filled with preserves, layered on top of each other, and coated with various glazes

title: Vlašský salát (Italian-Style Salad with Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peeled potatoes
- Meissen apples
- ham
- smoked tongue
- bacon
- wine vinegar
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Allow cooked peeled potatoes to cool
2. Peel several Meissen apples and chop finely
3. Cut pieces of ham, smoked tongue, and bacon (with bones removed) into small pieces
4. Slice the potatoes into rounds and spread on a platter
5. Sprinkle with apples and onion
6. Layer with pieces of ham, tongue, and bacon
7. Add another layer of potato rounds
8. Sprinkle with apples and onion again
9. Place cleaned and halved sardines on top
10. Mix wine vinegar with salt and Provençal oil and pour over everything

title: Vlašský salát (Italian-Style Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled green beans
- pickled asparagus
- cauliflower
- field salad
- watercress
- young dandelion centers
- wine vinegar
- good oil
- hard-boiled eggs
- bacon

## Directions

1. Combine pickled green beans, pickled asparagus, cauliflower, field salad, watercress, and young dandelion centers
2. Salt and mix well
3. Add wine vinegar and good oil
4. Arrange hard-boiled eggs sliced around the salad
5. Garnish with sliced and rinsed bacon

title: Vodnice (Turnip)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- turnip
- butter
- sugar
- caramelized sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the turnip, slice into discs, place in a pot, pour water over it, let it come to a boil, strain the water.
2. Place the turnip on a baking dish with fresh butter, salt it, add a good piece of sugar, and braise until soft; pay attention that the discs do not break.
3. Strain pale roux through a sieve into it and cook.
4. Thus prepared turnip is usually served around beef.
5. If the turnip is to be darker, add a piece of caramelized sugar.

title: Vodnice (Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- fresh butter
- sugar
- mutton broth
- flour
- white grated ginger
- new spices

## Directions

1. Slice peeled turnips into rounds and wash them in cold water
2. Put a heaping spoonful of fresh butter and a teaspoon of sugar in a pot and let it brown slightly
3. Now put the turnips in it and let them braise, basting with mutton broth and stirring frequently and carefully
4. Make a brown roux, pour in mutton broth, mix it well, pour it over the turnips
5. Season with white grated ginger and a little new spices and let it braise until tender

title: Vodnice (Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled turnips
- fresh butter
- sugar
- mutton broth
- white grated ginger
- allspice

## Directions

1. Slice peeled turnips into rounds and rinse in cold water
2. Place a heaping spoonful of fresh butter and a teaspoon of sugar on a pan and let it brown to golden
3. Add the turnips and braise them, basting with mutton broth and stirring frequently and carefully
4. Make a brown roux, mix it with mutton broth, pour it over the turnips, season with white grated ginger and a bit of allspice, and braise until tender

title: Vodnice - jiný způsob (Turnip - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton or pork meat
- water
- vegetables
- turnip
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- pale roux

## Directions

1. Clean mutton or pork meat and cook in just enough water as is needed; add all vegetables to the sauce.
2. Meanwhile, peel the turnip, slice into leaves, let it come to a boil, strain the water; salt and pepper the turnip.
3. Add only a small piece of sugar so it is not bitter.
4. Cook for a while, then add pale roux and meat, and cook everything for a while before serving.

title: Vodnice na kyselo (Sour Turnip)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- turnip
- caraway seeds
- salt
- wine vinegar
- lard or pork fat
- onion
- flour
- beef broth or water

## Directions

1. Peel the turnip and grate on a cabbage grater, place in a container with caraway seeds, salt and water mixed with wine vinegar; cook until soft.
2. When cooked, place separately on a small baking dish diced lard or pork fat with finely chopped onion and fry; add flour as needed and fry.
3. Add the greens, mix; if thick, pour beef broth or water, and cook for a while.
4. When there is no head cabbage, it is good with pork, partridges or pheasants.

title: Vodnice na Kyselo (Sour Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled turnips
- sugar
- butter or lard
- onion
- caraway
- salt
- flour
- wine vinegar
- roasted sausage or young pork

## Directions

1. Peel turnips, cut into slices and then into strips
2. Pour boiling water over them, cover and let sit briefly
3. In a pan, brown a teaspoon of sugar in butter or lard until golden
4. Add drained turnips, finely diced onion, caraway, and salt
5. Braise, then dust with flour and braise a bit longer
6. Pour wine vinegar over it and let it rest in warmth briefly
7. Transfer to a serving dish and serve with roasted sausage or young pork

title: Vodnice Rozmichangá (Mashed Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- pepper
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cook turnips in water; when it begins to boil, drain and replace with fresh hot water, as turnips can sometimes be bitter
2. Add a little salt and a teaspoon of sugar, cover and cook until tender
3. Drain all water and mash the turnips with a wooden spoon
4. Add a little more salt if needed, add a spoonful of good butter, a bit of pepper, and two or three tablespoons of good thick sweet cream
5. Let it rest a while and serve hot

title: Vodní Kroupová Polévka (Water Barley Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams barley
- water
- butter
- 2 liters boiling water
- salt
- green parsley
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the barley in water with butter
2. Add 2 liters boiling water
3. Season with salt
4. Add green parsley and 2 egg yolks

title: Voňavé oříšky (Fragrant Nuts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- nuts
- sugar
- jasmine extract
- reseda extract or similar

## Directions

1. When sugaring the nuts, add jasmine extract, reseda, and similar fragrant liqueurs to the sugar, which will give the nuts a very pleasant and sweet fragrance

title: Voňavé pokroutky (Fragrant Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine pounded sugar
- reseda, jasmine, musk or other pleasant-smelling essence

## Directions

1. Add a few drops of reseda, jasmine, musk or other pleasant-smelling essence to fine pounded sugar
2. Prepare everything together as with other brittle pieces and arrange as usual

title: Vsazená vejce (Baked Eggs in Muffin Tin)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Eggs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place butter in a muffin tin and heat it.
2. When hot, break an egg into each cup.
3. Salt the eggs.
4. Leave them in the oven until the yolks are set.
5. Do not allow them to overcook, as they will not be tasty.
6. Usually served on lentils or with beefsteaks.

title: Vsazená Vejce (Baked Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Place butter on a muffin pan; when hot, beat one egg into each indentation.
2. Salt them and leave until the yolk sets; do not let them overcook as they will not be palatable.
3. Usually served on lentils or with beefsteaks.

title: Vsazená Vejce se Sardelemi (Baked Eggs with Sardines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Clean the sardines and mash them with butter.
2. Place a little butter with sardines in each indentation of a muffin pan.
3. When melted, place one egg in each indentation and leave until the yolk sets.
4. Alternatively, place butter in the indentations, add eggs, and place sardine strips on top.
5. Sour cucumbers are usually served with these eggs.

title: Vydra (Otter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- otter
- 2 liters vinegar
- several onions
- dough
- marjoram
- sage
- half a lemon, sliced into leaves
- several cloves
- pepper
- butter
- lemon leaves
- bay leaf
- spices
- salt
- mushrooms, grated

## Directions

1. Cut off the head, feet, and tail; soak the meat in water for half an hour
2. Bake on a baking sheet and baste with marinade made from 2 liters of vinegar, several onions, dough, marjoram, sage, half a lemon sliced into leaves, several cloves, and pepper
3. After the meat has been marinated for 2 days, brown onions in butter
4. Add several lemon leaves, bay leaf, pepper, spices, salt, and several spoonfuls of vinegar
5. Baste the roast with this mixture
6. When it begins to turn red, add 1 1/4 liters of wine and as much vinegar
7. Add roux and grated mushrooms
8. Strain and add lemon juice with potatoes

title: Vyklopování rosolu z formy (Unmolding Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- set aspic in mold
- warm water

## Directions

1. Turn the mold with set aspic onto a plate
2. Surround the mold with a cloth dipped in warm water
3. In a few moments slowly pull the mold upward and the aspic will remain on the plate in its original shape
4. If any liquid melts during unmolding, remove it with a straw and discard
5. Molds can be greased with almond oil before pouring aspic, but this gives the aspic an unpleasant taste

title: Vzasená vejce v polévce (Baked Eggs in Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 6 tablespoons soup
- Butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Mix well 6 egg yolks, 2 egg whites, and 6 tablespoons of soup.
2. Strain and pour into greased small molds.
3. Place in the oven until the mixture sets.
4. Turn out onto a plate and serve with soup.

title: Vánočka (Christmas Twist)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 small cups flour
- half pound butter
- two whole eggs or four yolks
- 1 small cup sugar dissolved in milk
- grated lemon zest
- salt
- ground cloves or ground and sifted anise
- piece of pressed yeast, dissolved in sour cream or milk
- 1 small cup sultanas
- half small cup chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Put 8 small cups flour into a bowl, crumble half pound butter in it
2. Beat in two whole eggs or four yolks, add 1 small cup sugar dissolved in milk, a little grated lemon zest, salt, ground cloves or ground and sifted anise, a piece of pressed yeast dissolved in sour cream or milk and make the dough so thick as for dumplings
3. Beat it so long until it does not stick to the bowl or spoon, cover it with a warmed cloth, let it rise
4. Then add 1 small cup sultanas and half small cup chopped almonds
5. Then take the dough out onto a floured board, knead it again, divide it into four larger and five smaller pieces, let them rise a little more, then roll them out
6. From the first four make not too long a braid, place it on a sheet on paper brushed with butter
7. Make then a braid from three smaller pieces, place it straight on the first, press it on the ends
8. Finally twist two smallest pieces, place them on top of the second braid, arrange it nicely, press the ends together
9. Let the Christmas twist rise in warmth, then brush it with beaten egg and bake in moderate oven
10. If the Christmas twist is more risen, it must be baked at stronger heat
11. If you put whole eggs into the batter, it is more moist; with only yolks it is more porous; whole eggs are usually put into the Christmas twist

title: Vídenské řízky (Vienna Schnitzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlets from the leg
- salt
- flour
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- clarified butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cut neat cutlets from the veal leg
2. Beat each one nicely with a wooden mallet
3. Salt it
4. Coat in flour
5. Dip in beaten egg
6. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs
7. Fry in hot clarified butter or lard until golden
8. Arrange on a plate and surround with sliced lemon
9. Salted cutlets may also be coated only in flour and fried in hot butter or lard
10. You may also use half butter and half lard

title: Vídeňský dort (Viennese Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams finely crushed sifted sugar
- eight egg yolks
- four whole eggs
- finely cut lemon zest
- 375 grams fine potato flour
- 375 grams butter
- stiff snow made from eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 375 grams crushed sifted sugar and beat for at least half an hour in one direction with egg yolks and whole eggs, finely cut lemon zest, until completely thick
2. Gradually mix in 375 grams fine potato flour, 375 grams butter, and finally stiff snow made from eight egg whites
3. Make three cakes about half a centimeter thick from this dough
4. Bake slowly on a buttered sheet
5. Turn out of the pan, and when cool
6. Coat with various preserves and layer together
7. On top coat with some optional glaze

title: Víno pampeliškové (Dandelion Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- dandelion flowers
- water
- white sugar
- yellow sugar
- oranges
- lemons

## Directions

1. Chop a full basket of dandelion flowers
2. Place in a pot and pour five gallons of cold water over them
3. Boil for one hour
4. Let stand for 12 hours or overnight
5. Strain
6. Add seven pounds of white sugar and five pounds of yellow sugar to the liquid
7. Add the finely chopped flesh from half a dozen oranges and half a dozen lemons from which you have removed all the rind and white pith
8. Mix well and when the sugar has dissolved, pour into jugs to ferment
9. When fermented, cork and store

title: Víno z Březové Šťávy (Birch Sap Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Birch sap
- 200 grams of sugar per liter
- 3-4 full spoons of yeast (per 24 liters of sap)
- 4 liters of good wine (per 24 liters of sap)
- 1/8 of a wheel of sliced lemons without seeds (per 24 liters of sap)

## Directions

1. Boil birch sap for half an hour, add 200 grams of sugar per liter, strain and let cool.
2. Pour into a barrel, then to every 24 liters of sap add 3-4 full spoons of yeast, 4 liters of good wine, and 1/8 of a wheel of sliced lemons without seeds.
3. Let the sap ferment and seal the barrel tightly.
4. After four weeks transfer to bottles, cork them well and seal, and let rest peacefully for at least two to three months before drinking, which must then happen fairly quickly as it does not keep long.
5. The more sugar and wine are added, the longer the wine keeps.

title: Víno z Jahod (Strawberry Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 liters of beautiful strawberries
- 12 liters of cold water
- 4 kilograms of sugar
- 8 liters of apple must
- thin lemon peel
- 3 decagrams of ground wine stone
- 2 liters of lukewarm water with 1.5 kilograms of dissolved sugar
- 1 liter of strong liquor

## Directions

1. Crush 12 liters of strawberries
2. Pour 12 liters of cold water over them
3. Let stand for 24 hours
4. Strain and add 4 kilograms of sugar, 8 liters of apple must, thin lemon peel, and 3 decagrams of ground wine stone
5. Pour everything into a barrel, filling it over halfway
6. Close with a cork with a small hole and let stand for 4 weeks in a lukewarm place so the wine ferments completely
7. After this time, add 2 liters of lukewarm water in which 1.5 kilograms of sugar has been dissolved and close the barrel again
8. After about 8 weeks, pour off the clear wine, add 1 liter of strong liquor, and pour back into a cleaned barrel
9. Let lie in the cellar for two months, then pour into a smaller barrel so it is full
10. After 3-4 years, draw into bottles

title: Výhodné vaření zlojovatělých zemáků (Advantageous Cooking of Sprouted Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Sprouted potatoes
- Salt
- Parsley
- Water

## Directions

1. Partially peel the potatoes in small strips.
2. Place in salted water with parsley.
3. Cook slowly, but do not fully cook through.
4. Drain and allow to finish cooking in steam.

title: Výtečný Punč (Excellent Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters water
- 1 kilogram sugar
- juice of 3 lemons
- 1 liter good rum
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 2 liters of water with 1 kilogram of sugar several times
2. Skim off the foam
3. Add the juice of 3 lemons
4. Add 1 liter of good rum and a little sugar
5. Mix everything together
6. Let it rest and serve at table

title: Větrné Paštičky (Peppermint Paste)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- peppermint oil

## Directions

1. Cook sugar to the grain stage
2. Add a few drops of peppermint oil
3. Proceed as with the previous pastes

title: Větrové pokroutky (Airy Brittle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fine white sugar
- water
- peppermint oil

## Directions

1. Pound fine white sugar
2. Dissolve it in water and cook it, stirring constantly, until it thickens completely
3. Add a few drops of peppermint oil
4. Mix everything again and let it thicken further
5. Pour onto paper and when hardened, carefully peel off and cut into small pieces

title: Wafers (Oplatky)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram white flour
- 120 grams ground sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 4 egg yolks
- thick sweet cream as needed

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, butter, egg yolks, and enough sweet cream to form batter like cake batter
2. Oil wafer iron
3. Make wafers and bake on both sides until golden
4. Sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon
5. Place second wafer on top in form, press down, and hold briefly over heat
6. Store in dry place for long preservation

title: White Walnuts (Bilé ořechy)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- largest unripe walnuts
- fresh water
- soft water
- alum
- lemon juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select the largest unripe walnuts
2. Peel the green shell down to the white interior
3. Place in fresh water
4. Boil soft water with a small amount of alum and when boiling, add the walnuts
5. Constantly press them so they soften
6. After a short time, remove the walnuts and place in fresh water to which lemon juice has been added
7. Let everything cool
8. Remove the walnuts, place in a vessel, pour over with dissolved sugar, and let sit at least a day
9. On the second day, pour off the sugar, cook it thicker, skim foam, let cool and immediately pour off the sugar again
10. Repeat this same process for two more days
11. On the third day, cook the sugar to complete thickness together with the walnuts, repeating several times on the last day
12. When everything has cooled, place in jars

title: Yolk Tarts (Žloutkové koláčky)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight tablespoons cream
- two pieces pressed yeast
- one teaspoon sugar
- four tablespoons flour for starter
- one cup fresh butter
- two tablespoons sugar
- zest grated from half a lemon
- ground flower
- four eggs
- four egg yolks
- two cups sifted flour
- jam for filling

## Directions

1. Heat eight tablespoons of cream; when lukewarm, add two pieces of pressed yeast and one teaspoon sugar and mix well
2. Add four tablespoons flour to make a thin starter dough
3. Put one cup fresh butter in a deep bowl, add two tablespoons sugar, grated zest from half a lemon, and a pinch of ground flower
4. Cream well until foamy
5. Add four eggs one by one, mixing well after each
6. Add four egg yolks the same way, one by one
7. Add the risen starter dough and add a good two cups of sifted flour
8. If there is too much flour, the tarts will be tough; if too little, they will be loose
9. Beat the dough well and let rise, but not in too warm a place
10. When risen, make small thin tarts and place on a buttered baking sheet
11. Brush with beaten egg, place jam on top, let rise and bake
12. Sprinkle with sugar when baked

title: Zadélávané skopové maso s rýží (Stewed Mutton with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton meat, preferably from ribs
- vegetables
- two pounds of meat yields one cup of rice
- rice
- hot water
- butter
- broth from cooking the meat
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut mutton meat, preferably from ribs, into larger pieces and cook it with vegetables as for beef until tender.
2. For two pounds of meat, take one cup of rice, pick it over several times in hot water, pour broth in which you cooked the meat over it, and cook the rice until tender.
3. Then make a light butter roux, thicken the remaining broth with it, place the cooked meat on a shallow pan, pour the thickened broth over it, add the cooked rice to it, season with spices and let it simmer a little longer.
4. Then place the rice on a platter and top it with the cooked meat.

title: Zadélávaué žáby (Frogs à la Poulette)
body:
## Ingredients

- frog hind legs
- fresh water
- salt
- butter
- lemon juice
- flour
- broth
- egg yolks
- parsley

## Directions

1. Only the hind legs of frogs marked with a green stripe on the back are suitable for consumption
2. Frog legs are cut off, stripped and soaked in fresh water for twenty-four hours
3. Cleaned legs are blanched several times in hot salted water, then placed in a saucepan with a piece of fresh butter, season with lemon juice and let simmer for a while
4. Then thicken them with a pale roux, let them stew until tender and before serving bind with two or three egg yolks thinned with a little broth, add a tablespoon of chopped green parsley, pour into a platter and serve

title: Zadělané Husí Drobečky s Knedličky (Goose Giblets with Quennelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose giblets
- Vegetables
- One large tablespoon butter
- Three eggs
- Lemon peel, finely chopped
- Goose liver, grated or chopped
- Salt
- Cream
- Grated bread or crackers
- Garlic and marjoram (optional)

## Directions

1. Salt and cook the goose giblets with vegetables like beef meat until tender.
2. Stir one large tablespoon of butter, beat in three eggs, add a small piece of finely chopped lemon peel, grated or chopped goose liver (if using chopped liver, remove the veins), salt lightly and mix well.
3. Add several tablespoons of good cream and enough grated bread or crackers to make quennelles.
4. If desired, add a small amount of chopped garlic with marjoram to the quenelle mixture and set aside.
5. Put a piece of fresh butter in a small pot and make a light roux, mix it with half of the strained broth from the giblets, and pour the other half of the broth also into the pot and let it boil.
6. Shape the mixture into round quennelles and cook them in the boiling broth.
7. When the quennelles are sufficiently cooked, add the cooked goose giblets to them, pour the reduced broth over, season lightly with nutmeg, and let simmer a little longer.
8. Remove the giblets, place them on a serving dish, arrange the quennelles around them, pour the sauce over and bring to the table.

title: Zadělané jahody (Bottled Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- sugar (equal part to strawberries)

## Directions

1. Boil an equal part of sugar with an equal part of strawberries
2. When at a full boil, add the cleaned strawberries
3. Boil everything several more times
4. Remove foam and put the preserves into containers
5. When cooled, tie with a bladder

title: Zadělané maliny (Preserved Raspberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large, red, ripe raspberries
- sugar (equal amount to raspberries)

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, large, red raspberries before they become overripe
2. Boil an equal amount of sugar to the sixth degree
3. When boiling, add the raspberries and cook several times together
4. Skim the foam from the surface and pour all onto a dish to cool
5. The next day, strain everything through a fine sieve
6. Pour off the sugar and place raspberries in jars
7. Add a little currant juice to the sugar, boil until thickened, and when warm pour over the raspberries
8. Only after cooling, seal and store in a dry, cool place

title: Zadělané skopové maso s omáčkou ze zeleniny (Stewed Mutton with Vegetable Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted mutton
- butter or lard
- parsley, finely chopped
- shallots, finely chopped
- mushrooms
- flour
- wine
- beef broth or hot water
- salt
- pepper
- meat extract
- breadcrumbs, grated

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roasted mutton into neat pieces.
2. In two tablespoons of butter or lard, stew one finely chopped parsley root, the same amount of finely chopped shallots, and four tablespoons of mushrooms.
3. Dust with a little flour, add wine and twice as much beef broth or hot water, and cook slowly for half an hour, season with salt and pepper.
4. Place the cut meat into the gravy and ensure it is very hot, but do not let it boil.
5. Add one tablespoon of flour, one bowl of hot water, and if available, one teaspoon of meat extract.
6. Arrange everything on a dish and add three tablespoons of grated breadcrumbs.

title: Zadělané telecí osrdi (Stewed Veal Pluck)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal lungs and heart
- butter
- flour
- onion
- parsley
- beef broth
- lemon zest
- sugar
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean veal lungs with heart and boil until tender
2. Let cool and slice into strips
3. Meanwhile melt 2 spoonfuls of butter on the stove
4. Add a heaping spoonful of flour and roast it slowly on mild heat with finely chopped onion until the onion turns yellow
5. Then add finely chopped parsley and mix it with good beef broth
6. Add the sliced pluck, finely chopped lemon zest, a spoonful of sugar browned until dark, and vinegar
7. Let it simmer for another half hour
8. When bringing the pluck to the table, salt it and squeeze the juice from half a lemon over it

title: Zadělávaná zvěřina (Braised Venison)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young venison
- butter
- celery
- carrot
- parsley
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- cloves
- wine
- vinegar
- sugar
- flour
- lemon
- capers

## Directions

1. Place young venison in one piece on a baking sheet with butter, celery, carrot, parsley, onion, thyme, bay leaf, and cloves
2. Pour wine over it and braise until tender
3. Remove the venison
4. To the sauce in which the venison was braised, add vinegar and sugar
5. Boil for a while
6. Add a thickened butter sauce, a piece of burnt sugar, a long-cut lemon, and capers
7. Finally place the sliced venison in the sauce

title: Zadělávaná štika (Stewed Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- roll (žemličky) or bread soaked and squeezed in milk
- fresh butter
- eggs
- lemon rind
- nutmeg flower
- white ginger

## Directions

1. Clean and scrape the pike, remove the entrails and cut it into pieces
2. From several pieces remove the bones and chop the meat finely
3. Cook the remaining pieces in salted water, cover and set aside
4. Pound the chopped meat in a mortar with two hard-boiled egg yolks and half a roll or slice of bread soaked in milk and squeezed
5. Rub a piece of fresh butter, add the mixture to it, stir in two eggs, add finely chopped lemon rind, salt, season with a little nutmeg flower and mix well
6. If too thin, add more grated roll, shape into small dumplings and cook them in the broth in which the pike was cooked
7. Brown with white butter roux, place the cooked pike into it, season with nutmeg flower and white ginger, let it boil for a minute
8. Arrange the pike in the middle of a dish, surround with dumplings, pour sauce over and serve

title: Zadělávaná štika s knedlíčky (Stuffed Pike with Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- onion
- asparagus
- broth
- butter
- pike meat
- pike innards
- soaked bread
- crayfish
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Clean the pike and cut into slices
2. Braise with onion, asparagus, broth and butter
3. Chop some pike meat and mix with butter, pike innards, and soaked bread
4. Mix thoroughly until a thick paste forms
5. Pound the mixture in a mortar and form dumplings
6. Fry half the dumplings and boil the other half in the sauce
7. Place dumplings in the center of a dish with pike around them
8. Decorate with crayfish and place a cauliflower rose in the center
9. Note: Chicken can be prepared the same way and the dumplings can be made from veal

title: Zadělávané Brzlíky (Fricasseed Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- several beautiful white sweetbreads
- salt
- butter
- onion, grated
- fresh parsley, chopped
- lemon rind, chopped finely
- butter and white flour for pale sauce
- beef broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take several beautiful white sweetbreads and boil them for about five minutes in salted water
2. Place them in cold water and let them cool
3. Cut them into quarters
4. Place them in a pot with a piece of butter
5. Add grated onion, a little chopped fresh parsley, and a piece of finely chopped lemon rind
6. Season with salt and stew for several minutes
7. Meanwhile, make a pale butter sauce in another pot, dissolve it with beef broth and cook to a smooth sauce
8. Strain the sauce with a little lemon juice
9. Pour it over the sweetbreads
10. Let simmer for about 10 minutes
11. Place on a dish and serve

title: Zadělávané jehněčí (Fricassee of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb brisket
- salt water

## Directions

1. Wash the lamb brisket well
2. Cook it in salted water for a quarter hour
3. Place in cold water and let cool

title: Zadělávané Jehněčí (Fricassee of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb front quarter
- salt
- water
- three tablespoons butter
- flour
- lamb broth
- small onion
- fresh parsley
- lemon zest
- wine
- egg yolks or two whole eggs
- cream
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash the lamb front quarter well and boil it in salted water for a quarter hour
2. Transfer to cold water, let it cool, and cut into neat pieces
3. Make a thin roux from three tablespoons butter and the same amount of flour
4. Mix it with strained lamb broth
5. Add a small grated onion, a little chopped fresh parsley, a piece of finely cut lemon zest, and a glass of wine
6. Let it simmer to a smooth sauce
7. Add the cut lamb pieces and stew until tender
8. Remove the meat with a fork onto a plate
9. Beat four egg yolks or two whole eggs with a little cream in a cup
10. Mix this into the boiling sauce
11. Add a little lemon juice and pour the sauce over the meat

title: Zadělávané karbanátky (Meatballs in Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- calf meat
- a piece of goose liver
- a piece of marrow
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- butter sauce
- burnt sugar
- good broth
- bay leaf
- a little wine
- butter dough hearts

## Directions

1. Finely chop the calf meat, a piece of goose liver and marrow, season with pepper and salt
2. Form meatballs and bake them in butter
3. Prepare the sauce: add burnt sugar, good broth, bay leaf and a little wine to butter sauce
4. Boil the sauce, pour it over the meatballs, arrange butter dough hearts around them

title: Zadělávané Kuře na Divoko (Braised Chicken Hunter Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- pepper
- celery root
- parsley root
- carrot
- new spices
- thyme
- bay leaves
- lemon peel
- juniper berries
- good wine vinegar
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare chicken as for braising in general
2. Add pepper, piece of celery root, parsley root, piece of carrot, new spices, thyme, 2 bay leaves, lemon peel, 2 juniper berries, 2 spoonfuls of good wine vinegar and 15 grams butter
3. Braise everything covered
4. Place tender chicken on dish
5. Make light roux, strain through sieve into sauce

title: Zadělávané Kuře na Divoko (Chicken Braised Wild-Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken, prepared for braising
- pepper
- a piece of celery root
- a piece of parsley root
- a piece of carrot
- new spices
- thyme
- 2 bay leaves
- lemon zest
- 2 juniper berries
- 2 tablespoons good wine vinegar
- 15 gr. (1 lot) butter
- white sugar
- burned sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken as for braising in general, add pepper, a piece of celery root, parsley root, a piece of carrot, new spices, thyme, 2 bay leaves, lemon zest, 2 juniper berries, 2 tablespoons good wine vinegar and 15 gr. (1 lot) butter.
2. Braise everything under a lid.
3. When the chicken is tender, place it on a platter and strain the pale broth through a sieve into the sauce.
4. Boil with a piece of white and burned sugar and pour over the sliced chicken.

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Celerem (Chicken Braised with Celery)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- celery
- good beef broth
- pepper
- cloves
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken as with paprika, only instead of all the vegetables, add celery cut into small slices to it and cook everything together.
2. Also cook a whole celery in good beef broth.
3. Place the chicken on a platter.
4. Push the celery with which the chicken was cooked onto the chicken.
5. Let the second celery cool, grate it on a grater and add it with pepper and cloves to the chicken.

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Citrónovou Omáčkou (Chicken Braised with Lemon Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- butter
- 1 lemon
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into pieces and braise or cook it in butter.
2. Make a pale broth, strain it through a sieve into the broth that was strained from the chicken.
3. Squeeze the juice from one lemon into this and boil for a while longer.

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Hříbky (Chicken Braised with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken, chopped and cleaned
- mushrooms
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Braise the chopped, cleaned chicken with vegetables or cook it.
2. Scrape the mushrooms, slice them thinly, and braise them salted in butter.
3. Strain the pale broth from the chicken, add the chicken to it, bring to a boil, and finally add the mushrooms braised in butter.
4. Other mushrooms may be used instead of the ones specified.

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Karfiolem (Braised Chicken with Cauliflower)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- cauliflower
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare chicken as with mushrooms
2. Cook cauliflower in broth from chicken
3. Prepare sauce as with mushrooms
4. Carefully arrange cauliflower around chicken and pour sauce over it
5. May be sprinkled with chopped parsley
6. Chicken with asparagus is prepared the same way, asparagus having been cooked in salted water

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Knedlířky (Braised Chicken with Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared chicken
- 30 grams butter
- flower
- egg yolk
- whole egg
- salt
- grated bread roll
- cold water
- beef broth
- flour
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare chicken as braised chicken with rice, make similar sauce
2. Beat 30 grams butter with a little flower, one egg yolk, one whole egg and a little salt
3. Mix in enough grated bread roll to make dumplings
4. Dip fingers in cold water, take a piece of mixture, round it and place in prepared beef broth
5. Add dumplings to chicken with sauce
6. Alternatively, beat 30 grams butter with 2 egg yolks, a little flower and salt until foamy
7. Beat 2 egg whites into stiff peaks and mix in flour to make dumplings with coffee spoon
8. Cook in beef broth and arrange around chicken on dish
9. The sauce for dumplings is very tasty when lemon juice is added

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Mušlemi (Chicken Braised with Mussels)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- 3 1/2 deciliters (jezdlík) Austrian wine mixed with water
- pepper
- new spices
- cloves
- 1 onion, sliced
- mussels
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and divide the chicken, braise it with 3 1/2 deciliters (jezdlík) of Austrian wine mixed with water, pepper, new spices, cloves and sliced onion; braise everything.
2. When the chicken is tender, place it separately on a platter.
3. Strain the pale broth into the sauce, which is then boiled with the chicken.
4. Meanwhile, clean the mussels, wash them whole with their shells in wine, and boil them in wine until they open by themselves.
5. Pour the wine in which the mussels were cooked into the sauce, arrange the chicken on a platter, mussels around it and pour the sauce over everything.
6. If the sauce is pale, burn a piece of sugar.

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Paprikou (Chicken Braised with Paprika)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- vegetables
- paprika
- salt

## Directions

1. Divide the chicken into halves or quarters, braise it with vegetables.
2. When it is tender, add to it the following sauce: Strain the pale broth that formed during braising, let it boil, strain it, place the meat in it, and boil a knife-point of paprika in the sauce.

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Rajskými Jablky (Braised Chicken with Tomatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- vegetables
- Austrian wine
- vinegar
- pepper
- new spices
- cloves
- sugar
- tomatoes or tomato preserve
- butter
- flour
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Divide chicken into quarters and braise with vegetables
2. Strain broth and add one deciliter Austrian wine, 3 spoonfuls vinegar, several peppercorns, new spices, 3 cloves, piece of sugar and 3 tomatoes or a piece of tomato preserve
3. Boil well together
4. Add light butter roux with piece of burnt sugar
5. Strain sauce over chicken
6. On dish make a wreath of fried potatoes around chicken and strain sauce over it

title: Zadělávané Kuře s Rejží (Braised Chicken with Rice)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- 1/8 kilogram rice
- water
- butter
- salt
- parsley root
- carrot
- celery
- cabbage
- flower
- flour

## Directions

1. For one chicken calculate 1/8 kilogram rice
2. Rinse rice, add water, butter and salt, braise until soft
3. Cut chicken into 4 pieces and braise in pot on butter with parsley root, carrot, celery, cabbage and flower
4. When chicken is tender, remove it and strain the broth
5. Make light roux, strain into broth, boil and add chicken
6. After cooking together a while longer, place chicken on dish and arrange rice in a wreath around it

title: Zadělávané křepelky (Stewed Quail)
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- water
- wine
- bay leaf
- onion

## Directions

1. Season quail with salt and pepper, lightly roast them in butter
2. Dust with a little flour, pour equal parts water and wine over them
3. Add one bay leaf, half an onion, salt and let braise, turning them several times
4. When the quail are tender, remove them to a plate and keep in a warm place
5. Let the sauce boil down well, then place the quail on a platter and strain the sauce over them
6. You can also prepare grouse and other game birds this way

title: Zadělávané plíčky (Braised Lungs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Lungs
- Butter
- Onion
- Vinegar
- Wine, one and a half deciliters
- Spices
- Sauce from broth
- Lemon juice
- Roasted pastries
- Roasted larded liver

## Directions

1. Cut the lungs into noodles; on a baking pan brown the chopped onion with butter, add the lungs, pour a little vinegar and one and a half deciliters of wine and season with various spices; braise until all the wine evaporates, then pour in sauce from broth, add lemon juice, place roasted pastries around and roasted larded liver on top.

title: Zadělávané Skopové s Vodnicí (Braised Mutton with Melon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- melon
- water
- mutton meat
- mutton broth
- piece of sugar
- sugar caramelized to pink

## Directions

1. Peel melon and cut into thin slices
2. Place on a baking pan, pour water over it, and let it come to a boil
3. Drain water
4. At the same time, prepare mutton meat and pour broth over the melon
5. Add a piece of sugar and braise until tender, adding more broth as needed
6. Press pale gravy through a strainer and add a piece of sugar caramelized to pink
7. Cut meat into pieces, add to the braised melon and simmer briefly

title: Zadělávané telecí maso (Braised Veal)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal neck
- Butter
- Vegetables
- Salt
- Spices
- White roux
- Lemon juice or mushrooms or parsley

## Directions

1. Rinse the meat and cook it, add a good piece of butter, various vegetables but not too much, and salt
2. When the meat is tender, cut into pieces
3. Strain the broth and make a white roux in butter, strain into the broth
4. Mix in a little ground spice and cook
5. Add lemon juice for sour sauce, or add fresh or dried finely chopped mushrooms and chopped green parsley, or use mushrooms that can be cauliflower cooked in beef broth, button mushrooms or sliced morels

title: Zadělávaný Chřest (Creamed Asparagus)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Broth
- Egg yolks
- Smoked tongue or veal roast

## Directions

1. Cut the asparagus into pieces and cook in salted water or broth
2. Before serving, remove and mix with egg yolks until evenly thickened
3. Surround with either smoked tongue or veal roast

title: Zadělávaný Kapoun s Vinní Omáčkou (Capon Braised with Wine Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 capon, divided into 4 pieces
- butter
- 2 tablespoons water
- 3 1/2 deciliters (jezdlík) Austrian wine
- salt
- carrot
- parsley
- a piece of celery
- cabbage
- capon liver, stomach and heart
- 1/8 kilo (1/8 pound) calf liver
- 2 hard eggs
- fried bread roll
- cloves
- ginger
- pepper
- 2 cloves of garlic
- marjoram
- a piece of onion
- green parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Divide the capon into 4 pieces, add butter, 2 tablespoons water, 3 1/2 deciliters (jezdlík) Austrian wine, salt, carrot, parsley, a piece of celery and cabbage; braise everything.
2. When everything is tender, make a pale broth, strain it into the sauce and boil it.
3. Cook the capon liver, stomach and heart with 1/8 kilo (1/8 pound) of calf liver, then chop everything as finely as possible with 2 hard eggs and fried bread roll; add a bit of cloves, ginger, pepper, 2 cloves of garlic, marjoram and a piece of onion to this; mix everything together and bake the mixture on a greased baking sheet, stirring carefully so it does not burn.
4. Place the baked mixture on a platter around the capon and garnish with green parsley.

title: Zadělávaný kapoun se šnekovou omáčkou (Capon with Snail Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- 5 sardels
- 30 gr. butter
- 2 cloves garlic
- clove flower
- pepper
- marjoram petals
- new spice
- half a rusk

## Directions

1. Cook the capon as for wine sauce
2. Strain the broth
3. For the sauce, take 5 sardels, clean them well and remove all bones
4. Add 30 gr. butter to them, mix well
5. Add 2 cloves grated garlic, clove flower, pepper, marjoram petals, new spice and half a grated rusk
6. Place the capon with all the mixture into part of the broth
7. If the sauce is too thick, add more broth and simmer for a while

title: Zaděláváni pokrmů do rosolu (Layering Dishes in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- aspic
- various vegetables and meats
- red beets
- eggs
- cucumbers
- beans
- parsley
- cooked tongue
- decorative leaves and flowers

## Directions

1. Layer various dishes into molds with aspic in stages
2. First layer of aspic poured into molds must be completely set before adding food items for aspic
3. Place food items on the set first layer
4. Slowly pour new layer of melted aspic on top
5. When this layer sets, repeat the process as with the first layer
6. In the first layer place various items such as red beets, eggs, cucumbers, beans, cooked parsley, and tongue arranged in a pleasing pattern
7. Use various leaves and flowers of camellias and similar for the first layer of aspic for decoration

title: Zaječí zadek s bílou omáčkou (Hare Hindquarters with White Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare hindquarters
- Bacon
- Onions
- Pepper
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Lemon peel
- Blanquette sauce
- Wine vinegar
- White flour roux
- Beef broth
- Sugar
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove all skin from the hindquarters, wash and salt them
2. Lard with bacon from both sides
3. Chop 2 onions and add pepper, thyme, bay leaf, a piece of lemon peel, and a piece of blanquette sauce
4. Moisten with 3 spoonfuls of good vinegar and braise until tender
5. Cut the hare into pieces and place on a platter
6. To the juices left in the pan, add a white roux, a little vinegar and beef broth
7. Simmer, and if the sauce is sour enough, add a piece of sugar
8. Pour the sauce over the hare and add three-quarters of a deciliter of good thick sour cream
9. Leave in a warm place for a while and continually baste with the sauce
10. Alternatively, roast the hare in the marinade liquid until almost cooked out, add the juice of one lemon and strain the remaining sauce without roux over it

title: Zajíc sekaný (Chopped Hare)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole hare
- Bacon
- Bread
- Milk
- Pepper
- Flower spice
- Ginger
- Onion
- Eggs
- Lemon peel
- Capers
- Lard or bacon
- Broth

## Directions

1. Clean the whole hare, remove all skin and trim all meat from the bones
2. Chop the bones and pound in a mortar
3. Strain the bone stock through a sieve into a container
4. Chop the meat finely and pour the bone stock onto it
5. Chop 3/8 kilo (1/4 pound) of bacon
6. Soak 2 bread rolls in milk, squeeze them out
7. Add crushed pepper, a little crushed flower spice, a little ginger, finely chopped onion, 2 raw and 2 hard chopped eggs, a piece of finely chopped lemon peel, and several chopped capers
8. Mix everything well
9. Grease the pan with lard or bacon, arrange the meat like a hare hindquarter, lard the top with bacon, moisten with broth and roast in the oven until golden
10. Serve whole or sliced into strips

title: Zajíc Sekaný (Rabbit Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from rabbit front
- Two pounds of fatter pork meat
- Lard, scraped, if the pork is not fatty
- One roll soaked in wine and well squeezed
- Half a grated roll
- One or two eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Finely chopped lemon zest
- Lard for larding
- Butter
- Onion sliced into rounds

## Directions

1. Mince the meat from the rabbit front raw with 2 pounds of fatter pork meat.
2. If the pork is not fatty, add scraped lard, then one roll soaked in wine and well squeezed, and half a grated roll, one or two eggs, necessary salt, pepper and a little finely chopped lemon zest.
3. Mix everything well, form a small rabbit from it, lard it with bacon, place on a baking pan, dot with butter and onion sliced into rounds, and roast while basting frequently.
4. The mixture may also be covered with sour cream, though in this case the lemon zest must be omitted.

title: Zajíc z hovězího masa (Hare from Beef)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- bread crumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Prepare meat as in recipe 344 for meatballs
2. Shape the mixture to resemble a hare's hindquarters
3. Coat with bread crumbs and cold lard
4. Bake in the oven, then cut into pieces

title: Zajíc zadělávaný s černou omáčkou (Hare Braised with Black Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare
- Onion
- 6-7 peppers
- New spice berries
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Lemon peel
- Wine vinegar
- Water or broth
- Flour
- Black gingerbread
- White sugar
- Burnt sugar
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Hang the hare by its hind legs
2. Make an incision around the knees and skin the hare up to the head, removing ears and eyes
3. Remove the eyes completely, cut the upper and lower lips
4. Remove the gallbladder carefully
5. Cut off the skin and small ribs from the loins on both sides so the back with spine remains
6. Set aside the liver, lungs and heart
7. Remove the lower jaw, clean it well, remove the gullet, sever the feet and shoulder blades separately, wash everything in several waters to completely remove blood
8. Put the entire front with offal in a pot with a liter of water or broth
9. Add chopped onion, 6-7 peppers, several grains of new spice, thyme, bay leaf, lemon peel, and 4 spoonfuls of good wine vinegar
10. Simmer until the meat is tender
11. Place the meat on a platter and prepare the sauce
12. Mix a roux with a piece of black gingerbread, white sugar and burnt sugar, simmer and strain
13. If not sour enough, add vinegar
14. Place the hare in this sauce, simmer briefly, then pour onto a platter and arrange nicely
15. Usually peeled potatoes are added
16. The whole hare or rabbit may be prepared this way

title: Zapečené vepřové maso (Conserved Roast Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- slaughtered pork
- salt
- caraway

## Directions

1. When the meat from the slaughtered pig has cooled, slice it into neat pieces about half an inch thick
2. Remove all fat and bones
3. Salt the meat and let it lie in salt for at least one hour
4. Take a baking pan and lay the slices side by side on it
5. Sprinkle with caraway
6. Bake without adding any water until done
7. Remove the slices and lay them on a platter again side by side
8. When everything is done, let the meat cool
9. Take a large clay pot and pack the meat tightly into it
10. Pour lard over it so that the lard reaches two inches above the meat
11. When removing it, take care not to damage the pieces that are to remain in the pot; therefore it is best to remove more meat at once and pour the remainder well with lard so that air cannot reach it

title: Zapražená omáčka (Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons dry flour
- 2 cupfuls beef broth

## Directions

1. Make a brown smooth roux from two tablespoons of butter and the same amount of dry flour
2. Add two cupfuls of beef broth and mix well

title: Zapražená omáčka (Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons dry flour
- 2 cups beef broth
- bunch of vegetables
- peppercorns
- 2 cloves

## Directions

1. Make a smooth brown roux from two tablespoons of butter and the same amount of dry flour.
2. Add two cups of beef broth and mix well.
3. Let it simmer well and strain into a bowl.
4. This sauce is served on its own or serves as a base for other sauces.
5. A bunch of vegetables, several peppercorns and about two cloves may also be simmered in it.

title: Zavařená Jablka (Preserved Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, juicy apples, preferably sweet rather than sour
- sugar
- water
- cinnamon bark

## Directions

1. First the apples must be beautiful, juicy, preferably sweet rather than sour.
2. Peel them, cut into quarters, remove the cores well and anything that is spoiled; if the apple is quite large, cut it into more pieces and immediately throw them into cold water so they do not brown.
3. When finished with this, put a pot on the stove, add water according to the amount of fruit and for one gallon of water add about one quart of sugar and let it boil.
4. Then skim the foam and add the fruit and add a few pieces of broken cinnamon bark and let them boil until they can be easily pierced with a fork.
5. Then immediately pour into prepared jars which you have previously washed well with hot water and then cold water.
6. Most importantly, the lids must be well washed, particularly those that have been used before. Boil them in soda water, then rinse in several clean waters and if anything is stuck on, scrape it well.
7. Also each time when filling use new rubber strips.

title: Zavařená Malá Rajská Jablíčka (Preserved Pear-shaped Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful small pear-shaped unripe yellow tomatoes
- sugar
- lemon rind
- ginger

## Directions

1. In the evening, pour boiling water over and peel beautiful small pear-shaped unripe yellow tomatoes, prick each one with a needle so it does not crack during boiling and add as much sugar as the tomatoes weigh.
2. In the morning pour off all the juice from them, boil until it becomes thick like a syrup.
3. Then add the tomatoes to boil in it and add a piece of lemon rind and whole ginger and let boil until the tomatoes look transparent, then place into jars and screw shut while hot.

title: Zavařená Pláňata (Preserved Crab Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- crab apples
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Crab apples are preserved during boiling in the same way as pears, however one must add somewhat more water to the sugar - one small pot of water to one quart of sugar and of course they are cooked whole, with the stems.

title: Zavařené Broskve (Preserved Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- 1 pound of white sugar
- 1.5 small pots of hot water

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches, halve them, remove the pits and place the peaches in cold water.
2. Put 1 pound of white sugar into a pot, pour 1.5 small pots of hot water on it and let it boil into a syrup.
3. Fill jars with peaches and place them into a pot with water as stated in the general rules.
4. On each layer of peaches place a layer of sugar and when the jar is full, pour the hot sugar syrup over them, screw the lids on lightly and boil for 10 minutes.

title: Zavařené Hrušky (Preserved Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- firm pears
- 1 quart of white sugar
- about 2 tablespoons of water

## Directions

1. For preserving, take firmer pears that will not fall apart.
2. Peel them, cut in half or quarters depending on size, cut out the cores and place the pears in cold water so they do not brown.
3. Put 1 quart of white sugar and about 2 tablespoons of water into a glazed pot and when it boils, add 2 quarts of pears to the syrup and let it boil slowly for several hours.
4. When the syrup has thickened, place into jars according to the instructions in the general rules.

title: Zavařené Jahody (Preserved Strawberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 quarts of strawberries
- 2 pounds of white sugar
- half a small pot of water

## Directions

1. Pick beautiful strawberries, strain through a sieve and let drain well.
2. Put 2 pounds of white sugar and half a small pot of water into a glazed pot and let it boil for about 10 minutes.
3. Then add the strawberries, let them boil for about 25 minutes and skim the foam well, but do not stir much so you do not crush them.
4. Remove the strawberries with a skimming spoon and place into jars carefully so they remain as whole as possible.
5. The juice with sugar in which they were boiled is then cooked further until it becomes thick and pour over the strawberries so the jars are nicely full.
6. Then cook further as stated in the general rules.

title: Zavařené maliny (Preserved Raspberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil as much sugar to the sixth degree as the amount of raspberries to be preserved
2. Add the raspberries to the sugar
3. Turn more frequently but do not stir
4. Boil until the sugar is completely thickened
5. Place in jars

title: Zavařené Zralé Okurky (Sweet Pickled Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-ripened (yellow) cucumbers
- salt
- water
- 1 quart vinegar
- 1 pint hot water
- half a quart of sharp vinegar
- 2 heaping small pots of white sugar
- 1 teaspoon of cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon of cloves
- 1 teaspoon of black pepper
- 1 teaspoon of nutmeg or nutmeg seed
- 1 handful of raisins

## Directions

1. Take well-ripened (yellow) cucumbers, peel them, remove the seeds and also the soft parts where the seeds were, cut into neat pieces and place in salted water.
2. Then scald them with boiling water and let stand until morning, then remove them and measure out 1 quart of vinegar.
3. Into a pot put 1 pint of hot water, half a quart of sharp vinegar, 2 heaping small pots of white sugar, 1 teaspoon of cinnamon, 1 teaspoon of cloves, 1 teaspoon of black pepper, 1 teaspoon of nutmeg or nutmeg seed, 1 handful of raisins, then add the cucumbers to this and boil until they can be easily pierced with a fork; then pour the half quart of vinegar into it, after which immediately place them into jars.

title: Zavařované třešně v cukru (Preserved Cherries in Sugar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cherries
- ½ kilogram sugar per 2 decagrams cherries

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from fresh cherries
2. Pound the sugar and alternate layers of cherries and sugar
3. Seal well
4. Place in boiling water for 8 minutes
5. Dry the jars or boxes and store

title: Zavařované třešně v octě (Preserved Cherries in Vinegar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sorted cherries
- good vinegar
- sugar (1/2 kilogram)
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Pour good vinegar over the sorted cherries so all are submerged
2. After two days, pour off the vinegar and boil it with 1/2 kilogram sugar, cinnamon, and several cloves
3. Strain it hot over the cherries
4. After three days, boil again, pour over the cherries
5. When it cools, tie the containers

title: Zavařované třešně v octě s vanilkou (Preserved Cherries in Vinegar with Vanilla)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 liters of cherries
- 1/2 liter of vinegar
- 1/2 kilogram of pounded sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Pour 1/2 liter of vinegar over the cherries and let stand for two days
2. On the third day, boil the poured-off vinegar with 1/2 kilogram of pounded sugar
3. Sprinkle the cherries with 1/2 kilogram sugar and let stand 24 hours in the cold
4. Then boil gently in the vinegar, remove with a spoon
5. Add vanilla to the juice, boil it, pour over the cherries and seal the jars well

title: Zavařované Višně (Preserved Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the cherries and preserve them like plums, however add less water to the sugar because they are juicier.

title: Zavařování borůvek (Preserved Blueberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams of sugar
- water
- 1/2 kilogram of sorted blueberries
- rum

## Directions

1. Boil 375 grams of sugar with water until bubbles begin to appear
2. Add 1/2 kilogram of sorted blueberries and boil quickly without allowing them to break apart
3. The next day, pour off the juice and boil it until thick, so that it drips slowly from a spoon
4. Put the blueberries into jars, pour the boiled sugar over them
5. Cover with paper soaked in rum and store sealed

title: Zavařování brusnic s jablky (Preserved Cranberries with Apples)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries
- sugar (1 kilogram per 8 liters of cranberries)
- cinnamon
- apples (20 of better quality)

## Directions

1. Sort and clean the cranberries, blanch them, drain the water
2. For 8 liters of cranberries, add 1 kilogram of sugar and several pieces of cinnamon
3. Boil, remove the foam, and when individual berries begin to burst, remove them and cover with half the juice
4. Prepare 20 better quality apples

title: Zavařování malin (Preserving Raspberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries, not overripe
- ½ kilogram sugar
- ¼ liter water

## Directions

1. Pick raspberries during dry weather immediately after picking
2. Boil sugar in water, add raspberries
3. Cook slowly over gentle heat
4. Cover with a sieve on a plate and let stand until the next day
5. Carefully place raspberries in jars
6. Strain the boiled sugar through a sieve
7. Boil until completely thickened and pour the cooled syrup over the raspberries
8. Place the sealed jars in a cool place

title: Zavařování ryvízu (Preserving Currants)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kilograms currant berries
- ½ liter wine vinegar
- ½ kilogram sugar
- 15 grams (1 lot) cinnamon
- 8 cloves

## Directions

1. Wash the currant berries and allow to dry on a sieve
2. Boil wine vinegar with sugar, cinnamon, and cloves until thickened
3. Cook the currants in this mixture and pour into a stone jug
4. The next day, pour off the syrup, boil it again, and pour back over the currants
5. Repeat this for two days
6. Cover the jug well, tie it securely, and store in a cool place

title: Zavařování ryvízu v cukru (Preserving Currants in Sugar)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram cleaned and prepared currants
- ½ kilogram sugar
- ¼ liter water

## Directions

1. Boil sugar in water until it threads, then add the currants
2. Cook until the berries do not burst, stirring gently
3. Remove with a spoon
4. Boil the syrup once more
5. Pour over the currants
6. Place in jars and seal well

title: Zbytky drůbeže s podpoušťkou (Salmi of Fowl)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- water or beef broth
- lemon zest
- whole onion
- thyme
- bay leaves
- pepper
- allspice
- salt
- pale roux
- browned sugar
- white wine
- leftover fowl
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Put equal parts vinegar and water or beef broth in a bowl
2. Add a piece of sliced lemon zest, a whole onion, a piece of thyme, a few bay leaves, a little pepper, allspice and salt
3. Let it boil together
4. Then mix in a pale roux, a piece of browned sugar and a little white wine
5. Let it boil a bit more and strain it over neatly cut pieces of leftover fowl arranged on a baking sheet
6. Let the leftovers heat in the sauce
7. Arrange them on a dish, pour the sauce over them and surround with roasted or fried potatoes

title: Zelená naložená rajská jablíčka (Green Tomato Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon tomatoes sliced into circles
- 2 quarts vinegar
- 1 quart sugar
- 1/4 cupful mustard seed
- 2 teaspoons whole allspice
- 1 teaspoon ground pepper
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 12 small onions, sliced

## Directions

1. Cook all ingredients together for 10 minutes
2. Tomatoes must be completely green
3. Place in earthenware jars
4. Seal well so that air cannot reach them

title: Zelené broskve (Green Peaches)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Green nearly developed peaches with pits still not hardened
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Select green nearly developed peaches whose pits have not yet hardened and place in boiling water.
2. Remove them quickly, wipe with cloth, rinse, and prick them.
3. On the second day, place them in dissolved sugar, boil them with it, repeating this for several days, so that the syrup is boiled first and when it is boiling, the peaches are added to it.
4. When the peaches are properly candied, remove from heat and place in special containers; when cooled, bind them and place in a dry, cold location.

title: Zelené broskve - Jiný způsob (Green Peaches - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful green peaches
- Sugar
- Soft water

## Directions

1. Select beautiful green peaches, prick them in several places with a needle, place them in boiling water, leave them for a while, remove them, clean them and remove the fuzz.
2. Then place them in soft water in which they cook until soft, remove them, place in cold water to cool.
3. When dried, pour thin dissolved sugar over them.
4. For three days, drain the sugar and each time boil it thicker and thicker, also cooking it with the peaches, until on the sixth day they are completely candied and dry, then place them in containers.

title: Zelené Fazole (Green Beans)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Young green bean pods
- Salt
- Beef broth or water
- Sugar
- Spices
- Pale roux

## Directions

1. Wash the green bean pods and remove the tough strings from both sides of the pod
2. Slice the pods diagonally into thin strips
3. Place on a baking pan, salt, moisten with beef broth or water, add a piece of sugar and a little spice, and stew
4. Strain the pale roux
5. May be served as a standalone dish or around meat

title: Zelené Fazole s Jazykem (Green Beans with Tongue)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Green bean pods
- Salt
- Butter
- Parsley
- Butter sauce
- Vinegar
- Spices
- Egg yolks
- Cream
- Smoked tongue

## Directions

1. Remove the tough parts from the beans and slice diagonally
2. Cook in salted water until tender, then strain onto a baking pan
3. Simmer with butter, chopped parsley, butter sauce, vinegar, pepper and spices
4. Before serving, pour over egg yolks mixed with cream and surround with smoked tongue

title: Zelené Fazole, Jiný Způsob (Green Beans, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Green bean pods
- Butter
- Pepper
- Wine vinegar
- Salt
- Beef broth
- Roux
- Sugar
- White sugar
- Sour cream or lemon juice
- Boiled pork or veal cutlets

## Directions

1. Remove the tough strings from the green beans, slice diagonally into thin strips or into cubes, or leave whole
2. Place on a baking pan with fresh butter, pepper, wine vinegar, salt, and beef broth as needed, and stew until tender
3. If needed, moisten with good beef broth
4. When tender, add roux and a piece of white sugar and simmer
5. May also add good sour cream or juice from half a lemon
6. May be served with boiled pork or veal cutlets

title: Zelené srstky (Green Gooseberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, large, not fully ripe gooseberries
- Fresh water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, large, but not fully ripe gooseberries and clean them.
2. Carefully remove the seeds with straws and place them in fresh water.
3. Mash them thoroughly until they soften.
4. Heat the water with the gooseberries, but do not boil; as soon as the gooseberries float to the surface, remove from heat and cover the pot so they finish cooking away from the stove.
5. When cooled, place in cold water and then into clarified boiled sugar; cover again and let sit quietly.
6. On the second day, drain the sugar, boil it separately, and when it has boiled well, add the gooseberries to it, boil everything again, cover and let sit quietly.
7. Repeat the same on the third day.
8. On the fourth day, boil the sugar separately again until it thickens.
9. On the fifth day, boil it again longer, then add the gooseberries to it, boil with it and skim off any foam that rises, as with each previous boiling.
10. Place the prepared gooseberries in containers and when cooled, bind them well with bladder.

title: Zelený Hrách (Green Peas)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas from young pea pods
- Butter
- Sugar
- Salt
- Beef broth or water
- Pale roux
- Burnt sugar (if needed)
- Any fried fat

## Directions

1. Remove the peas from the young pea pods and wash them
2. Place on a baking pan with a piece of butter, sugar and salt, and stew until tender
3. If needed, moisten with beef broth or water
4. When tender, mix with pale roux
5. If it should not be pale, add a piece of burnt sugar
6. Pour and place any fried fat on top

title: Zelený hrách s mrkví (Green Peas with Carrots)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young carrots
- peas
- butter
- meat broth
- thickening agent (jíška)
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Clean young carrots and slice into rounds
2. Place carrots and peas on a baking sheet
3. Add butter and pour meat broth in which thickening agent has been mixed
4. Add chopped parsley and braise for a while
5. If carrots are older, cook them alone for an hour first, then add to peas

title: Zelený Hrách se Slaninou (Green Peas with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked meat
- butter
- flour
- broth
- peas
- onion
- parsley
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut smoked meat into cubes
2. Fry briefly in butter
3. Remove the meat
4. To the remaining fat add flour and broth
5. When this has simmered briefly, add the peas with the smoked meat
6. Add onion, parsley, and a little pepper
7. Let simmer gently for a while

title: Zelený Hrách, Jiný Způsob (Green Peas, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Green peas
- Butter
- Broth
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mash the green peas in cold water with a piece of butter
2. Strain the water
3. Place the peas on a baking pan and moisten with broth
4. Stew until tender
5. Before serving, add a piece of butter mixed with flour and three egg yolks

title: Zelený hrášek (Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of shelled green peas
- half a small cup of water
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of sugar
- half a tablespoon of good butter
- half a tablespoon of flour
- a small cup of cream or good milk
- a small amount of pepper

## Directions

1. Take a quart of shelled green peas and add half a small cup of water, a pinch of salt and a pinch of sugar and let it cook until soft, which takes about half an hour if it is true sweet and young peas.
2. If the water boils away, add a little, but not much at once.
3. When soft, rub half a tablespoon of good butter with half a tablespoon of flour and mix it into the boiling peas.
4. When it thickens, add a small cup of cream or good milk, season lightly with pepper and remove from heat.
5. It is especially good served with stewed chicken.

title: Zelený Hrášek (Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart shelled green peas
- 1/2 cup water
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Take one quart of shelled green peas and add 1/2 cup water, a little salt and a little sugar and let it cook until soft, which takes about half an hour if it is true sweet and young peas.
2. If the water boils away, add a little more, but not much at once.
3. When soft, mix in 1/2 spoonful of good butter with 1/2 spoonful of flour and blend into the peas.

title: Zelnice (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage water
- sour cabbage
- water
- egg yolks
- eggs
- butter
- cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Put into a pot a quart of cabbage water, a pint of sour cabbage and a quart of water and let cook
2. Whisk in a small pot six egg yolks or 3 eggs with a piece of butter and a glass of good cream
3. Properly whisk this into the soup, pour into a bowl and serve
4. Those who like thick soup can thicken the cream and egg yolks with a spoonful of nice white flour
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Zelnice čili Couračka (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cabbage water
- 1 pint sour cabbage
- 1 quart water
- 6 egg yolks or 3 eggs
- A piece of butter
- 1 cup good sour cream
- Optional: 1 spoon white flour for thicker soup

## Directions

1. Put cabbage water, sour cabbage, and water in a pot and cook
2. Beat 6 egg yolks or 3 eggs with a piece of butter and 1 cup sour cream in a small bowl
3. Optionally, add 1 spoon of white flour to the cream and eggs for a thicker soup
4. Temper the soup by slowly adding it to the boiling liquid, stirring constantly
5. Pour into a serving dish

title: Zelná Polévka na Jiný Způsob (Cabbage Soup, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 quarts water
- 1 cup sour cabbage
- 1 cup sour cream
- 2 spoons white flour
- 5 or 6 eggs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Boil water with sour cabbage for 15 minutes
2. Mix sour cream with 2 spoons white flour and beat well
