3. Pour the mixture into the soup and stir
4. Season with salt
5. Break eggs one at a time and drop them into the soup
6. Let simmer for 3 minutes and the soup is ready

title: Zelní polévka (Cabbage Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh head of cabbage
- Salt
- A little flower (kvetu)
- 15 grams (1 lot) sugar
- Lard, butter, or pork cracklings (škvarky)
- Roux (jíškou)
- Potatoes or bread

## Directions

1. Cut a fresh head of cabbage into pieces, salt it
2. Add a little flower and 15 grams of sugar
3. Cook with lard, butter, or pork cracklings until the cabbage falls apart
4. Brown with roux
5. Either strain the soup and add peeled potatoes, or don't strain and add cut bread

title: Zelní polévka (Cabbage Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh head of cabbage
- salt
- a little cauliflower
- 15 grams (1 lot) sugar
- lard, butter, or cracklings

## Directions

1. Cut a fresh head of cabbage, salt it, add a little cauliflower and 15 grams (1 lot) of sugar
2. Cook it either with lard, butter, or cracklings until the cabbage is fully cooked
3. Thicken with roux, then either strain the soup and add peeled potatoes, or leave it unstrained and add sliced bread

title: Zelníky (Cabbage Buns)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram flour
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) butter
- 4 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- sugar
- 3 dekagrams yeast
- milk
- sauerkraut

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, rub the butter, add eggs, 2 egg yolks, some ground sugar so the dough is not bitter
2. Add 3 dekagrams of yeast to the dough and milk as needed
3. When the dough has risen halfway, cut off a piece of dough, roll it out, make a small triangle, place a piece of ordinary sauerkraut in the middle, fold the triangle over it
4. Place the buns on a greased baking sheet in the oven until they turn red, or drop them into boiling water, cook them and butter them

title: Zelníky (Cabbage Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-pound cabbage head
- salt
- butter (about the size of a chicken egg)
- flour
- water (about half a cup)
- sugar (1 tablespoon)
- black pepper (1 tablespoon)
- dough (same as for kolaches)

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage well
2. Chop it finely on a vegetable grater
3. Salt lightly and squeeze out the water
4. Place on a baking pan in the oven to braise until soft
5. Make a roux with butter and flour
6. Add about half a cup of water to the sauce
7. Add the cabbage to the sauce
8. Add sugar and black pepper, and more salt if needed
9. Prepare dough as for kolaches
10. When risen, roll out and wrap the cabbage filling
11. Bake well, testing with a fork after about half an hour—if the fork comes out clean of dough, it is done

title: Zelí s hrachem (Cabbage with Peas)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- zelí (cabbage)
- voda (water)
- sůl (salt)
- sádlo vepřové nebo husí (pork or goose lard)
- hrách (peas)
- polévka masová (meat broth)
- upražená houska, jemně rozstrouhána (toasted bread crumbs)
- uzené maso, šunka nebo uzenky (smoked meat, ham or sausages)

## Directions

1. Zelí uvaří se ve vodě, voda se sleje, přidá se soli, sádla buď vepřového neb husího, vše se promíchá a poznovu povaří
2. Hrách měkce uvařený poleje se polévkou masovou, s kterou ještě chvíli se povaří
3. Načež se sýtem protlačuje, dá na teplá kamna a na mísu nandaný, posype se upraženou houskou, jemně rozstrouhanou
4. Zelí se nese na zvláštní míse, taktéž i hrách
5. K jídlu tomu přidává se uzené maso, šunka nebo uzenky

title: Zelí se šunkou (Cabbage with Ham)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- máslo (butter)
- zelí byronské (Byron cabbage)
- jablka míšeňská (Meissen apples)
- šunka (ham)
- máslo nebo vepřové sádlo (butter or pork lard)
- smetana kyselá tučná (sour fatty cream)

## Directions

1. Pekáček vytře se máslem, nandá se do něho vrstva zelí Byrového
2. Na ně dají se na koláčky rozkrájená jablka míšeňská, na tyto dá se šunka na plátky nařezaná, což se vrstevnatě tak dlouho opakuje, až pekáček neb forma naplněna je
3. Na každou vrstvu dá se důstatek másla neb vepřového sádla, vrchem pak naleje se kyselé tučné smetany
4. Vše se dokonale propeče a na stůl různě okrášlené dává

title: Zelí ve víně upravené (Cabbage Prepared in Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- zelí (cabbage)
- voda slená (salted water)
- sádlo vepřové nebo husí (pork or goose lard)
- bílé mléko (white milk)

## Directions

1. Zelí uvaří se ve vodě slané a omaštěné (sádlem vepřovým nebo husím) do měkká
2. Voda se slije, zelí dobrým bílým mlékem zaleje a poznovu dusí
3. Zelí takto upravené přidává se k bažantům, koroptvím a zvěřině

title: Zemáková nádivka (Potato Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- roasted sausage stuffing

## Directions

1. Peel raw potatoes, cut into cubes, wash and dry them
2. Braise with butter, parsley and onion
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Mix with roasted sausage stuffing
5. Cool completely before using for stuffing

title: Zemáková omáčka s vinem (Potato Sauce with Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled boiled potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- flour
- broth with wine

## Directions

1. Slice several peeled boiled potatoes
2. Shake over fire several times with butter, salt, pepper, chopped parsley and chives
3. Dust with flour
4. Pour with broth mixed with wine
5. Simmer for quarter of an hour

title: Zemáková omáčka se smetanou (Potato Sauce with Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- lemon peel
- juice
- half lemon
- quarter liter sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut cooked potatoes into slices
2. Braise on a pan with a piece of butter, salt, pepper, lemon peel, juice and half a lemon
3. Pour over quarter liter of sour cream
4. After a short time serve the sauce in a warm dish

title: Zemáková paštika (Potato Pâté)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- bacon
- sour cream
- pepper
- chopped onion
- cooked potatoes
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut equal amounts of ham and bacon into small cubes
2. Mix with sour cream, pepper and chopped onion
3. Into a buttered mold layer alternately sliced cooked potatoes then the mixture
4. Make the last layer of ham and bacon
5. Bake for one hour

title: Zemáková polévka (Potato Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- several potatoes
- onion
- celery
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Wash and slice potatoes, boil them with onion and celery in water
2. Mash, strain the soup, add butter, salt, pepper and chopped parsley

title: Zemáková Polévka (Potato Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled potatoes
- water
- butter
- salt
- onion
- vegetable roots
- 2 egg yolks
- fried bread roll

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes, cut into pieces and cook, drain the water; mash the potatoes, add butter, salt and pour water in which onion and vegetable roots were cooked
2. Boil the soup for a quarter hour, add 2 egg yolks and pour over fried sliced bread roll

title: Zemáková Polévka (Potato Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cold potatoes
- butter
- chopped parsley
- onion
- tablespoon of flour
- broth or water
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- fried bread roll

## Directions

1. Peel cold potatoes, grate them and cook with butter, chopped parsley, onion, a tablespoon of flour, broth or water, salt and pepper; beat eggs in it and pour over fried bread roll

title: Zemákové Chrupánky (Potato Croquettes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peeled potatoes
- salt
- seasoning
- melted butter
- bread crumbs (grated)
- butter for frying
- parsley

## Directions

1. Mash the cooked peeled potatoes
2. Press through a sieve
3. Mix with salt, seasoning, and melted butter
4. When cooled, form into small elongated croquettes
5. Coat with grated bread crumbs
6. Fry in butter
7. Arrange with parsley and serve

title: Zemákové knedlíky s houskou (Potato Dumplings with Bread Roll)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram (2 pounds) cooked grated potatoes
- 3-4 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls flour
- 1 spoonful salt
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) butter
- nutmeg
- snow from egg whites
- fried bread roll cut into cubes

## Directions

1. Mix cooked grated potatoes with egg yolks, flour, salt, butter, nutmeg, and snow from egg whites
2. Make small dumplings
3. Place fried bread roll cubes in center of each
4. Cook in salted water

title: Zemákové knedlíky s pohankou (Potato Dumplings with Buckwheat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 plate cooked potatoes
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) bacon cut into cubes
- 2 eggs
- salt
- buckwheat flour

## Directions

1. Grate cooked potatoes
2. Add bacon cubes, eggs, and salt
3. Add buckwheat flour until firm dough forms
4. Make small dumplings
5. Cook in salted water until they float to surface

title: Zemákové knedlíky se slaninou (Potato Dumplings with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram grated potatoes
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) bacon cut into cubes
- 1 grated bread roll
- 1 bread roll cut into cubes
- 1 spoonful salt
- 4-5 eggs
- bread roll grated and fried in butter

## Directions

1. Fry grated potatoes in bacon cubes
2. Add grated bread roll and bread roll cubes
3. Fry a while
4. Cool
5. Add salt and beaten eggs
6. Make dumplings
7. Dust with flour
8. Cook in salted water
9. Add grated bread roll fried in butter
10. Serve with sauces or roasts

title: Zemákové knedlíky se smetanou (Potato Dumplings with Sour Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter good sour cream
- 5 eggs
- 1 kilogram (2 pounds) cooked grated potatoes
- 4-5 spoonfuls flour
- 3 bread rolls cut into cubes and fried
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix sour cream with eggs
2. Add cooked grated potatoes, flour, fried bread roll cubes, salt, and nutmeg
3. Work dough until it does not stick to roller
4. Make dumplings by spoon
5. Cook in salted water

title: Zemákové Kotletky (Potato Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good floury potatoes
- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) grated cooked potato
- 75 grams (5 lots) creamed butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- egg for coating
- grated bread crumbs
- butter for frying
- stewed meat and various sauces

## Directions

1. Cook and grate good floury potatoes
2. Mix 24 dekagrams of the grated potato with 75 grams of creamed butter, 3 egg yolks, salt, and nutmeg
3. Form the mixture into cutlets the thickness of a finger
4. Brush with egg
5. Coat with grated bread crumbs
6. Fry in butter on both sides
7. Serve with stewed meat and various sauces

title: Zemákové krupky (Potato Groats)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kilogram peeled potatoes
- 6 dekagrams butter
- 4 tablespoons hot milk
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook kilogram peeled potatoes and press through a ricer
2. Mix in 6 dekagrams butter, 4 tablespoons hot milk, pepper and salt
3. Press everything through a sieve while moving it so the individual pieces look like groats
4. Add the hot potato groats to fried dishes

title: Zemákové lívance (Potato Pancakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- butter
- several eggs
- several spoonfuls flour
- a little sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- milk

## Directions

1. Mash cooked potatoes
2. Add butter, eggs, flour, sugar, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, and milk
3. Mix well to make batter
4. Fry pancakes on both sides in butter
5. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
6. Serve with sauce

title: Zemákové nuky (Potato Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) of grated butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon of flour
- 8 tablespoons of cooked, grated potatoes
- salt
- nutmeg
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Mix the butter with egg yolks, eggs, flour, cooked grated potatoes, salt, nutmeg and parsley
2. Shape dumplings with a spoon
3. Cook in salted water
4. Serve in soup

title: Zemákové piškoty (Potato Starch Sponge Cookies)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams (1 pound) grated cooked potatoes
- 500 grams flour
- 15 grams (1 lot) fennel
- 120 grams (8 lots) sugar
- salt
- 4 grated hard egg yolks
- raw eggs (quantity needed to make stiff dough)

## Directions

1. Mix 500 grams grated cooked potatoes with equal amount of flour
2. Add 15 grams fennel, 120 grams sugar, a pinch of salt, 4 grated hard egg yolks, and enough raw eggs to make stiff dough
3. Form cookies and bake on a waxed sheet or on wafers

title: Zemákové skyvy (Potato Flour Slices)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 195 grams (13 lots) butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 195 grams wheat flour
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) potato flour
- 6 decagrams (4 lots) raisins
- zest of lemon, grated
- snow from 6 egg whites
- glaze

## Directions

1. Whip the butter until foamy.
2. Gradually mix in the egg yolks, wheat flour, potato flour, raisins, grated lemon zest, and snow from 6 egg whites.
3. Either pour the mixture into paper molds or spread it thinly on a greased baking sheet.
4. Bake slowly until done.
5. When cooled, cut into oblong slices and coat with glaze.

title: Zemákové slížky (Potato Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- 1 liter hot milk
- 33 decagrams (22 lots) grated bread roll
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) butter
- 2 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Grate cooked potatoes
2. Mix with hot milk to make thin porridge
3. Add grated bread roll, butter, eggs, salt, and nutmeg, adding more milk to make firm dough
4. Drop small dumplings by spoon into salted water
5. Pour melted butter over them

title: Zemákové suchary (Potato Crisps)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- butter
- salt
- sour cream
- cheese
- several eggs

## Directions

1. Slice raw potatoes thin
2. Mix on baking sheet with butter, salt, and sour cream in which cheese and several eggs have been beaten
3. Bake crisps on both sides until potatoes are completely soft
4. Turn out onto plate
5. Serve hot

title: Zemákové Suchary (Potato Rusks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 to 10 mealy potatoes
- 8 beaten egg yolks
- lemon zest
- 240 grams sugar rubbed with lemon

## Directions

1. Bake 8 to 10 mealy potatoes, scrape out the inside with a spoon, press through a sieve
2. Mix in 8 beaten egg yolks, lemon zest, and 240 grams sugar rubbed with lemon
3. Stir for half an hour, then add snow from egg whites
4. Fill several boxes made of stiff paper halfway and bake for 20 to 25 minutes

title: Zemákové tašky (Potato Pockets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooled floury potatoes
- 1/4 liter milk
- 6 eggs
- salt
- flour
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Grate the cooled floury potatoes
2. Mix with 1/4 liter milk, 6 eggs, salt and flour to form a dough that can be rolled out
3. Brush with butter and sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs and roll up
4. When cooled, cut into pieces
5. Cook in salted water and pour melted butter over them
6. Serve with fruit or stewed meat

title: Zemákový dort (Potato Flour Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a kilogram (pound) butter
- half a kilogram sugar
- 14 egg yolks
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) sweet almonds
- several crushed bitter almonds
- grated lemon zest
- half a kilogram potato flour
- stiff snow from 14 egg whites
- pickled cherries
- glaze
- pickled fruit for decoration

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until foamy for 3/4 hour.
2. Add half a kilogram sugar, 14 egg yolks, 12 decagrams (8 lots) sweet almonds and several crushed bitter almonds, and grated lemon zest.
3. Continue mixing.
4. Add half a kilogram potato flour and stiff snow from 14 egg whites.
5. Bake two thin cakes from the mixture.
6. On one cake, place pickled cherries.
7. Cover with the second cake.
8. Glaze and decorate with pickled fruit.

title: Zemákový dort - Jiný způsob (Potato Flour Cake - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 24 decagrams (16 lots) sugar
- small spoonful of crushed cinnamon
- 24 decagrams grated potatoes
- snow from egg whites
- breadcrumbs for dusting the form
- fat for greasing the form

## Directions

1. Mix 10 egg yolks with 24 decagrams (16 lots) sugar, a small spoonful of crushed cinnamon, 24 decagrams grated potatoes, and snow from egg whites.
2. Bake in a greased form dusted with grated breadcrumbs.

title: Zemákový dort - Jiný způsob (Potato Flour Cake - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- floury potatoes
- 10 egg yolks
- 24 decagrams (16 lots) sugar
- lemon zest and juice
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) crushed almonds
- half a kilogram (pound) butter
- glaze
- sugar for dusting the form

## Directions

1. The day before use, boil floury potatoes, peel them, and when cooled, grate them and spread on a rolling pin or shallow grater.
2. Beat 10 egg yolks with 24 decagrams (16 lots) sugar, lemon zest and juice, and 12 decagrams (8 lots) crushed almonds.
3. After half an hour, add half a kilogram (pound) butter.
4. Add the grated potatoes and snow from egg whites.
5. Put the mixture into a greased form dusted with sugar.
6. Bake slowly for one hour.
7. When baked, glaze the cake with any glaze.

title: Zemákový ježek (Potato Hedgehog)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams cooked potatoes
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 15 decagrams (10 lots) ground sugar
- 3 decagrams (2 lots) ground almonds
- grated lemon zest
- 1 small spoon cinnamon
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) grated bread roll
- snow from egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate the cooked potatoes
2. Mix with butter, egg yolks, ground sugar, ground almonds, grated lemon zest, cinnamon, grated bread roll, and snow from egg whites
3. Place in a greased mold
4. Bake for one hour or boil in hot water for 2 hours

title: Zemákový ježek (Potato Hedgehog)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 grated baked potatoes
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) butter
- 12 decagrams ground sugar
- 6 decagrams ground almonds
- 8 egg yolks
- finely grated lemon zest
- 1/4 liter sweet sour cream
- snow from 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Pass baked potatoes through a sieve
2. Mix with butter, ground sugar, ground almonds, egg yolks, grated lemon zest, sour cream, and snow from egg whites
3. Grease a mold and sprinkle with grated bread roll
4. Place mixture in mold
5. Set in boiling water for one hour
6. Serve with lemon sauce

title: Zemákový ježek (Potato Hedgehog)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- 12 dekagrams butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 15 dekagrams sugar
- 3 dekagrams ground almonds
- lemon zest
- small teaspoon cinnamon
- 9 dekagrams grated breadcrumbs
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate cooked potatoes
2. To half kilogram potatoes add 12 dekagrams butter, 9 egg yolks, 15 dekagrams sugar, 3 dekagrams ground almonds, grated lemon zest, a small teaspoon cinnamon, 9 dekagrams grated breadcrumbs and beaten egg whites
3. Place in a buttered mold
4. Either bake for one hour or cook for 2 hours in hot water

title: Zemákový ježek s kaštany (Potato Flour Hedgehog with Chestnuts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- kilogram of chestnuts
- milk
- vanilla, chopped
- 75 grams (5 lots) potato flour
- three-quarters liter cream
- 15 decagrams (10 lots) crushed butter
- 10 egg yolks
- snow from egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel the kilogram of chestnuts and scald them in milk with chopped vanilla, cook until tender, and press through a sieve.
2. Cook 75 grams (5 lots) potato flour with three-quarters liter cream to make a paste.
3. Add to the chestnuts along with 15 decagrams (10 lots) crushed butter, 10 egg yolks, and snow from egg whites.
4. Put into a form and place in boiling water.

title: Zemákový ježek s masem (Potato Hedgehog with Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 decagrams (10 lots) butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 20 cooked grated potatoes
- snow from 8 egg whites
- finely chopped meat or any roast sliced thin

## Directions

1. Rub butter until foamy
2. Mix in egg yolks and cooked grated potatoes
3. Add snow from egg whites
4. Mix half the batter with finely chopped meat or thinly sliced roast
5. Place in a greased mold
6. Cover with remaining batter
7. Bake

title: Zemákový ježek, jiný způsob (Potato Hedgehog, Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams (16 lots) cooked potatoes
- 24 decagrams butter
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) sugar
- salt
- 12 decagrams raisins
- 6 whole eggs
- several spoonfuls of cognac

## Directions

1. Rub cooked potatoes with butter, sugar, and salt
2. Add raisins, beaten whole eggs, and cognac
3. Place in a greased mold
4. Bake or cook

title: Zemákový koláč (Potato Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cold potatoes
- 2 2/3 liters grated potatoes
- 1 liter heated flour
- 315 grams (32 lots) butter
- 5 eggs
- 9 decagrams (6 lots) diluted yeast
- a little sour cream
- salt
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) sugar
- a little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grate cooled potatoes
2. Work dough the day before
3. Let rise overnight in kitchen
4. Next day roll out into 2-3 thin pieces
5. Place on floured lukewarm baking sheets
6. Brush well with butter
7. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
8. Bake at mild heat for quarter hour

title: Zemákový koláč s máslem (Potato Cake with Butter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast
- lukewarm milk or water
- 1/2 liter flour
- cooked grated potatoes
- 3 eggs
- 12 decagrams (8 lots) butter
- salt
- 1-2 spoonfuls sugar
- butter for spreading
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Dissolve yeast in lukewarm milk or water
2. Mix with flour and let rise in warm place
3. Add cooked grated potatoes (while warm), eggs, butter, salt, and sugar
4. Roll out dough to finger thickness
5. Place on floured baking sheet and let rise
6. Pierce with fork
7. Brush with butter
8. Sprinkle thickly with sugar and cinnamon
9. Drizzle with butter
10. Bake for half hour
11. Serve warm with coffee

title: Zemákový Nákyp (Potato Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- 9 egg yolks
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) peeled beaten almonds
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- lemon juice
- 1/4 kilogram (1/4 pound) cooked grated potatoes
- salt
- 9 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of sugar with 9 egg yolks
2. Add 6 dekagrams (4 lots) of peeled beaten almonds, lemon zest rubbed with sugar, lemon juice, 1/4 kilogram (1/4 pound) of cooked grated potatoes, and salt
3. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 9 egg whites
4. Grease the mold and bake the mixture slowly for one hour

title: Zemákový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob (Potato Soufflé, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dekagrams (10 lots) butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/4 kilogram cooked grated potatoes
- salt
- nutmeg
- sour cream
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) grated cheese
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 15 dekagrams (10 lots) of butter until foamy
2. Add 8 egg yolks, 1/4 kilogram of cooked grated potatoes, salt, nutmeg, sour cream, and 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of grated cheese
3. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 8 egg whites
4. Put into a mold and cook in hot water
5. Usually served as an accompaniment to game

title: Zemákový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob II (Potato Soufflé, Another Method II)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) sugar
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) almonds
- salt
- half a kilogram cooked grated potatoes
- 12 egg whites
- cinnamon and sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of butter with 12 egg yolks and 18 dekagrams (12 lots) of sugar until foamy
2. Add 9 dekagrams (6 lots) of almonds, salt, and half a kilogram of cooked grated potatoes
3. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 12 egg whites
4. Put into a greased mold and bake either in the oven or cook in hot water
5. Sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and serve as an accompaniment

title: Zemákový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob III (Potato Soufflé, Another Method III)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 195 grams (13 lots) butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- 39 dekagrams (26 lots) cooked grated potatoes
- 6 dekagrams (4 lots) candied lemon peel, chopped
- 6 dekagrams raisins
- lemon zest rubbed with sugar
- 12 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 195 grams (13 lots) of butter and mix with 12 egg yolks and 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of sugar
2. Add 39 dekagrams (26 lots) of cooked grated potatoes, 6 dekagrams (4 lots) of chopped candied lemon peel, 6 dekagrams of raisins, and lemon zest rubbed with sugar
3. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 12 egg whites
4. Bake in a greased mold coated with grated bread roll

title: Zemákový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob IV (Potato Soufflé, Another Method IV)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- water
- white paper
- 15 egg yolks
- half a kilogram (one pound) beaten sugar
- a piece of vanilla or lemon zest
- 15 egg whites
- potato starch flour

## Directions

1. Peel and grate raw potatoes into water
2. Keep changing the water until it runs clear, leaving behind white flour
3. Spread this flour on white paper placed on a board and dry it on the stove or in the sun
4. Beat 15 egg yolks, half a kilogram (one pound) of beaten sugar, and a piece of vanilla or lemon zest for 1/4 hour
5. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 15 egg whites
6. Gradually add the dried potato flour, about one tablespoon at a time (half a kilogram or one pound total)
7. Bake in a greased mold coated with grated bread roll

title: Zemákový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob V (Potato Soufflé, Another Method V)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- flour
- sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- 60 grams (4 lots) butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Let cooked potatoes cool, peel, and grate them
2. Mix equal parts flour and sugar so the total weight equals half the weight of the potatoes
3. Mix everything together and add a little cinnamon
4. Beat 60 grams (4 lots) of butter with 10 egg yolks
5. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 10 egg whites

title: Zemákový příkrm (Potato Side Dish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- beef broth
- parsley
- fried onion

## Directions

1. Cut the potatoes into quarters
2. Wash them several times
3. Pour hot water over them and let them stand covered
4. Drain the water
5. Place the potatoes in a baking dish and cook in beef broth without letting them fall apart
6. Sprinkle with chopped parsley and fried onion
7. Serve usually with beef or veal

title: Zemákový salát (Potato Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cooked potatoes
- salt
- onion
- wine vinegar
- fine oil
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the cold cooked potatoes into thin slices and salt them
2. Finely chop raw onion and add to potatoes
3. Add wine vinegar, fine oil, and a little pepper
4. Mix well

title: Zemákový salát s omáčkou (Potato Salad with Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 raw egg yolks
- oil
- 5-6 tablespoons wine vinegar
- meat jelly
- beef broth
- pepper
- 5-6 tablespoons sour cream
- potatoes
- hot broth
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat together the 2 raw egg yolks, oil which is poured in slowly, 5-6 tablespoons wine vinegar, meat jelly, beef broth, pepper and 5-6 tablespoons sour cream to make a sauce
2. The sauce may be chilled on ice
3. Boil the potatoes, slice them into thin slices and pour hot broth over them
4. Pour half the sauce over the potatoes
5. Arrange them with butter and add the remaining sauce

title: Zemákový salát s omáčkou (Potato Salad with Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 small spoon mustard
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- 4 tablespoons oil
- 3 tablespoons wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the potatoes
2. While still warm, beat the hard-boiled eggs with a small spoon of mustard, salt, pepper, finely chopped onion, 4 tablespoons of oil, and 3 tablespoons of wine vinegar
3. Continue mixing until a sauce forms
4. Pour the sauce over 75 decagrams (40 lots) of potatoes

title: Zemákový sníh (Potato Flour Snow)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- white floury potatoes
- salted water

## Directions

1. Wash and peel white floury potatoes.
2. Cook slowly in salted water.
3. Drain the water and leave the potatoes in the oven until they fall apart.
4. Press through a fine sieve into a lukewarm bowl.
5. Leave fluffy and add to baked goods or sauces.

title: Zemákový Svítek (Potato Roll)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters liter milk
- 6 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- heaping tablespoon wheat flour
- tablespoon potato flour

## Directions

1. Mix 6 eggs and 2 egg yolks into three-quarters liter milk, add salt, wheat flour and potato flour
2. Mix everything well and bake the roll

title: Zemákový svítek (Potato Roll)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons sour cream
- 18 dekagrams cooked grated potatoes
- salt
- nutmeg
- 3 egg whites
- 3 dekagrams butter

## Directions

1. Beat 9 dekagrams butter until foamy
2. Mix in 3 egg yolks, 6 tablespoons sour cream, 18 dekagrams cooked grated potatoes, salt and nutmeg
3. Fold in the beaten egg whites from 3 eggs
4. Bake the dough in a shallow pan with 3 dekagrams butter
5. Cut into cubes and place in broth

title: Zemákový Svítek - Another Method (Potato Roll - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- parsley
- butter
- 3 or 4 eggs
- one-eighth liter flour
- salt
- cooked potatoes, grated

## Directions

1. Fry onion and parsley in butter, beat with 3 or 4 eggs and one-eighth liter flour
2. Salt it, mix with suitable amount of cooked grated potatoes and bake in butter in a baking pan

title: Zemákový Svítek - Another Method (Potato Roll - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams butter (6 lots)
- 7 egg yolks
- half liter milk
- 30 dekagrams cooked potato flour (20 lots)
- salt
- 7 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat butter until it foams
2. Add 7 egg yolks, half liter milk, cooled potato flour, salt and snow from 7 egg whites
3. Serve the potato roll with compote or salad

title: Zemákový Svítek - Another Method (Potato Roll - Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large tablespoon potato flour
- half liter sour cream
- 12 dekagrams almonds (8 lots)
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 dekagrams sugar (4 lots)
- 24 dekagrams cooked grated potatoes (16 lots)
- 12 egg whites
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat potato flour with half liter sour cream
2. Pound almonds, add 12 egg yolks, sugar, grated cooked potatoes, snow from 12 egg whites and salt
3. Bake the roll and serve with cherry or raspberry sauce

title: Zemáky Jiný způsob (Potatoes Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onion
- butter
- flour
- broth or water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes
- sour pickled cucumber

## Directions

1. Stew the chopped onion in butter
2. Add flour, pour in broth or water and vinegar
3. Add salt and pepper
4. Cook everything together
5. In this sauce stew potatoes either raw or cooked
6. May also add sliced sour pickled cucumber
7. Serves as a side dish for sausages and liver sausages

title: Zemáky Jiný způsob (Potatoes Another Way)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt water
- celery
- leek
- parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel and cut potatoes into pieces
2. Cook in salted water with celery and leek
3. Before placing on a dish, brush with butter
4. Sprinkle with chopped parsley

title: Zemáky na loupačku (Potatoes with Skin)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Good potatoes
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes thoroughly, preferably with a brush, each one separately.
2. Keep in cold water, then cook without completely covering with water.
3. Drain the water and leave in the oven until completely dry and split open.
4. Alternatively, cook potatoes halfway through, quickly drain the water, cover the pot with a folded cloth, and leave in the oven until done.

title: Zemáky s lanýži (Potatoes with Truffles)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram potatoes
- half kilogram truffles
- wine
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- wine vinegar
- cooked carrots
- beetroot

## Directions

1. Cook and slice half a kilogram of potatoes
2. Cook half a kilogram of truffles in wine and slice them
3. Mix with oil, salt, pepper, and wine vinegar
4. Arrange on a platter
5. Decorate with cooked carrots and beetroot cut into shapes

title: Zemáky s masem (Potatoes with Meat)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dough
- butter
- potato slices
- mutton cutlets
- good beef broth

## Directions

1. Make a border around a deep dish from dough
2. Brush the dish with butter
3. Into it place slices of potatoes alternating with mutton cutlets so that one tenth of the mixture is meat
4. Pour good beef broth over the mixture
5. Cover with dough and bake for 2 hours
6. Serve hot at the table

title: Zemáky s petruzelí (Potatoes with Parsley)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt water
- butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Trim potatoes
2. Cook in salted water without letting them fall apart
3. Place in a baking dish with butter and chopped parsley
4. Usually serve with fish

title: Zemáky s tuřínem (Potatoes with Turnip)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- turnip
- good beef broth
- floury potatoes
- hot water
- parsley
- cooked meat
- butter

## Directions

1. Scald sliced turnip
2. Cook in good beef broth
3. Add potatoes cut into quarters which have been scalded with hot water
4. Cook until completely soft, adding broth as needed
5. Add chopped parsley
6. Serve usually with cooked meat and cutlets
7. Alternatively, small cooked potatoes may be added to turnip which was cooked with butter in broth

title: Zemáky s vejci (Potatoes with Eggs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- chopped chives
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Slice potatoes and bake in butter
2. Mix several eggs with salt, pepper, chopped chives and several tablespoons sour cream
3. Pour over the potatoes so the eggs look scrambled

title: Zemáky s vínem (Potatoes with Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- tablespoon chopped parsley
- chives
- tablespoon flour
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- good broth
- 1/4 liter wine
- cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. Fry the tablespoon chopped parsley, some chives, tablespoon flour, pepper and salt in butter
2. Cook in good broth and 1/4 liter wine
3. Add several cooked potatoes sliced into thin slices
4. Stew them in the sauce for a while

title: Zemáky se sardelemi (Potatoes with Sardines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- olive oil
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- sardines

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes and slice into thin slices
2. On a baking dish pour olive oil over them
3. Add chopped parsley, chives, a piece of garlic, pepper, salt and a little vinegar
4. While frying, shake them several times
5. Finally add several chopped sardines

title: Zemáky se smetanou (Potatoes with Sour Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked peeled potatoes
- butter
- salt
- sweet or sour cream

## Directions

1. Slice cooked peeled potatoes
2. Brush with butter in a baking dish
3. Season with salt and bake for a while
4. When they begin to brown, pour sweet or sour cream over them
5. Stew for quarter hour

title: Zemáky se zeleninou (Potatoes with Vegetables)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young leek
- onion
- salt water
- raisins
- beef broth
- pale anchovy
- vinegar
- salt
- sugar
- beef

## Directions

1. Clean young leek, cut off the leaves
2. Clean and halve the onions
3. Cook in salted water for quarter hour
4. Drain on a sieve so water runs off
5. Cook with raisins in beef broth
6. Add pale anchovy, a little vinegar, salt and sugar
7. Add the sauce to beef and potatoes

title: Zemáky se zelím (Potatoes with Cabbage - Hunter's Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt water
- 12 dekagrams bacon
- flour
- 1/4 liter hot water
- vinegar
- pepper
- potatoes
- sausages

## Directions

1. Clean cabbage and cut finely
2. Pour several times with boiling water
3. Place on a sieve to drain
4. Meanwhile cut 12 dekagrams bacon into small pieces and fry
5. Remove the bacon
6. To the fat add 2 tablespoons flour
7. Pour 1/4 liter hot water and several tablespoons vinegar
8. Season with pepper and salt
9. Cook briefly and set aside so the sauce stays lukewarm
10. Place sliced potatoes in a baking dish with a little salted water
11. When they begin to cook, add the cabbage
12. Season with salt
13. Pour the sauce over it
14. Cover well and stew until potatoes are soft
15. Then mix the potatoes with cabbage
16. Add sausages and cabbage
17. Serve at the table

title: Zlatý skládaný dort (Gold Layer Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 full cup butter
- sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 teaspoon lemon extract
- flour
- baking powder
- currant or wine preserves
- yellow frosting

## Directions

1. Make the cake the same way as the silver layer cake, using 1 full cup butter instead of less.
2. Use 7 egg yolks and 1 whole egg instead of egg whites.
3. Use 1 teaspoon lemon extract instead of vanilla.
4. Spread frosting mixed with currant or wine preserves between the cakes.
5. Cover the cake with yellow frosting.

title: Zmrzlina (Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Line the container in which ice cream is to be made in a smaller container with ice broken into pieces and salted to produce greater cold
2. Chill the ice cream container for at least ten minutes by rapidly rotating it until completely cooled
3. Clean it, fill with ice cream mixture and rotate and whip it rapidly until it begins to cool and harden
4. Open the lid which must be firmly attached, scrape the sides of the container and in the sealed container the ice cream is again whipped and mixed much more rapidly than before
5. After a while stop again, scrape everything and repeat several times until the ice cream is completely ready
6. Place the ice cream in decorative containers and molds
7. Remove ice cream from molds by placing it in warm water, warming it and turning the ice cream out onto a plate
8. Paint the outside of the ice cream with fruit juice from the fruit which the mold should resemble, so that it also has that taste

title: Zmrzlina Citrónová (Lemon Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Half kilogram sugar
- Three lemons
- Three-quarters liter water
- Glass of white wine
- Juice of six to eight lemons

## Directions

1. Grate three lemons for half a kilogram of sugar
2. Dissolve sugar in three-quarters liter of water
3. Add a glass of white wine and juice from six to eight lemons
4. Proceed with everything else as with other kinds of ice cream

title: Zmrzlina Citrónová se Smetanou (Lemon Ice Cream with Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- Half liter cream
- 330 grams sugar
- One lemon

## Directions

1. Grate one lemon into 330 grams sugar
2. Place four eggs, half liter cream and the sugar on a warm stove
3. Constantly whip while heating
4. Remove from heat, let cool and constantly whip
5. Place in ice cream machines

title: Zralé broskve (Ripe Peaches)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Nearly ripe but still firm peaches
- Sugar
- Fresh water
- Apple pulp

## Directions

1. Select nearly ripe but still firm peaches, halve them, remove the pit, peel the flesh and cook slowly.
2. As soon as the peaches begin to cook, place them in fresh water to cool.
3. Then cook them several times in dissolved sugar, cover in a container and let sit until the next day.
4. On the second day, drain the sugar, boil it thicker, add the peaches to it, and cook them together several times.
5. On the third day, boil the sugar again thicker with apple pulp until the sixth degree, cook the peaches together with the sugar, skim off the foam, and when cooled, place in containers.
6. It is understood that preserved fruit must always be completely covered with sugar so it does not spoil and lasts longer.

title: Ztracená vejce (Poached Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- water (one pint)
- vinegar (two spoons)
- water (one spoon)
- eggs

## Directions

1. Pour one pint of boiling water into a deep pan
2. Add two spoons of vinegar and one spoon of water
3. Heat on the stove
4. Drop several eggs into the water carefully so they remain whole
5. Let them cook for five minutes
6. Remove with a slotted spoon onto a plate
7. Pour melted butter over them
8. Or remove and place on toast

title: Ztuhnutí rosolu (Setting Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- aspic
- crushed ice
- salt
- very cold water

## Directions

1. Pour aspic into crushed ice, salted snow or very cold water in any desired molds
2. Ensure the container stands firm and does not move

title: Zvěřina s omáčkou (Venison with Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of venison
- water
- vinegar
- ordinary wine
- salt
- whole spices
- juniper
- bay leaves
- onion
- clarified butter
- flour (2 spoons)
- grated black bread
- lemon zest
- brown gingerbread or ginger

## Directions

1. Clean three pounds of venison well and place in a pot
2. Pour water, vinegar and ordinary wine over it, salt it
3. Add whole spices, some juniper, several bay leaves, and onion
4. Let the venison cook until almost tender
5. Heat clarified butter in a pan, add two spoons of flour and a small handful of grated black bread
6. Let it brown in the butter, then mix it with some of the sauce in which the venison is cooking
7. Pour this onto a platter and strain the sauce over the meat
8. Sprinkle with finely chopped lemon zest
9. If desired, you can add a piece of brown gingerbread or ginger to the sauce at the end

title: Závitek Skořicový (Cinnamon Roll)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- good dough for strudel
- butter
- egg yolks
- sugar
- ground cinnamon
- sliced almonds
- raisins
- lemon zest
- roasted grated roll

## Directions

1. Prepare good strudel dough and stretch it well until thin
2. Brush with butter
3. Brush with beaten egg yolks
4. Sprinkle with sugar, finely ground cinnamon, sliced almonds and raisins mixed with grated lemon zest
5. Sprinkle with roasted grated roll on butter
6. Roll up and coil, bake slowly in oven
7. During baking, brush often with egg yolks, butter or sour cream, then sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Závitky z mladé zvěřiny (Rolls of Young Venison)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young venison
- bacon
- egg yolks
- butter
- houska (bread roll)
- cloves
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of young venison with bacon
2. On a baking sheet, beat together 6 egg yolks, a piece of butter, and 2 soaked houska rolls until a thick paste forms
3. Let it cool
4. In a mortar, pound everything with 4 raw egg yolks, cloves, and pepper
5. Press through a sieve onto a floured board
6. Roll it into an elongated patty

title: Zázvorky (Ginger-snaps)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 koflík másla
- 1 koflík syrobu
- 1 koflík cukru
- 1 koflík kyselého mléka nebo podmáslí
- 1 stolní lžíce zázvoru
- 1 stolní lžíce skořice
- 2 vejce
- mouka (dostatečné množství)
- 1 čajová lžička sody

## Directions

1. Rozhřeji koflík másla, dám do něho koflík syrobu, koflík cukru, koflík kyselého mléka neb podmáslí, stolní lžíci zázvoru, stolní lžíci skořice a dvě celá vejce.
2. Všechno se dobře smíchá.
3. Prosej dostatečné množství mouky s jednou čajovou lžičkou sody a s moukou se zadělá těsto.
4. Dá se na vál a dobře se vyválí v mouce a kulatou formičkou vykrájí.
5. Koláčky se dají na namaštěný plech péci do nepříliš horké trouby a nechají se dobře upéci.
6. Když jsou všechny upečeny, uschovej si je do suchého místa a vydrží třebas měsíc.

title: Zázvorový perník (Gingerbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 koflík cukru
- 1 koflík másla
- 2 vejce (ušlehaná)
- 1/2 lžičky kuchyňské sody
- 1 lžíce horké vody
- 1 koflík kyselého mléka
- 1 koflík syrobu
- 1 koflík hrozinek
- 1 lžička zázvoru
- mouka (dle potřeby)

## Directions

1. Rozetři koflík cukru a koflík másla až se zpění.
2. Přidej dvě ušlehané vejce a dobře zamíchej.
3. Rozpusť půl lžičky kuchyňské sody ve lžíci horké vody, vlej ji do koflíka kyselého mléka, rozmíchej a přidej to k tomu cukru.
4. Přimíchej k tomu ještě koflík syrobu, koflík hrozinek, lžičku zázvoru a mouky tolik, co je zapotřeby, aby bylo těsto dost tuhé, tak aby se mohlo vyválet.
5. Rozválej těsto na prst tlouštky a dej na pekáč a při mírném ohni peč asi půl hodiny.

title: Úhoř Marinovaný (Marinated Eel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- butter
- vinegar
- water
- onion
- pepper
- spices
- rosemary
- thyme
- bay leaf
- capers
- paprika

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the eel and cut into equal pieces
2. Rinse and dry
3. Line a baking dish with small sticks, butter and bake until golden
4. Allow to cool
5. In a pot, combine vinegar mixed with water enough to cover the eel
6. Layer at the bottom: sliced onion, whole pepper and coarsely crushed pepper, new spices, rosemary, thyme, bay leaf, capers, and paprika
7. Place the eel on top and continue layering spices until the pot is full
8. Cover with a lid and weigh down with something heavy
9. Store in a cool place
10. Before serving, remove all spices from the eel

title: Úhoř na modro (Blued Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- celery
- parsley
- carrot
- onion
- bay leaf
- sage
- peppercorns
- allspice
- salt
- vinegar
- wine
- white flour
- cold water
- egg yolks
- butter
- white pepper
- nutmeg
- lemon

## Directions

1. Fix the eel to the table with a fork
2. Make an incision around the head just below the skin and pull off the skin using a cloth, being careful not to break the gall bladder which is located under the skin near the head
3. Clean and cut the eel into pieces
4. Place celery, parsley, carrot and onion (all sliced) in a pot along with a bay leaf, a piece of sage, several peppercorns and allspice
5. Salt and add enough vinegar and let simmer for half an hour
6. Add the cut eel pieces to this sauce, add a glass of wine and enough more vinegar so the eel is completely covered
7. Continue simmering gently for another quarter hour
8. Arrange the eel on a dish and strain the sauce through a fine sieve into a serving dish
9. For the hollandaise-style sauce: place a spoonful of white flour in a bowl, add a little cold water and mix well, add six egg yolks, add four spoonfuls of fresh butter and mix well, add twelve white peppercorns and a little nutmeg
10. Pour in a pint of the water in which the eel was cooked
11. Place over heat and stir constantly until it is about to boil, but do not let it boil
12. Squeeze in the juice of half a lemon and strain through a fine sieve into a serving bowl

title: Úhoř Pečený (Roasted Eel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- anchovies
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the eel
2. Depending on size, rub with anchovies, then either roll the eel or cut into pieces
3. Mix anchovies with butter and coat the eel
4. Place in oven
5. When baking begins, baste frequently with good sour cream until completely soft
6. Pour remaining sour cream over the anchovies when the eel is plated
7. Strain sauce over the eel

title: Úhoř pečený na rošti (Broiled Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- ground pepper
- sage leaves
- butter
- breadcrumb rind, grated
- lemon, cut into quarters

## Directions

1. Skin the eel, clean it, dry it and cut it into pieces the length of a finger
2. Sprinkle it with salt and ground pepper and let it rest for an hour
3. Skewer three or four pieces at a time on thin wooden skewers
4. Place a sage leaf next to each piece of eel
5. Brush the cleaned grill with butter, place the prepared eel on it and broil it on both sides until brown
6. Sprinkle it with grated breadcrumb rind
7. Remove from skewers, arrange it on a platter
8. Surround it with lemon cut into quarters
9. Serve hot

title: Úhoř s majonézovou omáčkou (Eel with Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- mayonnaise sauce
- aspic

## Directions

1. Prepare the eel as directed for marinated carp
2. Arrange the cold eel in a ring shape on a dish
3. Place the mayonnaise sauce in the center and make a border of broken aspic around it

title: Úhoř s majónézovou omáčkou (Eel with Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- mayonnaise sauce
- aspic, broken up

## Directions

1. Prepare the eel as you would prepare marinated carp
2. Arrange the cooled eel in a ring on a platter
3. Place the mayonnaise sauce in the center
4. Create a border around it from the broken up aspic

title: Úhoř Smažený (Fried Eel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- flour
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Kill the eel, eviscerate, make an incision around the head and remove the skin
2. Cut into equal pieces
3. Rinse and dry with a clean cloth
4. Salt, roll in flour, dip in egg, coat with breadcrumbs
5. Fry in hot clarified butter until golden
6. Serve with cabbage, head lettuce, cucumber salad, or horseradish

title: Úkrop (Bread Broth)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- butter
- 1.5 litres water
- salt
- bread, sliced

## Directions

1. Finely chop onion and fry it with butter until pink
2. Boil about 1.5 litres of water, salt it and pour it into a bowl with sliced bread mixed with the fried onion

title: Úkrop (Bread Broth)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cloves garlic
- butter
- salt
- bread, fried
- water

## Directions

1. Rub 6 cloves of garlic with butter and salt
2. Fry bread in butter
3. Boil the rubbed garlic with water for a while and pour over the fried bread

title: Úkrop (Garlic Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- about 6 garlic cloves
- butter
- salt
- bread
- water

## Directions

1. Rub about 6 garlic cloves with butter and salt
2. Slice bread into small pieces and fry on butter
3. Boil the rubbed garlic with water for a while
4. Pour over the fried bread and serve
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Úprava jaternic a jelit (Preparation of Liver Sausages and Intestines)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork intestines
- Pork head
- Pork brain
- Belly meat
- Neck meat
- Lungs
- Heart
- Liver
- Pepper
- Marjoram
- Cloves
- Ginger
- Garlic
- Bread or breadcrumbs soaked in water
- Pork blood
- Millet groats
- Butter
- Onion

## Directions

1. Rinse pork intestines many times in cold water, turn inside out, and scrape well inside with a blunt knife
2. Salt each piece well, turn again, and rinse with water
3. For immediate use, keep in cold water; for later use, tie the end, inflate, and dry
4. If making liver sausages at home, cut the head in half, remove the brain, and cook the head with pieces of belly, neck, lungs, heart, and a piece of liver
5. When cooked, remove all bones and finely chop the meat
6. Mix with pepper, marjoram, cloves, grated ginger, and chopped garlic
7. Add some bread or breadcrumbs soaked in water for each portion of chopped meat
8. Thin with meat broth if needed, mix and stuff into intestines
9. Drop into boiling broth and cook carefully so they do not burst, occasionally adding cold water
10. Remove with a skimmer and arrange side by side
11. For another version, prepare intestines with a mixture of breadcrumbs, blood, and millet groats, seasoned with fried onion instead of the spices used for liver sausages

title: Úprava Kávy Dle Liebiga (Coffee Preparation According to Liebig)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee beans
- sugar (15 grams per 500 grams coffee)
- water (approximately 15 grams coffee per two cups water)

## Directions

1. Slowly roast the coffee beans only to a light brown color, to preserve the aroma and caffeine.
2. When nearly roasted, add fifteen grams of sugar per half kilogram of coffee.
3. Mix thoroughly until the coffee beans are properly coated with the dissolved sugar, which prevents the escape of fine aromatic particles.
4. Spread the roasted beans on a pan to cool.
5. Close them in tightly sealed containers and store in dry places.
6. When brewing coffee, grind or crush three-quarters of the amount you wish to brew, place in water, and steep for ten minutes.
7. Then add the remaining quarter of ground coffee and immediately remove from heat.
8. Cover tightly and let it stand for at least five minutes.
9. By this method, the best parts of the coffee are extracted, and the final addition of fresh grounds imparts a pleasant flavor to the beverage.

title: Ústricová omáčka (Oyster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of oysters
- oyster liquor
- 1 cupful milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the oysters finely and cook for five minutes in their own liquor
2. Cook a cupful of milk and rub a tablespoon of butter with a tablespoon of flour
3. Add to the milk when it boils with constant stirring
4. When the sauce is cooked, remove from heat and add the oysters to it
5. Mix in two egg yolks, salt and pepper

title: Ústricová omáčka (Oyster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint oysters
- milk
- butter
- flour
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the oysters finely and simmer for five minutes in their own juice.
2. Heat a pint of milk, blend a tablespoon of butter with a tablespoon of flour and add to the milk while stirring constantly.
3. When the sauce is cooked, remove from heat, pour the oysters into it and mix in two egg yolks, salt and pepper.

title: Ústřice pečené (Baked Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- butter
- grated breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- oyster liquid mixed with milk
- egg
- milk

## Directions

1. Grease a pan or deep dish with butter
2. Sprinkle the bottom with grated breadcrumbs
3. Place a layer of oysters, salt and pepper them
4. Place several small pieces of butter on them
5. Cover them with a layer of grated breadcrumbs
6. Moisten with oyster liquid mixed with milk
7. Continue this way until the dish is full
8. On top place a layer of breadcrumbs, dot with small pieces of butter
9. Pour over with egg beaten in milk
10. Bake in a hot oven for a quarter hour
11. If the dish is larger, it must bake a little longer
12. Serve at table in the dish in which it was prepared

title: Ústřice s bílou omáčkou (Oysters with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- juice from two dozen large oysters
- oysters
- salt
- butter
- lemon juice
- flour
- sour cream
- egg yolks or whole egg
- parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Let the juice from two dozen large oysters come to a boil
2. Place the oysters in it, let them set slightly, then remove them
3. Salt the juice, add a spoonful of butter, lemon juice, a spoonful of flour mixed with a cup of sour cream and two beaten egg yolks or one whole egg
4. Keep stirring constantly over the fire and let it heat well, but it must not boil
5. Place the oysters on a hot platter
6. Pour the sauce over them
7. Sprinkle with a spoonful of finely chopped parsley

title: Ústřice s podpouštkou (Fricasseed Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- juice from two dozen large oysters
- butter
- beef or fish broth
- pepper
- nutmeg
- oysters
- flour
- egg yolks or whole egg
- lemon juice
- fried breadcrumb cutlets of various shapes or small rice croquettes
- hot water

## Directions

1. Let the juice from two dozen large oysters simmer in a pot with a spoonful of butter, some beef or fish broth and a pinch of pepper and nutmeg
2. Place the oysters in it, let them simmer for about two minutes, then remove them and strain the juice through a sieve
3. Make a light roux from a spoonful of butter and a spoonful of flour in a pan
4. Add enough of the strained juice to make a thickened sauce
5. Salt, pepper and let simmer for about ten minutes
6. Beat two egg yolks or a whole egg with a spoonful of butter and lemon juice
7. Mix this into the sauce, strain it through a sieve, and keeping it constantly stirred over the fire, let it heat through—but it must not boil
8. Pour the sauce over the oysters placed in a deep heated dish and surrounded by fried breadcrumb cutlets of various shapes or small rice croquettes
9. The dish should be heated in the oven and the oysters kept warm by placing them in a covered pot in a dish of hot water

title: Ústřice v mléce (Oyster Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart can of oysters
- one quart milk
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of milk and watch that it does not scorch
2. Take a spoonful of butter and a small spoonful of flour, rub them well together and mix into the boiling milk
3. When the boil is complete, quickly pour the oysters into the milk with a little of their juice
4. As soon as they begin to rise, remove from heat
5. Salt and pepper
6. Serve at table

title: Ústřicová polévka (Oyster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 1 quart of oysters
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- salt
- pepper
- small crackers

## Directions

1. Let a quart of milk cook and be careful that it does not burn.
2. When the milk begins to boil, add a quart of oysters, a tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper.
3. Remove from heat before the milk comes to a boil again, as soon as the oyster edges begin to curl.
4. Serve with small crackers.

title: Čaj (Tea)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh water
- good tea

## Directions

1. Boil fresh water in a large pot
2. For every three cups of water, put one teaspoon of good tea into a teapot
3. Pour the boiling water over the tea
4. Leave it on a hot place where it will not boil for about 5 minutes
5. The longer it stands, the more bitter the tea becomes, as tannins from the tea leaves are extracted

title: Čaj s vejci (Tea with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- tea
- sugar
- cream or rum or lemon juice
- egg yolks
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare tea according to the previous method
2. Strain it and sweeten
3. Add either cream, rum or lemon juice
4. Beat several egg yolks with a little water
5. Pour them into the tea while constantly stirring
6. Heat over fire until it froths, but do not let it boil
7. Pour immediately into cups

title: Čajová Zmrzlina (Tea Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- At least one liter sweet cream
- About three-quarters kilogram sugar
- Three to four deca good tea

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs
2. Take at least one liter sweet cream, about three-quarters kilogram sugar and three to four deca good tea
3. Cook everything with constant whipping
4. Let cool
5. Strain
6. Process into ice cream

title: Čajový Creme (Tea Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- brewed tea
- four deciliters cream
- seven decagrams sugar
- six egg yolks
- rum
- orange sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the brewed tea with four deciliters of cream
2. Add seven decagrams of sugar and six egg yolks
3. Beat well and let cool
4. Add rum and orange sugar

title: Čajový creme (Tea Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- brewed tea
- four deciliters cream
- seven deka sugar
- six egg yolks
- rum
- orange sugar

## Directions

1. Mix brewed tea with four deciliters of cream
2. Add seven deka sugar and six egg yolks
3. When it has been whipped enough and cooled, add rum and orange sugar

title: Černá zvěř pečená (Roasted Game)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game haunch or shoulder
- salt
- wooden mallet
- 8 juniper berries
- thyme
- bay leaf
- ginger
- lemon zest
- whole pepper
- new spices
- 3 onions
- celery
- parsley root
- carrots
- vinegar
- water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Carefully scrape the haunch or shoulder with a sharp knife and marinate as follows: salt it, pound well with a wooden mallet.
2. Add 8 juniper berries, thyme, bay leaf, ginger, lemon zest, whole pepper, new spices, and 3 onions cut into slices, celery, parsley root and carrots, add vinegar and water as needed.
3. Turn the haunch occasionally.
4. When ready to roast, pour off half the liquid and roast in the other half until almost all the juices have cooked away.
5. Remove the outer skin from the roast, slice neatly, strain the remaining sauce and garnish with lemon.

title: Černá zvěř pečená (Roasted Game)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Hind leg or shoulder of game
- Salt
- 8 juniper berries
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Ginger
- Lemon peel
- Whole pepper
- New spice
- 3 onions
- Celery
- Parsley root
- Carrot
- Wine vinegar
- Water
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Scrape the hind leg or shoulder clean with a sharp knife
2. Salt it and beat well with a wooden mallet
3. Add 8 juniper berries, thyme, bay leaf, ginger, lemon peel, whole pepper, new spice, and 3 onions sliced into rounds, celery, parsley root and carrot as needed, and vinegar mixed with water
4. Turn the leg from time to time
5. When ready to roast, pour off half the liquid and roast in the remaining liquid until almost all the juices are cooked out
6. Remove the upper skin from the roast, slice neatly, strain the remaining sauce and garnish with lemon; something sour should generally accompany this roast
7. Note: For the game to be completely clean, it must first be singed and scraped well with a sharp knife

title: Černá zvěřina pečená (Roasted Venison)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch or shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- citron

## Directions

1. Prepare according to the roasted game recipe but finish by arranging on a serving platter.

title: Černá zvěřina rosolovaná (Game Aspic Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game meat
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- water
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- onion
- veal feet
- egg shells

## Directions

1. Prepare the game the same way as with aspic, only cut the meat into cubes or pieces, mix well and let cool.

title: Černá zvěřina s aspikem (Game with Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game haunch or shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- water
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- onion
- veal feet
- egg shells
- parsley
- lemon slices
- meat aspic

## Directions

1. Remove sinews from a game haunch or shoulder, salt and pepper it.
2. Add 1 part good vinegar and 2 parts water, thyme, bay leaf, a piece of lemon zest and plenty of onion.
3. Braise until the meat is completely tender.
4. Slice the meat and let it cool.
5. Add 2 or 4 veal feet to the sauce and cook until tender.
6. When almost cooked, add 2 or 3 eggshells to help it set better.
7. Tie a cloth to the legs of an inverted chair, place a dish underneath, and pour the liquid onto the cloth so it drips freely.
8. Arrange the roast on a dish decorated with green parsley and lemon slices.
9. Pour a quarter of the liquid over the roast and let the rest cool.
10. Cut the resulting aspic into cubes and arrange around the roast; part of the aspic can also be colored red.

title: Černá zvěřina s bílou omáčkou (Game with White Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game meat
- all spices
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- vinegar
- water
- egg yolk
- sour cream
- sugar
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Take a piece of game that can be well cut.
2. Add all spices, thyme, bay leaf, lemon zest and vinegar with water.
3. Marinate and clean the game, then braise until completely tender.
4. Slice the game, add an egg yolk to the sauce, 3.75 deciliters of sour cream and a piece of sugar.
5. Simmer for a quarter hour, arrange the game on a dish, strain the sauce over it and garnish with buttered potatoes.

title: Černá zvěřina s černou omáčkou (Game with Black Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game pieces
- thyme
- bay leaf
- new spices
- pepper
- onion
- white wine
- beef broth or water
- vinegar
- 4 juniper berries
- salt
- 2 pieces dark gingerbread
- 2 pieces white gingerbread
- egg yolk
- burnt sugar
- dumplings with ingredients: 3 decagrams fresh butter, salt, pounded clove, 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 1.5 deciliters sour cream
- flour
- bread

## Directions

1. Rinse game pieces and cook with thyme, bay leaf, new spices, pepper, onion, white wine, beef broth or water, vinegar, 4 juniper berries and salt.
2. When the game is tender, place on a dish.
3. Add 2 pieces dark and 2 pieces white gingerbread, an egg yolk and a piece of burnt sugar to the sauce.
4. Simmer and then press through onto the game.
5. For the dumplings: beat 3 decagrams fresh butter until fluffy, add a little salt, a little pounded clove, 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks.
6. Mix 1.5 deciliters sour cream with flour, alternating spoonfuls of flour and then sour cream into the butter.
7. Fry bread cut into cubes, make dough from everything and add enough flour so it does not stick to the hands.
8. Dip a spoon in boiling salted water, take a spoonful of dough with the spoon and drop into water.
9. After a quarter hour, test one dumpling to see if it is cooked.
10. Cut into quarters and arrange around the game.

title: Černá zvěřina se šípkovou omáčkou (Game with Rose Hip Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game meat
- salt water
- celery
- parsley root
- carrots
- 2 onions
- water
- wine
- vinegar
- egg yolk
- rose hip preserves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the game and rinse it thoroughly in several waters.
2. Add celery, parsley root, carrots, and 2 onions cut into slices or strips.
3. Add 2 parts water with 1 part wine and vinegar.
4. Cook until the game is tender.
5. Add an egg yolk to the sauce and rose hip preserves.
6. If it should be darker, burn a piece of sugar, cook, sweeten if needed, and strain over the sliced game.

title: Černá zvěřina zadělávaná (Game Covered in Pastry)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- game meat
- wine
- vinegar
- water
- thyme
- bay leaf
- pepper
- lemon zest
- new spices
- clove
- 2 garlic cloves
- fresh or dried cherries
- butter dough

## Directions

1. Cut the meat, rinse it, and place on a baking pan with wine, vinegar and water.
2. Add thyme, bay leaf, pepper, lemon zest, new spices, clove, 2 garlic cloves, and several fresh or dried cherries.
3. Braise until almost all liquid has evaporated.
4. Carefully remove the cherries and place the game on a serving dish.
5. Strain the remaining pan juices over it and add the cherries back.
6. Cover everything with butter dough.

title: Černá Ředkev (Black Radish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- black radish
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the black radish, cut into thin slices, salt them, cover with another plate and let them release their liquid, drain the water
2. In the absence of other radish, it can be served with beef roast

title: Černé jahody (Black Strawberries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sorted black strawberries
- sugar (to the sixth degree)

## Directions

1. Put sorted black strawberries into sugar boiled to the sixth degree
2. Boil them together again, pour onto a plate
3. Cover with paper and let stand
4. The next day, pour off the sugar so it boils even more
5. Pour the strawberries back into it and boil together
6. Remove foam and put the preserves into containers

title: Černý dort (Black Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups sugar
- 1.5 cups butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 cup black coffee
- flour
- 0.5 cup coffee teaspoon cinnamon
- 0.25 teaspoon cloves
- 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 5 egg whites
- 1.5 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup water
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and butter well.
2. Add 5 yolks and 1 cup black coffee and mix again.
3. Add enough flour to make the dough slightly thicker than pancake batter.
4. Add 0.5 tablespoon cinnamon, 0.25 teaspoon cloves, 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg, and 2 teaspoons baking powder and mix again.
5. Beat 5 egg whites and fold into the dough.
6. Grease and flour the plates and pour the dough into them.
7. Bake in a moderately heated oven.
8. When baked, make frosting by taking 1.5 cups sugar and about 0.5 cup water, bringing to a boil and cooking until it becomes a thick syrup.
9. While the syrup cooks, beat 2 egg whites.
10. When the syrup is ready, slowly pour it into the egg whites while beating rapidly.
11. As soon as it begins to thicken considerably, begin assembling the cake by layering one cake on a plate, spreading frosting on it, and repeating until finished.

title: Černý kuba (Pearl Barley with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small cups large pearl barley
- 1 small cup fresh mushrooms or 1/2 small cup dried mushrooms
- green parsley
- butter or lard
- raw lard cut into fine dice or grated butter
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- garlic

## Directions

1. Cook two small cups of large pearl barley according to the previous method
2. Meanwhile, let one small cup of fresh sliced mushrooms or half a small cup of dried mushrooms stew in butter or lard with green parsley
3. Mix the mushrooms with the barley
4. Add a piece of raw lard cut into small dice or grated butter
5. Season with a little pepper, marjoram and garlic rubbed with salt
6. Stir well and bake in the oven for half an hour
7. Instead of pearl barley, you can also use torn grits
8. Dried mushrooms are more substantial and tastier when soaked in cold water for several hours before use; pour only enough water over them to cover them

title: Čerstvá kukuřice dušená (Stewed Green Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- six corn cobs
- milk
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- sugar

## Directions

1. Shell or scrape the corn kernels from six cobs and place in a bowl
2. Pour about one cup of milk over them and cook
3. Cook for about 10 minutes
4. Mix one spoon of butter with one spoon of flour, blend well and add to the corn
5. Salt, pepper and add a pinch of sugar
6. When it comes to a boil, serve

title: Čerstvá kukuřice s vejci (Corn Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- six corn cobs
- milk
- two eggs
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Shell or scrape kernels from six corn cobs
2. Add one cup of milk and two beaten eggs
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Place in a bowl and bake in the oven for half an hour

title: Čerstvá kukuřice smažená (Fried Green Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn kernels from cobs
- butter
- lard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Shell or scrape corn kernels from the cobs
2. Heat one spoon of butter and one spoon of lard
3. When hot, add the corn, salt and pepper
4. Cover and fry for a quarter hour, stirring frequently

title: Červená řepa (Beet Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- red beets
- horseradish
- anise or fennel seeds
- salt
- wine vinegar
- cloves

## Directions

1. Clean a nice red beet well, cook until tender, peel and slice into rounds
2. Scrape horseradish and cut it into rounds
3. Put into a jar alternating layers of beet, border it with several rounds of horseradish, sprinkle with a little anise or fennel and a little salt; continue until everything is packed
4. Pour good wine vinegar over it and let it stand for twenty-four hours
5. Instead of anise or fennel you can also interlace it with several cloves
6. Serve on a separate plate and pour a little of the liquid over it

title: Červená řipa (Red Beet Pickle)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- red beets
- horseradish
- vinegar
- fennel

## Directions

1. Clean the red beets, rinse them and place whole in a pot; it is better to leave them whole than to cut them in half lengthwise, and cook until soft.
2. Place in cold water, let cool, and peel; slice into discs.
3. Scrape a nice piece of good horseradish and slice it likewise into discs.
4. If the beets are to be eaten immediately, boil the wine vinegar; if to be stored longer, do not boil it.
5. In a container, layer beets, sprinkle with fennel, then add a layer of horseradish.
6. When the container is full, pour vinegar over and seal.
7. Usually served with beef.

title: Červená řipa se zemáky (Red Beet with Potatoes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- red beets
- potatoes
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean several red beets and cook them, place in cold water, and when cooled, peel and slice into discs.
2. Slice an equal quantity of cooked, cooled potatoes likewise into discs.
3. In a pot, place a layer of potatoes, then a layer of beets, until the container is full.
4. Pour good wine vinegar over and let stand for a while; this mixture is served with beef.

title: Červená řípa (Red Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red beets
- Horseradish
- Anise or fennel
- Salt
- Cloves (optional)
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean and cook red beets until tender
2. Peel and cut into rounds
3. Grate horseradish and cut into rounds
4. Layer in a jar: beet round, several horseradish rounds, sprinkle with anise or fennel and salt
5. Continue layering until all ingredients are used
6. Pour good wine vinegar over
7. Let stand for twenty-four hours
8. Can substitute cloves for anise or fennel
9. Serve on a separate dish with a little of the liquid drizzled over
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: České halušky (Czech Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 15 dekagrams flour (10 lots)
- 2-3 eggs
- 9 dekagrams flour (6 lots)
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Boil 3/4 liter of milk, mix in 15 dekagrams of flour so that a thick porridge forms that does not stick to the pot, and let it cool
2. Then mix in 2-3 eggs, 9 dekagrams of flour, salt it
3. Dip a spoon in boiling water and cut dumplings of any size with it, which are cooked in salted water for a quarter hour

title: České Koláčky (Bohemian Tarts)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough (prepared as previously described)
- prepared filling
- egg
- sliced almonds or butter mixed with sugar and flour

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as previously described
2. Work until dough no longer sticks to the spoon
3. Let rise
4. Roll out thinly on a floured surface
5. Cut into small squares
6. Place a piece of prepared filling in the center of each square
7. Fold opposite corners together toward the center
8. Arrange on a buttered baking pan
9. Brush with beaten egg
10. Sprinkle with sliced almonds or with butter mixed with sugar and flour
11. Let rise briefly
12. Bake in a well-heated oven
13. Dust with sugar when done

title: České slejšky (Czech Pastries)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter sifted flour
- 125 grams hard butter, torn into pieces
- 3/8 liter sour cream
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 30 grams yeast
- 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Spread 3/4 liter sifted flour on a board, mix with 125 grams hard butter torn into pieces, 3/8 liter sour cream, 2 egg yolks, salt, 30 grams yeast and 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar
2. Mix thoroughly, work the dough and roll into long pastries
3. Brush with beaten egg and bake slowly on a greased sheet

title: České šišky (Czech Pine Cones)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter fine flour
- 16 grams yeast
- a little milk
- salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Make soft yeast from half liter fine flour, 16 grams yeast, a little milk, salt and 1 tablespoon sugar
2. Work thoroughly until bubbles appear
3. Form into thin, long sticks, let rise on a greased sheet
4. Brush with beaten egg, bake slowly until dark golden, brushing with water glaze during baking

title: Český dort (Czech Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- zest of 2 lemons
- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 320 grams fine potato flour
- juice of 2 lemons
- snow from 16 egg whites
- butter for greasing molds
- fine grated hardtack for dusting molds
- glaze

## Directions

1. Grate the lemon zest over the sugar
2. Crush the sugar by half, sift it, and beat and mix with the 16 egg yolks for about three-quarters of an hour in one direction
3. Add the 320 grams of fine potato flour, the juice of 2 lemons, and the snow from the 16 egg whites
4. Pour the prepared dough into a mold well buttered and lightly dusted with fine grated hardtack
5. Bake slowly for about an hour
6. Brush with glaze and let dry again in the oven

title: Český perník (Molasses Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 koflík syrobu
- 1 koflík cukru
- 1/2 koflíku másla
- 1/2 koflíku silné hořké černé kávy
- 1 lžička sody
- 2 vejce (žloutky)
- 2 koflíky mouky
- 1 lžička zázvoru
- hřebíček
- skořice
- trocha nového koření
- 1/2 libry dobrých hrozinek (volitelně)

## Directions

1. Dám koflík syrobu, koflík cukru, půl koflíku másla, půl koflíku silné hořké černé kávy, lžičku sody a ze dvou vajec žloutky.
2. Přimíchám dva koflíky mouky, lžičku zázvoru, hřebíček, skořici a trošku nového koření; někdy přidám dle libosti půl libry dobrých hrozinek.
3. Rozšleháím bílky na sníh, pak dám asi půl koflíku cukru vařit a když počne houstnout, přidám do něj bílky, dobře promíchám a tím studený již perník poleji.

title: Český perník (Molasses Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one small cup syrup
- one small cup sugar
- half small cup butter
- half small cup strong black coffee
- one teaspoon soda
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Combine one small cup syrup, one small cup sugar, half small cup butter, half small cup strong black coffee
2. Add one teaspoon soda and two egg yolks from two eggs
3. Mix together

title: Český Salát (Bohemian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- two peeled sour apples
- one onion
- a piece of cooked ham
- a piece of cold veal roast
- cooked smoked tongue
- a clean piece of herring, with bones removed
- hard-boiled eggs
- cleaned sardines
- beetroot
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel cooked potatoes while warm and slice into rounds
2. Cut two peeled sour apples into small cubes
3. Cut one onion, a piece of cooked ham, a piece of cold veal roast, cooked smoked tongue, and a clean piece of herring (with bones removed) into small cubes
4. Separate the egg whites and yolks from hard-boiled eggs and chop each separately
5. Cut cleaned sardines lengthwise
6. On a platter, layer potatoes, sprinkle with diced apples, onion, herring, meat and capers and repeat until the platter is full to the top
7. Arrange sardines on top in a star pattern
8. Fill the spaces between the sardines alternately with chopped egg yolk and egg white
9. Around the edge of the platter, arrange alternating identical rounds cut from potatoes and beetroot
10. Finally, pour the salad with oil mixed with vinegar

title: Český salát (Czech Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus tips
- green vegetables
- young green beans
- carrots
- salted water
- vinegar
- oil
- lettuce hearts
- hard-boiled eggs
- crayfish tails
- egg yolks
- pepper
- salt
- fine oil
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook asparagus tips, green vegetables, sliced young green beans, and sliced carrots in salted water until tender
2. Drain the water
3. Marinate in vinegar and oil
4. Place cleaned lettuce hearts in the center of a platter
5. Arrange the marinated vegetables in colorful groups around the lettuce
6. Garnish with sliced hard-boiled eggs and crayfish tails
7. Prepare a sauce from two hard-boiled egg yolks mixed with pepper, salt, fine oil, and good vinegar
8. Pour the sauce over the salad

title: Česneková omáčka s marjánkou (Garlic Sauce with Marjoram)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately three cloves of garlic
- marjoram
- beef broth
- salt
- roux

## Directions

1. Place three cloves of garlic with marjoram in beef broth
2. Season with salt
3. Simmer
4. Add roux and strain

title: Čistá rybí polévka (Clear Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces butter
- onion
- parsley
- carrot
- a piece of celery
- two quarts water
- salt
- a handful of dried mushrooms
- about two pounds of fish (heads and various scraps)
- two egg whites (for clarifying if needed)

## Directions
