1. Let onion, parsley, carrot and a piece of celery stew in two ounces of butter
2. add two quarts of water, salt and a handful of dried mushrooms
3. let it boil until soft
4. then add about two pounds of fish, namely heads and all kinds of scraps from them
5. let it boil for one hour and strain the soup through a cloth
6. if it is cloudy, clarify it with snow from two egg whites
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Čistá želví polévka (Clear Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- dva kvarty čisté hovězí polévky
- kus želvího masa
- kousek mrkve
- cibule
- pór
- celer
- zelená petrželka
- větvička dymiánu
- šalvěj
- marjánka
- bobkový list
- několik zrnek celého pepře
- nové koření
- dva hřebíčky
- kousek celého květu
- dva kvarty silné čisté hovězí polévky
- dvě sklenky sherry
- mletá paprika
- citron

## Directions

1. Do dvou kvartů čisté hovězí polévky dej kus želvího masa
2. Přidej kousek mrkve, cibule, póru, celeru a zelené petružele
3. Přidej větvičku dymiánu, šalvěje a marjánky
4. Přidej jeden bobkový list
5. Přidej v plátěném pytlíčku několik zrnek celého pepře, nového koření, dva hřebíčky a kousek celého květu
6. Nech to asi hodinu vařit
7. Želví maso pak vyndej
8. Rozkrájej na kostky a dej do mísy
9. Polévku proceď do jiného hrnku
10. Přidej dva kvarty silné čisté hovězí polévky
11. Přidej dvě sklenky sherry
12. Přidej špetku tlučené papriky
13. Nech to ještě varem obejít
14. Seber pěnu a vlej do mísy na rozkrájené želví maso
15. Na zvláštním talíři předlož rozkrájený citron
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Čistění rosolu (Cleaning Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- aspic
- egg white snow
- cold water

## Directions

1. From settled aspic, remove the fat accumulated on top and the sediment from the bottom
2. Select the clean aspic, put in a pot, heat to melt, add snow from egg whites
3. Whisk everything until it begins to set on one side, stir the aspic and clean it
4. Add a little cold water, set aside and let settle
5. Strain the aspic through heated cloth several times until completely clean
6. Place the cleaned aspic in a cool place or with ice until it sets solid enough to cut
7. The cleaned aspic is transparent, can be put in glasses and stored for long as a garnish for dishes

title: Čistění Soukenných Šatů (Cleaning Woolen Garments)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 45 gramů (3 loty) obyčejného tabáku
- dva litry a jeden decilitr (1½ mázu) vody

## Directions

1. 45 gramů (3 loty) obyčejného tabáku se svaří ve dvou litrech a jednom decilitru (1½ mázu) vody
2. Tuhý kartáč se omočí v odvaru
3. Dobře se soukenný šat všemi směry okartáčuje
4. Kartáč se tak často namáčí, až voda do sukna vnikne
5. Pak se okartáčuje po vlase

title: Čokoláda (Chocolate)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 deciliters cream
- 5 tablets grated chocolate
- three to four egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Place the grated chocolate in a saucepan with the cream
2. Mix on the stove until completely thick
3. If desired, mix in three to four egg yolks

title: Čokoláda (Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla chocolate
- water or milk and cream
- sugar
- egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. For the first method: Pour a pound of grated vanilla chocolate into a quart of boiling water
2. Let it dissolve completely with frequent stirring
3. Pour into cups and serve with lump sugar and hot whipped cream
4. For the second method: Boil half a pound of grated chocolate in a pint of milk or cream until completely dissolved
5. Add another pint of cream
6. Whip well and continue heating with constant stirring
7. Pour into cups
8. You can also beat a few egg yolks and add them
9. You can also place a teaspoon of whipped cream in each cup

title: Čokoláda s mlékem (Chocolate with Milk)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 20-30 grams good vanilla chocolate per cup
- milk
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the milk
2. Add the broken chocolate pieces
3. Let it foam and boil
4. Mix egg yolks and pour into the mixture

title: Čokoláda s mlékem - Jiný způsob (Chocolate with Milk - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- milk
- sufficient chocolate
- enough sugar

## Directions

1. Place egg yolks in a bowl and beat
2. Add milk, chocolate, and sugar to taste
3. Mix everything and boil
4. Remove from heat and beat constantly
5. Pour into a chocolate pot
6. The chocolate is very tasty and foams well with constant beating

title: Čokoláda ve vině vařena (Chocolate Boiled in Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water
- 250 grams chocolate
- 100 grams sugar
- some vanilla
- 1 bottle wine
- four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil water with chocolate, sugar, and vanilla
2. Let foam and simmer for at least fifteen minutes
3. Pour in a bottle of wine and boil again
4. Before serving, stir in four beaten egg yolks

title: Čokoláda ve vině vařena - Jiný způsob (Chocolate Boiled in Wine - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- chocolate
- wine
- water
- sugar
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil chocolate in wine the same way as chocolate in water
2. Sweeten more and add several beaten egg yolks

title: Čokoláda ve vodě vařená (Chocolate Boiled in Water)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- 3-4 grams finely grated chocolate per cup desired

## Directions

1. Pour as many cups of water into a coffee pot as cups of chocolate you wish to make
2. Add 3-4 grams of finely grated chocolate per cup
3. Boil everything together and remove from heat to settle
4. Warm the cups in hot water beforehand
5. Once the chocolate foams up well, pour into the warmed cups

title: Čokoláda ve vodě vařená - Jiný způsob (Chocolate Boiled in Water - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 grams fine chocolate per cup
- boiling water
- two egg yolks
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take 4-5 grams of fine chocolate per cup and break into pieces
2. Add to boiling water and stir constantly until completely boiled
3. Remove from heat and let it foam up again
4. Stir in two beaten egg yolks
5. Pour into cups and top with whipped cream foam

title: Čokoládová omáčka (Chocolate Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams grated chocolate
- 14 dekagrams ground sugar
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Boil everything together
2. Stir slowly until it thickens
3. Allow to cool

title: Čokoládová omáčka (Chocolate Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- vanilla, sliced
- chocolate
- sugar
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with sliced vanilla
2. Mix in chocolate, sugar, and 3 egg yolks

title: Čokoládová poleva (Chocolate Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 3 small cups sugar
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of 3 eggs into nice stiff snow
2. Slowly add 3 small cups of sugar while stirring constantly
3. Stir a little more
4. Then fold in grated chocolate to taste

title: Čokoládová rosoliua (Chocolate Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four cups milk
- One small cup grated sweet chocolate
- Six egg yolks
- One cup sugar
- One tablespoon butter
- Cornstarch flour
- Six egg whites

## Directions

1. Heat four cups of milk and add to it one small cup of grated sweet chocolate.
2. Beat six egg yolks with one cup of sugar and one tablespoon of butter, pour the milk with chocolate into this, add cornstarch flour as in the preceding recipe and cook.
3. Finally when you remove it from heat, fold in the snow from six egg whites.

title: Čokoládová Zmrzlina (Chocolate Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-quarters liter milk
- Quarter liter cream
- Six ounces grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Place three-quarters liter milk, quarter liter cream and six ounces grated chocolate in a pan
2. Heat and constantly stir
3. Let it cool
4. Proceed as with other ice creams

title: Čokoládová Zmrzlina (Chocolate Ice Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- One liter cream
- Quarter kilogram sugar
- One-eighth grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs and whip until foamy
2. Add one liter cream, quarter kilogram sugar and one-eighth grated chocolate
3. Mix and whip until it sets
4. Let cool
5. Proceed exactly as described for cream ice cream

title: Čokoládová Zmrzlina - Jiný Způsob (Chocolate Ice Cream - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter cream
- Vanilla
- 250 grams sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 250 grams fine chocolate

## Directions

1. Cut vanilla into very small pieces into one liter cream
2. Cook cream with 250 grams sugar
3. Strain it
4. Add 12 egg yolks and 250 grams fine chocolate
5. Whip everything over moderate heat
6. Place in ice until ice cream forms from it

title: Čokoládové cukrovinky (Chocolate Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup syrup
- 1 cup grated chocolate
- 1.5 cups butter
- 1.5 cups water
- 1/2 cup sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put 1 cup brown sugar, 1 cup syrup, 1 cup grated chocolate, 1.5 cups butter, 1.5 cups water, and 1/2 cup sweet cream in a pan
2. Cook on high heat long enough until it draws like threads
3. Pour onto a buttered oblong plate and let cool
4. Before it completely hardens, mark with a knife into squares

title: Čokoládové karamelky (Chocolate Caramels)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound grated bitter chocolate
- four tablespoons butter
- one pound yellow sugar
- half a small cup syrup
- half a small cup cream
- one teaspoon vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Place one quarter pound grated bitter chocolate in a bowl and add four tablespoons of butter, one pound yellow sugar, half a small cup syrup, half a small cup cream, and one teaspoon vanilla extract.
2. Stir over gentle heat until everything is perfectly mixed.
3. Continue boiling until it cracks when a drop is placed in very cold or iced water.
4. Butter a flat-edged plate, pour the mixture onto it to make a layer about half an inch thick, let cool.
5. When almost cold, cut into squares.

title: Čokoládové makaronky (Chocolate Macaroons)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroon mixture as in previous recipe
- three ounces melted fine chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare the macaroon mixture as in the previous recipe and stir in three ounces of melted fine chocolate.
2. Alternatively, you can stir in one ounce of finely pounded cinnamon instead of chocolate.
3. Instead of lemon zest, you can pound the sugar with a piece of vanilla for the macaroons.

title: Čokoládové máslo (Chocolate Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound freshly beaten butter
- Three hard-boiled egg yolks
- Two small tablets grated chocolate
- Sugar
- Vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat half pound of freshly beaten butter with three hard-boiled egg yolks and two small tablets of grated chocolate; if you wish, you may also add a little sugar and a little ground vanilla.
2. This butter is served either with freshly baked rolls or spread on wafers.

title: Čokoládové špalíčky (Chocolate Fudge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups fine sugar
- 1/2 cup cream (or 1/2 cup milk with 1 tablespoon butter)
- 2 ounces chocolate
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- peanuts or coconut (optional)

## Directions

1. Put 2 cups fine sugar in a bowl
2. Add 1/2 cup cream, or if not available, 1/2 cup milk with 1 tablespoon butter
3. Add 2 ounces chocolate
4. Cook on high heat for about 10 minutes
5. When cooked, add 1 teaspoon vanilla and you can mix in shelled peanuts or coconut
6. When nicely thickened, pour onto a plate and let it harden for about an hour
7. Then cut into squares

title: Čokoládový chleb (Chocolate Bread)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 200 grams crushed sugar
- 60 grams grated chocolate
- 375 grams ground almonds
- wafer sheets

## Directions

1. Whip the eggs into stiff snow
2. Add the crushed sugar, grated chocolate, and ground almonds
3. Mix everything together
4. Spread thinly on wafer sheets
5. Bake at moderate temperature
6. Cut the baked bread into long strips

title: Čokoládový Creme (Chocolate Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- seven decagrams chocolate
- four deciliters cream
- seven decagrams sugar
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil seven decagrams of chocolate with four deciliters of cream
2. Let cool
3. Mix with seven decagrams of sugar and six egg yolks

title: Čokoládový creme (Chocolate Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deka chocolate
- four deciliters cream
- seven deka sugar
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil seven deka of chocolate with four deciliters of cream
2. Let it cool, mix with seven deka sugar and six egg yolks

title: Čokoládový Creme, Jiný Způsob (Chocolate Cream, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deciliters cream
- 28 decagrams chocolate
- 14 decagrams sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 2 eggs
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil seven deciliters of cream with 28 decagrams of chocolate and 14 decagrams of sugar
2. Let cool
3. Mix in 12 egg yolks, 2 eggs, and a little vanilla
4. Pour cream into small pots and place in a water bath in the oven
5. Bake for half an hour
6. Spread a cloth on a plate, place the pots on it, and serve at table

title: Čokoládový Creme, Tmavý (Dark Chocolate Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ten decagrams chocolate
- four deciliters water
- sugar
- five egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil ten decagrams of chocolate with four deciliters of water
2. Sweeten to taste
3. Add five egg yolks

title: Čokoládový dort (Chocolate Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 dekagrams (24 lots) peeled ground almonds
- half kilogram sugar
- 15 egg yolks
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) grated chocolate
- a little vanilla
- lemon zest from one lemon
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- wafer
- sugar glaze

## Directions

1. Mix 36 dekagrams peeled ground almonds, half kilogram sugar, 15 egg yolks, 18 dekagrams grated chocolate, a little vanilla, and lemon zest from one lemon
2. If too thin, add a handful of grated breadcrumbs
3. Beat for an hour
4. Grease a baking form with butter and line with wafer
5. Bake the cake
6. Decorate with sugar glaze

title: Čokoládový dort (Chocolate Loaf Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coffee cup of butter
- two coffee cups of sugar
- half coffee cup of sweet milk
- six egg whites
- three coffee cups of flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- one teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a dough from the ingredients
2. divide it in half
3. grate one quarter tablet of chocolate into the second half of the dough
4. alternately place one spoonful of white dough and one spoonful of dark dough into the pan, continuing until the pan is full
5. bake until done
6. coat the surface with chocolate icing when baked

title: Čokoládový dort (Chocolate Loaf Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup sweet milk
- 6 egg whites
- 3 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 0.25 square chocolate

## Directions

1. Make a dough with 1 cup butter, 2 cups sugar, 0.5 cup sweet milk, 6 egg whites, 3 cups flour, 2 teaspoons baking powder, and 1 teaspoon vanilla.
2. Divide the dough in half.
3. To the second half, grate 0.25 square of chocolate.
4. Alternate in the mold: 1 tablespoon white dough, then 1 tablespoon dark dough, continuing until the mold is full.

title: Čokoládový dort na jiný způsob (Chocolate Loaf Cake, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- half coffee cup of sweet milk
- one beaten egg yolk
- half coffee cup of grated chocolate
- one coffee cup of sugar
- one heaping spoonful of butter
- half coffee cup of milk
- two coffee cups of sifted flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- one teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix the sweet milk, beaten egg yolk, and grated chocolate
2. place on the stove and cook while stirring constantly until thickened
3. remove from heat and let cool
4. add the sugar, butter, milk, and sifted flour with baking powder
5. add the vanilla and mix well
6. pour into four metal plates and bake over moderate heat
7. when baked and cooled, place one coffee cup of fine sugar with two spoonfuls of water on the stove
8. let cook briefly until it drips from the spoon like threads
9. pour onto one beaten egg white while stirring constantly
10. coat each layer and let dry slightly, then stack them together

title: Čokoládový Nákyp (Chocolate Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) sweet cream
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- juice from half a lemon
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) butter
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 12 dekagrams ground almonds
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) grated chocolate
- lemon zest from half a lemon
- 5 egg whites
- butter and flour for the mold

## Directions

1. Mix 7 deciliters (2 tablespoons) of sweet cream with 2 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, and juice from half a lemon
2. Cook this mixture until it curdles, then strain off the water
3. Beat 18 dekagrams (12 lots) of butter with 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of sugar and 10 egg yolks
4. Add 12 dekagrams of ground almonds and 9 dekagrams (6 lots) of grated chocolate
5. Add lemon zest from half a lemon
6. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 5 egg whites
7. Grease the mold with butter and dust with flour
8. Bake the soufflé, then sprinkle with sugar

title: Čokoládový Nákyp (Chocolate Soufflé)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients as for lemon soufflé
- finely grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as for lemon soufflé, but add finely grated chocolate to it

title: Čokoládový nákyp (Puffed Chocolate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons grated bread or white flour
- 3 cups milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablets grated chocolate
- lemon zest
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Gradually dissolve 3 tablespoons grated bread or white flour in 3 cups milk
2. Add 2 tablespoons butter and 2 tablespoons sugar
3. Keep stirring and let it cook, then allow to cool
4. Add 4 grated tablets of chocolate and a little lemon zest
5. Add 6 egg yolks and beat well
6. Fold in stiff peaks made from 6 egg whites
7. Put into a buttered mold lined with white flour
8. Bake slowly, then turn out onto a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Čokoládový Nákyp, Jiný Způsob (Chocolate Soufflé, Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of milk
- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) chocolate
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) butter
- 19 dekagrams (almost 13 lots) sugar
- 2 tablespoons grated bread roll
- a little ground vanilla
- 8 egg whites
- sponge cake (pishkotem) for coating

## Directions

1. Cook 24 dekagrams (16 lots) of chocolate in half a liter of milk, stirring continuously, and let it cool
2. Beat 12 dekagrams (8 lots) of butter until it froths
3. Add 19 dekagrams (almost 13 lots) of sugar, the cooled chocolate, 2 tablespoons of grated bread roll, and a little ground vanilla
4. Fold in stiff egg white snow from 8 egg whites
5. Bake in a mold coated with sponge cake and sprinkle the baked soufflé with sugar

title: Čokoládový Štrudel (Chocolate Strudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dough for ordinary strudel
- sugar
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) ground almonds
- 18 dekagrams (12 lots) grated chocolate
- 6 egg yolks
- egg white snow
- sour cream
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Make dough for ordinary strudel and work it well
2. Let rest under a lukewarm bowl
3. Stretch the dough
4. Sprinkle the buttered dough with sugar, 9 dekagrams ground almonds, 18 dekagrams grated chocolate
5. Add 6 grated egg yolks and snow from egg whites
6. Roll up strudel
7. Pour sour cream over it and sprinkle with grated chocolate

title: Čočka (Lentils)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- soft water
- salt
- butter or fried onions
- eggs, cooked in butter

## Directions

1. Pick through lentils and cook in soft water
2. When half-cooked, salt them
3. Add water if needed and finish cooking
4. Drain the water and turn lentils onto a plate
5. Pour melted butter or fried onion over them
6. Add eggs that have been cooked in butter

title: Čočka (Lentils)
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- eggs fried in butter, sausages, fried brain, or fried tongue

## Directions

1. Lentils are cooked in the same way as peas.
2. You can garnish them with either eggs fried in butter, sausages, fried brain, or fried tongue.

title: Čočka na kyselo (Sour Lentils)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- soft water
- butter
- flour
- vinegar
- fried onion
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Pick through lentils, wash and cook in soft water
2. Make a roux on butter
3. Add to lentils as needed
4. Pour in a little vinegar and cook
5. On the plate, pour fried onion over it
6. Hard-boiled eggs can be added to this lentil dish

title: Čočková polévka postní (Puree of Lentils)
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- parsley water
- butter
- marjoram
- clove
- bread
- garlic

## Directions

1. Cook lentils nicely until soft
2. Mix them
3. Pour parsley water on them and let simmer a bit
4. Strain the soup, fry it with brown roux, add a little marjoram, a little clove and pour over fried bread cubes
5. Whoever likes it can boil one or two cloves of garlic with the lentils before straining
6. It is also possible to cook grains into the strained soup and let cook until they soften
7. Lentils can also be soaked overnight in cold water and then placed in hot water for cooking
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Čínský dort (Chinese Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams butter
- 8 whole eggs
- 8 egg yolks
- 125 grams peeled and crushed or chopped almonds
- 500 grams sugar
- 12 hard-boiled eggs, pressed through a sieve
- lemon zest, finely grated
- 4 grams crushed cinnamon
- 250 grams finest flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat 500 grams butter until foamy
2. Slowly beat in 8 whole eggs and 8 egg yolks
3. Add 125 grams peeled and crushed or chopped almonds, 500 grams sugar, and 12 hard-boiled eggs pressed through a sieve
4. Add finely grated lemon zest and 4 grams crushed cinnamon
5. Mix in 250 grams finest flour
6. Season lightly with salt and mix into dough
7. Divide into 4-5 equal-sized cake portions
8. Place on buttered paper on a baking sheet
9. Bake slowly until completely done
10. Turn out onto a sieve to cool
11. When cooled, brush with preserves, stack together, decorate variously, and coat with glaze

title: Čínský dort (Chinese Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram butter
- eight whole eggs
- eight egg yolks
- 125 grams peeled and crushed or chopped almonds
- half kilogram sugar
- twelve hard-boiled eggs, pressed through a sieve
- finely grated lemon zest
- 4 grams crushed nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat half kilogram of butter until creamy
2. Gradually add eight whole eggs, eight egg yolks, 125 grams peeled and crushed or chopped almonds, half kilogram sugar, twelve hard-boiled eggs pressed through a sieve, finely grated lemon zest, and 4 grams crushed nutmeg

title: Ředkev (Red Radish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- red radish
- salt
- flour
- egg
- bread crust
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel the red radish, remove the outer leaves leaving only the inner ones, salt it, coat with flour, dip in beaten egg, roll in grated bread crust, drop into hot butter and fry until golden
2. Serve around beef roast

title: Řetkvičky (Radishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh radishes
- cold water
- ice
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Select nice fresh radishes - not hollow ones - place them in cold water for half an hour and then dry them well
2. Cut off the roots and leave only two or three of the smallest leaves from the tops
3. Place on a separate dish with several pieces of ice
4. Put fresh butter on the table so each person can serve themselves as they wish
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Řeřicha (Watercress)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- young, tender watercress leaves
- salt
- good oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Trim young, tender leaves
2. Rinse
3. Season with salt
4. Pour good oil and vinegar over them or prepare with salad sauce
5. Serve to table

title: Řeřichový salát (Watercress Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- sugar
- endive or cabbage
- sour apples

## Directions

1. Clean the selected watercress well and prepare it with vinegar and oil, a little salt and sugar
2. Put it on a plate either alone or border it with endive, cabbage, or peeled apples sliced into rounds

title: Řídké domácí kvasnice (Liquid Home Made Yeast Starter)
body:
## Ingredients

- šest bramborů
- galon vody
- jeden rozmočený čtvereček suchých nebo kousek lisovaných kvasnic, nebo koflík řídkých
- koflíček cukru
- půl koflíčku soli

## Directions

1. Uvařte u večer šest bramborů v galonu vody
2. Když uvařené, slejte z nich tu vodu do nějaké nádoby
3. Brambory rozmačkejte a protlačte sítkem na mísu
4. Když vychladly, nalejte na ně vodu, v které se vařily
5. Přidejte jeden rozmočený čtvereček suchých, aneb kousek lisovaných kvasnic, aneb koflík řídkých
6. Přidejte koflíček cukru a půl koflíčku soli
7. Zamíchejte a nechte státi do rána
8. Ráno odlejmete těchto kvasnic pint pro další potřebu a ostatními zadělmete na chléb
9. Pokaždé když je děláte, tedy ráno při zadělávání jich asi pint odlijete a v ovocné sklenici dobře zašroubované na chladném místě postavíte k budoucí potřebě

title: Řízky (Breaded Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- onion
- butter
- garlic
- lemon zest
- salt
- pepper
- 4 tablespoons beef broth
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil beef and chop finely
2. Fry half an onion in butter and add it
3. Add minced garlic, lemon zest, salt, pepper, and beef broth
4. Simmer for a quarter hour
5. Strain through a sieve so all liquid drains and meat cools
6. Flatten pieces like eggs, coat with egg and bread crumbs
7. Fry

title: Řízky (Sardine Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- 4 sardines
- 45 grams butter
- beef broth
- lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare meat as in the previous recipe
2. Remove bones from sardines and mix with 45 grams butter
3. Place on a pan, arrange cutlets on top
4. Pour beef broth over and braise
5. Usually add lemon cut into quarters

title: Řízky (Schnitzel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from leg
- salt
- pepper
- bacon
- 30 grams butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cut meat into slices two fingers thick
2. Remove all sinews and fat, beat with a mallet
3. Salt and pepper, lard with bacon
4. Place on a baking dish with butter or lard
5. Braise slowly

title: Řízky jazykové (Tongue Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tongues
- Egg
- Bread roll, grated
- Butter

## Directions

1. Peel cooked tongues and cut into thin oblong slices.
2. Dip these in egg, coat in grated bread roll and fry in butter.

title: Řízky s karfiolem (Schnitzel with Cauliflower)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- lard or butter
- onion
- 12 dekagrams sardines
- 12 dekagrams butter
- 2 cauliflower roses
- salt water

## Directions

1. Cut sirloin into thin slices, flatten with a mallet, salt, pepper, and dust with flour
2. Fry half an onion in lard or butter and brown the schnitzel well on both sides
3. Mix sardines with butter, coat schnitzel with this mixture and braise for a quarter hour
4. Boil two cauliflower roses in salt water, place one in the center
5. Arrange schnitzel around it and cover with the other rose
6. Pour sauce over schnitzel and top cauliflower with butter

title: Řízky ze svíčkovice (Sirloin Cutlets)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- beef broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Slice the sirloin into pieces the thickness of a finger
2. Salt and pepper them
3. Quickly roast on butter
4. Place on another baking sheet, baste with broth
5. Add juice from one lemon, braise briefly
6. Serve with buttered potatoes

title: Šampaňské Víno z Ovoce (Sparkling Fruit Wine)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Juicy, ripe pears
- Optional: raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Sparkling wine is best made from juicy, ripe pears.
2. Grate and press the pears; pour the juice into a barrel or bottles depending on the quantity, tie the opening with cloth only, and let everything rest peacefully.
3. Within two to three days the juice begins to ferment, foam develops and pushes out of the opening.
4. When the foam subsides, fill the bottles and barrel with fermented fruit juice, seal the opening tightly, and place the containers in a cool cellar for several weeks (five to six).
5. After this time, siphon the clarified juice through a tap into strong bottles, seal them tightly, bind with wire and seal with wax or pitch.
6. This method produces very tasty sparkling wine.
7. If three parts pear juice and one part raspberry juice are taken, with the same treatment a very good, lovely wine is obtained.
8. If the wine is allowed to ferment completely and rest in the cellar for a full year so that it loses carbonation, a very good drinkable wine is obtained.

title: Šampaňský Punč (Champagne Punch)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe pineapple
- sugar
- 2 liters good wine
- 1 liter champagne wine

## Directions

1. Rub the ripe pineapple against sugar
2. Pour 2 liters of good wine over the sugar
3. Let it stand until the sugar is completely dissolved
4. Strain the mixture
5. Add 1 liter of champagne wine
6. Serve at table

title: Šil s křenem (Pike with Horseradish)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, cleaned
- salt
- water
- onion
- bay leaf
- spices
- horseradish, grated
- butter, browned
- potatoes, boiled or fried

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned pike into smaller pieces
2. Boil in salted water to which onion, bay leaf, and other spices have been added
3. Remove from heat and arrange on a platter
4. Sprinkle generously with grated horseradish
5. Pour browned butter over it
6. Serve with boiled or fried potatoes around it

title: Šil s maionesou (Pike with Mayonnaise)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, cleaned and skinned
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- lemon juice
- mayonnaise sauce
- crayfish tails
- capers
- sardines
- aspic (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the pike, skin it, and remove all bones
2. Cut into small pieces and salt them
3. Braise in butter and remove to a sieve to drain and cool
4. Season with salt and pepper, and coat with lemon juice
5. Place the pike pieces on a platter in mayonnaise sauce
6. Garnish with crayfish tails, capers, sardines, or aspic

title: Šil s petruzelí (Pike with Parsley)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, cleaned and cut into pieces
- butter
- onion, sliced
- parsley roots
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- meat broth
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Coat the cleaned pike pieces with butter
2. Place in a casserole lined with sliced onion and parsley roots
3. Season with salt, pepper, and spices
4. Pour meat broth over it
5. Cook until tender
6. Arrange the cooked pike on a platter and pour over the thickened sauce from the casserole
7. Serve with roasted, mashed, or sliced potatoes around it

title: Šil s Ústřicemi (Sheatfish with Oysters)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- sheatfish
- butter
- oyster sauce or oyster ragout
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the sheatfish thoroughly and gut it
2. Rinse and cut in half along the back
3. Remove skin and bones from both halves
4. Slice the meat into small thin pieces and salt
5. Slowly stew in melted butter
6. Drain the butter, arrange the sheatfish pieces neatly on a platter, and pour oyster sauce over them or garnish with oyster ragout in the center

title: Šil se zelím (Pike with Cabbage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cooked or roasted pike
- sour cabbage
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove bones from leftover pike and cut into pieces
2. Arrange in layers with sour cabbage in a buttered mold lined with breadcrumbs
3. Top with breadcrumbs
4. Bake for half an hour until browned

title: Šil v Másle (Sheatfish in Butter)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- large sheatfish
- thin sticks
- onion, split
- bay leaves
- clove
- pepper
- other spices
- parsley roots
- butter
- salt
- green parsley
- crayfish tails
- buttered potatoes

## Directions

1. Tear the sheatfish, clean it, rinse, tie with threads, salt it, and place on a baking sheet with thin sticks arranged like a grill
2. Arrange split onion underneath, bay leaves, clove, pepper, and other spices, along with cleaned and chopped parsley roots and plenty of butter
3. Quickly stew the sheatfish; when soft, remove it, untie it, and arrange on a platter
4. Place green parsley in the mouth, surround with parsley and crayfish tails, buttered potatoes, etc.
5. Pour butter over everything and serve

title: Šipková omáčka (Rose Hip Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter fresh rose hips
- 3/4 liter water
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- 1/4 liter white wine
- 9 dekagrams sugar
- potato flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Clean 1/2 liter of fresh rose hips
2. Cook in 3/4 liter of water with cinnamon and lemon peel
3. Strain
4. Add 1/4 liter of white wine
5. Add 9 dekagrams of sugar
6. Add a small spoonful of potato flour and a little cold water

title: Šišky (Fried Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of warmed flour
- half a coffeepot of rubbed butter
- four egg yolks
- a bit of salt
- finely grated lemon peel
- ground cloves
- a piece of pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm cream
- two tablespoons of sultanas

## Directions

1. Put the warmed flour on a board, add the rubbed butter, egg yolks, salt, lemon peel, and cloves
2. Mix in a piece of pressed yeast that has been dissolved in enough lukewarm cream to make a thick dough
3. Knead thoroughly until the dough no longer sticks to the board or spoon
4. Add the sultanas, dust with flour, and let rise in a warm place
5. Turn the dough onto a flour-dusted board, cut into pieces, and shape into small elongated forms (šišky)
6. Let them rise again
7. Fry in heated clarified butter or lard, using enough fat so the šišky do not touch the bottom
8. When golden brown on both sides, remove from the pan and coat in powdered sugar

title: Šišky (Pine Cones)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 dekagrams (24 lots) butter
- 2 and 1 deciliters (6 tablespoons) flour
- 2 egg yolks
- sweet sour cream
- several tablespoons yeast (baking soda)
- lemon
- 1 tablespoon ground sugar
- flower
- salt
- wine
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) chopped almonds
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat 36 dekagrams butter, add 2 and 1 deciliters flour, 2 egg yolks, sweet sour cream, several tablespoons yeast, lemon, 1 tablespoon ground sugar and flower
2. Let stiff dough rise a while, then salt it, add wine and 12 dekagrams chopped almonds
3. Form into pine cones and sprinkle with sugar

title: Šištičky z mandlí (Almond Puffs)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams almonds with skin
- breadcrumb crust, grated
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 dekagrams butter
- 75 grams sugar

## Directions

1. Pound almonds with skin and sift
2. Mix with grated breadcrumb crust, 2 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, 3 dekagrams butter, and 75 grams sugar
3. When everything is beaten, form puffs
4. Fry them and drizzle with punch on a plate
5. Before serving at the table, pour liquor over them and set on fire

title: Škubánky z Jahlí (Pearl Barley Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter pearl barley
- hot water
- 1 liter milk
- butter
- 4 eggs
- sweet cream
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Scald the pearl barley in hot water to remove its distinctive taste.
2. Boil in 1 liter of milk with butter.
3. Pour onto a plate to cool.
4. Add 4 eggs, sweet cream, salt, and flour to form a thick dough.
5. Form dumplings and coat with flour.
6. Boil in salted water.
7. Serve with butter or preserve.

title: Škubánky z Krupice (Semolina Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- butter
- semolina

## Directions

1. Salt the water and add a piece of butter.
2. When the water begins to boil, add enough semolina to thicken sufficiently.
3. Heat either onion with butter or butter alone.
4. Dip a spoon in the butter, then use it to take dumplings.
5. Sprinkle on a plate with either gingerbread or sugar and cinnamon.

title: Škubánky z Rýže (Rice Dumplings)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams washed rice
- water or milk
- 4 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- grated bread roll
- salted water
- good broth

## Directions

1. Boil the washed rice in water or milk.
2. Mix with 4 eggs, salt, and nutmeg.
3. When cooled, form small dumplings rolled in grated bread roll.
4. Boil in salted water.
5. Serve in good broth.

title: Šlaky nadívané (Stuffed Woodcocks)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- woodcock livers
- woodcock hearts
- chicken livers
- butter
- bread
- egg yolks
- salt
- juniper berries

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcocks and cook them; boil the livers, hearts and several chicken livers.
2. Chop finely and mix with butter, grated bread and egg yolks.
3. Salt and add a few crushed juniper berries.
4. Stuff the woodcocks with this filling and sew them closed.
5. Slowly roast on both sides in butter.

title: Šlehaný Creme (Whipped Cream)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- very cold raw cream
- sugar
- spices or liqueur

## Directions

1. Take very cold raw cream and whip it until completely fluffy
2. Sweeten and flavor with spices or a pleasant liqueur
3. Optionally add a small amount of fish bladder to thicken
4. Mix with chopped almonds, ground chocolate, etc.
5. Use excellently for filling various pastries

title: Šneci (Snails)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- snails with shells
- beef broth or salted water
- 1/8 kilogram of butter
- 1/8 kilogram of sardines
- garlic, salt
- marjoram leaves
- grated roll
- broth from cooking the snails

## Directions

1. Wash the snails with their shells and cook in beef broth or salted water.
2. When the shells open, remove the snails with a fork or knife and cut off the tails.
3. Finely chop the snails.
4. Add 1/8 kilogram of butter and the same amount of sardines which have been boned.
5. Grind the sardines finely with garlic that has been rubbed with salt, marjoram leaves and grated roll.
6. Combine with some of the broth in which the snails were cooked.
7. Pour the mixture onto a baking sheet, bake in an oven and serve on a plate.
8. If the shells are kept and cleaned, they can later be filled with just the filling without snails.

title: Šneci - Jiný Způsob (Snails - Another Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- snails with shells
- beef broth or salted water
- 1/4 kilogram of butter
- more than 1/2 kilogram of sardines
- garlic in salt
- marjoram leaves
- ground pepper
- nutmeg
- grated roll
- broth from cooking the snails

## Directions

1. Wash the snails with their shells and cook in beef broth or salted water.
2. When the shells open, remove the snails with a fork or knife and cut off the tails.
3. Clean the shell interiors well with salt, cook out, place opening side down to drain, and dry with a clean cloth.
4. For 24 snails, use 1/4 kilogram of butter and slightly more than 1/2 kilogram of sardines.
5. Remove all bones from the sardines.
6. First grind the sardines alone, then together with the butter.
7. Add either 6 smaller or 4 larger pieces of garlic rubbed in salt, a pinch of well-ground marjoram leaves, a little ground pepper, nutmeg and enough grated roll so the filling does not run, and pour in a little of the broth in which the snails were cooked.
8. Into each shell place first a piece of filling, then a snail, then filling again; finally add grated roll and press down with a knife.
9. Place the shells opening side up next to each other on a baking sheet, pour broth over them and bake slowly in an oven.
10. Arrange neatly on a serving dish and cover with a cloth.

title: Šneci - Třetí Způsob (Snails - Third Method)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- snails in shells
- salted water
- beef broth or salted water
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Wash the snails in their shells and cook in salted water.
2. When the shells begin to open, remove the snails and cut off the tails.
3. If they are tough, cook them either in beef broth or in salted water.
4. When completely soft, serve on a plate with horseradish.

title: Šodó (Sabayon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks
- sweetened wine or fruit juice

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks with sweetened wine or fruit juice
2. Pour into a pot and heat on warm stove while constantly whisking with a whisk
3. When the liquid begins to rise, remove the pot from heat, continuing to whisk until it is completely foamy and serve at table
4. To serve cold, place the pot in cold water while continuing to whisk

title: Šodó Likérkové (Liqueur Sabayon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- wine sabayon
- good rum or liqueur
- or maraschino liqueur, orange juice, sugar, and eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare ordinary wine sabayon and add several tablespoons of good rum or liqueur
2. Alternatively, use half maraschino liqueur and half orange juice with enough sugar and eight egg yolks

title: Šodó Punčové (Punch Sabayon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one-half deciliters cooled tea
- juice from one lemon
- juice from two oranges
- sugar
- rum
- eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Add juice from one lemon and two oranges to two and one-half deciliters of cooled tea
2. Add sugar and rum
3. Add eight egg yolks

title: Šodó Šťavnaté (Juice Sabayon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- three deciliters strawberry or raspberry juice or similar
- lemon juice
- 15 decagrams sugar
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Add a little lemon juice to three deciliters of strawberry, raspberry, or similar juice
2. Add 15 decagrams of sugar and six egg yolks
3. Beat thoroughly and heat until it rises from the pot

title: Španělské Mandle (Spanish Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- white sugared almonds
- sugar
- carmine dye dissolved in gum water

## Directions

1. Take almonds prepared white and sugared as previously described
2. On hot stove with constant turning, coat with carmine dye dissolved in gum water
3. When the layer has hardened and dried, pour boiled sugar again
4. Turn almonds in it until they reach the size of pigeon eggs

title: Španělské mandle (Spanish Almonds)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds prepared white and sugared as previously described
- carmine dissolved in gum water
- dissolved sugar

## Directions

1. Coat the almonds with constant turning on warm stoves
2. Color them with carmine dissolved in gum water (both substances obtainable from a materials supplier)
3. Once the layer has hardened and dried, pour dissolved sugar over again
4. Turn the almonds in it until they reach the size of pigeon eggs

title: Španělské větry (Spanish Meringues)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg whites
- sugar (1 tablespoon per egg white)
- 24 dekagrams almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 10 egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Count 1 tablespoon of pounded sugar per egg white
3. Blanch and chop 24 dekagrams almonds into noodles, let them dry, and add to the meringue
4. Mix well together
5. Form mounds on a waxed or paper-covered baking sheet
6. Dry for 3 hours in a warm oven

title: Španělský sníh (Spanish Kisses)
body:
## Ingredients

- five egg whites
- one pound powdered sugar
- zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Whip five egg whites into stiff snow
2. Slowly mix in one pound powdered sugar
3. Add zest scraped from one lemon
4. Using a small spoon, form oblong mounds on a baking sheet thinly coated with wax
5. Bake in the oven until cooled, nice and crispy, and pale yellow
6. Remove mounds from sheet and arrange on a serving dish

title: Špargiové purée se žabími stehénky (Purée of Asparagus with Frog's Legs)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus heads
- asparagus lower parts
- salt
- water
- butter
- asparagus heads
- flower (seasoning)
- bread cubes, fried
- frog's legs
- butter
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Peel the asparagus heads, slice the lower parts and cook in salted water until soft
2. Press through a sieve and brown with light butter
3. Add the asparagus heads and let cook for about a quarter hour
4. Season with salt and a little flower (seasoning)
5. Pour into a dish with fried bread cubes and frog's legs that have been stewed in butter with chopped parsley
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Špargiové purée se žabími stehénky (Purée of Asparagus with Frog's Legs)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus heads
- asparagus stems
- salted water
- butter
- flour
- parsnip flower
- bread cubes
- frog legs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Reserve the asparagus heads, remove the lower part of the stems and cook in salted water until soft
2. Then press through a sieve and fry with a light roux
3. Add the asparagus heads, let cook about a quarter hour
4. Salt, season with a little parsnip flower and pour into a bowl over fried bread cubes and frog legs, stewed on butter with chopped parsley
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Šparglový salát (Asparagus Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Cut off the upper green part of nicely cleaned asparagus and cook it in salted water
2. Drain the water, let the asparagus drain, arrange it on a plate, pour with oil and vinegar and sprinkle with a little pepper
3. You can make a border of cauliflower cooked in salted water, broken into small florets and moistened either in oil and vinegar or in remoulade

title: Špekové koláče (Bacon Tarts)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- well-worked dough
- bacon
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Roll the well-worked dough into thin pieces
2. Sprinkle with chopped bacon and caraway seeds
3. Seal
4. Bake well until reddish

title: Špekový Salát (Bacon Salad)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- firm white bacon
- vinegar
- head lettuce
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut firm white bacon into small cubes
2. Fry until crispy
3. Add a little vinegar
4. While everything is warm—neither too hot nor too cold—pour over a well-prepared head lettuce salad
5. Mix and serve while the bacon is still warm

title: Špenát (Spinach)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach leaves
- salt
- butter
- onion
- flour or bread crust
- beef broth
- garlic (2 cloves)
- flower (garnish)
- raw egg yolks (2)
- smoked tongue, poached eggs, or other fat preparations (optional)

## Directions

1. Use only spinach leaves, discard the stems, rinse and cook in salted water until tender, strain and chop finely or press through a coarse sieve
2. On a small pan, place half or a whole chopped onion with butter according to the amount of spinach
3. When heated, add flour or grated bread crust until pink, then add the spinach and mix
4. Add good beef broth and let it cook
5. During cooking add minced 2 cloves of garlic, flower garnish and 2 raw egg yolks and mix
6. Can be served with smoked tongue, poached eggs or any other fat preparation

title: Špenát po Anglicku (English Spinach)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- tender young spinach leaves
- salt
- water
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Select tender young spinach leaves, rinse several times, cook in salted water, place on a strainer to cool so the water drains, squeeze out, chop
2. On a small pan, stew for a few minutes with salt, butter and pepper
3. Serve on a dish, decorated as desired with other dishes

title: Špenát pro Vegetariány (Spinach for Vegetarians)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- hot water
- butter
- onion
- salt
- bread crust

## Directions

1. Cook nice selected and cleaned spinach in hot water until tender, drain the water, chop the spinach
2. Melt butter until brown and fry, add onion and slowly stew the spinach with it, salt it and before serving sprinkle with fried grated bread crust

title: Špenát s Flíčky (Spinach with Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough for thick noodles
- Salt
- Water
- Spinach prepared as in recipe 499
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make dough for thick noodles and cut into flíčky (flat noodle pieces)
2. Cook in salted water and drain
3. On a platter, alternate layers of noodles and spinach prepared according to recipe 499
4. Repeat until all ingredients are used, ending with a spinach layer
5. Sprinkle generously with breadcrumbs and pour melted hot butter over top

title: Špenát s Kyselou Omáčkou (Spinach with Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Salt
- Water
- About 0.5 cup sour cream
- 3-4 tablespoons vinegar
- Same amount sugar
- 2 hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Clean spinach thoroughly and cook in boiling water with a pinch of salt
2. When cooked, cut finely with a knife
3. Mix together about 0.5 cup sour cream, 3-4 tablespoons vinegar and equal amount of sugar
4. Pour this mixture over the spinach
5. Garnish with 2 hard-boiled eggs cut into slices if desired

title: Špenát s Vejcemi (Spinach with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young tender spinach leaves
- Salt
- Water
- Beef broth
- 1 tablespoon butter
- Small teaspoon flour
- 2 whole eggs
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pick young tender leaves, best when about six are unfolded
2. Clean and wash the leaves thoroughly, then cook in salted boiling water for about ten minutes
3. Drain in a colander
4. Place on a wooden cutting board and chop finely with a knife
5. Return to a bowl and add 1.5 cups beef broth, or water if broth is unavailable
6. Season with salt and stew for about 20 minutes
7. Make a pale roux from 1 tablespoon butter and a small teaspoon of flour
8. Add to spinach
9. Break 2 whole eggs into the mixture
10. Season with pepper and mix well
11. Cook a bit longer
12. If desired, add a pinch of nutmeg

title: Špenát se Sladkou Omáčkou (Spinach with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young fresh spinach leaves
- Salt
- Water
- Shallots or young onion greens
- Beef broth
- Pepper
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 hard biscuits
- 0.5 cup thick sour cream

## Directions

1. Pick and clean beautiful young leaves
2. Wash in several waters
3. Place in a pot and pour boiling water over, cover and heat about five minutes
4. Squeeze out moisture, place on a board and add some shallots or young onion greens
5. Chop together finely
6. Cook in a clean pot with about 1 cup beef broth, or water if necessary, covered tightly until completely thickened
7. Season with pepper and salt
8. For each cup of chopped spinach, use 1 rounded tablespoon butter, 2 softened hard biscuits and 0.5 cup thick sour cream mixed in at the end

title: Špenát se Špekem (Spinach with Bacon)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked spinach
- bacon
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Let the cooked spinach cool so the water is released, place on a strainer
2. Cut bacon into cubes and fry, make a roux with flour, and while constantly stirring stew and mix with the spinach

title: Špenát v Masité Omáčce (Spinach with Meat Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- White flour
- Good beef broth
- Ground pepper
- Flowers

## Directions

1. Sort and clean spinach thoroughly in several waters
2. Blanch in boiling salted water
3. Drain, rinse with cold water and press out well
4. Chop finely
5. Make a thin roux from butter and white flour
6. Add chopped spinach and stew gently
7. Gradually add good beef broth
8. Season with ground flower and salt
9. Cook until spinach is very thick
10. Serve with veal cutlets, fried chicken, or fried veal udder if desired

title: Štere čili Ovčácké Buchty z Pohanky (Buckwheat Fried Cakes)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- buckwheat
- water or milk
- butter
- salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook buckwheat with constant stirring in water or milk with butter and salt
2. Let the porridge cool, add 4 egg yolks and snow from 4 egg whites
3. Mix everything, grease a baking pan and bake the štere in an oven

title: Štika na modro (Blued Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the pike au bleu in the same way as carp au bleu, only add a piece of fresh butter to the pike.

title: Štika na modro (Blued Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the pike in the same manner as carp blued, with the addition of a piece of fresh butter

title: Štika na modro (Pike Blue)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- vinegar
- water
- celery
- carrot
- onion
- parsley
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon peel
- pepper
- allspice
- egg with shell
- carp scales (if available)
- saffron (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the pike thoroughly and rinse in several waters
2. Cut into pieces and salt
3. Pour hot vinegar diluted with water over so the pike is submerged
4. Place underneath star-cut celery, carrot, 3 onions, parsley, thyme, bay leaf, a piece of lemon peel, several whole peppercorns and allspice
5. Let it cook together
6. Carefully place on a deep dish
7. Arrange several whole pieces of vegetables around it
8. Leave the rest on the baking dish and add a finely crushed whole egg with shell; if carp scales are available, cook for a quarter hour
9. If it should be golden-yellow, add saffron
10. Tie a cloth to the legs of an overturned chair, pour the liquid onto it, and when it has dripped through into the dish, pour it over the pike and place on ice
11. When serving, wrap the dish in a cloth soaked in hot water so it unmolds more easily
12. Serve with oil, vinegar and pepper on the side

title: Štika Pečená (Roasted Pike)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- sardines, sliced into pieces
- sour cream
- eggs

## Directions

1. Clean the pike and rinse it in several waters
2. Salt it and roll in an arch
3. Place on small sticks arranged crosswise on a baking sheet
4. Pierce it with sardines sliced into pieces
5. Spread with sour cream, place in the oven, and baste with sour cream until baked
6. Make eggs dressed in sour cream, place them around the fish, and serve warm

title: Štika po Holandsku (Pike, Holland Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salted water
- wine
- butter
- anchovy fillets
- parsley
- bread or roll
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scrape, clean and gut the pike, cut into pieces.
2. Bring two parts salted water and one part wine to boil, place the pike in it and let cook for half an hour.
3. Meanwhile melt a quarter pound of butter, add three ounces of cleaned and finely chopped anchovies, a teaspoon of chopped green parsley and let fry for five minutes.
4. Remove the pike onto a dish, sprinkle with grated bread.
5. Pour over it the prepared butter, into which you must first strain the juice from one lemon.

title: Štika s aspikem (Pike in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike head and tail
- water
- vinegar
- onion
- cloves
- salt
- bay leaves
- lemon
- pepper
- allspice
- egg whites
- wine
- gelatin
- oil
- capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and tail of the pike and let them sit salted for an hour
2. After an hour, dry and boil with water, vinegar, onion, cloves, salt, two bay leaves, sliced lemon, pepper and allspice
3. Skim foam as it rises; after a quarter hour remove the fish
4. Strain the sauce, boil, mix in 3 egg whites, a quarter liter of wine and a bit of gelatin
5. Oil a mold and decorate the bottom with sliced lemon, capers, and parsley leaves
6. Pour in aspic and let cool
7. Then place alternating layers of pike pieces and aspic until the mold is full

title: Štika s Brachovou Polévkou (Pike with Pea Soup)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 pike
- butter
- 1 3/4 deciliter (half a spoon) good wine
- 3 3/4 deciliter (1 spoon) good pea soup
- peppercorns
- piece of ginger
- flower
- pale roux
- roe from one small fish
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the pike thoroughly and salt it
2. If there are 2 smaller pike, roll them in an arch
3. Place a piece of butter underneath
4. Pour 1 3/4 deciliter of good wine and 3 3/4 deciliter of good pea soup
5. Add several peppercorns, a piece of ginger, and a little flower
6. Cook the pike until soft, carefully turn to the other side
7. Place the pike on a platter
8. Add pale roux to the soup and mix in ground roe from one small fish if desired
9. Boil briefly and strain over the fish

title: Štika s Bílou Omáčkou (Pike with White Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- 3 dekagrams (2 lots) butter
- small onion
- ground ginger
- flower
- nutmeg
- soup in which pike was cooked
- pale roux
- white sugar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse and salt the pike
2. Place butter on a pan, add onion, and heat until it becomes white
3. Add a little ground ginger, a little flower, and a small piece of nutmeg
4. Place the pre-cooked pike in salted water into this mixture and cover with a lid
5. Stew for a quarter hour; if the onion begins to burn, add soup in which the pike was cooked
6. Carefully place on a platter and add 7 deciliters (2 spoons) of soup
7. Make a pale roux and add it to the onion
8. Cook with a piece of white sugar, strain into a sauce bowl, and serve hot
9. The fish is served cold; mustard may also be added in a separate dish

title: Štika s kyselou omáčkou (Pike with Sour Cream Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- sardines
- butter
- white flour
- meat broth
- sour cream
- nutmeg flower
- white ginger
- capers

## Directions

1. Cook the pike in the same manner as blued pike
2. Remove the pike from the broth and arrange on a dish
3. Clean three or four sardines, rub them with a piece of butter
4. Add a tablespoon of white flour and mix well
5. Add about a small cup of meat broth and a small cup of thick sour cream, a little ground nutmeg flower and grated white ginger
6. Mix well and let simmer
7. Add a few capers, pour over the pike and serve

title: Štika s Rosolem (Pike with Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- pike head or carp head
- rosemary
- thyme
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- allspice
- saffron
- onion, finely chopped
- 4 hard eggs
- 3 1/2 deciliter (1 spoon) Austrian wine
- vinegar diluted with water, 5 1/4 deciliter (1 1/2 spoons)
- celery, split
- parsley
- carrot
- garlic
- lemon zest
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Scrape the pike, clean thoroughly and cook in salted water
2. Let cool, remove skin from the head, cut off the head, and split the pike lengthwise in half
3. Remove all bones and place pike in a mold or on a platter
4. Wash the pike head or carp head, rosemary, thyme, and bay leaf; add peppercorns, allspice, a little saffron, finely chopped onion, and 4 hard eggs
5. Pound everything in a mortar
6. Pour 3 1/2 deciliter of Austrian wine and vinegar diluted with water (5 1/4 deciliter) on a baking sheet
7. Salt lightly and add split celery, parsley, carrot, a little garlic, chopped onion, lemon zest, and stiff snow from 2 egg whites
8. Add the pounded mixture, boil for a quarter hour
9. Pour it through cloth tied to the legs of an overturned chair, let it drip
10. Pour half the liquid over the pike and put half in a separate container
11. Place both in a cold place
12. When cooled, turn out the aspic from the container, cut decorative pieces with a mold, and arrange around the pike
13. This method can also be used for carp, tench, and salmon; generally all white-fleshed fish

title: Štika s ústřicemi (Pike with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- butter
- anchovies
- pike liver
- oysters or mussels
- bread or roll
- wine
- flower
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook the pike in the ordinary way au bleu (like carp).
2. Place a piece of butter in a small pot, about four anchovies, well chopped pike liver and several oysters or mussels, add grated bread or slice of white bread soaked in wine, a little flower, a little finely chopped lemon peel, mix everything well with wine.
3. Add broth in which the pike was cooked and let it simmer just a little.
4. Pour this over the fish and finish it with lemon juice, or decorate the dish with rounded lemon quarters arranged in a ring.
5. Perch, tench and other white fish may be prepared the same way.

title: Štika se Šalvěji (Pike with Sage)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- fresh sage
- fresh butter
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the pike and rinse it in several waters
2. Cook in salted water
3. When cooked, place on a platter
4. Finely chop fresh sage and sprinkle over the fish
5. Pour heated fresh butter over it

title: Štika se Žampiony (Pike with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- 75 grams (5 lots) fresh butter
- bundle of parsley
- various vegetable roots
- vinegar
- cleaned mushrooms, sliced into leaves
- fish sauce
- water in which peas were cooked
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the pike into pieces and salt it
2. After half an hour, dry it and place on a baking sheet with fresh butter, bundle of parsley, various vegetable roots, and vinegar
3. Cook for a quarter hour in water in which peas were cooked
4. After a quarter hour, carefully remove the skin
5. Stew several cleaned mushrooms sliced into leaves and cook them with fish sauce
6. Pour the fish sauce over the pike

title: Štika se žampiony (Pike with Mushrooms)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- fresh butter
- parsley
- vegetable roots
- vinegar
- water
- pea cooking water
- mushrooms
- fish sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the pike into pieces and salt
2. After half an hour, dry
3. Place on a baking dish with 75 grams of fresh butter, a bundle of parsley, various vegetable roots, vinegar
4. Cook for a quarter hour in water in which peas were cooked
5. After a quarter hour, carefully remove the skin
6. Braise several mushrooms cleaned and sliced
7. Cook with fish sauce and pour over the pike

title: Štika se žloutkovou omáčkou (Pike with Egg Yolk Sauce)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- medium pike
- water
- vinegar
- vegetable roots
- salt
- onion
- bay leaves
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- broth
- egg yolks
- mushrooms
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the pike, roll it and cook gently in water with vinegar, vegetable roots, salt, onion, bay leaves and pepper
2. Place on a dish and cover
3. Prepare the sauce from 75 grams of hot butter, a spoonful of flour, broth mixed with 8 egg yolks
4. Add braised mushrooms and juice from half a lemon
5. Bring to a boil and pour over the pike

title: Štika sekaná (Chopped Pike)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- onion
- butter
- bread
- milk
- egg yolks
- anchovy butter
- salt
- marjoram
- bread crumbs
- cheese
- lard
- capers or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. From cleaned pike, cut the sides away from the ribs so the head and remaining skeleton remain joined by a strip of meat on the back
2. Chop the cut meat finely with two onions fried in butter, two bread rolls soaked in milk, three egg yolks, anchovy butter, salt and marjoram
3. Spread this mixture back on the cleaned ribs so the pike regains its original shape
4. Place in a greased pan
5. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and cheese
6. Brush with fat and cover with lardoons
7. Bake until done
8. Serve with caper or anchovy sauce

title: Štika smažená (Fried Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- pepper
- sardine butter
- flour
- egg
- roll grated or crackers
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Clean the pike well, cut into pieces
2. Salt, sprinkle with ground pepper and let lie covered for an hour
3. Dry the pike with a clean cloth, spread lightly with sardine butter
4. Coat in flour, dip in beaten egg, coat in grated roll or crackers
5. Fry in clarified butter

title: Štika v slané vodě vařená (Boiled Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- water
- butter
- potatoes
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the pike well, remove entrails and fins, coil it into a ring shape, place the tail in the mouth, bind the mouth firmly with thread.
2. Place the pike in a pot, pour enough water to cover it completely, bring to fire and let it cook until tender, then place it to the side on a warm spot.
3. When ready to serve, carefully remove it with a skimmer onto a dish, arrange around it small round potatoes boiled in salted water.
4. Pour generously with hot fresh butter in which you have fried finely chopped green parsley.
5. Be especially careful that the pike does not overcook.

title: Štika vařená (Boiled Pike)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salted water
- onion
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Scrape and clean the pike, removing innards
2. Bend it into an arch and tie it
3. Place in boiling salted water and cook for a quarter to half hour until done
4. Meanwhile, chop half an onion and fry it to golden color in butter
5. Carefully place the pike on a dish
6. Sprinkle with toasted bread crumbs and the fried onion

title: Štrudel (Apple Strudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams (16 lots) flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- water
- butter
- curd cheese
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg whites
- 9 dekagrams (6 lots) butter
- lemon zest, grated with half its sugar
- 9 dekagrams sugar
- 15 grams (1 lot) ground almonds
- cinnamon
- raisins
- sour cream or milk

## Directions

1. Mix 24 dekagrams flour with 2 eggs, 1 egg yolk, salt and water
2. Work the dough and cover with a warm baking pan, let it rest for a while
3. Roll out the dough, then stretch it as thinly as possible on a floured cloth
4. Brush the stretched dough with butter
5. Prepare a filling from curd cheese, 2 eggs, 2 egg whites, 9 dekagrams butter, lemon zest grated with half its sugar, 9 dekagrams sugar, 15 grams ground almonds, cinnamon and raisins
6. Spread the filling on the dough, roll it up
7. Place in a greased baking pan and pour sour cream or milk over it
8. Bake until golden

title: Štrudel (Apple Strudel)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 1 egg
- 3 tablespoons warm butter
- 1.75 deciliters (1/4 žejdlík) water
- apples
- 2 rolls
- almonds
- raisins
- 12 dekagrams (8 lots) sugar mixed with 7.5 grams (1/2 lot) cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board with a well in the center
2. Mix in a bowl 1 egg, 3 tablespoons warm butter, 1.75 deciliters water and pour into the flour
3. Work the dough for 1/2 hour without adding more flour, so it doesn't stick to the board
4. Cover with a warm pan and let rest for 1 hour
5. Meanwhile, core and slice apples into thin pieces
6. Grate 2 rolls and fry them in butter
7. Scald almonds, peel and chop them
8. Rinse and pick over raisins
9. Dissolve 12 dekagrams sugar mixed with cinnamon and a piece of butter
10. Spread a clean white cloth on the table, sprinkle with flour, place dough in the center
11. Carefully stretch the dough so it doesn't tear
12. Trim the edges, sprinkle dough with butter, then with fried roll crumbs, prepared raisins, almonds, apples and the cinnamon sugar mixture
13. Lift one edge of the cloth, roll up the strudel, carefully place in a greased pan
14. Brush with butter, bake in the oven
15. Slice into pieces and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar
16. Instead of apples, quark cheese may be used

title: Štěrbák (Endive)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8-10 pieces beautiful yellow endive
- Salt
- Water
- Fresh butter
- 2 cups sour cream
- 0.5 teaspoon ground sugar
- Ground flowers
- Various fried vegetables for garnish

## Directions

1. Choose 8-10 pieces of beautiful yellow endive with unblemished inner leaves not nibbled by snails
2. Remove outer green leaves
3. Wash yellow leaves and cut into pieces, pressing out water
4. Cook in salted water until tender like spinach that can be easily crushed between fingers
5. Drain and let cool
6. Chop finely
7. Melt fresh butter on the stove, add chopped endive and stew until thickened
8. Add 2 cups sour cream, salt, 0.5 teaspoon ground sugar and ground flowers
9. Simmer about 15 minutes
10. Stir in 1 more tablespoon fresh butter
11. Place on a platter and garnish with various fried vegetables
12. This is a delicate and rare dish that also makes an excellent salad

title: Štěrbák (Scorzonera)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh butter
- Scorzonera
- Salt
- 2 cups good sour cream
- 1/2 teaspoon ground sugar
- Ground flowers

## Directions

1. Slice the scorzonera, squeeze water from them, then cook in salted water so that you can easily mash them like spinach between your fingers.
2. Drain the water from the scorzonera, let it cool and chop it very finely.
3. Melt a piece of fresh butter on the stove, add the chopped scorzonera and let everything stew until it thickens.
4. Add two cups of good sour cream, salt, add 1/2 teaspoon ground sugar and a little ground flowers.
5. Let it stew for about another quarter hour, then mix in another spoonful of fresh butter and remove to a dish.
6. You can garnish it with various fried foods.

title: Štěrk čili ovčácké buchty z pohanky (Buckwheat Shepherd's Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Buckwheat
- water or milk
- butter
- salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook buckwheat in water or milk with butter and salt, stirring constantly.
2. Allow the porridge to cool.
3. Mix in 4 egg yolks and fold in snow made from 4 egg whites, mixing everything together.
4. Grease a baking pan and bake the mixture in the oven.

title: Šunka s Míchanými Vejci (Ham with Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked ham
- Butter
- Six eggs
- Green parsley or chives (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop cooked ham into small pieces
2. Place in a pan with hot butter
3. When slightly browned, break several eggs into it
4. Mix well together
5. Optionally mix in some chopped green parsley or chives

title: Šunka s vejci (Ham and Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled ham
- butter
- eggs
- potatoes

## Directions

1. From nice boiled ham, cut thin slices about the size of a palm, warm a piece of butter in a pan, let the ham brown on both sides, then arrange it on a heated platter and place one fried egg on each piece.
2. Serve with sliced and fried potatoes.

title: Šunka s Vejci (Ham and Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked ham
- Butter
- Eggs
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Slice cooked ham into thin slices about the size of a palm
2. Heat a piece of butter in a pan
3. Fry the ham on both sides
4. Arrange the ham on a heated dish
5. Place one fried egg on each slice of ham
6. Serve with sliced and fried potatoes

title: Šunka s vejci po anglicku (Ham with Eggs English Style)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Instead of bacon, one can also use ham cut into fine slices or cubes; everything else is arranged as in the previous recipe, or eggs are prepared scrambled in butter, and these are then mixed with the ham.

title: Šunka v aspiku (Ham in Aspic)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Small ham
- Meat broth or aspic
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Capers
- Oil

## Directions

1. Cook the small ham and allow to cool
2. Remove the skin from the ham
3. Grease a mold with oil and place in ice
4. Pour liquid meat broth or aspic on the bottom of the mold and allow to set
5. Arrange sliced ham tastefully in the mold
6. Layer sliced hard-boiled eggs on top of the ham
7. Sprinkle everything with capers
8. Pour aspic over and allow to set completely in ice
9. To empty the mold, place it briefly in lukewarm water and turn the contents out onto a plate

title: Šunka v teste pečená (Ham Roasted in Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- bread dough
- salt

## Directions

1. If the ham is quite salty as is customary here, soak it overnight in a large dish so it is completely submerged in water. If the ham is only slightly salted as it is usually salted in the old country, then you can do without soaking.
2. Wash the ham nicely, trim the skin and lard and wrap about a finger's thickness in bread dough, but so that no hole remains.
3. Pierce the dough on top several times so it does not crack, then place the ham thus prepared on a shallow roasting pan or roaster and let it bake slowly in the oven for four or five hours; then remove the bread crust from the ham and place it on a platter.

title: Šunková Nádivka (Ham Stuffing)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 deca grated hard bread roll
- broth
- 7 deca marrow
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- 2 eggs
- 23 deca finely chopped ham

## Directions

1. Soak grated bread roll in broth and squeeze out
2. Add marrow browned with onion and parsley
3. Add pepper, 2 eggs, and finely chopped ham
4. Mix everything together well
5. Use as a very tasty stuffing

title: Šunkové sandvice (Ham Sandwich)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-cooked ham
- bread slices
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove all fat from well-cooked ham
2. Slice ham thinly and equally
3. Cut these slices into smaller pieces so that three or four pieces fit in one sandwich
4. Butter prepared bread slices
5. Arrange ham pieces on one slice
6. Cover with another slice
7. Press together gently and serve
8. Alternatively, if only one large slice of meat is placed in the sandwich, use smaller pieces as described above to prevent the meat from falling out when bitten
9. Ham may also be finely chopped
source_title: Nová domácí kuchařka pro česko-americké hospodyně

title: Švestková nádivka (Plum Filling)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- dried plums
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Wash the dried plums
2. Cook them
3. Chop finely
4. Stew in their juice with sugar, cinnamon, and finely chopped lemon zest

title: Švestková povidla (Plum Preserves)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh or dried plums
- Sugar
- Cinnamon (optional)
- Lemon zest (optional)

## Directions

1. Carefully select and clean the plums in water
2. Cook them thoroughly
3. Remove the pits
4. Chop them completely and finely
5. Add broth from the cooked plums as needed
6. Many housewives add ground cinnamon, finely chopped lemon zest, and other optional additions for pleasant flavor
7. Store in containers

title: Švestková povidla (Prune Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried prunes
- piece of fresh butter
- finely chopped lemon zest
- ground clove
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cook dried prunes, chop them finely or pass through a machine, or use prune butter.
2. Place in a saucepan and add a piece of fresh butter, some finely chopped lemon zest, some ground clove, and enough sugar to make it sufficiently sweet.
3. Let simmer briefly, then cool.
4. If too thick, stir in some warm broth, or if you don't have that, some water.

title: Švestkové Knedlíky (Prune Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- two eggs
- sour cream or good milk
- salt
- beautiful prunes
- fried bread roll, curd cheese, gingerbread, or ground poppy seed
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour with two eggs and as much sour cream or good milk as needed for thick dough
2. Salt and knead well
3. Roll out on a board, cut into pieces about the size of a walnut, and wrap a beautiful prune in each piece
4. Boil the dumplings in boiling salted water, carefully lift them from the bottom of the pot with a spoon, and when they float to the top and cook a little longer, remove them with a strainer to a dish
5. Sprinkle them with either fried bread roll, curd cheese, gingerbread, or ground poppy seed, and pour melted butter over them
6. You can also add a piece of butter to the dough
7. The pits can be removed from the prunes beforehand; if the prunes are sour, you can put a piece of sugar in place of the pit before wrapping

title: Švestkový Dort Loupáčkový (Plum Pastry Cake)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe plums
- sugar
- cinnamon
- pastry strips

## Directions

1. Clean ripe plums, split them, and remove the pits
2. Arrange the plums on pastry cake
3. Sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon
4. Decorate with pastry strips
5. Bake well until golden
6. During baking, brush the cake with beaten egg yolk

title: Švestkový kompot (Plum Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe plums
- hot water
- sugar
- cold water
- blanched almonds
- lemon peel
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place ripe plums on a sieve and pour hot water over them
2. Peel them and place on a clay baking sheet one next to another
3. Sprinkle with sugar and pour cold water over
4. Cover and braise slowly
5. Alternatively: pit the parboiled and peeled plums and insert half a blanched almond in place of the pit
6. Place on a covered baking sheet, sprinkle with sugar, add lemon peel and whole cinnamon

title: Švestkový kompot (Plum Compote)
author: Marie Dumková
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe plums
- sugar
- cold water

## Directions

1. Pass the ripe plums through a sieve
2. Pour hot water over them
3. Peel them
4. Arrange them one next to another on an earthenware baking dish
5. Sprinkle with sugar
6. Pour cold water over them
7. Cover and cook slowly

title: Švestkový kompot (Prune Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe prunes
- Sugar
- A piece of cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Red wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut ripe prunes in half and remove the pits
2. Cook them with sugar, a piece of cinnamon and lemon peel cut into thin strips
3. When they soften, remove them to a dish and pour thickly boiled juice over them
4. If you wish them to be particularly delicate, peel them and add a little red wine

title: Švestkový kompot (Prune Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe plums
- sugar
- cinnamon stick
- lemon zest, cut into thin strips

## Directions

1. Halve ripe plums and remove the pits.
2. Cook them with sugar, a piece of cinnamon and lemon zest cut into thin strips, and when they become soft, take them out on a dish and pour thickly cooked syrup over them.

title: Švestkový nákyp (Prune Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 3 tablespoons melted butter or lard
- salt
- 1 cup flour
- 2 small teaspoons baking powder
- stewed prunes
- cinnamon
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat 2 whole eggs, add 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 cup milk, and 3 tablespoons melted butter or lard
2. Season with a pinch of salt and mix in 1 cup flour with 2 small teaspoons baking powder
3. Butter a metal dish and pour in half the batter
4. Layer with stewed prunes from which the pits have been removed, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
5. Pour the remaining batter over the top so the prunes are completely covered
6. Bake for about half an hour and serve with whipped cream
7. During peach season, fresh peaches may be used instead, making it even more delicious

title: Švestkový nákyp na jiný způsob (Prune Pudding, Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 cup sugar
- grated almonds
- lemon zest
- butter
- 1/2 pound dried prunes
- 2 grated houskas
- 6 egg whites
- rum

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar until it froths
2. Add several grated almonds, chopped lemon zest, and a piece of butter
3. Cook 1/2 pound dried prunes, strain the liquid onto 2 grated houskas
4. Chop the prunes and add them with the houska to the beaten yolks
5. Finally fold in stiff peaks made from 6 egg whites
6. Pour the batter into a well-buttered mold
7. Bake for one hour in a water bath by setting the mold in a deep dish of hot water, cover well, and place in the oven
